
======================================================================
PAGE: 'aucdet IX
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/'aucdet_IX
======================================================================

'aucdet IX
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Intact
(
2365
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
'aucdet IX
was the
inhabited
ninth
planet
of its
star system
. This planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
and was home to the
Federation Medical Collection Station
.
In
2365
, the
USS
Enterprise
-D
arrived at the planet to pick up
specimens
of
plasma plague
for transport to
Science Station Tango Sierra
. At that time,
Lieutenant Commander
Hester Dealt
was the
commander
of the station. (
TNG
: "
The Child
")
A clear spelling
While this planet's
name
was spelled as 'audet IX in the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 3rd ed., p. 1, it appeared as 'aucdet IX in both the episode's
script
and on an on-screen display monitor in the episode.
"O'det Nine" was the pronunciation for this planet's name from the
script
pronunciation guide.
The planet model first used here for 'aucdet IX was later used for
Gagarin IV
("
Unnatural Selection
"),
Galorndon Core
("
The Enemy
"),
Barzan II
("
The Price
"),
Jouret IV
("
The Best of Both Worlds
"), and
Bilana III
("
New Ground
"). For its appearance as 'aucdet IX, the model had a
blue
color
.
External links
'aucdet
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
'aucdet IX
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Re-Used Planets in TNG
at
Ex Astris Scientia

======================================================================
PAGE: 2279 PL
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/2279_PL
======================================================================

2279 PL
was a position in
space
located near
2466 PM
. It was approximately
22.3 parsecs
beyond
Cestus III
and the
Federation
's chart limits in
2267
.
In that year, a
Gorn starship
passed through this position as it was pursued by the
USS
Enterprise
. (
TOS
: "
Arena
")

======================================================================
PAGE: 2466 PM
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/2466_PM
======================================================================

2466 PM
was a position in
space
located near
2279 PL
. It was approximately
22.3 parsecs
beyond
Cestus III
and the
Federations
chart limits in
2267
. A previously unexplored
solar system
, in
Metron space
, was located at this position.
In that year, a
Gorn starship
neared this position as it was pursued by the
USS
Enterprise
and began to turn away from it when both starships were stopped by the
Metrons
. (
TOS
: "
Arena
")

======================================================================
PAGE: 40 Eridani A I
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/40_Eridani_A_I
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
40 Eridani A I
Class:
B
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
3196
)
Satellite(s):
None
Location:
In
orbit
of
40 Eridani A
Vulcan system
Vulcan sector
Beta Quadrant
~16
light years
from
Sol
40 Eridani A I
was the
first
planet
orbiting
the
star
40 Eridani A
in the
Vulcan system
. This planet was located in the
Vulcan sector
,
Beta Quadrant
, and was approximately 16
light years
from
Sol
.
This uninhabited
class B planet
had no
moons
.
History
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet was within the enclosed region. (
SA
: "
300th Night
")
Stellar cartography
In
2259
, the planet's name, its position in the system, and its orbital path were seen on a display graphic of the shuttle
Cervantes
. (
SNW
: "
Charades
")
This planet was only mentioned in writing.
In 2022, this graphic was first seen in a tweet by Timothy Peel. For the episode "Charades", text about the 40 Eridani system and the Kerkhovian system were added to the graphic.
[1]
[2]
The current location of 40 Eridani A I in the Beta Quadrant was based on the most recent star charts seen in
Star Trek: Strange New Worlds
and
Star Trek: Section 31
.
External link
Ket-Cheleb
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: 892-IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/892-IV
======================================================================

892-IV
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Diameter:
7,917
miles
Affiliation:
Non-aligned (
Roman Empire
)
Status:
Habitable
(
2268
)
Satellite(s):
2
Moons
Native species:
892-IV natives
Location:
System 892
Unnamed sector
Beta Quadrant
892-IV
was the
inhabited
fourth
planet
of the
892
system
. This planet was located in a
sector
of the
Beta Quadrant
that had not been surveyed by the
Federation
until the early
2260s
. Classified as
M-class
, the planet was orbited by two
moons
and was the
homeworld
of the
892-IV natives
, a
pre-warp
humanoid
species
.
Commander
Spock
, the
science officer
of the
USS
Enterprise
, when scanning the planet, noted that 892-IV had a striking resemblance to
Earth
. However, as much as there were similarities, there were differences. The proportion of
land
to
water
,
density
(5.5
g/cm
3
), and
atmosphere
(78%
nitrogen
, 21%
oxygen
) were exactly like those on Earth. However, unlike Earth, the
diameter
at the
equator
(7,917
miles
) and layout of the
land masses
were different. (
TOS
: "
Bread and Circuses
")
History
This planet was cited as evidence of
Hodgkin's Law of Parallel Planetary Development
. The
English language
had evolved, and a
parallel
of the
Roman Empire
existed here.
One of the
buildings
used from footage for shots of the City was the
California Palace of the Legion of Honor
whose front had the words
HONNEUR ET PATRIE
, suggesting that the
French language
also evolved on the planet.
By the mid-
23rd century
, the planet had reached the development of Earth's
20th century
. Technology included
radio
communications,
road
system and powered transportation (such as the
Jupiter 8
), but no
atomic
power.
Gladiatorial
arena games
became
televised
. With each
century
, even
slaves
received rights to
medicine
and
government
payments
in their
old age
; the Empire eventually managed to stop
rebellions
and even wars for four hundred years, achieving stability.
The Romans believed in a pantheon of
gods
, but a nascent movement known as
Children of the Son
, a parallel to
Christianity
on Earth, started to be embraced by gladiators, slaves, and even
senators
, disrupting the stability of the conservative Empire.
In
2262
, the
Federation
class 4 stardrive
survey vessel
SS
Beagle
was badly damaged by a
meteor
in the system and a
landing party
beamed
down to obtain
iridium
ore
for repairs. The party was met by the locals, led by
Proconsul
Claudius Marcus
who considered it potentially ruinous to his highly conservative society if word of its existence should be carried elsewhere; the crewmembers were regarded as captured
barbarians
and given a choice: Living quietly in the roman society while keeping their extraterrestrial background a secret or forced to fight as gladiators. The
Beagle'
s
captain
R.M. Merik
became
First Citizen
.
Captain
James T. Kirk
and the crew of the
Enterprise
visited the planet 892-IV
six years later
, on
stardate
4040.7
, after investigating the
debris
of the
Beagle
. They were held captives both by
fugitives
and later the proconsul and Merik. The
Enterprise
managed to escape and speculated that the new
religion
might bring the transformation of the Empire, much like Christianity did to the Roman Empire. (
TOS
: "
Bread and Circuses
")
Geography
892-IV had several
oceans
and at least three
continents
. The land that the
landing party
from the
Enterprise
visited was politically subdivided into a number of
provinces
.
City Arena
City Prison
Massachusetts Institute of Technology
Appendices
Background information
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 54),
FGC
-892 IV was located in the
Beta Quadrant
. The official name of the
government
was the Roman Empire. It was a non-aligned world that hadn't yet become
warp-capable
. The
capital city
was Rome (
β
). The dominant species were the humanoid
citizens
and barbarians. In
2378
, there were an estimated 8.6 billion people living on the planet. The
society
's technology was nearly equivalent to the mid-
20th century
on Earth.
Apocrypha
This planet was described in the
The Worlds of the Federation
(p. 108) as being very similar to Earth, to the point that the shape of its continents and other planets of its solar system were nearly identical. Both the original and the remastered version of "Bread and Circuses" showed a very different continental arrangement. This work established the indigenous name of the planet as
Magna Roma
. It was located in the UFC 892 (
β
) system.
The planet was revisited in the
novel
The Captains' Honor
set 100 years after the encounter by the original
Enterprise
crew
. The novel detailed how the alternate Rome conquered their world, with the key divergence being that this world's Rome was able to extend into all of
Africa
(or the equivalent of such) and use the captured resources and manpower to launch wars and take over the rest of the planet. After the visit of Kirk and company, the Children of the Son significantly pacified the planet, and a century later the Romans were now
Federation
members and were participating in galactic affairs. The Romans possess at least one all-Roman
Constitution
-class
starship, the former
USS
Farragut
renamed the USS
Centurion
, and run according to their own methods and principles.
Furthermore, the novel
The Captain's Honor
detailed how the history of Magna Roma diverged from Earth's when Lucius Aelius Sejanus succeeded in killing Emperor Tiberius and his entire family, making himself emperor in his place, thus strengthening the Roman Empire. In Earth's history, Sejanus' attempt to overthrow Tiberius failed and he was strangled in prison.
In the novel
The Poisoned Chalice
, the people of Magna Roma were descended from Humans transplanted from Earth by the
Preservers
. A Magna Romanii named Atia served as first officer of the USS
Lionheart
.
External links
Magna Roma
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Planets in TOS and TOS Remastered
at
Ex Astris Scientia

======================================================================
PAGE: AR-558
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/AR-558
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
AR-558
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Cardassian Union
(
24th century
)
Status:
Intact
(
3196
)
Location:
Chin'toka system
Unnamed sector
Alpha Quadrant
AR-558
was a small barren
planet
in the
Chin'toka
system
. This planet was found in a
sector
of the
Alpha Quadrant
. In the
23rd century
, this system was in the
Kiley Local
sector
.
The
terrain
of AR-558 consisted primarily of
rocky
surfaces and
caverns
and was generally inhospitable to
humanoid
species
for any extended period of time. (
SNW
: "
Strange New Worlds
";
DS9
: "
The Siege of AR-558
", "
When It Rains...
")
History
In the
24th century
, AR-558 was in
Cardassian
space
.
AR-558's symbol
During the
Dominion War
, the
Dominion
established a
communication
relay station
into a rock face on a steep
mountain
on this planet. It was the largest
communications array
in the sector. Following a victory in the Chin'toka system, (
See:
First Battle of Chin'toka
) the Dominion was driven from the array by
Starfleet
troops
who hoped to use the captured array to tap into Dominion communications. The array was held by these troops for five
months
without replacements.
The planet proved very difficult to defend. The Chin'toka system was the site of repeated confrontation, making re-supply to the planet impractical for extended periods. The
Federation
troops guarding the array selected a suitably defensible position at the closed end of a subterranean
cavern
but were besieged almost from the start. Repeated Dominion attacks sapped both force strength and
morale
. Even though the location of their strong point forced the Dominion to advance through a narrow
canyon
, the
casualties
suffered by the Federation rendered it impractical to take full advantage of the terrain and forced them to wait for each successive assault while hoping for re-supply.
Although there were initially one hundred and fifty personnel posted there, by the time the
USS
Defiant
made a
supply run
to the planet in mid-
2375
that number had been reduced to only forty-three surviving troops from repeated
Jem'Hadar
attacks and the prevalence of
subspace
anti-personnel mines
,
nicknamed
"
Houdinis
". It was only when the crew of the
Defiant
, working with the troops, were able to disarm the mines and re-purpose them to attack advancing Jem'Hadar that the Federation gained some semblance of advantage in the situation. The casualties were heavy, however, and included
Captain
Loomis
,
Commander
Parker
,
Lieutenant
Larkin
,
Crewman
Kellin
,
McGreevey
, and
Vargas
.
Relief troops and
engineers
were later sent aboard the
USS
Veracruz
to replace the surviving troops. (
DS9
: "
The Siege of AR-558
")
Nog
experienced
flashbacks
to the time he defended AR-558 several
weeks
later. (
DS9
: "
It's Only a Paper Moon
")
Subsequent to the defeat of the
Federation Alliance
in the
Second Battle of Chin'toka
, AR-558 was retaken by the Dominion. (
DS9
: "
The Changing Face of Evil
")
The subspace relay was active in the
32nd century
. (
SA
: "
Beta Test
", "
300th Night
", "
Rubincon
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, AR-558 was within the blast radius of one of the mines. (
SA
: "
300th Night
", "
Rubincon
")
Stellar cartography
In
3195
, this relay's position was labeled on a
Federation
star chart
which was displayed in
Starfleet Academy
's
Stellar Cartography
laboratory
. (
SA
: "
Beta Test
")
In
3196
, during the
Omega-47
crisis, the location of Subspace Relay AR-558 was depicted on star charts and readouts used across several locations on the
USS
Athena
to locate, monitor, and respond to the Omega-47 mines laid by the Venari Ral. (
SA
: "
Rubincon
")
Appendices
Background information
A map of the Chin'toka system
The name AR-558 was an
in-joke
, as the production number of "The Siege of AR-558" was 558. (
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 37)
AR-558's orbital position in the Chin'toka system was unclear. A map of Chin'toka, seen in "
Tears of the Prophets
", depicted at least four planets, two of which were clearly the two
binary planets
contested in that episode. This planet may be either of the two remaining planets or the moons of any of the planets.
The planet model for AR-558 was first used for the
Karemma homeworld
in "
The Search, Part I
". This model was used for
Cardassia Prime
, as well, in "
Trials and Tribble-ations
".
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
Display during teaser of "When It Rains..."
The
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine Technical Manual
identified the sector as the
Bajor sector
.
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I"), AR-558 was treated by the author,
Geoffrey Mandel
, as two separate places with both found in the Alpha Quadrant. The first was an antenna/communications relay. The second was a planet in a
binary star system
of two
F-type stars
. This planet was the site of a battle. This split was canonized in the
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
episode "
Beta Test
".
For
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Federation Historical Highlights, 2161-2385"), AR-558 was located close to the Chin'toka system in the Alpha Quadrant.
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 37 placed AR-558 in the Chin'toka system.
External links
AR-558
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Re-Used Planets in DS9
at
Ex Astris Scientia

======================================================================
PAGE: Aaamazzara
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Aaamazzara
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Aaamazzara
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
(
3196
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Native species:
Aaamazzarites
Location:
Epsilon Serpentis
system
Alpha Quadrant
~72
light years
from
Sol
Aaamazzara
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Epsilon Serpentis
system
. Located in the
Alpha Quadrant
, this planet was the
homeworld
of the
Aaamazzarites
and was approximately 72
light years
from
Sol
.
History
In the
24th century
,
Commander
Jerry Goldsmith
, a
crewmember
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
, was born on this non-
Federation
planet. In
2369
, his planet of origin was listed on the chart "USS
Enterprise
Personnel Database", which was displayed on a
PADD
accessed by
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
. (
TNG
: "
The Chase
",
okudagram
)
In the
32nd century
, Aaamazzara was a
Federation
member
planet. It was listed with other members on a video wall in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
Kids These Days
")
Stellar cartography
In the
3191
, this planet's special designation as a homeworld was noted on a
star chart
. (
DIS
: "
Red Directive
")
In
3195
, this planet's position was labeled on a
Federation
star chart
which was displayed in
Starfleet Academy
's
Stellar Cartography
laboratory
. Planets affiliated with the Federation, including this one, had
blue
labels. (
SA
: "
Beta Test
")
In
3196
, during the
Omega-47
crisis, the location of Aaamazzara (Therbia, Epsilon Serpentis) was depicted on star charts and readouts used across several locations on the
USS
Athena
to locate, monitor, and respond to the
Omega-47 mines
laid by the
Venari Ral
. This planet was outside the affected region. (
SA
: "
300th Night
", "
Rubincon
")
This planet was only mentioned in writing.
This planet was identified as the homeworld of the Aaamazzarites in background sources. This was an alien
species
seen in
Star Trek: The Motion Picture
.
In a listing of
Federation members
from the
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I"), Aaamazzara was listed as a member. The listing was dated in-universe to the year
2378
.
External link
Aaamazzara
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Aamaarazan homeworld
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Native species:
Aamaarazans
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
The
Aamaarazan homeworld
was the
home
planet
of the
Aamaarazans
. This planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
In
3196
, during the
Omega-47
crisis, the location of "Aamaarazan Homeworld" was depicted on
star charts
and readouts used across several locations on the
USS
Athena
to locate, monitor, and respond to the
Omega-47 mines
laid by the
Venari Ral
. This location was one of the few labeled with a star. (
SA
: "
300th Night
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Abaddon's space station
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Abaddon's_space_station
======================================================================

Abaddon's space station
Abaddon's space station
functioned as the hub of
Abaddon's Repository of Lost Treasures
. This
space station
was where
Abaddon
lived. It also housed his miscellaneous pieces of merchandise. (
VOY
: "
Alice
")
This space station was described in the episode's script as "
a weathered alien space station... surrounded by an expansive
shipyard
of clearly derelict vessels.
"
According to the
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 92), the USS
Voyager
visited this space station on
stardate
53220.

======================================================================
PAGE: Acamar III
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Acamar_III
======================================================================

Acamar III
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Acamarian Ruling Council
Status:
Habitable
(
2366
)
Native species:
Acamarians
Parthas
Location:
Acamar
system
Unnamed sector
Beta Quadrant
~165
light years
from
Sol
Acamar III
was the
inhabited
third
planet
in the
Acamar
system
. This planet was located in a
sector
of the
Beta Quadrant
and was approximately 165
light years
from
Sol
. It was the
homeworld
of the
Acamarians
, a
warp-capable
humanoid
species
.
In
2366
, the
USS
Enterprise
-D
visited the planet to enlist the aid of
Sovereign
Marouk
to end the
Gatherer
raids
on
Federation
outposts
. Upon completion of this
mission
, the
Enterprise
returned the sovereign to her planet. (
TNG
: "
The Vengeance Factor
")
Geography
Hospitals
Vogt Public Health Clinic
Lakes
Magab Lake
Outposts
Starbase 80
(
United Federation of Planets
-Acamarian condominium)
Tezber
Prisons
Bruno Prison
Dylhuc Detention Center
Regions
Jamholt Region
Jimbern Regions
Liu Region
Niwray Border
Settlements
Felker City
Molnar City
Mesrop Village
Appendices
Background information
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Discovery
episode "
Magic to Make the Sanest Man Go Mad
".
"AWK-uh-mar" was the pronunciation for this planet's name from the
script
pronunciation guide.
The planet model for Acamar III was first used for
Starbase 6
("
The Schizoid Man
"). It was later used for
Ventax II
("
Devil's Due
") and
Krios
("
The Mind's Eye
").
For the
Star Trek: Prodigy
episode "
Let Sleeping Borg Lie
", UI graphic designer
Dominique Rossier
created an info panel for the
USS
Dauntless
mess hall
depicting the
Milky Way Galaxy
with planet and
Starfleet
space station
locations labeled. Identified as a planet, Acamar III was one of the locations mentioned on the chart. Although the panel did not appear in the episode, it can be seen at Rossier's artist page at Art Station.
[1]
Apocrypha
In the
RPG
sourcebook
Planets of the UFP
, Acamar III's native
fauna
included a large flightless
bird
called the
cralluck
, a six-
legged
mammalian
pack hunter called the
gryll
and a large burrowing
predator
called the
mooshor
.
Plant
life included a
green
vegetable
called
parthas
and a
lichen
called
vorvoss
.
Ever since the inclusion of the New Romulus (
β
) area in
Star Trek Online
, players can revisit Acamar III to mediate problems between the two groups over certain problems since their
reunification
. If the mediation was successful,
Tal Shiar
ships will arrive, revealing they were behind the problems.
External links
Acamar III
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Re-Used Planets in TNG
at
Ex Astris Scientia

======================================================================
PAGE: Achrady VII
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Achrady_VII
======================================================================

Achrady VII
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2366
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Achrady VII
was the
inhabited
seventh
planet
in its
star system
.  This planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
In
2366
, when
Counselor
Deanna Troi
tried to convince
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
to take a
vacation
, she claimed her
mother
, returning from a
conference
on Achrady VII, was making a stop at
Starbase 12
to await the
USS
Enterprise
there. (
TNG
: "
Captain's Holiday
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Adarak Prime
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Adarak_Prime
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Adarak Prime
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Cardassian
Dominion
(
2373
-
2375
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Location:
Cardassian space
Alpha Quadrant
Adarak Prime
was the
prime
planet
in its system. Located in the
Alpha Quadrant
, this planet was in
Cardassian
space
.
History
In
2375
, during the
Dominion War
, Adarak Prime was the location for a
weapons depot
, which was defended by a single
Cardassian
garrison
. This depot was considered by
Odo
and
Garak
as a vulnerable facility that the
Cardassian Liberation Front
could target for attack. They identified a weakness in the depot's perimeter defenses which could be exploited by a small team. Garak would disable the depot's security protocols. The rebels were against raiding the planet and killing their own countrymen.
Kira Nerys
replied by stating that during the
Occupation of Bajor
, she didn't want to attack any facility populated by
Bajorans
, but she did because they were
collaborators
. She also said that the
Dominion
would place Cardassians at every single one of their bases once they learned the rebels would not attack their own people. (
DS9
: "
When It Rains...
")
While Damar was receptive to the plan, it was unknown if the decision was ultimately made to attack the base.
Stellar cartography
In
3196
, during the
Omega-47
crisis, the location of Adarak Prime was depicted on
star charts
and readouts used across several locations on the
USS
Athena
to locate, monitor, and respond to the
Omega-47 mines
laid by the
Venari Ral
. This system was outside the barrier, and was in or near to
Cardassian space
. (
SA
: "
300th Night
", "
Rubincon
")
External link
Adarak Prime
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

.

's ability to recreate almost any location,

and

that she had not gotten to go before
enlisting
in

. (

: "

")

For the act of speaking to the public, please see
address
.
An address in California
An
address
was a designation for the
location
where one
lived
or
worked
. A
forwarding address
was an individual's new address, to which all their
mail
arriving
at the old address was to be forwarded.
In
2364
,
Doctor
Beverly Crusher
offered to give the newly revived from
cryogenic stasis
Clare Raymond
her
great-great-great-great-great-grandson
Thomas Raymond
's address so she could re-connect with her
descendants
. (
TNG
: "
The Neutral Zone
")
In
2367
, Dr.
Berel
determined that the address "
Rivas Jakara
" gave as his home location was in reality that of a
restaurant
. (
TNG
: "
First Contact
")
In
2369
,
Sirco Ch'Ano
's
name
and address was printed on the
shipping codes
for the
self-sealing stem bolts
he'd ordered. (
DS9
: "
Progress
")
In
2377
,
Leosa
left no forwarding address behind after leaving her
apartment
. (
VOY
: "
Inside Man
")

,

-

,

's

-

-

In the mid-
24th century

hospital
on this world permitted its
medical staff
to perform
DNA resequencing
, a type of
genetic engineering
, on their
patients
. This medical procedure was considered illegal in the
Federation
.
In
2348
, a seven-year-old
Julian Bashir
was taken by his
parents
,
Richard
and
Amsha Bashir
, to Adigeon Prime to undergo
accelerated critical neural pathway formation
. There, young Julian's
brain
was genetically modified, leading to mental and physical enhancement. He recalled being excited to see all the
aliens
. (
DS9
: "
Doctor Bashir, I Presume
")

:

name
from the

pronunciation guide.

novel
,

Starfleet
Commander
Charles Tucker
in order to undergo surgical alteration. In the novel, the planet was neutral and home to the Adigeons (
β
) (first mentioned in
Cathedral
) an
avian
culture
adept at various plastic surgeries and reconstructions. It was said to be located near

.

was closely allied with the

by
2152
. This planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or

.
In the early
22nd century
, the planet suffered from massive
corruption
problems caused by powerful
criminal
factions
. In
2122
, when the ruling government wanted to forge better relations with the
Vulcan government
, they asked the Vulcans for assistance. To accomplish this goal, the Vulcans dispatched 109
surgically altered
agents
who infiltrated the most criminal groups with the aim of toppling them. After this mission was accomplished and an
alliance
was signed, the operatives were recalled, though nineteen refused to return. (
ENT
: "
The Seventh
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Aia
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Aia
======================================================================

Aia
AKA:
Grief World
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Zhat Vash
Status:
Devastated
(
2385
)
Native species:
Aia native
Location:
Eightfold Stars
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Aia
, the
Grief World
, was a desolate
planet
that was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
. This planet was found in the middle of the
Eightfold Stars
, an
octonary star system
created some
200,000 to 300,000 years ago
by a
synthetic species
. That
civilization
had utterly wiped out their creators, after the latter turned on them.
Before leaving to another
dimension
, the synthetics left behind a repository of
memories
meant for future synthetic species, promising that if summoned by artificial lifeforms, the synthetic civilization would return to protect them, by way of wiping out all known biological life forms in the
universe
, as they had concluded that organic species will inevitably turn on synthetic species and try to destroy them.
Hundreds of years before the
24th century
, a group of
Romulans
discovered Aia while investigating the Eightfold Stars. They experienced the memory repository, but because it was intended for synthetic, not organic, minds, they experienced it as a dizzying array of horrific images and scenes of mass devastation, resulting in them misinterpreting it as a warning left behind by the ancient organic race against the creation of synthetic life, a warning that if artificial lifeforms were created, they would inevitably summon the "Destroyers" who had annihilated their civilization. Naming this "warning" the
Admonition
, they formed the
Zhat Vash
, a secret society dedicated to eliminating synthetics anywhere in the universe they emerged. Subsequent generations of Zhat Vash continued to visit Aia and submit themselves to Admonition, even though the process drove the majority of organics who experienced it irreparably
insane
.
One such group in the
2380s
was led by
Oh
and included
Ramdha
and
Narissa
. (
PIC
: "
Broken Pieces
")
Aia exterior filming, described in production materials as the "Field of Foreboding," took place at the
Blue Cloud Movie Ranch
in Santa Clarita, California on
2 July
2019
.
[1]
External link
Aia
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Ajilon Prime
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ajilon_Prime
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Ajilon Prime
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
(
3196
)
Status:
Habitable
(
2373
)
Location:
Ajilon
system
Near the
Federation-Klingon border
Beta Quadrant
Ajilon Prime
was the
inhabited
Class M
prime
planet
in the
Ajilon
system
. This system was located near the
Federation-Klingon border
in the
Beta Quadrant
. In the
24th century
, this planet was home to a
Federation
colony
with several settlements. The colonial government was partially administrated by
Resource
. The planet was located "three
days
at best" from
Deep Space 9
.
In early
2373
, the planet was attacked by the
Klingons
during the
Federation-Klingon War
, breaking the
cease fire
that was initiated a few
weeks
earlier. In the ensuing
Battle of Ajilon Prime
, the Klingon forces were quickly able to capture two settlements in the northern
hemisphere
, trap over half the colonists on the planet and then advance further. During the battle, colonists were evacuated to a base on
Tananda Bay
by
Resource
.
Reinforcements were dispatched aboard the
USS
Farragut
, but the vessel was destroyed before reaching the planet. The
USS
Defiant
reached the planet two days later, and Klingon forces pulled out following the reinstatement of the ceasefire. (
DS9
: "
Nor the Battle to the Strong
")
In the late
32nd century
, Ajilon Prime issued a
warrant
for
Caleb Mir
, who was suspected of
theft
on this planet. This information was recorded into the
Federation Prisoner Database
and available to
Torothan
law enforcement officers
in
3195
. (
SA
: "
Kids These Days
")
In the
32nd century
, Ajilon Prime was a
Federation
member
planet. It was listed with other members on a video wall in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
The Life of the Stars
")
Inhabitants
Personnel
See
:
Ajilon Prime personnel
Species on the planet
Bolian
Human
Klingon
Markalian
Paradan
Rotciv
Appendices
Background information
"AZH-zha-lawn" was the pronunciation for this planet's
name
from the
script
pronunciation guide.
The planet model for Ajilon Prime was first used for the
Ennis/Nol-Ennis moon
in "
Battle Lines
". It was used again for a
Cardassian
moon
in "
Improbable Cause
" and "
Treachery, Faith  and the Great River
" and the
Teplan homeworld
in "
The Quickening
". It was later used for
Koralis III
in "
Chimera
" and
Sappora VII
in "
Prodigal Daughter
".
The planet's classification was derived from the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 13.
At
StarTrek.com
, it was said that Ajilon Prime was the sole M-class planet in its system and that it was located in the
Archanis sector
.
[1]
(X)
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I"), Ajilon Prime was listed as a
Federation member
in
2378

======================================================================
PAGE: Akritirian maximum security detention facility
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Akritirian_maximum_security_detention_facility
======================================================================

Akritirian maximum security detention facility
Type:
Maximum security detention facility
Affiliation:
Akritirian
Location:
Akritirian space
Delta Quadrant
Status:
Active
(
2373
)
The
Akritirian maximum security detention facility
was a
space station
used as a
prison
, operated by the
Akritirian
government
, located somewhere in their
space
.
The prison held close to fifty inmates in a single chamber, with no access points save for a single small tunnel, the "chute", sealed by a
force field
. The chute lead to an
airlock
on the exterior, where ships docked periodically to deposit food and new prisoners. The fact that the prison was in space was largely unknown to the inmates, most of whom believed that the prison was  three hundred
meters
underground.
Life in the prison was savage. Inmates lived in squalor, and were fitted with a
synaptic stimulator
called a
clamp
which induced them to kill each other by inducing edginess and jumpiness, leading to people becoming aggressive and turning crazy.
In
2373
,
USS
Voyager
senior officers
Lieutenant
Tom Paris
and
Ensign
Harry Kim
were sent to this facility after being falsely convicted of a bombing on
Akritiri
. They were eventually rescued by a team from
Voyager
, led by
Captain
Kathryn Janeway
and
Lieutenant
Tuvok
. (
VOY
: "
The Chute
")
The Akritirian facility was designed by
Rick Sternbach
. The original layout featured six call blocks or station "arms", though the producers had it reduced to three.
[1]
According to the
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 86), the Akritiri Prison Satellite was located in interstellar space and not in the Akritiri
star system
.
People
See
:
Akritirian maximum security detention facility personnel

was a celestial location in the

.

manager

once saw the

acid punk
band
Gik'Tal
perform "
Gre'thor Paradise
" live on Akstin, during which they sang over a
chorus
of lamenting
dishonored
Klingons
on stage. (
LD
: "
We'll Always Have Tom Paris
")
This location was only mentioned in dialogue.

Alastria
Class:

Status:
Habitable
(
2371
)
Location:
Alastria system
~40,000
light years
from
Sikaris
Delta Quadrant
Alastria
was a
planet
in the
system
of the same name
. This planet was located in the
Delta Quadrant
and was approximately forty thousand
light years
from
Sikaris
.
By the late
24th century
, Alastria was at the uttermost limit of the
spatial trajector
, a
device
invented by the
Sikarians
. The planet was known for its
euphoria
-inducing
erosene winds
, the
dawn zephyr
. For
Eudana
, a Sikarian, the
woods
of Alastria were her favorite
place
.
In
2371
, she took
Ensign
Harry Kim
there, when the
USS
Voyager
visited Sikaris. (
VOY
: "
Prime Factors
")
While the Sikarians explained that they could reach all planets in the quadrant, it was not explicitly stated that Alastria too was in the
Delta Quadrant
. However, according to
StarTrek.com
and the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 14, this planet was located in this quadrant.

, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 14.

======================================================================
PAGE: Alcor IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Alcor_IV
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Alcor IV
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
43rd century
)
Native species:
Cyclops owl
Sorrowhawk
Location:
Alcor system
Alpha Quadrant
~82
light years
from
Sol
Alcor IV
was the
inhabited
fourth
planet
in the
Alcor system
. Located in the
Alpha Quadrant
, this planet was approximately 82
light years
from
Sol
.
Lifeforms
native to the planet included the
sorrowhawk
and the
cyclops owl
. A
body of water
on the planet known as the
Reach
was known for its distinct
sound
when the
wind
hit the
sea
from the southwest.
Craft talking about "having a line out" also implied
aquatic
life.
History
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet was within the blast radius of one of the mines. (
SA
: "
300th Night
")
After the
32nd century
, Alcor IV was settled by
Humans
who engaged in
farming
,
fishing
, and
hunting
, and eventually developed a unique
culture
.
Alcorians
typically did not tell others their
true name
. Instead, when two people fell in
love
, it was customary for one to give a name to the other. These Humans were unfamiliar with the concept of a
Tuesday
. They were known to have
tattoos
.
In the
43rd century
, the Humans of Alcor IV engaged in a
war
with the
V'draysh
for at least ten years, which diminished their numbers. One
soldier
,
Craft
, sheltered aboard the derelict
USS
Discovery
for some time. The ship's
artificial intelligence
Zora
would give him a
shuttlecraft
,
Funny Face
, with which to return to Alcor IV, though it would be a long and arduous journey. (
ST
: "
Calypso
")
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the film
Star Trek: Section 31
.

PAGE: Aldea
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Aldea
======================================================================

Aldea
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Non-aligned
Status:
Habitable
(
2364
)
Native species:
Aldeans
Location:
Epsilon Mynos
system
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Aldea
was a legendary
inhabited
planet
that was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
. Found in the
Epsilon Mynos
system
, it was the
homeworld
of the
Aldeans
, an advanced
humanoid
species
. In
2364
, the planet was discovered by the
USS
Enterprise
-D
.
Aldea was hidden from view for
millennia
by its powerful
planetary defense system
, devised by the
Progenitors
and run by the
Custodian
. The system included an enormous planet-wide
cloaking device
that hid it from
marauders
and other threats. In addition to the cloaking device, Aldea was defended by an immensely powerful
shield
that could only be penetrated by Aldean
technology
and a
repulsor beam
powerful enough to push
starships
several hundred
light years
distant. The safeguards allowed the people to pursue
culture
and become
artisans
in complete safety, but as a
society
, they became too reliant on the Custodian, forgetting the necessary technological backgrounds of how their own technology worked.
For thousands of
years
, the existence of Aldea was considered to be only a rumor, similar to the cultural
myths
of
Atlantis
or
Neinman
. However, by the mid-
24th century
, the planet-wide cloaking device was responsible for cumulative damage to the planet's
ozone layer
, resulting in widespread
radiation poisoning
from the
Aldean
sun
's
ultraviolet rays
, causing the
species
to become sterile. To try to rectify their dire situation, they attempted to
kidnap
various
Enterprise
-D
children
, including
Wesley Crusher
, to repopulate their planet. After the failed attempt, the Aldeans agreed to accept help from the
Enterprise
-D to relearn how to properly use their technology, never again to use their shield and cloaking device, and medical treatment reversed the radiation poisoning in the
population
, which would restore
fertility
in time. (
TNG
: "
When The Bough Breaks
")
Appendices
Background information
"all-DAY-uh" was the pronunciation for this planet's
name
from
StarTrek.com
.
[1]
(X)
The planet model first used here for Aldea was later used for
Vagra II
in "
Skin Of Evil
".
"Aldea" was a
Spanish
word which translated as "small village" or "hamlet."
Apocrypha
In the
RPG
sourcebook
Planets of the UFP
, Aldea was the
third
planet in the Epsilon Mynos system.
External links
Aldea
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Re-used planets in TNG
at
Ex Astris Scientia

======================================================================
PAGE: Aldebaran (planet)
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Aldebaran_(planet)
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Aldebaran
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
(
3196
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Location:
Aldebaran
system
Beta Quadrant
~67
light years
from
Sol
Aldebaran
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Aldebaran
system
,
Beta Quadrant
. It was approximately 67
light years
from
Sol
.
In the
32nd century
, Aldebaran was a
Federation
member
planet.
In
3196
, during
Nus Braka
's broadcasted "
trial
" of
Nahla Ake
and the
Federation
, the
Venari Ral News Network
claimed that this planet and
Halka
had joined the
Venari Ral Free State
with
dozens
of other former Federation members rallying around the
pirate
's
leadership
. (
SA
: "
Rubincon
")
This planet was only mentioned in writing.

======================================================================
PAGE: Aldebaran III
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Aldebaran_III
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Aldebaran III
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
(
3196
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Location:
Aldebaran
system
Beta Quadrant
~67
light years
from
Sol
For the similarly named planet, please see
Aldebaron III
.
Aldebaran III
was the
inhabited
third
planet
in the
Aldebaran
system
. Located in the
Beta Quadrant
, this planet was approximately 67
light years
from
Sol
. (
TOS
: "
The Deadly Years
";
TNG
: "
Up The Long Ladder
",
okudagram
)
History
During the
2260s
,
Doctors
Janet
and
Theodore Wallace
performed experiments using various
carbohydrate
compounds
to slow down the
degeneration
of
plant
life
at the
Federation
outpost
on this planet. (
TOS
: "
The Deadly Years
")
In the
32nd century
, Aldebaran III was a
Federation
member
planet. It was listed with other members on a video wall in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
Kids These Days
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet was within the enclosed region. (
SA
: "
300th Night
")
See also
Aldebaran Music Academy
Appendices
Background information
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star charts
appearing in episodes of
Star Trek: Discovery
,
Star Trek: Picard
, and
Star Trek: Strange New Worlds
.
In the
script
for "Prophet Motive", Aldebaran was listed in the pronunciation guide, where the term was pronounced as "al-DEB-uh-ron".
In
StarTrek.com
, Aldebaran III was identified as a long-time
Federation member
.
[1]
(X)
This website, and the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 16 – in an article about the Aldebaran system – treated
Aldebaron III
and Aldebaran III as the same planet.
In a listing of
Federation members
from the
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I"), Aldebaran III was listed as a member. The listing was dated in-universe to the year
2378
.
The current location of Aldebaran III in the Beta Quadrant was based on the most recent star charts seen in
Star Trek: Strange New Worlds
and
Star Trek: Section 31
.
External link
Aldebaran III
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Aldebaron III
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Aldebaron_III
======================================================================

Aldebaron III
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Habitable
(
2371
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
For the similarly named planet, please see
Aldebaran III
.
Aldebaron  III
was the
inhabited
third
planet
of its
star system
. This planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
. This
Federation
planet was the home to the
Aldebaron Colony
and a
Starfleet
installation.
In
2265
, the
USS
Enterprise
made a stopover at this planet to pick up
Dr.
Elizabeth Dehner
from the Aldebaron colony. (
TOS
: "
Where No Man Has Gone Before
")
In
2371
, the
Ferengi
Belongo
,
nephew
of
Grand Nagus
Zek
, was detained on Aldebaron III by the local Starfleet
authorities
. (
DS9
: "
Past Tense, Part I
")
StarTrek.com
and the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 16 – in an article about the Aldebaran system – treated Aldebaron III and
Aldebaran III
as the same planet.
[1]
(X)
"al-DEB-uh-ron" was the pronunciation for this planet's
name
from the
script
pronunciation guide for "Past Tense, Part I".
[2]
The closed caption from the episode on DVD gave the name as Aldebaran III.

======================================================================
PAGE: Alderaan
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Alderaan
======================================================================

Alderaan
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2369
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Alderaan
was an
inhabited
planet
that was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
. The planet was home to the
Alderaan Spaceport
.
In
2365
, the
Federation
starships
USS
Atlantis
and
USS
Laser
were on
diplomatic
missions
to this planet. It was listed in the chart
Starship Deploy Status
, which was seen in the
Starbase 173
courtroom
. (
TNG
: "
The Measure Of A Man
"
okudagram
)
In
2369
, a
transport
heading for
Deep Space 9
departed from the Alderaan Spaceport. The
Bajoran
criminal
Ibudan
was a passenger on this transport. (
DS9
: "
A Man Alone
",
okudagram
)
Appendices
Background information
This planet was only mentioned in writing.
The reference to this planet, as well as the wording of several
okudagrams
, was an
in-joke
. Alderaan was the adopted
homeworld
of
Star Wars
'
Princess Leia
, who claimed to be on a diplomatic mission to Alderaan when captured by
Darth Vader
in the opening scene of
Star Wars Episode IV: A New Hope
,
and the planet ended up being destroyed by the Death Star's superlaser.
Original okudagram mentioning Alderaan
The
name
of this planet originally also appeared in "
Up The Long Ladder
", on an okudagram on
Picard
's
desktop monitor
as the destination of
HMS
New Zealand
in
2135
. A legible version of the original okudagram appeared in the
reference book
,
Star Trek: The Next Generation - The Continuing Mission
. In the remastered version of the episode, the planet was renamed to
Aldebaran
.
In the
reference book
Star Trek Chronology
, in the "A" section of the locations index, this planet was listed as "Never visited by the
crew
of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
."
External links
Alderaan
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Alderaan
at
Wookieepedia, the
Star Wars
Wiki

======================================================================
PAGE: Aldus Prime
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Aldus_Prime
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Aldus Prime
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
(
3196
)
Status:
Intact
(
3196
)
Location:
Ross 614
Beta Quadrant
~13
light years
from
Sol
Aldus Prime
was the
prime
planet
of the
Ross 614
system
. Located in the
Beta Quadrant
, this planet was approximately 13
light years
from
Sol
. (
ENT
: "
Cold Front
")
History
Sometime before
September
2151
, a major historical battle took place at this planet, and the
orange
color
used for the label "Aldus Prime (Ross 614)" on an
United Earth
star chart
was an indicator for this event. (
ENT
: "
Cold Front
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet was within the enclosed region. (
SA
: "
300th Night
", "
Rubincon
")
Stellar cartography
In the
2150s
, this system's position was labeled on an
United Earth
star chart
which was displayed in several crew and guest
quarters
aboard the
Enterprise
. (
Star Trek: Enterprise
,
set decoration
)
In
3196
, this planet's position was labeled on a Federation star chart which was displayed on the
main viewscreen
of the
USS
Athena
bridge
. (
SA
: "
Ko'Zeine
")
In
3196
, during the
Omega-47
crisis, the location of Aldus Prime (Ross 614) was depicted on star charts and readouts used across several locations on the
USS
Athena
to locate, monitor, and respond to the Omega-47 mines laid by the Venari Ral. (
SA
: "
300th Night
", "
Rubincon
")
This planet was only mentioned in writing.
The identification of orange with a historic battle was made in the key to the star charts in the
Star Trek: Star Charts
.
The star chart seen in the series
Star Trek: Enterprise
made its first appearance in
Crewman
Daniels
quarters in "
Cold Front
". It was also seen in several other episodes of the series, from
2151
to
2154
. (
For more information, see
Federation star charts#United Earth Alpha/Beta star chart
)
External link
Aldus Prime
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Alera
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Alera
======================================================================

Alera
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2384
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Alera
was a
planet
visited by crewmembers from the
USS
Dauntless
in
2384
. This planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
In that year, there were two teams on the planet. The first team, consisting of
Aaron Cavit
,
Tuvok
, and
Harry Kim
, were conducting an
analysis
of the planet. The second team, consisting of
Alexander Honigsberg
,
Ashmore
, and
Tabor
, were gathering resources on the planet.
Megan Delaney
, in a shuttle
piloted
by
Ahni Jetal
, was on her way to the planet to join up with the other crewmembers. (
PRO
: "
Supernova, Part 1
")
This planet was only mentioned in writing.

of the

======================================================================
PAGE: Alfa 177
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Alfa_177
======================================================================

Alfa 177
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2365
)
Native species:
Alfa 177 canine
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
For the similarly named planet, please see
Alfa 117
.
Alfa 177
was an
inhabited
Class M
planet
that was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
. A
species of dog
was indigenous to this planet.
This planet had a nighttime
temperature
that reached 120° below zero. The planet was capable of supporting
life
despite this, and it was also rich in a soft
yellow
magnetic
ore
with highly unusual properties.
History
In
2266
, the
USS
Enterprise
was on a specimen-gathering
mission
to this planet. Among the samples gathered was one of the planet's canines. While on the surface,
Geological Technician
Fisher
, a member of a
landing party
, became covered with yellow magnetic ore, which damaged the
transporters
, causing it to divide transporter signals. (
TOS
: "
The Enemy Within
")
The crew of the
Enterprise
...
...stranded on the cold planet
The location of Alfa 177 on "The Explored Galaxy" star chart
Stellar cartography
In
2293
, the location of Alfa 177 in the
Milky Way Galaxy
was labeled in the
star chart
"The Explored Galaxy", which was on display in
James T. Kirk
's
quarters
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-A
. (
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
,
okudagram
)
In
2365
, the location of Alfa 177 was labeled in a Subspace Comm Net Ops star chart, which was seen in the
courtroom
on
Starbase 173
. This planet was connected to
Alpha Carinae
by a subspace line. (
TNG
: "
The Measure Of A Man
")
Appendices
Background information
The planet's classification came from
StarTrek.com
and
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 16.
[1]
(X)
A concept sketch of the Alfa 177 surface, with an illustration of the scene in which "Sulu heats [a] rock"
This planet's name was spelled "Alfa" in script sources, on screen in the "The Explored Galaxy" star chart, and in such
reference books
as the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 16 (the planet's name is given as "Alfa 117"),
Star Trek Maps
(p. 22), and
Star Trek: The Original Series 365
(p. 043). However,
The Star Trek Compendium
(4th ed., p. 34) used "Alpha".
Alfa 117
was canonized in
Star Trek Generations
.
This planet was unnamed in the revised story outline of "
The Enemy Within
". In the final draft and revised final draft of the episode's script (dated
8 June
and
9 June
1966
respectively), it was called "Reconnaissance Planet Alfa 177" and the lowest nighttime surface temperature was said to be 250 degrees below zero, rather than (as it is, on-screen) 120 degrees below zero.
The surface of Alfa 177 was represented by a set built on
Desilu Stage 10
. (
To Boldly Go: Rare Photos from the TOS Soundstage - Season One
, p. 43)
For the pre-remastered version of "The Enemy Within", the brown/red planet model for Alfa 177 was first used for
Delta Vega
in "
Where No Man Has Gone Before
". It was used later for
M-113
in ("
The Man Trap
"),
Tantalus V
("
Dagger of the Mind
"),
Planet Q
("
The Conscience of the King
"),
Benecia
("
The Conscience of the King
"),
Gothos
("
The Squire of Gothos
"),
Lazarus' planet
("
The Alternative Factor
"),
Beta III
("
The Return of the Archons
"),
Janus VI
("
The Devil in the Dark
"),
Organia
("
Errand of Mercy
"),
Mudd
("
I, Mudd
"),
Argus X
("
Obsession
"), and
Ardana
("
The Cloud Minders
"). For the
remastered
version of "The Enemy Within", a new planet model was created by
CGI
artist
Max Gabl
.
In August 2024, an annotated computer paste-up of the star chart from "The Measure Of A Man" was put up for auction in the Entertainment Memorabiliia Live Auction: Los Angeles Summer 2024 by Propstore Auction.
[2]
This planet
orbited
a
M-type
star
in the
Alpha Quadrant
. (
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
, "History of the United Federation of Planets")
Apocrypha
FASA
's
RPG
sourcebook
The Federation
identified it as the
second
planet in the Alpha Honorus (
β
) system.
External links
Alfa 177
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Alfa 177
at
the Roddenberry Archive
Planets in TOS and TOS Remastered
at
Ex Astris Scientia

======================================================================

Algeron IV
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2364
)
Location:
Algeron
system
Near the
Federation-Romulan border
Beta Quadrant
Algeron IV
was the
fourth
planet
of the
Algeron
system
. Located near the
Federation-Romulan border
, this planet was located in the
Beta Quadrant
.
In
2364
, after a brief layover at
Relva VII
, the
USS
Enterprise
-D
set course for this planet. (
TNG
: "
Coming of Age
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
The planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Strange New Worlds
episode "
A Quality of Mercy
".
At
StarTrek.com
, it was stated that Algeron IV might have been located close to the
Romulan Star Empire
and was the namesake for the
Treaty of Algeron
.
[1]
(X)
External link
Algeron IV
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Algol I
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Algol_I
======================================================================

Algol I
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2259
)
Satellite(s):
1
moon
Ring system
Native species:
Algolians
Location:
Algol
system
Kiley Local
sector
Alpha Quadrant
~94
light years
from
Sol
Algol I
was the
inhabited
first
planet
in the
Algol
system
. This
Alpha Quadrant
planet was located in the
Kiley Local
sector
and was approximately 94
light years
from
Sol
.
This
ringed
planet was
orbited
by a single
moon
and was home to the
Algolians
. This
species
had become
warp-capable
and had established
first contact
with the
Federation
by
2259
.
By that year, their civilization had been or was in the process of being inducted into the Federation (
SNW
: "
Strange New Worlds
")
At
StarTrek.com
, it was said that the
Algolian
homeworld
was in the Algol system. This planet was later identified in the canon as Algol I.
[1]
(X)
External link
Algol I
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

Alondra

(
2269
)
Location:
Pallas 14
system
Alpha

Beta Quadrant
Alondra
was the
uninhabited
third
planet
of the
Pallas 14
system
. The planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
In
2269
, the
USS
Enterprise
attempted to intercept an unidentified
cosmic cloud
that had entered the system near Alondra. However, en route, the crew noticed Alondra disappeared from the navigation scan, only to observe that the planet was engulfed and consumed by the cosmic cloud
creature
. The position and
orbital
path of Alondra were seen on a system map displayed on the
main viewscreen
of the
USS
Enterprise
bridge
. (
TAS
: "
One of Our Planets Is Missing
")
External link
Alondra
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Alonzo's Vision
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Alonzo's_Vision
======================================================================

Alonzo's Vision
Status:
Intact
(
2371
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Alonzo's Vision
was a celestial location in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
Its position was labeled on a
star chart
in the guidance system
S/COMS
panel on aboard the
Amargosa observatory
. (
Star Trek Generations
)
This location was only mentioned in writing.
In August 2024, this panel was put up for auction in the Entertainment Memorabilia Live Auction: Los Angeles Summer 2024 by Propstore Auction.
[1]
This location was presumably named after the film's cinematographer
John A. Alonzo
.

of its

. This planet was located somewhere in the

.

.

aboard the

-D
in

, via the
commercial transport database
. (
TNG-R
: "
Inheritance
")

The planet was named after special effects artist
Richard Balder
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Alpha Carinae II
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Alpha_Carinae_II
======================================================================

Alpha Carinae II
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Non-aligned
Status:
Habitable
(
2328
)
Native species:
Alpha Carinae II natives
Location:
Canopus
system
Beta Quadrant
~309
light years
from
Sol
Alpha Carinae II
was the
inhabited
second
planet
in the
Canopus
system
. The planet was located in the
Beta Quadrant
and was approximately 309
light years
from
Sol
. Classified as
class M
, the planet was inhabited by
sentient
natives. (
TOS
: "
The Ultimate Computer
")
The planet was a conventional M-class world with an
oxygen
-
nitrogen
atmosphere
. On the planet's surface, there were two major
land masses
and a number of
islands
. There were at least twenty-nine other planets that were biologically similar to Alpha Carinae II known to the
Federation
.
In the mid-
23rd century
, the
geologist
Carstairs
once visited this planet while serving in the
Merchant Marines
on a
geology survey
for a
mining company
.
In
2268
, shortly after departing
Starbase 6
, the
USS
Enterprise
surveyed the planet as a test of the
M-5 multitronic unit
's capabilities. For that test, the
starship
was assigned to send a
general survey party
to the planet's
surface
. The party was to avoid all contact with the indigenous lifeforms. This test was cancelled by
Captain
James T. Kirk
after he was informed by
Chief Engineer
Montgomery Scott
that the M-5 computer was shutting down systems across the ship. (
TOS
: "
The Ultimate Computer
")
In
2328
, the planet was home to the
Spaceport Alpha Carinae II
. (
TNG-R
: "
Inheritance
")
Appendices
Background information
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Discovery
episode "
An Obol for Charon
".
For the pre-remastered episode, the planet model for Alpha Carinae II was first used for
Capella IV
. ("
Friday's Child
") It was used again for
Neural
in "
A Private Little War
",
Triskelion
in "
The Gamesters of Triskelion
", the
Kelvan planet
in "
By Any Other Name
",
Ekos
in "
Patterns of Force
",
Triacus
in "
And the Children Shall Lead
", an
unnamed planet
in "
Is There in Truth No Beauty?
", the
Medusan homeworld
in "
Is There in Truth No Beauty?
",
Beta XII-A
in "
Day of the Dove
", the
Kalandan outpost
in "
That Which Survives
",
Elba II
in "
Whom Gods Destroy
"), and
Sarpeidon
in "
All Our Yesterdays
".
For the remastered episode, a new planet model was created by
CGI
artist
Max Gabl
.
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I"), Alpha Carinae II was identified as Canopus II and listed as a
Federation member
in
2378
.
External links
Alpha Carinae II
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Planets in TOS and TOS Remastered
at
Ex Astris Scientia

======================================================================
PAGE: Alpha Centauri (planet)
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Alpha_Centauri_(planet)
======================================================================

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Alpha Centauri
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
(
3195
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3195
)
Location:
Alpha Centauri
system
Beta Quadrant
~4
light years
from
Sol
Alpha Centauri
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Alpha Centauri
system
. The planet was located in the
Beta Quadrant
and was approximately 4
light years
from
Sol
.
When Kirk was
detained
in
1969
on
Earth
, and
interrogated
on who he was by
Lieutenant Colonel
Fellini
, a member of the
US Air Force Air Police
at the
US 498th Airbase Group
, Kirk responded by saying,  "
the truth is, I'm a
little green man
from Alpha Centauri, a beautiful place. You ought to see it.
" (
TOS
: "
Tomorrow is Yesterday
")
In an
alternate timeline
, created by
Jonathan Archer
being infected by
interspatial parasites
, the Xindi destroyed
Earth
in
2154
and then proceeded to annihilate all other Human settlements, including
Mars
, Alpha Centauri, and
Vega colony
.
(
ENT
: "
Twilight
")
In the mid-
23rd century
, in a
cafe
on this planet,
Paul Stamets
met
Hugh Culber
for the first time and they began a romantic relationship, which eventually lead to
marriage
. (
DIS
: "
Magic to Make the Sanest Man Go Mad
")
In
2338
,
Greta Vanderweg
was born on this planet. (
DS9
: "
Field of Fire
")
In the
32nd century
, Alpha Centauri was a
Federation
member
planet. It was listed with other members on a video wall in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
Kids These Days
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet was within the enclosed region. (
SA
: "
300th Night
")
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
In the real world, a team of
astronomers
announced the discovery of
Alpha Centauri Bb
, an
Earth
-
size
terrestrial planet, in 2012. Three years later, however, another team of astronomers determined that this elusive ghost planet was all along nothing more than a measurement artefact. A second possible planet, known as
Candidate 1
, was detected in 2021 but has not yet been confirmed to exist. By 2024, there were two confirmed exoplanets orbiting Proxima Centauri.
The current location of Alpha Centauri in the Beta Quadrant was based on the most recent star charts seen in
Star Trek: Strange New Worlds
and
Star Trek: Section 31
.
See also
Alpha Centauri City
Centauri IV
Centauri VII
Proxima colony
University of Alpha Centauri
External link
Centauri VII
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

. This non-
Federation

Alpha Quadrant
and was approximately 2,615
light years
from

.

Treaty of Alpha Cygnus IX
was named after this planet. (
TNG
: "
Sarek
")

, a
lieutenant
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
, was from this planet. In
2369
, their planet of origin was listed on the chart "USS
Enterprise
Personnel Database", which was displayed on a
PADD
accessed by
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
. (
TNG
: "
The Chase
",
okudagram
)
StarTrek.com
stated that Alpha
Cygnus
was the classic
Bayer designation
for
Deneb
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Alpha Eridani II
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Alpha_Eridani_II
======================================================================

Alpha Eridani II
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Earth
Status:
Habitable
(
2156
)
Location:
Achernar
system
Beta Quadrant
~139
light years
from
Sol
Alpha Eridani II
was the
inhabited
second
planet
of the
Achernar
system
. The planet was located in the
Beta Quadrant
and was approximately 139
light years
from
Sol
. This planet was aligned directly between
Earth
and
Argelius II
.
In the
22nd century
, an
Earth
colony
was founded on the planet.
In
2156
, the
Redjac
entity
was responsible for
ten women
being
knifed to death
in the settlement of
Heliopolis
on Alpha Eridani II. (
TOS
: "
Wolf in the Fold
")
Appendices
Background information
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
In the
script
, dated
21 June
1967
, the
name
was written as "Alpha Proximi Two". The
26 June
1967
review of the script by
de Forest Research
, which adjusted for the misspelled "Proximi" as "Proxima" stated the following:
"
Alpha Proxima Two
– This name does not conform with astronomical nomenclature. Suggest: Alpha Eridani Two."
Based on the episode dialog, this suggestion was followed by the script writer, however, the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 3rd ed., p. 8 and
StarTrek.com
referred to the planet by the original name "Alpha Proxima II".
[1]
(X)
A subjective interpretation of the pronunciation of the planet's spoken name in the episode, as well as the closed captioning, suggested the name "Alpha Aradoni II". The correct spelling of "Alpha Eridani II" can be found in the Redjac entry on
StarTrek.com
and in the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 3rd ed., p. 403 as well as the
Star Trek: Star Charts
.
[2]
(X)
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 20 correctly identified the planet as "Alpha Eridani II".
Apocrypha
In the
Star Trek Concordance
and
Star Trek Maps
, the original name of "Alpha Proxima II" for this planet was mentioned. According to the
Star Trek Maps
(p. 22), Alpha Proxima II was one of the first extra-solar Human colonies, with major industries in
mining
,
farming
, and sea-farming. By the
23rd century
, the planet had a total
population
of 12 million with more than 3 million living in the port city Heliopolis.
External links
Achernar II
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Achernar Prime
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Alpha Eridani
at
Wikipedia

(

or

in its

. This planet was located somewhere in the

or

.
La'An Noonien-Singh
was born on this world on
December 8
,
2228
. (
SNW
: "
Strange New Worlds
")

("United Federation of Planets I"; "United Federation of Planets III") and
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Federation Historical Highlights, 2161-2385"), there was a star system named Alpha in the Alpha Quadrant. This was a single star system with a
yellow
star
. This could be the planet's system.

======================================================================
PAGE: Alpha III
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Alpha_III
======================================================================

Alpha III
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Earth
Status:
Habitable
(
23rd century
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Alpha III
was the
inhabited
third
planet
in its
star system
. This planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
and was home to an
Earth
colony
.
An important piece of
Human
rights legislation, the
Statutes of Alpha III
, was written on this planet by the
Tribunal of Alpha III
. (
TOS
: "
Court Martial
")
See also
Alpha I
Alpha VII
Colony Alpha 5
Appendices
Background information
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I"; "United Federation of Planets III") and
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Federation Historical Highlights, 2161-2385"), there was a star system named Alpha in the Alpha Quadrant. This was a single star system with a
yellow
star
. This could be the planet's system. In
2378
, Alpha III was listed as a
Federation member
.
Apocrypha
In the
Star Trek Maps
(p. 22), the colony on Alpha III was established during the
22nd century
as "Terra Four". By the
23rd century
, the planet had a
population
of 175 million, it was a
Federation member
, and one of the seven members of the
Federation
security council.
In the
The Worlds of the Federation
, it was called "Kerecindal" by its natives.
FASA
's
RPG
sourcebook
The Federation
described it as the
fourth planet
in the Alpha Canaris (
β
) system.
External link
Alpha III
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

~123

from

, both the

and the

transported

and

star system
. The planet was located in the
Kiley Local
sector
,
Alpha Quadrant
, and was approximately 123
light years
from
Sol
.
In
2259
, the planet's position in the system and its orbital path were seen on a display graphic. Its orbital path brought this planet close to the orbital path of
Alpha Majoris VI
. (
SNW
: "
Strange New Worlds
")

PAGE: Alpha Onias III
CATEGORIES: Planets

Alpha Onias III
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Non-aligned
Status:
Habitable
(
2367
)
Location:
Alpha Onias
system
Onias
sector
Near the
Federation-Romulan border
Beta Quadrant
Alpha Onias III
was the
uninhabited
third
planet
of the
Alpha Onias
system
. This planet was located near the
Federation-Romulan border
in the
Onias
sector
,
Beta Quadrant
.
Although it was
class M
,
Starfleet
survey teams
listed Alpha Onias III as barren and inhospitable with an
atmosphere
containing high, but non-
toxic
, levels of
volcanic
gases
, including
sulfur dioxide
,
methane
, and
hydrogen sulfide
.
Barash
, a
humanoid
child
, was hidden in a
cavern
by
his mother
after their
homeworld
was
invaded
in the mid-
24th century
. The cavern was equipped with
neural scanners
that were able to transform
matter
into any form imagined, so that Barash could live in safety. He later left the planet for the
USS
Enterprise
-D
in
2367
. (
TNG
: "
Future Imperfect
")
This planet's quadrant of origin wasinferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Discovery
episode "
Magic to Make the Sanest Man Go Mad
".
"OH-nye-is" was the pronunciation for this planet's
name
from the
script
pronunciation guide. The same script described it as "a dull,
gray
planet".
The planet model for Alpha Onias III was first used for
Ramatis III
. ("
Loud As A Whisper
") It was used again for
Gamma Hromi II
. ("
The Vengeance Factor
")
External links
Alpha Onias III
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Re-Used Planets in TNG
at
Ex Astris Scientia

Alpha VII

Planet
Status:

(

)

Alpha
or

Alpha VII
was the
inhabited
seventh
planet
in its
star system
. This planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
In
2328
, this was the destination of
Marion Cordry
. She had traveled from the
New Paris Colony Starbase
on the
SS
Kogin
. (
TNG-R
: "
Inheritance
",
okudagram
)
In
Brad Boimler
's
2381
holonovel
Crisis Point II: Paradoxus
, Alpha VII was one of the worlds labeled on the
star map
tattooed
on
Illustor
's loose
skin
. The "HA" in the name formed part of the word
Ki-ty-ha
when the skin flaps were pushed together. (
LD
: "
Crisis Point 2: Paradoxus
")
See also
Alpha I
Alpha III
Colony Alpha 5
This planet was only mentioned in writing.
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I"; "United Federation of Planets III") and
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Federation Historical Highlights, 2161-2385"), there was a star system named Alpha in the Alpha Quadrant. This was a single star system with a
yellow
star
. This could be the planet's system.

, and

. It was also home to the

======================================================================
PAGE: Alshain IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Alshain_IV
======================================================================

Alshain IV
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Native species:
Alshain
Location:
Alshain system
Alpha Quadrant
~44
light years
from
Sol
Alshain IV
was the
inhabited
fourth
planet
in the
Alshain
system
. Located in the
Alpha Quadrant
, this planet was approximately 44
light years
from
Sol
.
This
class M
world had an
oxygen
-
nitrogen
atmosphere
and a
forested
surface
environment with large
rock
formations floating in the
air
. It was the
homeworld
of the
Alshain
, a technologically advanced
humanoid
civilization
that lived in close association with a native
butterfly
species
that enabled them to fly.
History
In the late
29th century
, the
magnetic poles
of Alshain IV began to shift; over the next three centuries, they moved fourteen degrees west of where they had been for hundreds of thousands of years. As the Alshain relied upon the planet's
magnetic field
to navigate during flight, they constructed an
orbital
array of
geomagnetic compensator
satellites
to adjust for the shift. However, after
the Burn
in the
31st century
, the Alshain ran out of
dilithium
and could no longer keep the satellites powered.
In
3190
, the
USS
Discovery
jumped
to Alshain IV to renew relations between the Alshain and the
Federation
. Despite a brief misunderstanding,
Captain
Michael Burnham
and
Cleveland Booker
successfully completed their mission after restoring the Alshain satellite array. (
DIS
: "
Kobayashi Maru
")
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Discovery
episode "
Red Directive
".
In the real world, Alshain was the traditional name of the Beta Aquilae star system (and as of 2016 the official name of Beta Aquilae A, the system's primary star), but while
Beta Aquilae II
was mentioned in
TNG
: "
Eye of the Beholder
", the two names had not been connected in
Star Trek
canon until a star chart in the
Star Trek: Enterprise
episode "
Cold Front
" made the connection.
External link
Alshain IV
at
StarTrek.com

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain

.
Altair III
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2367
)
Location:
Altair
system
Sector 9
Alpha Quadrant
~17
light years
from
Sol
The location of Altair III on the tactical situation monitor
Altair III
was the
third
planet
in the
Altair
system
. The planet was located in
Sector 9
,
Alpha Quadrant
, and was approximately 17
light years
from
Sol
. (
TNG
: "
Encounter at Farpoint
")
History
In the early
2360s
, when the
USS
Hood
visited this system,
Lieutenant Commander
William T. Riker
refused to let
Captain
Robert DeSoto
transport
to the
surface
of Altair III with an
away team
because he deemed the action as too dangerous. By preventing his captain from beaming down, Riker disobeyed a
direct order
and risked a general
court martial
. (
TNG
: "
Encounter at Farpoint
", "
The Pegasus
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet was within the enclosed region. (
SA
: "
300th Night
")
Stellar cartography
In
2367
, the
location
of Altair III was labeled on a
tactical situation monitor
in
Captain
Benjamin Maxwell
's
ready room
on the
USS
Phoenix
. (
TNG
: "
The Wounded
",
okudagram
)
In
2366
, in an
alternate timeline
, during the
Federation-Klingon War
, Altair III's position was labeled on a tactical situation monitor on the
bridge
and in the
ready room
of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
.
(
TNG
: "
Yesterday's Enterprise
",
okudagram
)
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
". The sector where this system was located was mentioned in the
Star Trek: The Original Series
episode "
Amok Time
".
In
Gene Roddenberry
and
D.C. Fontana
's final draft
script
of "Encounter at Farpoint", the planet was originally named
Altair IV
.
"AL-tair" was the pronunciation for this
planet
's
name
from the
script
pronunciation guide for "
Prophet Motive
".
External link
Altair III
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

Warning!

, and thus may contain
