Argratha
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Argrathi Authority
Status:
Habitable
(
2372
)
Native species:
Argrathi
Location:
Gamma Quadrant
Argratha
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Gamma Quadrant
. This was the
homeworld
of the
humanoid
Argrathi
. The planet was governed by the
Argrathi Authority
. Instead of imprisoning criminals, their legal system punished offenders by imprinting the convicted with fabricated memories of incarceration.
In
2372
,
Miles O'Brien
visited the planet. (
DS9
: "
Hard Time
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
"ar-GRATH-a" was the pronunciation for this planet's
name
from the
script
pronunciation guide.
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 74), Argratha was located in the Argratha
system
. The
primary
was a
G-class star
. Argratha was a location visited by the Gamma Quadrant Bio-Survey (
2369
-
75
).

======================================================================
PAGE: Argus Array
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Argus_Array
======================================================================

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Argus Array
Type:
Subspace telescope
Owner:
United Federation of Planets
Operator:
Starfleet
Location:
Alpha Quadrant
Status:
Active
(
3196
)
The
Argus Array
(full name:
Argus Subspace Radio Telescope Array
) was a
Federation
subspace telescope
located in the
Alpha Quadrant
.
Technology specifications
An unmanned structure, the Argus Array consisted of eighteen hexagonal
subspace antennae
clusters, each powered by an individual
fusion reactor
and all regulated from a central
computer
. It was capable of collecting detailed images of
objects
many
light years
away. (
TNG
: "
The Nth Degree
", "
Parallels
")
History
The Argus Array had been in service with
Starfleet
since at least
2259
.  (
SNW
: "
Strange New Worlds
", "
Children of the Comet
")
During one of its studies of
subspace
, the Argus Array suggested an upper bound on a
phenomenon
known as the
Hofstadter effect
. (
TNG-R
: "
Redemption II
"
display graphic
)
The Argus Array malfunctioned in
2367
, following contact with a
Cytherian probe
, which disrupted the Array's
computer systems
. The problem was fixed by the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. (
TNG
: "
The Nth Degree
")
In different
quantum realities
, the
Enterprise
-D returned to the Argus Array in
2370
to investigate why it was not transmitting data. In these
parallel universes
visited by
Lieutenant
Worf
, the cause of the array's shutdown was varied. In one, the
Cardassians
had reprogrammed the array to spy on Federation
outposts
; in another, it had suffered a simple mechanical failure. In one universe, the Cardassians had destroyed the array; in another, the
Bajorans
did so.
(
TNG
: "
Parallels
")
Stellar cartography
The Argus Array was near the
Gamma Trianguli
,
Alpha Majoris
,
Talos
, and
Setlik systems
. In the
2360s
, it was positioned at the edge of
Federation space
, three
light years
from
Cardassian territory
.
In 2259, the location of this subspace telescope was labeled on a
stellar cartography
chart that was seen on the
USS
Enterprise
's
ready room
viewscreen
. (
SNW
: "
Strange New Worlds
", "
Children of the Comet
")
On
stardate
1292.4, the location of this sector was labeled on a
star chart
used during a
Section 31
Alpha Team
briefing
. (
Star Trek: Section 31
)
The Argus Array's location was labeled in a
Federation star chart
that was in
Fleet Admiral
Kirsten Clancy
's
office
at
Starfleet Headquarters
in
2399
and on the
bridge
of the
USS
Titan
-A
in
2401
. This subspace telescope was in or near to neutral space. (
PIC
: "
Maps and Legends
", "
The Next Generation
", "
Disengage
")
In
3195
, this telescope's position was labeled on a
Federation
star chart which was displayed in
Starfleet Academy
's Stellar Cartography
laboratory
. (
SA
: "
Beta Test
")
In
3196
, during the
Omega-47
crisis, the location of the Argus Array was depicted on star charts and readouts used across several locations on the
USS
Athena
to locate, monitor, and respond to the
Omega-47 mines
laid by the
Venari Ral
. (
SA
: "
Rubincon
")
The Argus Array was shown in at least three different locations on a single map in "
Rubincon
", both inside and outside the mine barrier. No explanation for this is given.
The array was named after
Argus Panoptes
, a giant in
Greek mythology
with a hundred
eyes
.
(
citation needed
•
edit
)
For the remastered "Redemption II", the old graphics were replaced, and the Argus Array was mentioned in the new text.
External link
Argus Array
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Argus X
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Argus_X
======================================================================

Argus X
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2268
)
Location:
Argus system
Argus sector
Alpha Quadrant
~1,000
light years
from
Tycho
system
Argus X
was the
uninhabited
tenth
planet
in the
Argus
system
. The planet was found in the
Argus sector
,
Alpha Quadrant
. Argus X was approximately 1,000
light years
from the
Tycho system
.
In
2268
, the
USS
Enterprise
conducted a
planetary survey
of Argus X, discovering a large
vein
of
tritanium
ore
.
Captain
James T. Kirk
sent a recommendation to
Starfleet
for a
survey vessel
to be dispatched to this planet as a result of the rich find.
Also discovered on the planet by the
Enterprise
landing party
was the dangerous
dikironium cloud creature
, which attacked and killed (or nearly killed) five
security guards
. (
TOS
: "
Obsession
")
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
For the original production of the episode, the planet model for Argus X was first used for
Delta Vega
("
Where No Man Has Gone Before
"). It was used again for
Alfa 177
("
The Enemy Within
",
Tantalus V
("
Dagger of the Mind
"),
Planet Q
("
The Conscience of the King
"),
Benecia
("
The Conscience of the King
"),
Gothos
("
The Squire of Gothos
"),
Lazarus' planet
("
The Alternative Factor
"),
Beta III
("
The Return of the Archons
"),
Janus VI
("
The Devil in the Dark
"),
Organia
("
Errand of Mercy
"), and
Mudd
("
I, Mudd
"). It was later used for
Ardana
("
The Cloud Minders
").
For the remastered episode, a new planet model was created by
CGI
artist
Max Gabl
.
External links
Argus X
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Planets in TOS and TOS Remastered
at
Ex Astris Scientia

======================================================================
PAGE: Ariannus
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ariannus
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Ariannus
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
(
3195
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3195
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Ariannus
was an
inhabited
planet
that was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
. This planet was located in a
region
of the
galaxy
that was governed by the
Federation
in the
23rd century
.
History
Ariannus was established as a vital transfer point for the regular space commercial lanes prior to
stardate
5730
. At that time, there were a billion people living on the planet. The nearest
starbase
was
Starbase 4
.
In 2268, Ariannus was infected by a
bacterial
invasion
that threatened to destroy all
life
on the planet. The crew of the
USS
Enterprise
, working in tandem with the planet's
ministry of health
, conducted an
orbital
decontamination
mission
to save the planet's inhabitants. (
TOS
: "
Let That Be Your Last Battlefield
")
In the
32nd century
, Ariannus was a
Federation
member
planet. It was listed with other members on a video wall in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
Kids These Days
")
Stellar cartography
The location of Ariannus in "The Explored Galaxy" star chart
In
2293
, the
location
of Ariannus in the
Milky Way Galaxy
was labeled in the
star chart
"The Explored Galaxy", which was on display in
James T. Kirk
's
quarters
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-A
. (
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
,
okudagram
)
In
2365
, the location of Ariannus was labeled in a Subspace Comm Net Ops star chart, which was seen in the
courtroom
on
Starbase 173
. This planet was connected to
Janus VI
and
Kling
by subspace lines. (
TNG
: "
The Measure Of A Man
")
In August 2024, an annotated computer paste-up of this star chart was put up for auction in the Entertainment Memorabiliia Live Auction: Los Angeles Summer 2024 by Propstore Auction.
[1]
For the pre-remastered episode, the planet model for Ariannus was first used for
892-IV
("
Bread and Circuses
"). It was used again for
Neural
("
A Private Little War
",
Elas
("
Elaan of Troyius
"), and
Amerind
("
The Paradise Syndrome
"). It was used later for
Eden
("
The Way to Eden
",
Holberg 917G
("
Requiem for Methuselah
"), and
Camus II
("
Turnabout Intruder
"). For the remastered episode, a new planet model was created by
CGI
artist
Max Gabl
.
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I"), Ariannus was listed as a
Federation member
in
2378
.
External links
Arianna
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

at

, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

PAGE: Arkaria
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Arkaria
======================================================================

Arkaria
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2369
)
Satellite(s):
Remmler Array
(
artificial satellite
)
Native species:
Arkarians
Arkarian horn fowl
Location:
Unnamed system
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Arkaria
was an
inhabited
planet
in its
star system
. and the
homeworld
of the
humanoid
Arkarians
and the
Arkarian horn fowl
.
Arkaria Base
was a facility on the planet's
surface
that remotely controlled the
Remmler Array
orbiting
the planet. According to
Data
, Arkaria featured some interesting
weather
patterns and its mean
temperatures
were slightly above what was considered comfortable for
Humans
. According to
Calvin Hutchinson
, the
commander
of the Arkaria Base, there was a network of
trails
through the
plains
, with one of the trails leading to a southern
promontory
where there were nesting grounds for the horn fowls.
In
2369
, the
USS
Enterprise
-D
arrived at Arkaria to be serviced by the Remmler Array. During the procedure, Arkaria Base was temporarily seized by Arkarian
terrorists
in league with a
mercenary
group attempting to steal
trilithium resin
from the
Enterprise
. (
TNG
: "
Starship Mine
")
Appendices
Background information
"ARE-care-ee-uh" was the pronunciation for this planet's
name
from the
script
pronunciation guide.
Although the planet hosted a Starfleet installation, it had never been clarified on screen whether Arkaria was also a
Federation member
.
Apocrypha
Though the planet's membership was uncertain, the
novel
New Worlds, New Civilizations
mentioned Arkarians as being participants in a
Starfleet Academy marathon
in
2323
.
External link
Arkaria
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

Armus IX
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
24th century
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
For the being, please see
Armus
.
Armus IX
was the
inhabited
ninth
planet
in its
star system
. This planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
Prior to
stardate
41636
,
William Riker
had to wear a
costume
decorated with
feathers
here during a
diplomatic
conference
. In 2364, Riker referenced this event in a
conversation
with
Natasha Yar
and
Deanna Troi
in his preparations for his meeting with
Beata
, the
Elected One
of
Angel I
, on the lengths to which he would go to meet local
customs
. (
TNG
: "
Angel One
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
There was some confusion about the spelling of this planet's name. At
StarTrek.com
, the planet's name was spelled Armis IX, whereas with the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 45, it was spelled as Armus IX.

Arnold's Planet
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(

)

Beta Quadrant
Arnold's Planet
was a
namesake
planet
in the
Beta Quadrant
.
In
2293
, Arnold's Planet position was labeled in the
star chart
for the "
Operation: Retrieve
" command briefing. The chart was from the
Starfleet Command Intelligence Database
. This star chart was seen in the
office
of the
Federation President
on
Earth
. (
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
,
set artwork
)
This planet's quadrant of origin is inferred based on the position of neighboring locations –
Starbase 24
and
Rura Penthe
– as seen in the
star charts
appearing in the
Star Trek: Discovery
episode "
Magic to Make the Sanest Man Go Mad
" and in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
This chart was seen in greater resolution at threads at Flare.Solareclipse.net.
[1]
(X)
[2]
(X)
Many of these locations shared their names with the last names of crew who worked on the sixth film.

======================================================================
PAGE: Arret
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Arret
======================================================================

Arret
Class:
M
(formerly)
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Non-aligned
Status:
Uninhabitable
(
500,000 years ago
)
Native species:
Arretans
(
extinct
)
Location:
An
unnamed star system
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
For the
antimatter universe
planet, please see
Arret (negative)
.
Arret
was an
inhabited
class M
planet
in an
unnamed star system
. The planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
History
In the ancient past, this was the
homeworld
of the
warp-capable
Arretans
, who went on to colonize the
Milky Way Galaxy
. It was located "
hundreds of
light years
past where any
Earth
ship ha[d] ever explored
" in
2268
, or "over three
weeks
" distance to
Starfleet
via
subspace radio
. (
TOS
: "
Return to Tomorrow
")
Originally
Earth-like
, though much older, the
atmosphere
of Arret was ripped away by a cataclysmic war fought
500,000 years
before the
USS
Enterprise
arrived in
orbit
. The
ship
's
sensors
detected no
life
on the planet's
surface
.
Eleven
survivors
of that great war had created a shelter 112.37
miles
below the surface. This vault was constructed of a material harder and stronger than anything
Commander
Spock
had measured at that time; its atmosphere was "a fraction richer in
oxygen
than usual".
Originally
humanoid
, the eleven surviving members of
Sargon
's
species
placed the essence of their
minds
into spheroid
receptacles
. In 2268, only two spheres besides Sargon's still contained a living mind: that of
Thalassa
, Sargon's
wife
, among the five spheres kept on the lower tier, and that of
Henoch
, a member of the conflict's "other side", on the upper. (
TOS
: "
Return to Tomorrow
")
Stellar cartography
The location of Arret on "The Explored Galaxy" star chart
In
2293
, the
location
of Arret in the
Milky Way Galaxy
was labeled in the
star chart
"The Explored Galaxy", which was on display in
James T. Kirk
's
quarters
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-A
. (
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
,
okudagram
)
In
2365
, the location of Arret was labeled in a Subspace Comm Net Ops star chart, which was seen in the
courtroom
on
Starbase 173
. This planet was connected to an unnamed location by a subspace line. (
TNG
: "
The Measure Of A Man
")
Appendices
Background information
In August 2024, an annotated computer paste-up of the Subspace Comm Net Ops was put up for auction in the Entertainment Memorabiliia Live Auction: Los Angeles Summer 2024 by Propstore Auction.
[1]
The name "Arret" (
Terra
backwards) was used in the final draft of the
script
for "Return to Tomorrow" (dated
15 November
1967
). Here it was described: "
The
Enterprise
is approaching a planet (Arret) about the
size
of
Earth
– with the notable difference that this planet is scarred, devastated and seemingly, very dead.
"
In a deleted line from the episode, Spock stated in his
analysis
of the planet that it was a "
Class "M" planet. Oblate spheroid,
ratio
1 to 296. Mean density 5.53.
Mass
.9.
" Furthermore, he added that the planet was "
about two-and-a-half billion years older [than Earth]
", while the dialog simply stated that it was "
much older than Earth.
" Additionally, in dialogue it was stated that the great cataclysm had happened to the planet "
about a half million years ago
", while the script made no notation of when it had happened. Finally, in more deleted lines, Kirk asked Spock if perhaps it was a "
Nuclear catastrophe of some sort?
", but Spock disagreed, stating that it was "
Something more powerful, I would say...
"
Apocrypha
The
novel
Q-Strike
implied that the
Beta XII-A entity
was responsible for provoking the war.
External links
Arret
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Planets in TOS and TOS Remastered
at
Ex Astris Scientia

======================================================================
PAGE: Arret (negative)
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Arret_(negative)
======================================================================

Arret (negative)
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2270
)
Native species:
Arret natives
Location:
An
unnamed star system
in the
antimatter universe
For the positive
universe
planet, please see
Arret
.
Arret
was an
inhabited
planet
in an
unnamed star system
. It was the
homeworld
of the
Arret natives
, a
warp-capable
humanoid
species
. This reverse negative
antimatter universe
planet was in the same galactic position as
Earth
in the forward positive matter universe.
Karla Five
, her
son
Karl Four
, and
her father
lived on this planet during the mid-
23rd century
. The inhabitants of the planet began their life at an old age and slowly grew younger.
Brain
functions,
language
and operational controls worked in reverse of what they did in the positive matter universe, as the flow of time was reversed.
In
2270
, while transporting
Commodore
Robert April
to his
retirement
ceremony on
Babel
,
USS
Enterprise
accidentally visited the antimatter universe. (
TAS
: "
The Counter-Clock Incident
")
"Arret" was "Terra" spelled backwards. It also meant "stop" in
French
.
In the novelization, the planet Arret did not actually exist. It was an illusion created by a race of energy beings called the Wanderers Who Play.
External link
Arret
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

PAGE: Arvada III

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Arvada III
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
(
3195
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3195
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Arvada III
was the
third
planet
in its
star system
. This planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
and was the home of a
Federation
colony
.
In the
2330s
, Arvada III was the site of a major disaster and the young
Beverly Howard
and her
grandmother
,
Felisa
, were present to help survivors. Traditional medical supplies quickly ran low, and Felisa learned how to use herbs and
roots
as medication despite the fact she was not a
physician
. She passed this knowledge down to Beverly, inspiring her to a career in medicine. (
TNG
: "
The Arsenal of Freedom
")
In the
32nd century
, Arvada III was a
Federation
member
planet. It was listed with other members on a video wall in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
Kids These Days
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I"), Arvada III was listed as a
Federation member
in
2378
.

Arvada III
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek

======================================================================

Aschelan V
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Cardassian Union
(
24th century
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Location:
Aschelan
system
Alpha Quadrant
Aschelan V
was the
fifth
planet
in the
Aschelan
system
,
Alpha Quadrant
.
History
In the
24th century
, this planet was in
Cardassian
space
. The
Cardassian military
maintained a
fuel depot
on this planet.
In
2370
,
B'Elanna Torres
reprogrammed the Cardassian
missile
Dreadnought
to destroy the depot on Aschelan V. However, the missile failed to reach its target, as one day after its launch it was swept into the
Delta Quadrant
by the
Caretaker
. There, it locked onto the similar planet
Rakosa V
, mistaking it to be Aschelan V due to their similar
sizes
,
radiothermic signatures
, atmospheric composition, and more. (
VOY
: "
Dreadnought
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
External link
Aschelan V
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

PAGE: Asp XXVII

Asp XXVII
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2328
)
Native species:
Plim
Location:
Milky Way Galaxy
Asp XXVII
was the
twenty-seventh
planet
of its
star system
. This planet was located somewhere in the
Milky Way Galaxy
and was the
homeworld
of the
Plim
. (
DIS
: "
Rosetta
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
Closed captioning stylized the
planet
's name as "Asp 27", rather than the Roman numeral version used here, and as expressed by
StarTrek.com
.
Currently, this is the highest number of planets a star system has ever been said to have in all of
Star Trek
.
External link
Asp XXVII
at
StarTrek.com

======================================================================
PAGE: Assenede
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Assenede
======================================================================

Assenede
was home to
Van Hoorebeke
, a
beer
provider to the town of
North Star
, on a
Skagaran colony
in the
Delphic Expanse
. (
ENT
: "
North Star
")
An existing crate was used as a prop, and thereby a connection was established to Assenede, a real location in
Belgium
. However, given the colonists' supposed isolation from Earth, it is unlikely they imported beer from Earth and this Assenede might instead be a location on their own planet.
External link
Assenede
at
Wikipedia

Atalia VII
was the
inhabited
seventh
planet
of the
Atalia
system
. This planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
In
2369
, this planet was the location of a
diplomatic
conference
. The conference had been scheduled six
months
in advance and was to have been attended by the
USS
Enterprise
-D
, with
Jean-Luc Picard
acting as a
mediator
. His participation was delayed when the
Enterprise
became involved with the late
Professor
Galen
's quest to decipher an ancient message coded in the
genetic material
of several
Alpha
and
Beta Quadrant
species
. (
TNG
: "
The Chase
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.

Atbar Prime

(

)

Alpha Quadrant
Jem'Hadar battleship
firing on the Atbar Prime base
Atbar Prime
was the
prime
planet
in the
Atbar
system
,
Alpha Quadrant
. This base was in or near to
Cardassian space
.
During the
Dominion War
, this planet was host to a
Cardassian Liberation Front
base
. The base was one of eighteen destroyed by
Dominion
and
Breen
forces in late
2375
. (
DS9
: "
The Dogs of War
")
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of the Cardassian Union as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
"AT-bar" was the pronunciation for this planet's name from the

pronunciation guide.

Athan Prime
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Intact
(
2401
)
Satellite(s):
At least
one moon
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant

was the
prime
planet
in its
star system
, This planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
and was
orbited
by at least one
moon
. It was the location of the
Federation
Fleet Museum
in the early
25th century
.
In
2401
, the
USS
Titan
-A
traveled to Athan Prime to seek
Commodore
Geordi La Forge
's assistance in extracting their
away team
from
Daystrom Station
. (
PIC
: "
The Bounty
")
Two days later, after the
Borg
seized control of the Federation fleet during
Frontier Day
celebrations,
Admiral
Jean-Luc Picard
and his comrades returned to Athan Prime in a
shuttle
to retrieve the rebuilt
USS
Enterprise
-D
from the Museum. (
PIC
: "
Vox
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Athos IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Athos_IV
======================================================================

Athos IV
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Maquis
Status:
Habitable
(
2373
)
Location:
Demilitarized Zone
(
2370
-
73
)
Badlands
Alpha Quadrant
Athos IV
was the
uninhabited
fourth
planet
in its
star system
. This planet was located in the
Badlands
and, in the
2370s
, was located in the
Demilitarized Zone
. It was a barren,
fog
-shrouded world.
This was briefly, in
2373
, the home of an underground
Maquis
base, one of the last surviving pockets of the Maquis after the
Dominion
massacred the resistance group. The Maquis at the base supposedly fired a volley of
cloaked
missiles
towards
Cardassia Prime
as a final act of
revenge
.
Captain
Benjamin Sisko
persuaded
Michael Eddington
to guide him to the planet and help him stop the missiles, whereupon he discovered that they did not in fact exist, and the message announcing the attack was to signal Eddington that the Maquis there were still alive.
Unfortunately, the
Jem'Hadar
had discovered and overran much of Athos IV as well, and Eddington was killed during the evacuation. The remaining Maquis at the base were taken to
Federation
space
aboard Sisko's
runabout
. (
DS9
: "
Blaze of Glory
")
Appendices
Background information
"AH-thoes" was the pronunciation for this planet's
name
from the
script
pronunciation guide.
[1]
Athos IV was mentioned in the final draft script of
TOS
: "
The Galileo Seven
", where it was apparently not uninhabited as it was said to be the
homeworld
of "the
Lake People
", who were characterized as having large, distinctive
axes
.
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 49 classified Athos IV as a
class M
planetoid
.
Apocrypha
In the
Star Trek: Mirror Universe
novel
Rise Like Lions
,
Miles O'Brien
ordered the
Terran Rebellion
to regroup on the
mirror universe
Athos IV after
Terok Nor
was destroyed.
External link
Athos IV
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

Atifs IV
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
24th century
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Atifs IV
was the
inhabited
fourth
planet
in its
star system
. This planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
This
planet
was known to the
Federation
by the mid-
24th century
. The
oligarchy
of the planet performed a ceremony of thanks during mealtime that had echoes in
Betazoid
and
Oolan
culture
. (
TNG
: "
Manhunt
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
The way that Data pronounced this word made it appear that there was an 'r' in the
name
, so that it would be spelled, "Artifs". The spelling of this planet was from the
script
, and this spelling was used at
StarTrek.com
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Atlec
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Atlec
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Atlec
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Coalition of Madena
Status:
Habitable
(
2365
)
Native species:
Atlec inhabitants
Location:
Omega Sagitta
system
Alpha Quadrant
Atlec
was an
inhabited
Class M
planet
in the
Omega Sagitta
system
, The planet was located in the
Alpha Quadrant
.
History
In the early
2160s
, this planet, and its twin
Straleb
, were
colonized
by a
humanoid species
. These twin planets formed the
Coalition of Madena
, which was created by a treaty.
A conflict between Atlec and Straleb nearly broke out in
2365
when the Straleb
Jewel of Thesia
was thought to have been stolen by
Thadiun Okona
, who was also accused of impregnating
Yanar
, the
daughter
of the
leader
of Atlec,
Debin
.
Following a confrontation involving the
Federation
starship
USS
Enterprise
-D
, an
Atlec vessel
and a
Straleb security vessel
, it was revealed that the jewel, which was in Okona's possession, was destined for Yanar, from
Benzan
, the
heir
of the jewel. (
TNG
: "
The Outrageous Okona
")
Stellar cartography
In
3196
, during the
Omega-47
crisis, the location of Omega Sagitta (Madena, Atlec, Straleb) was depicted on
star charts
and readouts used across several locations on the
USS
Athena
to locate, monitor, and respond to the
Omega-47 mines
laid by the
Venari Ral
. This system and its planets were outside the barrier. (
SA
: "
300th Night
", "
Rubincon
")
People
Atlec inhabitants
Appendices
Background information
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star charts
appearing in the
Star Trek: Discovery
episode "
Die Trying
" and in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
StarTrek.com
's entry for the planet described the planet as one half of a pair of
Trojan
planets.
[1]
(X)
The planet's name was spelled as "Altec" in the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 3rd ed., p. 9 and in the
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I"); however, it was spelled as "Atlec" in the
script
[2]
and at
StarTrek.com
.
[3]
(X)
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 21 classified Atlec, spelled incorrectly here as Altec, as a
class M
planet.
External link
Atlec
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Atrea IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Atrea_IV
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Atrea IV
AKA:
Atrea
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
(
3195
)
Status:
Habitability preserved
(
2370
)
Native species:
Atreans
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Atrea IV
, or, more simply, as
Atrea
, was the
inhabited
Class M
fourth
planet
in its
star system
. The planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
. It was the
homeworld
of the
humanoid
Atreans
. The planet was governed by the
Atrean government
.
In
2370
, it was discovered that Atrea IV's molten
core
was solidifying, which was weakening the planet's
gravitational field
and
seismic
activity had increased by a factor of three.
Dr.
Juliana Tainer
, a
geologist
living on the planet, estimated that it would become uninhabitable in thirteen
months
. The
USS
Enterprise
-D
was sent in response to a request by the planet's
government
for help in averting this
natural disaster
.
Lieutenant Commanders
Data
and
Geordi La Forge
were teamed up with the Atrean
geologist
Dr.
Pran Tainer
and his
wife
Dr. Juliana Tainer. They developed a means to re-liquify the core using a
ferro-plasmic infusion
, which injected heat into the planet to stabilize the core for
centuries
. (
TNG
: "
Inheritance
")
In the
32nd century
, Atrea IV was a
Federation
member
planet. It was listed with other members on a video wall in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
The Life of the Stars
")
"A-tree-uh" was the pronunciation for this planet's
name
from the
script
pronunciation guide.
The planet's classification came from
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 50.
According to the
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I"), in
2378
, Atrea IV was listed as a
Federation member
.
External link
Atrea IV
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Automated repair station
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Automated_repair_station
======================================================================

Automated repair station
Type:
Drydock
Status:
Active
(
2152
)
Docking facilities:
2 adjustable space docks
"
Your inquiry was not recognized.
"
–
Automated repair station
,
2152
("
Dead Stop
")
The
automated repair station
was an
automated
alien
space station
operational in the
22nd century
which repaired vessels for payment. The civilization that built the station was not widely known during this time.
Design
This station possessed advanced
technology
that was capable of automatically repairing
vessels
as well as tending to a wide range of
humanoids
'
medical
injuries at remarkable speed. The station's two docking berths consisted of
docking arms
,
umbilical connect ports
and a
docking port
for boarding. The berths could expand and contract to fit a reasonably wide range of vessels.
Internal schematic
The interior of the station was designed to be very accommodating to the wide range of humanoid species during repairs. Its
environmental systems
were capable of adapting via
temperature
and
atmosphere
. A
recreation room
was available that included
tables
,
chairs
, and
food replicators
with a view of the repairs.
Once a ship entered its docking berths, the station's
computer
diagnosed its repair needs as well as scanned its crew for medical injuries. It also scanned the ship's
databases
to find out
food
recipes
and their needs for atmosphere. In return for its repair services, the station would "charge" a fee consisting of an item from a list of payments of the ship's available resources, such as a quantity of
warp plasma
. Attempting to enter restricted areas of the station would activate security procedures, such as physically blocking further access and transporting trespassers back to their ship. Should these initial procedures be disabled, the station would use its connections and repair arms to impair the ship's functions - including life support - until the crew relented.
The main room of the station in which the computer interacted with its customers consisted of a
holographic projector
which displayed the customer's ship and damaged areas. A screen displayed the payment options and payment was delivered to that room. The computer would not answer any questions other than ones relating to the transaction.
The station's
computer core
used the
synaptic pathways
of humanoid hosts to enhance its processing capabilities. It accomplished this task by integrating the hosts' synaptic
brain
functions into the station's computer core. These hosts were abducted discreetly by the station. One known method of abduction was by deceiving a crew member to enter a restricted area currently under repair by the station,
transporting
them into its computer core room, and then producing an almost identical and deceased replica of the individual to be found by the rest of the crew.
Diagnostic room
Cooling ducts
History
This station served several vessels including
a Tellarite freighter captain
while discreetly abducting a few of each of their crews.
Enterprise
wasn't the end of the repair station
On the recommendation of the same Tellarite
captain
, the
Earth
starship
Enterprise
NX-01
used the station in
2152
, following their need of repairs after an incident in a
Romulan
minefield
. (
ENT
: "
Minefield
") The station began repairing the ship in record time, and repaired
Malcolm Reed
's left
leg
, which had been wounded by a
Romulan mine
. The
Humans
at the time were amazed by the technology, including the replicators. The station also attempted to add
Travis Mayweather
to its collection of hosts, creating
a replica
; However, an extensive
autopsy
of the
doppelgänger
by
Doctor
Phlox
discovered a discrepancy: The station could not reproduce living, single-celled organisms like those present in a vaccine he had recently administered. This made it clear that the body was not the actual Travis Mayweather. Captain
Jonathan Archer
and
Sub-commander
T'Pol
accessed the restricted computer core room, and, after rescuing Mayweather, were able to escape. They also attempted to destroy the station as they left, but unbeknownst to them, the station began to repair itself after they left. (
ENT
: "
Dead Stop
")
Abducted species
Axanar
Cardassian
Ellora
Kago's species
Klingon
Kreetassan
Pelian
Vaadwaur
Vulcan
Xepolite
Unnamed humanoids
In the final draft script of "
Dead Stop
", not all the abducted species were identified. The script at first described the group merely as "at least forty humanoid bodies, pale and dissected," of which they were "all different species, wearing uniforms in various stages of decay." The script went on to comment, "
Some of the aliens have apparently been here for months... others for years.
" Subsequently, the Klingon and Vulcan species were identified, in that order.
The call sheet for Tuesday
20 August
2002
identified the Klingon, Vulcan, Kreetassan, Elloran, Vaadwaur, the Pelian (as "Asparagus Head"), and the member of Kago's species (as Big Head). Also listed is a "Knuklehead", played by actor
Michael Doherty
.
Background information
The station was designed by
Doug Drexler
, who was inspired by a
Chinese finger puzzle
, and modeled by
Pierre Drolet
.
[1]
(X)
Dialogue suggests that the station was at least 130
light years
from Earth.
Given the presence of the
Vaadwaur
corpse, it may be that this station dates back to before the Vaadwaur went into stasis in the fifteenth century, making this station more than six hundred years old.
The station's
computer voice
was supplied by
Star Trek: Voyager
actress and "Dead Stop" director
Roxann Dawson
. She was not credited for the role.
In retrospect,
Star Trek: Enterprise
co-creator and Executive Producer
Brannon Braga
noted, "
This idea of a weird automated space station is terrific – totally creepy.
" (
Star Trek: Communicator
issue 145
, p. 25)
The diagnosis hologram component was among the items which were sold off on the
It's A Wrap! sale and auction
on eBay.
[2]
(X)
External link
Ware repair station
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

Avenal VII
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Cardassian Union
(
24th century
)
Dominion
(
2373
-
2375
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Location:
Avenal
system
Alpha Quadrant
Avenal VII
was the
seventh
planet
in the
Avenal
system
,
Alpha Quadrant
.
In
2375
,
Chancellor
Gowron
ordered
General
Martok
to launch an ill-advised
attack
on this planet. The attack was a disaster, as the
Klingons
were outnumbered six to one: seven Klingon
warships
were destroyed, and five more severely damaged, with General Martok himself in the
sickbay
of the
IKS
Rotarran
in critical condition. The attack was nothing short of a waste of ships, manpower and war materials for the allies. (
DS9
: "
Tacking Into the Wind
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
"AV-in-ell" was the pronunciation for this planet's name from the script pronunciation guide.
[1]
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of the Cardassian Union as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".

at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek

======================================================================
PAGE: Avery III
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Avery_III
======================================================================

Avery III
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Vidiian Sodality
Status:
Habitable
(
2371
)
Location:
Avery
system
Vidiian space
Unnaemd sector
Delta Quadrant
Avery III
was the
inhabited
Class M
third
planet
in the
Avery
system
. Found inside
Vidiian
space
, this planet was located in a
sector
of the
Delta Quadrant
.
Hidden in its
cave
system was a
Vidiian Sodality
base, where
organ processing
was performed and
chief surgeon
Sulan
conducted his research on the
phage
.
Tunnel
digging, using captured
aliens
, also went on. The area cloaked by the Vidiian technology was more than six hundred
kilometers
in
circumference
.
Rodents
were present inside the caves and tunnels, as well as
something
with a distinct cry.
In
2371
, an
away team
from
USS
Voyager
, consisting of
Lieutenants
B'Elanna Torres
,
Tom Paris
, and
Pete Durst
, were investigating
magnesite
deposits on Avery III when they were captured by the Vidiians. Durst was
killed
and his
organs
harvested before the team could be rescued. (
VOY
: "
Faces
")
Species on the planet
Alien species
Vidiian
Human
Talaxian
This planet was not named in the episode but rather was identified from
StarTrek.com
and the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 53. The planet's classification came from this source.
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 83), it was stated that the
primary
of the Avery system was a
A-type star
. The
Voyager
visited this system on
stardate
48784.
External links
Avery III
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Axanar (planet)
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Axanar_(planet)
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Axanar
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
(
3196
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Native species:
Axanar
Location:
Epsilon Eridani
system
Beta Quadrant
~11
light years
from
Sol
Axanar
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Epsilon Eridani
system
. This planet was the
homeworld
for the
Axanar
, a
warp-capable
humanoid
species
.
History
In
2151
,
Starfleet
first learned of this planet upon making
first contact
with the Axanar. (
ENT
: "
Fight or Flight
")
Prior to
2252
,
Captain
Garth of Izar
won a major victory at Axanar. A written account of this battle was
read
by
James T. Kirk
when he was a
cadet
at
Starfleet Academy
. Into the
2260s
, this account was required reading at the Academy. (
TOS
: "
Whom Gods Destroy
")
Later, while still a cadet, Kirk was on a peace mission to this planet. After the successful completion of this mission, he was awarded the
Palm Leaf of Axanar
. (
TOS
: "
Court Martial
", "
Whom Gods Destroy
")
In the
32nd century
, Axanar was a
Federation
member
planet. It was listed with other members on a video wall in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
The Life of the Stars
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet was within the enclosed region. (
SA
: "
300th Night
", "
Rubincon
")
Stellar cartography
In
3195
, this planet's position was labeled on a
Federation
star chart
which was displayed in
Starfleet Academy
's
Stellar Cartography
laboratory
. Planets affiliated with the Federation, including this one, had
blue
labels. (
SA
: "
Beta Test
")
In
3196
, this planet's position was labeled on a Federation star chart which was displayed on the
main viewscreen
of the
USS
Athena
bridge
. (
SA
: "
Ko'Zeine
")
Later that year, during the
Omega-47
crisis, the location of Axanar (Epsilon Eridani) was depicted on star charts and readouts used across several locations on the
USS
Athena
to locate, monitor, and respond to the Omega-47 mines laid by the Venari Ral. (
SA
: "
300th Night
", "
Rubincon
")
See also
Battle of Axanar
Axanar Peace Mission
Palm Leaf of Axanar Peace Mission
Axanar Starbase Terminal
Appendices
Background information
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
In the
script
of "Fight or Flight", the
name
of this planet was phonetically notated to be pronounced "AX-a-nar".
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
(pp. 60 & 66; "United Federation of Planets I") and
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Federation Historical Highlights, 2161-2385"), Axanar was located in the Axanar system, which was also known as
Epsilon Eridani
. On May 6,
2151
, this system was visited by
Enterprise
NX-01
. In
2378
, Axanar was listed as a
Federation member
.
Apocrypha
In
FASA
's
RPG
sourcebook
The Federation
, Axanar was identified as the
first
planet in the Delta Orcus (
β
) system.
External link
Axanar
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

Axilus
was a celestial location that was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
Beckett Mariner
once
stranded
Brad Boimler
on Axilus. In
2381
, Boimler commiserated with
Malvus
about this incident. (
LD
: "
An Embarrassment Of Dooplers
")
This location was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Azati Prime
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Azati_Prime
======================================================================

Azati Prime
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Xindi Council
(
2154
)
United Federation of Planets
(
2259
)
Satellite(s):
At least one
moon
Location:
Azati Prime system
Delphic Expanse
(prior to
2154
)
Beta Quadrant
For the
ENT
episode of the same name, please see "
Azati Prime
".
Azati Prime
was the
Xindi
-
inhabited
Class M
prime
planet
in the
Azati Prime
system
. Found in the
Alpha Quadrant
, this planet had had at least a single
moon
. (
ENT
: "
Azati Prime
")
The planet was mostly covered by
water
, with the few small landmasses not containing any
life
. The water was suited for
Xindi-Aquatics
to move in without protection.
Degra
described it as bleak and desolate, barely habitable. All of the Xindi facilities on Azati Prime were located underwater, including the construction yards for the
Xindi weapon
. (
ENT
: "
Stratagem
", "
Azati Prime
")
According to Degra, it was a diverse community with over a thousand
Xindi-Arboreals
and
Xindi-Primates
. Degra also claimed it contained an
Insectoid
deuterium facility
. (
ENT
: "
Stratagem
")
Degra
visited this planet shortly before traveling to the
Calindra system
to test a prototype of the
Xindi weapon
in
December
2153
. (
ENT
: "
Stratagem
")
In
February
2154
,
Enterprise
reached the system after a long search for the Xindi weapon. Azati Prime was one of two inner planets detected to have substantial Xindi activity at this time, and the weapon launch was imminent.
The
Xindi-Insectoid assault shuttle
obtained by the crew located the weapon on Azati Prime. Once the Xindi realized the
Humans
knew where the weapon was, they relocated it to the
Xindi Council planet
. (
ENT
: "
Azati Prime
")
By
2259
, multiple
star charts
showed the
Azati Prime system
as part of
Federation territory
. (
SNW
: "
Strange New Worlds
")
Appendices
Background information
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
". The current location of Azati Prime in the Beta Quadrant was based on the most recent star charts seen in
Star Trek: Strange New Worlds
and
Star Trek: Section 31
.
Any information Degra gave about the planet might be considered suspect, given that he gave the information to Archer, which he later claimed to have distrusted to some extent from the beginning. Degra also implied it would naturally be one of the first planets the Insectoids might attack should they turn on the other species.
The planet's classification came from
StarTrek.com
, which said there a colony population of 1,000 Xindi-Primates and Xindi-Arboreals.
[1]
(X)
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 55 located this planet in the Azati system.
External link
Azati Prime
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

PAGE: Ba'Neth space station

A cloaked Ba'Neth space station
The
Ba'Neth space station
was a type of armed
outpost
operated as a hub by the
Delta Quadrant
species
Ba'Neth
.
After an attack by the Ba'Neth on the
Delta Flyer
in
2376
, the crew of the
USS
Voyager
located one of these
space stations
in a
star system
in
Grid 216
of a
sector
of
space
shared with the
Kesat
. It was protected by a nine million
terawatt
cloaking field
. The station housed approximately three thousand Ba'Neth and
twenty-two ships
. (
VOY
: "
Riddles
")
For more information on this
studio model
, see:
VOY studio models
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Ba'ku (planet)
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ba'ku_(planet)
======================================================================

Ba'ku
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2375
)
Satellite(s):
A
ring system
Native species:
Ba'ku fish
Ba'ku goat
Ba'ku hummingbird
Ba'ku llama
Rhyl
Location:
Ba'ku system
Briar Patch
Sector 441
Beta Quadrant
"
A small group of us set off to find a new home. A home that would be isolated from the threats of other worlds.
"
–
Sojef
,
2375
(
Star Trek: Insurrection
)
Ba'ku
was an
inhabited
Class M
planet
in the
Ba'ku
system
. Found in the
Briar Patch
, this planet was in
Sector 441
,
Alpha Quadrant
. It was the adopted
homeworld
of the
Ba'ku
, a
humanoid
race. The planet's
rings
produced a unique form of
metaphasic radiation
that granted those on the surface health and
eternal life
.
History
The planet was settled in
2066
by a small group who had fled their
star system
, fearing that their civilization was headed for self-destruction. Coming to call themselves the Ba'ku, they established an agrarian society without advanced
technology
. In the mid-
23rd century
, a group of young Ba'ku who wished to follow the ways of the
offlanders
were
exiled
from the planet after a failed attempt to take over their village. They became the
Son'a
. (
Star Trek: Insurrection
)
In
2257
, during the first
Federation-Klingon War
, this star's system was occupied by the
Klingons
. (
DIS
: "
The War Without, The War Within
")
In
2375
, there were six hundred Ba'ku living on the planet. As the Briar Patch was now
Federation
territory, the Son'a colluded with elements of Starfleet to relocate the Ba'ku and collect the metaphasic particles from their planet, which would have destroyed all life on the surface. These plans were thwarted by the crew of the
USS
Enterprise
-E
. The
Federation Council
subsequently halted the relocation plans while a top-level review was conducted. Meanwhile, the
Son'a
and the
Ba'ku
began the process of reconciliation. (
Star Trek: Insurrection
)
On Ba'ku, the name "
Zora
" meant "dawn" or "new day". (
DIS
: "
Stormy Weather
")
Environment
A rich variety of flora and fauna were found on Ba'ku. Near the Ba'ku village, there were hills and mountains containing veins of
kelbonite
, which blocked
transporter
function. The mountains contained
caves
that could shelter large numbers of people. (
Star Trek: Insurrection
)
Lifeforms
Ba'ku fish
Ba'ku goat
Ba'ku hummingbird
Ba'ku llama
Rhyl
Ba'ku fish
Ba'ku hummingbird
Rhyl
A Ba'ku children's book illustrating several species
Furthermore, a book produced for the film but of which only the cover was seen on screen depicted a number of other lifeforms, including animals similar to a
pterodactyl
, a
turtle
, and a
butterfly
.
Appendices
Background information
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star charts
appearing in the
Star Trek: Discovery
episode "
Magic to Make the Sanest Man Go Mad
" and in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
". The current location of Ba'ku in the Beta Quadrant was based on the most recent star charts seen in
Star Trek: Strange New Worlds
and
Star Trek: Section 31
.
The planet's classification came from the
script
for
Star Trek: Insurrection
, as well as the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 57. A line from
Troi
would have made this explicit but was deleted from the final film. A further
deleted scene
would have established that the Federation would ultimately make Ba'ku a
protectorate
.
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 62) and
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Federation Historical Highlights, 2161-2385"), the Ba'ku's
primary
was a
K-type
star.
Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I") suggests Ba'ku became a
Federation member
by
2378
.
Design and filming
Exterior scenes in the Ba'ku village were filmed on the shore of Lake Sherwood in southern California on a quiet inlet to the south of the lake. (
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 3rd ed., p. 583) The exact coordinates are 34.135038, −118.865526. A luxury housing development was built over the location in 2017, now gated to visitors and inaccessible.
Visual effects in scenes set on Ba'ku were handled by
Blue Sky/VIFX
. (
Cinefex
, No. 77, p. 71)
Zimmerman started to arrive at the final design of the village in December 1997
Herman Zimmerman
's
concept designs
for the Ba'ku village, described in an early version of the script as "a rural community along a vast sparkling lake," was inspired by several cultures along the Pacific Rim. One image of a hotel in Bali in particular sparked Zimmerman's and
Rick Berman
's direction for the design. (
Star Trek: The Magazine
Volume 1, Issue 1
, pp. 30-31)
Apocrypha
In
Star Trek: Hidden Evil
, it was revealed that the planet was originally a colony of the
Progenitors
millennia before the arrival of the Ba'ku.
In the RPG sourcebook
Planets of the UFP
, it was stated that Ba'ku's native fauna included a bird with a bee-like stinger called the
jenkaw
and a llama-like animal called the
e'kar
.
External link
Ba'ku (planet)
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Babel
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Babel

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
For the
DS9
episode of the same name, please see "
Babel
".
Babel
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Earth
United Federation of Planets
(
3196
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Location:
Wolf 424
Unnamed sector
Beta Quadrant
~14
light years
from
Sol
Babel
was the
code name
for an
inhabited
neutral
planet
in the
Wolf 424
system
.   Found in a
sector
of the
Beta Quadrant
, the planet was used by
United Earth
, and later the
United Federation of Planets
, as a venue for conferences, which were mediated in council chambers. (
TOS
: "
Journey to Babel
";
TAS
: "
The Counter-Clock Incident
";
ENT
: "
Babel One
")
History
The planet was planned to host negotiations to settle trade disputes between the
Andorians
and the
Tellarites
and mediated by
Starfleet
on behalf of United Earth in
2154
. However, the meeting was postponed due to
tensions between the two powers
caused by a raiding
Romulan
scout vessel
in the area. (
ENT
: "
Babel One
", "
United
")
In
2267
, on
stardate
3850.3, the
Babel Conference
considered
Coridan
's admission to the Federation. Two things came of this conference: the
Babel Resolutions
and Coridan's admission to the Federation. (
TOS
: "
Journey to Babel
";
TAS
: "
The Pirates of Orion
";
TNG
: "
Sarek
")
In
2270
, the
USS
Enterprise
transported
Commodore
Robert April
and his
wife
Sarah
to Babel, where
ambassadors
from all
Federation worlds
had converged to honor April as he had reached his
mandatory retirement
age. (
TAS
: "
The Counter-Clock Incident
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this system was within the enclosed region. (
SA
: "
300th Night
")
Stellar cartography
Babel was approximately 14
light years
from
Sol
.
Relative to
Tellar Prime
, Babel was located on the far side of
Andorian space
. (
ENT
: "
Babel One
")
In the
2150s
, this planet's position was labeled on an
United Earth
star chart
which was displayed in several crew and guest
quarters
aboard the
Enterprise
. (
Star Trek: Enterprise
,
set decoration
)
In
2259
, the location of this planet was labeled on a
stellar cartography
chart that was seen on the
USS
Enterprise
's
ready room
viewscreen
. This planet's symbol had a
blue
color
, indicating that it was affiliated with the
United Federation of Planets
. (
SNW
: "
Strange New Worlds
", "
Spock Amok
", "
A Quality of Mercy
")
In
2293
, the
location
of Babel in the
Milky Way Galaxy
was labeled in the
star chart
The Explored Galaxy
, which was on display in
James T. Kirk
's
quarters
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-A
. (
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
,
okudagram
)
On
stardate
1292.4, the location of this planetoid was labeled in a chart used during a
Section 31
Alpha Team
briefing
. (
Star Trek: Section 31
)
In
2365
, the location of Babel was labeled in a Subspace Comm Net Ops star chart, which was seen in the
courtroom
on
Starbase 173
. This planet was connected to the
Sol system
and
Kling
by subspace lines. (
TNG
: "
The Measure Of A Man
")
In August 2024, an annotated computer paste-up of this star chart was put up for auction in the Entertainment Memorabiliia Live Auction: Los Angeles Summer 2024 by Propstore Auction.
[1]
In
2367
, the location of Babel was labeled on a
tactical situation monitor
in
Captain
Benjamin Maxwell
's
ready room
on the
USS
Phoenix
. (
TNG
: "
The Wounded
",
okudagram
)
In
2366
, in an
alternate timeline
, during the
Federation-Klingon War
, Babel's position was labeled on a tactical situation monitor on the
bridge
and in the
ready room
of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
.
(
TNG
: "
Yesterday's Enterprise
",
okudagram
)
The planet's location was labeled in a
Federation star chart
that was in
Fleet Admiral
Kirsten Clancy
's
office
at
Starfleet Headquarters
in
2399
and on the
bridge
of the
USS
Titan
-A
in
2401
. Babel was in or near to
Federation space
. (
PIC
: "
Maps and Legends
", "
The Next Generation
", "
Disengage
")
In 2401, the location of Babel was labeled on a star chart that was seen on the
USS
Stargazer
's
bridge
viewscreen
. The chart was used to show the location of the combined Starfleet-Borg armada and where it needed to go to face a galactic-level threat. (
PIC
: "
Farewell
")
Later that year, Babel's position was labeled on a
star chart
used by
Captain
William T. Riker
during his attempt at finding the last known location of the
SS
Eleos XII
. (
PIC
: "
The Next Generation
")
In
3196
, this planet's position was labeled on a Federation star chart which was displayed on the
main viewscreen
of the

Athena
bridge
. (
SA
: "
Ko'Zeine
")

, during the

to locate, monitor, and respond to the Omega-47 mines laid by the Venari Ral. (

Appendices
Background information
Babel was described as a
planetoid
in its first mention in "Journey to Babel", but as a planet in "The Counter-Clock Incident" and "Babel One".
StarTrek.com
described Babel as a
rogue planet
.
[2]
(X)
Information on Babel was derived from the episodes, the
reference works
Star Trek: Star Charts
and
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
, and real-world sources. The reference works were used extensively in the creation of
star charts
seen in
Star Trek: Discovery
,
Star Trek: Picard
,
Star Trek: Strange New Worlds
,
Star Trek: Prodigy
,
Star Trek: Lower Decks
, and
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
. The real-world information added to the article was current as of 2026.
The star chart seen in the series
Star Trek: Enterprise
made its first appearance in
Crewman
Daniels
quarters in "
Cold Front
". It was also seen in several other episodes of the series, from
2151
to
2154
. (
For more information, see
Federation star charts#United Earth Alpha/Beta star chart
)
The current location of Babel in the Beta Quadrant was based on the most recent star charts seen in
Star Trek: Strange New Worlds
and
Star Trek: Section 31
.
Apocrypha
In the
Star Trek Maps
(p. 24 and chart D), Babel was located in the Wolf 424 system. The Babel planetoid had three small
moons
called Zion, Ba, and Ruim. Babel had a
diameter
of 4,200
kilometers
and orbited a
red dwarf
star.
FASA
's
The Federation
sourcebook identified Babel as the
twelfth
planet in the Wolf 424 system.

Bahar
was a location referenced in the
Children of Tama
language
metaphorical
phrase, "
Callimas
at Bahar
".
In
2368
, after an outburst at
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
, Captain
Dathon
spoke this phrase, implying his intention to make up for his outburst. (
TNG
: "
Darmok
")
This location was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to the
script
, Bahar was pronounced as "buh-HAHR".

======================================================================
PAGE: Bahrat's space station
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Bahrat's_space_station
======================================================================

Bahrat's space station
Type:
Supply depot
Operator:
Bahrat
Location:
On the border of the
Nekrit Expanse
Delta Quadrant
Status:
Active
(
2373
)
Bahrat's space station
was a
supply depot
for mining and trading ships located on the border of the
Nekrit Expanse
.
It was operated by
Bahrat
during the early-
2370s
. Bahrat took twenty
percent
commission
on all trade on the station. He also monitored all visitors closely, and reserved the right to monitor ship's communications. Regulations included station code 4279, subsection beta 325. Criminals might face their ships being impounded or
cryostatic suspension
in his prison as long as fifty years. Despite this,
murder
was not an uncommon occurrence, and while Bahrat thought very little
narcotics
trafficking
went on on the station,
Kolaati
traders had made a fortune from it by doing things like disabling surveillance systems and masking sensors.
Wixiban
got stuck on the station for three years after his ship was impounded and he couldn't give Bahrat what he wanted to get it back.
In
2373
, the
USS
Voyager
visited the
station
before venturing into the Expanse. (
VOY
: "
Fair Trade
")
It was later visited by a
Voth
scientist
,
Forra Gegen
, and his assistant,
Tova Veer
, where they acquired a sample of
Voyager
's
warp plasma
from a
Tak Tak
trader
. (
VOY
: "
Distant Origin
")
According to one draft of the
script
, this structure was named the "Nekratis border station", which was further described as "
an immense space station. Rickety and sprawling, it's a hodgepodge of habitat cylinders,
cargo pods
,
docking ports
, and instrument arrays. Hull damage and corrosion is markedly evident. The general impression of the place is something just this side of a floating
junkyard
.
"
The "Nekrit Supply Depot" exterior, according to the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 72, was designed by
Rick Sternbach
.
People
See
:
Bahrat's space station personnel

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Bajor
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Bajoran Republic
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Satellite(s):
Derna
Jeraddo
Three additional moons
Native species:
Bajoran
Location:
Bajoran
system
Bajoran space
Bajor
sector
Alpha Quadrant
"
'Nice'? That hardly describes it at all. There are parts of the
Eastern Province
that are like
Eden
itself – lush green
valleys
covered in
wildflowers
that seem to spring up overnight, hundreds of small, crystal clear
ponds
interconnected by
waterfalls
.
"
–
Benjamin Sisko
,
2374
("
Favor the Bold
")
Bajor
was an
inhabited
planet
, the
eleventh
of the
Bajoran system
. Found inside
Bajoran space
, this system was in the
Bajor sector
of
Alpha Quadrant
. Bajor was the largest planet in the system and had five
moons
, including
Derna
and
Jeraddo
. The planet was the
homeworld
of the
Bajorans
, a
warp-capable
humanoid
species
. After
decades
of
Cardassian
rule, the planet regained its independence under the
Bajoran Republic
and became affiliated with the
United Federation of Planets
in
2369
, with the intention of joining. Their membership eventually came to pass by the year
3191
. (
DIS
: "
Lagrange Point
")
In the
3190s
, this planet's system was located in or near to the
Venari Ral Zone
. (
SA
: "
300th Night
", "
Rubincon
")
The fact that Bajor was the largest planet in the Bajoran system comes from
DS9
: "
The Nagus
".
History
See also:
Bajoran history
Early history
Icon painting
of the ancient Bajoran city of B'hala
Bajor was the homeworld of the Bajorans, one of the oldest Alpha Quadrant civilizations, which began to flourish about
half a million years ago
and undertook its first interstellar flights around the
16th century
. (
TNG
: "
Ensign Ro
";
DS9
: "
Explorers
")
The planet was once a center of learning, with non-Bajorans attracted by the cultural aspects of the planet. (
DS9
: "
In the Hands of the Prophets
")
Cardassian occupation and recovery
From about
2319
to
2369
(
DS9
: "
Emissary
"), Bajor was
occupied by the Cardassian Union
. During this time, the Cardassians killed most of the Bajoran
population
and destroyed large parts of its infrastructure, but also
poisoned
several areas of the planet in order to compromise the food supply after their withdrawal. Affected regions included the
Dahkur
and
Rakantha Provinces
as well as the
Northwest peninsula
. Following the occupation, the
Bajoran Provisional Government
took steps to revitalize the planet's agricultural sector, such as using special
grain processing centers
and irrigating the
Trilar Peninsula
. In
2371
, the
Bajoran Agricultural Ministry
developed
soil reclamators
that could detoxify the soil, but their number was limited, causing quarrels about their usage. (
DS9
: "
Dramatis Personae
", "
Shakaar
", "
Sanctuary
")
The Federation also assisted in reclaiming the planet's full natural potential, such as in 2369 when the
USS
Enterprise
-D
assisted in the reconstruction of the Bajoran
aqueduct
system. (
TNG
: "
Birthright, Part I
") Later measures included conducting an
agrobiology
expedition to the
Janitza Mountains
in 2371, during which it was discovered that the
ecosystem
was much more diverse than
orbital
scans had indicated. (
DS9
: "
The House of Quark
", "
Fascination
") Signs of progress in
healing
Bajor's wounds included the opening of a new
nature preserve
in
Hedrikspool Province
in
2370
and the fruitful
katterpod
season of
2373
. (
DS9
: "
Shadowplay
", "
Rapture
") That year, Bajor almost became a Federation member but halted the process at the last moment at the behest of the Emissary of the Prophets Captain Sisko. (
DS9
: "
Rapture
") When the
Dominion War
broke out later that year, this allowed Bajor to remain neutral and thereby avoid a
Dominion
occupation. (
DS9
: "
Sacrifice of Angels
")
Jack Crusher
, under his
alias
"James Cole", was charged with possession of unregistered
liquors
on Bajor in the late
24th century
. (
PIC
: "
Disengage
")
Advertisement for Bajor
In
2402
,
Deanna Troi
and
William T. Riker
considered taking a
beach
vacation
on Bajor. (
PIC
: "
The Last Generation
")
By
3191
, Bajor was a Federation member. (
DIS
: "
Lagrange Point
") Captain
Nahla Ake
became a teacher at the
Little Blooms
school on Bajor where
Fleet Admiral
Charles Vance
visited to offer her the position of
Chancellor of Starfleet Academy
. (
SA
: "
Kids These Days
")
Political structure
During the occupation, the Cardassians installed a puppet regime called the
Bajoran Occupational Government
. Following their withdrawal from Bajor in 2369, a Provisional Government (later known as the
Bajoran Republic
) was established, which consisted of
Ministers
who were organized in the
Bajoran Council
, the
Board of Ministers
, the
Chamber of Ministers
, and the
Council of Ministers
. (
DS9
: "
Emissary
", "
The Collaborator
", "
Dramatis Personae
", "
The Alternate
", "
The Homecoming
", "
Rapture
", "
In the Cards
") Some Bajoran organizations, like the
Kohn-Ma
or the
Alliance for Global Unity
, also criticized or even openly opposed the Provisional Government. (
DS9
: "
Past Prologue
", "
In the Hands of the Prophets
", "
The Homecoming
", "
The Circle
", "
The Siege
")
The planet was also further divided into different Provinces. Additionally, nation-like factions, like the
Paqu
and the
Navot
, continued to exist on Bajor after the occupation and maintained clearly defined borders between each other. (
DS9
: "
The Storyteller
", "
Shakaar
", "
What You Leave Behind
",
et al.
)
Although Bajor maintained a secular government,
religious
leadership, personified by the
Kai
as the head of the
Vedek Assembly
, was also an important aspect of social cohesion on Bajor and, thus, also held great political influence. (
DS9
: "
Emissary
", "
In the Hands of the Prophets
", "
Shakaar
",
et al.
)

DS9
: "
Past Tense, Part I
")
Glyrhond River

Parallel universes
In another
quantum reality
, Bajor defeated the Cardassian Union. Following their success, the Bajorans became more aggressive towards the Federation. In 2370, they destroyed a
subspace telescope
, believing that it was being used to spy on them. (
TNG
: "
Parallels
")
Terok Nor orbiting Bajor in the mirror universe
In the
mirror universe
, Bajor was occupied by the
Terran Empire
before being liberated by the
Klingon-Cardassian Alliance
. Following the end of the occupation, Bajor petitioned the Alliance for membership, was accepted, and ultimately became an influential member world. The
space station
Terok Nor
orbited Bajor. In
2370
, the
Intendant
of Bajor was
Kira Nerys
. (
DS9
: "
Crossover
")
Stellar Cartography
The distance between Bajor and the
Denorios belt
was approximately 160 million
kilometers
. (
DS9
: "
Emissary
")
In
2259
, the location of this planet was labeled on a
stellar cartography
chart that was seen on the
USS
Enterprise
's
ready room
viewscreen
. This planet's symbol had a
yellow
color
, indicating that it was affiliated with
Cardassia Prime
. (
SNW
: "
Strange New Worlds
")
In
2372
, the location of Bajor was labeled on a tactical cartography map that was displayed in the
wardroom
of
Deep Space 9
. The planet was located near the
Badlands
and the
Demilitarized Zone
. (
DS9
: "
For the Cause
",
okudagram
)
In
2375
, the location of Bajor was labeled on another tactical cartography map that was displayed in Deep Space 9's wardroom. Bajor was located approximately five
light years
from
Cardassia Prime
, twenty-five light years from
Starbase 375
, and sixty light years from
Ferenginar
. (
DS9
: "
When It Rains...
",
okudagram
)
The system's location was labeled in a
Federation star chart
that was in
Fleet Admiral
Kirsten Clancy
's
office
at
Starfleet Headquarters
in
2399
and on the
bridge
of the
USS
Titan
-A
in
2401
. Bajor was in or near to
Federation space
. (
PIC
: "
Maps and Legends
", "
The Next Generation
", "
Disengage
")
In 2401, this planet's position was labeled on a
star chart
used by
Captain
William T. Riker
during his attempt at finding the last known location of the
SS
Eleos XII
. (
PIC
: "
The Next Generation
")
In
3191
, the position of Bajor was labeled on a Federation
star chart
. Like other key members of the Federation, its position was denoted by a five-pointed star. (
DIS
: "
Lagrange Point
")
According to
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine Technical Manual
(p. 3), the exact distance between Cardassia and Bajor was 5.25 light years.
