======================================================================

Chalna
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Non-aligned
Status:
Habitable
(
2371
)
Native species:
Chalnoth
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Chalna
was an
inhabited
planet
. This was the
homeworld
for the
Chalnoth
, an
anarchistic
humanoid
species
.  The planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
While
commanding
the
USS
Stargazer
in
2354
,
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
visited Chalna. He recalled his visit there to
Esoqq
when they were imprisoned together
twelve years later
. (
TNG
: "
Allegiance
")
In
2371
, the
location
of Chalna was labeled in the
star chart
Data
and
Picard
were studying in
stellar cartography
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. (
Star Trek Generations
,
okudagram
)
Appendices
Apocrypha
In the
comic book
Children of Chaos
, the
Federation
name
for Chalna was Kaus Borealis V.
External links
Chalna
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Kaus Borealis
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Chaltok IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Chaltok_IV
======================================================================

Chaltok IV
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Romulan Star Empire
Romulan Free State
Status:
Habitable
(
2401
)
Location:
Chaltok
system
Romulan space
Beta Quadrant
Chaltok IV
was the
inhabited
fourth
planet
in the
Chaltok
system
. The planet was inside
Romulan space
in the
Beta Quadrant
.
In the
23rd century
, Chaltok IV was home to a
Romulan
research colony
. The
colony
was nearly destroyed during the testing of a
polaric energy
device
, which led to the signing of the
Polaric Test Ban Treaty
in
2268
. (
VOY
: "
Time and Again
")
In
2401
,
Laris
traveled to Chaltok IV to set up
diplomatic
security
.
Jean-Luc Picard
meant to accompany her, but was unable to do so due to receiving a
distress call
from
Beverly Crusher
. He promised to meet Laris on the planet later at a "great
bar
" where they could watch the
sunset
. (
PIC
: "
The Next Generation
")
Appendices
Background information
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
This planet's quadrant of origin is inferred based on the position its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Strange New Worlds
episode "
Strange New Worlds
".
Apocrypha
In
Star Trek Online
, Chaltok IV is home to a
Yridian
colony by 2409.
External links
Chaltok
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Chaltok IV
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

Chandra V
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Non-aligned
Status:
Habitable
(
2366
)
Native species:
Chandrans
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Chandra V
was the
inhabited
fifth
planet
in its
star system
, and was the
homeworld
of the non-
humanoid
Chandrans
. This planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
Tam Elbrun
was stationed here as the only
Federation
delegate
in
2366
, before he was called to assist in the
Gomtuu
incident. (
TNG
: "
Tin Man
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
"CHON-druh" was the pronunciation for this planet's
name
from the
script
pronunciation guide.
[1]

at

, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek

at

PAGE: Chaya VII
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Chaya_VII
======================================================================

Chaya VII
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
24th century
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Chaya VII
was the
inhabited
seventh
planet
in its
star system
. This planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
This planet was the location of a series of intergalactic caucuses that
Dr.
Leah Brahms
attended in the mid-
24th century
. Using recordings of these
debates
and a
Starfleet
personality profile
analysis
,
Geordi La Forge
was able to create a 90.7
%
accurate personality profile of the doctor to integrate into the
holographic
representation of her that he was working with in
2366
. (
TNG
: "
Booby Trap
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
External link
Chaya VII
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Checkpoint
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Checkpoint
======================================================================

This article or section is incomplete
This page lacks essential detail, and is therefore
in need of attention
.
Information regarding expansion requirements may be found on the article's
talk page
. Feel free to
edit
this page to assist with this expansion.
A
checkpoint
was a
location
where a
barrier
was established by a
security force
and people
traveling
through had to be cleared by members of the force.
In
2063
, the crew of the
USS
Enterprise
established defense checkpoints on the ship during their struggle against the
Borg
. After their
interplexing beacon
was destroyed, the Borg advanced and overran three of the checkpoints, according to
Lieutenant
Daniels
. (
Star Trek: First Contact
)
In
2267
, during the search for the
Mother Horta
on
planet
Janus VI
,
Captain
James T. Kirk
established a checkpoint on
level
23, code-named "
Tiger
".
Administrative head
Vanderberg
was to assemble his team of
miners
and rendezvous with the
USS
Enterprise
search parties
at this place. (
TOS
: "
The Devil in the Dark
")
In the
32nd century
, a checkpoint was placed at the entrance to the
Mercantile
in
Requiem
,
Hima
. This checkpoint was manned by
regulators
who were assisted in their duties by
drones
. In
3188
,
Cleveland Booker
and
Michael Burnham
attempted passing through this checkpoint. The drone scanned each of their left
sleeves
for a piece of technology which identified them as
couriers
. Unlike Booker, who passed the scan, Burnham failed the scan and was initially denied entrance because she had no identification. After Booker convinced one of the
bosses
that Burnham had a thing of value (her
23rd century
tricorder
) which might interest an antique collector of
vintage
technology, she was admitted into the marketplace. (
DIS
: "
That Hope Is You, Part 1
")
External link
Checkpoint
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Cheron
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Cheron
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Cheron
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2268
)
Native species:
Cheron natives
(
extinct
, except for off-world individuals)
Location:
Beta Quadrant
For the battle, please see
Battle of Cheron
.
Cheron
was a formerly
inhabited
class M
planet
in the
Beta Quadrant
. It was the
homeworld
of the
Cheron natives
, a nearly extinct
warp-capable
humanoid
species
that destroyed itself in internecine warfare. The planet was located in the southern region of the
Milky Way Galaxy
, on a course that led to the
Coalsack Nebula
.
History
Prior to
stardate
5730.2, this planet and its inhabitants were unknown to the
United Federation of Planets
. In
2268
,
first contact
was made with two of this planet's inhabitants.
Unfortunately, in the course of that
year
, when the
USS
Enterprise
scanned the planet, its instruments revealed
large uninhabited cities
, extensive
traffic systems
that were now barren of any traffic, and lower
animal
lifeforms
and
vegetation
encroaching on the cities. There was no sign of
sapient
life
, and there were vast numbers of unburied
corpses
in the still burning ruins of the
cities
. The entire Cheronian
civilization
had evidently totally destroyed itself in a war generally fueled by
racism
, and specifically fueled by hate. The
Enterprise
left the last two known surviving members of the
species
,
Bele
and
Lokai
, behind on this world, to which Bele had forced Kirk to return him and Lokai. (
TOS
: "
Let That Be Your Last Battlefield
")
Lokai
imagines
himself running down a
street
of a
burning
Cheron
city
.
Bele
imagines
himself running down a
street
of a
burning
Cheron
city
.
While Bele and Lokai believed themselves to be the last surviving members of the species, others were later known to have survived off-world, such as
Virgil
in the early
24th century
or a
Starfleet Academy
cadet in the late
32nd century
. (
Star Trek: Section 31
;
SA
: "
Kids These Days
")
Stellar cartography
Cheron labeled in stellar cartography (top)
In
2371
, the location of Cheron was labeled in the
star chart
Data
and
Picard
were studying in
stellar cartography
on the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. (
Star Trek Generations
okudagram
)
Appendices
Background information
The quadrant of origin for this planet was inferred from its nearness to the Coalsack Nebula, which was located in the Beta Quadrant.
In the
script
for "Blood Oath", Cheron was listed in the pronunciation guide, where the term was pronounced as "CHE-ron". However, Cheron was not mentioned elsewhere in the final script and the pronunciation used throughout the actual shooting of "Let That Be Your Last Battlefield" was "sheh-ROHN."
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 137 classified Cheron as a
class M
planet.
Apocrypha
In the
reference book
Star Trek: Federation - The First 150 Years
, Cheron was the planet where the
Battle of Cheron
of the
Earth-Romulan War
took place.
In the
novels
The Buried Age
,
Kobayashi Maru
, and
To Brave the Storm
, the planet Cheron was the
fifth
planet in the 83 Leonis B star system, and was the site of the Battle of Cheron in
2160
. Cheron was a strategically important planet for the
Romulan Star Empire
and was occupied with a military
outpost
at the time. Cheron was also where a
Romulan
fleet
of 81 ships massed in preparations for an all-out attack on
Earth
. The fleet was defeated and forced to retreat by allied starships. After the war, Cheron remained on the Federation side of the
Romulan Neutral Zone
.
In the novel
No Time Like the Past
, the
Enterprise
briefly returned to Cheron, with scans revealing that Bele and Lokai had set up bases for themselves on different sides of the planet; a time-displaced
Seven of Nine
noted that, even in her era, the Federation avoided Cheron due to the unpredictable power and
life span
of the surviving natives. The novel also revealed an attempt by Bele's side to create a
virus
that would infect only their enemies resulted in the virus being released during an enemy assault before it was ready, and both sides were destroyed (although Kirk and Seven, temporarily trapped in the past, were unaffected by the virus).
External links
83 Leonis
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
83 Leonis B
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
83 Leonis B V
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
83 Leonis
at
Wikipedia
Planets in TOS and TOS Remastered
at
Ex Astris Scientia

PAGE: Chin'toka scrapyard
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Chin'toka_scrapyard
======================================================================

Chin'toka scrapyard
Type:
Space
junkyard
Status:
Intact
(
2401
)
Location:
Chin'toka
system
Unnamed sector
Alpha Quadrant
The
Chin'toka scrapyard
was an unmanned
space
junkyard
in the
Chin'toka
system
. This junkyard was found in a
sector
of the
Alpha Quadrant
.
In
2401
, the
USS
Titan
-A
hid in the Chin'toka scrapyard while attempting to recruit allies to expose the
Changeling
infiltration
of
Starfleet
. After these efforts failed, the crew retrieved the derelict
VSS
T'Plana
from the scrapyard for use in a scheme to lure in and trap
Captain
Vadic
. (
PIC
: "
Dominion
")
It was possible, if not likely, the Chin'toka scrapyard largely or wholly consisted of debris from the the
First
and
Second
Battles of Chin'toka.

======================================================================
PAGE: Cirrus IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Cirrus_IV
======================================================================

Cirrus IV
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
24th century
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Cirrus IV
was the
fourth
planet
in its
star system
. The
Emerald Wading Pool
was located on this planet. This planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
In
2368
, aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
,
Dr.
Crusher
recommended a
holodeck
simulation of the Emerald Wading Pool on Cirrus IV to
crewmember
Kristin
. By this point, she had been injured twice in a simulation of the
Cliffs of Heaven
of
Sumiko IV
when attempting to dive off the
cliffs
. (
TNG
: "
Conundrum
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
The
script
originally referenced the Cliffs as being on Cirrus IV, and the
Emerald Wading Pool
being on Sumiko III. The final cut of the episode instead referenced the Cliffs as being on Sumiko IV and the Wading Pool on Cirrus IV.
External link
Cirrus IV
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

PAGE: Coltar IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Coltar_IV
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Coltar IV
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
(
3195
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3195
)
Location:
Unnamed sector
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Coltar IV
was the
inhabited
fourth
planet
in its
star system
. The planet was located in a
sector
of the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
Before
stardate
41697
, a
farming colony
had been established on the planet. In
2364
, this planet, along with the
freighter
USS
Lalo
, were the first to report that they had experienced the
Manheim Effect
. They were in the same
sector
at the time. (
TNG
: "
We'll Always Have Paris
")
In the
32nd century
, Coltar IV was a
Federation
member
planet. It was listed with other members on a video wall in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
The Life of the Stars
")
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I"), Coltar IV was listed as a Federation member in
2378
.
External link
Coltar IV
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Coopersworld
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Coopersworld
======================================================================

Coopersworld
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2328
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Coopersworld
was an
inhabited
namesake
planet
. This planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
In
2328
, the commercial transport
SS
Manoa
arrived at this planet, and collected the passenger
T. Roller
, then departed for
Mars City Station
.
Records of this were accessed by
Lieutenant Commander
Data
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
in
2370
, via the
commercial transport database
. (
TNG-R
: "
Inheritance
")
This planet was only mentioned in writing.
The planet was named after camera and electrical department electrician
Brian Cooper
.
In the original version of the episode, the original okudagram stated that
Ronnie Knox
boarded a
transport ship
at Coopersworld bound for
Johnson Station
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Coppelius
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Coppelius
======================================================================

Coppelius
AKA:
Ghulion IV
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Coppelius androids
Federation
(
protectorate
)
Status:
Habitable
(
2399
)
Satellite(s):
2
moons
Location:
Ghulion system
Vayt sector
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Coppelius
, also called
Ghulion IV
, was the
fourth
planet
in the
Ghulion system
. This planet was located inside the
Vayt sector
in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
. It was a
class M
planet that was smaller, but
denser
, than
Earth
. The planet was orbited by two
red
moons
.
While the android
Soji Asha
was undergoing the
Zhal Makh
, she had a
vision
that this planet had
electrical storms
as part of its
weather patterns
, though the natural environment was quite
sunny
, with a hot
desert
-like environment. (
PIC
: "
The Impossible Box
", "
Et in Arcadia Ego, Part 1
")
After the
Federation
banned
synthetic
life
in
2385
,
Doctor
Bruce Maddox
came to this world with
Altan Soong
and made their base at
Coppelius Station
, where they created a new generation of
organic
synths
. This facility showed up in the
dreams
of Soji Asha. The
Romulans
concluded that it was her
home
. (
PIC
: "
The Impossible Box
", "
Nepenthe
", "
Broken Pieces
")
General
Oh
led a squadron of
Red Lady
-type
warbirds
to
destroy
the planet's population of synths, but encountered
orchids
and a fleet of Starfleet starships led by the
USS
Zheng He
with
Riker
in command. After the synths activated a beacon they had built using knowledge from the
Admonition
, tentacles from an extra-galactic
alliance of synthetic life
appeared in a portal above Coppelius. Soji chose to destroy the beacon once she listened to a plea from
Jean-Luc Picard
. (
PIC
: "
Et in Arcadia Ego, Part 1
", "
Et in Arcadia Ego, Part 2
")
Coppelius became a Federation
protectorate
in
2399
. (
PIC
: "
Et in Arcadia Ego, Part 2
")
In an Instagram post, showrunner Michael Chabon said that Coppelius was a reference to the short story "
The Sandman
", written by
E.T.A. Hoffman
in 1816, which had as a theme the creation of automata. It was later adapted for the ballet
Coppélia
.
[1]

======================================================================
PAGE: Cor Caroli V
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Cor_Caroli_V
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Cor Caroli V
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Location:
Cor Caroli
system
Alpha Quadrant
~107
light years
from
Sol
Cor Caroli V
was the
inhabited
fifth
planet
in the
Cor Caroli
system
. Located in the
Alpha Quadrant
, this planet was approximately 107
light years
from
Sol
.
History
Cor Caroli V saw an outbreak of
Phyrox plague
in
2366
. A medical
away team
from the
USS
Enterprise
-D
, including
Doctor
Beverly Crusher
, successfully eliminated the plague, and
Starfleet Command
would subsequently consider the incident
classified
. (
TNG
: "
Allegiance
")
When
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
was
kidnapped
and held along with three other
individuals
of other
species
, one of which was actually one of
the kidnappers
posing as a
Bolian
Starfleet
cadet
named
Mitena Haro
, her knowledge of the
Enterprise
-D's visit to this planet led him to realize she might be an
impostor
. (
TNG
: "
Allegiance
")
The members of the
Enterprise
medical away team were recognized for their actions by being made
honorary
citizens
of the planet. Dr. Crusher would keep a plaque representing the honor with her in
2384
, and later on the
SS
Eleos XII
in
2401
. (
PRO
: "
Ouroboros, Part II
";
PIC
: "
The Next Generation
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet was within the enclosed region. (
SA
: "
300th Night
")
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".

Cor Caroli V
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Coral reef
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Coral_reef
======================================================================

A
coral reef
was a large patch of
coral
that acted as a
marine
ecosystem
.
The
2047
Hermosa Quake
sank a large part of
Los Angeles
200
feet
under
water
, which led to the creation of one of
Earth
's largest coral reefs, home to thousands of marine
lifeforms
. (
VOY
: "
Future's End
")
Bracas V
had coral reefs. In
2367
,
Geordi La Forge
recalled
skindiving
there. (
TNG
: "
The Loss
")
External link
Coral reef
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Corazonia
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Corazonia
======================================================================

Corazonia
Type:
Dyson ring
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Habitable
(
2381
)
Location:
Bespoke star system
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Corazonia
was an ancient
Dyson ring
located in a
bespoke
star system
. It was designed by an advanced
alien
species
that
evolved
into
fifth-dimensional energy beings
some six million years ago. The
megastructure
subsequently became inhabited by the
humanoid
Corazonians
, who were
members
of the
Federation
.
The building on Corazonia housing Vexilon
The internal environment of Corazonia was controlled by
Vexilon
: a
sentient computer
installed by the ring's builders. Vexilon was responsible for the ring's
weather
,
geography
, and
day
-
night
cycle. He ensured that the Corazonians lived in perpetually ideal conditions, and they shared a deep bond with him. The
climate
of the ring included
monsoon
seasons
. One of the landforms on the interior surface was
Inspiration Peak
.
During
first contact
with the Corazonians, the Federation installed a
power relay
in the ring.
Much later in
2381
, the Corazonians requested
Starfleet
assistance as Vexilon's performance had been degrading, leading to unpredictable weather and geographical anomalies. The
USS
Cerritos
, already tasked with
retrofitting
the power relay on Corazonia, responded.
Captain
Carol Freeman
discovered that Vexilon's
operating system
had not been updated since his creators vanished. However, after she initiated the update, Vexilon suffered a malfunction that caused massive
meteorological
upheaval throughout the ring.
While attempting to activate
safe mode
, Freeman inadvertently triggered a full
reboot
of the system. As a result, Vexilon began
regenesis
: creating
fjords
and
volcanoes
, distributing
miasma
, and stirring
primordial ooze
to return the ring to its default settings. Before he could disperse
anaerobic bacteria
, which would have had dire consequences, Freeman determined that Vexilon could force a
restart
if
primary power
were rerouted to the power relay. This allowed Vexilon to complete the update and restore normal operation, though the relay was destroyed. Freeman estimated that he would continue to function without issue for over a thousand years more. (
LD
: "
In the Cradle of Vexilon
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Coridan
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Coridan
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Coridan
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Coalition of Planets
United Federation of Planets
(
3196
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Native species:
Coridanites
Location:
Coridan
system
Unnamed sector
Beta Quadrant
~28
light years
from
Sol
Coridan
was an
inhabited
Federation
planet
in the
Coridan
system
. This planet was located in a
sector
of the
Beta Quadrant
and was approximately 28
light years
from
Sol
. This planet was the
homeworld
of the
Coridanites
, a
warp-capable
humanoid
species
. As of
2151
, the planetary
government
was headed by an elected
chancellor
.
The planet Coridan was rich in valuable resources, most significantly
dilithium
. (
TOS
: "
Journey to Babel
";
ENT
: "
Shadows of P'Jem
";
DS9
: "
One Little Ship
")
History
A 2151 map of the capital city of Coridan and its surrounding shanty towns
In 2151, Coridan had a
population
of three billion, with the majority living in several
cities
in the
equatorial
region. The
capital city
was surrounded by a
shanty town
, and there were almost as many
bio-signs
outside the city as there were within it. The planet was known for having the largest
shipyards
in its sector, building advanced
warp-capable vessels
, some of which were rumored to reach warp 7. However, Coridan was also embroiled in an internal conflict between the government, backed by the
Vulcan High Command
, and rebel forces, secretly supported by the
Andorian Empire
. The
Vulcan
agenda was to keep the planet's chancellorship stable while the
Tau Ceti Accords
prohibited any Andorian presence on the planet, which in turn assured Coridan's dilithium exports to
Vulcan
. (
ENT
: "
Shadows of P'Jem
")
Chancellor
Kalev
invited
Captain
Jonathan Archer
and
Sub-Commander
T'Pol
of
Enterprise
to visit the planet, but they were captured by rebels and held for
ransom
. As a result, a
commando
force led by
Sopek
, the Vulcan senior officer of the sector, as well as the
Andorian Imperial Guard
, led by
Shran
, who was thereby violating the Tau Ceti Accords, came to assist the rescue. (
ENT
: "
Shadows of P'Jem
")
A
year
later, in
2152
, Jonathan Archer, playing the role of a
smuggler
, claimed to have outrun a
customs vessel
near Coridan. (
ENT
: "
Canamar
")
In
2155
, Coridan took part in talks on
Earth
to form a
Coalition of Planets
. Here, the Coridanites rejected the
Tellarite
proposal of a
trade embargo
against the
Orion Syndicate
. The Coridan
representative
claimed his people had been trading with the
Orions
for
centuries
and that the accusation of Orions attacking
Tellarite freighters
was slander. (
ENT
: "
Demons
")
Over the next century, Coridan became under-populated to the point that it could not defend its dilithium
mines
.
In
2268
, Coridan sought admission to the
United Federation of Planets
. However, due to the wealth of minerals available on Coridan and the presence of illegal
mining
operations, Coridan's admission was a controversial subject between Tellarites and Vulcans. The
Babel Conference
was convened to settle the matter and ultimately approved Coridan's admission, although Orion smugglers initially tried to
sabotage
the meeting.
Ambassador
Sarek
of Vulcan was credited with achieving the consensus towards admitting the planet to the Federation. (
TOS
: "
Journey to Babel
";
TNG
: "
Sarek
")
Shortly before the start of the
Dominion War
,
Kandra Vilk
moved from
Deep Space 9
to a neutral world near Coridan. (
DS9
: "
In the Cards
")
During the
Dominion War
, Coridan's dilithium
mines
came under attack by the
Dominion
in
2374
, due to their strategic importance.
Gelnon
left
Kudak'Etan
in command of the
USS
Defiant
while he and
his ship
left to launch an attack on Coridan. (
DS9
: "
One Little Ship
")
In the
32nd century
, Coridan was a
Federation
member
planet. It was listed with other members on a video wall in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
Kids These Days
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet was within the enclosed region. (
SA
: "
300th Night
")
Stellar cartography
In
3196
, during the
Omega-47
crisis, the location of "Coridan (Chi 1 Orionis)" was depicted on star charts and readouts used across several locations on the
USS
Athena
to locate, monitor, and respond to the Omega-47 mines laid by the Venari Ral. (
SA
: "
300th Night
")
Appendices
Background information
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
episode "
300th Night
".
In the
script
for "Sarek", Coridan was listed in the pronunciation guide, where the term was pronounced "CORE-ah-don".
In the first draft script of "Shadows of P'Jem" (written while the episode had the
working title
"Untitled Andorians Return"), the capital city of Coridan was referred to, in dialogue, as "an architectural marvel" and, in the stage directions, as featuring "crystalline, high-rise
buildings
," which Archer speculated "must be a half-
kilometer
tall." The planet was spoken about as having "fabrication facilities" better than any on
Earth
. In the stage directions from the same early script, the planet was also described as having a "
cloud
layer" whose coloration wasn't identified, to be depicted on "a bright, sunlit
day
." However, in the final draft of the script, Coridan was instead described as having
gray
clouds and a "crystalline city," with the other descriptions no longer included in the script.
In a
deleted scene
from
Star Trek Into Darkness
, a news feed announced that
Magustav Pachangara
became the new chancellor of Coridan. Information on this planet appeared in a
powerwall
in the
office
of
Admiral
Alexander Marcus
.
[1]
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 53) and
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Federation Historical Highlights, 2161-2385"), Coridan (Coridan III) was classified as a
M-class
planet governed by the People's Republic of Coridan (
β
) from its seat at New Coridan (
β
). The dominant
species
were the humanoid Coridanites, who were warp-capable in
2093
. In the mid-
22nd century
, the planet had a population of three billion. By the mid-
23rd century
, a centuries-long
civil war
had reduced the population. In the late
24th century
, the planet had a population of 185 million.
The current location of Coridan in the Beta Quadrant was based on the most recent star charts seen in
Star Trek: Strange New Worlds
and
Star Trek: Section 31
.
Apocrypha
In
The Worlds of the Federation
, Coridan was the
third
planet in the Coridan system. It was called Desotriana by its natives.
In
FASA
's
RPG
sourcebook
The Federation
, Coridan was the
fourth
planet in the Danthos system.
In
Decipher
's RPG sourcebook
Worlds
, Coridan was the
fifth
planet in its system.
In the non-
canon
novel
The Good That Men Do
, Coridan was referred to as "Coridan Prime", on the verge of developing warp-7 technology. To prevent such advances from spreading to other Coalition of Planets member worlds, the
Romulans
launched a vicious attack on Coridan, flying a
vessel
into the planet at
maximum warp
.
Anti-matter
from the suicide attackers' ship mixed with Coridan's abundance of dilithium, causing a fireball that spread across the planet, killing billions. The planet's population was said to be further depleted by civil wars that continued after the Romulan attack.
In
Star Trek: The Original Series - Core Game Book
, Coridan was described as a cold, dry, class-M world, with a chronically corrupt government, in the 2260s.
External link
Coridan III
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
People's Republic of Coridan
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

.

======================================================================
PAGE: Crime scene
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Crime_scene
======================================================================

A
crime scene
was the location where a
crime
was committed. A summary of the conditions of a crime scene, called a
crime scene report
, was compiled after the area was examined. (
DS9
: "
The Darkness and the Light
")
For years, the story told about
Joran Belar
was that he was killed trying to escape the murder scene of the
doctor
that recommended dropping him as a
Trill initiate
. (
DS9
: "
Equilibrium
")
In
2367
,
Jean-Luc Picard
referred to the
T-Tauri system
as the scene of the crime (i.e. where
Data
was believed to have been compromised) when ordering the
USS
Enterprise
-D
to return there. (
TNG
: "
Clues
")
In
2369
,
Odo
noted that his being called to the scene of
Ibudan
's
murder
would explain why traces of his
DNA
were present. (
DS9
: "
A Man Alone
")
Also that year,
Aquiel Uhnari
's
blood
being found at
Keith Rocha
's murder scene led everyone to (falsely) assume she was dead, too. (
TNG
: "
Aquiel
")
In
2371
, after initially examining
Tolen Ren
's murder scene,
Tuvok
expressed his interest in inviting everyone concerned back there in two
hours
. (
VOY
: "
Ex Post Facto
")
Later that year,
Seska
framed
herself for her own crimes to point the finger at
Joe Carey
, making it appear that he was framing her. She did this by leaving evidence at the scene of the crime (inputting her own
security code
). (
VOY
: "
State of Flux
")
External link
Crime scene
at
Wikipedia

Crivo
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(

)

or

Crivo
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
. It was renowned as a
tourist
destination, featuring such attractions as the
Crivian Planetary Museum
, the
Glass Islands
, and the
Smoke Lakes
.
In
2259
,
Marie Batel
proposed that she and
Christopher Pike
take their first
vacation
as a
couple
on Crivo. Pike, however, thought that Crivo was too "
touristy
", and the two ultimately decided to go elsewhere. (
SNW
: "
Subspace Rhapsody
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.

Cybulski's Planet
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2293
)
Location:
Beta Quadrant
Cybulski's Planet
was a
namesake
planet
in the
Beta Quadrant
.
In
2293
, Cybulski's Planet's position was labeled in the
star chart
for the "
Operation: Retrieve
" command briefing. The chart was from the
Starfleet Command Intelligence Database
. This star chart was seen in the
office
of the
Federation President
on
Earth
. (
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
,
set artwork
)
This planet's quadrant of origin is inferred based on the position of neighboring locations –
Starbase 24
and
Rura Penthe
– as seen in the
star charts
appearing in the
Star Trek: Discovery
episode "
Magic to Make the Sanest Man Go Mad
" and in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
This chart was seen in greater resolution at threads at Flare.Solareclipse.net.
[1]
(X)
[2]
(X)
Many of these locations shared their names with the last names of crew who worked on the sixth film. This location was probably named after the assistant chief rigging electrician
John Cybulski
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Cygnet XIV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Cygnet_XIV
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Cygnet XIV
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
(
3195
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3195
)
Native species:
Cygnet IV natives
Location:
Cygnet
system
Alpha Quadrant
Cygnet XIV
was the
inhabited
fourteenth
planet
of the
Cygnet
system
. The planet was located in the
Alpha Quadrant
. The
native
society
of the planet was
dominated by women
.
In
2267
, the
USS
Enterprise
put in to Cygnet XIV for general repair and maintenance. During this overhaul, the planet's inhabitants, deciding that the ship's
computer
lacked sufficient
personality
, modified its
programming
to give it a
female
one. This caused some annoyance to the
crew
, especially
Captain
Kirk
, whom the
computer voice
often affectionately addressed as "dear", and Mr.
Spock
, who noted that the computer had "an unfortunate tendency to giggle."
Spock explained the computer's odd behavior to
John Christopher
when the
pilot
briefly visited the
Enterprise
in
1969
and witnessed its amorous interactions with Kirk. (
TOS
: "
Tomorrow is Yesterday
")
In the
32nd century
, Cygnet XIV was a
Federation
member
planet. It was listed with other members on a video wall in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
Kids These Days
")
Appendices
Background information
The planet's location was inferred from the position of its system as seen in the star chart appearing in the
Star Trek: Strange New Worlds
episode "
Strange New Worlds
".
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I", "United Federation of Planets III") and
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Federation Historical Highlights,
2161-2385
"), Cygnet XIV was listed as a Federation member in
2378
.
Apocrypha
The
novels
Beneath the Raptor's Wing
and
Synthesis
suggested that this planet was related to
Omega Cygni
.

Cygnet XIV
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

PAGE: Cygnia Minor
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Cygnia_Minor
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Cygnia Minor
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
(
3195
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3195
)
Location:
Alpha Quadrant
Cygnia Minor
was an
inhabited
minor
planet
in its
star system
. This planet was located in the
Alpha Quadrant
. In the
23rd century
, the planet was home to an
Earth colony
.
In
2266
, this
colony
was facing the threat of
famine
.
Doctor
Thomas Leighton
, a
scientist
living in a colony on the nearby
Planet Q
, sent a signal to the
USS
Enterprise
, claiming to have discovered an extraordinary new synthetic
food
which would end the crisis on Cygnia Minor. It was soon discovered, however, that Leighton had lied about the discovery in order to lure the
Enterprise
to Planet Q. He had hoped to convince
Captain
Kirk
that he had found
Kodos the Executioner
. (
TOS
: "
The Conscience of the King
")
In the
32nd century
, Cygnia Minor was a
Federation
member
planet. It was listed with other members on a video wall in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
Kids These Days
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I", "United Federation of Planets II") Cygnia Minor was listed as a
Federation member
in
2378
. This planet was located in the
Albireo
(
Beta Cygni
)
star system
. The
primary
of this system was classified as a
K-class star
.
External link
Albireo II
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

PAGE: Cytrax

Cytrax
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
24th century
)
Satellite(s):
5 moons
Location:
Delta Quadrant
Cytrax
was a
planet
in the
Delta Quadrant
with at least
five moons
After
biomimetic copies
of
Tom Paris
and
B'Elanna Torres
were
married
aboard the duplicate
USS
Voyager
in
2375
,
Neelix
suggested a
holoprogram
of a
mountain
resort
on the fifth moon of Cytrax for their
honeymoon
. The calls of the native
Cytraxian crickets
there were reputed to be an auditory
aphrodisiac
. (
VOY
: "
Course: Oblivion
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: D'Arsay archive
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/D'Arsay_archive
======================================================================

D'Arsay archive
Type:
Archive
Affiliation:
D'Arsay
Status:
Active
(
2370
)
A
D'Arsay archive
was a data-storage facility launched into
space
by the
extinct
D'Arsay
civilization
.
One such archive was launched over 87 million
years
ago. Made of
fortanium
and other minerals, it contained records of
artifacts
and thousands of personalities from
myths
within the D'Arsay culture. It had the capability of scanning foreign
computer
systems and imparting its data while re-programming it to exhibit its records. It also had a
tractor beam
.
After launch, it spent 87 million years drifting in space for over two sectors and eventually was located in
sector 1156
. It had accumulated minerals over time, completely obscuring its structure and making it appear as a
comet
.
The
USS
Enterprise
-D
discovered the comet in
2370
. After
scanning
the
starship
, it began to use the
replicators
on board to change parts of the vessel into different mythical settings contained within the records in the archive. These settings were from a drama involving
Masaka
, a
sun
goddess
, and involved locations such as an
aqueduct
, a
temple
, and a
swamp
.
The archive also altered
Data
's programming, giving him personalities of key mythical figures, such as a terrified D'Arsay boy, a person named
Ihat
, an elderly man who portrayed Masaka's
father
, a victim of Masaka, and Masaka herself. Furthermore, the program turned the internal mechanism of a
photon torpedo
into
snakes
and turned
engineering
into an inferno.
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
sought to remedy this by playing along in the myth, using the persona of
Korgano
, another mythical figure, to persuade Masaka, and in turn the archive, to restore Data as well as the
Enterprise
-D to their normal states.
Following the incident, the archive was placed under study by a
Starfleet
archaeological
team. (
TNG
: "
Masks
")
The
USS
Cerritos
encountered several D'Arsay archives. On the third such occasion in
2381
,
Captain
Carol Freeman
became possessed by the
spirit
of
Minooki
and partially transformed the ship into a
temple
. After being freed, Freeman noted in her
captain's log
that
Starfleet
needed a better way of handling D'Arsay archive situations. (
LD
: "
Room for Growth
")
By
2401
, data from a D'Arsay archive was kept in storage among
Section 31
's high-security artifacts at
Daystrom Station
. (
PIC
: "
The Bounty
")
Appendices
Background information
According to science consultant
André Bormanis
, the explanation given in the original script was that the archive was an "advanced
Genesis Device
" that mistook the
Enterprise
for a world on which to recreate the ancient society. (
Star Trek: The Next Generation Companion
, 2nd ed., p. 285)
Studio model
Curry's mock-up model (upside down from the finished product)
SBS' CGI model in various build-up stages...
...as ultimately featured
The archive was one of the first
CGI models
, but not the first – as there were digital starship models previously constructed for evaluation purposes (
see:
here
) – , of a space-bound artificial structure in the
Star Trek
franchise
. It was however, the first one not realized as a physical
studio model
, but rather directly as a fully textured CGI model, and as such, the very first one actually seen by the general
Star Trek
audience. Based upon a foot-high sculpture
Dan Curry
made,
Santa Barbara Studios
(SBS) constructed a CGI model. "
I considered doing a drawing of the library but finally opted for a 3D approach that allowed me to determine how it would look from different angles. The art director
Richard James
wanted the architecture to have a hint of Mayan look so I created that by sculpting it from wooden blocks that I cut together in the shop of
Image G
.
Ron Moore
flew it up to Santa Barbara Studios and they translated it into the CGI model of the library that is revealed when the comet starts to melt.
", Curry revealed. (
Cinefantastique
, Vol. 25, No.6/Vol. 26, p. 58) The studio also provided the comet and "steam" effects in the episode when the archive was freed from its icy enclosure by the
Enterprise
-D's phaser fire. Curry has retained the mock-up sculpture as part of his personal collection. (
TNG Season 6 DVD
-special feature, "Departmental Briefing, Year Six:
Dan Curry Profile")
When the
remastered
version of the episode came along in
2014
, it turned out that the computer files for the archive most likely did not exist anymore, partly because SBS – who had constructed their model in their own proprietary
Dynamation software
– was no longer in existence, partly because it was discovered that SBS had not been in the habit to archive their digital models.
[1]
The visual effects company responsible for the remastering project,
CBS Digital
, was left with no choice other than to faithfully recreate the structure in their own software,
LightWave 3D
, as featured at the top of this entry.
[2]
External link
D'Arsay archive
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Daimanta
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Daimanta
======================================================================

Daimanta
was a place located in the
Qiris sector
, a region of
space
in the
Beta Quadrant
.
Former
Borg
and
Starfleet
science officer
Icheb
, on leave from the
USS
Coleman
, was performing a reconnaissance mission near Daimanta on behalf of the
Fenris Rangers
when he was lured into an ambush by
Bjayzl
. Subsequently, he ended up on
Vergessen
, where his Borg implants were stripped out. (
PIC
: "
Stardust City Rag
")
In
2399
, petty
warlord
Kar Kantar
was known to be "
running wild
" from
Vashti
to Daimanta in an
antique
Bird-of-Prey
. (
PIC
: "
Absolute Candor
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Dakala
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Dakala
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Dakala
Class:
M
Type:
Rogue planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
(
3196
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Native species:
Bore worms
Drayjins
Fire wolves
Wraiths
Location:
Interstellar space
Alpha Quadrant
Dakala
was a small
inhabited
Class M
rogue planet
in the
Alpha Quadrant
. The planet was thought to have broken out of its
orbit
and, upon leaving its
star system
, began to travel through
interstellar space
. Due to this, the planet existed in a state of perpetual
night
.
History
When the
NX-class
starship
Enterprise
NX-01
visited Dakala in
2151
, the
Starfleet
crew
detected a diverse
animal
population
native to the
planet
. Although
Captain
Jonathan Archer
,
Enterprise
's
commanding officer
, expected the planet's
surface
to be frozen solid, his crew found that hot
gases
were venting from Dakala's interior and keeping the planet warm. Most of Dakala's
lifeforms
were concentrated in the areas where these vents could be found.
Sub-Commander
T'Pol
, the starship's
science officer
, detected no
evidence
of
humanoid
life on Dakala.
Strangely, a
power signature
near the planet's
equator
indicated the presence of a
vessel
. When
Enterprise
attempted to contact the detected ship, there was no response. Shortly thereafter, an
away team
from
Enterprise
traveled to Dakala in a
shuttlepod
. The group experienced difficulty using
infrared
to scan the ship, as the
heat
generated by the planet's thermal vents was too intense. As the shuttlepod arrived high above its destination, T'Pol attempted to scan for a hole in the canopy which surrounded the planet. Ultimately, it turned out that the planet concealed an
Eska
hunting party
. The Eska had been using Dakala as a hunting reserve for nine
generations
. They were allowed to hunt there only four
days
a
year
.
A Dakalan bug
The primal volcanic vents were similar to
Yellowstone National Park
, according to Archer, and reminded T'Pol of the
grottoes
of
Deneva Prime
. The planet was covered by
forest
similar to the
rain forests
of
New Zealand
on
Earth
. Indigenous lifeforms included
bore worms
,
drayjins
,
fire wolves
, some
species
of large
reptiles
, and a species of
bioluminescent
"
bug
". The planet had no indigenous humanoid life, but it had some higher
primates
which the Eska did not
hunt
. However, the Eska did hunt for several species such as
drayjin
, and most notably they hunted a
shapeshifting
intelligent species called the
wraiths
. The Eska did not think the wraiths were intelligent, but
Enterprise
discovered they were and helped them avoid being detected by the Eska. (
ENT
: "
Rogue Planet
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planetoid was within the enclosed region. (
SA
: "
300th Night
", "
Rubincon
")
Stellar cartography
In
3195
, this planet's position was labeled on a
Federation
star chart
which was displayed in
Starfleet Academy
's
Stellar Cartography
laboratory
. (
SA
: "
Beta Test
")
In
3196
, during the
Omega-47
crisis, the location of Dakala was depicted on star charts and readouts used across several locations on the
USS
Athena
to locate, monitor, and respond to the Omega-47 mines laid by the Venari Ral. (
SA
: "
300th Night
", "
Rubincon
")
A set on
Paramount Stage 9
was used for the forest-covered surface of Dakala. (
Star Trek: Communicator
issue 138
, p. 63)
Both the
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 60) and
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Federation Historical Highlights, 2161-2385") stated that the rogue planet was discovered by the
Enterprise
in the Alpha Quadrant.
It is unclear how plant life could survive on Dakala. One scene, however, shows the surface covered in the dim light of a nearby star, perhaps supplying enough energy for some form of
photosynthesis
. Another theory, from the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 177, is that the plant and animal life was supported by the surface heat from volcanic vents. More specifically, the authors of this
reference work
hypothesized that the plants lived on a form of
chemosynthesis
(using chemicals in volcanic gases) or a form of
thermosynthesis
(using geothermal heat). The same source also classified Dakala as a
class M
planet.
External link
Dakala
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Daled IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Daled_IV
======================================================================

Daled IV
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Non-aligned
Status:
Habitable
(
2365
)
Satellite(s):
At least
three moons
Native species:
Allasomorphs
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Daled IV
was the
inhabited
fourth
planet
of its
star system
. This planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
and was the
homeworld
for a
species
of
shapeshifters
known as the
allasomorphs
. An important facility on the planet was
Command Headquarters
.
Daled IV had an almost identical
atmosphere
to
Klavdia III
. The
troposphere
of the planet distorted normal communications signals and required a lot of
energy
to penetrate. The environment was hostile to
humanoid
lifeforms
. Daled IV had at least three
moons
, and its third
moon
was inhabited and similar in appearance to
Earth
's moon
Luna
.
The planet
rotated only once per a revolution
around its
primary
, meaning on one side of the planet it was constantly light, and on the other side it was constantly night. Disparate
cultures
evolved in each
hemisphere
which resulted in a
civil war
lasting for the entirety of their recorded
history
. (
TNG
: "
The Dauphin
")
The planet model for Daled IV was first used for Klavdia III. ("
The Dauphin
") It was later used for
Tau Cygna V
, ("
The Ensigns of Command
")
Moab IV
, ("
The Masterpiece Society
") and
Devidia II
. ("
Time's Arrow
")
External links
Daled
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Daled IV
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Re-Used Planets in TNG
at
Ex Astris Scientia

Federation

.

2374
,
a Federation Magistrate
suggested that an
adult
Molly O'Brien
(older after returning through a
time portal
on
Golana

Tarkalean
in
Quark's
. (

======================================================================
PAGE: Dannus V
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Dannus_V
======================================================================

Dannus V
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Diameter:
13,983
kilometers
Status:
Habitable
(
3189
)
Satellite(s):
One
moon
,
ring system
Location:
In
orbit
of
Dannus
Alpha Quadrant
, near
Gamma Quadrant
and
galactic
rim
Dannus V
was the
uninhabited
fifth
planet
orbiting
the
primary
Dannus
. This planet was located in the
Alpha Quadrant
. It was near the
galactic
rim
and just shy of the
Gamma Quadrant
, at
coordinates
011-8308211.4302, 011-8437857.6.
Planetary data
Dannus V had a single
moon
and a
ring system
. Its
rotation period
was 31.4
hours
and its
orbital period
was 470
days
. It had a
diameter
of 13,983
kilometers
and a
gravity
of 9.834
meters
/
second
2
. Categorized as
class M
, the planet featured a
forested
,
snowy
landscape with an average
temperature
of 0.4
degrees
Celsius
.
History
In
3189
, the
USS
Discovery
jumped
to Dannus V after its
computer
had calculated a 5% chance of survival for
Philippa  Georgiou
if she were brought to the planet. On the surface, Georgiou and
Commander
Michael Burnham
encountered an enigmatic
entity
named
Carl
, who offered Georgiou a way to shift into her past in the
mirror universe
. (
DIS
: "
Terra Firma, Part 1
")
Following Georgiou's return from the past in the mirror universe, Carl revealed that he was the
Guardian of Forever
and during the
Temporal Wars
had relocated from
his original home
to Dannus V in order to hide from the various factions attempting to use him to travel through time. With Georgiou having passed his test, Carl sent her back to a time when the prime and mirror universes were still aligned, a place where she could survive. (
DIS
: "
Terra Firma, Part 2
")

". This planet was located somewhere in the

======================================================================
PAGE: Dantang
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Dantang
======================================================================

Dantang as mentioned in Danar's file
Dantang
was a location.
During the
Tarsian War
, the
Robmeybur Rebellion
began in Dantang and spread out.
In
2366
, this location was mentioned in
Roga Danar
's military service records, which were accessible to the crew of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
via the
Angosian senatorial database
. (
TNG-R
: "
The Hunted
",
okudagram
)
This location was only mentioned in writing.
Dantang is named after
Angelo Dante
, a production assistant at
CBS Home Entertainment
, who worked on the remastered
TNG
project.

)

or
Beta Quadrant

was the

second
planet
in its

. This planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
In
April
2323
, the
Starfleet Academy marathon
was held on Danula II. It was a forty
kilometer
race, and the winner was

Cadet

. He was the first freshman to win the race. (

. (

,

)

PAGE: Daran V

Daran V
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2376
)
Native species:
Daran V natives
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Daran V
was the
inhabited
fifth
planet
in its
star system
. This
Class M
planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
. This planet, as of
2268
, had a
population
of 3.724 billion
individuals
.
History
In 2268, the crew of the
USS
Enterprise
discovered the ten thousand-
year
-old
Fabrini
asteroid
vessel
, the
Yonada
, 396
days
away from Daran V. The vessel had veered off
course
and was about to impact Daran V, killing all of the planet's population along with those onboard. Fortunately, the
Yonada
's course was altered away from Daran V by the actions of the crew of the
Enterprise
. (
TOS
: "
For the World is Hollow and I Have Touched the Sky
")
Stellar cartography
In
2293
, the
location
of Daran V in the
Milky Way Galaxy
was labeled in a
star chart
that was in
Captain
James T. Kirk
's
quarters
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-A
. (
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
,
okudagram
)
In
2365
, the location of Daran V was labeled in a Subspace Comm Net Ops star chart, which was seen in the
courtroom
on
Starbase 173
. This planet was connected to
Aldebaran
and an unnamed location by subspace lines. (
TNG
: "
The Measure Of A Man
")
In
2371
, the location of Daran V was labeled in a star chart
Data
and
Picard
were studying in
stellar cartography
on the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. (
Star Trek Generations
,
okudagram
)
In
2376
, the location of Daran V was labeled in a star chart in
astrometrics
on the
USS
Voyager
. (
VOY
: "
Child's Play
",
okudagram
)
The location of Daran V in "The Explored Galaxy" wall chart
Daran V in relation to
Dimorus
,
Kaldra IV
,
Nimbus III
, and
Peliar Zel
Appendices
Background information
StarTrek.com
classified Daran V as an
class M
Federation
planet.
[1]
(X)
In August 2024, an annotated computer paste-up of the star chart from "The Measure Of A Man" was put up for auction in the Entertainment Memorabiliia Live Auction: Los Angeles Summer 2024 by Propstore Auction.
[2]
Apocrypha
The
novel
Ex Machina
revealed that Daran V was home to a
pterodactyl
-like
race
called the Shesshran (
β
). They had
silver
skin
, a long sharp
beak
,
bat
-like
wings
and a
saurian
tail
. The Shesshran called their home planet Kachissat.

at

, the wiki for licensed

======================================================================
PAGE: Davlos III
CATEGORIES: Planets

======================================================================

Davlos III
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2371
)
Location:
On the
Klingon border
Davlos
system
Beta Quadrant
~116.9
light years
from
Sol
Davlos III
was the
inhabited
third
planet
in the
Davlos
system
. The planet was located in the
Beta Quadrant
and was approximately 116.9
light years
from
Sol
. The planet was found on the
Klingon border
, and as such, ninety
percent
of its
trade
was with the
Klingon Empire
.
When
Klingon Intelligence
sent a covert
strike force
to
Deep Space 9
to conduct surveillance operations on a group of
Romulans
visiting the station, they used a
device
manufactured on this planet to turn a
replicator
into a
transporter
for beaming surveillance equipment in the Romulan
quarters
. (
DS9
: "
Visionary
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
"DAV-los" was the pronunciation for this planet's
name
from the
script
pronunciation guide.
[1]
According to
StarTrek.com
, Davlos III was an independent planet.
[2]
(X)
External link
Davlos III
at

, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek

======================================================================
PAGE: Dayos IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Dayos_IV
======================================================================

Dayos IV
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2374
)
Location:
Dayos
system
Beta Quadrant
Dayos IV
was the
inhabited
fourth
planet
in the
Dayos
system
. This planet was located in the
Beta Quadrant
.
History
In
2260
, Dayos was within the area of effect of a
quantum uncertainty field
. (
SNW
: "
Subspace Rhapsody
")
In
2363
, the planet was the home of a
former wife
to
the Albino
when she was discovered by
Kang
. She was still deathly afraid of her ex-husband and refused to reveal his whereabouts to the vengeful
Klingon
. In
2370
, she
died
, but not before dispatching her
amulet
to Kang disclosing the
location
of the Albino's sanctuary in the
Secarus system
. (
DS9
: "
Blood Oath
")
Miles O'Brien
's
Starfleet Intelligence
created
alias
of
Connelly
, a down on his luck fix-it man, got two
years
for dealing in
stolen
goods
on this planet.
Liam Bilby
sympathized with "Connelly" while discussing his past, and regarded the incident as a "tough break." (
DS9
: "
Honor Among Thieves
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
This planet's quadrant of origin is inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Discovery
episode "
An Obol for Charon
".
"DA-yos" was the pronunciation for this planet's
name
from the
script
pronunciation guide for "Blood Oath".
External link
Dayos IV
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Daystrom Station
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Daystrom_Station
======================================================================

Daystrom Station
Type:
Offsite station
Owner:
United Federation of Planets
Operator:
Daystrom Institute
Section 31
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Status:
Active
(
2401
)
Daystrom Station
was a
Federation
space station
that served as a high-security
black site
of the
Daystrom Institute
during the late
24th
and early
25th centuries
.
History
During the
Dominion War
, Daystrom Station was the location of
Project Proteus
, a top secret endeavor that conducted
torturous
experiments on
Changeling
prisoners of war
in an effort to create perfect infiltrators. The project ended when the main Changeling subject, later known as
Vadic
, killed the lead
scientist
and escaped with her brethren. (
PIC
: "
Dominion
")
In
2399
,
Doctor
Altan Soong
sent
Jean-Luc Picard
's original
organic
body to Daystrom Station, as he suspected that the
brain
abnormality that had caused that body's
death
was in fact not
Irumodic Syndrome
but something unprecedented. (
PIC
: "
Vox
")
Schematic of the Daystrom Station vault
In
2401
, Vadic returned to Daystrom Station with "
Titus Rikka
" and other renegade Changelings swayed to her cause. They broke into the station's
vault
and stole experimental
portal weapons
. To gain access, they hired the
Vulcan
criminal
Krinn
, who brokered for a
key
that could exploit the "illogical" flaws in the station's
AI
security system. Afterwards, the station was left mostly unmanned, though it was
patrolled
by
Starfleet Security
every hour. (
PIC
: "
Seventeen Seconds
", "
Imposters
", "
The Bounty
")
Starfleet Intelligence
commenced
Operation: Daybreak
to investigate the theft, for which they recruited
Worf
as a
subcontractor
. Worf acted as the
handler
for
Commander
Raffi Musiker
, who spent months undercover in
District Six
on
M'talas Prime
. They found that the theft of the portal devices, and its use in the subsequent
terrorist attack
on a
Starfleet
recruitment center, were in fact diversions from something else the Changelings took from the station.
With Starfleet compromised by Changelings, Worf and Musiker were denied access to Daystrom Station to continue their investigation. Thus, they tracked down Krinn to obtain his key that would allow them to break into the station themselves. They were then picked up by the
USS
Titan
-A
, which delivered them and Captain
William T. Riker
to the station. However, the
Titan
's arrival triggered an alert, and it was forced to withdraw by the
USS
Sternbach
and
USS
Cole
.
After encountering
holograms
of a
crow
and
Professor
James Moriarty
, along with
musical notes
from "
Pop Goes the Weasel
", the
away team
realized that the station's security AI was attempting to communicate with them. This led to the discovery that the AI was
Daystrom Android M-5-10
, a repurposed
golem
originally created by Doctor
Altan Soong
as a synthesis of the memories and personalities of previous
Soong-type androids
. With Starfleet Security personnel closing in, Riker remained behind to hold them at bay while Worf, Musiker, and M-5-10 were extracted to the
Titan
. Once rebooted, M-5-10 informed them that the Changelings' true target had been Picard's original body. Meanwhile, Riker was taken to the
Shrike
by Vadic. (
PIC
: "
The Next Generation
", "
Seventeen Seconds
", "
The Bounty
")
Technical data
Daystrom Android M-5-10 connected within the station's primary vault
Daystrom Station housed
Starfleet
's "most off-the-books" technology, experimental
weapons
, and
alien
contraband
. These included some of
Section 31
's "most nefarious table scraps".
The station was guarded by Daystrom Android M-5-10, connected to the disassembled parts of
B-4
; this highly lethal and adaptive system was installed after the Federation's ban on
synthetics
was lifted in
2399
. This system could recognize specific individuals and devise custom security responses, using
holo-projectors
installed throughout the facility. The station was also equipped with
transport inhibitors
to prevent unauthorized beaming. (
PIC
: "
The Bounty
")
Known inventory
Arretan android
"
Attack tribble
"
Borg Queen
remains
Borg
vinculum
D'Arsay archive
Exo III
android duplicator
Genesis II Device
Kemocite
James T. Kirk
's body
Jean-Luc Picard
's original body
Kataan probe
Koinonian
explosive device
Memory virus
Oscillation overthruster
Portal weapons
"
Pup
"
probe
Probability device
Self-sealing stem bolt
Thalaron generator
Personnel
Daystrom Station personnel
Appendices
Appearances
PIC
:
"
Imposters
"
"
The Bounty
"
"
Dominion
"
Background information
The station was designed and built as an approval
CGI model
by
Doug Drexler
, who incorporated structures resembling those of
Jupiter Station
, the
MIDAS array
, the
Epsilon IX station
, and
Y class
cargo modules. After Drexler's model was given the go-ahead by showrunner
Terry Matalas
and Production Designer
Dave Blass
, it was sent to the
VFX department
to receive further detailing and refinements, as well as to prepare it for use as a live-action production asset.
[1]
In addition to those visible onscreen, storage label graphics were also produced for an
Arretan energy sphere
, an
Echo Papa drone
, the
Harvester
virus, the
M-5 multitronic unit
, the
morphogenic virus
, and
sporocystian lifeform
remains. (
TRR
: "
The Bounty
")
According to production art, the station measures 8,924 feet (2,720 meters) across its longer axis.
[2]

Decos Prime
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Habitable
(
2375
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Decos Prime
was the
inhabited
prime
planet
in its
star system
. This planet was located in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
. This
Federation
planet was the site of a
Starfleet
base, located less than a day and a half's journey from
Deep Space 9
.
In
2375
, the Starfleet
Chief of Operations
at the base was
Al Lorenzo
. Lorenzo borrowed and returned
Sisko
's desk in the space of three days and agreed to provide an
induction modulator
as payment, in a series of trades organized by
Nog
to accelerate repairs of the
USS
Defiant
. (
DS9
: "
Treachery, Faith and the Great River
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.

Dedestris
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2371
)
Location:
Delta Quadrant
Material imported from Dedestris
Dedestris
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Delta Quadrant
.
Sikaris imported a delicate
fabric
from Dedestris spun from the petals of a
flower
that only bloomed in
moonlight
.
In
2371
, Sikarian
Magistrate
Gathorel Labin
offered a
scarf
made of the fabric to
Captain
Kathryn Janeway
of the
USS
Voyager
. (
VOY
: "
Prime Factors
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to both
StarTrek.com
and the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 193, this planet was located in the
Delta Quadrant
.
[1]

======================================================================
PAGE: Deep Space 11
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Deep_Space_11
======================================================================

Deep Space 11
Owner:
United Federation of Planets
Operator:
Starfleet
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Status:
Active
(
2401
)
Deep Space 11
, informally called
DS11
, was a
Federation
deep space
station
in service with
Starfleet
during the early
25th century
.
In
2401
, while attempting to talk
Captain
Liam Shaw
into diverting the
USS
Titan
to the
Ryton system
,
Admiral
Jean-Luc Picard
told him that afterward the
Titan
would undertake a final engineering inspection at
Deep Space 4
. When Shaw pointed out that DS4 had been shut down for a year,
Commander
Seven of Nine
tried to cover for Picard by claiming that he actually meant DS11, but Shaw was not fooled. (
PIC
: "
The Next Generation
")
This station was only mentioned in dialogue.
Its full designation was not spoken, but was extrapolated from the context of the characters' conversation.

PAGE: Deep Space 12

======================================================================

Deep Space 12
Owner:
United Federation of Planets
Operator:
Starfleet
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Status:
Active
(
2399
)
Deep Space 12
was a
Federation
starbase
that was in service with
Starfleet
in the late
24th century
.
In
2399
,
Jean-Luc Picard
had
Captain
Cristóbal Rios
set
La Sirena
on a course for Deep Space 12 to
rendezvous
with a Starfleet
squadron
. At that time, DS12 was the closest Federation starbase to
Nepenthe
. (
PIC
: "
Broken Pieces
")
This starbase was only mentioned in dialogue.
For the
Star Trek: Prodigy
episode "
Let Sleeping Borg Lie
", UI graphic designer
Dominique Rossier
created an info panel for the
USS
Dauntless
mess hall
depicting the
Milky Way Galaxy
with planet and
Starfleet
space station
locations labeled. Identified as a space station, Deep Space 12 was one of the locations mentioned on the chart. Although the panel did not appear in the episode, it can be seen at Rossier's artist page at Art Station.
[1]

Deep Space 12
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek

======================================================================
PAGE: Deep Space 2
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Deep_Space_2
======================================================================

Deep Space 2
Type:
Starbase
Owner:
United Federation of Planets
Operator:
Starfleet
Location:
Beta Quadrant
Status:
Active
(
2324
)
Deep Space 2
, abbreviated as
DS2
, was a
Federation
deep space
station
that was in service with
Starfleet
in the mid-
23rd century
.
History
In
2260
, this station was within the area of effect of a
quantum uncertainty field
. (
SNW
: "
Subspace Rhapsody
")
Stellar cartography
In
2259
, the location of this station, relatively close to the
Galdonterre system
and the
Cestus system
, was labeled on a Federation
star chart
. (
SNW
: "
The Broken Circle
")
In
2260
, Deep Space 2 was labeled on a
star chart
denoting the spread of a quantum uncertainty field across the region. (
SNW
: "
Subspace Rhapsody
")
Later that year, the location of this station was shown on a star chart depicting the expansion of the
Gorn Hegemony
. It was near the demarcation lines between
Gorn space
and
Federation space
. (
SNW
: "
Hegemony, Part II
")
In
2261
, the location of this station was depicted on a star chart used by the crew of the
USS
Enterprise
to highlight the location of
Vadia
. (
SNW
: "
Through the Lens of Time
")
In the early
24th century
, the location of this station was labeled on a star chart used during a
Section 31
Alpha Team
briefing
. (
Star Trek: Section 31
)
This deep space station was only mentioned in writing.
External link
Deep Space 2
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

Owner:
United Federation of Planets
Operator:
Starfleet

)
Deep Space 23
was a
Federation
starbase
that was in service with
Starfleet
in the late
24th century
.
In
2384
,
Deep Space 23
was providing medical assistance and was the nearest starbase to
USS
Voyager
-A
and
USS

. This information appeared on the
pilot desk
and

aboard
Voyager

: "

======================================================================
PAGE: Deep Space 253
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Deep_Space_253
======================================================================

Deep Space 253
Owner:
United Federation of Planets
Operator:
Starfleet
Status:
Active
(
3189
)
Deep Space 253
was a
Federation
space station
located in a remote region of space.
Following
the Burn
in the late
31st century
, DS253 was functionally cut off from the remnants of the Federation and had not received a visit from
Federation Headquarters
since
3102
. However, it had been trading with an
Emerald Chain
mercantile exchange
since the
3090s
.
In
3189
,
Minister
Osyraa
proposed to
Fleet Admiral
Charles Vance
that the Federation sanction the Emerald Chain's trade with DS253 and allow them to establish an
embassy
on the station, thus signalling that they regarded the Chain's
culture
to be fundamentally valid and ethical. (
DIS
: "
There Is A Tide...
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Deep Space 3
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Deep_Space_3
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Deep Space 3
Type:
Starbase
Owner:
United Federation of Planets
Operator:
Starfleet
Location:
Alpha Quadrant
Status:
Active
(
3196
)
Deep Space 3
, abbreviated as
DS3
, was a
Federation
deep space
station
that was in service with
Starfleet
.
History
In
2370
, the
commanding officer
of this station was
Vice Admiral
Marcus Holt
.
In that
year
, the station hosted the annual
palio
, wherein the
Ferengi
were accused of trying to bribe the
Breen
pilot
into throwing the race.
Nine
days
before Holt contacted
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
, the
USS
Hera
, commanded by
Captain
Silva La Forge
, departed Deep Space 3 on a routine
courier mission
. For five days, the station was in contact with the
starship
. After the ship vanished without a trace, Holt recruited the
USS
Nobel
and the
USS
Excelsior
for the task of finding the lost
Hera
. For seventy-two
hours
, the ships retraced the
course
of the
Hera
, without success. Holt planned on having the ships continue the search for another seventy-two hours; however, he did not think the additional time given to the ships would make a difference. (
TNG
: "
Interface
")
On one occasion, the
Zebulon Sisters
performed the
Chu Chu dance
at Deep Space 3, an event
Beckett Mariner
attended. She spoke of it in mid-
2380
, when she said she was just then coming down from the high. (
LD
: "
Terminal Provocations
")
Stellar cartography
On
stardate
1292.4, the location of this station was labeled in a chart used during a
Section 31
Alpha Team
briefing
. (
Star Trek: Section 31
)
In
3191
, this base's location in the
Alpha Quadrant
was labeled on a
star chart
. (
DIS
: "
Red Directive
")
In
3195
, this station's position was labeled on a
Federation
star chart
which was displayed on the
bridge
of the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
Come, Let's Away
")
In
3196
, this station's position was labeled on a Federation star chart that displayed the placement of
Omega-47 mines
by the
Venari Ral
around the center of the Federation in the Alpha and
Beta Quadrants
. This station was located outside this region. (
SA
: "
300th Night
")
Appendices
Background information
Deep Space 3 was mentioned in
Max Grodénchik
's "
Rom's Song
", as the station that "Rom" thought
Odo
ought to transfer to.
Apocrypha
The station appears in
War Dragons
and was also referenced in the
novel
A Singular Destiny
as well as in the
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine - Core Game Book
.
External link
Deep Space 3
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Deep Space 4
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Deep_Space_4
======================================================================

Deep Space 4
Owner:
United Federation of Planets
Operator:
Starfleet
Location:
Beta Quadrant
Status:
Decommissioned
(
2400
)
Deep Space 4
, abbreviated as
DS4
, was a
Federation
deep space
station
in service with
Starfleet
during the
24th century
. It was located in the
Beta Quadrant
. (
PIC
: "
Disengage
")
For his investigation of the
ancient humanoids
'
DNA
puzzle
in
2369
,
Professor
Galen
planned to travel from the
USS
Enterprise
-D
to Deep Space 4 on a
Vulcan transport
, and from there gain passage to
Caere
on an
Al-Leyan transport
. (
TNG
: "
The Chase
")
Captain
Kathryn Janeway
on the
USS
Voyager
, while searching for information on
Seven of Nine
in
2374
, could only find a single entry on the
Hansen family
, in DS4's
database
. (
VOY
: "
The Gift
")
Deep Space 4 was shut down in
2400
. Unaware of this, the
following year
Admiral
Jean-Luc Picard
claimed to
Captain
Liam Shaw
of the
USS
Titan
-A
that their proposed, unscheduled excursion to the
Ryton system
would be followed by a final engineering inspection at DS4. This confirmed Shaw's suspicion that he was being deceived. (
PIC
: "
The Next Generation
")
In 2401, Deep Space 4's location was labeled on a
star chart
used aboard the
SS
Eleos XII
. (
PIC
: "
Disengage
")
Appendices
Background information
In "The Chase", the location of Deep Space 4 was pointed out by Richard Galen from a star chart of the
Milky Way Galaxy
.
In the episode "Disengage", the location of this deep space station was seen on the bridge of the
SS
Eleos XII
on what appears to be an updated chart from
Star Trek: Star Charts
.
Dave Blass
published pictures of the set on
his website
.
Apocrypha
Deep Space 4 was also mentioned in the
novels
A Singular Destiny
and
Watching the Clock
.
External link
Deep Space 4
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================

CATEGORIES: Space stations

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Deep Space 5
Type:
Regula I
-type
Owner:
United Federation of Planets
Operator:
Starfleet
Location:
Beta Quadrant
Status:
Active
(
3196
)
Deep Space 5
, abbreviated as
DS5
, was a
Federation
Regula I
-type
deep space
station
that was in service with
Starfleet
in the late
24th century
and early
25th century
. DS5 was located near the
Ivor
star system
, which was home to a
Federation
colony
on its
planet
Ivor Prime
. (
Star Trek: First Contact
;
PIC
: "
Disengage
")
HIstory
In an alternate
quantum reality
visited by
Worf
in
2370
, Deep Space 5 was responsible for new
starship
developments and the object of covert surveillance by the
Cardassians
, along with
Starbase 47
, the
Iadara colony
, and the
Utopia Planitia Fleet Yards
.
(
TNG
: "
Parallels
")
In
2373
, Deep Space 5 reported the complete destruction of the
colony
on Ivor Prime. It was subsequently determined that the
Borg
were responsible and were mounting a new attempt at an
invasion
of the Federation. (
Star Trek: First Contact
)
Stellar cartography
In the early
24th century
, the location of this station was labeled in a chart used during a
Section 31
Alpha Team
briefing
. (
Star Trek: Section 31
)
In
3191
, this base's location in the
Alpha Quadrant
was labeled on a
star chart
. (
DIS
: "
Red Directive
")
In
3195
, this base's position was labeled on a
Federation
star chart
which was displayed in
Starfleet Academy
's
Stellar Cartography
laboratory
. (
SA
: "
Beta Test
")
In
3196
, this station's position was labeled on a Federation star chart that displayed the warp dead zone created by the
Omega-47 mine
test, performed by the
Venari Ral
. This station was located outside this region. (
SA
: "
300th Night
")
Appendices
Background information
In the episode "Disengage", the location of this deep space station was seen on the bridge of the
SS
Eleos XII
on what appears to be an updated chart from
Star Trek: Star Charts
.
Dave Blass
published pictures of the set on
his website
.
Apocrypha
In the
novel
Balance of Power
,
Geordi La Forge
mentions that Deep Space 5 was smaller than other deep space stations and smaller than the
Enterprise
Deep Space 5 was also mentioned in the
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine - Core Game Book
as well as in the
New Frontier
novel
House of Cards
. In the latter, the station was located near Sector 221-G (
β
), a
region
of
space
that included the planets Danter (
β
) and Xenex (
β
) and the former Thallonian Empire (
β
). The station was under the command of
Admiral
Edward Jellico
. The refitted
USS
Excalibur
launched from Deep Space 5 on its
mission
to render humanitarian aid to Sector 221-G.
External link
Deep Space 5
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

Deep Space 6
Type:
Starbase
Owner:
United Federation of Planets
Operator:
Starfleet
Location:
Beta Quadrant
Status:
Active
(
2382
)
Deep Space 6
, abbreviated as
DS6
, was a
Federation
deep space
station
that was in service with
Starfleet
in the late
24th century
. It was located in the
Beta Quadrant
. A
Federation
starship
traveling from the vicinity of
Starbase 80
at
impulse
would reach this station in 400
years
. (
LD
: "
Starbase 80?!
")
In
2324
, the location of this starbase was labeled on a
Section 31
star chart
used in a briefing presented by
Control
to Alpha Team.  (
Star Trek: Section 31
)
This deep space station was only mentioned in dialogue.
External link
Deep Space 6
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
