Eblar Prime
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2378
)
Location:
Delta Quadrant
Eblar Prime
was the
inhabited
prime
planet
in its
system
.
Zet
, a former member of the
Hierarchy
, intended to profit enough from the
theft
and sale of the
USS
Voyager
's
warp core
in
2378
that, within a
month
, he and his companion
Nar
would be soaking in the
mud baths
of Eblar Prime. (
VOY
: "
Renaissance Man
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Eden
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Eden
======================================================================

Eden
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Romulan Star Empire
(
2259
)
Status:
Intact
(
2269
)
Satellite(s):
At least
two moons
Location:
Eden system
Near the
Federation-Romulan border
Beta Quadrant
Eden
was an
uninhabited
planet
in the
Eden system
. Located in the
Beta Quadrant
, this planet was on the
Romulan
side of the
Federation-Romulan border
.
Eden had
two moons
. While the planet's
atmosphere
was capable of supporting
humanoid
lifeforms
, the planet was completely devoid of
fauna
. All the
plant
life, even the
grass
, was filled with
acid
that resulted in severe burns upon touch. The
fruit
on the
trees
was
poisonous
. This made the world virtually uninhabitable to humanoid life. (
TOS
: "
The Way to Eden
";
PIC
: "
Disengage
")
History
In
2269
, the
Tiburonian
research engineer
Doctor
Sevrin
began a quest to locate the
mythical
planet Eden, which he believed to be an idyllic, undiscovered planet. Gathering a group of loyal followers, Sevrin
stole
the
space cruiser
Aurora
and embarked on his search. Sevrin and his followers were eventually located and apprehended by the crew of the
USS
Enterprise
, although they were later able to take control of the ship.
Sevrin set the
Enterprise
on a
course
for Eden in
Romulan space
.
Spock
and
Pavel Chekov
had discovered the planet earlier by searching for an uncharted celestial body that corresponded with what Sevrin was looking for. They found the world by studying
star charts
and projecting the
orbits
of various planets. By mathematical processes, they were able to discern the
location
of this world by the effect it had on other known planets.
After the ship had crossed the Neutral Zone, the
Enterprise
arrived at the planet, to the
surface
of which Sevrin and his followers traveled in
a shuttlecraft
. Sevrin and the others were distressed to find, however, that the planet was uninhabitable to humanoid life. The party received severe burns on their bare
feet
, and
Adam
died
from eating poisonous fruit. Sevrin himself also ate poisonous fruit and died to stay on Eden. After returning to the ship, Spock told
Irina Galliulin
, one of Sevrin's followers, not give up the search for Eden, including making it themselves. (
TOS
: "
The Way to Eden
")
See also
Garden of Eden
Paradise
Qui'Tu
Vorta Vor
Sha Ka Ree
Appendices
Background
The background of the remastered version appeared to possibly be a modification of a photo of the floor of
Yosemite Valley
.
The
book
The Way to Eden
might pertain to this planet, or to the
Garden of Eden
.
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
Apocrypha
In the
Star Trek Maps
(p. 25), Eden was the only planet that
orbited
the moderate white star
UFC
3676543. It was first discovered by shipwrecked
survivors
of the
Earth-Romulan War
, who passed on
stories
of its mythical beauty.
External links
Eden
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Planets in TOS and TOS Remastered
at
Ex Astris Scientia

======================================================================
PAGE: Edos
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Edos
======================================================================

Edos
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
24th century
)
Native species:
Edosians
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
For the similarly named species, please see
Edo
.
Edos
was an
inhabited
planet
, and the
homeworld
of the
Edosians
. The
Edosian orchid
,
Edosian slug
, and
Edosian suckerfish
were also from this planet. (
DS9
: "
Broken Link
";
ENT
: "
The Catwalk
", "
Regeneration
", "
These Are The Voyages...
")
Appendices
Background information
Edos was the name of the homeworld of
The Animated Series
's
Lt.
Arex
, as given in a biography published in a
1974
Lincoln Enterprises
catalog.
[1]
The biography noted that Edos was not a
Federation
member
despite Arex being a
Starfleet
officer
. It also noted that Edos was located in the
Triangulum
constellation
, which was located at the edge of the
Milky Way Galaxy
, near the
galactic barrier
.
From
StarTrek.com
:
Home planet of Lt. Arex, the
Enterprise
navigator
from
2269
-
70
, and the tripodal Edosian race. Edos lies in the Triangulum constellation, on the rim of the galaxy. It is a jumping-off point for
science
teams studying the great energy barrier beyond the rim. It is also a peaceful planet which has never been involved in a war. The technology is as advanced as
Earth
's; the architectural style of its
cities
is strictly functional rather than aesthetically pleasing. The society has no class levels. All beings on Edos are equal.
Edos is not a member of the Federation. Rather, it remains in loose alliance with the Federation, coming under its agreements for trade, science, and aid in an emergency.
[2]
(X)
Edos was also mentioned as Arex's homeworld in
Star Trek Log 4
.
Apocrypha
According to
Star Trek Maps
, Edos was the second planet in the 92 Trianguli-Rho system.
According to the RPG sourcebook
The Federation
, "Edo" was the third planet in the Epsilon Minora system. It was sometimes referred to as Sauria.
External link
Edos
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Ekos
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ekos
======================================================================

Ekos
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2268
)
Native species:
Ekosians
Location:
Unnamed system
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Ekos
was an
inhabited
planet
in an
unnamed system
. This
Class M
planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
and was the
homeworld
of the
Ekosians
, a
pre-warp
humanoid
species
. Ekos was located closer to the center of its system than the sister planet
Zeon
.
In the mid-
23rd century
, Ekos was in the throes of
anarchy
. Dispatched by the
United Federation of Planets
to the upheaval in the mid-
2260s
as a
cultural observer
, noted
historian
John Gill
chose to abandon the
non-interference
policy, believing a modified form of
National Socialism
could be installed to impose order without the disastrous results wrought to
German
culture
in the
years
leading up to
Earth
's
Second World War
. Gill then proceeded to establish a passive version of the Nazi state, complete with recreations of such notorious German organizations as the
SS
, using the forms, symbols and
uniforms
of
20th century
Earth
.
Isak is savagely beaten by Ekosian Nazi officers.
Having arrived on Ekos, Captain
James T. Kirk
and Commander
Spock
— dressed in civilian wear — witnessed a young Zeon fugitive named
Isak
being captured and savagely beaten by Ekosian Nazi officers. Kirk and Spock also saw a black-and-white Nazi propaganda film broadcast on a large video screen set up on the street.
Soon enough, however, the inherent opportunities for abuse of power within such a system reverted the Ekosian
government
to the established
fascist
pattern and the Ekosians began a
genocide
campaign against the neighboring planet Zeon. (
TOS
: "
Patterns of Force
")
Photograph of Ekos and Zeon
A
photograph
of Ekos and Zeon was displayed on one of the
walls
in dive
bar
on
Starbase 25
in
2381
. (
LD
: "
An Embarrassment Of Dooplers
")
Appendices
Background information
The term "ekos" was also a word in ancient and modern Greek (spelled
oikos
), meaning "house" or "home".
StarTrek.com
located Ekos in the M34 Alpha system;
[1]
(X)
however, the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 230 located the planet in the M43 Alpha system. Furthermore, the latter source identified Ekos as a
class M
planet.
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 155
Apocrypha
In the
Star Trek Maps
(p. 27) and
The Worlds of the Federation
(p. 122), the name of the system was M43-Alpha. It was a
trinary star system
, Ekos being the
fourth
planet. The total
population
of the two planets was 16.1 billion.
In
FASA
's
The Federation
sourcebook, Ekos was the
third
planet in the M43-Alpha system.
External links
Ekos
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Planets in TOS and TOS Remastered
at
Ex Astris Scientia

======================================================================
PAGE: El-Adrel IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/El-Adrel_IV
======================================================================

El-Adrel IV
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2371
)
Native species:
El-Adrel IV entity
Location:
El-Adrel
system
El-Adrel
sector
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
"
Picard
and
Dathon
at El-Adrel.
"
–
Tamarian
first officer
,
2368
("
Darmok
")
El-Adrel IV
was the
uninhabited
fourth
planet
in the
El Adrel
system
. Located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
, this
Class M
planet was close to
territory
claimed by
The Children of Tama
. This planet was in the same
sector
as
Kanda IV
,
Malindi VII
,
Razna V
, and
Shantil III
. It was the
homeworld
of a non-sentient
species
.
In
2368
, a
Tamarian deep space cruiser
entered
orbit
of El-Adrel IV and began sending a signal to the
Federation
. The
USS
Enterprise
-D
arrived three
weeks
later and attempted to establish communications but was unable to understand
the Tamarians' language
.
Scan of the planet
Captain
Dathon
of the
Tamarians
then
transported
himself and Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
of the
Enterprise
to the
surface
of El-Adrel IV, knowing that there was a hostile
energy
creature
there. Dathon hoped that the shared danger would bring Picard to understand him, and he succeeded though at the cost of his own life. The Tamarian vessel had established a
scattering field
in the planet's
ionosphere
to prevent the
Enterprise
from interfering, as well as disabling (but not damaging) a
shuttlecraft
sent to land on the planet. The
Enterprise
took down the scattering field in time to rescue Picard from the creature. (
TNG
: "
Darmok
")
In
2371
, the
location
of El-Adrel was labeled in the
star chart
Data
and Picard were studying in
stellar cartography
aboard the
Enterprise
. (
Star Trek Generations
,
display graphic
)
"el-AH-drel" was the pronunciation for this planet's
name
from the
script
pronunciation guide.
[1]
The day scenes on the planet's surface were filmed on
location
at
Bronson Canyon
on
23 July
1991
and
24 July
1991
and the night scenes on
Paramount Stage 16
on
26 July
1991
.
The planet's classification came from
StarTrek.com
.
[2]
(X)
External link
El-Adrel IV
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Elas
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Elas
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Elas
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Klingon Empire
(
2257
)
United Federation of Planets
(
2259
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Native species:
Elasians
Location:
Tellun
system
Beta Quadrant
Elas
was an
inhabited
planet
, the inner of the two, the other being
Troyius
, found within the
Tellun
system
. This planet was located in the
Beta Quadrant
and was the
homeworld
of the
Elasians
, a spacefaring
humanoid
species
.
History
In the mid-
23rd century
, this planet's system was located in a border area near the
Federation-Klingon border
.
In
2257
, during the first
Federation-Klingon War
, this planet's system was occupied by the
Klingons
. (
DIS
: "
The War Without, The War Within
")
Prior to the visit by the
USS
Enterprise
in
2268
,
Federation
scientists
had studied the
culture
of the Elasians. In that
year
, both planets possessed the capability of mutual destruction. The
wedding
of the
Dohlman
of Elas,
Elaan
, to the
ruler of Troyius
preserved the
peace
.
Dilithium
crystals
were abundant on the planet, where it was considered to be a rather common
stone
. The
Klingon Empire
became aware of the Elasian dilithium deposits by
2268
, and held the
secret
closely while they argued with the
Federation
over jurisdiction of the Tellun system. (
TOS
: "
Elaan of Troyius
")
In
2369
, when shown his
guest quarters
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
by
Ensign
Kane
,
Montgomery Scott
reminisced to him about the time that the Dohlman of Elas was transported aboard the earlier
Enterprise
. He remembered her whining and complaining about the quarters. (
TNG
: "
Relics
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet was within the enclosed region. (
SA
: "
Rubincon
")
Stellar cartography
In
2256
, this planet was mentioned with the
star system
's name on the
star chart
"Alpha/Beta Quadrant Overview" in the
ready room
aboard the
USS
Discovery
. (
DIS
: "
Magic to Make the Sanest Man Go Mad
")
In
2258
, the planet's location was labeled on a star chart seen on the
main viewscreen
of the
USS
Enterprise
. (
DIS
: "
Such Sweet Sorrow, Part 2
")
In
2259
, the location of this planet was labeled on a
stellar cartography
chart that was seen on the
USS
Enterprise
's
ready room
viewscreen
. This planet's symbol had a
blue
color
, indicating that it was affiliated with the
United Federation of Planets
. (
SNW
: "
Strange New Worlds
", "
Children of the Comet
", "
Spock Amok
")
On
stardate
1292.4, the location of this planet was labeled in a chart used during a
Section 31
Alpha Team
briefing
. (
Star Trek: Section 31
)
The planet's location was labeled in a
Federation star chart
that was in
Fleet Admiral
Kirsten Clancy
's
office
at
Starfleet Headquarters
in
2399
and on the
bridge
of the
USS
Titan
in
2401
. Elas was in or near to
Federation space
. (
PIC
: "
Maps and Legends
", "
The Next Generation
", "
Disengage
")
In 2401, this planet's position was labeled on a
star chart
used by
Captain
William T. Riker
during his attempt at finding the last known location of the
SS
Eleos XII
. (
PIC
: "
The Next Generation
")
In
3196
, during the
All-Worlds Day
break, the location of "Tellun (Elas, Troyius)" was depicted on a star chart on the main viewscreen of the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
Ko'Zeine
")
Later that year, during the
Omega-47
crisis, the location of "Tellun (Elas, Troyius)" was depicted on star charts and readouts used across several locations on the USS
Athena
to locate, monitor, and respond to the Omega-47 mines laid by the Venari Ral. (
SA
: "
Rubincon
")
In the
script
for "Relics", Elas was listed in the pronunciation guide, where the term was pronounced as "EE-las".
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I"), Elas was listed as a
Federation member
in
2378
.
The current location of Elas in the Beta Quadrant was based on the most recent star charts seen in
Star Trek: Strange New Worlds
and
Star Trek: Section 31
.
External links
Elas
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Planets in TOS and TOS Remastered
at
Ex Astris Scientia

Elba II
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Intact
(
2328
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Elba II
was the
inhabited
second
planet
in its
star system
. The planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
. The planet had an
atmosphere
that was
poisonous
to
humanoids
and was home to a
Federation
rehabilitation colony
.
Prior to
stardate
5718
, the Federation took advantage of the inhospitable environment for the establishment of an
asylum
for the criminally insane – the
Elba II asylum
, The asylum was administered by a
governor
. In
2269
, the governor was
Doctor
Donald Cory
.
In that
year
, the
Federation
starship
USS
Enterprise
delivered a revolutionary new medicine to this asylum. The hope with this medicine was that it would permanently eliminate mental illness. A
patient
at this asylum,
Garth
of
Izar
, who was a former
Starfleet
Fleet Captain
, was treated with this medicine. (
TOS
: "
Whom Gods Destroy
")
In
2328
, this planet was home to the
Elba II Orbital Transit Station
. (
TNG-R
: "
Inheritance
")
Appendices
Background information
In the
Star Trek Concordance
, it was stated that "
the planet is named after the
island
of
Napoléon I
's first exile.
"
Apocrypha
In the
Focus on... Star Trek
comic "Captain's Personal Log", this was the eventual destination of
Janice Lester
.
External links
Elba II
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Planets in TOS and TOS Remastered
at
Ex Astris Scientia

2328

Eminiar VII Starbase

transport
.

",
okudagram
)

Emila II
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2366

Location:
Alpha
or

Emila II
was the
second
planet
of its
system
. This planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha Quadrant
or
Beta Quadrant
. This was the destination of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
after its
mission
at
Tanuga IV
in
2366
. (
TNG
: "
A Matter of Perspective
")
Appendices
Background information
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
"em-EEL-ah" was the pronunciation for this planet's
name
from the
script
pronunciation guide.
[1]
Apocrypha
In personnel data created for the
video game
Star Trek: Starship Creator
, the
biography
of
Jimenez
stated that he was born on Emila II.
External link
Emila
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Emila II
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Eminiar VII
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Eminiar_VII
======================================================================

Eminiar VII
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Eminian Union
Status:
Habitable
(
2375
)
Native species:
Eminians
Location:
Eminiar
system
NGC 321
Unnamed minor quadrant
Alpha Quadrant
Eminiar VII
was the
inhabited
seventh
planet
in the
Eminiar
system
. This
Class M
planet was located within an
unnamed minor quadrant
of the
Alpha Quadrant
and was the
homeworld
for the
Eminians
, a
pre-warp
humanoid
species
. In the
23rd century
, Eminiar VII was the principal planet of the
NGC 321
star cluster
. (
TOS
: "
A Taste of Armageddon
")
In the
24th century
, the
Eminiar VII Starbase
was located on this planet. (
TNG-R
: "
Inheritance
",
okudagram
)
Government
For over five hundred
years
, the
Eminian Union
was the planetary
government
of this world. This government was headed by the
First Councilman
of the
High Council of Eminiar
. The council conducted its governmental duties in the Council Room, which was located in the
Division of Control
. There was a direct channel between this council and the
Vendikar High Council
, which, by
2267
, hadn't been used for
centuries
. In 2267, the First Councilman was
Anan 7
.
History
The
civilization
was advanced, having had space flight for several hundred years, but it had elected not to venture outside of its
star system
.
Eminiar VII had been fighting a
war
with
Vendikar
, its
colony
on the
third
planet in the system, for five hundred years. The conditions of this war were stipulated in a
treaty
signed by both worlds. This was a simulated war with
casualties
determined by
computer
projections, and the people willingly killing themselves in
disintegration stations
. Every year, on Eminiar, one to three million were killed by this process. Legitimate targets in the war included
vessels
orbiting the planet, a fact that caused Kirk considerable difficulty during his visit. The reneging of this treaty by either side could result in a real war.
In
2217
, the
USS
Valiant
visited this planet. During its visit, the
star cruiser
and its crew were declared casualties of this war. The Eminians successfully destroyed the ship and killed its crew.
In 2267, the
USS
Enterprise
visited this planet on a
mission
to open diplomatic relations with Eminiar VII. During the visit, when his ship and crew were threatened with the same fate as the
Valiant
,
Captain
James T. Kirk
ended the war abruptly by destroying the computers that were conducting the simulated war, leaving the Eminians with the
choice
of either ending the war or starting a real conflict with unpredictable casualties.
Ambassador
Robert Fox
began work with the two planets to create a lasting peace and to establishment of a Federation treaty port. (
TOS
: "
A Taste of Armageddon
")
In
2268
, when held captive by the
Kelvans
, Captain Kirk asked
Lieutenant Commander
Spock
if he could repeat what he did with the
guard
on Eminiar VII by tricking him into believing the
landing party
had escaped. The attempt worked, but only briefly before the Kelvans recaptured the landing party. (
TOS
: "
By Any Other Name
")
In
2328
, three
individuals
arrived to the
Eminiar VII Starbase
from the
Elba II Orbital Transit Station
via commercial transport.
Niel Wray
and
William Foster
on the
SS
Kogin
and
David Ramirez
on the
SS
Wisconsin
. (
TNG-R
: "
Inheritance
",
okudagram
)
Lieutenant Commander
Richard James
, a
crewmember
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
and
USS
Voyager
, was born on this non-
Federation
planet
. In
2369
, this planet was listed on the chart
USS
Enterprise
Personnel Database
, which was seen on
Jean-Luc Picard
's
PADD
on aboard the
Enterprise
. (
TNG
: "
The Chase
",
okudagram
)
In the
2370s
, a view of the surface of Eminiar VII was depicted on several
viewscreens
on
Deep Space 9
's
Promenade
and
Replimat
advertising a trip to the "scenic Eminiar VII". (
DS9
: "
Whispers
", "
The Muse
", "
What You Leave Behind
")
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
.
Both
StarTrek.com
and the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 237 classified Eminiar VII as a
M-class
planet.
[1]
In the first draft script of
VOY
: "
Flashback
", Eminiar VII was established as a member of the Federation as of the
24th century
. All mention of the planet was excised from the episode by the time the final draft of the script was issued.
See also
Beta Eminiar VII
External links
Eminiar VII
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Planets in TOS and TOS Remastered
at
Ex Astris Scientia

======================================================================
PAGE: Empok Nor
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Empok_Nor
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Empok Nor
Type:
Terok Nor
-type
Affiliation:
Cardassian Union
(until
2372
)
Cult of the Pah-wraiths
(
2375
)
Location:
Trivas
system
Alpha Quadrant
Status:
Intact
(
3196
)
Diameter:
ca. 1451 meters
Draft:
ca. 969 meters
Docking facilities:
6 large
docking pylon
ports; 3 medium docking ring ports; 9 small docking ring ports; 6
landing pads
For the
DS9
episode of the same name, please see "
Empok Nor
".
"
Why would the Dominion pick a deserted Cardassian space station to make the trade?
"
"
They didn't pick it, Quark did. I've been there. It's the same design as Deep Space 9, so we know the layout.
"
–
Brunt
and
Nog
,
2374
("
The Magnificent Ferengi
")
Empok Nor
was a
Cardassian
Terok Nor
-type
space station
in the
Trivas
system
,
Alpha Quadrant
. This station was almost identical to
Terok Nor
(later known as
Deep Space 9
), which was no more than three
light years
away from its sister station.
History
The Cardassians abandoned it in
2372
, leaving behind standard
booby traps
that targeted non-Cardassians. The station had been the site of a failed
experiment
that used
psychotropic drugs
to enhance the Cardassians' natural
xenophobia
. The test subjects, three members of the
Third Battalion
,
First Order
, were left on the station in
stasis tubes
. One stasis tube was subsequently hit by
debris
and the occupant died. (
DS9
: "
Empok Nor
")
In late
2373
, a
Starfleet
salvage team
from Deep Space 9 was sent to Empok Nor for spare parts, because of a recent plague of technical failures on its sister station. During the
mission
, the two surviving
soldiers
in
stasis
were re-awakened and killed three members of the team before they were stopped.
Elim Garak
was also exposed to the psychotropic drug and killed a fourth member of the team before he was neutralized by
Chief
Miles O'Brien
. Eventually the
technology
was successfully salvaged. (
DS9
: "
Empok Nor
")
Nog
reminded Garak of the events that took place on Empok Nor several
months
later, telling him that he would never turn his back on the Cardassian again. (
DS9
: "
Rocks and Shoals
")
Later in
2374
,
Quark
chose Empok Nor to be the site for the exchange between several
Ferengi
and the
Dominion
of
Keevan
for
Ishka
, who had been taken prisoner by the Dominion. Keevan was accidentally killed before the exchange was to take place, but Quark and the others managed to bluff the opposing side long enough for Ishka to be returned. Keevan's
body
was left aboard the station. (
DS9
: "
The Magnificent Ferengi
")
In
2375
,
Dukat
brought fifty members of the
Cult of the Pah-wraiths
to establish a community on Empok Nor with him as their master.
At the time, only one of the
fusion generators
was operational, so the station lacked power for such amenities as
replicators
, but the group was able to turn several of the station's lower levels into
hydroponic bays
. They also maintained a few
cargo shuttles
to bring supplies from
Bajor
.
Following the establishment of the new colony on the station, Dukat had
Colonel
Kira Nerys
kidnapped
from Deep Space 9 in an effort to convert her. He fled the station when his control over his followers unraveled, and the remaining cult members decided to return to Bajor with Kira aboard the
USS
Defiant
, leaving the station abandoned once more. (
DS9
: "
Covenant
")
Stellar cartography
In
3196
, during the
Omega-47
crisis, the location of Trivas (Empok Nor) was depicted on
star charts
and readouts used across several locations on the
USS
Athena
to locate, monitor, and respond to the
Omega-47 mines
laid by the
Venari Ral
. This station was outside the affected region. (
SA
: "
300th Night
", "
Rubincon
")
Personnel
See
:
Empok Nor personnel
Appendices
Appearances
DS9
:
"
Empok Nor
"
"
The Magnificent Ferengi
"
"
Covenant
"
Background information
According to the
script
for "Empok Nor", the pronunciation for Empok Nor was "EM-pahk NORE".
[1]
Apocrypha
In the
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine - Millennium
series of
novels
, following the apparent destruction of DS9 with the opening of the second wormhole, Empok Nor was moved into the station's previous position by
Weyoun 5
, who regarded it as fulfilling a
prophecy
that "the Gateway" will be restored.
Several years later, the
Deep Space Nine
relaunch novels
featured Empok Nor being towed to the
Bajoran system
by the
Corps of Engineers
. Its
central core
was to be used to replace that of DS9, which had been destroyed several
weeks
earlier.
In
Star Trek Online
, Empok Nor was used as a
headquarters
for the
True Way
in
2409
. The station (and the mission it was part of) were removed in 2015 when the 'Cardassian Struggle' arc was revamped. Its mirror counterpart was encountered in the
Badlands
battle zone.
External links
Empok Nor
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Empok Nor
at
Wikipedia

Enara Prime
AKA:
Enara
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2373
)
Native species:
Enarans
Location:
Delta Quadrant
Enara Prime
, or, more simply,
Enara
, was the
inhabited
prime
planet
in its
star system
. This system was located in the
Delta Quadrant
. This was the
homeworld
of the
Enarans
, a
warp-capable
humanoid
species
. (
VOY
: "
Remember
")
The
Enaran hogfish
was also native to the planet. (
LD
: "
A Farewell To Farms
")
Sights included the
Danius Cliffs
, which stood above a molten
sea
.
Rain
occasionally occurred on the planet.
(
VOY
: "
Remember
")
According to
StarTrek.com
, Enara Prime was classified as a
M-class
planet.
[1]
According to the
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 86), the Enara system's
primary
was a
F-class star
. The
USS
Voyager
visited this system on
stardate
50203.
External link
Enara Prime
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Environment
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Environment
======================================================================

An
environment
was the natural and/or man-made surroundings in which one lived and worked. Environments could be
described
in terms of physical or intangible
characteristics
.
In
2364
, after listening to
Geordi La Forge
's
joke
,
Data
clarified, "
So the difficulty in attaining such complex positioning in
zero gravity
environment, coupled with the adverse
effect
it would have on the psychological
well-being
of the average
Human
male
, is what makes this
anecdote
so
amusing
.
" (
TNG
: "
Conspiracy
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Epicenter
CATEGORIES: Locations, Physics
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Epicenter
======================================================================

The
epicenter
was the central origin point of an
earthquake
.
In
2269
, the
USS
Enterprise
changed the epicenter of an quake on
Argo
to the
planet
's
north polar seas
by targeting unpopulated areas with
phaser
fire. This was done so that the
Aquans
wouldn't be affected by it. (
TAS
: "
The Ambergris Element
")
In
2368
, two earthquakes on
Penthara IV
had their epicenters beneath the two
drill sites
furthest to the
south
. (
TNG
: "
A Matter Of Time
")
External link
Epicenter
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Epsilon Canaris III
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Epsilon_Canaris_III
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Epsilon Canaris III
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
(
3195
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3195
)
Location:
Epsilon Canaris
system
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Epsilon Canaris III
was the
inhabited
third
planet
in the
Epsilon Canaris
system
. This planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
and was near the
Gamma Canaris region
.
In
2267
, Assistant
Commissioner
Nancy Hedford
was dispatched there to prevent the outbreak of war. The commissioner developed
Sakuro's Disease
and had to be evacuated; subsequent events made her return impossible, and necessitated the assignment of another mediator. (
TOS
: "
Metamorphosis
")
In the
32nd century
, Epsilon Canaris III was a
Federation
member
planet. It was listed with other members on a video wall in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
The Life of the Stars
")
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I"), Epsilon Canaris III was a Federation member in
2378
.
Based on the structure of their names, both Epsilon Canaris and Gamma Canaris were stars in the same
constellation
.
In the
Bayer designation
, which was a system of stellar classification, the Greek letter indicated the brightness of a star in a constellation. In this case, Epsilon Canaris would be the 5th brightest star in its constellation.
External link
Epsilon Canaris III
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Epsilon IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Epsilon_IV
======================================================================

Epsilon IV
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Klingon Empire
Status:
Intact
(
2367
)
Location:
Epsilon Hydrae
system
'etnap Nebula
Klingon space
Beta Quadrant
~129
light years
from
Earth
An entry about Epsilon IV in the service record of Captain Ransom
Epsilon IV
was the
fourth
planet
in the
Epsilon Hydrae
system
. Located in the
'etnap Nebula
, this planet's system was inside
Klingon space
in the
Beta Quadrant
and was identified as a system of the
Klingon Empire
on
Federation
star charts
. It was approximately 129
light years
from
Sol
.  (
DIS
: "
The War Without, The War Within
", "
An Obol for Charon
", "
Such Sweet Sorrow, Part 2
")
In
2367
, on
stardate
44322.82,
Captain
Rudolph Ransom
shadowed a
Klingon
Bird-of-Prey
near this planet. After discovery, Ransom managed to elude the
Klingons
in the nebula for three days until stardate 44325.18 when they gave up the chase and he disengaged. (
VOY
: "
Equinox, Part II
")
In star charts seen in
Star Trek: Discovery
, a location in the Beta Quadrant was labeled with the name of this planet's system. The planet was named in parenthesis next to the star, cementing a connection between the two.
External link
Epsilon IV
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Epsilon IX station
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Epsilon_IX_station
======================================================================

Epsilon IX station
Type:
Comm station
Owner:
United Federation of Planets
Klingon Empire
(
2257
)
Operator:
Starfleet
Klingon Imperial Fleet
(
2257
)
Location:
Near the
Federation-Klingon border
Epsilon IX sector
Beta Quadrant
Status:
Active
(
2369
)
The
Epsilon IX station
was a
23rd century
Federation
outpost
and
comm station
operated by
Starfleet
. Located in the
Beta Quadrant
, this
space station
, in
Federation space
, was in the
Epsilon IX
sector
. It was near the
Federation-Klingon border
. This station was protected by
deflector shields
.
History
In
2257
, during the first
Federation-Klingon War
, this outpost was occupied by the
Klingons
. (
DIS
: "
The War Without, The War Within
")
In the early
2270s
, the station's
commander
was
Commander
Branch
. During the same time period, as the
V'ger
crisis unfolded, Epsilon IX transmitted detailed information to
Starfleet Command
about the engagement, and subsequent destruction by
V'ger
, of a Klingon patrol led by the
IKS
Amar
. Probably misinterpreting the station's
sensor
scans as a hostile action,
V'ger
subsequently destroyed Epsilon IX as it passed near the station. (
Star Trek: The Motion Picture
)
In
2285
, two models of an
Epsilon IX
-type station were mounted on two separate walls of the
Starfleet Officers' Lounge
. One was near where
Admirals
Kirk
and
Morrow
had a conversation, while another was in the lounge's entry room. (
Star Trek III: The Search for Spock
)
In
2369
,
Geordi La Forge
, when investigating the
murder
of
Lieutenant
Keith Rocha
, requested a display of the personal records of the lieutenant from the
Relay Station 47
computer
for
stardates
46455 to 46460. One of these records mentioned this station's replacement.
In the log entry, Rocha wrote, "
More heavy traffic to the Epsilon IX station. I think Starfleet's got some big science project brewing there with at least half a dozen
starships
running around.
" (
TNG
: "
Aquiel
")
For the remastered episode, the log entry stardate was changed from 46460 to 45460.
The monitor room exterior
The monitor room interior
The panel section
Epsilon IX
-type station display model
Epsilon IX personnel
See
:
Epsilon IX station personnel
Stellar cartography
Alpha/Beta Quadrant Overview
In
2256
, this outpost's location was labeled on the
star chart
"Alpha/Beta Quadrant Overview" in the
ready room
aboard the
USS
Discovery
. (
DIS
: "
Magic to Make the Sanest Man Go Mad
")
In
2259
, the location of this outpost was labeled on a
stellar cartography
chart that was seen on the
USS
Enterprise
's
ready room
viewscreen
. The outpost had been returned to the Federation after a brief occupation by the Klingons in 2257. (
SNW
: "
Strange New Worlds
", "
Spock Amok
")
In the early
24th century
, the location of this outpost was labeled in a chart used during a
Section 31
Alpha Team
briefing
. (
Star Trek: Section 31
)
Appendices
Background information
According to the
shooting script
, Epsilon IX was a
monitor station
located on a rocky
planetoid
. The monitor station was described as a "
...small Starfleet outpost, antennas sprouting everywhere.
" Workers working on an antenna would have established the size of this outpost.
The set for the interior of Epsilon IX was built on
Paramount Stages 12
and
14
at a construction cost of US$40,000. (
The Making of Star Trek: The Motion Picture
, p. 95) The footage filmed on this set was shot during
The Motion Picture
's post-production period. (
The Making of Star Trek: The Motion Picture
, p. 193)
Upon visiting the set for the scenes aboard Epsilon IX,
Susan Sackett
stated that "
The set they work in is so crisp and clean that it takes some getting used to. It's bright, well-lit, with beautiful floors and colorful consoles. But I notice that the art department had some fun with this one, too. Above one of the consoles is a panel showing the duty roster for the outpost. Among the names up there (which I doubt were picked up by the camera) are the following:
R. D. Enberry
,
M. Minor
,
L. Cole
,
R. McKenzie
and
Gort
. In case you're wondering,
Mike Minor
,
Lee Cole
, and
Richard McKenzie
are all members of the
Star Trek
art department. As for R. D. Enberry and
Gort
, I'll leave those for you to figure out!
" (
Starlog
#27,
October 1979
, p. 29)
According to
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 3rd ed., p. 141, the Epsilon IX station may have been located in the
Epsilon IX sector
, mentioned in
TNG
: "
Samaritan Snare
".
An Epsilon IX uniform patch was among many items that were sold off on the
It's A Wrap!
sale and auction
on eBay.
[1]
(X)
According to the
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 62) and the
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Federation Historical Highlights, 2161-2385"), Epsilon IX was located in the
Beta Quadrant
. In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 62), Epsilon IX was one of the Epsilon Outposts. These eleven outposts were located near to the
Federation-Klingon border
.
Studio models
Unused reverse beauty shot of the six-foot model
Unused beauty shot of the six-foot model
Three
studio models
of the Epsilon IX station's exterior were built, to show the outside of the station at different scales. (
text commentary
,
Star Trek: The Motion Picture (The Director's Edition)
) These were designed and built at
John Dykstra
's company,
Apogee, Inc.
Up until then that station had not even been envisioned as being space based in the storyboards of
Astra Image Corporation
, but rather as a location on the surface of a planet. (
Star Trek: The Magazine
Volume 2, Issue 8
, pp. 64-65) It was one of the very few, if not only, designs not executed by either Astra or
Magicam
for the movie.
When asked about them Dykstra stated, "
Actually, there were about two or three of them. There was the entire station which you saw from a distance in the early shots. That was about six feet long and three-and-half feet wide–and just unbelievably detailed. On top of that,
Roger Dorney
, our chief optical guy, came up with a way to pull
bluescreen
mattes off that sucker–which was really awesome. With all the latticework and stringers that thing had, the registration had to be
incredibly
accurate, and he came up with results that damn near exceeded the resolution capability of the film.
" (
Cinefex
, issue 2, p. 59)
In regard to the complete, main model, Dykstra later added in somewhat more detail, "
This was about a six-foot long model, and it was an interesting technique we used to create it. We used acid-etched brass. The reason we used this acid-etching technique was, that it gave us very square edges. And square edges in fact are a dead give-away on miniatures. If they are not square, if they're round, then you get the sense that the scale of the miniature is not what it's being represented as.
" (
Star Trek: The Motion Picture
(The Director's Edition)
, "
audio commentary
")
Michael Lawler
setting up an onstage shot of the model
David Beasley
(l) and McCune working on the large panel section
The envisioned visuals and script requirements necessitated the build of additional enlarged sections of the station. Dykstra, also praising his crew of model makers, continued,
"In addition to the basic Epsilon 9 miniature, we also had one isolated panel for the closer shots that was about five feet wide and six feet long. And there was a larger-scale version of the conning tower where we used straight rear projection to put the people in. We made an inordinate number of miniatures for this picture and
Grant McCune
and the dozens of people he had working for him back in the model shop did just a phenomenal job.
"They were literally working around the clock, just days ahead of the camera crew. As one end of a model was being rolled onto the stage to be photographed, the other end of a model was getting a last shot of spray paint. And the fact that they made those things work under those kind of circumstances, and put the stuff together so they would last through the shots–but not by putting so much time into any one thing that it kept them from completing all the other stuff–well, it was just a miracle."
The demise of the station in the movie, being digitized by
V'ger
, was executed utilizing the same methodology as has been used with the digitizing of the
Klingon
K't'inga
-class battle cruiser model
at the start of the movie. "
Essentially, though, what happens to Epsilon 9 was a replay of what happened to the Klingons–the electrical discharge material and the laser scan stuff. There were a couple nice shots that didn't get into the film, where the
puppets
are being chased by the destruction in a different way than we've got them appearing now. But we ran out of time, and weren't able to get them to go together right.
" Dykstra concluded. (
Cinefex
, issue 2, p. 59)
Not having been involved with the design,
Andrew Probert
nevertheless managed to get some of his input in, "
The communications station "Epsilon-9" was briefly looked at but the design and building went to John Dykstra's Apogee group. I had a second brush with that station however, when Trumbull asked me to write some appropriate dialogue to run over scenes of the station as background chatter. This introduced the station without a big sign hanging on it. I wrote several levels of dialogue and even stuck my own name in a lower level, but when Trumbull saw it, he had that level moved to the top, and you can hear a message from
Commodore
Probert
if you listen closely.
" (
Starlog photo guidebook Special Effects
, vol. 5, p., 96)
Two of the studio models were reused as the
display models
on the walls of the
Starfleet Officers' Lounge
in
Star Trek III: The Search for Spock
, the master model on the wall in the room itself, while the enlarged panel section model was in the lounge's entry room, only ever so fleetingly seen as
Admiral
Kirk
leaves the lounge. (
Star Trek III: The Search for Spock (Special Edition)
DVD
,
text commentary
)
None of the Epsilon IX models were afterwards ever sighted again, either in a production or outside of one, and their fate or whereabouts are currently unknown.
External link
Outpost Epsilon 9
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

PAGE: Epsilon Indi II
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Epsilon_Indi_II

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Epsilon Indi II
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Location:
Epsilon Indi
system
Beta Quadrant
~12
light years
from
Sol
Epsilon Indi II
was the
inhabited
second
planet
in the
Epsilon Indi
system
. Located in the
Beta Quadrant
, this planet was approximately 12
light years
from the
Sol system
. It was affiliated with the
United Federation of Planets
.
History
In the early-to-mid
24th century
, on
stardate
15720.4,
Lieutenant
Walter Pierce
was born on Epsilon Indi II. This lieutenant's
personnel file
appeared in a
hallucination
of
Deanna Troi
's. (
TNG
: "
Eye of the Beholder
",
okudagram
)
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet was within the enclosed region. (
SA
: "
300th Night
")
The location of Epsilon Indi on a star chart
This planet was only mentioned in writing.
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on its star system's proximity to neighboring stars –
61 Cygni
,
Alpha Centauri
,
Procyon
,
Sol
,
Tau Ceti
, and
Wolf 359
– as seen in the
star charts
appearing in the
Star Trek: Discovery
episode "
Magic to Make the Sanest Man Go Mad
" and in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".

fight off a pandemic. (

Epsilon Juday
Status:
Intact
(
2371
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Epsilon Juday
was a location in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
Its position was labeled on a
star chart
in the guidance system
S/COMS
panel on aboard the
Amargosa observatory
in
2371
. (
Star Trek Generations
)
Background information
This location was only mentioned in writing.
In August 2024, this panel was put up for auction in the Entertainment Memorabiliia Live Auction: Los Angeles Summer 2024 by Propstore Auction.
[1]
This location was named after the film's production assistant
Penny Juday
.

Erabus Prime
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2360s
)
Native species:
A
species
of
insect
Location:
Gamma Quadrant
Erabus Prime
was the
prime
planet
in its
star system
, located in the
Gamma Quadrant
.
Erabus Prime was visited by
Vash
while in the company of
Q
. Vash was bitten by an
insect
on the planet that would have caused an
allergic reaction
to appear all over her
body
, were it not for Q's intervention. (
DS9
: "
Q-Less
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to
StarTrek.com
and the
script
pronunciation guide, the pronunciation for Erabus was "AIR-uh-bus".
[1]
[2]
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 75), this was a
quaternary
system with a pair of
G-class stars
and a pair of M-class stars.

at

planet
in the

system
,
Alpha Quadrant
.

23rd century
, this planet's system was in
Non-Federation space
.

Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of Federation space in

, this planet's system was within the blast radius of one of the mines. (

", "

crew. (

")

,
Saru
displayed a
hologram
of the Kaminar system, including Erb'oh, in the

chambers. (

: "
Kobayashi Maru
")

's tenth

2236
, her
foster

shuttle

Errikang VII

was the

seventh
planet
in its
star system
. This system was located in the
Gamma Quadrant
.
The inhabitants of Errikang VII were previously acquainted with
Q
;
Vash
was nearly killed visiting the planet while in Q's company. (
DS9
: "
Q-Less
")

StarTrek.com
, the
primary
of the Errikang system was named Errikang. StarTrek.com gave the pronunciation for Errikang VII as "AIR-ih-kang"
[1]
(X)
[2]
(X)
; however, according to the
script
pronunciation guide, the pronunciation was "ERR-i-kang".
[3]
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 75), the Errikang system's primary was an
A-class star
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Eternal Archive
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Eternal_Archive
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Eternal Gallery and Archive
Type:
Space station
Location:
Badlands
Alpha Quadrant
Status:
Active
(
3196
)
Crew:
1,000
Defenses:
Deflector shields
The
Eternal Gallery and Archive
, usually simply called the
Archive
or the
Eternal Archive
, was an interstellar
library
and repository that operated from at least the
23rd
to the
32nd centuries
. It was located on a large
space station
that changed location approximately every fifty years.
The Archive boasted the largest known collection of
historical
and
cultural
artifacts
in the
Alpha
and
Beta Quadrants
. Its permanent staff of one thousand, originating from a variety of backgrounds, considered themselves an order sworn to the preservation of
galactic
knowledge. (
DIS
: "
Erigah
", "
Labyrinths
")
History
Interior of the Archive
Jett Reno
knew of the Archive from her time
smuggling
folios
for a "shady antiquarian
archivist
" in the
23rd century
.
In the mid-
2370s
,
Doctor
Marina Derex
concealed the fifth and final piece of a
map
to
Progenitor
technology
inside a
crystal
in Viewing Room 7 at the Archive. She also placed in storage her original handwritten
manuscript
of
Labyrinths of the Mind
, containing a
mindscape
that would test the worthiness of those seeking the map piece. Derex subsequently became an
archivist
and lived the remainder of her life at the station. (
DIS
: "
Erigah
", "
Labyrinths
")
In the
31st century
, the Archive moved to an "
oasis
" of calm
space
within the
Badlands
. Contact with the outside was handled via a
communications beacon
.
In
3191
, the
USS
Discovery
was directed to the Archive by Derex's library card, which they had found on
Halem'no
. While
Captain
Michael Burnham
attempted Derex's test, Archivist
Hy'Rell
presented cuttings from
Kwejian
's
World Root
to
Cleveland Booker
. Shortly afterward, Breen
Primarch
Ruhn
also arrived at the Archive in
his dreadnaught
in pursuit of the map piece. Ruhn inserted
soldiers
into the Archive using
shield-tunneling technology
, but was unable to prevent the
Discovery
team from obtaining the map piece.
Ruhn's dreadnaught attacks the Archive
Ruhn proceeded to fire upon the Archive, inflicting significant damage and casualties. He threatened to destroy the station unless Burnham surrendered the map; Burnham replied that she would only comply if he swore a sacred
tergun
oath
to spare the Archive. Ruhn agreed, but prepared to destroy the Archive anyway after he had the map. This blatant act of dishonor contributed to his crew ultimately siding with
Moll
and
Lieutenant
Arisar
in a
mutiny
against him. (
DIS
: "
Labyrinths
")
By
3196
, the location of the Eternal Archive was being tracked by Starfleet, and was marked on
Federation star charts
. (
SA
: "
Ko'Zeine
", "
Rubincon
")
Stellar cartography
In
3196
, the path of the Eternal Archive for over 500 hundred years, from the
Beta Quadrant
to the
Badlands
in the
Alpha Quadrant
, was depicted on
Federation
star charts
with stops marked by
red
circles
. (
SA
: "
Ko'Zeine
")
In
3196
, during the
Omega-47
crisis, the location of the Eternal Archive was depicted on
star charts
and readouts used across several locations on the
USS
Athena
to locate, monitor, and respond to the
Omega-47 mines
laid by the
Venari Ral
. This station was outside the affected region. (
SA
: "
Rubincon
")
Policies
The Archive was politically neutral and open to all, so long as they followed the rules. It refused to involve itself in conflicts between
species
, though direct threats to the Archive itself could result in a species losing access. Hy'Rell also noted, perhaps in jest, that they imprisoned violators in an
oubliette
. Access to specific items in the Archive was granted by means of a
library card
composed of a distinctive
metallic
alloy
.
Part of the Archive's mandate was to ensure that lost cultures were not forgotten. Artifacts from such cultures were displayed in places of honor, though survivors of that culture were entitled to claim ownership of them if they so desired. (
DIS
: "
Erigah
", "
Labyrinths
")
Collected items
The Theory and Practice of Psychiatry
by
Fredrick C. Redlich
and
Daniel X. Freedman
The Reformation
by
Will Durant
Scar Tissue
by
Michael Ignatieff
A Comprehensive Guide to Talaxian Hair Styles
Euclidean Geometry
Hupyrian Folk Tales
Labyrinths of the Mind
Several
Breen
artifacts
Original
Valaric manuscripts
Two
World Root
cuttings
Personnel
Named
Marina Derex
Hy'Rell
Unnamed
Eternal Gallery and Archive personnel
Interior scenes at the Archive were filmed at the
University of Toronto
's
Thomas Fisher Rare Book Library
.
[1]
[2]

into the Archive using
shield-tunneling technology
, but was unable to prevent the
Discovery
team from obtaining the map piece.
Ruhn's dreadnaught attacks the Archive
Ruhn proceeded to fire upon the Archive, inflicting significant damage and casualties. He threatened to destroy the station unless Burnham surrendered the map; Burnham replied that she would only comply if he swore a sacred
tergun
oath
to spare the Archive. Ruhn agreed, but prepared to destroy the Archive anyway after he had the map. This blatant act of dishonor contributed to his crew ultimately siding with
Moll
and
Lieutenant
Arisar
in a
mutiny
against him. (
DIS
: "
Labyrinths
")
By
3196
, the location of the Eternal Archive was being tracked by Starfleet, and was marked on
Federation star charts
. (
SA
: "
Ko'Zeine
", "
Rubincon
")
Stellar cartography
In
3196
, the path of the Eternal Archive for over 500 hundred years, from the
Beta Quadrant
to the
Badlands
in the
Alpha Quadrant
, was depicted on
Federation
star charts
with stops marked by
red
circles
. (
SA
: "
Ko'Zeine
")
In
3196
, during the
Omega-47
crisis, the location of the Eternal Archive was depicted on
star charts
and readouts used across several locations on the
USS
Athena
to locate, monitor, and respond to the
Omega-47 mines
laid by the
Venari Ral
. This station was outside the affected region. (
SA
: "
Rubincon
")
Policies
The Archive was politically neutral and open to all, so long as they followed the rules. It refused to involve itself in conflicts between
species
, though direct threats to the Archive itself could result in a species losing access. Hy'Rell also noted, perhaps in jest, that they imprisoned violators in an
oubliette
. Access to specific items in the Archive was granted by means of a
library card
composed of a distinctive
metallic
alloy
.
Part of the Archive's mandate was to ensure that lost cultures were not forgotten. Artifacts from such cultures were displayed in places of honor, though survivors of that culture were entitled to claim ownership of them if they so desired. (
DIS
: "
Erigah
", "
Labyrinths
")
Collected items
The Theory and Practice of Psychiatry
by
Fredrick C. Redlich
and
Daniel X. Freedman
The Reformation
by
Will Durant
Scar Tissue
by
Michael Ignatieff
A Comprehensive Guide to Talaxian Hair Styles
Euclidean Geometry
Hupyrian Folk Tales
Labyrinths of the Mind
Several
Breen
artifacts
Original
Valaric manuscripts
Two
World Root
cuttings
Personnel
Named
Marina Derex
Hy'Rell
Unnamed
Eternal Gallery and Archive personnel
Interior scenes at the Archive were filmed at the
University of Toronto
's
Thomas Fisher Rare Book Library
.
[1]
[2]

PAGE: Etheenia
CATEGORIES: Locations

======================================================================

Etheenia
was a location visited by
Enterprise
NX-01
during its historic mission of exploration in the
22nd century
.
In
2152
, while stranded on a
moon
with an increasingly rising
temperature
with an
Arkonian
named
Zho'Kaan
,
Commander
Charles Tucker III
reminisced about some of the experiences he had while on
Enterprise
, including diving in the
ice
caves
of Etheenia. (
ENT
: "
Dawn
")
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 252 identified Etheenia as a
planet
.

Evadne IV
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2367
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Evadne IV
was the
fourth
planet
in its
star system
. This planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
In
2367
, after completing its
mission
at
Harrakis V
, the
USS
Enterprise
-D
set course for this planet. En route, the
starship
encountered an unstable
wormhole
near the
Ngame Nebula
. (
TNG
: "
Clues
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
"ee-VARD-nee" was the pronunciation for this planet's
name
from the
script
pronunciation guide. Furthermore, in lines cut from the episode, it was revealed that the planet was located in the
Evadne system
and the
Enterprise
-D was on a
diplomatic
mission to this world. The system was ten
days
journey from the Ngame Nebula at an unspecified speed.

======================================================================
PAGE: Excalbia
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Excalbia
======================================================================

Excalbia
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2328
)
Native species:
Excalbians
Location:
Excalbia's solar system
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Hundreds of
light years
from
Earth
Excalbia
was an
inhabited
planet
in an
unidentified solar system
, which was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
. This was the
homeworld
for the
Excalbians
, a
species
of intelligent and advanced
carbon cycle life forms
.
The planet had a
poisonous
atmosphere
and a
surface
that consisted mostly of molten
lava
. The planet was located hundreds of
light years
from
Earth
.
In
2269
, the
USS
Enterprise
was sent to Excalbia by
Starfleet
to confirm several "old
space legends
" about the planet, which suggested that the planet was home to intelligent life.
Orbital
scans of Excalbia revealed several scientifically anomalous readings, with indications of artificial
power
being generated in
factor seven
quantities, indicating the presence of a considerable
civilization
.
As the
Enterprise
prepared to
warp
out of orbit, the ship was subjected to an incredibly swift deep probe from the planet. Moments later, the ship was contacted by the
image of
Abraham Lincoln
, who eventually convinced
Captain
Kirk
and
Spock
to beam down to Excalbia's surface, of which, a
Earth-like
area of approximately 1,000
square kilometers
was created. On the planet's surface, Spock and Kirk were subjected to an Excalbian experiment on the nature of
good
and
evil
conducted by
Yarnek
with Kirk and Spock's unwilling participation. Yarnek recreated several historical figures thought to embody the concepts of good and evil, and pitted them in combat against each other before the
Enterprise
crew was allowed to depart. (
TOS
: "
The Savage Curtain
")
Excalbia diorama
In
2328
, this was home to the
Excalbia Tourist Resort
. (
TNG-R
: "
Inheritance
",
okudagram
)
A diorama of Excalbia was created by noted
collector
Kerner Hauze
aboard
his ship
. Following his death in
2381
, the display was severely damaged by the ship's own
automated defenses
as it pursued the
USS
Cerritos
away team
that was aboard cataloguing the collection. (
LD
: "
Kayshon, His Eyes Open
")
Appendices
Background information
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 23), Excalbia was classified as an
E class planet
.
Apocrypha
In
The Worlds of the Federation
, Excalbia was the
first
planet in its system.
External links
Excalbia
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Planets in TOS and TOS Remastered
at
Ex Astris Scientia

======================================================================
PAGE: Exo III
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Exo_III
======================================================================

Exo III
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2293
)
Native species:
"
Old Ones
" (
extinct
)
Location:
Unnamed system
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Exo III
was the formerly
inhabited
third
planet
of an
unnamed system
. The planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
and was the
homeworld
of the
extinct
Old Ones
.
The planet's
atmosphere
was within safety limits by
Federation
standards. Its
gravity
was 1.1 of
Earth
's, to which it also used to bear a remarkable resemblance before its
sun
began to dim
500,000 years ago
. (
TOS
: "
What Are Little Girls Made Of?
")
History
As the star of the Exo system began to gradually dim, the average
surface temperatures
on Exo III eventually fell down to one hundred
degrees
below zero over the course of a half million
years
. The Old Ones, a
humanoid
species
, fled their
surface
dwellings to the dark underground
caverns
to escape the inhospitable surface conditions.
The species exchanged their open and free society for an increasingly mechanistic one. The Old Ones over time built more and more advanced
android
servants. They gave the androids a sense of pride in their abilities and a desire to survive. The Old Ones, however, at some point became fearful of their creations and began to deactivate them. As their survival was threatened, the androids were able to exceed their programming, and they murdered their creators in self-defense, causing the
extinction
of the Old Ones.
Exo III planetary profile
The android duplicator
In
2261
, famed Federation
medical
archaeologist
Roger Korby
was leading an expedition on this planet with his
assistant
Dr.
Brown
. Korby sent messages back to the Federation through 2261. Around that time, the expedition encountered the android
Ruk
, still tending the
machines
of the ancient
civilization
centuries
after the extinction of the Old Ones. Among the machines, Korby discovered a still functional
android duplicator
. Korby had at the time lost the ability to use his
legs
and was freezing to
death
. He created an android duplicate of himself before his
body
died.
When communications with Korby were lost, two further expeditions were sent to Exo III in an effort to locate the Korby expedition, but both failed to find them.
In
2266
, the
USS
Enterprise
, under the command of
Captain
James T. Kirk
, traveled to Exo III in a third attempt to learn the fate of the Korby expedition. Android duplicates of
Korby
and
Brown
contacted the
Enterprise
as it arrived in
orbit
. The crew later discovered that both Korby and Brown had died on the planet five years prior along with the rest of the expedition.
The android duplicate of Korby attempted to convince Kirk that the technology of the Old Ones should be used to secretly replace living people in the
Federation
with android duplicates. Since the duplicator could imprint the
memories
and the
consciousness
of the people being duplicated to the androids, the Korby android was convinced that people would in time embrace the chance to live forever in android bodies. Kirk and
Christine Chapel
were not convinced, as the android duplicates had no sense of remorse or ability to experience genuine
emotions
. All the androids on the planet were destroyed in various conflicts that developed with them during the mission. (
TOS
: "
What Are Little Girls Made Of?
")
In
2268
, the planetary profile chart of Exo III was displayed in one of the
science labs
aboard the
USS
Defiant
before the ship was pulled into the
mirror universe
. (
TOS
: "
The Tholian Web
";
ENT
: "
In A Mirror, Darkly
")
Stellar cartography
In
2293
, the location of this planet was labeled in a
star chart
found on the
navigation console
of the
USS
Enterprise
-B
. (
Star Trek Generations
)
Appendices
Background information
This planet was originally to have been named "Exo IV".
Kellam de Forest
found fault with this choice of name. He made a suggestion, included among a series of research notes (dated
9 May
1966
), that the planet be renamed "Gamma Exo IV". However, the name remained "Exo IV". In another series of notes (dated
11 May
1966), de Forest recommended, "
The first number could be dropped.
"
In the revised final draft script of "What Are Little Girls Made Of?", the surface of Exo III as seen from orbit was initially described thus; "
The coloration of the planet suggests extremely low temperatures on the surface. However, not totally snow-covered.
" The script detailed a closer view of the surface by calling it, "
A semi-twilight landscape of barren wasteland buried in deep snow; jagged ice peaks loom in b.g. against a dark and sinister sky.
"
[1]
The caves of Exo III were represented with a set built on
Desilu Stage 10
. (
To Boldly Go: Rare Photos from the TOS Soundstage - Season One
, p. 81)
The
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 27) classified Exo III as a
P-class planet
.
For the pre-remastered version of "What Are Little Girls Made Of?", the blue planet model for Exo III was first used for
Rigel XII
("
Mudd's Women
"). This planet model was used for
Psi 2000
("
The Naked Now
"),
M-11
("
Court Martial
" and "
The Menagerie, Part I
"), and
Eminiar VII
("
A Taste of Armageddon
"). For the
remastered
version of "What Are Little Girls Made Of?", a new planet model was created by
CGI
artist
Max Gabl
.
Apocrypha
The recreation of
Quark's Bar
which was located in the
Star Trek: The Experience
attraction in the
Las Vegas
Hilton Hotel served an alcoholic drink called a
Warp Core Breach
that was reportedly served with ice crystals from Exo III.
In the novella "
The Worst of Both Worlds
", the
mirror universe
James T. Kirk
of the
ISS
Enterprise
destroyed the last surviving member of the android society (presumably Ruk's mirror counterpart) on Exo III, who had likewise turned on their masters and killed them.
In the novel
Immortal Coil
, a flashback sequence reveals that
Noonien Soong
,
Ira Graves
, and Vaslovik – the current alias of the immortal
Flint
– visited the planet after Kirk's
Enterprise
had left to study its android technology.
External links
Exo III
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Planets in TOS and TOS Remastered
at
Ex Astris Scientia

======================================================================
PAGE: Exo-Port
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Exo-Port
======================================================================

Exo-Port
was a location.
In
2401
, the
SS
Eleos XII
fled to Exo-Port after being attacked by an
unknown enemy
at
Kaphar
. The same force attacked them again at Exo-Port where, according to
Jack Crusher
, "
three guys in
Starfleet uniforms
tried to
Prime Directive
[him] into an early
grave
.
" After they escaped into space, an enemy vessel forcibly docked with the
Eleos XII
.
Doctor
Beverly Crusher
managed to defeat the
boarding party
and initiate another
warp
jump to the
Ryton system
, but was critically injured in the process. (
PIC
: "
The Next Generation
", "
Disengage
")
After their
mission
to
Daystrom Station
,
Worf
and
Commander
Raffi Musiker
traveled to Exo-Port to monitor
Starfleet Security
. They returned to the
USS
Titan
-A
after finding no mention of
Captain
William T. Riker
being in custody. (
PIC
: "
Dominion
")
The
Star Trek: Picard Logs
mention the "Exo-Port Takeover" as a conflict between the first
USS
Titan
and the
Klingon Empire
in the
2290s
.

Fahleena III

)

Alpha Quadrant
Fahleena III
was the
inhabited
third
planet
in the
Fahleena
system
,
Alpha Quadrant
. This planet's system was in or near to the
Venari Ral Zone
.
In the
24th century
, Fahleena III, along with
Mariah IV
and
Ultima Thule
, was a stop on a
trade route
formerly taken by
Valerian
traders
supplying weapons-grade
dolamide
to the
Cardassians
.
The Valerian
transport ship
Sherval Das
visited this planet in
2369
. (
DS9
: "
Dramatis Personae
";
SA
: "
300th Night
", "
Rubincon
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
"fah-LEE-nuh" was the pronunciation for this planet's name from the
script
pronunciation guide.
[1]
However,
StarTrek.com
has the pronunciation as "fell-LEE-nuh".

======================================================================
PAGE: Farius Prime
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Farius_Prime
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Farius Prime
AKA:
Farius
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Neutral
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Native species:
Farians
Location:
Farius
system
Alpha Quadrant
Farius Prime
, also known simply as
Farius
, was the
inhabited
prime
planet
in the
Farius
system
,
Alpha Quadrant
. This was the
homeworld
of the
humanoid
Farians
. (
DS9
: "
Honor Among Thieves
") It was located at a
bearing
of 216
mark
177 from
Deep Space 9
. (
DS9
: "
The Maquis, Part I
")
History
A
2370s
trade route
connected Farius Prime and
Galador II
, which was served by
Galador freighters
. Deep Space 9 was a
way station
along this trade route. (
DS9
: "
The Maquis, Part I
")
The planet was politically neutral and hosted a
Klingon
embassy
. The
Klingon ambassador
in
2374
was an opponent of
Gowron
and his posting there was considered a form of
punishment
.
Paper
currency
was in use on the planet, races were held and
prostitution
existed. A
location
on Farius Prime was the
Jinami Street
. The planet maintained both a
police force
and a
Municipal Sanitation
department.
Different species lived on the planet besides the native Farians, including
Humans
,
Bolians
,
Dopterians
,
Nasari
, and several other humanoid species.
Farius Prime was also a major hub for activities of the
Orion Syndicate
, a
criminal
organization. In 2374,
Miles O'Brien
was sent undercover by
Starfleet Intelligence
to the planet to infiltrate the organization and build a case against some of its members. Among the Farius Prime-base members of the Syndicate were
Liam Bilby
,
Graife
,
Flith
,
Krole
,
Yint
and
Raimus
. (
DS9
: "
Honor Among Thieves
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet was within the blast radius of one of the mines. (
SA
: "
300th Night
", "
Rubincon
")
In an early version of the script of "Honor Among Thieves", the planet was called "Palomar Prime". (
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine Companion - A Series Guide and Script Library
)
In
StarTrek.com
and in the scripts for "The Maquis, Part I" and "Honor Among Thieves", the pronunciation for Farius was "FAIR-ee-us".
[1]
(X)
[2]
[3]
The position of this planet in the Alpha Quadrant was gleaned from the location of its system which was seen in the
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
episode "
Beta Test
".
External links
Farius Prime
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Farrar's World
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Farrar's_World
======================================================================

Farrar's World
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2293
)
Location:
Beta Quadrant
Farrar's World
was a
planet
in the
Beta Quadrant
.
In
2293
, Farrar's World's position was labeled in the
star chart
for the "
Operation: Retrieve
" command briefing. The chart was from the
Starfleet Command Intelligence Database
. This star chart was seen in the
office
of the
Federation President
on
Earth
. (
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
,
set artwork
)
This planet's quadrant of origin is inferred based on the position of neighboring locations –
Starbase 24
and
Rura Penthe
– as seen in the
star charts
appearing in the
Star Trek: Discovery
episode "
Magic to Make the Sanest Man Go Mad
" and in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
This chart was seen in greater resolution at threads at Flare.Solareclipse.net.
[1]
(X)
[2]
(X)
Many of these locations shared their names with the last names of crew who worked on the sixth film. This location was probably named after the visual effects supervisor
Scott Farrar
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Federation Headquarters station
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Federation_Headquarters_station
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Federation Headquarters
Type:
Headquarters
Owner:
United Federation of Planets
Operator:
Starfleet
Location:
Alpha Quadrant
Status:
Active
(
33rd century
)
Federation Headquarters
(
Federation HQ
or
FHQ
) was the administrative
headquarters
of the
United Federation of Planets
. Considered the Federation's home
planet
, Federation Headquarters was located on
Earth
until the
31st century
. (
DIS
: "
People of Earth
")
History
Earth
At the founding of the
United Federation of Planets
, the seat of governance was established at
Paris
,
France
.
While hosting
Chancellor
Gorkon
and his party aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-A
in
2293
,
Captain
James T. Kirk
claimed that one of the advantages of being a thousand
light years
from Federation Headquarters was the availability of
Romulan ale
on board the ship. (
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
)
In
2372
, during a suspected
Changeling
infiltration of Earth,
Captain
Benjamin Sisko
, acting as
Chief of Starfleet Security
on Earth, ordered
phaser sweep
units installed in every room at both
Starfleet Headquarters
and Federation Headquarters. (
DS9
: "
Homefront
")
After the Burn
In
3069
,
the Burn
inexplicably destroyed every
starship
with an active
warp core
, "most" of which were Federation. Uncertain if it was an attack or whether it could happen again, the people of Earth felt having Federation Headquarters on Earth made it a potential target. By
3089
, Earth had left the Federation, and new Federation and
Starfleet Headquarters
were needed. (
DIS
: "
People of Earth
")
Mobile headquarters
By
3189
, the
USS
Federation
, a
Pax
-class
starship that could also be configured as a mobile-
space station
, came to serve as the headquarters of both Starfleet and the entire
civilian
government
of the Federation. (
DIS
: "
Die Trying
")
In
3190
, the location of Federation Headquarters in the
Alpha Quadrant
was labeled on a star chart used by
Commander
Paul Stamets
for tracking the movement of the
Dark Matter Anomaly
through the
galaxy
. (
DIS
: "
The Examples
")
Return to planetside
In
3195
, the Federation government was debating where to re-establish a more permanent planetary headquarters for the Federation, to serve as the new official seat of government. While many possibilities were proposed, the leading option was to re-establish the old location in Paris.
That year, a delegation of
Betazoids
from the First House came to the reopened Starfleet Academy at San Francisco on behalf of their Youth Advancement to determine if rejoining Starfleet was possible. In the end, concessions were made in which Admiral
Charles Vance
suggested Federation Headquarters should be based on
Betazed
instead of the old location of Paris. Betazoid president
Emerin Sadal
accepted the proposal, and cemented the groundwork for Betazed to return to the Federation.(
SA
: "
Beta Test
")
In
3196
, during the
Omega-47
crisis, the location of Federation HQ was depicted between systems on
star charts
and readouts used across several locations on the
USS
Athena
to locate, monitor, and respond to the
Omega-47 mines
laid by the
Venari Ral
. The dedication ceremony for the new planetside Federation Headquarters happened shortly after the crisis was resolved, officially moving the seat of governance from the USS
Federation
to a location on the surface of Betazed. (
SA
: "
Rubincon
")
Appendices
Background information
The exterior of the
Federation Council
meeting in
Star Trek IV: The Voyage Home
was described in the script as "Federation Headquarters", in the "San Francisco Bay Area."
[1]
In the first draft script of
DS9
: "
When It Rains...
", Federation Headquarters was stated to be the location of the
Department of Cartography
.

A
feeding ground
was a large open expanse where
animals
could find
food
.
In
2368
,
Jean-Luc Picard
speculated
that the
Pelloris asteroid field
would make a good feeding ground for
nitrium parasites
, being rich in
nitrium
. (
TNG
: "
Cost Of Living
")

at

Felton Prime
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Cardassian Union
Status:
Habitable
(
3190
)
Location:
Alpha Quadrant
Felton Prime
was the
inhabited
prime
planet
in its
star system
, controlled by the
Cardassians
.
During the
Dominion War
, the planet was the site of a
military
camp and
relay station
. In
2375
, the
Klingon
General
Martok
destroyed the base before the Cardassian defenders could react, earning him a great victory. (
DS9
: "
Once More Unto the Breach
")
In
3190
,
Captain
Michael Burnham
promoted
betting
on
Lieutenant Commander
Joann Owosekun
in a ring fight match on the
Karma Barge
by calling her "
the most ferocious fighter this side of Felton Prime.
" (
DIS
: "
All In
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
This planet's quadrant of origin is inferred based on the position of the Cardassian Union as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".

at

, the wiki for licensed

.

limbs
. (

. (
