======================================================================
PAGE: Fenris
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Fenris
======================================================================

Fenris
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Fenris Rangers
Status:
Habitable
(
2399
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Fenris
was a
planet
on which the
Fenris Rangers
were based. By
2399
, the planet was said to be going through tough times.
Seven of Nine
and
Bjayzl
first met on Fenris, where the latter was posing as someone who wanted to help. (
PIC
: "
Stardust City Rag
")
Fenris shares its name with the
Fenris Wolf
, also called Fenrir, of Norse mythology. Fenrir is chained by the gods, but fated to break free at the final battle of Ragnarok, where he will slay Odin before being killed in revenge by Odin's son. Coincidentally,
a ship
named for Odin appeared in
TNG
: "
Angel One
".

======================================================================
PAGE: Ferenginar
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ferenginar
======================================================================

Ferenginar
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Ferengi Alliance
United Federation of Planets
(
3191
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Native species:
Ferengi
Location:
Ferenginar system
Ferengi Territory
Alpha Quadrant
"
Ah, to stand once more in those rivers of muck...
"
–
Leck
,
2374
("
The Magnificent Ferengi
")
"
Wow, oh, it's what heaven would look like if God was stupid!
"
–
Lt. Junior Grade
Beckett Mariner
,
2380
("
Parth Ferengi's Heart Place
")
Ferenginar
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Ferenginar system
of the
Alpha Quadrant
. This was the
homeworld
of the
Ferengi
, a
warp-capable
humanoid
species
, and the
capital planet
of the
Ferengi Alliance
. The planet was known for having nearly constant, planet-wide torrential
rains
, rotting
vegetation
, and
rivers
of muck.
Location
Ferenginar's location relative to Bajor and Cardassia
Ferenginar was surrounded by
star systems
, including
Clarus
and
Irtok
. (
DS9
: "
Profit and Lace
") Ferenginar was located approximately fifty-five
light years
from
Cardassia Prime
and
Starbase 375
, and sixty light years from
Bajor
. In
2375
, the location of Ferenginar was labeled on a tactical cartography map that was displayed in the
wardroom
of
Deep Space 9
. (
DS9
: "
When It Rains...
",
okudagram
)
In
3191
, this planet's location was labeled on a
star chart
. Accompanied by a star, the label identified the planet as a homeworld. (
DIS
: "
Red Directive
")
Later that year, the position of Ferenginar was labeled on a Federation
star chart
. Like other key members of the Federation, its position was denoted by a five-pointed star. (
DIS
: "
Lagrange Point
")
History
See also:
Ferengi history
The Ferenginar capital city in 2381
In the mid-
24th century
, Ferenginar experienced rampant inflation and currency devaluation after the
Great Monetary Collapse
. Many accounts, including some belonging to
Quark
, were devastated. (
DS9
: "
Homefront
")
In
2374
, after
Grand Nagus
Zek
added an amendment to the
Ferengi Bill of Opportunities
allowing
females
to wear clothes and make
profit
, Ferenginar was plunged into financial chaos, and a
communications blackout
was imposed. (
DS9
: "
Profit and Lace
")
In
2375
, Grand Nagus Zek appointed
Rom
as the new Grand Nagus. (
DS9
: "
The Dogs of War
")
Regarding how Ferenginar is depicted in "The Dogs of War",
René Echevarria
noted, "
Ferenginar has changed drastically, and is becoming more and more of a 24th-century socialist state, like any
Federation
planet.
" (
Cinefantastique
, Vol. 32, Nos. 4/5, pp. 81-82) However, Ferenginar wasn't portrayed as undergoing such drastic alterations in the first draft of the script. "
All the stuff about Ferenginar becoming a kinder, gentler place was added later on,
" noted
Armin Shimerman
. (
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine Companion
, p. 695)
In
2381
, Grand Nagus Rom signed an official application for Ferenginar to join the United Federation of Planets. (
LD
: "
Parth Ferengi's Heart Place
")
Landmarks
Dominion War Memorial
Ferengi Center for Forged Arts
Ferengi Hall of Fame
Ferenginar Historic Public Library
Museum of Bribery
Museum of Gambling
Museum of Haggling
Nagal Residence
Sacred Marketplace
Tower of Commerce
Destitution Rock
was referenced in the first draft script of
DS9
: "
Body Parts
", but not in
canon
.

:

Parth Ferengi's Heart Place
"
Background information
In
DS9 Season 3
installment "
Family Business
", the Ferengi homeworld first received its name. "fer-ENG-ih-nar" was the pronunciation for this planet's name from the script pronunciation guide for
fourth season
episode "
Bar Association
".
[1]
While writing "Family Business",
Ira Steven Behr
and
Robert Hewitt Wolfe
had a lot of fun with devising details about Ferenginar, for viewers' initial glimpse of the planet. Thinking about Ferengi anatomy, Wolfe decided the planet should have "a moist climate." The writers also wanted to differentiate Ferenginar from other major homeworlds in
Star Trek
, which commonly had "hot and dry" environments, related Wolfe. Seeing as rain hadn't been depicted much on
alien
planets, the writers chose to give Ferenginar a virtually permanent rainy climate. (
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine Companion
, p. 241)
A scene description from the script for "Family Business" stated, "
Ferenginar, the homeworld of the Ferengi, is a fetid dismal swamp, subject to a near-endless downpour.
"
[2]
Following the debut appearance of Ferenginar in "Family Business", Ira Behr remarked, "
It was an interesting look at the Ferengi homeworld [....] I'm not sure we'll be going back to the Ferengi homeworld.
" (
Captains' Logs Supplemental - The Unauthorized Guide to the New Trek Voyages
, p. 97)
In ultimately excised dialogue from the first draft script of DS9 Season 4 outing "
Body Parts
",
Garak
commented about Ferenginar, "
I must admit it's a magnificent city... I quite enjoyed my time there.
" This would probably imply either that Ferenginar had only one city or that the planet's capital city was at one point intended to have the same name as the planet.
Creating the sets to represent Ferenginar in
DS9 Season 5
's "
Ferengi Love Songs
" was fairly easy, with the Grand Nagus'
Chamber of Petitioners
in the Tower of Commerce being the only new set. Because Ishka's house had been established in "Family Business", DS9's art department had to merely assemble and redress that set, so that it could reappear in "Ferengi Love Songs". (
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine Companion
, p. 445)
The
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine Technical Manual
(p. 3) depicts Ferenginar in the
Bajor sector
.
[3]
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
(pp. 35 & 36) and
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Federation Historical Highlights, 2161-2385"), Ferenginar was an
M-class
planet located in the Ferenginar
star system
. The capital of this world, and of the Ferengi Alliance, was Ferenginar. In
2378
, there were 78.2 billion Ferengi living on this world. The Ferengi have been
warp-capable
since antiquity. Major points of interest were the Sacred Marketplace and Tower of Commerce.
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 269 classified Ferenginar as a class M planet.
Apocrypha
According to
The Worlds of the Federation
, the Ferengi homeworld was called Ferengal.
According to the
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine - Narrator's Toolkit
, Ferenginar was the fourth planet in the Ventarus Idrilon system.
According to the RPG sourcebook
Worlds
, Ferenginar was the fifth planet in the Alpha Krellek system.
External link
Ferenginar
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

. The planet was featured in the

======================================================================
PAGE: Finibus III
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Finibus_III
======================================================================

Finibus III
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Habitable
(
2260
)
Satellite(s):
Two
moons
Location:
Finibus system
Beta Quadrant
Finibus III
was the
third
planet
of the
Finibus system
. A
class M
world, it had two
moons
and was home to a remote
Federation
colony
with a
population
of several hundred in the
23rd century
. (
SNW
: "
Memento Mori
")
Circa
2260
, the position and
orbital
path of this planet was depicted in a system map aboard the
USS
Enterprise
. (
SNW
: "
Memento Mori
", "
Hegemony, Part II
")
Astronomical data
Planetary data for Finibus III
Finibus III was located at coordinates 2721.4386. It
orbited
a
black hole
and was approximately two hundred million
kilometers
distant from a
brown dwarf
in the system. The brown dwarf emitted
radio wave
interference that could potentially hamper communications in the area.
The planet had a
rotation period
of 23.5
hours
and a
revolution period
of 346.5
solar days
. Its
mass
was estimated to be 4.93532
e24
×10
24
kilograms
and its age at 4.7 billion
solar years
. The
atmosphere
was composed of 77% molecular
nitrogen
, 21% molecular
oxygen
, 1.2%
carbon dioxide
, and 0.8%
nitrous oxide
, with trace amounts of
methane
and
neon
. The mean
surface temperature
was 14.9
degrees
Celsius
. (
SNW
: "
Memento Mori
")
History
In
2259
, the atmosphere of Finibus III was in danger of becoming
unbreathable
. Thus, the
USS
Enterprise
was dispatched to deliver an upgraded
AP 350
atmospheric processor
to the colony there. However, they arrived to find that the colony had been recently devastated by
suborbital
plasma bombardment
, and its
communications satellite
destroyed. No colonists remained on the surface, alive or dead; streaks of
blood
on the ground indicated that casualties had been dragged to a central location before being removed.
Meanwhile, the
Enterprise
found a
Federation cargo vessel
containing approximately a hundred survivors. From eyewitness accounts,
Lieutenant
La'An Noonien-Singh
surmised that the attack had been perpetrated by the
Gorn
, and that they had deliberately left the survivors as
bait
for any rescuers. Shortly after, the
Enterprise
was ambushed and heavily damaged by a
Gorn hunter
hiding near the second moon. Noonien-Singh later noted that the Gorn had never before ventured so far into
Federation space
. (
SNW
: "
Memento Mori
")
Finibus system
Black hole
/
Brown dwarf

•
Finibus II
•
Finibus III
•
Finibus IV
•
Finibus V
•
Finibus VI

Finibus III
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed

PAGE: Finnea Prime
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Finnea_Prime
======================================================================

Finnea Prime
AKA:
Finnea
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Non-aligned
Status:
Habitable
(
2373
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Finnea Prime
, or, more simply,
Finnea
, was the
inhabited
prime
planet
in its
star system
. This was a non-
Federation
planet and a
colony
world or
homeworld
of the
Finnean
species
.
Life on Finnea was not considered easy. However, the planet had
technology
sufficient to allow for the implantation of
dataports
. Some
females
on the planet were given dataports in order to become
net-girls
.
According to
memories
implanted in the
Idanian
operative
Arissa
, one learned not to trust the
police
on Finnea, who evidently had a low threshold of
evidence
required in order to imprison one.
The planet's
economy
apparently featured the use of
money
, and the world was believed to have been a base of operations for
Draim
of the
Orion Syndicate
. (
DS9
: "
A Simple Investigation
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
"fih-NAY-uh" was the pronunciation for this planet's
name
from the
script
pronunciation guide.
[1]
External link
Finnea Prime
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Forcas III
CATEGORIES: Planets

======================================================================

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Forcas III
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Klingon Empire
Status:
Inhabited
(
2370
)
Location:
Forcas
system
Beta Quadrant
Forcas III
was the
inhabited
third
planet
in the
Forcas
system
of the
Beta Quadrant
.
History
In
2260
, Forcas was within the area of effect of a
quantum uncertainty field
. (
SNW
: "
Subspace Rhapsody
")
In
2370
, a
bat'leth
competition
was held here.
Worf
entered the competition, and won
Champion Standing
.
In another
quantum reality
, Worf only achieved
Ninth Place
, due to the judges ignoring his opponent's use of an illegal
T'Gha maneuver
. In yet another, he was not able to attend the competition, and his younger
brother
Kurn
went in his place. (
TNG
: "
Parallels
")
Champion Standing
bat'leth trophy
Ninth Place
bat'leth trophy
Exports
Forcas III was the origin of a drink that
Guinan
served in
Ten Forward
on the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. When
Data
tried this drink in
2371
, during one of his first
experiences
with his
emotion chip
, he described the Forcas III drink as "revolting" (and immediately agreed to an offer for more). (
Star Trek Generations
)
Appendices
Background information
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
This planet's quadrant of origin and affiliation is inferred based on the position and affiliation of its star system as seen in the Federation
star charts
appearing in the
Star Trek: Strange New Worlds
episodes "
Under the Cloak of War
" and "
Children of the Comet
".
"FOUR-cuss" was the pronunciation for this planet's name from the
script
pronunciation guide for "Parallels".
[1]
In
Star Trek Generations
, Guinan mispronounces it as "FRO-cuss."
Apocrypha
Forcas III is mentioned as one of
The Albino
's past whereabouts in the
novel
Excelsior: Forged in Fire
. Taking place in
2409
, the computer game
Star Trek Online
requires the player to participate at a bat'leth tournament on Forcas III for the
House of Martok
.
External link
Forcas III
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Forlat III
CATEGORIES: Planets

======================================================================

Forlat III
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Devastated by
Crystalline Entity
attack
(
24th century
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Forlat III
was the formerly
inhabited
third
planet
in its
star system
. A
colony
was located on this
Class M
planet.
This world was attacked by the
Crystalline Entity
.
Settlers
on the planet attempted to seek refuge in
tunnels
with strong deposits of
kelbonite
but were still absorbed by the
entity
.
Forlat III was one of the eleven attack sites recorded, and one of the three where settlers took refuge in
caves
.
Dr.
Kila Marr
examined this planet before the
attack
on
Melona IV
in
2368
. (
TNG
: "
Silicon Avatar
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
The planet's class came from the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 281.

Forlat III
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

(covers information from several

)
Foster-D'Angelo
Type:
Star system
Status:
Intact
(
2367
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Foster-D'Angelo
was a
star system
in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
In
2367
, Foster-D'Angelo's position was labeled on a
tactical situation monitor
in
Captain
Benjamin Maxwell
's
ready room
on the
USS
Phoenix
. (
TNG
: "
The Wounded
",
okudagram
)
In
2366
, in an
alternate timeline
, during the
Federation-Klingon War
, Foster-D'Angelo's position was labeled on a tactical situation monitor on the
bridge
and in the
ready room
of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
.
(
TNG
: "
Yesterday's Enterprise
",
okudagram
)
This star system was only mentioned in writing.
It can't be deduced from the star chart in which quadrant Foster-D'Angelo was located as there were
planets
and star systems from both quadrants spread throughout the chart.
Foster-D'Angelo was named after swing gang members
Don Foster, Jr.
and
Dick D'Angelo
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Free Haven
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Free_Haven
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Free Haven
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Cardassia Prime
(
2259
)
Bajor
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Location:
Alpha Quadrant
Free Haven
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Alpha Quadrant
.
History
In the
24th century
, this planet was the home for a
Bajoran
colony
.
In
2372
, this colony came under attack by
Breen
privateers
. The Breen were repulsed by the
USS
Defiant
. (
DS9
: "
To the Death
")
Stellar cartography
In
2259
, the location of the planet was labeled on a
stellar cartography
chart that was seen on the
USS
Enterprise
's
ready room
viewscreen
. This system's symbol had a
yellow
color
, indicating that it was affiliated with
Cardassia Prime
. (
SNW
: "
Strange New Worlds
")
In the early
24th century
, the location of this system was labeled on a
star chart
used during a
Section 31
Alpha Team
briefing
. (
Star Trek: Section 31
)
The planet's location was labeled in a
Federation star chart
that was in
Fleet Admiral
Kirsten Clancy
's
office
at
Starfleet Headquarters
in
2399
and on the
bridge
of the
USS
Titan
-A
in
2401
. Free Haven was in or near to
Federation space
. (
PIC
: "
Maps and Legends
", "
The Next Generation
", "
Disengage
")
In
3196
, during the
Omega-47
crisis, the location of Free Haven (Bajor) was depicted on star charts and readouts used across several locations on the
USS
Athena
to locate, monitor, and respond to the
Omega-47 mines
laid by the
Venari Ral
. This world was outside the affected region. These maps showed this planet in or near the
Venari Ral Zone
. (
SA
: "
300th Night
", "
Rubincon
")
Appendices
Background information
In the original
script
, the colony was called "
Prophet
's Haven"'. (
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine Companion - A Series Guide and Script Library
)
According to
StarTrek.com
,
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 46), and
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Federation Historical Highlights, 2161-2385"), this colony
orbited
a
K-class star
.
[1]
(X)
Apocrypha
According to the
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine - Narrator's Toolkit
from
Last Unicorn Games
, Free Haven was located in the Sanelar
system
.

at

, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

at

======================================================================
PAGE: Freecloud
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Freecloud
======================================================================

Freecloud
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2399
)
Location:
Alpha Doradus system
Beta Quadrant
~169
light years
from
Sol
Freecloud
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Alpha Doradus system
. The planet was located in the
Beta Quadrant
and was approximately 169
light years
from
Sol
.
It contained a number of
urban areas
, including
Stardust City
, and hosted a diverse population.
Spacecraft
approaching Freecloud were subjected to aggressive, personalized
holographic
advertisements
projected inside said craft. Dismissal of the advertisements required physical interaction with the holograms. Space traffic was handled by
Freecloud Orbital Control
.
Freecloud welcome banner, complete with dice
Freecloud was a mecca for
businesses
of all kinds, due to its maximum-
security
commercial and information services, and minimal restrictions on personal liberties. Its welcome banner displayed the name prominently and included rolling
dice
. An
interfacers' guild
was present on the planet, and
Bjayzl
's base of operations was also located there. The
Fenris Rangers
stored their
funds
on Freecloud, as well. (
PIC
: "
Stardust City Rag
")
Sometime prior to
2380
, the
Exocomp
Peanut Hamper
wanted to become a
dabo girl
on Freecloud, but didn't succeed and chose a career in
Starfleet
instead. (
LD
: "
A Mathematically Perfect Redemption
")
In
2399
,
Raffi Musiker
tracked
Dr.
Bruce Maddox
to this place, and relayed this information to
Jean-Luc Picard
. This place was the intended destination of
La Sirena
after leaving
Earth
. (
PIC
: "
The End is the Beginning
")
An advertisement for Freecloud was displayed on a building in
District Six
of
M'talas Prime
in the early
25th century
. (
PIC
: "
The Next Generation
")
Inhabitants
Freecloud inhabitants
Appendices
Background information
Star Trek: Picard
showrunner
Michael Chabon
described Freecloud as a "non-aligned Crypto Libertarian Pseudorepublic".
[1]
On
29 January
2021
, Chabon wrote a short piece on Freecloud titled "
Some Notes on Freecloud
" for his blog. In this piece, he stated that Freecloud, otherwise known as Alpha Doradus IV, was a
Venus
-
sized
,
Class M
planet
orbiting
the
binary stars
of the Alpha Doradus system. This system was located in the
Iconia
sector
of the
Beta Quadrant
.
The planet had a 700-
day
Earth
year
. Its
climate
was considerably warmer than Earth's and it had lush
vegetation
.
This planet had a single large
continental
land mass
named Panterra, which occupied one face of the planet in both the
northern
and
southern hemispheres
. This supercontinent had a
million
mile
coastline
that was largely
mountainous
. Where it was not mountainous, there were the mouths of the major
rivers
and the inlets of three large
lakes
or seas, the Yin, the Yang, and the Perfume Sea. The interior of the supercontinent was an arid or semi-arid lowland. This super continent was surrounded by a global
ocean
named Panpontine.
The planet was settled by a "schismatic group of Earth-descended Anarcho-Existentialists from the planet Silentum" in
2180
. The initial settlements were located on the coastline. In
2201
, a short five-sentence
constitution
was ratified, calling for universal service by all able-bodied
citizens
after they turned sixteen years of age in the Freecloud Defense Network.
The
capital city
of Freecloud was
Stardust City
, which was the largest city on the planet.
[2]
External link
Freecloud
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Frylon IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Frylon_IV
======================================================================

Frylon IV
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Habitable
(
2381
)
Location:
Frylon system
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Frylon IV
was the
fourth
planet
in the
Frylon system
, located in
Federation space
. The planet's surface featured
jungles
with a profusion of native life, including
bioluminescent
plants
and a type of
bird
. Their
bioelectric signatures
made it impossible to pinpoint specific
lifeforms
using
sensors
.
In
2381
, two
Denobulans
studying flora on Frylon IV were attacked by a
mugato
, which was not native to the planet. An
away team
from the
USS
Cerritos
discovered that it came from a camp of
Ferengi
poachers
, led by
Kynk
, who were
trafficking
in mugato parts. After a violent confrontation during which almost the entire team was captured,
Ensigns
Brad Boimler
and
Sam Rutherford
convinced the Ferengi that it would be more profitable long-term to keep the mugato alive as a
tourist attraction
. Thus, Kynk proceeded to establish
Kynk's Mugatoland
on the planet. (
LD
: "
Mugato, Gumato
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Gagarin IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Gagarin_IV
======================================================================

Gagarin IV
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Habitable
(
2365
)
Location:
Gamma 7
sector
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Gagarin IV
was the
inhabited
fourth
planet
of its
system
. The planet was located in the
Gamma 7 sector
, a
spatial
region
in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
In the mid-
24th century
, this planet was the home of the
Darwin Genetic Research Station
, a
Federation
outpost
. (
TNG
: "
Unnatural Selection
")
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 290 stated that the planet was named after Soviet cosmonaut
Yuri Gagarin
(1934-1968), the first
Human
in
space
.
In the
script
, Gagarin IV's system was named the
Gagarin
system.
The planet model for Gagarin IV was first used for
'aucdet IX
("
The Child
"). It was later used for
Galorndon Core
("
The Enemy
"),
Barzan II
("
The Price
"),
Jouret IV
("
The Best of Both Worlds
"), and
Bilana III
("
New Ground
"). For its appearance as Gagarin IV, the model had a
green
color
.
External links
Gagarin IV
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Re-Used Planets in TNG
at
Ex Astris Scientia

======================================================================
PAGE: Gaia
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Gaia
======================================================================

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Gaia
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2373
)
Location:
Unnamed system
Gamma Quadrant
Gaia
was the
uninhabited
fourth
planet
in
its
star system
and described as
Earth
-like. The system was located in the
Gamma Quadrant
.
There were several major
land masses
and a wide variety of climatic and
surface
conditions, ranging from tundra to
desert
. The vast majority of the land surface was temperate or semi-temperate. The planet was encased in a
quantum
energy barrier
, and this barrier was known to cause severe
temporal displacement
. Several
weeks
after this planet's discovery, the energy barrier surrounding the planet would become impossible to travel through, making this planet inaccessible, sealing it off from the rest of the
universe
.
Native
lifeforms
found on Gaia included the
chattel
, the
torga
, and the
yar-bear
.
In
2373
, the
USS
Defiant
, after a weeklong reconnaissance mission in the quadrant, discovered this planet.
In an
alternate timeline
, the planet was
colonized
in the
22nd century
by the
crew
of the
USS
Defiant
who had been transported through
time
. The ship
crashed
on the surface and was unable to send a
distress call
, as communications through the
Bajoran wormhole
were not possible at the time. By the
24th century
, the planet had become an
inhabited
world with 8,000 people living in scattered settlements on a
southern peninsula
. This
timeline
was erased when the crew of the
Defiant
discovered their
descendants
, and the future version of
Odo
that existed in this time reprogrammed the ship's
flight plan
to avoid the accident, thus giving his other self another chance at
love
with
Kira Nerys
.
(
DS9
: "
Children of Time
")
Inhabitants
See
:
Gaia inhabitants
"GUY-uh" was the pronunciation for this planet's
name
from the
script
pronunciation guide.
[1]
According to the
Star Trek: Star Charts
(pp. 70, 75), Gaia IV was quarantined. The Gaia system was a
binary
system of a
K-class star
and a G-class star. The system was a location visited by the Gamma Quadrant Bio-Survey (
2369
-
75
).
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 290 classified Gaia as a
class M
planet.
External links
Gaia IV
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

, and thus may contain

.
Galador II
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Native species:
Galadorans
Location:
Galador
system
Alpha Quadrant
Galador II
was the
inhabited
second
planet
in the
Galador
system
,
Alpha Quadrant
. This was the
homeworld
of the
Galadorans
.
History
By
2370
,
Galador freighters
regularly operated on a
trade route
between Galador II and
Farius Prime
. (
DS9
: "
The Maquis, Part I
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this system was within the blast radius of one of the mines. (
SA
: "
300th Night
", "
Rubincon
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
"GAL-uh-door" was the pronunciation for this planet's name from the
script
pronunciation guide.
[1]
The position of this planet in the Alpha Quadrant was gleaned from the location of its system which was seen in the
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
episode "
Beta Test
".

at

======================================================================

Galardon
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2381
)
Native species:
Galardonians
Galardonian insect
Spider cow
Location:
Galar system
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Galardon
was the
capital planet
of the
Galar system
and apparent
homeworld
of the
Galardonians
. It was also home to such
fauna
as the
spider cow
and a
stinging
insect
.
First contact
between this planet and the
USS
Quito
occurred in
2379
.
One year later
, the
USS
Cerritos
was sent to the planet to set up a
subspace comm array
during a
second contact
mission. (
LD
: "
Second Contact
")
In
2381
, the
Cerritos
revisited the planet along with the
USS
Aledo
as part of the "
mission race
" to prove the worth of the
California
-class
against the
Aledo
's fully-automated
Texas
-class
. (
LD
: "
The Stars At Night
")

Galdonterre
AKA:
Galdonterre III
Class:
B
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Klingon Empire
Status:

(
23rd century
)
Satellite(s):
Ring system
Location:
Galdonterre system
Beta Quadrant
Galdonterre
, also called
Galdonterre III
, was the
inhabited
third
planet
in the
Galdonterre system
. It was
class B
and possessed a
ring system
.
As of
2260
, the planet was uninhabited. (
SNW
: "
Hegemony, Part II
")
By the end of the
23rd century
, Galdonterre had been
colonized
by the
Klingon Empire
. It was where
the Albino
hid after he exacted his
revenge
against
Kang
,
Kor
, and
Koloth
. The three
Klingon
warriors
and
Curzon Dax
, who had together sworn a
blood oath
to kill the Albino, nearly caught him on the planet. However, the Albino had somehow intercepted their
subspace communications
and escaped before their arrival. (
DS9
: "
Blood Oath
")
The pronunciation of this planet's name is given as "gal-dont-AIR" in the
script
and "ghel-don-TEER" on
StarTrek.com
.
[1]
(X)
The former is used in the episode.

Galdonterre V
Class:

Type:

Galdonterre V
was the
uninhabited
fifth

of the
Galdonterre system
. It was
class D
.
Circa
2260
, the position and
orbital
path of this planet were depicted in a system map aboard the
USS
Enterprise
. (
SNW
: "
Hegemony, Part II
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Galen IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Galen_IV
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Galen IV
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
(
3195
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3195
)
Satellite(s):
None
Native species:
Galen IV natives
Location:
Galen system
Near the
Federation-Talarian border
Alpha Quadrant
Galen IV
was the
inhabited
fourth
planet
of the
Galen system
. Located in the
Alpha Quadrant
, this
Class M
forested
planet had no
moons
and was home to both a native species and a
Federation
colony
. This planet was near the
Federation-Talarian border
.
The Federation had established
first contact
with this planet by
2259
. (
SNW
: "
Strange New Worlds
")
During the late
2350s
, Galen IV was disputed between the Federation and the
Talarian Republic
, who saw the Federation as encroaching on their
territory
. In
2357
, the planet was attacked during the
Galen border conflict
by
Talarian
forces, commanded by
Endar
. All the colonists were believed to have been massacred, the colony destroyed, but a single
survivor
emerged years later as Endar's
adopted
son
Jono
. (
TNG
: "
Suddenly Human
")
In the
32nd century
, Galen IV was a
Federation
member
planet. It was listed with other members on a video wall in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
The Life of the Stars
")
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of the Talarian Republic as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 292 classified Galen IV as a
M-class
planet and located it in the Galen system.
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I") , Galen IV was listed as a Federation member in
2378
.
External link
Galen IV
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

. Located somewhere in the

annex
of the

.

had created another
Soong-type android

" to be sent to Galor IV for study. (

in

.

.

(

or

Galorda Prime
was the
prime
planet
in its
star system
.
In early
2372
, a
Klingon civilian transport ship
with 441 people on board
crashed
in a
mountainous
area of the planet. There were no
survivors
.
Advocate
Ch'Pok
would later use the
crew manifest
as a faked list of
casualties
allegedly caused by the actions of
Worf
while commanding the
USS
Defiant
. (
DS9
: "

")

name
from the
script
pronunciation guide.
[1]
"Rules of Engagement" neither mentions the location nor the affiliation of Galorda Prime, although we might presume it to be within the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrants
.
According to
StarTrek.com
, Galorda Prime was a Klingon planet.

======================================================================
PAGE: Galorndon Core
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Galorndon_Core
======================================================================

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Galorndon Core
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Klingon Empire
(
2257
)
United Federation of Planets
(
2259
)
Status:
Intact
(
2401
)
Satellite(s):
At least
one moon
Location:
Galorndon Core system
Unnamed sector
Beta Quadrant
~0.5
light years
from the
Federation-Romulan border
"
Welcome to Galorndon Core, where no good deed goes unpunished.
"
–
Geordi La Forge
,
2366
("
The Enemy
")
Galorndon Core
(often referred as
the
Galorndon Core
) was a
geologically
unstable
uninhabited
Federation
planet
in the
Galorndon Core
system
. This planet was found in a
sector
of the
Beta Quadrant
. It had at least
one moon
.
The
atmosphere
of the
planet
created severe
electromagnetic storms
with
charged particle
precipitation that interfered with
sensors
and
transporters
. While the atmosphere was capable of supporting
humanoid
lifeforms
, its electromagnetic characteristics had detrimental effects on their
nervous systems
. (
TNG
: "
The Enemy
")
History
Chulak
of
Romulus
suffered a defeat at Galorndon Core sometime prior to
2372
. (
VOY
: "
The Thaw
")
In the
Pocket ENT
novel
To Brave the Storm
, Chulak was a Romulan Commander who attacked
Enterprise
at Galorndon Core during the
Earth-Romulan War
(see Apocrypha).
In
2257
, during the first
Federation-Klingon War
, this planet's system was occupied by the
Klingon Empire
. (
DIS
: "
The War Without, The War Within
") It had returned to
United Federation of Planets
control by
2259
, according to a
stellar cartography
chart that was seen on the
USS
Enterprise
's
ready room
viewscreen
. (
SNW
: "
Strange New Worlds
", "
Spock Amok
", "
A Quality of Mercy
") The system was near the
Federation-Romulan border
, just 0.5 lightyears away. (
SNW
: "
A Quality of Mercy
";
TNG
: "
The Enemy
"; )
In the
alternate
2259
, a mysterious
wreckage
was discovered on the
mountainous
surface
of this planet. This event was reported in a news ticker, which was in a collection of graphics and video media that were seen on a
powerwall
in the
offices
of
Christopher Pike
and
Alexander Marcus
at
Starfleet Headquarters
.
(
Star Trek Into Darkness
)
The ticker can be seen in the video "
Star Trek: Into Darkness
- User Interface VFX" at vimeo.com.
[1]
The planet was still under Federation control in
2366
, by which time it was along the
Barolian
trade route
with the Romulans. (
TNG
: "
The Enemy
", "
Unification II
")
That same
year
, the
Romulan scout ship
Pi
crashed
on this planet and the
USS
Enterprise
-D
was sent to investigate. The
away team
sent to the planet found the scout ship destroyed (deliberately, after it had crashed) and recovered a surviving
crewmember
. Unfortunately,
Lieutenant Commander
Geordi La Forge
was trapped on the surface of the planet with another
Romulan
survivor
,
Centurion
Bochra
. Prolonged exposure of humanoids to the intense
magnetic fields
on Galorndon Core would result in
synaptic
breakdown. Bochra suffered from erratic
metabolism
and loss of sensation in his lower
limbs
, while La Forge became
blind
, even though his
VISOR
worked perfectly. (
TNG
: "
The Enemy
")
According to
Admiral
Alidar Jarok
, this incident at Galorndon Core was common knowledge amongst Romulans. However, he may have exaggerated how well-known the incident was, as he was covering up his true identity as an admiral. (
TNG
: "
The Defector
")
In
2368
, Galorndon Core was the
rendezvous
point where
Ferengi
arms dealer
Omag
delivered the stolen
deflector array
of the
T'Pau
to a
Barolian freighter
, which would later transfer it to the Romulans. In the search for the stolen deflectors, the
Enterprise
-D diverted to the planet from where it later detected three Romulan-controlled
Vulcan ships
, including the
T'Pau
, trying to covertly transport
invasion
troops
across the Romulan Neutral Zone to
Vulcan
. (
TNG
: "
Unification II
")
Appendices
Background information
Galorndon Core as it originally appeared in the un-remastered "Unification II"
"gal-OHRN-dan core" was the pronunciation for this planet's name from the
script
pronunciation guide for "Unification, Part II".
[2]
For the remastered "Unification II", the appearance of Galorndon Core was corrected to match how it appeared in "The Enemy".
[3]
Apocrypha
In the computer game
Star Trek Generations
, Galorndon Core was a major Romulan base of operations and the site of a secret launch point for
Soran
's weapon. The base was headed by a
Commander
Tala, who appears twice in-game and who was helping Soran synthesize
trilithium
from Galorndon Core I.
The
Pocket ENT
novel
To Brave the Storm
depictd Galorndon Core as a
xenoecological
paradise in the
22nd century
until the
Earth-Romulan War
. In a manner similar to the Romulan attack on
Coridan Prime
(as depicted in
Beneath the Raptor's Wing
), Romulan Commander
Chulak
led his ship into a suicidal attack against the
Enterprise
in
2157
. He intentionally collided with the planet's surface at
high warp
, causing a destructive global cascade that rendered the planet uninhabitable.
In
Star Trek Online
, Galorndon Core was located in the Narendra Sector of the Tau Dewa sector block (
β
). A pod of
Gekli
have gotten lost in the system; the player must lead them past
Hirogen
hunting
parties and back to
fluidic space
. In the
Agents of Yesterday
expansion mission, "The Core of the Matter", it was revealed that, like the depiction in
To Brave the Storm
, the planet was actually hospitable, if not a paradise of sorts. It was here that the Romulans and the
Na'kuhl
were in the makings of a partnership with the latter offering them a
planet killer
to become a great power. However, the Na'kuhl betrayed them and Chulak and the player character fights off the Na'kuhl. However, one Na'kuhl ship gets sucked into the machine, causing a chain reaction that sends the weapon spiraling to the planet, rendering it inhospitable.
External links
Galorndon Core
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Re-Used Planets in TNG
at
Ex Astris Scientia

Gamelan V
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2367
)
Native species:
Gamelan V natives
Location:
Gamelan
system
Unnamed sector
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
Gamelan V
was the
inhabited
fifth
planet
in the
Gamelan
system
. This
Class M
world was located in a
sector
of the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
This was the
homeworld
of a peaceful
native
humanoid
species
. This planet had no
planetary defense system
and was headed by a
chairman
.
In
2367
, an ancient
unmanned sublight freighter
carrying toxic waste was pulled into
orbit
around Gamelan V, and began leaking massive
radiation
into the
atmosphere
. Ambient radiation levels increased by upwards of 3,000
%
.
Chairman
Songi
, the planet's
leader
, contacted the
USS
Enterprise
-D
, under the
command
of
William Riker
, for help. The
Enterprise
-D was able to save the planet by
tractoring
the barge into the
sun
. (
TNG
: "
Final Mission
")
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 295 classified Gamelan V as a
M-class
planet.
"GAM-uh-lin" was the pronunciation for this planet's name from the
script
pronunciation guide.
[1]

at

homeworld
for the Gamma Argus II natives, a
pre-warp

. In

, full
Prime Directive
protections were in force for this planet.

year
, the

starships

Callisto
,

-D
, and

Mustang
, acting under
orders
from
Starfleet
, observed the cultural evolution of this planet. Furthermore, according to those orders, the ship's
captains
,
Seth Goldberg
,
Jimmy Arakaki
, and/or
Jean-Luc Picard
, had the discretion to determine the desirability of making contact with the
culture
. The captain would be acting as a
representative
on behalf of the
United Federation of Planets
. (

: "

",
okudagram

sector

~134

from

Gamma Hydra II
was the
second
planet
in the

system
. This planet was located in the
Beta Quadrant
and was approximately 134
light years
from
Sol
. This planet was located in both the administrative area of
Starbase 10
and the
Gamma Hydra
sector
.
When
crewmembers
of the
USS
Enterprise
, including
Captain
Kirk
, were afflicted by
radiation
on

, causing them to age rapidly, Kirk several times mistakenly stated that the
Enterprise
orbited
Gamma Hydra II because of the decreased mental capabilities due to his "

". (

's

======================================================================
PAGE: Gamma Hydra IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Gamma_Hydra_IV
======================================================================

Gamma Hydra IV
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2267
)
Location:
Gamma Hydra
system
Gamma Hydra
sector
Beta Quadrant
~134
light years
from
Sol
Gamma Hydra IV
was the
uninhabited
fourth
planet
in the
Gamma Hydra
system
. This planet was located in the
Beta Quadrant
and was approximately 134
light years
from
Sol
. This planet was located in both the administrative area of
Starbase 10
and the
Gamma Hydra sector
. It was a conventional
M-class
planet with an
oxygen
-
nitrogen
atmosphere
and a normal
mass
.
In the mid-
23rd century
, this planet was the home for an experimental
science
colony
.
In
2267
, Gamma Hydra IV was
irradiated
by a
rogue comet
,
killing
all six colonists by an unknown
radiation
type which accelerated the ageing of anyone on the
surface
. The
leader
of the colony was
Robert Johnson
, who was
married
to colonist
Elaine Johnson
.
The planet was visited for the annual examination of the science expedition by a
landing party
from the
USS
Enterprise
, but four colonists were already dead, and the Johnson couple was dying. Five of the six members of the
landing party
to Gamma Hydra IV were afflicted by the same radiation; all except one of those five could be, and subsequently were, healed from the effects. (The one of those five who could not and was not healed,
Lieutenant
Arlene Galway
, aged too rapidly and died too soon for the treatment formulated to be used on her.) (
TOS
: "
The Deadly Years
")
See also
Gamma Hydra II
Gamma Hydra IV colonists
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the location of its system to the nearby
Sigma Nelvana
's
system
, whose location was seen in the
star charts
appearing in the
Star Trek: The Next Generation
episode "
The Defector
" and in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".

at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

at

======================================================================
PAGE: Gamma Tauri IV
CATEGORIES: Planets

======================================================================

Gamma Tauri IV
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Intact
(
2364
)
Location:
Gamma Tauri
system
Alpha Quadrant
~154
light years
from
Sol
Gamma Tauri IV
was the
uninhabited
fourth
planet
in the
Gamma Tauri
system
. The planet was located in the
Alpha Quadrant
and was approximately 154
light years
from
Sol
. (
TNG
: "
The Last Outpost
").
History
In
2260
, this planet was within the area of effect of a
quantum uncertainty field
. (
SNW
: "
Subspace Rhapsody
")
Sometime prior to
2364
, the
Federation
established an unmanned monitor
outpost
on this planet. In 2364, the
images
of a
Ferengi
D'Kora
-class
warship
were captured on the outpost's
automatic scanners
as a
T-9 energy converter
was heisted by crew members of the
starship
. Alerted by the
theft
, the starship
USS
Enterprise
-D
was tasked with pursuing the warship by
Starfleet
. During
negotiations
with the Ferengi,
DaiMon
Tarr
of the
Ferengi
claimed this system was in Ferengi territory, a claim dismissed by
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
of the
Enterprise
-D. Later, with the persuasion of the
Portal
, the Ferengi returned the converter to the Federation. (
TNG
: "
The Last Outpost
")
In the
2370s
, this planet was near the
Federation-Cardassian border
. (
DS9
: "
Tears of the Prophets
",
okudagram
).
Stellar Cartography
In
2374
, the
location
of Gamma Tauri IV was labeled on a tactical map of the plan for the
First Battle of Chin'toka
. (
DS9
: "
Tears of the Prophets
",
okudagram
)
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Strange New Worlds
episode "
Children of the Comet
".
External link
Gamma Tauri IV
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

light years
from

was the

in the

. The planet was located within the

of the

and was approximately 112.3

2369

Federation

, a
lieutenant commander
aboard the

======================================================================
PAGE: Gamma Trianguli VI
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Gamma_Trianguli_VI
======================================================================

Gamma Trianguli VI
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2328
)
Native species:
Vaalians
Pod plants
Location:
Gamma Trianguli system
Gamma Trianguli
sector
Alpha Quadrant
~112
light years
from
Sol
Gamma Trianguli VI
was the
inhabited
sixth
planet
in the
Gamma Trianguli system
. This
Class M
planet was located within the
Gamma Trianguli
sector
of the
Alpha Quadrant
and was approximately 112
light years
from
Sol
.
This was the
homeworld
of the
Vaalians
, a
pre-warp
humanoid
species
. This was, also, the home of the
pod plants
.
Vaal
, a computer-machine with rudimentary intelligence, controlled this planet and its inhabitants for approximately
ten thousand years
until
2267
. (
TOS
: "
The Apple
";
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
,
okudagram
;
ENT
: "
In A Mirror, Darkly, Part II
",
display graphic
)
Astronomical data
The planet, described as an "
Eden
"-like
paradise
, consistently enjoyed a tropical climate of 76
°F
, with only minor variation at the
poles
. These conditions were previously thought to be impossible and were only made possible under the direct environmental control of Vaal, which extended deep beneath the planet's
surface
over the entire globe. The
atmosphere
was
oxygen
-
nitrogen
. The majority of the
flora
was very attractive. The
soil
was remarkably rich and fertile, excellent for husbandry. Its
atmosphere
filtered out all harmful
radiation
from its
sun
. Aside from the natives, no
animal
life was encountered by initial surveys. Vaal was obviously constructed on Gamma Trianguli VI by a
species
to care for the native inhabitants for some unknown purpose, although the natives had no memory or historical record of this.
History
The Federation had established
first contact
with this planet by
2259
. This civilization had developed warp travel and had or was in the process of being inducted into the Federation by this year. (
SNW
: "
Strange New Worlds
")
Although an Eden at first glance, under Vaal's control the planet soon proved to be the opposite. When a
USS
Enterprise
landing party
beamed
down on a survey mission, they were immediately assaulted by the planet's dangerous defense mechanisms. Deadly
flora
, artificial lightning strikes, and
explosive mineral formations
killed three crewmembers and injured a fourth. The initially friendly natives, at Vaal's behest, also became hostile and assaulted the landing party, killing a fourth crewmember. Vaal itself also began pulling the
Enterprise
out of
orbit
, prompting
Captain
James T. Kirk
to take retaliatory action and destroy Vaal. After its destruction Kirk predicted a hard time for the natives, but eventually they would learn to organize their lives themselves. (
TOS
: "
The Apple
")
The planet's name on the manifest
In
2328
, this was the point of embarkation for several individuals.
Zayra Cabot
traveled to
Tarsus IV
on the
SS
Manoa
,
Eric Bruno
and
Douglas Drexler
traveled to
Gothos Space Center
on the
SS
Kogin
,
Jack Litchfeld
traveled to
Tarsus IV
on the
Kogin
,
Daryl Baskin
traveled to Gothos Space Center on the
SS
Wisconsin
, and
David Stipes
and
Lorine Mendell
traveled to Tarsus IV on the
Wisconsin
. (
TNG-R
: "
Inheritance
",
okudagram
)
Stellar cartography
In
2293
, the location of this planet was labeled in a
star chart
found on the
navigation console
of the
USS
Enterprise
-B
. (
Star Trek Generations
)
In
2365
, the location of Gamma Trianguli VI was labeled in a Subspace Comm Net Ops star chart, which was seen in the
courtroom
on
Starbase 173
. This planet was connected to
Capella
by a subspace line. (
TNG
: "
The Measure Of A Man
")
Appendices
Background information
In August 2024, an annotated computer paste-up of this star chart was put up for auction in the Entertainment Memorabiliia Live Auction: Los Angeles Summer 2024 by Propstore Auction. On the chart, Gamma Trianguli and Gamma Trianguli VI are two separate, unique locations.
[1]
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 296 classified Gamma Trianguli VI as a
class M
planet with a tropical climate.
Apocrypha
In the
TOS
-
VOY
crossover
novel
No Time Like the Past
, Kirk and a space and temporally displaced
Seven of Nine
visited this planet to find one of four pieces of an
artifact
. Seven was transported by the piece she already possessed to the planet's past, not long after Vaal had been put into operation.
External links
Gamma Trianguli VI
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Planets in TOS and TOS Remastered
at
Ex Astris Scientia

. This system was located within

in the

.
In

, during the

,

forces forced the

to retreat from Ganalda IV.

on

theorized this loss to be responsible for the Klingons' increased
bloodlust
. (

: "

")

Background information
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.

appearing in the

episode "

" and in the

episode "

".

from the

pronunciation guide.
[1]

that was threatened with being attacked by a Klingon

in

.

considered sending the

to the defense of this colony.

Garadius IV
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2368
)
Location:
Garadius
system
Beta Quadrant
~91
light years
from
Sol
Garadius IV
was the
inhabited
fourth
planet
in the
Garadius
system
. This planet was located in the
Beta Quadrant
and was approximately 91
light years
from
Sol
.
In
2368
, the
USS
Enterprise
-D
was on the way to this planet, when the ship helped a disabled
Romulan
science ship
. After finishing the repairs of the
science ship
, the
Enterprise
received a message stating that the
diplomatic
situation on Garadius IV was deteriorating, and they were needed there as soon as possible. Just before going to
warp
en route to Garadius IV, which would have destroyed the ship due to the Romulans secretly transferring
muons
to the
dilithium chamber
, the ship was saved – by
Geordi La Forge
and
Ro Laren
, who had been accidentally "
cloaked
", thrown out of phase, and once restored told the crew of the Romulan plot. (
TNG
: "
The Next Phase
")
Appendices
Background information
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
The location of this planet was inferred from the position of its star system in the Beta Quadrant as seen on a
star chart
from the
Star Trek: Strange New Worlds
episode "
A Quality of Mercy
".
Apocrypha
The events of the Garadius IV mission are referenced in the
eBook
Absent Enemies
.

Garadius IV
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

A
garbage dump
was a place where
garbage
was
stored
.

2154
,

was less than enthused about having perused a
Klingon
garbage dump. (
ENT
: "

")
In
2369
,

quipped that
Bopak III
smelled like a garbage dump. (

: "
Hippocratic Oath
")

PAGE: Garon II
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Garon_II
======================================================================

Garon II
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2360s
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Garon II
was the
second
planet
of its
star system
. This planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
The
USS
Wellington
experienced a disastrous
away mission
at Garon II in the mid-
2360s
. During the
mission
,
Lieutenant
Ro Laren
disobeyed
direct orders
, resulting in the
deaths
of
eight of her fellow officers
. Ro was subsequently
court martialed
for her actions. She was
demoted
to the
rank
of
ensign
and sentenced to imprisonment in the
stockade
on
Jaros II
. (
TNG
: "
Ensign Ro
", "
Conundrum
")
During Ro's
memorial service
in
2368
,
Jean-Luc Picard
stated that if she hadn't lost her rank as a result of the incident, he felt she would have been a
lieutenant commander
. (
TNG
: "
The Next Phase
")
Appendices
Background information
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
"GAH-run" was the pronunciation for this planet's name from the
script
pronunciation guide for "Ensign Ro".
[1]
In the remastered "Conundrum", the incident on Garon II was mentioned as a note in Ensign Ro Laren's
personnel file
from the Crew Manifest Database.
In "The Next Phase", Picard noted that the incident resulting in Ro's demotion took place on "Garon IV" rather than "Garon II."
External link
Garon II
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Gault
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Gault
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Gault
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
(
3195
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3195
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
For the similarly named planet, please see
Galt (planet)
.
"
Gault is a
farming
colony!
"
–
Korris
,
2364
("
Heart of Glory
")
Gault
was an
inhabited
class M
planet
that was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
. This was the home of a
Federation
colony
with a sparse
population
of no more than 20,000 inhabitants. It was widely regarded as a
farmworld
. (
TNG
: "
Heart of Glory
";
DS9
: "
Let He Who Is Without Sin...
")
In
2346
,
Sergey
and
Helena Rozhenko
lived on Gault with their
son
,
Nikolai
; the following
year
, their
adopted
son
Worf
went to live there. Both left to attend
Starfleet Academy
when they came of age; Nikolai hated it and returned to Gault instead. (
TNG
: "
Heart of Glory
")
One of the
secondary schools
on Gault had a
soccer
team which Worf
captained
in
2353
at the age of thirteen. Worf headed a
ball
into the goal and accidentally killed the opposing
player
named
Mikel
. (
TNG
: "
Homeward
";
DS9
: "
Let He Who Is Without Sin...
")
In the
32nd century
, Gault was a
Federation
member
planet. It was listed with other members on a video wall in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
The Life of the Stars
")
Appendices
Background information
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
"GALT" was the pronunciation for this planet's
name
from the
script
pronunciation guide for "Let He Who Is Without Sin".
[1]
At
StarTrek.com
, Gault was classified as a
M-class
planet.
[2]
(X)
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I"), Gault was a Federation member in
2378
.
External link
Gault
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Gedi Prime
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Gedi_Prime
======================================================================

Gedi Prime
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
24th century
)
Location:
Milky Way Galaxy
Gedi Prime
was the
inhabited
prime
planet
in its
star system
. This
vacation
planet
was touted as making
Risa
look like a "
tourist trap
". It featured
kilometers
of
crystalline
beaches
,
mood reefs
,
bioluminescent
waterfalls
, and a championship
golf course
.
In
2377
,
B'Elanna Torres
planned a
romantic
getaway for herself and
Tom Paris
in a
holographic
re-creation of Gedi Prime, but Paris became involved in preparing for the
Antarian Trans-stellar Rally
and forgot. (
VOY
: "
Drive
")
Later that
year
,
The Doctor
asked a holographic
Reginald Barclay
if he wanted to play
golf
on the "back nine" on Gedi Prime or
Pebble Beach
at sunset. (
VOY
: "
Inside Man
")
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 300 postulates that the name was a tip of the hat to
Frank Herbert
's
Dune
novels.

======================================================================
PAGE: Gemulon V
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Gemulon_V
======================================================================

Gemulon V
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Location:
Gemulon
system
Venari Ral Zone
Alpha Quadrant
Gemulon V
was the
uninhabited
fifth
planet
in the
Gemulon
system
,
Alpha Quadrant
. This planet was in the
Venari Ral Zone
.
History
Gemulon V was to be the home of a
Federation
colony
in the late
2350s
, but the
SS
Santa Maria
never reached its destination. Officially, it developed
life support
problems while traveling through the
Orellius system
and was forced to
crash land
on a planet, where the would-be colonists made a home for themselves before finally being discovered by
Benjamin Sisko
and
Miles O'Brien
over ten
years
later.
Unofficially,
Alixus
had never intended for the vessel to reach Gemulon V, and its true destination was always meant to be the uninhabited
planet
in the Orellius system. (
DS9
: "
Paradise
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
"GEHM-you-lahn" was the pronunciation for this planet's name from the
script
pronunciation guide.
[1]
However, "GHEMM-you-lon" was the pronunciation for this planet from
StarTrek.com
. This website stated that this planet, presumably
M-class
,
orbited
the
star
Gemulon. This star was located in a Federation
sector
adjacent to the
Bajor sector
.
[2]
(X)
[3]
(X)
External link
Gemulon V
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

.

======================================================================
PAGE: Genesis (planet)
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Genesis_(planet)
======================================================================

Genesis
Type:
Planet
Status:
Destroyed
(
2285
)
Satellite(s):
None
Native species:
A species of worm
Location:
Genesis sector
Mutara sector
Beta Quadrant
"
My God, Carol. Look at it.
"
–
James T. Kirk
to Carol Marcus
,
2285
(
Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan
)
The
Genesis Planet
was an
uninhabited
planet
located in the
Genesis
sector
. This sector was a
subsector
within the
Mutara sector
,
Beta Quadrant
. When the
Genesis Device
exploded, which resulted in the destruction of the
USS
Reliant
, the planet was formed from the coalescence and cooling of the
gas
and
dust
of the
Mutara Nebula
. The
explosion
occurred at the conclusion of a
fierce battle
between
Khan Noonien Singh
and
Admiral
James T. Kirk
in
2285
. (
Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan
;
Star Trek III: The Search for Spock
)
History
Genesis' creation
Following the initial creation of the planet,
Captain
Spock
's
photon torpedo
casing was launched towards it, following his
funeral service
. Because
gravitational fields
were in flux, his casing soft-landed on the planet's
surface
. (
Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan
;
Star Trek III: The Search for Spock
)
The Genesis Planet immediately became a galactic controversy, and
Starfleet
declared it off limits until the
Federation Council
could decide what to do. In the meantime, the
USS
Grissom
was dispatched with a scientific team that included the planet's co-creator,
Dr
.
David Marcus
, to carry out a thorough investigation. While exploring the planet with
Saavik
, Marcus admitted that he'd used
protomatter
to complete the Genesis matrix. The unstable and dangerous properties of the protomatter were a contributing factor in the planet's rapid aging and instability.
Genesis' final sunrise
A battle
occurred in
orbit
during the final degradation of the planet in which both the USS
Grissom
and the
USS
Enterprise
were destroyed and a
Klingon
Bird-of-Prey
HMS
Bounty
was commandeered by Admiral Kirk and his remaining crew. (
Star Trek III: The Search for Spock
)
The Bird-of-Prey departs from Genesis as it disintegrates
Three
months
later, in an angry tirade directed at the
Federation Council
and the
Federation President
, the
Klingon Ambassador
alleged that this planet would have housed a secret base from which Kirk would have launched a genocidal campaign using the Genesis Device against the Klingons. His allegations were debunked by
Sarek
, who pointed out that Genesis was named for creation of life and that the Klingons shed the first blood in trying to possess its secrets. (
Star Trek IV: The Voyage Home
)
Ecology
Early evolution of space microbes
Later evolution of space microbes
Initial scans of the new planet, from the
Grissom
, indicated several fascinating revelations, including "all the varieties of
Earth
's land and
weather
, within a few
hours
' walk," according to Dr. Marcus.
Sector 1: Had foliage in fully-developed state of growth;
temperature
: 22.2
°Celsius
Sector 2: Had
desert
terrain. Minimal
vegetation
; temperature: 39.4 °Celsius
Sector 3: Had subtropical vegetation; temperature decreasing rapidly, indications of
snow
Diverse ecology
The same effect that hyper-evolved the
plantlife
on the planet seemed to have had the same effect on
microbes
on the
surface
of the burial
photon torpedo
that soft landed on the planet shortly after it was created. The microbes evolved into multicellular organisms in a very short period of time.
Unstable
protomatter
, used in the matrix's construction by David Marcus in his haste to complete the Genesis Project, caused the
ecosystems
it had created to become dangerously erratic. This instability manifested itself first in an accelerated rate of growth, then in micro-climatic upheavals. Instead of flourishing, the planet began to experience heavy geologic activity that resulted in the entire surface becoming molten. (
Star Trek III: The Search for Spock
)
Appendices
Background information
This planet's quadrant of origin is inferred based on the position of Spock's last known position in the Mutara sector (
DIS
: "
Light and Shadows
") as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Discovery
episode "
An Obol for Charon
".
A lot of Genesis space microbes were sold off on the
It's A Wrap! sale and auction
on eBay.
[1]
(X)
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 301 classified the Genesis Planet as a
class M
planet.
Apocrypha
In the
Genesis Wave
series, Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
reads a
report
on the Genesis project.  The report speculates that it was not the fact that protomatter was used, but instead the circumstances in which the torpedo had been detonated that caused the destruction of the planet.  The report stated the Genesis Planet collapsed because it was formed from a nebula and the device had to build an entire solar system with a G-type star and ran out of energy while forming the planet.  Also the device had created two gas giants which caused gravitational stress to the Genesis Planet.  The report concluded by suggesting that Genesis would have worked if it had been fired at a planet as it was originally intended.
External link
Genesis Planet
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

Ghorusda
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Non-aligned
Status:
Habitable
(
24th century

Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Ghorusda
was an
inhabited
planet
and the
homeworld
of the
Ghorusdans
. This planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
It was the
location
of a
conflict
between the Ghorusdans and the
Federation
on a
first contact
mission
during which
forty-seven
crewmembers
of the
USS
Adelphi
were
killed
. (
TNG
: "
Tin Man
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
"gor-OOSH-dah" was the pronunciation for this planet's
name
from the
script
pronunciation guide.

======================================================================
PAGE: Gideon (planet)
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Gideon_(planet)
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Gideon
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
(
3195
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3195
)
Native species:
Gideons
Location:
Gideon system
Alpha Quadrant
Gideon
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Gideon system
. This
Class M
planet was located in the
Alpha Quadrant
and was the
homeworld
for the
Gideons
, a
warp-capable
humanoid
species
.
The head of the planetary
government
, known as the
Gideon Council
, was a
prime minister
. In
2268
, the
Ambassador
of Gideon was
Hodin
.
History
The
Federation Bureau of Planetary Treaties
tried for many
years
to send
Federation
ambassadors
to visit the
planet
in an attempt to persuade them to join the Federation.
Gideon finally consented to the Bureau's request in 2268. The physio-cultural
reports
submitted by Gideon to the
Federation
described environmental, physical, and cultural conditions that indicated that the planet was a literal paradise. This was, in part, due to the planet's
germ
-free
atmosphere
, which – combined with their love of life – lead to the inhabitants' ability to regenerate their
bodies
and increase their
life spans
.
With the absence of
disease
, the
birth rate
far exceeded the planet's
death rate
.
Death
only occurred when an
individual
's body could no longer regenerate itself. By the mid-
23rd century
, the
population
explosion on Gideon inevitably saturated the planet to a point of being a living mass of individuals. To keep this fact secret, the planet had
screens
placed around it so as to prevent ships from scanning the
surface
. They also made the specific request that no scan attempts were to be made by
orbiting
vessels
.
When the
USS
Enterprise
finally arrived on
stardate 5423.4
to conduct
negotiations
, the Gideon Council wished only to allow a single Federation
representative
to visit the planet:
James T. Kirk
. The Gideon Council, aware that Kirk was a carrier for
Vegan choriomeningitis
, chose him in a radical attempt to solve their overpopulation problem.
Ultimately, Kirk was used as a means to introduce the
disease
, on a voluntary basis, to the planet's population. This was done in an attempt to introduce death back into their
society
and to end their malaise caused by overpopulation. (
TOS
: "
The Mark of Gideon
")
In the
32nd century
, Gideon was a
Federation
member
planet. It was listed with other members on a video wall in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
The Life of the Stars
")
Appendices
Background information
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 304 classified Gideon as a
class M
planet.
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I"), Gideon was a Federation member in
2378
.
Apocrypha
In
The Worlds of the Federation
, prior to the events of
The Mark of Gideon
, the planet's population exceeded that of the entire
United Federation of Planets
, several hundred billion; the introduction of disease killed more than 99% of the existing population, leaving only a few hundred thousand.
External links
Gideon (planet)
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Planets in TOS and TOS Remastered
at
Ex Astris Scientia

Gigean

Milky Way Galaxy
.
Security guard
Pierre of Gigean
was from here. (
PRO
: "
Mindwalk
")
This location was only mentioned in writing.
This location was mentioned in association with Pierre of Gigean in an operations panel on the
USS
Dauntless
bridge
. A clear view of the panel was provided by Dominique Rossier of Wardenlight Studio.
[1]

sector

Bajoran wormhole
by a line. (
DS9
: "
Duet

.

======================================================================
PAGE: Glish
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Glish
======================================================================

Glish
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2381
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Glish
was a
planet
.
In
2381
,
Lieutenant jg
Beckett Mariner
was assigned to lead
Ensigns
Asif
,
Karavitus
, and
Amadou
on an
away mission
to Glish. However, en route their
shuttlecraft
Kings Canyon
encountered a
magnetic anomaly
and
crashed
inside a
cave
. In order to obtain
pergium
with which to restore the shuttle's
power
, they needed to negotiate the
biotemporal flux
from a
mineral
vein that emitted
chronitons
. (
LD
: "
Caves
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Goja V
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Goja_V
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Goja V
Type:
Asteroid
Affiliation:
Independent
Status:
Inhabited
(
3196
)
Location:
Goja system
Betazoid space
Alpha Quadrant
Goja V
was an
inhabited
asteroid
, and the
fifth
planet
in the
Goja
system
,
Alpha Quadrant
.
History
When the
Betazoids
erected a
psionic wall
around
Betazoid space
in the
3090s
, this asteroid was inside the barrier. Due to its concealment, it was unknown to
Starfleet
databases
until
3195
. The settlement there was mostly controlled by
gangs
, with no central
authority
. (
SA
: "
Beta Test
")
After breaking out of
prison
in
3194
,
Nus Braka
and
Anisha Mir
parted ways on Goja V, with Mir fooling Braka into believing she had been killed trying to escape. When Braka confronted her
son
Caleb
a year later
, he told him that it was "
a damn shame, what happened to her
" there. (
SA
: "
Kids These Days
", "
300th Night
")
Caleb was eventually able to locate Goja V by being granted access to the
Betazed governmental star charts
by
Tarima Sadal
.
Captain
Nahla Ake
subsequently informed him that
Starfleet Intelligence
had reached Goja V and discovered Anisha to have departed for the
Romov Sector
three
months
prior. (
SA
: "
Beta Test
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet's system was within the enclosed region. (
SA
: "
300th Night
", "
Rubincon
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Golana
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Golana
======================================================================

Golana
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2374
)
Native species:
Golanans
(
extinct
)
Location:
Alpha Quadrant
For the similarly named
Delta Quadrant
planet, please see
Gotana
.
Golana
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Alpha Quadrant
. This was the home of a
Bajoran
colony
since the beginning of the
24th century
.
Archaeologists
from the
Bajoran Archaeological Institute
were studying the ruins of a
technologically
advanced
civilization
that had gone
extinct
over
two thousand years
before.
In
2372
,
Miles O'Brien
and
Keiko O'Brien
visited this planet. At the time, Keiko was
pregnant
with
Kirayoshi O'Brien
.
In
2374
, the O'Briens visited the planet again. After running off into a
cave
,
Molly O'Brien
discovered an active
time portal
from this lost civilization and fell in. The portal sent Molly
three hundred years
into the past, when the planet was uninhabited. She was recovered after having experienced 10
years
all by herself, and returned a different
person
than she once was.
As she was unable to re-acclimate to her former life, her
parents
made the difficult decision to return her to Golana's past, where she was happy and safe. Fortunately, older Molly encountered young Molly again only moments after her fall and sent her younger self back to her
family
.
The planet had an indigenous
species
of small
melons
called
Golana melons
. (
DS9
: "
Time's Orphan
")
Molly's
drawings
suggested the planet had
two moons
.
According to the
script
, this planet's
name
was pronounced as "goh-LA-nah". It was simply described as "an earth-like planet".
[1]
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 307 classified Golana as a
class M
planet. Furthermore, the
authors
of this
reference work
state the Bajorans colonized this planet around the year
2299
.
External link
Golana
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

. This planet was the
homeworld
for a species of
mermaids
.
Q, Junior
suggested to
Icheb
that they take a
dip
with these mermaids, during a brief visit to their system in
2378
. (
VOY
: "
Q2
")
This planet was mentioned in dialogue, and possibly represented by one of the planets seen from the
Delta Flyer
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Goralis III
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Goralis_III
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Goralis III
Type:
Planet
Location:
Goralis
system
Alpha Quadrant
Goralis III
was the
uninhabited
third
planet
in the
Goralis
system
,
Alpha Quadrant
. The planet was near the
Badlands
and was considered to be far from
Breen
space
. It had a
breathable
atmosphere
and
plant
and
animal
life
.
History
In
2375
, the
runabout
Gander
, carrying
Lieutenant
Ezri Dax
and
Lieutenant Commander
Worf
, was destroyed in the
atmosphere
of this planet following an attack by two
Jem'Hadar attack ships
. Fortunately, Dax and Worf were able to
transport
to the planet's
surface
prior to the runabout's destruction.
During their
time
on Goralis, Worf and Dax's relationship grew to a boiling point, and an argument between the two ultimately led to their sleeping together. This new development in their relationship was cut short, however, when they were captured by the Breen shortly thereafter. (
DS9
: "
Penumbra
")
Stellar cartography
In
2374
, the location of Goralis was labeled on a tactical
map
that was on display in the
wardroom
of
Deep Space 9
. (
DS9
: "
Tears of the Prophets
",
okudagram
)
In
3196
, this planet's system was shown to be in or near to the
Venari Ral Zone
on
Federation
star charts
. (
SA
: "
300th Night
", "
Rubincon
")
This planet was referred to by its full name in
DS9
: "
Strange Bedfellows
". "gore-ah-luss" was the pronunciation for this planet's name from the script pronunciation guide for "Penumbra".
[1]
In the first draft script of
DS9
: "
When It Rains...
",
Gowron
notified
Admiral
Ross
and
Captain
Sisko
that he planned for the
Sixth Fleet
to engage the
Dominion
at Goralis.
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of the Cardassian Union as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
External link
Goralis
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

Gorath
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Klingon Empire
Status:
Intact
(
32nd century
)
Location:
Gorath system
Klingon space
Klingon Zone
Beta Quadrant
~115
light years
from
Sol
Gorath
was a
Klingon
planet
in the
Gorath
system
of the
Beta Quadrant
. In the
32nd century
, this planet was located in the
Klingon Zone
. It was approximately 115
light years
from
Sol
.
There were
sulfur
lagoons on Gorath, and on the
Day of Honor
, a
warrior
must traverse them as part of the
ritual
. (
VOY
: "
Day of Honor
")
Appendices
Background information
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
This planet's quadrant of origin is inferred based on the position of its star as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Discovery
episode "
Magic to Make the Sanest Man Go Mad
" and in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
Apocrypha
Gorath is also the
name
of a character from a Klingon fable called "The Legend of Gorath", as told in the
StarTrek.com
exclusive animation
FF
: "
The Young Hunter
". This Gorath was a contemporary of
Kahless
.

Gorath system
at
