======================================================================
PAGE: Gothos
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Gothos
======================================================================

Gothos
Type:
Artificial planet
Affiliation:
Trelane
Status:
Habitable
(
2328
)
Location:
Star desert
Quadrant 904
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
900
light years
from
Earth
"
Welcome to an island of peace on my stormy little planet of Gothos.
"
–
Trelane
,
2267
("
The Squire of Gothos
")
Gothos
was an
inhabited
iron
-
silica
planet
-sized body of a
magnitude
1
E
which was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
. This planet was discovered in
2267
by the
USS
Enterprise
in a
star desert
within
Quadrant 904
. The planet was over nine hundred
light years
from
Earth
. As an
artificial planet
created by the
entity
Trelane
, Gothos had no
primary
and a variable spatial position.
History
In 2267, when the USS
Enterprise
was first intercepted by this body, the
starship
was eight
days
away from
Beta VI
at
warp factor
three.
The normal
surface
of the planet had no detectable
soil
or
vegetation
, an extremely hot,
toxic
atmosphere
swept by
tornadic storms
, and continuous
volcanic action
–
deadly
to terrestrial and most other sorts of
life
without
oxygen
and
life support
.
In reality, Gothos was artificially constructed by the
entity
Trelane
sometime around 2267. Trelane manufactured the world, and various
artifacts
on it, for the purpose of interacting with
individuals
kidnapped
from the
Enterprise
. Because Gothos was 900 light-years from Earth, Trelane viewed Earth as it was centuries ago.
Upon
beaming
down to the surface, a
landing party
consisting of
McCoy
,
Jaeger
, and
DeSalle
discovered an oasis-like condition – flourishing
vegetation
, standard Earth
atmosphere
, and a large
castle
-like structure, all manufactured by Trelane.
Although most of Trelane's tricks were accomplished with the help of his
mirror machine
, a "medium of instrumentality," Spock theorized that the local conditions were maintained by a different
machine
– a machine too large to fit within
Trelane's house
. Evidently, this was true, since the atmospheric conditions persisted after
Kirk
destroyed the mirror machine.
Trelane was able to move Gothos at
speeds
fast enough to outpace the
Enterprise
's flight. The method used to accomplish this was not revealed, but the planet was sufficiently maneuverable to thwart standard evasive tactics. After Trelane's parents said it was time to come in, they told Kirk they would sustain the planet's life support conditions before he left. (
TOS
: "
The Squire of Gothos
")
In
2328
, this planet was home to the
Gothos Space Center
. (
TNG-R
: "
Inheritance
")
Stellar cartography
In
2293
, the location of this planet was labeled in a
star chart
found on the
navigation console
of the
USS
Enterprise
-B
. (
Star Trek Generations
)
Appendices
Background information
Despite being pushed through
space
and described as a "lone planet" by Spock, Kirk ordered Sulu to take them "[out of] their
solar system
", suggesting this planet was in
orbit
of a
star
instead of a
rogue planet
.
Indeed, in an early story outline for "The Squire of Gothos" (dated
11 August
1966
), the planet Gothos was part of a solar system, which suddenly started to be detected 1,000 light years from Earth and emitted an incredibly powerful "super-gravity" force that pulled the
Enterprise
into the star system. Gothos was relatively large and was described as an "odd-looking, crazy-quilt planet."
In the writer's first draft
script
of "The Squire of Gothos", Gothos was still referred to as part of a solar system, featuring one star. With a magnitude of one and orbited by a
moon
, it was a
hydrogen
-
helium
"radio-star", which the script's stage directions described as "a brilliant sun, with crescented planets orbiting it." The script notes went on to describe Gothos as "one of the
cloud
-shrouded spheres."
However, upon being visually introduced in later drafts of the episode's script (both the first draft, dated
18 October
1966
, and the final draft, dated
26 October
1966), this planet was instead described as "a brilliant, crescented planet." Additionally, Gothos was no longer firmly established as being part of a solar system. The fact that "The Squire of Gothos" was written while rogue planets were not yet known to exist led
Kellam de Forest
to remark (in a series of notes he submitted on 26 October 1966), "
If this artificially created body has
no sun
, that is the scientific impossibility which will be the first item to be questioned by the crew of the
Enterprise."
A line of dialogue in the script (specifically, in the first draft and the final draft) referred to Gothos as an "iron-silica planet, magnitude One-E." In his notes, Kellam de Forest stated, "In absence of sun, this object would not be termed 'planet,' and lacking knowledge of its precise nature would be called 'object' or 'body.' The term 'magnitude' is used in reference to
stars
and is a measurement of brightness. Suggest: Designation One-E.
" The description from the episode's teleplay was slightly altered in a script revision page (dated
28 October
1966), which instead referred to the subject as an "iron-silica body, planet-sized magnitude One-E," which is how the line is also phrased in the final version of the episode.
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 22), Gothos was an example of an
A-class planet
under normal circumstances.
Apocrypha
In the
Star Trek Maps
(p. 26), Gothos was a rogue class H
planetoid
. It was listed as a prohibited planetoid, due to the dangerous life form that may be present on the surface.
External links
Gothos
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Re-Used Planets in TOS
at
Ex Astris Scientia

.

") and

("

======================================================================
PAGE: Graviton catapult
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Graviton_catapult
======================================================================

Tash's catapult locked on to Tash's starship
"
Can we ask you what it's supposed to do?
"
"
Catapult a vessel across space in the time it takes to say 'Catapult a vessel across space'.
"
–
Chakotay
and
Tash
,
2376
("
The Voyager Conspiracy
")
A
graviton catapult
was a unique form of
technology
, designed by an
alien
named
Tash
, that was created to
propel
a
spacecraft
across interstellar
space
.
Design
Entry point of graviton catapult
Profile of graviton catapult
The
power core
of the catapult generated a massive
graviton surge
that locked onto a ship through an array of projectors. The surge sent the ship hurtling into
null space
for some hours. Upon emerging back to normal space, the vessel had jumped many hundreds or even thousands of
light years
. The distance was determined by the
graviton field
strength. (
VOY
: "
The Voyager Conspiracy
")
History
In
2376
,
USS
Voyager
discovered a stranded
alien
starship
, commanded by
Tash
. Their crew had built such a device as a means of getting home in a shorter period of time than by
warp drive
. The first test had destabilized the
tetryon
reactions of the
tetryon reactor
used as the power core, causing it to leak bursts of
epsilon radiation
.
Voyager
used
graviton pulses
fired from the
deflector dish
to stabilize the reactions.
Having helped
Tash's starship
to successfully use the catapult to get home, Tash sent recommendations for
deflector shield
adjustments to the
Voyager
so they would not experience similar system overloads during mid-flight. Following the enhancements to the shield variance, the crew of the
Voyager
used this device to travel across thirty
sectors
in less than an
hour
, taking three
years
off their journey home.
A confused
Seven of Nine
theorized that the catapult technology may have been derived from the
displacement wave
technology used by the
Caretaker
to bring ships to the
Delta Quadrant
. Both the catapult and the
Caretaker's array
were powered by tetryon reactors, the only two ever encountered as of 2376.
According to Seven of Nine, the catapult constructed by Tash's crew could also be used to bring ships from the
Alpha Quadrant
to the Delta Quadrant. (
VOY
: "
The Voyager Conspiracy
")
Appendices
Background information
The
script
described this structure, which it offhandedly called a "space catapult", as "
[a] strange piece of technology. A vast portal-like structure that looks cobbled together with sections of different design and alloys of different colors –­ as though the pieces came from several alien sources.
" It was identified as a "graviton catapult" in an
okudagram
, but otherwise was unidentified as such in dialog or the script.
Tash's catapult was designed by
Rick Sternbach
, and the
studio model
was built by
Digital Muse
. Digital Muse produced six widely different effect "ingredients" for the catapult-effect, which
Ronald B. Moore
then combined in the editing bay. (
Star Trek: The Magazine
Volume 1, Issue 18
, p. 30)
The design of the catapult station was later used as a Cardassian shipyard in the video game
Star Trek: Armada II
.
External link
Graviton catapult
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

PAGE: Grottonus

Grottonus
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2381
)
Native species:
Carnivorous moss
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Grottonus
was a
planet
.
In
2381
,
Lieutenants jg
Beckett Mariner
,
Brad Boimler
,
D'Vana Tendi
, and
Sam Rutherford
were assigned to investigate
moss
inside a
cave
on Grottonus. During the
away mission
, they were trapped inside the cave when
tremors
collapsed the entrance. While attempting to contact the
USS
Cerritos
, they discovered that the cave moss was
carnivorous
and
sentient
, and interested in hearing stories of their past adventures. Unbeknownst to the four, the situation had been engineered as a
morality
test by the
Vendorians
. (
LD
: "
Caves
")
The name Grottonus is a play on the word "
grotto
", or cave.

PAGE: Ha'Dara
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ha'Dara
======================================================================

Ha'Dara
Class:
Y
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2377
)
Location:
Delta Quadrant
Ha'Dara
was a
lifeless
Y class
planet
in the
Delta Quadrant
. The planet had a
toxic
atmosphere
and
sulfuric
deserts
.
The word Ha'Dara meant "home of light" in the
Bajoran language
and was named by
Iden
, a
Hirogen
prey
hologram
. Iden intended to found a new community there for the
holograms
he
liberated
from the
Hirogen
in
2377
, by installing
photonic field generators
on the
southern continent
.
However, the holograms arrived at Ha'Dara under pursuit by two Hirogen
hunting vessels
. When the
USS
Voyager
disabled the Hirogen ships in a surprise attack, Iden had the hunters
transported
to the surface of Ha'Dara so that they could be hunted as prey. The holograms were deactivated by
B'Elanna Torres
and
Kejal
while Iden was destroyed by
The Doctor
, though all but five of the Hirogen were massacred. (
VOY
: "
Flesh and Blood
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Habitat
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Habitat
======================================================================

A
habitat
was a living area suitable for the needs of the
organisms
dwelling there.
When the
Ledosians
were inadvertently granted
access
to
Ventu
territory, they immediately conducted an
expedition
where they made scans to evaluate the
potential
for
anthropological
research and resource development of the once off-limits habitat. As for the Ventu themselves, the Ledosians intended to help them with better
opportunities
, through the introduction of
medicine
,
infrastructure
,
education
, and
technology
. (
VOY
: "
Natural Law
")

Habitat
at
Wikipedia

seventh
planet
in the
Hakton system
. This system was located in the
Alpha Quadrant
and, between
2370
and
2373
, was inside the
Demilitarized Zone
.
This planet was home to a
Federation
colony
.
In
2370
, three
settlers
were killed on Hakton VII by
Cardassians
, who claimed it was in response to the
Maquis
bombing of the Cardassian
freighter
Bok'Nor
. (
DS9
: "
The Maquis, Part II
")

name
from the
script
pronunciation guide.

StarTrek.com
, the pronunciation for Hakton was "HAWK-tun". Presumably, the planet was
M-class
.
[2]

The Autobiography of Jean-Luc Picard
,
John Harriman
was
governor
of Hakton VII around the
year
2342
, when it was
raided
by the Cardassians. Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
and the crew of the
Stargazer
responded to Harriman's
distress call
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Halem'no
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Halem'no
======================================================================

Halem'no
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
3191
)
Native species:
Halem'nites
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Halem'no
was an
inhabited
class M
planet
and
homeworld
of the
Halem'nites
, a
pre-industrial
humanoid
civilization
. Most of its surface was inhospitable, covered by massive
dust storms
and devoid of
precipitation
.
Environment
All of Halem'no was once fertile and temperate, with
forests
that contained
birds
,
fruit
trees
, and
moss
. However, rising aridity and dust storms threatened to
extinguish
all native
life
. The planet's
atmosphere
was composed of 73% molecular
nitrogen
, 23% molecular
oxygen
, 2.3%
argon
, and 0.8%
carbon dioxide
. (
DIS
: "
Whistlespeak
")
Locations
Dry Lands
Healing Grove
High Summit
History
According to
Halem'nite
mythology
, the coming of Halem'no's dust storms was a punishment from the
gods
for the
wars
that their ancestors fought among themselves.
During the
24th century
, Halem'no was one of fifteen M-class planets along
Denobulan
trade routes
. The Denobulans empathized with the Halem'nites' struggles, which echoed their own
history
. They thus installed five
weather modification towers
, designed by
Doctor
Hitoroshi Kreel
, on the planet that preserved zones of habitability. In deference to the
Prime Directive
, the towers were concealed within pillars of
rock
. The Denobulans' actions profoundly affected the
culture
of the Halem'nites, who identified them with their gods and developed a
religion
around them.
Kreel secretly returned to Halem'no in the late 24th century and hid a piece of a
map
, containing the location of
Progenitor
technology
, in the fifth weather tower along with a clue to the fifth and final piece.
Due to lack of maintenance, the weather towers began to fail as their
energy distribution matrices
fell out of alignment, destroying the Halem'nite settlements that depended on them. By the
32nd century
, only the third tower remained functional, though it was in the process of suffering the same fate as the others, reducing the amount of
rain
it produced. The local Halem'nites had begun offering
ritual sacrifices
to the tower, which they called the
High Summit
, in exchange for rain. They believed that the other weather towers had been destroyed by the gods.
In
3191
, the
USS
Discovery
arrived at the planet in search of the fourth map piece.
Captain
Michael Burnham
and
Lieutenant
Sylvia Tilly
beamed
to the surface, disguised as Halem'nites, to investigate the tower. After determining the state of the third tower, Burnham repaired it with the assistance of
Ensign
Adira Tal
. However, Tilly had unknowingly volunteered herself to be sacrificed; Burnham chose to save her by breaking the Prime Directive and telling the Halem'nites the truth about their history and the tower's provenance. Though the transition would be difficult, Burnham was optimistic that the Halem'nites were ready to evolve into a new belief system, and she further ensured that they were instructed in the future maintenance of the tower.
Discovery
's crew proceeded to recover the map piece and the clue. (
DIS
: "
Whistlespeak
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Hanon IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Hanon_IV
======================================================================

Hanon IV
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Native species:
Hanonian
Hanonian land eel
Location:
Hanon system
Delta Quadrant
Hanon IV
was the
inhabited
planet
in the
Hanon
system
. This world was located near
Kazon-Nistrim
space
in the
Delta Quadrant
. It appeared to be a
M-class
planet in the
Pliocene
stage of evolution, comparable to
Earth
several million years earlier.
Hanon IV was the
homeworld
for a
humanoid
species
, on the verge of civilization. They were divided into
tribes
of approximately twenty people, with a central leading figure, similar to an
elder
.
History
In late
2372
, the
crew
of the
USS
Voyager
were marooned there by
First Maje
Jal
Culluh
. Left without any
technology
, they were forced to live there for days to weeks, with little
food
and
water
. While there, they made
first contact
with the natural humanoid inhabitants. After a
volcanic
eruption and
Commander
Chakotay
saved one of their kind, their relations improved, initially being one of violence. They were rescued a short time later after
Voyager
had been retaken, but two crewmembers were killed on the planet. (
VOY
: "
Basics, Part I
", "
Basics, Part II
")
Later, in
2373
,
Voth
scientists
Forra Gegen
and
Veer
visited the planet and excavated the remains of
Ensign
Hogan
. (
VOY
: "
Distant Origin
")
Environmental
In the late
24th century
, Hanon IV suffered a period of severe
seismic
and volcanic activity.
Earthquakes
were not rare, and volcanic eruptions were periodic. There were various caverns, some looking almost constructed.
The planet's temperature varied greatly from day to night. During the day, it was often intensely hot, while at night very cold temperatures were common. In the area surveyed, water was not initially found.
A lava-flow after a recent eruption
A male inhabitant of Hanon IV
Botanical
Cucumber-like plants
Plants
with nutritional value were hard to find, but the proto-humanoid inhabitants found some plants, and
herbs
that could be used for
medicinal
purposes. Shrubbery existed, and wooden branches could be found lying around. The planet was also home to a species of edible
cucumber
-like
vegetable
growing plentifully on vines.
Zoological
Apart from the humanoid natives, Hanon IV was home to several other known species of
animals
. These included insects, and small, edible
worm
-like grubs, that lived under rocks. Large white
eggs
were also observed.
Probably the most dramatic animal was the
Hanonian land eel
, a large
cave
-dwelling creature that was capable of eating humanoids.
Furthermore, one of the humanoid natives had a container that appeared like it might have been made from a horn.
Eel-like lifeform
Second most scary worm-like lifeform on the planet
Large eggs found in a
nest
References
VOY
:
"
Basics, Part I
"
"
Basics, Part II
"
"
Distant Origin
"
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 86), the
USS
Voyager
was at this planet on
stardate
50032.
The Hanon IV scenes were filmed at
Alabama Hills
, near
Lone Pine
,
California
. However, the
lava
(or rather footage from it) was taken from
Hawaii
. ("Red Alert: Amazing Visual Effects",
VOY Season 3 DVD
special feature)
External link
Hanon IV
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

.

. The

.

======================================================================
PAGE: Hansen's Planet
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Hansen's_Planet
======================================================================

Hansen's Planet
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
23rd century
)
Native species:
Hansen's Planet anthropoids
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Hansen's Planet
was an
inhabited
namesake
Class M
planet
that was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
. It was the
homeworld
for a
species of anthropoids
, which had been studied and classified by
Federation
anthropologists
.
In
2267
, while looking for
survivors
of the downed
Galileo
on
Taurus II
,
Lieutenant
Kelowitz
's
landing party
was attacked by a
species of anthropoids
. When back aboard the
USS
Enterprise
, the lieutenant stated that these anthropoids were similar to those found on Hansen's Planet. (
TOS
: "
The Galileo Seven
")
The
location
of Hansen's Planet was labeled on the
star chart
Data
and
Picard
were studying in
Stellar cartography
aboard
USS
Enterprise
-D
in
2371
. (
Star Trek Generations
,
okudagram
)
Appendices
Background information
The planet's classification came from the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 325.
Apocrypha
In the
Star Trek Maps
, Hansen's Planet was identified as the
first
planet in the UFC 2848832 system.

======================================================================
PAGE: Harlak
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Harlak
======================================================================

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Harlak
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Terran Empire rebellion
(
Mirror universe
)
Status:
Devasted by
orbital bombardment
(
2256
)
Satellite(s):
At least one
moon
Location:
Harlak system
,
Alpha Quadrant
Harlak
was a
planet
in the
Harlak system
in the
Alpha Quadrant
. (
DIS
: "
The Wolf Inside
";
Star Trek: Section 31
)
In the mirror universe, Harlak had at least one moon and its
surface
was dotted with the ruins of large
cities
.
In
2254
, the
Terran Empire rebellion
established a camouflaged base in the northern hemisphere of this planet. For two years, the base coordinated the rebellion against the
Terran Empire
before
Imperial Intelligence
discovered its location in
2256
.
Captain
Michael Burnham
of the
ISS
Shenzhou
was ordered to destroy the base and kill all rebels on Harlak, but unknown to them Burnham had been replaced by her
counterpart
from the
prime universe
. She instead chose to warn the rebels, who began evacuating.
Before the evacuation could be completed, however, the
ISS
Charon
entered the system and carried out Burnham's mission, unleashing an
orbital bombardment
that devastated both the base and a wide expanse of the planet's surface.
(
DIS
: "
The Wolf Inside
")
The exterior scenes, showing the surface of the planet, were shot at
Lafarge Quarry
outside the city limits of Toronto. (
AT
: "
The Wolf Inside
")

PAGE: Harstedt's Planet

======================================================================

Harstedt's Planet
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2293
)
Location:
Beta Quadrant
Harstedt's Planet
was a
planet
in the
Beta Quadrant
.
In
2293
, Harstedt's Planet's position was labeled in the
star chart
for the "
Operation: Retrieve
" command briefing. The chart was from the
Starfleet Command Intelligence Database
. This star chart was seen in the
office
of the
Federation President
on
Earth
. (
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
,
set artwork
)
This planet's quadrant of origin is inferred based on the position of neighboring locations –
Starbase 24
and
Rura Penthe
– as seen in the
star charts
appearing in the
Star Trek: Discovery
episode "
Magic to Make the Sanest Man Go Mad
" and in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
This chart was seen in greater resolution at threads at Flare.Solareclipse.net.
[1]
(X)
[2]
(X)
Many of these locations shared their names with the last names of crew who worked on the sixth film. This location was probably named after the video coordinator
Jeffrey Harstedt
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Haven
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Haven
======================================================================

Haven
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
protectorate
Status:
Habitable
(
2364
)
Satellite(s):
At least 1
moon
Native species:
Haven natives
Location:
In
orbit
of
Beta Cassius
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
For the
TNG
episode of the same name, please see "
Haven
".
Haven
was a
M-class
planet
orbiting
the
primary
Beta Cassius
. The planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
and was
inhabited
by a
humanoid
civilization
. The planet was renowned for its tranquil beauty to the point that many believed it possessed
mystical
healing powers, though this claim was unsupported by scientific evidence. Haven had a
legendary
reputation as being the place where
souls
were mended and broken
hearts
were healed. The planet had at least
one moon
.
In
2364
, the
USS
Enterprise
-D
visited Haven to receive
Lwaxana Troi
and
Steven Miller
's family. Soon after, a
Tarellian starship
carrying the last
survivors
of a
plague
approached the planet, seeking its reputed healing powers. The ship bypassed the system's
stargate
and failed to reply to attempts at
communication
.
First Electorine
Valeda Innis
entreated
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
to destroy the Tarellian ship before its crew could disembark and spread the plague to the
population
. Thanks to the intervention of
Wyatt Miller
, the Tarellians changed
course
. (
TNG
: "
Haven
")
The original graphic of the planet Haven was also used to represent the planet
Bynaus
in "
11001001
".
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 329 stated that Beta Cassius was another name for Haven.
External links
Re-Used Planets in TNG
at
Ex Astris Scientia
Haven
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

Klingons

's supposed incursion into Hectaron and the associated
enslavement

total

.

======================================================================
PAGE: Hekaras II
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Hekaras_II
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Hekaras II
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
(
3195
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3195
)
Native species:
Hekarans
Location:
Unnamed star system
Hekaras
sector
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Hekaras II
was the
inhabited
second
planet
in its
star system
, in the
Hekaras
sector
. Located near the
Hekaras Corridor
, it was the only inhabited planet in its
region
. It was, as well, the
homeworld
of the
Hekarans
, a
member
of the
United Federation of Planets
. The planet was governed by the
Hekaran government
.
In the
2360s
, the planet underwent major
climate
changes due to
gravitational
shifts in the system and in danger of becoming uninhabitable. Hekaran
scientists
Serova
and
Rabal
concluded that
warp
travel through the Corridor was the cause. In
2370
,
Dr.
Serova
sacrificed
herself to prove her
theory
, by breaching her
ship
's
warp core
and creating a large
subspace rift
, 0.1
light years
in
diameter
. With this news, the Federation ordered a
weather control matrix
installed on the planet to forestall the existing effects of the Corridor's instability. The
Federation Council
also issued a directive restricting essential travel only to Hekaras II. (
TNG
: "
Force of Nature
")
In the
32nd century
, Hekaras II was a Federation member planet. It was listed with other members on a video wall in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
Kids These Days
")
The planet's class came from
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 333.

======================================================================
PAGE: Helicon Gamma
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Helicon_Gamma
======================================================================

Helicon Gamma
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Destroyed
(
2261
)
Location:
Beta Quadrant
Helicon Gamma
was an
uninhabited
M-class
planet
located outside
Federation space
. It harbored
carbon-based
native
animal
and
plant
life. (
SNW
: "
The Sehlat Who Ate Its Tail
")
Astronomical data
Planetary data for Helicon Gamma
The planet was located at
coordinates
3780.7292. It had a
rotation period
of 25.1
hours
and a
revolution period
of 305.8
days
. Its
mass
was estimated at 5.03835
e24
×10
24
kilograms
and its age at 5.9 billion
solar years
. The
atmosphere
was composed of 76% molecular
nitrogen
, 20% molecular
oxygen
, 0.7%
argon
, 0.7%
carbon dioxide
, and 0.5%
water vapor
, with trace amounts of
methane
and
glycine
. The surface had several
continents
, including a
northern
continent, and 90%
water
coverage. The mean surface
temperature
was 15.1
degrees
Celsius
. The planet contained significant deposits of
aldentium
. (
SNW
: "
The Sehlat Who Ate Its Tail
")
History
Farragut
at Helicon Gamma before its destruction
In
2261
, the
USS
Farragut
was assigned to conduct a
planetary survey
of Helicon Gamma.
Lieutenant Commander
James T. Kirk
recommended sending an
away team
to the surface, though
Captain
V'Rel
judged that
geological instability
posed an unacceptable risk. Before the survey could begin, the planet was struck by a powerful
gravitational beam
that shattered much of its
crust
. Debris from the planet impacted the
Farragut
, causing heavy damage.
Responding to the
Farragut
's
distress call
, the
USS
Enterprise
arrived and encountered the responsible party: a massive
scavenger ship
. The
Enterprise
was engulfed by the vessel, which then departed on a course to
Sullivan's Planet
. After conducting repairs, the
Farragut
set off in pursuit. (
SNW
: "
The Sehlat Who Ate Its Tail
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Hemikek IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Hemikek_IV
======================================================================

Hemikek IV
AKA:
Hemikek
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2372
)
Location:
Hemikek
system
Delta Quadrant
Hemikek IV
, or, more simply,
Hemikek
, was the
inhabited
M-class
fourth
planet
in the
Hemikek
system
. This world, rich in
minerals
, was located in the
Delta Quadrant
. In the
2370s
, the planets'
mining rights
belonged to a consortium of nonaggressive people.
In
2372
,
Seska
told
USS
Voyager
crewmember
Michael Jonas
, who was covertly spying for the
Kazon-Nistrim
, about a plan to capture the ship, which would include her waiting for
Voyager
at Hemikek IV. (
VOY
: "
Lifesigns
")
Shortly after, Jonas
sabotaged
Voyager
and the ship was forced to divert to Hemikek IV in order to acquire some much needed
verterium cortenide
and
monocrystal
cortenum
. The
Kazon-Nistrim
were waiting to ambush them there with ground
troops
. Fortunately,
Voyager
was alerted of the plan and avoided the
trap
. (
VOY
: "
Investigations
")
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 85), the Hemikek system was located in non-aligned space between the
Vidiian Sodality
and the
Kazon Collective
. The system's
primary
was a
G-class star
.
External link
Hemikek IV
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Hetemit IX
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Hetemit_IX
======================================================================

Hetemit IX
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2259
)
Location:
Hetemit
system
Alpha Quadrant
Hetemit IX
was the formerly
inhabited
ninth
planet
in the
Hetemit
star system
. It was a
class M
world, with 47% of its surface covered by
water
. It had a
mantle
composed of molten
iron
-
sulfur
alloy
. The planet was regularly swept by dangerous
ion storms
. (
SNW
: "
Ghosts of Illyria
")
Astronomical data
Planetary data for Hetemit IX
The planet was located at coordinates 2723.5389. It had a
rotation period
of 22.9
hours
and a
revolution period
of 537.2
solar days
. Its
mass
was estimated to be 6.3402
e24
×10
24
kilograms
and its age at 6.1 billion
solar years
. The
atmosphere
was composed of 71% molecular
nitrogen
, 26% molecular
oxygen
, 1.9%
argon
, 0.3%
carbon dioxide
, and 0.2%
nitrous oxide
, with trace amounts of
methane
and
carbon monoxide
. The mean
surface temperature
was 12.6
degrees
Celsius
. (
SNW
: "
Ghosts of Illyria
")
History
Hetemit IX was once home to an
Illyrian
colony
that sought to join the
Federation
. Due to the Federation's prohibition on
genetic engineering
, the colonists were conducting research into reversing their genetic modifications. The
population
eventually succumbed to a
light virus
that induced a
suicidal
attraction to
light
. However, some of the afflicted colonists ran into ion storms, where charged storm
particles
unexpectedly bonded with their
electromagnetic
patterns and transformed them into beings of
plasma
.
In
2259
, the
USS
Enterprise
visited Hetemit IX as part of a standing
Starfleet
mission to investigate the fate of the colony. Some members of its
away team
were infected by the light virus on the planet and caused an epidemic on the
Enterprise
, which was resolved thanks to
Lieutenant Commander
Una Chin-Riley
's Illyrian genetic modifications. Meanwhile,
Captain
Christopher Pike
and
Lieutenant
Spock
were temporarily stranded on the surface by an ion storm, during which the surviving colonists in their plasma forms protected them from the storm's effects. The former Illyrians then directed them to a record of what they had been trying to accomplish. (
SNW
: "
Ghosts of Illyria
")
Pike referred to the fate of the Hetemit IX colonists while attempting to convince
Neera Ketoul
to represent Chin-Riley in her
court martial
. (
SNW
: "
Ad Astra per Aspera
")
Exterior scenes on the planet were filmed at
Ontario Place
.

PAGE: Heva VII
CATEGORIES: Planets

Heva VII
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2373
)
Location:
Delta Quadrant
Heva VII
was the
inhabited
seventh
planet
in its
star system
, in the
Delta Quadrant
. This planet was located near
Akritirian
space
and was the location of a
refueling port
.
When
Neelix
, while using
his ship
to rescue
Harry Kim
and
Tom Paris
from an
Akritirian prison
in
2373
, was stopped by an
Akritirian patrol ship
, he claimed to be searching for Heva VII. (
VOY
: "
The Chute
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 86), the Heva system was a
binary
system with a
pair
of
F-class stars
. The
USS
Voyager
visited this system on
stardate
50156.
External link
Heva VII
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

PAGE: Heva VII refueling port
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Heva_VII_refueling_port
======================================================================

The
Heva VII refueling port
was a
space station
located at
Heva VII
which provided
fuel
to
spacecraft
.
During the rescue of
Tom Paris
and
Harry Kim
from the
Akritirian maximum security detention facility
in
2373
,
Neelix
claimed he mistook the facility for the refueling port when the
Akritirians
demanded that
his ship
leave immediately. (
VOY
: "
The Chute
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Hilgroen
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Hilgroen
======================================================================

Hilgroen as mentioned in Danar's file
Hilgroen
was a theater of operations during the
Robmeybur Rebellion
.
Following the outbreak of the Rebellion at
Dantang
,
Roga Danar
was transferred to
Anabar Squadron
at Hilgroen for the duration of the campaign, during which he was injured twice.
In
2366
, this location was mentioned in Danar's military service records, which were accessible to the crew of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
via the
Angosian senatorial database
. (
TNG-R
: "
The Hunted
",
okudagram
)
Hilgroen was named after
CBS Digital Technical Operation Department
employee
Hilary Groner
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Hima
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Hima
======================================================================

Hima
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Emerald Chain
Status:
Habitable
(
3188
)
Satellite(s):
At least
four moons
Location:
Milky Way Galaxy
Hima
was an
inhabited
planet
. It had a single
sun
and at least
four moons
. The planet hosted a variety of native
lifeforms
, including a
bird
, a
predatory
insect
, a winged
insect
, a
fish
, and a mobile
aquatic plant
with healing properties.
The main settlement on Hima was
Requiem
, above which was the
Mercantile
, an entrepôt for
couriers
controlled by the
Emerald Chain
. There was a large
debris field
in
orbit
containing remnants of
Federation
starships
.
After leaving the
23rd century
in the
Red Angel suit
,
Michael Burnham
emerged from a
wormhole
over Hima in
3189
. Seconds later, she collided with
Cleveland Booker
's
ship
, causing both to
crash
on Hima. Burnham subsequently helped Booker obtain more
dilithium
from the Mercantile, allowing them to depart. (
DIS
: "
That Hope Is You, Part 1
")
The exterior scenes for Hima were filmed in
Iceland
.
[1]
People
Hima visitors
External link
Hima
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

was the
first

in the
Kaminar
system
,
Alpha Quadrant
.
History
In the
23rd century
, this planet's system was in
Non-Federation space
.
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of Federation space in

, this planet's system was within the blast radius of one of the mines. (

: "
300th Night
", "
Rubincon
")
Stellar cartography
In
2257
, this planet was identified on
star charts
of the Kaminar system accessed by the
bridge crew
of the

. (

: "

")

======================================================================
PAGE: Hirogen training facility
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Hirogen_training_facility
======================================================================

Hirogen training facility
Type:
Space station
Owner:
Hirogen
Operator:
Hirogen
Location:
Delta Quadrant
Status:
Active
(
2377
)
The
Hirogen training facility
was a
space station
built by the
Hirogen
incorporating
holographic technology
from the
Federation
starship
,
USS
Voyager
.
In
2374
,
Captain
Kathryn Janeway
provided
Alpha Hirogen
Karr
with the means to create hunts as they see fit using Federation holographic technology in exchange for her ship, which Karr and his group had successfully captured.
By
2377
, the Hirogen had constructed a training facility in the
Delta Quadrant
which utilized the holo-technology to create various species of holographic prey the Hirogen could hunt again and again. However, the Hirogen soon grew tired of using the built-in holographic
safety protocols
and so deactivated them. This resulted in the holographic enemies being able to overpower the Hirogen hunters on the station and steal one of their ships. The situation was eventually contained by Captain Janeway and the crew of
Voyager
. (
VOY
: "
The Killing Game
", "
Flesh and Blood
")
Personnel
Donik
- technician

PAGE: Holberg 917G

Holberg 917G
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2328
)
Satellite(s):
Two
moons
Location:
Omega
system
Omega
sector
Beta Quadrant
Holberg 917G
was a small
inhabited
planet
in the
Omega
system
,
Omega
sector
. Located in the
Beta Quadrant
, this planet had
two
moons
and a large quantity of naturally occurring
ryetalyn
.
History
In
2239
, the planet was purchased by a private concern, a gentleman named
Mr.
Brack
. This was later revealed to be one of many
aliases
of the planet's only inhabitant,
Flint
the
immortal
, when the Federation
starship
USS
Enterprise
visited in
2269
. (
TOS
: "
Requiem for Methuselah
")
In
2328
, this was the destination for passengers traveling from
Dallas
,
Texas
,
Earth
via commercial
transport
.
Amanda Vackrinos
,
Kevin Ryan
, and
Paulina Ryan
traveled on the
SS
Kogin
. (
TNG-R
: "
Inheritance
",
okudagram
)
Stellar cartography
The location of Holberg 917G on "The Explored Galaxy" star chart
In
2293
, the
location
of Holberg 917G in the
Milky Way Galaxy
was labeled in a
star chart
that was in
Captain
James T. Kirk
's
quarters
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-A
. (
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
,
okudagram
)
Appendices
Background information
Flint's home on Holberg 917G, as it appeared in the original episode
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
In the original version of the episode,
Flint's house
was depicted by a re-used
matte painting
of a
Kalar
castle
on
Rigel VII
from "
The Cage
".
Apocrypha
In the
Star Trek Maps
(p. 28, chart D), Holberg 917G was the
seventh
planet in the system and had once supported a
neanderthaloid
population
similar to the Kalar. The single
moon
of the planet seen in the original version of the episode was named Tanisse.

at

, the wiki for licensed

in the

During the

. In
Damar
and

's proposed changes to the

in

, Holna IV would have ended up in

Lauren
once said that the mizinite could have kept the Dominion's

, but the Dominion were willing to cede it to the Federation in order to get control of the

. (

as seen in the

appearing in the

episode "

".

from the

pronunciation guide.

======================================================================
PAGE: Hubble Space Telescope
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Hubble_Space_Telescope
======================================================================

Hubble Space Telescope
Type:
Manned
space
telescope
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Status:
Active
(
2328
)
Hubble Space Telescope
was a manned
space
telescope
.
In
2328
, this was the destination for
John Crunsfeld
. He had traveled from the
Kennedy Space Center
on the
SS
Kogin
. (
TNG-R
: "
Inheritance
",
okudagram
)
This telescope was only mentioned in writing.
The Hubble Space Telescope, named after
Edwin Hubble
, is a major currently existing space telescope, built by NASA. It is unmanned and explicitly not expected to survive into the 24th century, suggesting that the telescope mentioned in the database was a different structure. The original Hubble telescope was the source of numerous images on display in
astrometrics
aboard
USS
Voyager
.
[1]

at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Hunhau
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Hunhau
======================================================================

Hunhau
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Emerald Chain
Status:
Habitable
(
3189
)
Location:
Milky Way Galaxy
Hunhau
was a sparsely
inhabited
class M
planet
. An
Emerald Chain
"
salvage
planet", the sole population center on the surface was a private
salvage yard
managed by
Tolor
, which was protected by heavy
shielding
and
auto-defense systems
. There was a large
debris field
in
orbit
left by
the Burn
, along with many derelict
starships
from earlier eras, including
Hiawatha
-type
,
Cardenas
-class
, and
Hoover
-class
vessels.
In
3189
,
Cleveland Booker
was captured by the Emerald Chain while attempting to acquire a
black box
on Hunhau. Consequently,
Michael Burnham
and
Philippa Georgiou
undertook an unsanctioned mission to the planet in
Booker's ship
, Georgiou posing as a buyer of pre-
2400
technology
and Burnham her
servant
. They engineered a mass escape of the
slave
laborers, after which Georgiou sent several derelict ships crashing into the salvage yard, destroying it. (
DIS
: "
Scavengers
")
Osyraa
subsequently arrived at Hunhau aboard her
flagship
, the
Viridian
. Inspecting the ruins of the yard and noting that Tolor had lost
Ryn
, she
beamed
him to her vessel to be devoured by a
trance worm
. (
DIS
: "
The Sanctuary
")
Hunhau
is the Mayan god of the underworld.
According to graphic designer
Timothy Peel
, the planet was pictured on a hologram in "
Red Directive
".
[1]

Hurada III
was the

third

.
Hurada III was one of eleven planets visited by a group of
Ullian
telepathic
historians
in the
2360s
. During their visit, two cases of

were reported. These were later found to be a
result
of

. (

Federation

("United Federation of Planets I"), in
2378

Federation member
.

-like

Hysperia
was an
inhabited

. It had native
lifeforms
that resembled
dragons
from
Earth
mythology
, and was thus colonized by the
Hysperians
: "
Ren faire
type"
Humans
who sought to follow a
medieval
fantasy
-themed lifestyle. The Hysperians lived in
castles
on the planet's
surface
, and kept the

as
pets
. The planet was ruled by a

, who in
2381
was

. (

Before committing to

======================================================================
PAGE: Iconia
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Iconia
======================================================================

Iconia
AKA:
Iconia Prime
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Iconian Empire
(
200,000 years ago
)
Klingon Empire
(
2257
)
Non-aligned
(
2259
)
Status:
Devastated
(
2365
)
Native species:
Iconians
Location:
Iconia system
In the
Federation-Romulan border
Beta Quadrant
Iconia
, also known as
Iconia Prime
, was a formerly
inhabited
planet
located in the
Beta Quadrant
. This planet was located at
coordinates
227 mark 359. The planet was located in the
Federation-Romulan border
.
History
In the
distant past
, this was the
homeworld
of the
Iconian
species
and the
capital planet
of their
empire
. The planet was devastated by a massive
orbital bombardment
approximately
200,000 years ago
. The Iconians were believed to have been annihilated, although a part of the
population
may have escaped through their
gateways
. By the 24th century, the planet had one working automated structure which launched
probes
at orbiting
ships
.
In
2257
, during the first
Federation-Klingon War
, Iconia's system was occupied by the
Klingons
. (
DIS
: "
The War Without, The War Within
")
The precise location of Iconia was lost to
history
until
2365
, when an
artifact
bearing an ancient starmap was discovered in an
archaeological dig
on
Denius III
. From there,
Captain
Donald Varley
of the
Federation
starship
USS
Yamato
was able to locate Iconia, and chose to violate the Neutral Zone to reach the planet in order to prevent Iconian technology from falling into
Romulan
hands. After the
Yamato
was destroyed by an
Iconian software transmission
, the
USS
Enterprise
-D
and the
Romulan warbird
Haakona
both traveled to Iconia, where
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
had the last operational structure on the
surface
destroyed. (
TNG
: "
Contagion
")
In
2372
,
Worf
told the
crew
of the
USS
Defiant
of the events that had taken place on Iconia when they were on a
mission
to destroy an Iconian gateway on
Vandros IV
in the
Gamma Quadrant
. (
DS9
: "
To the Death
")
Appendices
Background information
In the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 356, it was stated that the surface of Iconia was a
matte painting
designed by visual effects supervisor
Dan Curry
. The Iconian control building, a
model
used as part of the painting, was designed by
Mike Okuda
, based on two swimming pool filter covers and part of the
Star Trek: The Motion Picture
drydock model.
The planet model first used here for Iconia would later be used for
Cor Caroli V
("
Allegiance
").
Michael Chabon
, the former showrunner for
Star Trek: Picard
, named a
Beta Quadrant
sector
after this planet in the blog article "Some Notes on Freecloud". Along with the
Alpha Doradus system
, which contained the inhabited planet
Freecloud
, this sector included the negotiated terminus of the Romulan Neutral Zone.
[1]
External links
Iconia
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Re-Used Planets in TNG
at
Ex Astris Scientia

In

,

that, while journeying on the

, a traveler might blink and, before they knew it, they had "missed" a visit to Ilari. (

.

======================================================================
PAGE: Ilidaria
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ilidaria
======================================================================

Ilidaria
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2371
)
Native species:
Ilidarians
Location:
Ilidaria system
Delta Quadrant
Ilidaria
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Ilidaria system
of the
Delta Quadrant
. It was the
homeworld
of the
warp-capable
Ilidarian
civilization
. The planet was located less than three
light years
from a
type-4 quantum singularity
.
When
USS
Voyager
was trapped within the
event horizon
of the quantum singularity in
2371
, Neelix suggested asking for help from Ilidaria. He noted that its inhabitants had sophisticated technology and were "
quite friendly, most of the time
". (
VOY
: "
Parallax
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 83), this planet was visited by
Voyager
on
stardate
48734, the date of
Chakotay
and
Tuvok
's trade mission to the Ilidarians mentioned in "
Cathexis
".

======================================================================
PAGE: Illyria
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Illyria
======================================================================

This article might be inaccurate
This page contains factual errors and is therefore
in need of attention
.
Please visit the article's
talk page
to see what needs fixing and feel free to
edit
this page to assist with this task.
Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Illyria
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
(
3195
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3195
)
Location:
Illyria system
Vaultera Nebula
Alpha Quadrant
Illyria
was an
inhabited
planet
located in the
Illyria system
in the
Vaultera Nebula
. Located in the
Alpha Quadrant
, it hosted a
Federation colony
populated by
Illyrians
.
In the early
23rd century
, Illyria accepted provisional
membership
in the
Federation
, under the proviso that they refrain from engaging in
genetic engineering
as dictated by Federation
law
. However, some Illyrian
families
continued to bio-engineer their
children
in secret, in accordance with their traditions.
After one such child,
Ivan Ketoul
, was exposed as genetically modified and
arrested
along with his
parents
, the persecution of Illyrians in the
colony
escalated. Illyrians were subjected to verbal
harassment
, denial of services, and even attacks; some moved to non-Federation worlds. The worsening civil unrest eventually prompted the local
government
to segregate the population into Illyrian and non-Illyrian
cities
. There were Illyrians who chose residence in the non-Illyrian settlement, though they lived in constant fear of discovery.
Una Chin-Riley
and her childhood best friend
Neera Ketoul
were
born
on Illyria. (
SNW
: "
Ad Astra per Aspera
")
In the
32nd century
, Illyria was a
Federation
member
planet. It was listed with other members on a video wall in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
Kids These Days
")
The name of this planet was only given in Chin-Riley's personnel file in "Ad Astra per Aspera".
The name was originally introduced by way of the episode title "
Ghosts of Illyria
".
In Chin-Riley's personnel file, she was said to have been born on Illyria or on Colony 9-A. However, a notation to her file stated that verification of her being born on this colony was pending and that the information came from an unreliable source.

PAGE: Ilyra VI
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ilyra_VI

Ilyra VI
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2260s
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Ilyra VI
was the
inhabited
sixth
planet
of its
star system
. The planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
Harry Mudd
described it as a "charming planet" that had an "
innocent and friendly populace
."
At some point prior to
stardate
4978
, Harry Mudd escaped
the robot planet
he was imprisoned on by
stealing
a
ship
, and found refuge on this planet. While on Ilyra VI, Mudd committed
fraud
by selling
Starfleet Space Academy
to its inhabitants. His sale earned him enough
credits
to get him to
Sirius IX
. (
TAS
: "
Mudd's Passion
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
In the
novelization
of "Mudd's Passion" (as published in
Star Trek Log 3
), Harry Mudd said that Ilyra VI was granted full
Federation
status not long before his visit there.

at

, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek

Inavar Prime
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2370s
)
Location:
Delta Quadrant
Inavar Prime
was the
inhabited
prime
planet
in its
star system
. This system was located in the
Delta Quadrant
.
Former
Borg drones
Two of Nine
,
Three of Nine
, and
Four of Nine
had their
Borg implants
removed on Inavar Prime. However, the
surgeon
was unskilled in the procedure and so their
organs
sustained damage and their bodies were left
scarred
. (
VOY
: "
Survival Instinct
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.

PAGE: Indri VIII

Indri VIII
Class:
L
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Neutral
Status:
Extinguished
(
2369
)
Location:
Indri
system
Beta Quadrant
Indri VIII
was the
L-class
eighth
planet
in the
Indri
system
, in the
Beta Quadrant
. It was
uninhabited
, neutral, and had no strategic importance whatsoever. The planet was covered by
deciduous
vegetation
until
2369
, but possessed no
animal
life
or
evidence
of past or present
civilization
.
The planet became known to the
United Federation of Planets
in the
2300s
when it was visited by
starships
.
Indri VIII pointed to on a star chart
Indri VIII was one of the planets seeded with part of a
genetic
program by the
Progenitors
4.5 billion years ago
. In 2369, the
IKS
Maht-H'a
collected a
DNA
sample from Indri VIII, then destroyed the planet's lower
atmosphere
with a
plasma
reaction. All life on the
surface
was extinguished, precluding any others from collecting a sample. (
TNG
: "
The Chase
")

at

Inferna Prime
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
(
3196
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3195
)
Location:
Zeta Herculis
system
Alpha Quadrant
~35
light years
from
Sol
Inferna Prime
was the
inhabited
prime
planet
in the
Zeta Herculis
system
. The planet was located in the
Alpha Quadrant
and was eight
days
away from
Deep Space 9
at the
cruising
warp speed
of a
Danube
-class
runabout
.
History
In
2373
, the
Federation Grand Jury
was convened on this world to hear a case concerning the
Orion Syndicate
.
Quark
was a
witness
in the case and was called to Inferna Prime to testify, but the Syndicate attempted to have him killed en route. (
DS9
: "
The Ascent
")
In the
32nd century
, Inferna Prime was a
Federation
member
planet. It was listed with other members on a video wall in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
Kids These Days
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet was within the blast radius of one of the mines. (
SA
: "
300th Night
")
Stellar cartography
inferna Prime's system was visible from
Earth
in the
constellation
Hercules
. It was approximately 35
light years
from
Sol
.
In
2259
, the planet's location was labeled on a
stellar cartography
chart that was seen on a
USS
Enterprise
's
ready room
viewscreen
. This planet's symbol had a
blue
color
, indicating that it was affiliated with the
United Federation of Planets
. (
SNW
: "
Spock Amok
", "
A Quality of Mercy
")
On
stardate
1292.4, the location of this planet was labeled in a chart used during a
Section 31
Alpha Team
briefing
. (
Star Trek: Section 31
)
The system's location was labeled in a
Federation star chart
that was in
Fleet Admiral
Kirsten Clancy
's
office
at
Starfleet Headquarters
in
2399
and on the
bridge
of the
USS
Titan
-A
in
2401
. Inferna Prime was in or near to
Federation space
. (
PIC
: "
Maps and Legends
", "
The Next Generation
", "
Disengage
")
In 2401, the location of Inferna Prime was labeled on a star chart that was seen on the
USS
Stargazer
's
bridge
viewscreen
. The chart was used to show the location of the combined Starfleet-Borg armada and where it needed to go to face a galactic-level threat. (
PIC
: "
Farewell
")
In
3189
, the location of Inferna Prime was denoted on a
holographic
star chart
of the
galaxy
at
Federation Headquarters
. (
DIS
: "
Die Trying
")
In
3190
, the location of Inferna Prime was labeled on a star chart used by
Commander
Paul Stamets
for tracking the movement of the
Dark Matter Anomaly
through the
galaxy
. (
DIS
: "
The Examples
")
The location of Inferna Prime labeled on a star chart (2401)
The location of Inferna Prime labeled on a star chart (3190)
Appendices
Background information
"in-FER-nuh" was the pronunciation for this planet's name from the
script
pronunciation guide.
[1]
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 363 identified Inferna Prime as a Federation world.
StarTrek.com
identified the world as a UFP member or as an associate member.
[2]
(X)
Information on this world was derived from the episodes, the
reference works
Star Trek: Star Charts
and
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
, and real-world sources. The reference works were used extensively in the creation of
star charts
seen in
Star Trek: Discovery
,
Star Trek: Picard
,
Star Trek: Strange New Worlds
,
Star Trek: Prodigy
,
Star Trek: Lower Decks
, and
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
. The real-world information added to the article was current as of 2026.
The name of Inferna Prime's system came from the
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I") and
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Federation Historical Highlights, 2161-2385"). In
2378
, Inferna Prime was listed as a Federation member.

Ingraham B
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2328
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Ingraham B
was an
inhabited
planet
which was located in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
. This planet was in the same section of the
galaxy
as the
Beta Portolan
system
,
Levinius V
,
Theta Cygni XII
, and
Deneva
.
History
In the
23rd century
, this planet was invaded by the
flying parasites
, which been transported to this planet from the
civilization
on Levinius V. By
2265
, the civilization on Ingraham B was destroyed in the
mass insanity
caused by the parasites. A vessel of infected visitors from Ingraham B carrying the parasites arrived at the
Deneva colony
in
2266
. (
TOS
: "
Operation -- Annihilate!
")
In
2328
, this planet was home to the
Ingraham B Transit Station
. (
TNG-R
: "
Inheritance
")
Stellar cartography
In
2293
, the
location
of Ingraham B in the
Milky Way Galaxy
was labeled in the
star chart
"The Explored Galaxy", which was on display in
Captain
James T. Kirk
's
quarters
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-A
. (
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
,
okudagram
)
Appendices
Apocrypha
In the novel
Losing the Peace
, after the incident with the neural parasites,
Denevan
leaders created an emergency plan to evacuate citizens to Ingraham B in the event of a similar worldwide crisis. This plan was put into motion when Deneva was attacked and annihilated by the
Borg Collective
during their
2381
invasion of the Alpha Quadrant, saving the lives of thousands of Denevans.

International Space Station
Type:
Scientific Research Facility
Affiliation:
NASA
Russian Space Agency
European Space Agency
Location:
In
orbit
of
Earth
Sol
system
Sector 001
Alpha Quadrant
Status:
Active
(
2022
)
Crew:
At least 4
Docking facilities:
At least one airlock for
space shuttle orbiters
The
International Space Station
was a
space station
and one of
Earth
's first internationally combined efforts in exploring
space
. The station was jointly serviced by
American
space shuttle orbiters
and
Russian
Soyuz
and
Progress
spacecraft.
In
2000
,
astronaut
Lieutenant
McMillan
was scheduled to be the co-
pilot
on a joint mission between
NASA
and the
Europeans
for a four
month
tour on the International Space Station that began in
2003
. (
VOY
: "
11:59
")
The
space shuttle
Atlantis
delivered a
Spacehab
module to the ISS in the early
21st century
. (
Star Trek: Enterprise
, opening credits)
In
2020
,
Dragon 2
delivered astronauts to the station. (
PIC
: "
Two of One
")
The
assignment patches
for the ISS Program and for a three-man team were on display in the
602 Club
in
2143
. (
ENT
: "
First Flight
")
The ISS in the time stream
After
Jonathan Archer
restored a damaged timeline, the ISS could be seen in the
time stream
as the timeline realigned itself. (
ENT
: "
Storm Front, Part II
")
A sketch depicting an overhead view of the ISS was contained in the
library computer
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
in
2254
. (
TOS-R
: "
The Cage
")
A model of the Space Station Freedom
Beginning with the year
2370
,
Benjamin Sisko
had a
model
in his office aboard
Deep Space 9
of the
Space Station Freedom
, which had the space shuttle
Enterprise
docked with it. The model was housed in a glass casing in the beginning but was later displayed without the casing in his office next to
Ops
. (
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine
)
ISS crew
Gidzenko, Yuri
Krikalev, Sergei
McMillan
Shepherd, William
Appendices
Selected appearances
DS9
:
"
The Homecoming
"
"

"
"

"
"

"
"

"

"
ENT
: "
First Flight
"
Background information
The model of the Space Station seen in
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine
was given to the series by
Majel Barrett
as a gift. The DS9 art department added a model of the
Enterprise
(OV-101) and displayed it docked to the space station. (
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 372)
Although often referred to as the International Space Station, the model is actually of the then-proposed 1993 American-led Space Station Freedom.
A montage of the International Space Station (including the since cancelled Russian Science Power and Research modules) being constructed is featured in the
opening credits
of
Star Trek: Enterprise
.
The names Gidzenko, Krikalyov, and Shepherd were names seen on a mission insignia in the 602 Club. They were the first inhabitants of the space station on Expedition 1. Their mission patch, seen in the 602 Club, showed the space station on a blue background and the names of the Russian astronauts were written in their native Cyrillic alphabet.
E. Michael Fincke
, who appeared along with fellow NASA astronaut
Terry Virts
in
ENT
: "
These Are The Voyages...
", has spent a year total aboard the ISS, from April to October 2004 (speaking with
Scott Bakula
while in space) and again from October 2008 to April 2009. Virts himself piloted the shuttle
Endeavour
on a mission to the station in February 2010. He later returned to the station in November 2014 as Flight Engineer of Expedition 42/43.
According to the reference work
Ships of the Line
(p. 8), in the 2150s, the ISS has remained in Low Earth Orbit and hasn't been destroyed by a controlled re-entry. (In the real world, the ISS is planned for controlled re-entry 2030, a similar fate to MIR.)
From the
Encyclopedia
(4th ed., vol. 1, p. 372), "
The International Space Station (ISS) was featured in the main title sequence of
Star Trek: Enterprise
, beginning with the first episode, "
Broken Bow
". The decision to add the ISS to the
Enterprise
titles was a last-minute decision on the part of the producers. Because there was so little time to obtain accurate reference information for the construction of a digital model for visual effects, NASA
engineer
(and
Star Trek
fan) Brian J. Young, who at the time was a technician working for the
United Space Alliance
at NASA's
Kennedy Space Center
in
Florida
, went back to work on a Friday night and faxed us the plans for the station!
"
The Window Observational Research Facility (WORF), installed in 2010, is an experiment facility, through which crew members and remotely operated payloads can perform Earth and space science research, including photography, at the US Laboratory (known as Destiny) Science Window on the International Space Station. It was admitted that the naming was in tribute to the
Star Trek
character and the mission patch produced even had Klingon text on it.
[1]
External links
International Space Station
at
Wikipedia
Expedition 1
at
Wikipedia
Space Station Freedom
at
Wikipedia
Window Observational Research Facility
at
Wikipedia

in its

. This was the
homeworld
of the

. The planet was subject to
ionic storms
and had diplomatic relations with the

,

on the planet. It was after witnessing the

that a young

decided to become a

. (

: "

")

======================================================================
PAGE: Io Facility
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Io_Facility
======================================================================

Alternate reality
(
split 2233
)
Io Facility
Type:
Construction hangar
Owner:
United Federation of Planets
Operator:
Starfleet
(
Section 31
)
Location:
In
orbit
of
Io
,
Sol system
,
Alpha Quadrant
Status:
Active
(
2259
)
The
Io Facility
was a top secret
Starfleet
Section 31
construction hangar
located in
orbit
of the
Jovian
moon
Io
, at
coordinates
23.17.46.11 in the
Sol system
.
Shuttlecraft
could enter the interior bay of the large enclosed drydock when panel sections on the side of the dock were rotated and moved out of the way. These craft ferried parts for a
starship
to at least one of twelve
loading docks
.
The
Dreadnought
-class
starship
USS
Vengeance
was constructed at the facility and launched in
2259
, under the command of
Admiral
Alexander Marcus
. Before the launch,
Montgomery Scott
piloted
a shuttlecraft
into the interior of the facility, after
Captain
James T. Kirk
asked him to investigate the hangar's spatial coordinates, supplied by
Khan Noonien Singh
. Scott was able to stow away aboard the USS
Vengeance
before it departed from the hangar. (
Star Trek Into Darkness
)
Appendices
Background information
The Starfleet Top Secret Security Review, page 1
The Starfleet Top Secret Security Review, page 2
The facility was only referred to as a "construction hangar" in the film, and the nearby bodies not explicitly identified. The name "Io Facility" derives from a non-
canon
Starfleet Top Secret Security Review, released as a tie in to the movie. Khan, in the guise of
John Harrison
, had been stationed there.
[1]
A concept illustration of the facility was made available.
[2]
The black-colored hull of the construction facility proved difficult to light in space. Explained Co-Visual Effects Supervisor
Patrick Tubach
, "
Working with our art director
Yanick Dusseault
we came up with some concepts where we would pass him something and say what do you think we should be doing, how should we light the black hangar, where do we put the light? We know where the sun is, but that doesn’t really provide a very interesting frame, and you would get back images that were almost like models or miniatures lit in space, and you start to realize that’s what looks cinematic and good and meets with your expectations about the way you should be lighting something.
”
[3]
Apocrypha
The Io Facility in
Star Trek: Khan
According to the
novelization
of
Star Trek Into Darkness
, the facility's proximity to Jupiter's
magnetosphere
and Io's
plasma
torus caused spectrum distortions and upper hybrid resonance instabilities. These phenomena affected scanning and allowed Scott's shuttle to remain undetected by the facility.
The Io Facility is featured in the
fourth issue
of the comic
Star Trek: Khan
, in which Admiral Marcus and
Lieutenant
Yuki Sulu (
β
) used the station as a office to oversee
Section 31
missions, Khan's projects, and the progress on the
Vengeance
's construction.
External link
Io Facility
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Iota Geminorum IV
CATEGORIES: Planets

Iota Geminorum IV
AKA:
Tribble Prime
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Klingon Empire
Status:
Destroyed
(
23rd century
)
Location:
Iota Geminorum
system
Klingon space
Beta Quadrant
~120.4
light years
from
Sol
Iota Geminorum IV
, also known as
Tribble Prime
, was the
uninhabited
fourth
planet
in the
Iota Geminorum
system
. This planet was located inside
Klingon space
in the
Beta Quadrant
and was approximately 120.4
light years
from
Sol
. In the
23rd century
, this planet was within the
Klingon Empire
's
sphere of influence
.
The planet was
home
to the
tribble
, as well as an abundance of
reptiles
, the latter keeping the former in check. (
ENT
: "
The Breach
";
TOS
: "
The Trouble with Tribbles
")
History
Around
stardate
1421.9,
Starfleet
scientist
Edward Larkin
genetically-engineered
sample specimens of tribbles from the planet. Larkin, who was later described as an idiot, believed them to be a suitable food source save for their very slow
breeding rate
, which he increased with disastrous consequences. (
ST
: "
The Trouble with Edward
")
In
2260
, this planet was within the area of effect of a
quantum uncertainty field
. (
SNW
: "
Subspace Rhapsody
")
In order to fulfill their self-given task to
hunt
the tribble
species
to
extinction
, a Klingon
armada
obliterated the tribbles' homeworld, and by the late
23rd century
, they believed that they had entirely eradicated the species. (
DS9
: "
Trials and Tribble-ations
")
Stellar Cartography
In
2257
, this planet's
location
was labeled on the
star chart
showing the
trajectory
of
Lieutenant
Spock
's
shuttle
. (
DIS
: "
An Obol for Charon
")
In
2259
, the location of this planet was labeled on a
stellar cartography
chart that was seen on the
USS
Enterprise
's
ready room
viewscreen
. This planet's symbol was a
red
Klingon Empire
logo, indicating its affiliation with the
empire
. (
SNW
: "
Strange New Worlds
", "
Children of the Comet
")
In
2260
, the location of this planet was labeled on a star chart that was seen on the
Enterprise
when a
subspace fissure
was discovered. (
SNW
: "
Subspace Rhapsody
"
display graphic
)
Diagram of a tribble listing its home planet
In
2369
, a chart illustrating various creatures, including tribbles, from around the known
galaxy
was displayed in a
classroom
aboard
Deep Space 9
. The tribble homeworld was mentioned on this chart. (
DS9
: "
The Nagus
"
okudagram
)
The back of the
Hairy Berry
Tribbles cereal box (not seen on screen) featured a puzzle maze labeled "Help Jimmy the Tribble find its way back to Iota Geminorium IV", including a depiction of the planet.
[1]
The "comparative
xenobiology
" okudagram used the spelling "Geminorium", though it wasn't legible on screen.
According to
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
, a map of the Klingon Empire, which was prepared by the Scribe K'Tark for the
Klingon High Council
in the
Year of Kahless
893 (
2366
), identified Tribble Prime as a Klingon planet.

======================================================================
PAGE: Ivor Prime
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ivor_Prime
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Ivor Prime
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
(
3196
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Location:
Ivor
system
Beta Quadrant
-74
light years
from
Sol
Ivor Prime
was the
inhabited
prime
planet
in the
Ivor
system
,
Beta Quadrant
. It was approximately 74
light years
from
Sol
.
History
This planet hosted a
Federation
colony
in the
24th century
.
In
2373
, the colony was destroyed by a
Borg cube
en route to
Earth
, as part of another attempt at an
invasion
of the Federation.
Deep Space 5
informed
Vice Admiral
Hayes
of the colony's fate, who then passed on the news to
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
on the
USS
Enterprise
-E
in
2373
. (
Star Trek: First Contact
)
In the
32nd century
, Ivor Prime was a
Federation
member
planet. It was listed with other members on a video wall in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
Kids These Days
")
In
3196
, during the
Omega-47
crisis, the location of Ivor (Eta Scorpii) was depicted on star charts and readouts used across several locations on the
USS
Athena
to locate, monitor, and respond to the Omega-47 mines laid by the Venari Ral. This planet was outside the affected region. (
SA
: "
300th Night
")
Dialog implied that the colony was outside of the
Federation border
.
The location of this planet was inferred from the location of its star system as seen on the star chart seen in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Disengage
".
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I"), Ivor Prime was listed as a Federation member in
2378
.
External link
Ivor Prime
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

Iyar

)
Native species:
Iyaarans
Location:
Alpha
or

Iyar
was an
inhabited
planet
. This planet was known for its spectacular
crystal
formations. It was located in the same
sector
as a
class M
planet
, where the remains of a
Terellian
cargo freighter
had been discovered by the
Iyaarans
, containing the logs of the
Human
Anna
.
The planet was the
homeworld
of the Iyaaran

and the planet's

was headed by a

. (
TNG
: "

")

Planets of the UFP

======================================================================
PAGE: Izar
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Izar
======================================================================

Izar
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Habitable
(
32nd century
)
Location:
Izar system
Alpha Quadrant
~203
light years
from
Sol
Izar
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Izar system
. Located in the
Alpha Quadrant
, this planet was remembered the most as the place where
Fleet Captain
Garth
of
Starfleet
came from. It was approximately 203
light years
from
Sol
.
He was known as "Garth of Izar" during the
2260s
. While in a fit of
madness
and upon hearing
James T. Kirk
's address of him as "Garth" or "Captain Garth", he corrected Kirk, calling himself instead, "
Lord
Garth, formerly of Izar.
" (
TOS
: "
Whom Gods Destroy
";
PIC
: "
Disengage
")
In a
commercial
for
Tribbles
cereal
, Izar was shown on a
computer screen
of a
food synthesizer
in the
mess hall
of the
USS
Ravenous
. It was listed along with other locations such as
Earth
,
Vulcan
,
Andoria
,
Cestus
,
Tellar
,
Rigel VI
, and
Rigel X
. (
ST
: "
The Trouble with Edward
",
credit cookie
)
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
(pp. 32, 36) and
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
, Izar was the
third
planet in the system. in
2183
, after the first manned landing by the SS
Horizon
, an
Earth
colony
was founded on this planet. Within months of being founded, the Izar colony was admitted to the
United Federation of Planets
. The dominant species were
Humans
. In
2370
, the census counted 185 million living on this planet. The planetary capital was New Seattle. Major attractions on this planet included the Starfleet Tactical School and the Izar Institute of Meteorology. This world was a destination on a major space lane in the late
24th century
.
External link
Izar
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
