Jaffeworld
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2293
)
Location:
Beta Quadrant
Jaffeworld
was a
planet
in the
Beta Quadrant
.
In
2293
, Jaffeworld's position was labeled in the
star chart
for the "
Operation: Retrieve
" command briefing. The chart was from the
Starfleet Command Intelligence Database
. This star chart was seen in the
office
of the
Federation President
on
Earth
. (
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
,
set artwork
)
This planet's quadrant of origin is inferred based on the position of neighboring locations –
Starbase 24
and
Rura Penthe
– as seen in the
star charts
appearing in the
Star Trek: Discovery
episode "
Magic to Make the Sanest Man Go Mad
" and in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
This chart was seen in greater resolution at threads at Flare.Solareclipse.net.
[1]
(X)
[2]
(X)
Many of these locations shared their names with the last names of crew who worked on the sixth film. This location was probably named after the producer and second unit director
Steven-Charles Jaffe
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Janlee
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Janlee
======================================================================

Janlee as mentioned in Danar's file
Janlee
was a location in the
Tarsian War
.
After the
Todfrey Campaign
,
Roga Danar
was assigned his second
tour of duty
. He was assigned to Janlee, where he was under the command of
Marjmacl
. In his time at Janlee, Danar was
promoted
to
third hadar
.
In
2366
, this location was mentioned in Danar's military service records, which were accessible to the crew of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
via the
Angosian senatorial database
. (
TNG-R
: "
The Hunted
",
okudagram
)
This location was only mentioned in writing.
The location is named after
Janice Lee
, compositor at
CBS Digital
, who worked on the remastered
TNG
project.

======================================================================
PAGE: Janus VI
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Janus_VI
======================================================================

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Janus VI
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Geologically dead
(
2371
)
Native species:
Horta
Location:
Janus
system
Alpha Quadrant
Janus VI
was the
inhabited
sixth
planet
of the
Janus
system
. The planet was located in the
Alpha Quadrant
and was the
homeworld
for the intelligent
Horta
, a non-
humanoid
species
. (
TOS
: "
The Devil in the Dark
")
History
A long-established
colony
, the
Janus VI colony
contained production facilities that as of
2267
had been in operation for over fifty
years
. The colony was a major producer of
pergium
, a dozen planets depending on it. Janus IV had no current
volcanic
activity, and no
atmosphere
. Though the planet was rich in
mineral
resources, reaching them was difficult with standard
mining
technology
. Minerals present included
gold
,
pergium
,
platinum
,
cerium
, and
uranium
.
The Federation was unaware of the Horta until that year, when a new level was opened up and the miners inadvertently began destroying Horta
eggs
, causing the
mother Horta
to retaliate by sabotaging equipment and killing fifty colonists by the time she was stopped three
months
later. Once it was realized that the Horta was a sapient creature, an agreement was created so the two sides could share the planet. (
TOS
: "
The Devil in the Dark
")
In
2269
, when attempting to understand the threat they faced on the
Kalandan outpost
, after
Captain
James T. Kirk
asked if the
rocks
could have life,
Lieutenant
Hikaru Sulu
thought it possible that the captain was thinking back to his time on Janus VI and his experience with the Horta.
Lieutenant Commander
Leonard McCoy
disagreed with the captain arguing that the Horta registered as a
lifeform
and the
medical tricorder
wasn't picking up
life signs
from the rocks. (
TOS
: "
That Which Survives
")
In
2328
, this was the destination for passengers traveling from
Denkir Orbital City
via commercial
transport
.
Hilary Groner
and
Robert Levinson
traveled there on the
SS
Kogin
. (
TNG-R
: "
Inheritance
",
okudagram
)
Stellar cartography
In
2293
, the
location
of Janus VI in the
Milky Way Galaxy
was labeled in the
star chart
"The Explored Galaxy", which was on display in
Captain
James T. Kirk
's
quarters
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-A
. (
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
,
okudagram
)
In
2365
, the location of Janus VI was labeled in a Subspace Comm Net Ops star chart, which was seen in the
courtroom
on
Starbase 173
.
This planet was connected to
Kling
and
Ariannus
by subspace lines. (
TNG
: "
The Measure Of A Man
")
In
2367
, the
location
of Janus VI was labeled on a
tactical situation monitor
in
Captain
Benjamin Maxwell
's
ready room
on the
USS
Phoenix
. (
TNG
: "
The Wounded
",
okudagram
)
In
2371
, the location of Janus VI was labeled on a star chart that
Data
and
Picard
were studying in
stellar cartography
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. (
Star Trek Generations
, okudagram)
In
2366
, in an
alternate timeline
, during the
Federation-Klingon War
, Janus VI's position was labeled on a tactical situation monitor on the
bridge
and in the
ready room
of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
.
(
TNG
: "
Yesterday's Enterprise
",
okudagram
)
The location of Janus VI on "The Explored Galaxy" star chart
The location of Janus VI on a tactical situation monitor
Appendices
Background information
In August 2024, an annotated computer paste-up of the Subspace Comm Net Ops star chart was put up for auction in the Entertainment Memorabiliia Live Auction: Los Angeles Summer 2024 by Propstore Auction.
[1]
The
script
notes described Janus VI as "
an ugly planet...
reddish
-
brown
, with a thick, turbulent layer of
clouds
, slowly rotating.
" Further script notes described it as "
hostile looking, reddish-brown, a
boiling
atmosphere
.
"
For the
2006
"
remastered
" version of "The Devil in the Dark", a new, computer-generated version of Janus VI was created to replace the stock footage used in so many
TOS
episodes. New shots of the planet's
surface
were also created.
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 23), Janus VI was classified as a
class-F planet
.
Additional unused text prepared for
Jinaal Bix
's
personnel file
in
DIS
: "
Jinaal
" referenced extensive
zoological
-study
safaris
on Janus VI.
[2]
Apocrypha
In the
Star Trek Maps
(p. 26), Janus VI was a class-H planet and one of ten planets orbiting the moderate yellow star Janus.
In
The Worlds of the Federation
(p. 110), Janus was a class-E planet known as Shaul by the Horta.
In
Decipher
's
RPG
sourcebook
Worlds
, Janus VI was located in the Alpha Janus system.
Janus VI was additionally mentioned in the
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine
Pocket Books
novel
Devil in the Sky
and, in the
IDW
comic book
A Matter of Perspective
, the
Enterprise
was enroute to revisit the planet before being redirected for a
distress call
from a pleasure cruise.
External links
Janus VI
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Planets in TOS and TOS Remastered
at
Ex Astris Scientia

PAGE: Jaros II
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Jaros_II
======================================================================

Jaros II
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Habitable
(
2368
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Jaros II
was the
inhabited
second
planet
in its
star system
. This planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
In the
2360s
, this
Federation
planet was the home for a
Starfleet
stockade
.
Sometime during that
decade
,
Ensign
Ro Laren
of the
USS
Wellington
was
court martialed
for disobeying
direct orders
on an
away mission
to
Garon II
. Her disobedience was alleged to have led to the
deaths
of
eight of her crewmates
. At her court martial, Ro refused to speak in her defense, and the
court
found her guilty. She was imprisoned in the stockade on Jaros II until her release in
2368
. Her advice that same year was that if one was sent to the Jaros II stockade, a
room
in the
east
wing
was preferable to one on the
west
, as the latter became "awfully hot in the
afternoon
."  (
TNG
: "
Ensign Ro
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
In the
script
, the pronunciation for Jaros was "JAHR-ohs".
[1]
See also
Jaros colony
External link
Jaros
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Jaros II
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

Jaxxa Prime
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
24th century
)
Native species:
A species of
rectal insectoids
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Jaxxa Prime
was the
prime
planet
in its
star system
. It was home to a
species
of
rectal insectoids
.
During their reunion in
2380
,
K'orin
reminisced with
Beckett Mariner
about a
commander
they met there who didn't believe her about rectal insectoids. Mariner
laughed
about the incident, recalled, "
Yeah, I got
chewed out
, but his
ass
got chewed up.
" (
LD
: "
Envoys
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Jengus IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Jengus_IV
======================================================================

Jengus IV
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2381
)
Satellite(s):
At least one
moon
Native species:
Scrubble
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Jengus IV
was the
inhabited
fourth
planet
in its
star system
and
homeworld
of the
Scrubble
, a
silicate
humanoid
species
. It had at least one
moon
. Its surface was
rocky
, with many pinnacles and
caves
; the latter were occupied by a type of "
cave bug
".
Believing the remote planet to be uninhabited, in the
24th century
the
Federation
established
Outpost 76
on Jengus IV for scientific research. The intrusion of the scientists apparently incensed the Scrubble, who began a conflict in
2381
using
psychic mines
that turned their victims to
stone
. Responding to the
outpost
's
distress call
,
Captain
Murakami
of the
USS
Hood
was able to broker a tentative
peace treaty
.
The
USS
Cerritos
and
USS
Carlsbad
subsequently coordinated to relocate the outpost and dispose of the psychic mines. In the course of doing so, their
away teams
discovered that the conflict between the Scrubble and the scientists had in fact been contrived between them for a scheme to harvest sensitive
Starfleet
intelligence and sell it on the
black market
. (
LD
: "
Mining The Mind's Mines
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Jongri
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Jongri
======================================================================

Jongri as mentioned in Danar's file
Jongri
was the location of a battle during the
Tarsian War
.
According to
Roga Danar
's service record,
Primehadar
Erbrun
and
Marvhild
recommended an early promotion for Danar to
subhadar
because of his performance at
Dylhuc
and Jongri.
In
2366
, this location was mentioned in Danar's military service records, which were accessible to the crew of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
via the
Angosian senatorial database
. (
TNG-R
: "
The Hunted
",
okudagram
)
This location was only mentioned in writing.
The location is named after
Jon Grinsberg
, Editor at
CBS Digital
, who worked on the remastered
TNG
project.

======================================================================
PAGE: Jouret IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Jouret_IV
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Jouret IV
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
(
3195
)
Status:
Inhabited
(
3196
)
Location:
Jouret
system
Beta Quadrant
Jouret IV
was the
fourth
planet
in the
Jouret
system
of the
Beta Quadrant
. There were no known
avifaunal
or crawling
vermicular lifeforms
on Jouret IV.
History
In
2366
, it was an
inhabited
planet. Founded over a
century
before, the
New Providence
colony
was one of the Federation's outermost colonies.
That
year
, Jouret IV was attacked and the New Providence colony was destroyed by the
Borg
. Before its destruction, the colony sent a
distress signal
which was intercepted by the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. The starship responded to the call; however, by the time the ship arrived it found the colony totally destroyed. An investigation confirmed this was the work of the Borg. (
TNG
: "
The Best of Both Worlds
";
DIS
: "
Magic to Make the Sanest Man Go Mad
")
In the
32nd century
, Jouret IV was a
Federation
member
planet. It was listed with other members on a video wall in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
Kids These Days
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this system was within the enclosed region. (
SA
: "
Rubincon
")
Stellar cartography
Jouret IV
In
2376
, the
location
of Jouret IV was labeled on a
star chart
in
astrometrics
on
USS
Voyager
. (
VOY
: "
Child's Play
",
okudagram
)
Inhabitants
See
:
Jouret IV inhabitants
Appendices
Background information
Jouret (mislabeled "Journet") was located in the Beta Quadrant on star charts seen in the
Star Trek: Discovery
episode "
Magic to Make the Sanest Man Go Mad
" and in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
". The colony's
name
was mentioned with the system's name in the former chart.
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I"), Jouret IV was listed as a Federation member in
2378
.
Apocrypha
In the computer
game
Star Trek Online
, a starbase-sized
Iconian gateway
was revealed near the planet in 2409 which was previously hidden in
subspace
. It was hinted that readings of this hidden gateway drew the Borg to this system
decades
earlier in the first place.
External link
Jouret IV
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Juhraya
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Juhraya
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Juhraya
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
(formerly)
Cardassian Union
(
24th century
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Location:
Juhraya
system
Alpha Quadrant
Juhraya
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Juhraya
system
,
Alpha Quadrant
.
History
Juhraya was colonized by the
United Federation of Planets
.
Between
2368
and
2373
, this planet's system was in the
Demilitarized Zone
.
In
2370
, the planet's system moved over to the
Cardassian
side of the zone, with the
Cardassian Union
taking over governance of the Juhraya system.
Maquis
leader
Macias
was once a resident on Juhraya, until he was beaten without provocation by the Cardassians. His was one of many incidents designed to "encourage" Federation settlers to leave their colonies.
The colony operated
freighters
by 2370; one of them was destroyed by Cardassian
colonists
this
year
. (
TNG
: "
Preemptive Strike
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
External link
Juhraya
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Jupiter
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Jupiter
======================================================================

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Jupiter
AKA:
Sol V
Class:
Class J
Type:
Planet
Diameter:
142,984
kilometers
(88,865
miles
)
(
equatorial
diameter
)
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Intact
(
3196
)
Satellite(s):
Europa
Ganymede
Io
Many other
moons
and a
ring system
Native species:
None
Location:
Sol system
Sector 001
Alpha Quadrant
For the Roman god, please see
Jupiter (mythology)
.
For the automobile, please see
Jupiter 8
.
Jupiter
, also known as
Sol V
, was the
fifth
and largest
planet
of the
Sol system
. This
Class J
gas giant
was located in
Sector 001
of the
Alpha Quadrant
. The planet was known for the Great Red Spot, its many moons, including the
Galilean moons
, and bands of
cloud
layers. This planet was located near to the
asteroid belt
. (
TOS
: "
The Changeling
", "
By Any Other Name
";
VOY
: "
Future's End
";
Star Trek: The Motion Picture
;
Star Trek Into Darkness
)
Dimensions
Jupiter's
diameter
was more than 11 times the diameter of
Earth
and its
volume
was over 1,300 times that of Earth. In other words, 1,300 Earths could fit inside this gas giant. (
PIC
: "
Two of One
")
According to
Arex
, the
cosmic cloud
encountered by the
Enterprise
in
2269
was twice the
diameters
of Jupiter,
Saturn
, and
Neptune
in
size
. (
TAS
: "
One of Our Planets Is Missing
")
History
Before the invention of the
telescope
,
Humans
had discovered this planet in the night sky. (
TNG
: "
Loud As A Whisper
"
okudagram
)
In the late
1970s
, this planet was surveyed by
Pioneer 11
, which discovered the planet's
magnetic field
and
magnetosphere
. (
TNG
: "
Loud As A Whisper
"
okudagram
)
Prior to the release of Season 2 on
Blu-ray
, the text containing information on Jupiter in "Loud As A Whisper" was illegible. The original source for the information was from
Pioneer: First to Jupiter, Saturn, and Beyond
(NASA Publication SP-446, 1980)
[1]
According to the
Star Fleet Medical Reference Manual
, (p. 17) Humans discovered
dilithium
in
2049
on the "fifth moon of Jupiter", which would be
Io
by today's numbering.
Launched in
2024
to arrive in
2028
, the manned
Europa Mission
visited Jupiter and its moons, including landings on
Europa
and
Io
. On the latter, a
microorganism
was discovered. (
PIC
: "
Watcher
", "
Fly Me to the Moon
")
In
2143
, the
NX-Alpha
was destroyed near Jupiter shortly after breaking the
warp 2 barrier
. By
2151
,
Jupiter Station
in orbit of Jupiter served as a repair facility to Earth
Starfleet
and the
Earth Cargo Service
. (
ENT
: "
Silent Enemy
", "
Fortunate Son
", "
First Flight
", et al.)
That year,
Charles Tucker III
planned to take
Enterprise
NX-01
up to warp 4.5 once the ship had passed Jupiter. (
ENT
: "
Broken Bow
")
An image of Jupiter appeared in
Daniels' database
on two occasions when
Captain
Jonathan Archer
accessed it in
2152
. (
ENT
: "
Shockwave
", "
Future Tense
")
In
2257
, the
Klingon
fleet
passed through the Jovian system on its way towards Earth. Later, on a course to
Vulcan
, the
USS
Discovery
passed through the system, flying close to Jupiter. (
DIS
: "
Will You Take My Hand?
")
Jupiter viewed from Starbase 1 in 2259
In
2259
,
Starbase 1
had been towed to Jupiter orbit (after having previously been stationed in the outer solar system). In this year, the
USS
Enterprise
docked at Starbase 1 to receive comprehensive
inspections
,
systems checks
, and
upgrades
from the
chief fleet inspector
,
Commander
Pelia
, and her team from Operational Support Services. (
SNW
: "
The Broken Circle
") Over the next few years, Jupiter and Starbase 1 continued to be the home port of the
Enterprise
and other ships such as the
USS
Farragut
. They visited frequently, including for the
2261
Federation Day
centennial celebrations. (
SNW
: "
Wedding Bell Blues
")
In
2259
of the
alternate reality
, the
USS
Vengeance
was launched from the
Io Facility
, a
spacedock
operated by
Section 31
in orbit of Jupiter's moon Io.
(
Star Trek Into Darkness
)
A close-up image of Jupiter was contained in the
library computer
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
. This image was flashed on a
viewscreen
when the
Talosians
scanned the
Enterprise
computer
in
2254
. (
TOS-R
: "
The Cage
")
In the
2270s
, the
Enterprise
flew past Jupiter on its way to intercept
V'ger
. (
Star Trek: The Motion Picture
)
By the early
24th century
a
shuttle
route had been established "
from Jupiter to
Saturn
and back, once a
day
, every day,
" know as "
the
Jovian Run
.
" Both
Edward Jellico
and
Geordi La Forge
used to
pilot
shuttles on this route early in their
Starfleet
careers
. (
TNG
: "
Chain Of Command, Part II
")
In
2367
,
Jupiter Outpost 92
reported visual contact of a
Borg cube
at 12:13. (
TNG
: "
The Best of Both Worlds, Part II
")
According to a
sarcastic
remark by
Lewis Zimmerman
, tours of the
lava
flows on Jupiter's third moon were popular by
2377
. (
VOY
: "
Life Line
")
According to real-world science, the third moon of Jupiter is
Amalthea
, a small asteroid. This is more likely an intended reference to
Ganymede
, which is numbered as Jupiter III, as it was the third-closest moon of the four large moons discovered by
Galileo Galilei
. Ganymede has the evidence of ancient lava flows on its surface, but it is not known if it is still volcanically active.
In
2401
, a
Borg cube
emerged from a
transwarp conduit
in Jupiter's atmosphere to meet
Jack Crusher
. (
PIC
: "
Võx
") After detecting the ship hiding in the Great Red Spot, the
USS
Enterprise
-D
engaged and ultimately destroyed it above Jupiter. (
PIC
: "
The Last Generation
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet was within the enclosed region. (
SA
: "
300th Night
")
Jupiter and
Europa
in 2024

NX-Alpha
near Jupiter in 2143
Jupiter in
Daniels' database

Io
in 2259

, 2259
Enterprise
in orbit of Jupiter, 2259

USS
Enterprise
traveling past Jupiter in the 2270s

in 2377

A Borg Cube emerging from within Jupiter
Stellar cartography
In
2024
, the position and orbital path of Jupiter were depicted on a map of the Solar System, which was seen at the
gala
held for the
Europa Mission
astronauts
. (
PIC
: "
Two of One
")
In
2267
, the position and orbital path of Jupiter were depicted on "Chart 14A: The Sol System", stored in the
USS
Enterprise
library computer. It was identified by name and by its
astronomical symbol
. The chart was scanned by the
probe
Nomad
in
auxiliary control
. (
TOS
: "
The Changeling
"
production art
)
In the mid-
2270s
, after one point eight
hours
from launch in
Earth
orbit
, the
Enterprise
traveling at warp point five reached the Jovian system. The
ship
, staying close to the planet, passed through the system. Later, a star chart that labeled the location of Jupiter in a neighborhood of astronomical objects, stored in the
Enterprise
library computer, was scanned by
V'ger
. (
Star Trek: The Motion Picture
production art
)
The position and orbital path of Jupiter on "Chart 14A: The Solar System"
Sol and Jupiter among other nearby astronomical objects

:
Sol

:

•
Venus
•
Earth
(
Luna
) •

(
Deimos
) •

(

;
Ganymede
;
Europa
) •

Appendices
Background information
Jupiter was named after the
Roman
god
Jupiter
, who was the
king
of the gods.
A balloon-borne craft in the atmosphere of Jupiter
Jupiter as seen in the TNG opening sequence
In the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 394, the authors wrote that Jupiter had a "
equatorial
diameter
of 142,700
kilometers
, and it orbits its sun at about 778 million
kilometers
.
" Additionally, the authors of the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 149 classified Jupiter as a
Class J gas giant
.
A
painting
seen in several episodes aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
was originally created by
Rick Sternbach
to illustrate a (non-
Trek
)
science fiction
story
called "The Anvil of Jove". As such, it depicted a
airplane
-like craft tethered to a
balloon
, cruising the
atmosphere
of Jupiter.
[2]
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine
set decorations mentioned the
Jupiter Mining Corporation
, which may be associated with this planet.
The chart of Sol/Jupiter in relation to nearby astronomical objects seen in
Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan
was taken from page T0:02:07:02 of the
Star Fleet Technical Manual
by
Franz Joseph
.
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
(Pgs. 22, "United Federation of Planets I"), Jupiter was classified as a
J-class planet
. This planet was a charter member of the
United Federation of Planets
in
2161
.
Jupiter was one of the
astronimcal
objects
seen in the opening sequence of
Star Trek: The Next Generation
, alongside the
Earth
,
Luna
,
Sol
, and
Saturn
, shortly before the
Enterprise
-D started its exploration of unknown space.
External links
Jupiter
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Jupiter
at
Wikipedia
Moons of Jupiter
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Jupiter Station
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Jupiter_Station
======================================================================

Jupiter Station
Type:
Orbital Station
Owner:
United Earth
United Federation of Planets
Operator:
Starfleet
Military Assault Command Operations
Location:
In
orbit
of
Jupiter
Sol
system
Sector 001
Alpha Quadrant
Status:
Active
(
2376
)
Jupiter Research Station
, also more simply known as
Jupiter Station
, was a
Starfleet
research station
located in
orbit
of
Jupiter
.
History
22nd century
In the
22nd century
, Jupiter Station served as a maintenance and
repair facility
for Starfleet vessels, as well as a supply stop for
cargo
ships and a training facility for the
Military Assault Command Operations
.
Prior to setting out on its
mission
in
2151
, the
cargo freighter
ECS
Fortunate
stopped at Jupiter Station to take on cargo, where the
cargo handlers
failed to properly
balance
the
load
in one of the
Fortunate
's
cargo modules
. This caused a
vibration
in the module, which was eventually corrected when the
crew
redistributed the cargo. (
ENT
: "
Fortunate Son
")
Later that year, in the face of constant attack by
an unknown species
,
Captain
Archer
decided to reverse course and take
Enterprise
NX-01
back to Jupiter Station, where the
armory team
could install state-of-the-art
phase cannons
, with which
Enterprise
could better defend itself. Feeling a sense of personal pride in the project, however,
Commander
Charles Tucker
and
Lieutenant
Malcolm Reed
took it upon themselves to complete the modifications, constructing and installing two phase cannons in forty-eight hours, whereas it was estimated that it would take two weeks for the armory team to complete the project. (
ENT
: "
Silent Enemy
")
In
2152
, MACO
Major
J. Hayes
and his team developed a specialized training system at Jupiter Station, wherein a trainee would utilize a
particle rifle
in an attempt to hit as many
holographic
targets as possible in a ten-second period. The MACOs later utilized this program to help train officers on board
Enterprise
, where they had been assigned to augment the crew in their mission in the
Delphic Expanse
. (
ENT
: "
Harbinger
")
MACO
Corporal
J. McKenzie
did a six-week tour on Jupiter Station, where she gained experience operating in
zero gravity
conditions when the
grav-plating
would periodically fail. In
2153
, McKenzie related this story to Captain Archer when justifying her presence on an
away mission
to a derelict alien vessel where the environmental systems were offline. (
ENT
: "
Anomaly (ENT)
")
In the year
2154
of an
alternate timeline
, where half of
Enterprise
's
warp coils
were fused in a ramming maneuver,
Trip Tucker
estimated their repair to take three weeks if they were at Jupiter Station, but at least six months out in the
Delphic Expanse
.
(
ENT
: "
Twilight
")
24th century
During the mid-
2360s
,
Tuvok
was temporarily assigned to Jupiter Station where he often wrote to
Kathryn Janeway
. (
VOY
: "
Tuvix
")
In the
2370s
, the
commanding officer
of Jupiter Station was a
female
. (
DS9
: "
Doctor Bashir, I Presume
")
In his
last will and testament
,
Dr.
Lewis Zimmerman
requested that, after his death, his
holographic
assistant
Haley
be allowed to remain active on the station for as long as the station continued to be operational. (
VOY
: "
Life Line
")
It's unclear if the 22nd and 24th century Jupiter Stations are one and the same, or if a new facility was built, using the same name as the old one.
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 394 failed to provide clarity on this issue, as the
authors
of this
reference work
only mentioned events connected with the 24th century Jupiter Station in the entry about the space station.
Design
Jupiter Station's structure as it existed in the 24th century was that of two sets of three
saucer section
hulls
on top of each other, with a series of antennae and
sensors
extending from the bottom.
Similar to a
starship
's
mess hall
, the station had a
café
. In
2373
, that cafe's owner decided to leave, and its ownership was offered to
Leeta
by the station's commander (at the behest of Dr. Zimmerman) but she refused, deciding to remain on
Deep Space 9
. (
DS9
: "
Doctor Bashir, I Presume
")
Holoprogramming Center
The front desk to Dr. Zimmerman's lab
By the
2360s
, the station was the location of the
Jupiter Station Holoprogramming Center
, run by
Dr.
Lewis Zimmerman
. (
VOY
: "
Projections
", "
Life Line
")
It was on Jupiter Station where Dr. Zimmerman created the
Emergency Medical Hologram
holoprogram
. (
VOY
: "
The Cloud
")
Zimmerman offered Leeta a job in the café on the station during his visit to
Deep Space 9
in
2373
to interview
Julian Bashir
for the template of
Long-term Medical Holographic program
. (
DS9
: "
Doctor Bashir, I Presume
")
Personnel
See
:
Jupiter Station personnel
Appendices
See also
Jupiter Outpost 92
Background information
The Jupiter Station
studio model
was designed by
Rick Sternbach
.
[1]
(X)
Sternbach drew inspiration from the
Spacedock One
model as well as
a painting
he had done for
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
's quarters. Sternbach has suggested the saucers may be recycled from
decommissioned
vessels, perhaps
Ambassador
-class
or similar. Sternbach also speculated that the dual extensions at the bottom of the station provided power to the station.
[2]
The Jupiter Station insignia was designed by
Star Trek: Voyager
scenic artist
Wendy Drapanas
. (
Star Trek Sticker Book
, pg. 14)
Apocrypha
Jupiter Station appears in the
Star Trek Online
episode "Red Shift". In it,
Mirror universe
versions of
Kathryn Janeway
,
Sylvia Tilly
, Kuumaarke, and the mirror version of the player character, referred to as the "Inquisitor", invade the Prime universe and the Jupiter Station in search of what is called the "Emperor's Prize". The four ravage the station and reach their goal: the recently returned
Ilia
, though Tilly, Kuumaarke and the Inquisitor turn against Janeway and steal Ilia for themselves.
External link
Jupiter Station
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

Jura'assic IV
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2370
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Jura'assic IV
was the
fourth
planet
in its
star system
.
In
2370
, Jura'assic IV was listed on the
Deep Space 9
arrival roster
as the point of departure for the
CGM
Gh'aster
. (
DS9
: "
Whispers
",
production art
)
This planet was only mentioned in writing.

in-joke
reference to the film
Jurassic Park
.

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
K'Normia
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
(
3195
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3195
)
Native species:
K'normians
Location:
Psi Tauri
system
Alpha Quadrant
K'Normia
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Psi Tauri
system
,
Alpha Quadrant
. This was the
homeworld
of the
K'normians
.
In the
32nd century
, K'Normia was a
Federation
member
planet. It was listed with other members on a video wall in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
Kids These Days
")
In
3195
, this planet's position was labeled on a
Federation
star chart
which was displayed in
Starfleet Academy
's
Stellar Cartography
laboratory
. (
SA
: "
Beta Test
")
This planet was only mentioned in writing.
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I"), K'Normia was listed as a Federation member in
2378
.

======================================================================
PAGE: K'Tuevon Prime

K'Tuevon Prime
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2380
)
Satellite(s):
At least
one moon
Native species:
Primes
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
K'Tuevon Prime
was the
inhabited
prime
planet
in its
star system
and the
homeworld
of the
Primes
, a technologically advanced
humanoid
civilization. The planet had at least one
moon
. A
species
of
eel
was native to the planet.
In
2380
, the
USS
Cerritos
delivered
Imperium Magistrate
Clar
to this planet after rescuing him from the
Romulans
. (
LD
: "
Veritas
")
The pattern of nightside lights on K'Tuevon Prime are those of present-day
Asia
, tilted sideways. The
Indian
coastline can be seen just below the equator.

======================================================================
PAGE: K'ushui
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/K'ushui
======================================================================

K'ushui
Type:
Astronomical
object
Status:
Intact
(
2285
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
K'ushui
was an
astronomical
object
in the same
region
as
Sol
/
Jupiter
.
In
2285
, K'ushui's position was displayed on a
star chart
on the
bridge
of the
USS
Enterprise
. (
Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan
)
This astronomical object was only mentioned in writing.
The image was taken from page T0:02:07:02 of the
Star Fleet Technical Manual
by
Franz Joseph
. The map claimed to depict principal
star systems
in the
Federation
. Based on the scale for the chart, K'ushui was located approximately two and a half
parsecs
from Sol.
External link
K'ushui
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Kabatris
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kabatris
======================================================================

Kabatris
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
24th century
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Kabatris
was an
inhabited
planet
that was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
Prior to
stardate
41636
,
William T. Riker
spoke with the
leadership council
of Kabatris, wearing
furs
in keeping with their wishes.
In 2364, Riker made reference to this event while talking about the compromises required for
diplomacy
. For a meeting with the
Elected One
,
Beata
, on
Angel I
, Riker wore a revealing outfit which was the traditional
male
garb. When
Natasha Yar
and
Deanna Troi
confronted him about his
chest
-baring get-up, he compared the outfit to the fur adornments he'd worn to the Kabatris meeting, as well as another meeting on
Armus IX
, where he wore
feathers
. (
TNG
: "
Angel One
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
At
StarTrek.com
, the pronunciation for Kabatris was given as "kuh-BATT-triss".
[1]
(X)

======================================================================
PAGE: Kabrel I
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kabrel_I
======================================================================

Kabrel I
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Habitable
(
2374
)
Native species:
Protozoa
Tri-nucleic fungus
Location:
Kabrel
system
Alpha Quadrant
Kabrel I
was the
uninhabited
first
planet
in the
Kabrel
system
. This system was located in
Federation
space
, near the
Federation-Cardassian border
in the
2370s
, in the
Alpha Quadrant
. The planet was home to simple
protozoa
and
tri-nucleic fungi
.
In
2374
, the
Dominion
attempted to negotiate with the Federation for control of Kabrel I, as they could utilize the fungi to replenish their supplies of
ketracel-white
for an indefinite period. (
DS9
: "
Statistical Probabilities
")
This planet's quadrant of origin is inferred based on the position of the
Cardassian Union
as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
According to the
script
, the pronunciation for Kabrel was "kuh-BRELL".
[1]
The
name
of the planet was not mentioned in the episode, where it was described as the "first planet" of the system, and was from the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 397.
See also
Kabrel II

,
2367
("

")
Kaelon II
was the
inhabited
second
planet
in its
star system
. Located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
, this
Class M
world was the
homeworld
of the
Kaelons
, a
warp-capable
isolationist
humanoid
species
.
The gradual expansion of the
Kaelon sun
into a
red giant
threatened to destroy or sterilize the planet by the
2390s
, and the Kaelons directed their efforts to revitalize it, refusing to evacuate. In
2367
, a leading
scientist
of their world was
Dr.
Timicin
. (
TNG
: "
Half a Life
")
In
2371
, the
location
of Kaelon II was labeled in the
star chart
Data
and
Picard
were studying in
stellar cartography
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. (
Star Trek Generations
display graphic
)
The planet's class came from the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 398 and
StarTrek.com
.

Kaldra IV
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
(
3195
)
Status:

(

)

or
Beta Quadrant
Kaldra IV
was the
inhabited
fourth
planet
in its
star system
.
History
The
USS
Enterprise
-D
was transporting three
Ullian
telepathic
historians
to this planet in
2368
, whose inhabitants they hoped to add to their library of individual
memories
. However, the
Enterprise
was diverted to the Ullian
homeworld
after
Jev
's perpetration of
telepathic memory invasion
was discovered. (
TNG
: "
Violations
")
In the
32nd century
, Kaldra IV was a
Federation
member
planet. It was listed with other members on a video wall in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
Kids These Days
")
Stellar cartography
In
2376
, the
location
of Kaldra IV was labeled on a
star chart
in
astrometrics
on
USS
Voyager
in
2376
. (
VOY
: "
Child's Play
",
display graphics
)

Sector 22139
.

("United Federation of Planets I") and the

, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 400 identified Kaldra IV as a Federation planet.

Status:

Kalla III
was the
uninhabited
third
planet
of the
Kalla
system
.
Kalla III had rich
magnesite
deposits and, although the planet was located within
Pakled
territory, they did not know about the magnesite or its value.
Therefore, in
2370
, the
Duras sisters
and their
partner
Gorta
undertook a
mining
enterprise on this planet, mining over 10,000
kilograms
of magnesite. The sisters then betrayed Gorta, took all the
ore
and left Kalla III for the
Ufandi system
without their partner. He later was taken aboard by the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. (
TNG
: "
Firstborn
")
"KALL-uh" was the pronunciation for this planet's name from the
script
pronunciation guide.

======================================================================
PAGE: Kaminar
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kaminar
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Kaminar
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Kelpien and Ba'ul Alliance
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Satellite(s):
Two
moons
Ring system
Native species:
Kelpiens
Ba'ul
Location:
Kaminar
system
Alpha Quadrant
For the 24th century Human, please see
Kaminer
; for the Enolian penal colony, please see
Canamar
.
"
If I didn't know your people were
oppressed
, I'd think Kaminar was a
paradise
."
"Oh, in many ways, it is. We live in harmony with each other, in a world without
hunger
or
poverty
. The only violence we know is the culling, which
Kelpiens
have been misled to accept as a mercy by the
Ba'ul
.
"
–
Michael Burnham
and
Saru
,
2257
("
The Sound of Thunder
")
Kaminar
was a
ringed
class M
planet
, the
fourth planet
in the
Kaminar
system
,
Alpha Quadrant
. It was
home
to two sapient
species
– the
warp-capable
Ba'ul
and the
pre-warp
Kelpiens
. (
ST
: "
The Brightest Star
";
DIS
: "
An Obol for Charon
")
Unlike
Earth
, which had a
food chain
, the
species map
of this planet was
binary
, where a
lifeform
was either
predator
or
prey
. (
DIS
: "
The Vulcan Hello
")
Planetary features
Airiam reviews planetary data on Kaminar gathered from the
Sphere
Kaminar had a
nitrogen
/
oxygen
atmosphere
, with a
mean surface temperature
of
24 °C
, and a
volume
equivalent to 1.1454
Earths
. (
DIS
: "
The Sound of Thunder
")
The data Airiam reviewed from the Sphere (when reversed) listed "Satellites: None", though Kaminar was usually depicted with two moons. Saru also recalled harvesting
kelp
"under the moonlight" in "
Forget Me Not
".
Narrows Firth
was an
estuary
on Kaminar. (
DIS
: "
That Hope Is You, Part 2
")
There was a small,
equatorial
desert
on Kaminar, whose
climate
was comparable to
Ni'Var
. (
DIS
: "
...But to Connect
")
Flora and fauna
Lifeforms
on the planet included flying
animals
. Animal sounds could be heard constantly. The Kelpiens
harvested
a
kelp
-like
aquatic
plant, as well as
flowers
, including
fredalias
. (
ST
: "
The Brightest Star
") A type of Kaminar
succulent
grew in an equatorial desert on the planet. (
DIS
: "
...But to Connect
")
Fredalia
Kaminar sea frog
Swampkelp
Valebeast
History
In the first
century
of the
1st millennium BC
, Kaminar was sparsely populated. The planet's
population
was divided into three groups: the Ba'ul, the pre-
vahar'ai
Kelpiens, and the post-
vahar'ai
Kelpiens. The post-
vahar'ai
Kelpiens were the main predatory species on the planet, preying on the Ba'ul. Within eighty
years
, the pre-
vahar'ai
Kelpiens had either
evolved
into being post-
vahar'ai
Kelpiens or had died out. Starting in the first century
AD
, the Ba'ul were being driven to near
extinction
by the post-
vahar'ai
Kelpiens. Then, there was a shift, wherein the Ba'ul population rebounded, with the post-
vahar'ai
Kelpiens dying out.
At some point, there was a resurrection of the pre-
vahar'ai
Kelpiens. This reborn species had the potential of becoming post-
vahar'ai
Kelpiens. Their numbers started to increase, yet they were always smaller in number than the Ba'ul. These events were recorded by the
alien
lifeform
known to the
Federation
as the
Sphere
. Data from these recordings was later used in the creation of the
Kaminar population history
by the
USS
Discovery
crew in
2257
. (
DIS
: "
The Sound of Thunder
")
At some point between the
23rd century
and the events of "
The Burn
", Kaminar joined the Federation. As of the late
32nd century
, Kaminar was too far out of range to be considered an active member of the reduced
United Federation of Planets
but was reportedly unharmed and `status quo`. (
DIS
: "
Die Trying
")
In the Kaminar Population History, the pre-
vahar'ai
Kelpiens and the post-
vahar'ai
Kelpiens were labeled as unevolved Kelpiens and as evolved Kelpiens, respectively. No explanation was given for the rebirth of the Kelpien species.
Kelpien village situated along a beach,
2239
.
By the
23rd century
, the Ba'ul were a technologically advanced species who required the pre-warp Kelpiens to
ritualistically
sacrifice themselves to
their ships
. (
DIS
: "
The Vulcan Hello
";
ST
: "
The Brightest Star
")
In the
23rd century
, this planet's system was in
Non-Federation space
.
Sometime before the
32nd century
, the Ba'ul and the Kelpiens would put aside their differences and Kaminar would become a member world of the Federation. (
DIS
: "
Die Trying
")
A city on Kaminar in 3189
In
3189
, the
Emerald Chain
engaged in
military exercises
near Kaminar, prompting
Fleet Admiral
Charles Vance
to dispatch part of the
Federation
fleet to protect the planet. Vance speculated that
Osyraa
was threatening Kaminar in a
ploy
to draw out the
USS
Discovery
. (
DIS
: "
Su'Kal
")
After the discovery of
Theta Zeta
, the Federation delivered
dilithium
to this planet as a
gift
. In
3190
, the
Kaminar High Council
had a
debate
on what to do with this gift, help their
citizens
or use it to return to the
stars
. (
DIS
: "
Kobayashi Maru
")
In
3190
, the people of Kaminar voted to resume
space travel
. Saru's
village
was involved in retrofitting the planet's
spaceport
. (
DIS
: "
Choose to Live
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of Federation space in
3196
, this planet's system was within the blast radius of one of the mines. (
SA
: "
300th Night
", "
Rubincon
")
Appendices
Background information
Scenes on the planet for "
The Brightest Star
" and "
The Sound of Thunder
" were filmed at
Scarborough Bluffs
in Toronto.
[1]
For the
Star Trek: Prodigy
episode "
Let Sleeping Borg Lie
", UI graphic designer
Dominique Rossier
created an info panel for the
USS
Dauntless
mess hall
depicting the
Milky Way Galaxy
with planet and
Starfleet
space station
locations labeled. Identified as a planet, Kaminar was one of the locations mentioned on the chart. Although the panel did not appear in the episode, it can be seen at Rossier's artist page at Art Station.
[2]
Apocrypha
The
Gallery DIS
novel
Desperate Hours
called Saru's homeworld "Kelpia". This was later contradicted by another Discovery novel entitled
Fear Itself
, which first established the planet's name as Kaminar months before the release of "The Brightest Star".
External link
Kaminar
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

Gamma Quadrant
, was the
homeworld
of the
Karemma
, an important member of the Dominion. (
DS9
: "
The Search, Part I
")
In an effort to locate the
Founders
of the Dominion, the
USS
Defiant
visited Karemma in
2371
. (
DS9
: "
The Search, Part I
")
Minister
Hanok
, who worked for the
Karemma Commerce Ministry
, once saw a design
schematic
of a
Jem'Hadar
torpedo
in the
Ministry of Trade
on Karemma.
After the
USS
Defiant
rescued the
crew
of a
Karemma ship
in
2372
, they were brought to
Deep Space 9
until transport could be arranged back to Karemma. (
DS9
: "
Starship Down
")
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 71), Karemma was a
M-class
planet. The official name of the planetary
government
was the Karemma Foundation (
β
). This government was seated in the
capital city
of Kecemen (
β
). Dominant
species
on this planet was the
humanoid
Karemman. In
2378
, there were an estimated 4.5 billion Karemmans living on Karemma. This planet became
warp-capable
circa
2300
. In
2372
, trade relations were established between the
Ferengi Alliance
and the Karemma Foundation.
External links
Karemma (planet)
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Re-Used Planets in DS9
at
Ex Astris Scientia

Karzill IV
Type:
Planet

Pakleds
Status:
Habitable
(
2381
)
Satellite(s):

one moon
Native species:
Karzill IV inhabitants
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Karzill IV
was the
inhabited
fourth
planet
in its
star system
. It was the site of a
mining colony
where
Varuvian ore
was extracted. The planet had at least
one moon
.
In
2381
, the colony was invaded and taken over by the
Pakleds
. The
USS
Titan
dispatched an
away team
, disguised as
miners
, to infiltrate the colony and plant a
tracking device
on the Pakleds'
shuttle
. However, the team was discovered and the
Titan
was forced to extract them under enemy fire. (

: "

")

PAGE: Kasar

Kasar
AKA:
Lutani I
Type:
Planet
Status:
Inhabited
(
2261
)
Native species:
Kasar natives
Location:
Lutani system
Alpha Quadrant
Kasar
was the
inhabited
innermost
planet
of the
Lutani system
. It was the
sister planet
of
Lutani VII
, and hosted a
spacefaring
civilization
.
On
stardate
2177.9, the
inhabitants
of Kasar launched a
war
to seize the entire Lutani system and its resources. By stardate 2191.4, they had inflicted nine million casualties on the Lutani, while suffering only 119,000 in return. (
SNW
: "
What Is Starfleet?
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Kataan
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kataan
======================================================================

Kataan
Type:
Planet
Status:
Destroyed
(
14th century
)
Native species:
Kataan natives
Location:
Kataan
system
Silarian
sector
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Kataan
was a formerly
inhabited
planet
in the
Kataan
star system
. This system was located within the
Silarian sector
. It was a dead world,
home
to a
humanoid
culture
until the late
14th century
when, after suffering through a protracted
drought
due to increased solar
radiation
, all
life
on the planet became
extinct
.
A tree being planted on the Ressikan town square
At the time of its demise the
culture of Kataan
appeared to be on the cusp of post-industrial evolution. Though there was no strong evidence of heavy manufacturing and the lifestyle of its
citizens
more closely resembled a pre-industrial agrarian society, they did possess electronic instruments (such as electric lights and doors), and the technological capacity to create
interstellar probes
, though they could not send people into
space
. They also possessed considerable knowledge in advanced sciences, including
microbiology
and
genetics
, and had people undergoing
surgery
"all the time".
A typical greeting on the planet was "Happy day"; a standard set phrase when leaving was "Go carefully". When a child was born, a
naming ceremony
took place shortly after
birth
. People on Kataan were interested in
astronomy
,
art
,
botany
,
mathematics
, and many other things;
iron weaver
and
sculptor
were only two of the many professions possible. The houses were arranged around open spaces and
town squares
where sparse
vegetation
was growing. Every
house
,
window
, and
door
was decorated with Kataan writing.
The probe is launched
Typical foods consumed by the natives were
soups
and
vegetable stew
.
Water
had to be rationed as less and less was available.
Councils
, led by  council leaders, decided about the fate of their communities; an
administrator
was responsible for several communities. The
government
was structured in a participatory manner, in which all citizens had a say.
During the final
years
of its existence, the people of the
Ressik
community, located in the
northern province
, realized that their
sun
was about to go
nova
. The death of the planet was predicted by
scientists
in the
government
but hidden from the
population
at large. They launched a
probe
containing a memory record of their
civilization
and a musical instrument, the
Ressikan flute
, native to their culture.
This probe later encountered
Jean-Luc Picard
and transferred its memory record to him. (
TNG
: "
The Inner Light
")
According to the episode's
script
, the planet was pronounced "K'-TAHN".
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 403 classified Kataan as a
class M
planet.

======================================================================
PAGE: Kavaria
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kavaria
======================================================================

Kavaria
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Native species:
Kavarian tiger-bat
Location:
Kavaria
system
Alpha Quadrant
Kavaria
was a
planet
in the
Kavaria
system
,
Alpha Quadrant
. It was close to the
Badlands
and the
Dreon system
. The planet was in or near to the
Venari Ral Zone
.
History
In
2372
,
Jake Sisko
was doing some research for a
story
that included a Kavarian tiger-bat, and tried to find out how it smelt.
Kasidy Yates
suggested that
Quark
might know someone who had been there, since she hadn't. (
DS9
: "
For the Cause
")
Appendices
Background information
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
This planet might also be home to
Kavarian olives
.
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the star chart appearing in the
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
episode "
300th Night
".
Benjamin Sisko
stated Kavaria and the Dreon system to be neighbors, but Kasidy (perhaps maliciously) didn't agree. She claimed Kavaria was located too close to the Badlands for her comfort, although Sisko then implied Dreon was close to the Badlands too.
According to
StarTrek.com
, Kavaria was a
Class M
planet that was at some distance from the Bajor-Dreon trade run.
[1]
(X)

at

, the wiki for licensed

at

, the wiki for licensed

Kavis Alpha IV
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2366
)
Location:
Kavis Alpha
sector
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Kavis Alpha IV
was the
uninhabited
fourth
planet
in its
star system
. This system was found in the
Kavis Alpha
sector
in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
In
2366
,
nanites
threatened the
USS
Enterprise

with destruction of its main
computer core
during an
experiment
in the system. After conflict with the
crew
of the
Enterprise
-D, and particularly the experiment's lead
scientist
Paul Stubbs
, the nanites were
relocated
to Kavis Alpha IV, which Stubbs'
influence
had helped designate as their new
home
. The nanites left the
Enterprise
-D and began creating their own
civilization
on this planet. (
TNG
: "
Evolution
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
The

, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 404 located this planet in
Federation
space
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Kei
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kei
======================================================================

Kei
AKA:
Braslota III
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2365
)
Location:
Braslota
system
Oneamisu
sector
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
For person with the same name, please see
Kei
.
Kei
, also known as
Braslota III
, was the
uninhabited
third
planet
in the
Braslota
system
. This system, which was in the
Oneamisu
sector
, was selected by
Starfleet Command
in
2365
as the
location
for the
battle simulation
Operation Lovely Angel
.
In the simulation, the
starships
USS
Hathaway
and
USS
Enterprise
-D
had simulated objectives that either ship had to complete before progressing to the next objective. In the first objective, either ship would attempt a successful capture of control of near-planetary
space
in the
region
of
Yuri
, while, at the same time, maintaining a defensive posture from a simulated threat from Kei.
Kei was labeled on a diagram of the Braslota system. In this diagram, the planet's position and
orbital
path were depicted. This diagram was on display in the
observation lounge
of the
Enterprise
-D. (
TNG
: "
Peak Performance
",
okudagram
)
This planet was only mentioned in writing.
Most of the simulation information was
in-joke
references to the Japanese
anime
(Japanese animation) series
Dirty Pair
, this planet included. (
Star Trek: The Next Generation Companion
, 2nd ed., p. 92)

.

, on
stardate

hypothermic charges
at the planet. The subsequent explosions burned the

, resulting in the instant death of eleven thousand civilians. (

: "

star chart
(Grid reference AM/07) appearing in the

======================================================================
PAGE: Kellerun (planet)
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kellerun_(planet)
======================================================================

Kellerun
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
32nd century
)
Native species:
Kellerun
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Kellerun
was an
inhabited
planet
. It was the
homeworld
of the
Kellerun
. The
golor beetle
was native to the planet. (
DIS
: "
Mirrors
", "
Erigah
")
In the
32nd century
, Kellerun was invaded without warning by
Primarch
Tahal
of the
Breen Imperium
, who sought to use it as a
munitions
resupply hub for her campaign to control the
throne
. Her forces clear-cut the planet's
forests
,
poisoned
the
seas
, and blackened the skies. Countless Kellerun died during the occupation, though they resisted fiercely. When the Kellerun rebelled at the
Twin Gates
, Tahal killed all involved save for
Rayner
, who impressed her by fighting "almost like a
Breen
". (
DIS
: "
Erigah
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Kelva
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kelva
======================================================================

Kelva
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Kelvan Empire
Status:
Habitable
(
1960s
)
Native species:
Kelvans
Location:
Andromeda Galaxy
Kelva
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Andromeda Galaxy
. This was the
homeworld
for the
Kelvans
, a
warp-capable
non-
humanoid
species
. In the mid-
20th century
, Kelva was the
capital planet
of the
Kelvan Empire
.
Kelvans predicted rising
radiation
levels in Andromeda would render their home uninhabitable to their species within
ten thousand
years, and were seeking new territories to conquer and occupy.
According to an
idea
suggested by
Spock
, a
robot ship
could be sent to Kelva along with the
Federation
proposal of peaceful relations and to suggest some
planets
in the
galaxy
that the Kelvans could
colonize
. The Kelvan response would, however, take
six hundred years to arrive
.
A rapidly forming
crystalline
structure called
sahsheer
, reminiscent of
flower
blossoms found in the
Milky Way Galaxy
, was native to Kelva. (
TOS
: "
By Any Other Name
")
In
Brad Boimler
's
2381
holonovel
Crisis Point II: Paradoxus
, Kelva was one of the worlds labeled on the
star map
tattooed
on
Illustor
's loose
skin
. The "K" in the name formed part of the word
Ki-ty-ha
when the skin flaps were pushed together. (
LD
: "
Crisis Point 2: Paradoxus
")
External link
Kelva
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

PAGE: Kelvas Prime
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kelvas_Prime
======================================================================

Kelvas Prime
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Cardassian Union
(
24th century
)
Dominion
(
2373
-
2375
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Location:
Kelvas
system
Unnamed sector
Alpha Quadrant
Kelvas Prime
was the
inhabited
prime
planet
in the
Kelvas
system
. This planet was in a
sector
of the
Alpha Quadrant
.
History
In the
24th century
, this planet's system was in
Cardassian
space
.
Following the supposed
demise
of
Damar
,
Mila
overheard a
rumor
that
someone's cousin
had seen Damar on Kelvas Prime. (
DS9
: "
The Dogs of War
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
Since
Kelvas V
and the
Kelvas facility
were both Cardassian, this might have been a Cardassian planet.
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of the Cardassian Union as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".

======================================================================
PAGE: Kelvas V
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kelvas_V
======================================================================

Kelvas V
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Cardassian Union
(
24th century
)
Dominion
(
2373
-
2375
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Native species:
Cardassian
Location:
Kelvas
system
Unnamed sector
Alpha Quadrant
Kelvas V
was the
fifth
planet
in the
Kelvas
system
. This planet was in a
sector
of the
Alpha Quadrant
.
History
In the
24th century
, this planet's system was in
Cardassian
space
.
In
2375
, during the
Dominion War
,
Odo
contacted one of his contacts on this planet to get information about
Dominion
activity in the Kelvas system. He learned that within a
week
the
Breen
energy-dampening weapons
would be installed aboard
Jem'Hadar
warships
at the
Kelvas facility
. Using this intelligence, a small team of the
Cardassian Liberation Front
were able to infiltrate the station and steal one of the modified ships. (
DS9
: "
Tacking Into the Wind
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
In the first draft script of "Tacking Into the Wind", this planet was instead named "Semora V".
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of the Cardassian Union as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".

at

, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek

======================================================================
PAGE: Kelvas facility
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kelvas_facility
======================================================================

Kelvas facility
Type:
Monac
-type
Owner:
Cardassian Union
Dominion
Location:
Kelvas
system
Unnamed sector
Alpha Quadrant
Status:
Active
(
2375
)
The
Kelvas facility
was a
Cardassian
Monac
-type
repair facility
in the
Kelvas system
. This facility was in a
sector
of the
Alpha Quadrant
.
History
In the
24th century
, this station's system was in
Cardassian
space
.
In
2375
,
Breen
engineers
were installing
energy-dampening weapons
aboard
Jem'Hadar attack ships
at the
repair facility
. A five-person team from the
Cardassian Rebellion
was able to infiltrate the station and steal one of the retrofitted attack ships, bringing the weapon back to the
Federation
for study. (
DS9
: "
Tacking Into the Wind
")
The Kelvas repair facility appeared on the cover sleeve of the
UK VHS release
of "
When It Rains...
" and "
Tacking Into the Wind
".
Since
Luaran
was surprised to see the Female Founder in her sector, while she was on Cardassia shortly before, the facility seems to have been in a different sector then Cardassia Prime.
For more information on this
CGI model
, please see
DS9 studio models
.

at

Star Trek

PAGE: Kenda II

URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kenda_II

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Kenda II
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
(
3196
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Kenda II
was the
inhabited
second
planet
in its
star system
.
Doctor
Dalen Quaice
was from Kenda II. In
2367
, the
USS
Enterprise
-D
transported Quaice back to his
homeworld
after he
retired
from his post on
Starbase 133
. (
TNG
: "
Remember Me
")
In the
32nd century
, Kenda II was a
Federation
member
planet. It was listed with other members on a video wall in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
The Life of the Stars
")
In
3196
,
Commander
Jett Reno
logged a
Starfleet Academy
cadet
departing for Kenda II to spend the
All-Worlds Day
break. (
SA
: "
Ko'Zeine
")

The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 412 identified Kenda II as a
Federation
world.
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I"), Kenda II was listed as a Federation member in
2378
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Kenfori
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kenfori
======================================================================

Kenfori
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2261
)
Location:
Kenfori system
Beta Quadrant
Kenfori
was an
uninhabited
planet
in the
Kenfori system
, located between
Federation
and
Klingon space
. Its
orbit
lay just inside of a massive
dust cloud
. The surface was lush with
vegetation
but lacked any
animal
life. This was one of the few known locations where the
chimera blossom
could be found. The planet was also home to a type of
perennial moss
that consumed all other plants around it to proliferate itself.
In the
23rd century
, the
Federation
operated the
Kenfori Agricultural Research Facility
, specializing on
crop
longevity, on Kenfori. The researchers developed
an agent
, derived from the chimera blossom, that could insert perennial moss
genes
into
food
plants to create
hybrid
"supercrops" able to survive in any
environment
.
At the start of the
Federation-Klingon War
in
2256
, the
Klingon Empire
invaded the Kenfori facility without regard for
safety protocols
, causing an uncontrolled release of the chimera agent. The agent transformed all Federation and
Klingon
personnel inside the facility into mindless "
zombies
" that attacked and devoured living beings on sight. Before succumbing, the facility's
chancellor
ordered a
lockdown
to contain the agent and recommended the planet be abandoned.
After the war's end, the Federation and the Klingons mutually relinquished their claim to Kenfori and placed the system in a
restricted zone
, with access prohibited by multiple
treaties
. The
mines
in the area were removed, and a Klingon
warning beacon
was left in
orbit
.
In
2261
,
Captain
Christopher Pike
and
Doctor
Joseph M'Benga
embarked on an off-the-books
mission
to obtain chimera blossoms from Kenfori, with which to treat Captain
Marie Batel
's
Gorn
egg
infection. They were followed by a
Klingon
force chartered by
Bytha
, who sought to restore the
honor
of the
House of Ra'Ul
by killing M'Benga. Though the chimera agent had long since broken down, both
Starfleet
and Klingon
landing parties
ran afoul of the zombies inside the abandoned facility. Pike and M'Benga were able to secure a single chimera blossom sample before they were confronted by Bytha on the facility
roof
. After being defeated by M'Benga in
ritual combat
, Bytha sacrificed herself to the zombies to give Pike and M'Benga a chance to escape.
Meanwhile, the
USS
Enterprise
encountered a
D7-class
Klingon battle cruiser
in the dust cloud above the planet. With Pike and M'Benga in need of retrieval,
Commander
Una Chin-Riley
ordered the
Enterprise
to sneak by the cruiser. However,
Lieutenant
Erica Ortegas
disobeyed orders and allowed the Klingons to detect them, so as to force her preferred course of action: a risky
warp
jump into Kenfori's
thermosphere
. Though the maneuver successfully allowed Pike and M'Benga to be
beamed
back to the ship, Chin-Riley consequently relieved Ortegas of duty. (
SNW
: "
Shuttle to Kenfori
")
The name of the planet may be a reference to
Dawn of the Dead
actor Ken Foree.

======================================================================
PAGE: Kentanna
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kentanna
======================================================================

Kentanna
was the legendary home of the
Skrreeans
. In their sacred texts, Kentanna was described as being beyond the "
Eye of the Universe
". It was a
planet
of sorrow where the Skrreeans would plant
seeds
of joy.
In
2370
, they believed it would provide them with salvation when they fled the
T-Rogorans
, who had occupied the Skrreean
homeworld
centuries ago but had been recently been conquered by the
Dominion
.
When
Haneek
and her mates found their way to the
Alpha Quadrant
through the
Bajoran wormhole
, she became the
de facto
leader of the Skrreea. She came to believe
Bajor
was Kentanna, but the
Bajoran Provisional Government
was forced to turn them away due to
famines
and other problems that had arisen on the planet after the
Cardassian Occupation
. Upon leaving
Deep Space 9
for
Draylon II
, Haneek said to
Major
Kira
, "
You were right. Bajor is not Kentanna.
" (
DS9
: "
Sanctuary
")
See also
New Kentanna

======================================================================
PAGE: Kepler-174d
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kepler-174d
======================================================================

Mirror universe
(
coexisting on another dimensional plane
)
Kepler-174d
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
1950s
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Kepler-174d
was an
inhabited
planet
.
In the
mirror universe
,
Emperor
Philippa Georgiou
once
scouted
the planet and found it "quite beautiful". She found a
family
of
artists
there who produced sublime
paintings
and
sculptures
unmatched anywhere else.
In
2255
,
Captain
Michael Burnham
carried out a mission to Kepler-174d. She later commented favorably about its
flora
and
architecture
to Georgiou, and also mentioned that she had the aforementioned artists
blinded
and their
hands
removed, so that their works would appreciate in value. (
DIS
: "
Terra Firma, Part 1
")
Inhabitants
See
:
Kepler-174d inhabitants
Kepler-174d is the first real exoplanet to feature in
Star Trek
.
External link
Kepler-174d
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Kepler-22b
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kepler-22b
======================================================================

Kepler-22b
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2259
)
Location:
Kepler 22
system
Alpha Quadrant
640
light years
from
Sol
Kepler-22b
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Kepler- 22
system
. This planet was located in the
Alpha Quadrant
. The planet's system was visible from
Earth
in the
constellation
Cygnus
and was approximately 640
light years
from
Sol
.
Before
2259
, the
v'tosh ka'tur
Barjan T'Or
caused an
uprising
here. For this reason, his capture was sought by agents of the
El-Keshtanktil
. (
SNW
: "
Spock Amok
")
Appendices
Background information
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
Kepler-22b was discovered in 2011, about 600 light-years from Earth. It became the first known
exoplanet
to orbit within the habitable zone of a Sun-like star, where liquid water could exist on the planet's surface.
Information on this planet was derived from the episode and real-world sources. The real-world information added to the article was current as of 2025.
The placement of this planet in the Alpha Quadrant is inferred from the location of
astronomical
objects
associated with the constellation Cygnus.
External link
Kepler-22b
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Kerkhov
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kerkhov
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Kerkhov
Class:
J
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
3196
)
Satellite(s):
Kerkhovian moon
Location:
In
orbit
of
40 Eridani C
Vulcan system
Vulcan space
Vulcan sector
Beta Quadrant
~16
light years
from
Sol
Kerkhov
was a
class J
gas giant
planet
orbiting
the star
40 Eridani C
, This planet was located inside
Vulcan space
in the
Vulcan sector
,
Beta Quadrant
, and was approximately 16
light years
from
Sol
. Its
coordinates
were 2701.6513. It was
orbited
by a
single moon
, which was the
homeworld
of the long-vanished
Kerkhovian
civilization
.
Planetary data
Planetary data for Kerkhov
The planet had a
rotation period
of 10.8 hours and a
revolution period
of 4,198.5
solar days
. It had an estimated
mass
of 1.98921 x 10
27
kilograms
and an estimated age of 4.7 billion
solar years
. Its
atmosphere
was composed of 82% molecular
hydrogen
, 16%
helium
, 0.16%
water
, and 0.07%
methane
, with trace amounts of
argon
,
ammonia
,
hydrogen sulfide
,
carbon monoxide
,
neon
,
xenon
, and
krypton
. The mean surface
temperature
was -146.1
degrees
Celsius
.
History
In
2259
, the
USS
Enterprise
conducted a
survey
of the Kerkhovian moon and discovered that the Kerkhovians still existed, having migrated into
interdimensional space
. (
SNW
: "
Charades
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet was within the enclosed region. (
SA
: "
300th Night
")
The quadrant of this planet was inferred from the position of its star system in charts seen in several
Star Trek: Strange New Worlds
episodes.
In a display graphic seen aboard the shuttle
Cervantes
, this planet was identified as
class L
.
[1]
The current location of Vulcan (Ni'Var) in the Beta Quadrant was based on the most recent star charts seen in
Star Trek: Strange New Worlds
and
Star Trek: Section 31
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Kesef
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kesef
======================================================================

Kesef
was a
location
on the
Kyrian and Vaskan homeworld
.
In the mid-
31st century
, the
EMH backup module
was unearthed beneath the
ruins
of Kesef. (
VOY
: "
Living Witness
")
Kesef was named by
Bryan Fuller
. He took the name from a book about angels. (
Information from
Larry Nemecek
)

======================================================================
PAGE: Kesprytt III
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kesprytt_III
======================================================================

Kesprytt III
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Kes
Prytt Alliance
Status:
Habitable
(
2370
)
Native species:
Kesprytt
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Kesprytt III
was the
inhabited
third
planet
of its
star system
, and was the
homeworld
of the
Kesprytt
. The planet was divided into two large
countries
, the
Kes
and the
Prytt Alliance
.
During the
24th century
, the Kes was the larger of the two nations, comprising three quarters of the planet's
population
, while Prytt contained the remaining one quarter of the population. The two nation-states had a paranoid dislike of each other, bordering on
xenophobia
.
Locations
The Kes maintained a security station called
Security Relay Station One
.
The border between the Kes and Prytt nations was enforced using a large
force field
. The Prytt
village
of
Ohn-Kor
was near the border, and the Prytt
capital city
was within
hiking
distance.
An
island
named
Kolrod Island
was located on the planet, and contested by both
governments
.
History
In
2370
, the Kes applied for an "associate"
Federation
membership, as an exception to the normal rule requiring a planet be unified socially and politically for membership. The application for the admission of Kes to the Federation was rejected due to the unresolved social, political, and military problems between the two nations. (
TNG
: "
Attached
")
According to the
script
, the pronunciation for Kesprytt was "KES-prit".
[1]
The
surface
scenes were filmed on a two day
location shooting
at
Griffith Park
's
Bronson Canyon
. (
Star Trek: The Next Generation Companion
, 2nd ed., p. 272)
Both
StarTrek.com
and the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 414 classified Kesprytt as an
M-class
world.
[2]
(X)
A planetary map of Kesprytt III (Kes territory = purple, Prytt territory = orange)
External links
Kesprytt III
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Re-Used Planets in TNG
at
Ex Astris Scientia

======================================================================
PAGE: Kessik IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kessik_IV
======================================================================

Kessik IV
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Habitable
(
2378
)
Location:
Kessik
system,
Beta Quadrant
Kessik IV
was the
inhabited
Class M
fourth
planet
of the
Kessik
star system
in the
Beta Quadrant
. (
VOY
: "
Faces
";
SNW
: "
Hegemony, Part II
")
By
2260
, the planet was the site of a
Federation
colony
. (
SNW
: "
Hegemony, Part II
")
B'Elanna Torres
grew up on Kessik IV, where she and her
mother
Miral
were the only
Klingons
. (
VOY
: "
Faces
") Aware of this fact,
Kohlar
took that into account while interpreting a
prophecy
that was "
written that the mother of the
Kuvah'magh
would be an
off-worlder
", but Torres rebuffed his insinuation, indicating that "
a lot of
Klingons
are born off-world.
" (
VOY
: "
Prophecy
")
John Torres
later visited Kessik IV for some business in
2378
. He was surprised at what the old Torres family
house
looked like. (
VOY
: "
Author, Author
")
Appendices
Background information
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue in
Star Trek: Voyager
and displayed on a screen in
SNW
: "
Hegemony, Part II
".
Kessik IV was also mentioned in a
deleted scene
, from "
Workforce, Part II
", where it was remembered by the
amnesiac
B'Elanna, who described it as the last place she remembered being, "
pregnant
, scared and alone
", prior to arriving at her job on
Quarra
.
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 64; "United Federation of Planets I") and
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Federation Historical Highlights, 2161-2385"), the Kessik system was located in the
Beta Quadrant
. The system's
primary
was a
G-class star
. This system was a vertex of the
Delta Triangle
. In
2378
, the planets Kessik III and Kessik IV were listed as
Federation members
.
Apocrypha
Kessik IV was also featured in
Star Trek Online
until it was renamed to "Omicron Kappa II".
External link
Kessik IV
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Keto-Enol
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Keto-Enol
======================================================================

Keto-Enol
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Enolians
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Keto-Enol
was an
inhabited
class M
planet
settled by the
Enolians
. It was the busiest trading
outpost
in its
star system
.
During the mid-
22nd century
,
smuggling
, among many other
crimes
, was rampant in Keto-Enol's system. To enforce their
laws
, the Enolians set up strict rules throughout the
star
system, including the fact that people caught would be sent to a
prison camp
, and then tried.
In
2152
,
Captain
Archer
and
Commander
Tucker
visited Keto-Enol, and had a successful
first contact
. They landed and played
pan zan
, a local
sport
similar to
water polo
. However, on their way out, they were arrested for what the
Enolian Guard
thought was contraband, and they were sent to
Canamar
.
Later,
Enterprise
NX-01
visited the planet to pick up an Enolian official, to help them find their missing
crew
. (
ENT
: "
Canamar
")
The final draft script of "Canamar" identified this planet as
class M
.
Keto-Enol is presumably not the
homeworld
of the Enolians, as it was referred to as a mere trading outpost.

======================================================================
PAGE: Khefka IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Khefka_IV
