Khefka IV
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
24th century
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Khefka IV
was the
inhabited
fourth
planet
in its
star system
. This world was the
location
of a
Federation
Consulate
in the mid-
24th century
.
Verad
was stationed there as a
communications clerk
at some point before
2370
, and he took a native called
Mareel
under his care, rescuing her from the rough
street
-life on the planet, and taking her with him when he was recalled to
Trill
. (
DS9
: "
Invasive Procedures
")
Appendices
Background information
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
Robert Hewitt Wolfe
named this planet after
Franz Kafka
. (
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine Companion
, p. 88)
According to
StarTrek.com
, the pronunciation for Khefka was "KEFF-kuh";
[1]
(X)
however, according to the
script
pronunciation guide, the pronunciation was "KEF-kah".
[2]
Furthermore, according to StarTrek.com, Khefka was the name of a
star
in the Federation sphere of influence, and that Khefka IV was
Class M
.
[3]
(X)
External link
Khefka IV
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Khionia
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Khionia
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Khionia
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Khionian Realm
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Satellite(s):
Sunset Moon
Native species:
Khionians
Location:
Khionian Realm
Alpha Quadrant
Khionia
was an
inhabited
planet
and
homeworld
of the
Khionians
. It was
orbited
by
Sunset Moon
. An
oceanic
world, Khionia was inhabited by
aquatic
lifeforms
such as
coral
and
sea tendrils
.
Ryllios
was a location on the planet. (
SA
: "
Ko'Zeine
")
History
Darem Reymi
had a
trophy
that he won by fighting a man with
three legs
on Khionia. (
SA
: "
Beta Test
")
A massive celebration was scheduled on Khionia, involving
parades
,
fireworks
, and copious amounts of
alcohol
, in early
3196
on the day following the
royal
sealing
of Reymi and
Kaira
and their ascension to the
throne
. However, Reymi
abdicated
immediately after the
ceremony
, at Kaira's urging. (
SA
: "
Ko'Zeine
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in 3196, Khionia was within the blast radius of one of the mines. (
SA
: "
300th Night
", "
Rubincon
")
Stellar cartography
In
3196
, during the
Omega-47
crisis, the location of Khionia was depicted on
star charts
and readouts used across several locations on the
USS
Athena
to locate, monitor, and respond to the Omega-47 mines laid by the Venari Ral. (
SA
: "
300th Night
", "
Rubincon
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Khitomer
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Khitomer
======================================================================

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Khitomer
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Klingon Empire
Romulan Star Empire
Status:
Habitable
(
2399
)
Location:
Khitomer
system
Unnamed sector
Klingon Zone
Beta Quadrant
Khitomer Outpost
Type:
Outpost
Affiliation:
Klingon Empire
Location:
Khitomer
system
Unnamed sector
Beta Quadrant
Status:
Destroyed
(
2346
)
Khitomer
was an
inhabited
Class M
planet
in the
Khitomer
system
. The planet was located in a
sector
of the
Beta Quadrant
.
Khitomer played a vital role in the development of the
Klingon
-
Federation
alliance
, being the site of the
Khitomer Conference
of
2293
as well as the victim of a
Romulan sneak attack
on the planet's Klingon
colony
in
2346
, which, in turn, emphasized the importance of the Klingon-Federation
friendship
. (
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
;
TNG
: "
The Neutral Zone
", "
Sins of The Father
")
History
Sunset over Khitomer
In the
alternate
2259
, the planet was discovered to have a Klingon
colony
. This event was reported in a news ticker, which was in a collection of graphics and video media that were seen on a
powerwall
in the
offices
of
Christopher Pike
and
Alexander Marcus
at
Starfleet Headquarters
.
(
Star Trek Into Darkness
)
The ticker can be seen in the video "
Star Trek: Into Darkness
" - User Interface VFX at vimeo.com.
[1]
In
2293
, Khitomer was considered to be a neutral site by both the Federation and the Klingon Empire, and was located near the
Romulan border
. In that
year
, the
Khitomer Conference
was held at
Camp Khitomer
on the planet. The conference started the first full
peace
negotiations
between the United Federation of Planets and the Klingon Empire and gave birth to the
Khitomer Accords
. Originally,
Earth
had been selected as a venue, but after
Klingon chancellor
Gorkon
was
assassinated
on his way to Earth by members of the
Khitomer conspiracy
, new chancellor
Azetbur
relocated the conference to Camp Khitomer. (
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
)
By
2346
, a Klingon
outpost
-
colony
existed on Khitomer. On
stardate
23859.7, the colony fell victim to a
Romulan
attack
. In the attack – which later became known as the
Khitomer Massacre
– 4,000
colonists
were killed, among them
Mogh
and
Ja'rod
. (
TNG
: "
Heart of Glory
", "
Sins of The Father
")
Only two
survivors
were believed to have been rescued by the
USS
Intrepid
; however, a group of colonists captured by the Romulans were allowed to live and were taken to a
prison camp
in the
Carraya sector
, remaining undiscovered until
2369
. (
TNG
: "
Birthright, Part I
", "
Birthright, Part II
")
An
obelisk
was erected on Khitomer as a
memorial
. (
VOY
: "
Memorial
")
It was not explicitly stated that the memorial was for the Khitomer Massacre, although the context does imply a military or otherwise violent event.
Some time prior to
2375
,
Admiral
William Ross
met
Chancellor
Gowron
at Khitomer. (
DS9
: "
When It Rains...
")
Khitomer inhabitants
See
:
Khitomer inhabitants
Stellar cartography
Khitomer on a star chart (2369)
In
2369
, the "Deep Space 9 Traffic Control Database"
star chart
labeled the location of Khitomer within the
Romulan Star Empire
. (
TNG
: "
Birthright, Part II
",
okudagram
)
In
2399
, a
Federation star chart
that was in
Fleet Admiral
Kirsten Clancy
's
office
at Starfleet Headquarters labeled the location of Khitomer within
Klingon space
. (
PIC
: "
Maps and Legends
")
Appendices
Background information
A matte painting of Khitomer is prepared for filming
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star charts
appearing in the
Star Trek: Discovery
episode
DIS
: "
Magic to Make the Sanest Man Go Mad
" and in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
The
script
for "Redemption" specified that the planet should be pronounced "KIT-to-mur".
[2]
However, the script for "Birthright, Part I" suggested "KIT-oh-mer".
[3]
The script for "Sins of the Father" had Kurn state to Worf that the Khitomer Outpost was "new... unfinished" at the time that Mogh visited.
[4]
The Khitomer hall was represented by the
Brandeis-Bardin Institute
, located in southern
California
.
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 415 classified Khitomer as a
M-class
planet.
Apocrypha
The events surrounding the Khitomer Massacre were documented in the
Star Trek: The Lost Era
novel
,
The Art of the Impossible
.
During the events of the
Star Trek: Destiny
novel
Gods of Night
, Khitomer was saved from annihilation by the
Borg
in
2381
by a
kamikaze
attack from the USS
Ranger
(
β
). But in the novel
Lost Souls
the planet was again attacked and fell to the Borg Invasion of 2381 (
β
). None of the
population
survived this attack, including presumably
Governor
Talgar (
β
).
In
Star Trek Online
, the Klingon Empire reclaimed the Khitomer sector in
2382
during the political crisis in the
Romulan Star Empire
that followed
Praetor
Shinzon
's
death
. In the mission "Turning Point" the planet was the site of a conference between the Federation, the Klingon Empire, and the Romulan Star Empire, regarding the future of the Romulan Republic (
β
). The conference center later became the headquarters of the Khitomer Alliance (
β
) (formed by the Federation, the Klingon Empire, the Romulan Republic, and the
Dominion
, among others) until it was destroyed by a
mycelium
weapon
in "Khitomer Discord".
In the
RPG
sourcebook
Worlds
, Khitomer was the
third
planet in the Beta Guarada system.
In the
Star Trek: Ongoing
story arc
The Khitomer Conflict
set in the
alternate reality
, construction of the Khitomer colony was underway in the year
2261
, but on stardate 2261.147, a Romulan
fleet
led by
Commander
L'Nar (
β
) attacked and destroyed the settlement while it was still under construction. The
USS
Enterprise
responded to the
distress call
about the attack and a
landing party
led by
James T. Kirk
visited the colony site but found no
survivors
. They end up captured by Commander
Kor
, who believed the Federation was responsible for the attack. While the
Enterprise
was forced to flee, Kirk and his officers were brought to
Qo'noS
as
prisoners
. The brazen attack led to the Khitomer Conflict between the three
major powers
. It was eventually revealed that
Section 31
traded advanced weaponry to the Romulans in an attempt to destroy the Klingon Empire, but Section 31 double-crossed the Romulans by installing a self-destruct program onto the modified
warbirds
, which they destroyed. Section 31 had accomplished their goals; they had the Klingon and Romulan fleet cut to pieces, the
red matter
in their possession, and assurances that the Klingons would retaliate against the Romulans, all to the Federation's advantage.
External links
Khitomer
at
Wikipedia
Khitomer
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Khosla II
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Khosla_II
======================================================================

Khosla II
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2370
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Khosla II
was the
inhabited
second
planet
in its
star system
. This was the place of residence of the
Ferengi
entrepreneur
Plegg
, who was largely credited with the
invention
of
holosuite
technology
, in the late
24th century
.
In
2370
,
Odo
informed
Quark
of
Plegg
's whereabouts when Quark attempted to defraud customers into buying
counterfeit
desiccation discs
allegedly containing Plegg's remains. The
Changeling
had tracked the very much alive Ferengi down. (
DS9
: "
The Alternate
")
Background information
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to the
script
, the pronunciation for Khosla was "KOH-slah".
[1]
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 416 and the closed captioning for "The Alternate" spelled the planet's
name
as Kholfa II. This spelling would re-appear in the
Star Trek: Star Charts
.
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I"), the Kholfa system was located in the
Alpha Quadrant
. The system's
primary
was a
M-class star
.
According to
StarTrek.com
, the primary was named Khosla and located this star in the
Beta Quadrant
.
[2]
(X)

======================================================================
PAGE: Khwopa
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Khwopa
======================================================================

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Khwopa
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2380
)
Satellite(s):
At least
one moon
Native species:
Khwopians
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Khwopa
was an
inhabited
bog
planet
and
homeworld
of the
Khwopians
. It had at least
one moon
.
A
Starfleet
report
described
first contact
with the planet as "a little tricky."
In
2380
, Starfleet dispatched the
USS
Cerritos
, under the temporary command of
Captain
Amina Ramsey
, to repair the
waste pipe
of a
water filtration system
on Khwopa. (
LD
: "
Much Ado About Boimler
")
In
2382
of an
alternate universe
, the
Anaximander
and the
USS
Beagle
were forced to land on Khwopa for repairs. The crews of the two vessels were imprisoned by the Khwopians, who were hostile in this reality. The alternate
Beckett Mariner
serving on the
Anaximander
revealed that she was familiar with the planet and the inhabitants from her own reality.
(
LD
: "
Fissure Quest
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Kiley 279
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kiley_279
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Kiley 279
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Satellite(s):
At least 5
moons
Native species:
Kiley
Location:
Kiley system
Kiley 279
was the
inhabited
second
planet
in the
Kiley system
. An
M-class
world, it had at least five
moons
and was
home
to the
Kiley
, a
pre-warp
humanoid
species
. A type of flocking
bird
was also native to the planet. (
SNW
: "
Strange New Worlds
")
Astronomical data
Planetary data for Kiley 279
Kiley 279 was located at coordinates 2348.8711, in the
Alpha Quadrant
. Its
rotation period
was 27.1 hours and its
revolution period
was 404.6 solar days. The planet had an estimated
mass
of 5.86335 x 10
24
kilograms
and an estimated age of 5.5 billion solar years. Its
atmosphere
was composed of 76% molecular
nitrogen
, 22% molecular
oxygen
, 0.8%
argon
, 0.6%
carbon dioxide
, and 0.5%
nitrous oxide
, with trace amounts of
methane
and
glycine
. It had a mean surface temperature of 15.4 degrees
Celsius
. (
SNW
: "
Strange New Worlds
")
History
Kiley 279 had long been divided into two
factions
, whose mutual animosity had nearly escalated to the point of global
civil war
by the mid-
23rd century
. In
2259
,
Starfleet
detected a
warp signature
from the planet and sent the
USS
Archer
to investigate. Its crew of three, led by
Commander
Una Chin-Riley
,
beamed
down to make
first contact
but were imprisoned by the Kiley.
The
USS
Enterprise
subsequently arrived at Kiley 259 to find the
Archer
abandoned in
orbit
and no evidence of
warp-capable civilization
on the surface. A disguised
landing party
, led by
Captain
Christopher Pike
, rescued the
Archer
's crew and learned that the warp signature had in fact originated from a
warp bomb
, constructed by the Kiley after they observed the
battle against Control
a year earlier
.
Given the Federation's inadvertent contribution to the looming crisis on the planet, Pike elected to disregard
General Order 1
and reveal themselves so as to broker peace between the factions. He showed the Kiley leaders and population visual records of the devastation suffered by
Earth
during
World War III
, and urged them to avoid a similar fate and join the Federation instead. The Kiley thus decided to put aside their differences and pursue space travel as a united people. (
SNW
: "
Strange New Worlds
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, Kiley 279 was within the enclosed region. (
SA
: "
Rubincon
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Kim-Tara
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kim-Tara
======================================================================

Kim-Tara
was an astronomical object with unstable
gravimetric
conditions.
Starfleet
landing pods
were originally designed for a mission to Kim-Tara. (
DIS
: "
Brother
")
Since
Michael Burnham
was one of the test pilots for the pods, this mission was presumably planned during the first
Federation-Klingon War
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Kir (location)
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kir_(location)
======================================================================

Kir
was an ancient location on the
planet
Vulcan
that was home to a
Vulcan
temple
.
In "
Falor's Journey
", a Vulcan story, when
T'Para
offered
Falor
truth, he crossed the barren
Fire Plains
to get to the silent
monks
of Kir. Still unfulfilled, he journeyed home. (
VOY
: "
Innocence
")
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 419 identified Kir as a
province
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Klaestron IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Klaestron_IV
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Klaestron IV
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Independent
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Native species:
Klaestron
Location:
Klaestron system
Alpha Quadrant
Klaestron IV
was the
inhabited
fourth
planet
in the
Klaestron
system
,
Alpha Quadrant
.
This was the
homeworld
of the
Klaestrons
, a
warp-capable
humanoid
species
. The planet was unified under a single
government
from a
capital city
. Outside the capital, there was an
inn
where
Curzon Dax
and
Enina Tandro
spent some time.
An
extradition treaty
existed between the
governments
of Klaestron IV and the
Federation
; however, Klaestron IV had no extradition treaty with
Bajor
.
History
In the
24th century
, the planet was witness to the
Klaestron Civil War
. During the
civil war
, the capital city was the location of a military headquarters. In
2339
,
General
Ardelon Tandro
was
ambushed
and
killed
on the route from the capital to the front. Subsequently, he was elevated to national hero and his enraged
troops
ended the war with a victory. A lasting result of the civil war was that
statues
of the general were erected at many places on the planet.
In
2371
,
Julian Bashir
visited Klaestron IV to learn a new
treatment
for
burn
victims. According to him, the
technology
had to be seen to be believed.
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet was within the blast radius of one of the mines. (
DS9
: "
Dax
", "
Second Skin
";
SA
: "
300th Night
", "
Rubincon
")
Appendices
Background information
This planet's quadrant of origin is inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
The image of the
surface
of Klaestron IV shown in "Dax", was one of many reuses of the
matte painting
for
Angel I
. However, the image of Klaestron IV at
night
had a new
building
, that wasn't present in the
day
shot of Angel I, and was so forgotten in the day shot of Klaestron IV.
According to
StarTrek.com
, the pronunciation for Klaestron is "CLAY-stron".
[1]
(X)
However, according to the
script
for "Dax", the pronunciation was "KLY-stron".
[2]
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 47; "United Federation of Planets I"), in
2378
, Klaestron IV was listed as a
Federation member
.
External links
Klaestron IV
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Re-Uses of the Angel One Matte Painting
at
Ex Astris Scientia

PAGE: Klavdia III
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Klavdia_III
======================================================================

Klavdia III
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2365
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Klavdia III
was the
inhabited
third
planet
in its
star system
. The planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
. This planet had an almost identical
atmosphere
to
Daled IV
. The
troposphere
of the planet distorted normal communications signals. The environment was hostile to
humanoid
lifeforms
.
In
2349
, two
parents
from Daled IV sent their
daughter
Salia
to the research establishment on Klavdia III so she could be trained to govern her
homeworld
, and to be protected from those on Daled IV that might have wanted harm to come to her, such as the people who killed her parents shortly after she left.
In
2365
, the
USS
Enterprise
-D
visited Klavdia III to pick up Salia and her
governess
,
Anya
, for transport back to Daled IV. (
TNG
: "
The Dauphin
")
"KLAVH-dee-ah" was the pronunciation for this planet from the
script
pronunciation guide.
[1]
The planet model first used here for Klavdia III was later used for Daled IV,
("
The Dauphin
")
Tau Cygna V
,
("
The Ensigns of Command
")
Moab IV
,
("
The Masterpiece Society
")
and
Devidia II
.
("
Time's Arrow
")
External links
Klavdia
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Klavdia III
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Re-Used Planets in TNG
at
Ex Astris Scientia

Klendth
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
23rd century
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Klendth
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
. This planet was home to the
Loonkerian outpost
.
Sometime prior to
2286
, a piece of
bioengineering
technology
, known as the
universal atmospheric element compensator
, was made at the Loonkerian outpost on Klendth.
Spock
was asked about this technology in his
memory test
on
Vulcan
following his "rebirth" on
Genesis Planet
several
months
earlier. (
Star Trek IV: The Voyage Home
)
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.

Klowahka

(

)

Location:

or
Beta Quadrant
Klowahka
was an
inhabited
planet
and the
homeworld
(i.e. "nestworld") of the
warp-capable
Klowahkans
. Other
lifeforms
native to the planet included
Klowahkan oysters
and
grubfruit
. All arrivals to Klowahka were greeted with a traditional
amuse-bouche
.

, the

transported famed
food critics
Legnog
and
Gonald
from
Praxon IV
to Klowahka. (

: "

")

Knoll's Planet
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2371
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Knoll's Planet
was a
planet
whose position was labeled on a
star chart
in the guidance system
S/COMS
panel on aboard the
Amargosa observatory
in
2371
. (
Star Trek Generations
)
Background information
This planet was only mentioned in writing.
In August 2024, this panel was put up for auction in the Entertainment Memorabiliia Live Auction: Los Angeles Summer 2024 by Propstore Auction.
[1]
This location was named after the film's ILM visual effects supervisor
John Knoll
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Kobliad (planet)
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kobliad_(planet)

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Kobliad
Affiliation:
Non-aligned
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Native species:
Kobliad
Location:
Kobliad
system
Alpha Quadrant
Kobliad
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Kobliad
system
,
Alpha Quadrant
. This was the
homeworld
of the
Kobliads
. This planet's system was in or near to the
Venari Ral Zone
.
History
This planet was unified under a global
government
.
The
species
was dying off in the mid-
24th century
. This planet was known to the
Federation
, who were attempting to supply the
species
with
deuridium
so they could prolong their
life span
.
Rao Vantika
was one of the
planet
's most notorious
criminals
. He
murdered
many of his own kind in his pursuit of prolonging his own life span. According to
Ty Kajada
, the description of
murderer
for Vantika, "
doesn't do
justice
to the horrors he's responsible for on my world.
" (
DS9
: "
The Passenger
";
SA
: "
300th Night
", "
Rubincon
")
This world was only mentioned in dialogue.
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
Its name originated from an article on Rao Vantika from
StarTrek.com
.
[1]
(X)
Another StarTrek.com article placed it near the
Bajoran system
.
[2]
(X)

======================================================================
PAGE: Kolarus III
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kolarus_III
======================================================================

Kolarus III
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2379
)
Native species:
Kolarans
Location:
Kolarin system
Beta Quadrant
Kolarus III
was the
third
planet
in the
Kolarin
system
, orbiting at a distance of 1.2
au
about
Kolarus Prime
. It was located in
Federation
territory near the
Romulan Neutral Zone
.
The planet had a mass of 5.972 × 10
24
kg and an
O
2
/
N
2
atmosphere
. Its surface was dominated by vast savannas and deserts with little surface
water
. It was
inhabited
by isolated pockets of
Kolarans
, an early industrial
humanoid
civilization.
In
2379
,
Shinzon
planted the parts of the
Soong-type
android
B-4
on Kolarus III as part of his plan to capture
Jean-Luc Picard
. The
positronic
emissions from B-4 drew the attention of the
USS
Enterprise
-E
, the first Federation
starship
to visit the planet. An
Enterprise
away team
of Picard, Worf and Data collected the scattered parts, though they came under fire from the Kolarans. Their presence at Kolarus III meant that the
Enterprise
was the closest ship to
Romulus
when Shinzon made a peace overture to the Federation. (
Star Trek Nemesis
)
Appendices
Background information
This planet's quadrant of origin is inferred from its nearness to the Romulan Neutral Zone, which was canonically located in the Beta Quadrant in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
The Impossible Box
".
This planet was named after the Kalahari desert. (
Star Trek: The Next Generation Companion
, 3rd ed., p. 354)
During production on
Star Trek Nemesis
, Kolarus was represented by a
filming location
: a desert midway between
El Mirage Dry Lake Bed
in El Mirage, San Bernardino County, and Edwards Air Force Base, at the east side of Lancaster, off Avenue C between 235th and the San Bernardino County line, Lancaster, Los Angeles County.
[1]
(
Star Trek: The Next Generation Companion
, 3rd ed., p. 351)
CGI
sky replacement was thereafter done at
Digital Domain
. (
Star Trek: The Next Generation Companion
, 3rd ed., p. 351)
Map showing of Kolarus III's surface. This actually shows a part of the surface of
Mars
, specifically the Elysium Montes (left), the Tharsis region (center) and adjacent Valles Marineris (right).
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
claims that the topographical map of an area of Kolarus III, which was used by Data for locating the components of B-4, was based on a map of the
Hadley-Apennine
region of
Luna
. This was the landing site for
Apollo 15
. (
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 435) In fact however, it clearly shows an area of
Mars
.
External link
Kolarus III
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

,

.
After the

's
withdrawal
from

,

relocated to Kora II, where he became an

======================================================================
PAGE: Koralis III
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Koralis_III
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Koralis III
Type:

Status:

(

)

system
Alpha Quadrant

was the

third

of the

,

. This planet's system was located in or near to the

.

, which became abandoned by

. One of the notable features associated with this colony was an
orbital tether
. In that year,

told
Laas
to go to the planet, after she helped him escape
Deep Space 9
.
Odo
later met Laas on the planet. (
DS9
: "
Chimera
";
SA
: "
300th Night
", "
Rubincon
")
According to the
script
, the pronunciation for Koralis was "kuh-RAWL-uhs".

,

.
History
In the
24th century
, this planet, lying within the
territory
of the

, was the home for a

.
In
2372
, a

between the Cardassians and the

was held there to share intelligence on the
Klingons
. During the conference, the outpost was attacked and every
building
on the planet destroyed by Klingon forces, with no
survivors
. The outpost had been defended by several
system-5 disruptors
, which were disabled in the attack. One of those
disruptors
was
salvaged
by the
freighter
Groumall
and used successfully against one of the
Klingon ships
responsible. (
DS9
: "
Return to Grace
")

Korma Pass

star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "

".
"KORE-mah" was the pronunciation for this planet's
name
from the
script
pronunciation guide.

Type:

Status:

(

)
Native species:

Location:

Minister Campio in 2368
Kostolain
was an
inhabited
planet
that was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
. The planet was the
homeworld
to a
native species
.
In
2368
,
Campio
served as third
minister
to the

on Kostolain during the time he was to be
married
to
Lwaxana Troi
.
The
culture
of Kostolain was reserved and exceptionally formal. Strict protocol was always followed and, in many cases, enforced by the presence of a
Protocol Master
. (
TNG
: "
Cost Of Living
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets IV") and
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Federation Historical Highlights, 2161-2385"), there was a
star system
named Kostolain, in which this planet might possibly have been located, in the
Beta Quadrant
. It was a
binary
system with a
G-class star
and a K-class star.

======================================================================
PAGE: Kreetassa
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kreetassa
======================================================================

Kreetassa
Type:
Planet
Native species:
Kreetassans
Alvera trees
Location:
~120
light years
from
Earth
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Kreetassa
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
that was located within 120
light years
of
Earth
. It was home to a
humanoid
,
warp-capable
species called the
Kreetassans
.
First contact
with the Kreetassans was made in
2151
by the crew of the
Earth
Starfleet
starship
Enterprise
NX-01
. (
ENT
: "
Vox Sola
") However, it was not until a year later, in
2152
, that the ship visited Kreetassa.
In addition to humanoid species, Kreetassa was home to plant life in the form of large
trees
including sacred
Alvera trees
. The planet also supported pathogenic lifeforms, harmless to
Humans
but deadly to Earth
canines
. (
ENT
: "
A Night In Sickbay
")
Appendices
Background information
The image of the planet contains a reverse image of the
Great Lakes
, surrounded entirely by land (i.e., without the St. Lawrence Seaway). The same reverse image is used for a shot of
Enterprise
orbiting
Earth
in "
Affliction
".
In the final draft script of "A Night in Sickbay", Kreetassa was not physically described, only that it be "an alien planet." The planet's
capital city
was described as "a series of formally arranged buildings, monuments, and parks."
StarTrek.com
identified the Kreetassans as an
Alpha Quadrant
species, placing their homeworld in that
quadrant
.
[1]
(X)
In the
reference book
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 60, "United Federation of Planets III"), Kreetassa was located in the
Psi5 Aurigae
system
, in the
Alpha Quadrant
. This system was a single star system. The primary was a
class G star
with a magnitude of +5, which was the same brightness as
Sol
. In the mid-
22nd century
, this system was in non-aligned space. In the late
24th century
, this system was in or near to Federation space. The planet Pyrellia, home to
Pyrellian ginger tea
, was located in this system as well.
External links
Kreetassa
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Pyrellia
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Krios Prime
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Krios_Prime
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Krios Prime
AKA:
Krios
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Klingon Empire
(formerly)
Status:
Habitable
(
3194
)
Native species:
Kriosians
Location:
Kriosian
system
Beta Quadrant
Krios Prime
, or simply
Krios
, was the
inhabited
prime
planet
in the
Kriosian
system
in the
Beta Quadrant
. It was the
homeworld
of the
Kriosians
. (
TNG
: "
The Mind's Eye
")
Areas of the planet's surface were covered by
forests
. Native
lifeforms
included the
nightbird
and the
Veqlargh toQ
, as well as a large
bioluminescent
insect
. (
SA
: "
Vox In Excelso
")
History
This planet was named after
Krios
, who ruled the ancient Kriosian Empire with his
brother
Valt
. The brothers became divided over the
love
for a woman named
Garuth
, so eventually their empire fell. Krios
kidnapped
Garuth from her homeworld,
Valt Minor
, and took her to Krios Prime, which started a
centuries
-long war between the two worlds. (
TNG
: "
The Perfect Mate
")
By the
22nd century
, the Kriosians were under the rule of the
Sovereign Dynasty of Krios Prime
, a family known on hundreds of worlds. The royal guardsmen of the Dynasty were known as the
Sovereign Guard
. Their
leader
was known as the
First Monarch
, a post which
Kaitaama
ascended to in
2153
. (
ENT
: "
Precious Cargo
")
By the mid-
24th century
, after conquering Krios and suspending Kriosian sovereignty, the
Klingons
established a
colony
on this planet. It was the only Klingon colony on the border of
Federation space
. In
2367
, the
Federation
was accused by the planet's
governor
,
Vagh
, of aiding the planet's rebels, who hoped to free the planet from Klingon rule. However, it was revealed that Klingon
Ambassador
Kell
, in an attempt to split the Klingon-Federation alliance, was in league with the
Romulans
, who were secretly aiding the rebels with forged
Starfleet
weapons
. (
TNG
: "
The Mind's Eye
")
In
2368
, Krios and Valt Minor took their first steps towards ending an ongoing war as
Kamala
, the first female
metamorph
born on Krios since the mid-
23rd century
, was delivered for
marriage
to
Chancellor
Alrik
of Valt Minor. (
TNG
: "
The Perfect Mate
")
In
3194
, the
House of Kraag
was encamped in the
wilderness
on Krios Prime, living as
hunter-gatherers
. They had intermittent contact with a nearby
trading post
, but rejected all
alien
technology
as had become customary for the
Klingon diaspora
. After
Thar
was killed in a altercation with a
trader
and his
brother
Jay-Den
fell out with their
father
Drekol
over joining
Starfleet Academy
, the House departed the planet in their ship, leaving Jay-Den behind. (
SA
: "
Vox In Excelso
")
Appendices
Background information
This planet's quadrant of origin is inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star charts
appearing in the
Star Trek: Discovery
episode "
Magic to Make the Sanest Man Go Mad
" and in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".  In the case of the latter, the Krios system is no longer depicted as being in
Klingon space
, with it being now in neutral space, and far from the border of Federation space.
"Krios" in the 2368 setting of "The Perfect Mate"
It was never implied or otherwise indicated that the planet depicted in "The Perfect Mate" (and referenced in "Precious Cargo") was supposed to be the same as (or different from) the planet that was portrayed a year prior in "The Mind's Eye".
Though unseen in "Precious Cargo", the final reference to Krios was further complicated in a
conversation
between
Trip Tucker
and Kaitaama, when Tucker asks to visit (simply) Krios, causing Kaitaama to correct him by saying "Krios Prime". The inclusion of this trivia by the writers was unnecessary for advancing the plot, but suggested that there was more than one planet with this name, or, among other reasons, she was extremely proper about her planet's name, which easily follows the air of superiority she carried throughout the episode.
Furthermore, while both planets were called "Krios", in both dialogue and in
script
notes, visually, the planet's
color
changed, quite noticeably, between the two episodes in which the planet was shown. For that matter, "The Perfect Mate" also made no effort to reconcile, or altogether mention the prominent Klingon connection made in the planet's first appearance. An additional, and unrelated, script note from "The Mind's Eye" described the
Kriosian Capital City
as thus; "
The
architecture
is simple,
Spartan
, very Klingon.
" The visual discrepancy between both episodes was reproduced for the remastered version of TNG.
On
StarTrek.com
, it was stated explicitly that there were two different planets and/or systems that shared the same name: "Krios".
[1]
(X)
In contrast, the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 445 only listed a single entry for Krios. The planet was classified as a
class M
planet, which – by the 24th century – was located in
Klingon space
. The Kriosian system was named as a
protectorate
of the Klingon Empire. Furthermore, the
Encyclopedia
's entry for "Krios Prime" directs the reader to "Krios", indicating the authors treat "Krios Prime" and "Krios" as interchangeable.
Apocrypha
In the video game
Star Trek: Infinite
, the planet Krios Prime in the Krios system is under Klingon control at the start of the game in
2346
, which identifies it as the planet from both "The Mind's Eye" and "The Perfect Mate".
External links
Krios Prime
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Re-Used Planets in TNG
at
Ex Astris Scientia

======================================================================
PAGE: Krulmuth-B
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Krulmuth-B
======================================================================

Krulmuth-B
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2381
)
Satellite(s):
At least four
moons
Location:
Krulmuth
system
,
Alpha Quadrant
Krulmuth-B
was an uninhabited
class M
planet
in the
Krulmuth
system
that was home to the
Krulmuth-B portal
. It had at least four
moons
and was located in the same
sector
as the
Setlik system
. (
SNW
: "
Those Old Scientists
";
Star Trek: Section 31
)
Astronomical data
Planetary data for Krulmuth-B
Krulmuth-B was located at
coordinates
3119.7266. Its
rotation period
was 24.3 hours and its
revolution period
was 371
solar days
. The planet had an estimated
mass
of 5.89104
e24
×10
24
kilograms
and an estimated age of 5.984 billion
solar years
. It had a
desert
landscape with a mean surface
temperature
of 14.4
degrees
Celsius
. (
SNW
: "
Those Old Scientists
")
History
The ancient
Nausicaans
were present on Krulmuth-B and left
glyphs
on the portal reading "this is a
time portal
".
In
2259
, the
USS
Enterprise
detected activity at the portal while transporting
tritriticale
grain
to
Setlik II
. They arrived at Krulmuth-B to find
Ensign
Brad Boimler
of the
USS
Cerritos
, who had been pulled through from
2381
when the portal was accidentally activated by the
radioisotopes
from a
holo-camera
. Concurrently, the
Orion
science vessel
D'Var
also arrived at the planet; after a terse exchange between
Captains
Christopher Pike
and
Harr Caras
, the Orions seized the portal and fled.
With Boimler's assistance, the
Enterprise
tracked down the
D'Var
and traded its
cargo
of tritriticale for the portal. However, before Boimler could use it to return to the future, Ensign
Beckett Mariner
also traveled through it in an attempt to save him. This exhausted the portal's existing supply of
horonium
, though the
Enterprise
crew were able to extract more from a component of the
Enterprise
NX-01
that had been built into the ship.
Boimler and Mariner prepare to return to their time through the portal
Upon returning to the surface, the
Enterprise
team encountered Caras and his
landing party
. Boimler and Mariner appealed to Caras by noting that, in their time,
Orions
would be known for more than
piracy
, and that they served with the
great-granddaughter
of one of his
crew members
. Pike proposed that the Orions could receive credit for discovering the portal in exchange for their access. Caras agreed, allowing Boimler and Mariner to return to 2381 without incident.
In the ensuing 120 years until the
Cerritos
' visit, no further activity was recorded from the portal. The
Cerritos
'
mission
at the portal was simply to take routine
sensor
readings and check for problems. (
SNW
: "
Those Old Scientists
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Ktaria VII
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ktaria_VII
======================================================================

Ktaria VII
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Ktarians
Status:
Habitable
(
24th century
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Ktaria VII
was the
seventh
planet
in its
star system
. This world was under
Ktarian
jurisdiction. Ktarians were known to
bury
their
dead
there.
Chakotay
once went on a
tomb excavation
on Ktaria VII. He wanted to keep a memento of his first experience there by picking up a
rock
from the
burial site
. He later found out that it wasn't just a rock but a sacred stone. He later learned that Ktarians lay thousands of them in the tombs of loved ones, each one representing a special
prayer
. His actions were, in turn, a desecration of
the man's
burial site. (
VOY
: "
Emanations
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 3rd ed., p. 254 identified this planet as the Ktarian
homeworld
.
StarTrek.com
considered it as a possibility that it was the Ktarian homeworld.
[1]
(X)
This was before "
Child's Play
" where the homeworld's
name
was given as
Ktaris
. The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 446 stated that Ktaris, misspelled in the book as
Katarus
, was an alternate name for Ktaria VII.

PAGE: Ktaris
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ktaris
======================================================================

Ktaris
Type:
Planet
Native species:
Ktarian
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Ktaris
was an
inhabited
planet
and the
Ktarian
homeworld
.
For the first
Annual Voyager Science Fair
in
2376
,
Naomi Wildman
programmed an impressive model of
her father
's homeworld, including its geophysical and
atmospheric
conditions. In her model, she programmed a massive
storm
system over the
Arpasian Range
, which was known for high winds and
hail
. (
VOY
: "
Child's Play
")
The Ktarian glaciers were a popular
ski
destination. (
VOY
: "
Macrocosm
")
In "
The Voyager Conspiracy
",
Seven of Nine
states, "
The Ktarians were officially with the
Federation
, but they sympathized with the
Maquis
.
" See
Ktarian
for more information on the question of their relationship with the Federation.
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 446 states that Ktaris, misspelled in the book as "
Katarus
", was an alternate name for
Ktaria VII
.
See also
Ktarian moonrise simulation

Ktaris
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Kurill Prime
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kurill_Prime
======================================================================

Kurill Prime
was the
planet
claimed by
Eris
to be her home, which she said was conquered by the
Dominion
in
2370
.
According to Eris, the planet had been offered entry to the Dominion because the Dominion believed that the telekinetic powers of its inhabitants would be useful to them. When the natives had refused, the
Jem'Hadar
came in and seized control. They took over the
communications center
and
executed
the
leaders
.
However, Eris was a
Vorta
spy
, so at least part of her
story
, her planet of origin, may have been a fabrication. (
DS9
: "
The Jem'Hadar
")
Appendices
Background information
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to the
script
, the pronunciation for Kurill was "kuh-RILL".
[1]
StarTrek.com
suggested that perhaps these natives of Kurill were called Kurillians, with the episode making no mention of it and Eris simply presenting herself as being from Kurill Prime.
[2]
(X)
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 71), Kurill Prime was the
homeworld
of the Vorta. This
class M
world was where the
Changelings
discovered the Vorta as the small, timid,
ape
-like
forest
dwellers. They originally were living in hollowed-out
trees
to avoid
predators
. Changelings genetically engineered the Vorta into intelligent
humanoids
and employed them at the highest level of the Dominion, as the story was told in "
Treachery, Faith and the Great River
".
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 74), the Kurill system was a
binary
system with an
F-class star
and a G-class star.
External link
Kurill Prime
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Kurl
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kurl
======================================================================

Kurl
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2369
)
Native species:
Kurlans
Location:
Kurlan
system
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Kurl
was a formerly
inhabited
planet
in the
Kurlan
system
, many
light years
outside
Federation
territory
. Kurl was home to the
extinct
Kurlan
civilization
, whose remains were of great
archaeological
interest. In the
24th century
, the only planet near Kurl capable of supporting
life
was
Loren III
.
Federation
archaeologist
Galen
completed a study of Kurlan
artifacts
in the
2360s
. He returned to Kurl briefly in
2369
on his way to Loren III, during which he obtained a
Kurlan naiskos
for his former
student
Jean-Luc Picard
. (
TNG
: "
The Chase
")
Appendices
Background information
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
Apocrypha
In the
novella
Unjoined
, it was revealed that the
parasitic beings
from
TNG
: "
Conspiracy
" were genetically created from
Trill symbionts
in an ancient
Trill
colony
on Kurl.
External link
Kurl
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Kwejian (planet)
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kwejian_(planet)
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Kwejian
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Diameter:
11,480
kilometers
Affiliation:
Independent
Status:
Destroyed
(
3190
)
Satellite(s):
One moon
Native species:
Kwejian
Location:
Kwejian system
Alpha Quadrant
Kwejian
was a
class M
planet
with
one moon
in the
Kwejian system
. It was
inhabited
by the
Kwejian
people, who resided in a shielded area called the
Sanctuary
on one of the
continents
. The planet was destroyed by the
Dark Matter Anomaly
in
3190
. (
DIS
: "
The Sanctuary
", "
Kobayashi Maru
", "
Anomaly (DIS)
")
Planetary data
Kwejian planetary data
Kwejian was located at
coordinates
22-45330.72020-11, some two weeks' travel from
Federation Headquarters
at
full warp
. Its
catalog number
in the
Starfleet database
was Beta-340403-342018.
The planet's
orbit
had a
semi-major axis
of 108.208 million
kilometers
(0.723332
au
), an
aphelion
of 108.939 million kilometers (0.728213 au), and a
perihelion
of 107.477 million kilometers (0.718440 au). Its
orbital eccentricity
was 0.006772, and its average
orbital speed
was 35.02
kilometers per second
. Its orbit was
inclined
3.39458
degrees
to the
ecliptic
, 3.68 degrees to its
sun
's
equator
, and 2.19 degrees to the
invariable plane
. Its
orbital period
was 23.549
hours
and its
year
was 583.92
days
long. The planet had a
diameter
of 11,480
kilometers
and a
surface gravity
of 9.8. (
DIS
: "
The Sanctuary
", "
There Is A Tide...
")
History
Kwejian before its destruction
In the late
31st century
,
subspace
damage caused by the
the Burn
shifted the orbit of Kwejian's moon, which in turn changed the
tides
on the surface. As a result, vast numbers of sea locusts emerged from the ocean and devoured the Kwejian
harvest
, leading to millions starving. The
Emerald Chain
offered them
repellent
to drive the locusts away, in exchange for the planet's trance worms.
In
3174
, Cleveland Booker left his
homeworld
in disgust over their deal with the Emerald Chain, and dedicated himself to saving trance worms.
In
3189
,
Osyraa
withheld the sea locust repellent from Kwejian, threatening them again with
famine
, as leverage for her demand that
Steward of the Sanctuary
Kyheem
deliver Booker to her. In response to Kyheem's message, the
USS
Discovery
arrived and, after a brief standoff with Osyraa's
flagship
Viridian
, forced her to withdraw. The
Discovery
crew then devised a means to amplify the
electromagnetic
connection between the sea locusts, allowing Kyheem and Booker to combine their
empathic
abilities and persuade the creatures to return to the ocean. (
DIS
: "
The Sanctuary
")
In
3190
,
Steward
Kyheem
of the
Sanctuary
and his brother,
Cleveland Booker
noticed saw a flock of birds behaving strangely. At the same time,
Deep Space
Repair Beta Six
experienced gravitational instability. Commander
Nalas
asked for assistance from
Federation Headquarters
. Starfleet's
Commander in chief
, Fleet Admiral
Charles Vance
sent the
USS
Discovery
-A
under Captain
Michael Burnham
and
Federation President
Laira Rillak
to assist. (
DIS
: "
Kobayashi Maru
")
Kwejian and its moon were struck by
gravitational waves
from what was later termed the
Dark Matter Anomaly
. Kwejian experienced catastrophic
gravitational shear
as it passed within twelve
AU
of the anomaly's
ergosphere
. All life on the surface was extinguished, and the remnants of the planet were thrown hundreds of thousands of
kilometers
from its previous position. The only known survivor of the disaster was Booker, who was aboard his ship at the time. Debris from the planet was later found in the anomaly's
accretion disk
. (
DIS
: "
Kobayashi Maru
", "
Anomaly (DIS)
", "
Choose to Live
")
Booker took his ship to investigate the birds' strange activity. All flock of birds then fell from the sky. Booker told his brother to take his son
Leto
somewhere safe. Booker then saw a massive gravitational distortion destroy Kwejian's moon. The moon's debris hit Booker's ship and sent if flying away. Booker's ship was able to return to
Discovery
via autopilot following the evacuation of Deep Space
Repair Beta Six
. Booker and
Discovery
's crew discovered that the distortion had somehow pushed. Kwejian was completely destroyed. (
DIS
: "
Kobayashi Maru
")
Two days later, Starfleet began to search for other survivors who were off-world during the destruction. Booker was traumatized by the loss of his home world in addition to his adopted brother and nephew.
Discovery
along with
Ni'Var
's
Science Institute
began investigating and studying the anomaly that Commander
Paul Stamets
dubbed the
Dark Matter Anomaly
. (
DIS
: "
Anomaly (DIS)
", "
Choose to Live
")
In
3191
, Booker learned that at some point in the past, someone had deposited cuttings of Kwejian's sacred
World Root
in the
Eternal Gallery and Archive
which was moved to a place of honor following the planet's destruction as a part of the Archive's sacred duty to preserve the memory of lost cultures. Booker was allowed to take the cuttings with him, and he would later plant them on
Sanctuary Four
where a new World Root would eventually grow. (
DIS
: "
Labyrinths
", "
Life, Itself
")
In
3195
, the location of the Kwejian Homeworld was depicted on a
Federation
star chart
which was used in
Starfleet Academy
's
Temporal Mechanics
classroom
. It was specifically labeled as destroyed. (
SA
: "
Beta Test
")
This star chart helped with placing Kwejian in the Alpha Quadrant, as other labeled locations near it have been confirmed to be in the Alpha Quadrant.
Environment
Map of Kwejian's surface showing the continent where the Sanctuary is located
Described as "one of the most beautiful planets in the known
galaxy
" by
Hugh Culber
, Kwejian contained
oceans
and
forests
, including the
World Root
, a network of
tree
roots
that spanned the entire planet. (
DIS
: "
The Sanctuary
", "
Kobayashi Maru
", "
All Is Possible
")
Kwejian's
atmosphere
was composed of 75%
nitrogen
, 23%
oxygen
, and 2%
water vapor
. The
Great Storms of Naillem'kwai
were a significant
weather
phenomenon on the planet. The mean
surface temperature
was 15
degrees
Celsius
. (
DIS
: "
There Is A Tide...
", "
All Is Possible
")
The environment of
Sanctuary Four
resembled that of Kwejian. (
DIS
: "
That Hope Is You, Part 1
")
Geographic locations
Mameckx'sha River
Tulí Forests
Flora and fauna
Carrion reaver
Jajtspat
Kowolian nightsprey
Sea locust
Trance worm
Tuli tree

======================================================================
PAGE: Kyana Prime
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kyana_Prime
======================================================================

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Kyana Prime
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Krenim Imperium
Status:
Habitable
(
2174
)
Location:
Grid 005
Delta Quadrant
Kyana Prime
was the
inhabited
prime
planet
in its
star system
, located in
Grid 005
in the
Delta Quadrant
. It was home to a remote
Krenim
colony
during the
22nd century
, where the
temporal scientist
Annorax
and
his wife
lived.
In an
alternate
2174
, Annorax constructed a
weapon ship
in an attempt to return the
Krenim Imperium
to power by erasing its enemies from
history
. In his second
temporal incursion
, he accidentally eliminated the Kyana Prime colony, and with it his wife. Annorax proceeded to labor, without success, for the next two centuries to restore Kyana Prime and the Imperium. Not until
2374
, when the weapon ship itself was erased from history due to the actions of
USS
Voyager
, were Annorax and his wife restored to their original lives at the colony
. (
VOY
: "
Year of Hell
", "
Year of Hell, Part II
")
According to the
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 86), the Kyana system was a
binary star system
with a
pair
of
Class K stars
.
External link
Kyana Prime
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Kylata II
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kylata_II
======================================================================

Kylata II
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2371
)
Location:
Kylata
system
Gamma Quadrant
Kylata II
was the
second
planet
in the
Kylata
system
. This system was located in the
Gamma Quadrant
.
In
2371
, this
M-class
planet was
scanned
by the
USS
Defiant
; however, the
crew
didn't have the time to beam down to the
surface
of the planet for a closer look. (
DS9
: "
Meridian
")
Background information
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
The planet's name was from
StarTrek.com
and the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 449.
[1]
(X)
In the episode, it was not given a
name
and was described as the "second planet in the Kylata system".
According to the
script
, the pronunciation for Kylata was "KIE-lah-tuh".
[2]
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 75), the Kylata system was a destination on the Gamma Quadrant Bio-Survey (
2369
-
75
).

======================================================================
PAGE: Kyron 4
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kyron_4
======================================================================

Kyron 4
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2381
)
Location:
Alpha Quadrant
Kyron 4
was the
fourth
planet
in its
star system
. The surface of the world experienced dangerous
ion storms
that could last for years.
In
2381
,
Lieutenant jg
Brad Boimler
and
Lieutenant
Steve Levy
were assigned to a routine
dilithium
survey
on Kyron 4. During the
away mission
, they were forced to shelter inside a
cave
by a sudden ion storm. After finding a suspiciously convenient
ground vehicle
that could accommodate only a single occupant, they discovered that the situation had been engineered as a
morality
test by the
Vendorians
. The two managed to establish a rapport with the Vendorians thanks to Levy's knowledge of their
culture
, and ultimately pass the morality test by rising above their earlier interpersonal strife. (
LD
: "
Caves
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Kzin
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kzin
======================================================================

Kzin
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Kzinti
Status:
Habitable
(
2365
)
Native species:
Kzinti
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Kzin
was an
inhabited
planet
that was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
. This was the
homeworld
of the
Kzinti
. The
leader
of the
Kzinti government
was the
Highest of Kzin
.
History
In the late
21st century
, the Kzinti fought a series of
four wars
with
Humankind
, and lost them all. Under the
Treaty of Sirius
, they were forbidden all weapons except
police vessels
and related
equipment
. Nevertheless, some Kzinti illegally possessed
phasers
and sought other armaments.
In
2269
, two
Slaver
stasis boxes
were discovered by
archaeologists
on Kzin. One was turned over to
Starfleet
, and the other was kept by the Kzinti, but found to be empty. With the secret support of their government, a group of Kzinti
privateers
led by
Chuft-Captain
of the ostensibly stolen
police ship
Traitor's Claw
undertook a
mission
to reacquire the unopened box and gain control of a powerful
Slaver weapon
hoped to be inside. This weapon self-destructed and killed the privateers before it could be returned to Kzin. (
TAS
: "
The Slaver Weapon
")
Stellar cartography
The
location
of Kzin in the
Milky Way Galaxy
was labeled on a
star chart
in
Captain
James T. Kirk
's
quarters
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-A
in
2293
and at
Starfleet Headquarters
in
2364
. (
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
;
TNG
: "
Conspiracy
")
In
2365
, the location of Kzin was labeled in a Subspace Comm Net Ops star chart, which was seen in the
courtroom
on
Starbase 173
. This planet was connected to the
Sol system
by a subspace line. (
TNG
: "
The Measure Of A Man
")
In August 2024, an annotated computer paste-up of this star chart was put up for auction in the Entertainment Memorabiliia Live Auction: Los Angeles Summer 2024 by Propstore Auction.
[1]
In
Larry Niven
's
Known Space Universe
, the central star of the
Kzinti
's homeworld was identified as
61 Ursae Majoris
.

======================================================================
PAGE: L-374 I
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/L-374_I
======================================================================

L-374 I
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2267
)
Location:
L-374
system
Unnamed sector
Beta Quadrant
L-374 I
was the
uninhabited
first
planet
of the
L-374
system
. This planet was located within a
sector
of the
Beta Quadrant
and was near the
L-370
system. This planet was close to the
Rigel
system.
This was one of two planets spared in the
planet killer
attack of
2267
; the other was
L-374 II
. The other two planets in the system,
L-374 III
and
L-374 IV
, were destroyed. When the
crew
of the badly damaged
USS
Constellation
was unaccounted for after the planet killer's attack,
Spock
considered it improbable that the ship's
crew
had fled to L-374 I, as this planet had a
surface temperature
of molten
lead
(approximately 327
°C
). (
TOS
: "
The Doomsday Machine
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
The quadrant of origin for this planet was inferred from the trajectory of the planet killer. This ancient machine destroyed, in this order, L-370 and L-374 and the next system targeted was the
Rigel system
. This system had been established to be in the Beta Quadrant by
star charts
seen in
Star Trek: Enterprise
,
Star Trek: Discovery
,
Star Trek: Picard
,
Star Trek: Strange New Worlds
, and
Star Trek: Section 31
.

======================================================================
PAGE: L-374 II
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/L-374_II
======================================================================

L-374 II
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2267
)
Location:
L-374
system
Unnamed sector
Beta Quadrant
L-374 II
was the
uninhabited
second
planet
of the
L-374
system
. This system was located within a
sector
of the
Beta Quadrant
and was near the
L-370
system. This planet was close to the
Rigel
system.
This was one of two planets spared in the
planet killer
attack of
2267
; the other was
L-374 I
. The other two planets in the system,
L-374 III
and
L-374 IV
, were destroyed. When the
crew
of the badly-damaged
USS
Constellation
was unaccounted for after the planet killer's attack,
Spock
considered it improbable that the ship's crew had fled to L-374 II, as this planet had an
atmosphere
poisonous
to
Humans
. (
TOS
: "
The Doomsday Machine
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
The quadrant of origin for this planet was inferred from the trajectory of the planet killer. This ancient machine destroyed, in this order, L-370 and L-374 and the next system targeted was the
Rigel system
. This system had been established to be in the Beta Quadrant by
star charts
seen in
Star Trek: Enterprise
,
Star Trek: Discovery
,
Star Trek: Picard
,
Star Trek: Strange New Worlds
, and
Star Trek: Section 31
.

======================================================================
PAGE: L-374 III
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/L-374_III
======================================================================

L-374 III
Type:
Planet
Status:
Destroyed
(
2267
)
Location:
L-374
system
Unnamed sector
Beta Quadrant
L-374 III
was the
uninhabited
third
planet
in the
L-374
system
. This planet was located within a
sector
of the
Beta Quadrant
and was near the
L-370
system. This planet was close to the
Rigel
system.
It was one of two planets destroyed in the
planet killer
attack of
2267
; the other was
L-374 IV
. The other two planets in the system,
L-374 I
and
L-374 II
, were spared. In
2267
, the entire crew of the
Federation
starship
USS
Constellation
, consisting of four hundred men and women, was
beamed
down to this
planet
for safety against the
planet killer
which was attacking the
Constellation
with her
Captain
,
Commodore
Matt Decker
still aboard the
starship
trying to outrun the
machine
. When the
Constellation
was disabled, the doomsday machine turned onto L-374 III. Severely damaged and with
transporters
offline, there was nothing Decker could do to save his helpless crew. As a result, the doomsday machine destroyed the third planet and blasted it to rubble and
debris
, and Decker's entire crew was killed. (
TOS
: "
The Doomsday Machine
")
The quadrant of origin for this planet was inferred from the trajectory of the planet killer. This ancient machine destroyed, in this order, L-370 and L-374 and the next system targeted was the
Rigel system
. This system had been established to be in the Beta Quadrant by
star charts
seen in
Star Trek: Enterprise
,
Star Trek: Discovery
,
Star Trek: Picard
,
Star Trek: Strange New Worlds
, and
Star Trek: Section 31
.

======================================================================
PAGE: L-374 IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/L-374_IV
======================================================================

L-374 IV
Type:
Planet
Status:
Destroyed
(
2267
)
Location:
L-374
system
Unnamed sector
Beta Quadrant
L-374 IV
was the
uninhabited
fourth
planet
in the
L-374
system
. This planet was located within a
sector
of the
Beta Quadrant
and was near the
L-370
system. This planet was close to the
Rigel
system.
This was one of two planets destroyed in the
planet killer
attack of
2267
; the other was
L-374 III
. The other two planets in the system,
L-374 I
and
L-374 II
, were spared. In
2267
,
Science Officer
Masada
of the
USS
Constellation
picked up
scans
that the planet was being broken up by what was later known as the "
planet killer
". The
captain
of the
Constellation
,
Commodore
Matt Decker
, ordered his crew to attack and destroy the
machine
. Despite their efforts, the doomsday machine destroyed the entire planet. (
TOS
: "
The Doomsday Machine
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
The quadrant of origin for this planet was inferred from the trajectory of the planet killer. This ancient machine destroyed, in this order, L-370 and L-374 and the next system targeted was the
Rigel system
. This system had been established to be in the Beta Quadrant by
star charts
seen in
Star Trek: Enterprise
,
Star Trek: Discovery
,
Star Trek: Picard
,
Star Trek: Strange New Worlds
, and
Star Trek: Section 31
.

======================================================================
PAGE: L-S VI
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/L-S_VI
======================================================================

L-S VI
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2370
)
Native species:
Changeling
-like
vegetation
Location:
Gamma Quadrant
~6
light years
from terminus of
Bajoran wormhole
L-S VI
was a formerly
inhabited
planet
which once hosted a
civilization
, though by
2370
only ruins remained. The planet was located about six
light years
from the
Gamma Quadrant
terminus of the
Bajoran wormhole
.
In 2370,
Dr.
Mora Pol
of the
Bajoran Institute of Science
mounted an expedition with
Starfleet
assistance to L-S VI, where
Bajoran science probes
had discovered
DNA
similar to
Odo
's.
Silicate
-based
lifeforms
were found on the planet, including a small
vegetative
organism
with
Changeling
-like metamorphic properties.
During the expedition,
Jadzia Dax
, Mora Pol, and
Weld Ram
were injured from inhaling a ground eruption of
volcanic
gas
. Odo was also unknowingly affected by the gas, which caused him to change into a large, instinct-driven
creature
, back aboard
Deep Space 9
. (
DS9
: "
The Alternate
")
Appendices
Background information
L-S VI was depicted using a set on
Paramount Stage 18
. "
We [...] had to shoot a planet set,
" recalled
David Livingston
. "
I think
David [Carson
, the director,] and [DP]
Marvin [Rush]
shot it successfully; you really believed you were on this ruined planet. It's hard to do that kind of stuff.
" (
Captains' Logs Supplemental - The Unauthorized Guide to the New Trek Voyages
, p. 68)
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 74), L-S VI was located in the L-S system. This system's
primary
was a
K-class star
.
It is unclear whether the obelisk which, in "The Alternate", is found at the center of the ruins on L-S VI and described by Mora Pol as a possible "relic of Odo's people" was indeed genuine. "
To tell you the truth,
" admitted Odo actor
Rene Auberjonois
, "
I think the obelisk was simply a red herring. In fact, I asked [director] David Carson if anything is going to happen with this thing and he just grinned and said it's just a red herring to confuse the audience so they don't get too far ahead of us [....] But I never assume anything. Watch, three stories into
next season
there will be a whole story about the damned obelisk still floating around the station.
" (
Cinefantastique
, Vol. 25/26, No. 6/1, p. 103). The same obelisk-prop was used in
DS9
: "
The Search, Part II
" on the
Founders' homeworld
, but no connection about the similarity with the one from L-S VI was ever made on-screen.
According to
StarTrek.com
, L-S VI was an
M-class
planet
orbiting
the
primary
L-S.
[1]
(X)
[2]
(X)
External link
Re-Used Planets in DS9
at
Ex Astris Scientia

======================================================================
PAGE: L5 Test Station
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/L5_Test_Station
======================================================================

The
L5 Test Station
was an installation associated with the
NX Project
. Its
assignment patch
, along with a number of others, was on display in a case at the
602 Club
. (
ENT
: "
First Flight
")
L5 presumably refers to
Earth
's fifth
Lagrange point
.
See also
L5 colony
External link
L5 Lagrangian point
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: LT-358
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/LT-358
======================================================================

LT-358
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2381
)
Satellite(s):
At least
one
moon
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
LT-358
was an uninhabited
planet
in a
binary star system
. Described as a "barren
rock
", it had a
mountainous
desert
environment with a
Human
-breathable
atmosphere
, and at least
one
large
moon
.
In
2381
,
Starfleet
was scheduled to construct an
outpost
on LT-358, which an earlier
probe
had indicated possessed no indigenous
lifeforms
.
Captain
Carol Freeman
convinced
Starfleet Command
to make this task the second leg of a "
mission race
" between her ship, the
USS
Cerritos
, and the newly introduced
automated starship
USS
Aledo
, so as to demonstrate the continued viability of the
California
-class
.
The
Cerritos
arrived at LT-358 first, but progress was stalled when
Ensign
D'Vana Tendi
detected signs of
microbial life
in the
soil
. A subsequent
self-awareness assessment
conducted by
Doctor
T'Ana
found that Tendi's readings were a false positive caused by a
metaphasic charge
, though the delay cost the
Cerritos
the race. However, Freeman later realized that the
Aledo
had skipped analyzing signs of life at LT-358, in violation of the
Prime Directive
, indicating a flaw in its
artificial intelligence
. (
LD
: "
The Stars At Night
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Lactra VII
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Lactra_VII
======================================================================

Lactra VII
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Neutral
Status:
Habitable
(
2365
)
Native species:
Lactrans
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Lactra VII
was an
inhabited
M-class
planet
in its
star system
. This planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
This planet had
Earth normal
atmosphere
and
gravity
. It was home to the
Lactrans
, a highly advanced
non-humanoid
and
telepathic
species
. There were several nearby celestial bodies in the
sky
of Lactra VII.
The
planet
contained at least one
major city
, considered admirable. Inside the
city
, and surrounding it up to almost a hundred
kilometers
, was a
zoo
in which a variety of
lifeforms
from other planets were kept in habitats
manufactured
to their needs. One such example included a
desert
and a
rainforest
separated by a
river
. The
water
discovered on Lactra VII was clear and palatable, but was, according to
Spock
, "too pure." (
TAS
: "
The Eye of the Beholder
")
History
The Lactrans had a
history
spanning back more then tens of thousands of their own
centuries
.
The
Starfleet
survey ship
USS
Ariel
was the first to explore the planet in
2269
, and no information was available about it prior to its
mission
. However, the
Ariel
away team
disappeared, and the rest of the crew disappeared after
beaming
down to search for the team.
Six
weeks
later, the
USS
Enterprise
reached the planet to search for the
Ariel
crew. The crew discovered that the three members of the original away team had perished, and the rest had been imprisoned.
Sensors
later revealed a
large concentration
of
lifeforms
98.5
kilometers
northeast
of the desert portion of what turned out to be a zoo.
After some initial trouble, a semblance of communication with the Lactrans was devised. (
TAS
: "
The Eye of the Beholder
")
Stellar cartography
The location of Lactra VII in "The Explored Galaxy" star chart
In
2293
, the
location
of Lactra VII in the
Milky Way Galaxy
was labeled in a
star chart
that was in
Captain
James T. Kirk
's
quarters
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-A
. (
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
,
okudagram
)
In
2365
, the location of Lactra VII was labeled in a Subspace Comm Net Ops star chart, which was seen in the
courtroom
on
Starbase 173
. This planet was connected to
Rigel
by a subspace line. (
TNG
: "
The Measure Of A Man
")
Appendices
Background information
In August 2024, an annotated computer paste-up of the Subspace Comm Net Ops star chart was put up for auction in the Entertainment Memorabiliia Live Auction: Los Angeles Summer 2024 by Propstore Auction.
[1]
Kirk implied that the planet was far from
Canopus III
, by saying "but that's
light years
from here" when confronted with the unlikely sight of a
Canopus dinosaur
on the planet.
Apocrypha
In
The Worlds of the Federation
, Lactra VII was called Sessaline by its natives.
External links
Lactra
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Lactra VII
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

,

.

'
mission

mineral

======================================================================
PAGE: Landing site
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Landing_site
======================================================================

This article or section is incomplete
This page lacks essential detail, and is therefore
in need of attention
.
Information regarding expansion requirements may be found on the article's
talk page
. Feel free to
edit
this page to assist with this expansion.
The landing site on the Akaali homeworld
A
landing site
was a site in a
landing zone
where there were one or more
landing points
for a
spacecraft
or for
individuals
beamed
down from a spacecraft to a celestial body's
surface
. A landing site where a spacecraft had
crashed
was called a
crash site
or, in some rare cases, an
emergency landing site
.
In
2151
, a landing site near the
Terra Nova colony
was chosen for the
landing party
to
investigate
the fate of the
colony
. During the second landing on the planet,
Shuttlepod 1
crashed below the
surface
a few
meters
deep into a
cave
. (
ENT
: "
Terra Nova
")
Also in 2151,
Captain
Jonathan Archer
and
Sub-commander
T'Pol decided a
farm
outside of the
city
on the
Akaali homeworld
as landing site for
Shuttlepod 1
to minimize the risk of
cultural contamination
. (
ENT
: "
Civilization
")
Not long after,
T'Pol
suggested an area near one of the two
poles
on the surface of
Archer's Comet
as landing site because this area was out of direct
sunlight
and the surface
ice
more stable. (
ENT
: "
Breaking the Ice
")
In
3189
, the crash site of the
KSF
Khi'eth
was located by the
USS
Discovery
two hundred
kilometers
inside the
Verubin Nebula
, on
Theta Zeta
. (
DIS
: "
Su'Kal
")
See also
Crash landing
Landing pad

Landris II

Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2369
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Landris II
was the
second
planet
in its
star system
.
In
2369
, there was an
archaeological
excavation headed by
Dr.
Mowray
on this planet.
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
, who was interested in the dig on Landris II, attempted to contact the doctor. However, the attempt failed because
Stellar Cartography
requested a communications blackout while they ran an
experiment
. When Picard attempted to have the excavation schematics from this dig displayed on his
ready room
monitor
, the
USS
Enterprise
-D
computer
informed him that the
library computer
was offline, with
Library Systems
being allocated to Stellar Cartography. (
TNG
: "
Lessons
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Lapeeria
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Lapeeria
======================================================================

Lapeeria
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2381
)
Native species:
Lapeerians
Location:
Laap system
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Lapeeria
was the
inhabited
third
and outermost
planet
in the
Laap system
. This
class M
planet was the
homeworld
of the
Lapeerians
, a technologically advanced
humanoid
civilization.
In
2381
,
Starfleet
conducted a
first contact
mission to Lapeeria. The mission was to be headed by the
USS
Archimedes
, with the
USS
Cerritos
following up by installing
subspace transponders
. However, after the two
starships
arrived in the system, an unpredicted
solar flare
from the
Lapeerian sun
shattered an unstable
planetoid
and created a massive
debris
field that struck the
Archimedes
. The
magnetized ionic plasma
within the debris disabled the
Archimedes
, which became caught in Lapeeria's
gravity well
. The
Cerritos
managed to traverse the debris field and arrest the
Archimedes
with a
tractor beam
before it impacted the surface.
Afterwards, first contact with the Lapeerians proceeded without incident, with
Captain
Carol Freeman
now taking the lead. Three more Federation starships then arrived at the planet to render assistance. (
LD
: "
First First Contact
")
The first schematic of the Laap system in the episode shows Lapeeria and one of the gas giants both in the fourth orbital path. A later schematic shows Lapeeria in the fifth and sixth orbital paths.
External link
Lapeeria
at
StarTrek.com

appearing in the

episode "

".
"

" established that Zan Periculi was already known to the Federation in
2256
, which might mean the planet was known also.

Lashmir
was a location referenced in the
Children of Tama
language
metaphorical
phrases, "
Uzani
at Lashmir.
"
In
2368
, on the
planet
El-Adrel IV
, before engaging the
El-Adrel IV entity
,
Captain
Dathon
uttered this phrase to the
Human
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard. Picard, in an attempt to understand Dathon's words, interpreted this phrase as a description of their situation and how it was similar to what happened at Lashmir. (
TNG
: "
Darmok
")
This location was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to the
script
, Lashmir was pronounced as "lahsh-MEER".

and

Lav'oti V
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2370s
)
Location:
Delta Quadrant
The Doctor
presents the holoimage he captured of Tom Paris after he fell into the mud pits.
Lav'oti V
was the
fifth
planet
in its
star system
, in the
Delta Quadrant
.
The
USS
Voyager
once visited Lav'oti V, during which
Tom Paris
fell into the "fetid mud pits" of
Palomar
.
The Doctor
took a
holoimage
of this incident, which he presented in an exhibition in
2375
. (
VOY
: "
Nothing Human
")
"La'voti V" was the spelling for this planet's name in the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 451.
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 89), the Lav'oti system was located in or near space claimed by the
Devore Imperium
. This system was a
binary star system
with a
G-class star
and a M-class star

======================================================================
PAGE: Ledonia III
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ledonia_III
======================================================================

Ledonia III
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2360s
)
Native species:
A
species
of exotic
plant
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
A plant from Ledonia III
Ledonia III
was the
third
planet
in its
star system
. This planet was home to an exotic
plant
.
Ledonian
soil
contained a benevolent
mycorrhizal fungus
that helped the native plants retain
water
. When the plant life was deprived of this soil, it would quickly die.
Jadzia Dax
owned a potted plant from Ledonia III. After some time, all the fungus in the plant's potting soil had almost completely died out.
Dr.
Bashir
took the remaining fungus to the
infirmary
and cultivated a new batch that could sustain Dax's plant again. (
DS9
: "
The Wire
")
Appendices
Background information
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to the
script
, the pronunciation for Ledonia was "luh-DOE-nee-a".
[1]
The plant was later re-used and seen again in the
Incredible Tales
office on
Roy Ritterhouse
's desk.
According to Startrek.com, the
M-class
planet Ledonia III
orbited
a
star
named Ledonia.
[2]
(X)
[3]
(X)
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 458 located Ledonia III in the Ledonia system.
