Ledonia III
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Ledos
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ledos
======================================================================

Ledos
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2378
)
Native species:
Ledosians
and
Ventu
Location:
Delta Quadrant
Ledos
was an
inhabited
planet
located in the
Delta Quadrant
. It was the
homeworld
of the
Ledosians
, as well as their genetic offshoot, the
Ventu
.
While the Ledosians were a highly advanced
warp-capable
species, the Ventu were a nomadic and simplistic people. Centuries ago, when aliens known only by their
Borg
designation,
Species 312
, encountered their world, they constructed an
energy barrier
separating the still primitive Ventu from the rest of their society.
Ledos had very strict rules governing
starship
pilots
. Pilots deemed reckless could face consequences including a mandatory three day piloting course, or the revocation of their right to fly in Ledosian space.
In
2378
, Ledos was visited by the
USS
Voyager
, where the crew conducted
shore leave
and attended a conference. When a
shuttle
became trapped in the energy barrier,
Seven of Nine
and
Chakotay
were trapped within it. They interacted with the Ventu, who assist in treating their wounds.
Voyager
attempted to bring down the barrier using its
phasers
in order to retrieve their crew, only to find it was already being brought down from the inside by Seven of Nine.
The Ledosions were extremely grateful, and began sending research teams to study the Ventu. Realizing that the
cultural contamination
could destroy the Ventu society,
Voyager
re-initialized the barrier, and promptly left Ledos. (
VOY
: "
Natural Law
")
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 458 classified Ledos as a
class M
planet.
According to the
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 95), the Ledos/Ventu homeworld was located in non-aligned space between the
Hierarchy
and the
R'Kaal Collective
. This system was a
trinary star system
. Primary was a
class F star
with a magnitude of +3, which was ten times brighter than
Sol
. Secondary was a
class M star
. Tertiary was a
class K star
. The
USS
Voyager
visited this planet on
stardate
54868.

PAGE: Lemma II
CATEGORIES: Planets

======================================================================

Lemma II
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Bilana III
Status:
Habitable
(
2368
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Lemma II
was the
inhabited
second
planet
in its
system
. A
colony
with a sister facility to the
Science Institute of Bilana III
was located on this planet.
In
2368
, this planet was the intended destination of an experimental
soliton wave
generated from
Bilana III
, approximately 3.083
light years
away. The Lemma II facility was intended to generate a
particle scattering field
to disperse the wave. However, the wave destabilized and accelerated out of control, threatening to destroy most of Lemma II. Fortunately, the
USS
Enterprise
-D
disrupted the wave before it arrived. (
TNG
: "
New Ground
")
External link
Lemma II
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

was the

.
In

and

into disguising himself as

USS
Voyager
's
warp core
. Suspecting something was amiss,
Commander
Chakotay
tested "Janeway" by inventing a story about how she, as a
lieutenant
, had saved an
away team
on Lessek Prime in
2363
by disobeying her captain's orders. (
VOY
: "
Renaissance Man
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
It is unclear whether the planet itself was also invented by Chakotay or only the specific away team mission. The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 461 sides with the mission being an invention of Chakotay.

======================================================================
PAGE: Levinius V
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Levinius_V
======================================================================

Levinius V
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2328
)
Location:
Alpha Quadrant
Levinius V
was the
inhabited
fifth
planet
of its
star system
. The planet was located in the
Alpha Quadrant
and was in the same section of the
galaxy
as the
Beta Portolan
system
,
Theta Cygni XII
,
Ingraham B
, and
Deneva
.
In the
21st century
, this planet was invaded by
flying parasites
, which had been transported to this planet from the ancient
civilizations
in the Beta Portolan system. By
2067
, the civilization on Levinius V was destroyed in the
mass insanity
caused by the parasites. The inhabitants transported this parasite over to Theta Cygni XII. (
TOS
: "
Operation -- Annihilate!
")
In
2328
, this planet was home for the
Levinius V Transit Complex
. (
TNG-R
: "
Inheritance
")
A
deleted scene
from the
Star Trek: Enterprise
episode "
Fight or Flight
" mentioned a planet called "
Lavinius
".
The location of Levinius V was inferred from the known positions of the
Theta Cygni
and
Deneva
systems
. Levinius V was between Beta Portolan and Theta Cygni on a straight line of invasions by the
flying parasites
which started at Beta Portolan and ended at Deneva
External links
Levinius
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Levinius V
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

was the

tenth

Central Gallery

paintings
created by

, painted upon huge
canvases
.

humanoid

, which

a
star
named Ligobis. The star and its planets were located in the Ligobis system.

======================================================================
PAGE: Ligon II
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ligon_II
======================================================================

Ligon II
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Non-aligned
Status:
Habitable
(
2364
)
Native species:
Ligonians
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Ligon II
was the
inhabited
second
planet
of its
star system
. This
Class M
planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
and was the
homeworld
of the
humanoid
Ligonians
. The planet was not a
Federation member
.
In
2364
, Ligon II was
orbited
by an
orbital control station
and was the source of a rare
vaccine
that was needed on
Federation
planet
Styris IV
. The
USS
Enterprise
-D
visited the planet in an attempt to secure some of the vaccine, and in the process
Lieutenant
Yar
was
kidnapped
.
Ligonian Centerplace
The Ligonian
Centerplace
, an imposing structure overlooking a
city
, was the place where the
leader
of Ligon II,
Lutan
, and later
Hagon
in early 2364, resided. The Centerplace was located on the
northern
hemisphere
of the planet, close to an
ocean
. It was, in essence, a
palace
.
It was characterized by a large
tower
in the center of the structure and a large open courtyard, with a small
fountain
in the center. Lutan's
throne
was located on one end of the courtyard; a large
wall
of
hand-held weapons
, including
crossbows
and
spears
, was located on the other side. Two sentries were often standing guard next to two giant
gongs
, which were located close to the fountain.
The Centerplace was adorned with many different
plants
and the characteristic, hexagonal shape of the Ligonian logo was present everywhere, including on
doors
and the weapon wall.
A fighting range consisting of different levels of steps, lights, and
metal
rods could be installed in the middle of the Centerplace.
Yareena
, Lutan's
First One
, fought
Natasha Yar
on this structure in a fight to the
death
. Several
seats
were arranged around the fighting range for spectators to attend the ceremony. (
TNG
: "
Code of Honor
")
The courtyard of the Centerplace
A map showing the location of the Centerplace
Both the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 465 and
StarTrek.com
classified Ligon II as a
class M
planet.
[1]
(X)
In the latter, it was stated that the planet was known to the Federation since the
2320s
when a Ligonian participated in a
wrestling match
at
Starfleet Academy
.
In discussing the Ligon II
matte painting
,
Syd Dutton
described the planet as "an
African
planet", where "the
buildings
were reminiscent of African buildings". ("The Art of Alien Worlds",
VOY Season 4 DVD
, special feature)
External links
Ligon II
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Re-Used Planets in TNG
at
Ex Astris Scientia

PAGE: Ligos VII
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ligos_VII
======================================================================

Ligos VII
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2369
)
Location:
Ligos
system
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Ligos VII
was the
seventh
planet
in the
Ligos
system
. There were rich deposits of
vendarite
on this planet. By
2369
, there was a
Starfleet
science team
, with a complement of 103 personnel, on the planet.
That
year
, the
USS
Enterprise
-D
received a
distress signal
from the team.
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
speculated that, as the planet had periodic
volcanic
activity, the planet might be entering into an active phase and the team might be threatened by this activity.
It was quickly revealed that the team was briefly captured by
DaiMon
Lurin
, a
Ferengi
privateer
. The team members were made to work as
slave
laborers
, extracting the
mineral
from dangerous
mines
on the planet. When Lurin's men captured the
Enterprise
, its
crew
was forced into slavery as well. However,
Commander
William Riker
and the children of the
Enterprise
(led by a transformed Captain Picard) retook the ship, rescued the crew, and shut down Lurin's operation. (
TNG
: "
Rascals
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Lissepia
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Lissepia
======================================================================

Lissepia
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
24th century
)
Native species:
Lissepian
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Lissepia
was an
inhabited
planet
and
homeworld
to the
Lissepian
species
.
During the
24th century
, the Lissepians were active trade partners with the
Cardassians
. Lissepians were often employed as intermediaries between the Cardassians and their
colonies
in the
Demilitarized Zone
, following its creation in
2370
. (
DS9
: "
The Maquis, Part II
")
In
2365
, while the entire planet of Lissepia was celebrating
Mother's Day
, five
thieves
broke into the
Central Bank of Lissepia
and stole 1,000
bricks
of
gold-pressed latinum
in what became known as the "
Lissepian Mother's Day Heist
".
Morn
was one of a group that stole the latinum, and later deposited the
gold
in the
Bank of Bolias
and stored the latinum in his second
stomach
. After the
statute of limitations
ran out in
2374
, Morn faked his own
death
to throw his partners off of his trail. (
DS9
: "
Who Mourns for Morn?
")
They hosted the
Lissepian Lottery
. (
DS9
: "
Who Mourns for Morn?
")
In
2366
, the
Cardassian ship
Ravinok
was scheduled to
rendezvous
with another
freighter
, which would take
Tora Naprem
and
Tora Ziyal
to Lissepia to live out their lives in peace. The Cardassian
transport
, however, was attacked by two
Breen warships
and forced to
crash land
on
Dozaria
, where the
survivors
remained until
2372
. (
DS9
: "
Indiscretion
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
External link
Lissepia
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

Little Risa
"
Little Risa
" was the name for the
Risian
district in the
city
on
Tulgana IV
where
Risians
tended to congregate.
It was located
south
of
Andoria Town
and contained a
fountain
.
In
2380
,
Ensign
Brad Boimler
encountered an
Anabaj
female
while he was in this
district
. (
LD
: "
Envoys
")
Later that year on the
USS
Titan
, while discussing their upcoming
mission
to the
Tulgana system
,
Captain
William T. Riker
told
Commander
Deanna Troi
that there was a Little Risa on Tulgana IV. (
LD
: "
No Small Parts
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Locarno
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Locarno
======================================================================

Locarno
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2381
)
Satellite(s):
None
Location:
Detrion system
Alpha Quadrant
"
...I guess
he
did some good after all.
"
–
Carol Freeman
,
2381
("
Old Friends, New Planets
")
Locarno
was a
class M
planet
created in the
Detrion system
in
2381
as a result of the detonation of a
Ferengi
-made
Genesis Device
on the
USS
Passaro
; it was formed from the
matter
of the
Passaro
, along with the nearby
Nova One
and the level-7
ion storm
that surrounded them. The planet was so named by
Starfleet
because it incorporated the
atoms
of
Nick Locarno
, the sole individual aboard the
Passaro
at the time of the explosion. The planet did not possess
moons
.
The
orbit
of Locarno had an
eccentricity
of 0.00647185, an
inclination
of 0.00018 degrees, and an
equatorial inclination
of 3.27 degrees. It had a
rotation period
of 0.5456
solar days
and a sidereal
revolution period
of 0.873
solar years
. The planet's
radius
was 3,771
kilometers
and its
equatorial
circumference
was 23,698 kilometers. Its
mass
was 1.06386
e24
×10
24
kilograms
, with a mean
density
of 4.736
grams
per
cubic centimeter
. It had a surface
gravity
of 4.99 meters per second per second, and an
escape velocity
of 6.13 kilometers per second. The mean
temperature
on the surface was 268
kelvin
.
The Genesis Device detonation that created the planet was triggered by
Beckett Mariner
to deny Locarno its use as leverage, having been prevented from reaching
Detrion 9
, a lifeless planet. Locarno had refused rescue in the arrogant belief that he could deactivate the device, only for a
paywall
to thwart him at the last second. With Locarno dead and his
Nova Fleet
dispersed, Starfleet began monitoring the planet's long-term stability, hoping to eventually use it as a home for
refugees
. (
LD
: "
Old Friends, New Planets
")
Map of the Detrion system
Based on the location of the ion storm as shown on the diagram of the Detrion system in the episode, Locarno should be located in the outer reaches of the system, between the orbits of the original sixth and eighth planets.

======================================================================
PAGE: Location 5499, section 16
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Location_5499,_section_16
======================================================================

Location 5499, section 16
was a location fifty thousand
light years
from the
Sol system
in the
Beta Quadrant
.
In
3188
,
Michael Burnham
ordered the
red angel suit
to set
temporal coordinates
for this location, with a
course
heading of 214 mark 5. There, upon the closing of the
spatial rift
, the suit would transmit the final signal and initiate the
self-destruct sequence
. This final signal was detected by
Starfleet
in
2258
. (
DIS
: "
Such Sweet Sorrow, Part 2
", "
That Hope Is You, Part 1
")

.

======================================================================
PAGE: Loracus Prime
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Loracus_Prime
======================================================================

Loracus Prime
Type:
Planet
Native species:
Loracus devil mite
Spitting termite
Location:
Loracus system
Gagarin Radiation Belt
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Loracus Prime
was the
prime
planet
in the
Loracus
system
, located in the
Gagarin Radiation Belt
.
The planet's
atmosphere
contained purple and pink clouds of
gas
and it was known to have over five thousand
subspecies
of
termites
. Among those subspecies were the
Loracus devil mite
and the
spitting termite
. The planet was visited by
A.E. Hodgkin
prior to
2151
and was crucial for the development of
Hodgkin's Law of Parallel Planetary Development
. (
ENT
: "
Strange New World
")
Background information
The planet was presumably located less than ninety
light years
from
Earth
, as Hodgkin would have visited it before
Enterprise
visited
Risa
, which at ninety light years from Earth was the furthest Humans had traveled at the time.
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 60) falsely states the planet explored in "Strange New World" to be Loracus Prime, which actually is a misinterpretation. More so, the planet was later identified as
Archer IV
.
Cutler
's prop book about Loracus Prime insects is the
Handbook of Exobiology
. When the episode aired, many viewers thought that Cutler said "
Arrakis
Prime", in reference to the
Dune Chronicles
, but this was subsequently disproved.
According to
StarTrek.com
, this planet was located in the
Alpha Quadrant
.
[1]

======================================================================
PAGE: Loren III
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Loren_III
======================================================================

Loren III
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2369
)
Location:
Loren
system
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Loren III
was the
uninhabited
third
planet
in the
Loren
system
. This was the only planet capable of supporting
life
near the
Kurlan system
. Loren III was located outside
Federation
territory
.
Loren III was one of the planets seeded with part of a
genetic
program
by the
Progenitors
4.5 billion years ago
. In
2369
,
Professor
Galen
collected a
DNA
sample from Loren III as part of his quest to piece together the program, though it was taken by the
Yridians
. The
USS
Enterprise
-D
returned to the planet to collect another sample, where it encountered two
Cardassian
cruisers
and the
IKS
Maht-H'a
. (
TNG
: "
The Chase
")
When Loren III is first mentioned, Picard quite clearly says that it is
in
the Kurlan system, despite the
script
using the word
near
instead. However, once the ship arrives at the planet it is specifically described as the third planet in the Loren system.

Loresworld
Type:
Planet
Status:

(

)

Loresworld
was a
planet
where, in
2369
,
Lore
and his rogue
Borg
faction
planned their conquest of the
galaxy
by
synthetic lifeforms
. Their
base
was located in a
hilly
region
of the planet.
The planet had an unusual level of EM activity in the
magnetosphere
, which interfered with
sensors
, and had at least
two
moons
. (
TNG
: "
Descent
", "
Descent, Part II
")
The planet was not named in the episode; rather, the name came from
StarTrek.com
, which also described it as an
M-class
planet.

PAGE: Loval
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Loval

Loval
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Cardassian Union
(
24th century
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Location:
Loval
system
Alpha Quadrant
Loval
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Loval
system
,
Alpha Quadrant
. The planet
Rakal
and the
Amleth system
were located in the same
sector
as this world.
History
In the
24th century
, this planet was in
Cardassian
space
.
This planet was home to a
civilian
outpost
with a small
subspace
relay station
.
Less commonly known, Loval was home to a secret weapons research installation operated by the
Cardassian military
. This facility was targeted by a
Klingon
Bird-of-Prey
commanded by
K'Temang
in
2372
.
Gul
Dukat
, aboard the
freighter
Groumall
, anticipated K'Temang's intentions and set a
trap
for him in Loval's
orbit
. (
DS9
: "
Return to Grace
")
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of the Cardassian Union as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
"low-VAL" was the pronunciation for this planet's
name
from the
script
pronunciation guide.
[1]
External link
Loval
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

language
metaphorical

to the

PAGE: Lunar Receiving

URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Lunar_Receiving
======================================================================

Lunar Receiving
Type:
Space station
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Status:
Active
(
2370
)
Lunar Receiving
was a
space station
.
In
2370
, Lunar Receiving was listed on the
Deep Space 9
arrival roster
as the point of departure for the
C-57-D
. (
DS9
: "
Whispers
",
production art
)
This station was only mentioned in writing.
In the era of the
Apollo program
,
Lunar Receiving Laboratory
was a facility located within
NASA
's
Lyndon B. Johnson
Space Center in
Texas
. Upon returning from Luna, the astronauts and the samples they gathered from this
natural satellite
of
Earth
were quarantined for a set amount of time to prevent contamination.
See also
Luna
Lunar Colonies
Lunar V

======================================================================
PAGE: Lungha
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Lungha
======================================================================

Lungha
was a location referenced in
Children of Tama
language
metaphorical
phrases. The location, which included
Ubaya
and was described as having a grey
sky
, was associated with a meeting between the individuals
Rai
and
Jiri
.
It was mentioned in a greeting by
Captain
Dathon
to the
Federation
starship
USS
Enterprise
-D
in
2368
. (
TNG
: "
Darmok
")
This location was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to the
script
, Lungha was pronounced as "LOON-guh".

Luria
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2374
)
Native species:
Lurians
Location:
Near the
Ionite Nebula
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Luria
was an
inhabited
planet
near the
Ionite Nebula
and
homeworld
of the
Lurian
race
. Its
government
was controlled by the
Royal Family of Luria
. (
DS9
: "
The Way of the Warrior
", "
Who Mourns for Morn?
")
Appendices
Background information
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
Apocrypha
The
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine - Core Game Book
claimed that Luria was the
fourth
planet in its system and was located in the Ionite Nebula.
Decipher
's
Aliens
sourcebook described Luria as the
sixth
planet in the Hainault system.
External link
Luria
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Lutani VII
CATEGORIES: Planets

======================================================================

Lutani VII
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Lutani
Status:
Inhabited
(
2261
)
Native species:
Lutani
Location:
Lutani system
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Lutani VII
was the
inhabited
seventh
planet
of the
Lutani system
. It was home to the
Lutani
, a
spacefaring
humanoid
civilization
, and was considered the
sister planet
of
Kasar
.
On
stardate
2177.9, Lutani VII came under attack by Kasar, whose inhabitants aimed to seize the entire
star system
and its resources. In
2261
, the
USS
Enterprise
was assigned to transport a
Jikaru
from
Tychus-B
to Lutani VII, ostensibly to help in their rebuilding efforts. (
SNW
: "
What Is Starfleet?
")
Lutani VII was called Rukari VII in the script.
[1]

======================================================================
PAGE: Lya III
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Lya_III
======================================================================

Lya III
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Habitable
(
2366
)
Location:
Sector 30/489
Beta Quadrant
Lya III
was the
inhabited
third
planet
in its
star system
. This system, in
Sector 30/489
, was located near the
Giles Belt
and the
Nel Bato system
. This planet was home to a
Federation
starbase
administered by
Starfleet
.
Subspace
messages sent from this starbase to
ships
positioned near the
outpost
Sierra VI
, which was located on the Federation side of the
Federation-Romulan border
, had a delay time of two
hours
and twenty-two
minutes
.
In
2366
,
Vice Admiral
Haden
was assigned to this starbase. During the
Nelvana Incident
, he was the
representative
of
Starfleet Command
and sent messages to
Captain
Picard
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. (
TNG
: "
The Defector
")
Later that
year
, after completing its
mission
at
Angosia III
, Lya III was the destination of the
Enterprise
-D. (
TNG
: "
The Hunted
")
In
2384
, the Starfleet base on this planet was the closest base to the
USS
Voyager
-A
. The base was capable of providing medical assistance and engineering resources to the starship. (
PRO
: "
Into the Breach, Part I
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred from it being in Sector 30, which contained a section of the Federation-Romulan border. This border was canonically located in the Beta Quadrant in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
The Impossible Box
".
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 47) and
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Federation Historical Highlights, 2161-2385"), the Lya system was a
binary
system with a
pair
of
A-class stars
, located in the
Alpha Quadrant
.
See also
Lya IV
Lya Station Alpha

======================================================================
PAGE: Lya IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Lya_IV
======================================================================

Lya IV
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Habitable
(
2366
)
Location:
Sector 30/489
Beta Quadrant
Lya IV
was the
inhabited
fourth
planet
in its
star system
. This planet, in
Sector 30/489
, was located near the
Giles Belt
and the
Nel Bato
system
.
In the mid-
2360s
, a
Federation
outpost
was located on this planet.
In
2366
, the
freighter
Jovis
, commanded by
Kivas Fajo
, spent more than half a
day
in
orbit
of this planet. While at this planet, Fajo entertained his
friend
Palor Toff
. The outpost would later send a message to the
USS
Enterprise
that Kivas Fajo's ship had been seen at the planet. (
TNG
: "
The Most Toys
")
See also
Lya III
Lya Station Alpha
Appendices
Background information
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred from it being in Sector 30, which contained a section of the
Federation-Romulan border
. This border was canonically located in the Beta Quadrant in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
The Impossible Box
".
This planet was located in a
subsector
with the reference number of 321-053.
In the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 324, the outpost at Lya IV, which was referred to once in the episode as a station, was identified as a
space station
.
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 47) and
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Federation Historical Highlights, 2161-2385"), the Lya system was a
binary
system with a
pair
of
A-class stars
; according to these sources, it was located in the
Alpha Quadrant
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Lya Station Alpha
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Lya_Station_Alpha
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Lya Station Alpha
Type:
Spacedock
-type
Owner:
United Federation of Planets
Operator:
Starfleet
Location:
Alpha Quadrant
Status:
Active
(
3196
)
Lya Station Alpha
was a
Federation
Spacedock
-type
space station
orbiting
a
planet
in the
Alpha Quadrant
, near
Merak
,
Kressari
,
Tagra
, and
Capella
. It had been in service with
Starfleet
since at least the mid-
23rd century
. (
SNW
: "
Strange New Worlds
")
History
In
2368
, the
USS
Enterprise
-D
traveled to Lya Station Alpha with
survivors
from the
attack
on the
Solarion IV
colony
.
Ensign
Ro Laren
came aboard the
Enterprise
at this station, and
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
met with
Admiral
Kennelly
there regarding suspected
Bajoran
terrorist
activity. (
TNG
: "
Ensign Ro
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this station was within the enclosed region. (
SA
: "
Rubincon
")
Stellar cartography
Named on a Federation star chart (2259)
In
2259
, the location of this space station was labeled on a
stellar cartography
chart that was seen on the
USS
Enterprise
's
ready room
viewscreen
. (
SNW
: "
Strange New Worlds
", "
Children of the Comet
", "
Spock Amok
")
On
stardate
1292.4, the location of this station was labeled in a chart used during a
Section 31
Alpha Team
briefing
. (
Star Trek: Section 31
)
The station's location was labeled in a
Federation star chart
that was in
Fleet Admiral
Kirsten Clancy
's
office
at
Starfleet Headquarters
in
2399
and on the
bridge
of the
USS
Titan
-A
in
2401
. Lya Station Alpha was in or near to
Federation space
. (
PIC
: "
Maps and Legends
", "
The Next Generation
", "
Disengage
")
In
3196
, the location of Lya Station Alpha was depicted on star charts and readouts used across several locations on the
USS
Athena
to locate, monitor, and respond to the Omega-47 mines laid by the Venari Ral. (
SA
: "
300th Night
", "
Rubincon
")
The exterior of Lya Station Alpha was a reuse of footage of
Starbase 74
from "
11001001
", which in turn was reuse of footage of the
Spacedock One
from
Star Trek III: The Search for Spock
.
Lya Station Alpha was identified by the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 491 as a Federation
starbase
.
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 47) and the
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Federation Historical Highlights, 2161-2385"), Lya Station Alpha was located in the
Lya
system. This system was not the same as the one found in
Sector 30
, Beta Quadrant, which had a starbase on
Lya III
and an
outpost
on
Lya IV
. However, the star charts in "
Rubincon
" show Lya Station Alpha near to, but still several systems away from, the Lya system.
External link
Lya Station Alpha
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Lyrek
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Lyrek
======================================================================

Lyrek
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
3191
)
Satellite(s):
Three
moons
Location:
Vileen system
Beta Quadrant
Lyrek
was an
uninhabited
class M
planet
in the
Vileen system
, located in an outer
sector
of the
Beta Quadrant
. It had three
moons
, two of which moved in perfect synchrony. Once every seven
years
, these "twin moons" cast a double
eclipse
on a particular area of the surface. (
DIS
: "
Red Directive
", "
Under the Twin Moons
")
Astronomical data
Planetary data for Lyrek
Lyrek was located at
coordinates
64+1234432.8134252+14.2. It
orbited
at a distance of 1.853
AU
, with a
period
of 949
solar days
and a
Keplerian ratio
of 1.013. Its
rotation period
was 64.1
hours
long. The planet had a
radius
of 7025
kilometers
and an estimated
mass
of 7.483
e24
×10
24
kilograms
, giving it a surface
gravity
of 1.04
g
. Its age was estimated to be 4.958 billion
solar years
.
The
atmosphere
of Lyrek was composed of 78% molecular
nitrogen
, 20% molecular
oxygen
, 0.3%
carbon dioxide
, 1.1%
argon
, and 0.4%
water vapor
, with trace amounts of
methane
. The surface had an atmosphere pressure of 1.469
bars
and a mean
temperature
of 296
K
, and was lushly
forested
. (
DIS
: "
Under the Twin Moons
")
History
Prior to their
extinction
in the
14th century
, the
Promellians
interred their dead within a large
necropolis
complex on Lyrek, centered on the location where the eclipse shadows fell. The site was defended from
grave robbers
by automated
drones
, powered by a strong natural
electromagnetic field
that enveloped the area and precluded
transporter
usage.
In the late
24th century
,
Doctor
Vellek
concealed the first piece of a
map
to the location of
Progenitors
'
technology
under the plinth of a broken
statue
in the Lyrek necropolis, along with an
inscribed
clue to the next piece in the form of a
Romulan
revlav
. At the time, the planet had not been explored for well over a century. Vellek included a sketch of Lyrek and its twin moons in
his
journal
.
After taking Vellek's journal in
3191
,
Moll
and
L'ak
traveled to Lyrek and found part of Vellek's clue. Believing this to be all there was, they departed for
Betazed
, which was in fact a
red herring
.
Captains
Michael Burnham
and
Saru
subsequently investigated the site and, despite coming under attack by Promellian defenses, uncovered the rest of Vellek's clue along with the map piece. Afterwards, Burnham ordered
DOT-23s
deployed to repair the damage to the necropolis
monuments
. (
DIS
: "
Under the Twin Moons
")
Once they realized their mistake, Moll and L'ak returned to Lyrek, which was detected by
sensors
Discovery
had left behind. (
DIS
: "
Jinaal
")

CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Lyshan
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Lyshan
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
(
3195
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3195
)
Location:
Lyshan
system
Alpha Quadrant
Lyshan
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Lyshan
system
.
In the
32nd century
, Lyshan was a
Federation
member
planet. It was listed with other members on a video wall in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
Kids These Days
")
This planet was only mentioned in writing.
The planet's location was inferred from the position of its system in the
Star Trek: The Next Generation
episode "
Chain Of Command, Part II
".
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I"), Lyshan was listed as a Federation member in
2378
.

PAGE: Lysia

Lysia
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Lysian Alliance
Status:
Habitable
(
2368
)
Native species:
Lysians
Location:
Lysian
system
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Lysia
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Lysian
system
. The planet was the
homeworld
of the
Lysians
and the
capital planet
of their
Lysian Alliance
.
According to a falsified
intelligence report
in
2368
, over a two
month
period, the
crews
of fourteen
Federation ships
were captured and
imprisoned
on Lysia. (
TNG
: "
Conundrum
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.

Lysia
at

, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Lysian Central Command
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Lysian_Central_Command
======================================================================

Lysian Central Command
Type:
Space station
Owner:
Lysian Alliance
Operator:
Lysian
military
Location:
Lysian system
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Crew:
15,311
Armament:
4
laser cannons
,  39
cobalt fusion warheads
with
magnetic
propulsion
Defenses:
Deflector shields
(maximum output 4.3
kilojoules
)
The
Lysian Central Command
was a large
space station
in the
Lysian system
that served as the headquarters for the
Lysian Alliance
military
.
In
2368
, a
Satarran
operative was able to suppress the memories of the crew of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
and falsify
computer
records in order to convince them that the
Federation
was at war with the Lysians, and their ship had been ordered to destroy their base in order to end the conflict.
Upon arriving at the station, however, the crew quickly realized that, despite its massive size, its defenses were no match for a
Galaxy
-class
starship such as the
Enterprise
;
William T. Riker
commented that a single
photon torpedo
would completely obliterate the base.
Fortunately, the
Enterprise
crew realized how under-powered the Lysian Central Command was, and, stating that he would not fire on defenseless people,
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
ordered the ship to stand down before opening fire. The Satarran operative,
masquerading
as
First Officer
"
Kieran MacDuff
," attempted to overpower the ship's bridge crew and trigger the ship's weapons, but he was fortunately subdued before he could do so. (
TNG
: "
Conundrum
")
The
Lysian Central Command model
was a reuse of the
Edo God
model from
TNG
: "
Justice
".

======================================================================
PAGE: M'Kroon (planet)

M'Kroon
Type:

Native species:

Location:

or

M'Kroon
was an
inhabited

of the
M'Kroon

.
The ancient M'Kroon performed
ritual sacrifices
on M'Kroon in order to stand before the "
deities
of
time
". (
SNW
: "
Through the Lens of Time
")

======================================================================
PAGE: M'kemas III
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/M'kemas_III
======================================================================

M'kemas III
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Tzenkethi
Status:
Habitable
(
2371
)
Location:
Alpha Quadrant
M'kemas III
was the
inhabited
third
planet
in its
star system
. It was located in
Tzenkethi
space
in the
Alpha Quadrant
.
In
2371
, a
Changeling
disguised as
Ambassador
Krajensky
commandeered the
USS
Defiant
and attempted to attack the Tzenkethi
settlement
on M'kemas III to incite war between the Tzenkethi and the
Federation
. He was stopped by the
Defiant
crew
before they reached M'kemas III. (
DS9
: "
The Adversary
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
This planet's quadrant of origin is inferred based on the position of the
Tzenketh system
as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
According to the
script
, the pronunciation for M'Kemas was "muh KEM-ass".
[1]
External link
M'kemas III
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: M'talas IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/M'talas_IV
======================================================================

M'talas IV
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2401
)
Location:
M'talas system
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
M'talas IV
was the
inhabited
fourth
planet
in the
M'talas system
. It was described by
Jack Crusher
in
2401
as a "
a vile place, a real dump.
"
Beverly
and Jack Crusher once made a
medical supply
run to M'talas IV. During this trip, Jack was stuck in a
cargo hold
with an
Andorian
who had a
broken
antenna
that he attempted to
reset
. (
PIC
: "
No Win Scenario
")

======================================================================
PAGE: M'talas Prime
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/M'talas_Prime
======================================================================

M'talas Prime
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2401
)
Satellite(s):
At least
two moons
Location:
M'talas system
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
M'talas Prime
, or simply
M'talas
, also sometimes spelled as
Matalas
, was the
inhabited
prime
planet
of the
M'talas system
. It was noted for its
ringed
moons
. The populated areas of the planet were divided into
districts
, including
District Six
and
District Seven
. (
ENT
: "
Dawn
";
PIC
: "
The Next Generation
")
History
Doctor
Phlox
was once nearly overwhelmed by
fifty patients
in a
refugee camp
on M'talas. In a correspondence with Doctor
Jeremy Lucas
in
2151
, Phlox recalled this incident in relation to his being asked to take responsibility for the health of over fifty million
Valakians
. (
ENT
: "
Dear Doctor
")
The
Enterprise
NX-01
visited M'talas Prime during its historic mission of exploration in the mid-
22nd century
. In
2152
, while stranded on an uninhabited and rapidly heating
moon
with an
Arkonian
named
Zho'Kaan
,
Commander
Charles Tucker III
reminisced about some of the experiences he had while on
Enterprise
, including seeing the
ringed moons
of M'talas Prime. (
ENT
: "
Dawn
")
La Sirena
in District Six
In
2401
,
Commander
Raffi Musiker
was assigned to an undercover mission in District Six on M'talas Prime as part of
Operation: Daybreak
. She uncovered a plot to destroy the
Starfleet
recruitment center in District Seven using
experimental weapons
stolen from
Daystrom Station
, but was unable to warn the authorities in time to prevent the attack. Subsequent investigation revealed that the attack was masterminded by renegade
Changelings
as a diversion from their true plans against the
Federation
. (
PIC
: "
The Next Generation
", "
Imposters
")
Inhabitants
M'talas Prime inhabitants
Appendices
Appearances
PIC
:
"
The Next Generation
"
(
Season 3
)
"
Disengage
"
"
Seventeen Seconds
"
"
Imposters
"
Background information
The name of this planet was a "tip-of-the-hat" to executive producer
Brannon Braga
's assistant and
Star Trek: Enterprise
writer,
Terry Matalas
. (
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 26) The same Matalas went on to become showrunner on
Star Trek: Picard
.
Matalas stated on Twitter that the planet seen in "
The Next Generation
" was intended to be the same planet referenced in
Star Trek: Enterprise
.
[1]
He also noted that it was far outside Federation jurisdiction and still used
currency
.
[2]
In an interview with Trekmovie.com, Matalas added, "
I think it was Chris Black who came up with it on
Enterprise
. The idea was to name the most horrible place in the galaxy after me. The most vile place in the galaxy was of course named "Matalas". So on
Picard
when we were trying to name a place for sort of the epicenter of organized crime in the galaxy, somebody said, “Shouldn’t it be named after that vile place named for you?
"
[3]
On his Twitter, production designer
Dave Blass
shared behind the scenes photographs of M'Talas Prime's shops and streets. Art Direction was by
Kit Stølen
and set decoration by
Shauna Aronson
.
[4]

======================================================================
PAGE: M-11
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/M-11
======================================================================

M-11
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Habitable
(
2399
)
Location:
Federation space
Alpha Quadrant
~2
light years
from
Talos IV
M-11
was an
inhabited
world
in the
Alpha Quadrant
. This world was located in
Federation space
and was the home of
Starbase 11
from the
23rd
to the early
25th century
. M-11 was located near
O'Ryan's Planet
,
Benecia
, and
Beta Niobe
. It was six
days
' travel at
maximum warp
from the
Talos star group
, approximately 2
light years
away.
The busy orbit of M-11
The world was
illuminated
by at least one
sun
, and located very close to several other bodies, including a large world with an imposing
ring system
which dominated its
sky
. It had a
breathable
atmosphere
, but the surroundings of the
starbase
appeared bleak and lifeless.
The
USS
Enterprise
visited M-11 on two occasions in
2267
, once on
stardate
2947.3 and again on stardate 3012.4. (
TOS
: "
Court Martial
", "
The Menagerie, Part I
";
SNW
: "
Strange New Worlds
")
Appendices
Background information
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of Starbase 11 as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
The original planetscape
Despite appearing in two episodes, this planet was not directly named on-screen. The planet was identified as "M-11" in the
script
for "The Menagerie", where originally the starbase was called "Starbase M-11". The
name
was evidently carried over from "Court Martial", which features an establishment there named the
M-11 Starbase Club
, which does display the name "M-11" on-screen. However, that episode's first draft story outline (dated
2 May
1966
) and a revised draft story outline (dated
26 June
1966
) referred to the planet as "811" (while the base was named "Star Base 811" and the episode itself had the
working title
"Court Martial at Star Base 811"). The first draft of the story outline also depicted the planet as being protected by armed
patrol ships
.
In a six-page memo of script notes that
Gene Roddenberry
sent
Gene L. Coon
(on
15 August
1966
), Roddenberry proposed depicting this planet as a background in a
matte painting
which he imagined appearing in "Court Martial" (meanwhile still titled "Court Martial at Star Base 811"). He also used the document, which concerned the first draft script of the episode, to suggest using a planet set on
Desilu Stage 10
to feature as views of the planet
surface
seen from
windows
in the starbase. Roddenberry, in yet another note from the memo, also commented on the use of orbital shots of the planet, stating they were to be stock shots. He added, "
We cannot handle the night and day routine unless it became absolutely
vital
to a script.
"
Judging by on-screen evidence only, it seemed quite plausible that the starbase was on a
moon
of a larger, ringed planet, visible in the sky in "The Menagerie". However, the scripts of "Court Martial" and "The Menagerie" described this world itself as a planet. In the revised final draft script of "Court Martial", a "special note" at the start of the script stated, "
All Planet Locations – though not presently indicated in body of script – are
NIGHT
.
" Furthermore, the script of "The Menagerie" characterized M-11 as a "dead world" with weird and desolate
purple
landscape. Another possibility to explain the large world seen in the sky of M-11 was that both worlds were components of a
binary planets
pairing. An example of this would be
T'Khut
and
Ni'Var
in the
Vulcan system
.
The surface of the planet was depicted with a matte painting, which was created by Matte Artist
Albert Whitlock
. He painted the image with oils on Masonite. (
Star Trek: The Original Series 365
, p. 089)
Apocrypha
This world was named Yko in
Decipher
's
Star Trek Roleplaying Game
supplement
Starfleet Operations Manual
.
External link
Yko
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: M-113
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/M-113
======================================================================

M-113
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2328
)
Native species:
M-113 creature
Borgia plant
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
For the planet containing Starbase 11, please see
M-11
.
M-113
was an
inhabited
M-class
planet
that was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
. It was the
homeworld
of a type of
poisonous
plant
known as the
borgia plant
, as well as the home of a
civilization
of
M-113 creatures
, which went nearly
extinct
long before
2266
.
The planet was hotter and more arid than
Earth
, so
Human
inhabitants needed to ingest larger amounts of
salt
to adjust for the loss of the
mineral
due to
sweating
. M-113 was once rich in salt, but the planet's supply of the substance became depleted in antiquity, which was the cause of the eventual near extinction of its native
species
of creatures, and by 2266, no salt could be found on the planet, so it had to be imported. Apart from the aforementioned borgia plant, M-113's landscape also featured dry brushes.
A winged beast
Several
stone
sculptures
depicting
various animals
were found on the
surface
of the planet among vast ruins, including sculptures of a winged
creature
holding a
sword
, a small stone sculpture resembling a
dolphin
as depicted in Earth's
Renaissance
, a four-legged
deer
-like creature seated like a traditional
Egyptian
sphinx
statue
, and a sculpture of a
lion
-like
animal
.
In
2261
,
archaeologist
Robert Crater
and his wife
Nancy
began an expedition on M-113 to conduct a survey of the planet's many ruins. They moved into a large, deserted ruin on the planet surface and began their archaeological research. During their first four
years
there, they shipped out large amounts of
artifacts
, gathered from several digging sites on the planet, made numerous reports, and were visited by a number of ships. A large drop in shipments was noted in their fifth year, however.
Unknown at the time of the Crater expedition, a single surviving M-113 creature continued to live on the planet. It eventually killed Nancy and continued to live along with Robert until
2266
. Crater buried his wife on a
hill
on the planet, but was no longer as efficient in his archaeological work, causing the drop in shipments. (
TOS
: "
The Man Trap
")
In
2328
, this world was home to the
M-113 Archaeological Center
and
M-113 Tourist Center
. (
TNG-R
: "
Inheritance
")
Appendices
Background information
By broadcast order, M-113 was the first planet ever seen in
Star Trek
.
At least two
humanoid
skulls
were among the artifacts in Crater's collection.
M-113 has several connections to religious practices. In the final draft
script
of "The Man Trap", the planet was referred to as having once featured many "
temples
" used by the M-113 creatures, one of which was a large
gray
-colored
building
which, in the final version of the episode, is visible on the planet's surface. Since the
name
"M-113" (pronounced "M-one hundred thirteen") did not fit
Star Trek
's planet-naming conventions (and was originally suggested by
Gene Roddenberry
precisely because it was an unusual name),
Kellam de Forest
at one point suggested calling the planet "Mithras 113", referencing
Mithraism
.
The
novelization
of the episode, in
Star Trek 1
, called this planet Regulus VIII, suggesting that it was in the
Regulan system
.
For the pre-remastered version of "The Man Trap", the brown/red planet model for M-113 was first used for
Delta Vega
in "
Where No Man Has Gone Before
". This planet model would be used for
Alfa 177
("
The Enemy Within
"),
Tantalus V
("
Dagger of the Mind
"),
Planet Q
("
The Conscience of the King
"),
Benecia
("
The Conscience of the King
"),
Gothos
("
The Squire of Gothos
"),
Lazarus' planet
("
The Alternative Factor
"),
Beta III
("
The Return of the Archons
"),
Janus VI
("
The Devil in the Dark
"),
Organia
("
Errand of Mercy
"),
Mudd
("
I, Mudd
"),
Argus X
("
Obsession
"), and
Ardana
("
The Cloud Minders
"). For the
remastered
version of "The Man Trap", a new planet model was created by
CGI
artist
Max Gabl
.
Apocrypha
In the
Star Trek Maps
(p. 31) and
The Worlds of the Federation
(p. 118), M-113 was the
fourth
of seven planets that orbited a
yellow dwarf
star
, UFC-113 (
β
). The M-113 creatures were in fact the ancient, advanced civilization that had built the vast
cities
that were now in ruins. In
The Worlds of the Federation
, the indigenous name of the planet was Fotialla.
FASA
's
The Federation
RPG
sourcebook described M-113 as the
second planet
in the Gamma Zeta IV system, while
Decipher
's
Worlds
sourcebook described M-113 as the
third
planet in the Alpha Kezar system.
External links
M-113
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
UFC 113
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Planets in TOS and TOS Remastered
at
Ex Astris Scientia

PAGE: Maarek III

Maarek III
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable; possibly a fictious location
(
22nd century
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Maarek III
was a
planet
mentioned by
Jonathan Archer
in
2153
in a deceitful ploy.
He told
Degra
that a fellow inmate in a
Xindi-Insectoid
prison
had used it as a staging area while
smuggling
dilithium
. It was described as "
beautiful, with
beaches
that [went] on for thousands of
kilometers
.
" (
ENT
: "
Stratagem
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
Given the nature of the mention, it is unclear if there really is a Maarek III. The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 7 described Maarek III as an "imaginary planet".
According to
StarTrek.com
, if the planet was not part of Archer's Degra ruse, Maarek III was a
M-class
planet possibly located in the
Delphic Expanse
.
[1]
(X)

Mab-Bu VI
Class:
Gas giant

Planet

Intact
(

)

, nominally
class M
Location:
Mab-Bu
system
Sector 25712
Alpha Quadrant
Mab-Bu VI
was the
sixth
planet
in the
Mab-Bu
system
,
Alpha Quadrant
. Located in the same
sector
as
Starbase 12
, this
gas giant
planet had a
M-class
moon
. (
TNG
: "

")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet was within the blast radius of one of the mines. (

: "
300th Night
", "
Rubincon
")

, 3rd ed., p. 281, Mab-Bu VI was located in

.

======================================================================
PAGE: Magus III
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Magus_III
======================================================================

Magus III
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2380
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Guinan with a weapon from Magus III
For the similarly named planet, please see
Makus III
.
Magus III
was the
inhabited
third
planet
in its
star system
. Located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
, this world had been visited by
Guinan
by
2367
.
In that
year
, when
REM sleep
-deprived
crewmembers
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
began to draft
conspiracy theories
about their inability to escape the
Tyken's Rift
in
Ten Forward
, Guinan was able to defuse the situation by firing an
energy-beam
rifle
at the
ceiling
. She said she had acquired the
weapon
on Magus III. (
TNG
: "
Night Terrors
")
At some point in or prior to
2380
,
Beckett Mariner
acquired a
scar
on Magus III in what she described as a "high-concept
fight
". (
LD
: "
Temporal Edict
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Majalis
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Majalis
======================================================================

Majalis
Class:
N
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Independent
Status:
Habitable
(
2261
)
Satellite(s):
At least one
moon
Native species:
Majalan
Location:
Majalan system
Alpha Quadrant
Majalis
was an
inhabited
class N
planet
in the
Majalan system
, at the edge of
Federation space
. It was
orbited
by at least one
moon
. This was the
homeworld
of the
Majalans
, a
technologically
advanced
humanoid
species
. (
SNW
: "
Lift Us Where Suffering Cannot Reach
")
Astronomical data
Planetary data for Majalis
Majalis was located at
coordinates
2614.8337 in the
Alpha Quadrant
. Its position was indicated on
23rd century
Federation
star charts
. (
SNW
: "
Lift Us Where Suffering Cannot Reach
", "
Subspace Rhapsody
")
The planet had a
rotation period
of 31.9
hours
and a
revolution period
of 226.7
solar days
. Its
mass
was estimated at 4.8694
e24
×10
24
kilograms
and its age at 4.522 billion
years
. Its
atmosphere
was composed of 81.1%
carbon dioxide
, 12.9% molecular
nitrogen
, 5.9% molecular
oxygen
, and 0.9%
argon
, with trace amounts of
methane
and
water vapor
.
The surface of Majalis was virtually uninhabitable to
humanoids
, characterized by
lava
flows and
lakes
of
acid
. The mean
surface temperature
was 18.1
degrees
Celsius
. The planet exhibited a stratified
habitable zone
, with 13% water-based indigenous
carbon-based
plant
and
animal
life present only in the atmosphere. (
SNW
: "
Lift Us Where Suffering Cannot Reach
")
History
The Sacred Chamber on Majalis
In ancient times, the
founders
of Majalan society developed the technology to build a
civilization
on levitating platforms high above the hostile surface of Majalis. However, the functioning of this machinery required the
neural network
of a
child
, who would be irreversibly condemned to a slow, painful
death
once connected. The equipment was housed in a protected
cavern
known as the
Sacred Chamber
.
Unable to find an alternative means of sustaining their society, the Majalans chose to venerate the
sacrifice
of the child as the "
ascension
" of the "
First Servant
". Those who could not abide the practice departed Majalis to establish their own
colony
on
Prospect VII
, the nearest inhabitable planet.
First contact
between the Federation and the Majalans occurred in
2249
, which led to an invitation of
Federation membership
that Majalis declined.
In
2259
, dissidents from Prospect VII, working with sympathizers on Majalis, sent
combat cruisers
to abduct the First Servant and prevent his impending sacrifice. They were thwarted by the
USS
Enterprise
, which proceeded to escort the First Servant to Majalis.
Minister
Alora
initially attempted to obfuscate the nature of the attempted abduction to
Captain
Christopher Pike
, but ultimately chose to reveal the First Servant's true purpose to him. Though Pike was horrified, he could do nothing as
Starfleet
had no
jurisdiction
on Majalis. (
SNW
: "
Lift Us Where Suffering Cannot Reach
")
Stellar cartography
In
2260
, the location of Majalis was labeled on a
star chart
denoting the spread of a
quantum uncertainty
field across the region. It was outside of the area effect of the phenomena. It was marked as an independent location. (
SNW
: "
Subspace Rhapsody
")
In
2261
, the location of "Eminiar (in FGC-321)" was depicted on a
star chart
used by the crew of the
USS
Enterprise
to highlight the location of
Vadia
. It was labeled as an independent location. (
SNW
: "
Through the Lens of Time
")
Scenes in the hovering Majalan city were filmed at the
Parkwood Estate
in Oshawa, Ontario.
[1]

Makus III
Type:

Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
(

)
Status:

(
3195
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Makus III
was the
inhabited
third
planet
of its
star system
. The planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
and was three
days
away from
Murasaki 312
at
warp 1
. (
TOS
: "
The Galileo Seven
")
History
In
2267
, the
USS
Enterprise
carried emergency
medical
supplies to Makus III, for transfer to the
New Paris
colonies
. (
TOS
: "
The Galileo Seven
")
In
2328
, this planet was home for the
Makus III Spaceport
. (
TNG-R
: "
Inheritance
")
In the
32nd century
, Makus III was a
Federation
member
planet. It was listed with other members on a video wall in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
Kids These Days
")
Stellar cartography
In
2293
, the
location
of Makus III in the
Milky Way Galaxy
was labeled in a
star chart
that was in
Captain
James T. Kirk
's
quarters
aboard the
USS

-A
. (
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
okudagram
)

2371
, the location of Makus III was labeled in the star chart
Data
and

were studying in
stellar cartography
aboard the
USS

-D
in

. (

okudagram

Appendices
Background information
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I"), Makus III was listed as a Federation member in
2378
.
In the
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Federation Historical Highlights, 2161-2385"), the Makus system was located in the Alpha Quadrant. The
primary
was classified as a
K-type star
.
Apocrypha
In the
Star Trek Maps
(p. 27), Makus was a recently colonized frontier world. It was home to a
Starfleet
supply base and a thriving
agricultural colony
, with a
population
of 71,000, that exported local foods and pharmaceuticals.

======================================================================
PAGE: Malaya IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Malaya_IV
======================================================================

Malaya IV
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2367
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Malaya IV
was the
inhabited
fourth
planet
in its
star system
. This planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
In the
24th century
, a
Federation
medical facility
was located on this planet.
Dr.
Noonien Soong
and
Juliana O'Donnell
went there in
2328
to get secretly
married
. Their
wedding
was witnessed by a
Corvallen trader
and a
Klingon
and their
marriage certificate
was kept at the
Registrar's Office
.
After 2328, in the chaos that ensued after the planetary
government
was overthrown, many
documents
, including the Soong
marriage certificate
were destroyed.
In
2370
, in an effort to confirm then Doctor
Juliana Tainer
's
story
,
Data
was checking the
passenger manifests
of the period to see if she traveled to Malaya IV. This planet was mentioned in the
commercial transport database
archive of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. (
TNG
: "
Inheritance
")
Lieutenant
Paul Hickman
had a routine
physical
on the planet and passed it "with flying colors" shortly before he was compelled by the
DNA
infection with the
Tarchanne
to return to
Tarchannen III
in
2367
. (
TNG
: "
Identity Crisis
")
For the planet in "Inheritance", the
script
, the actual dialogue in-episode pronunciation,
[1]
, the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 27, and the closed captioning spells the planet's name as "Mavala IV"; however, the
okudagram
spelled the planet's name as Malaya IV .
In the script for "Inheritance", the pronunciation for Mavala was "mah-VAY-lah".
[2]

======================================================================
PAGE: Malcor III
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Malcor_III
======================================================================

Malcor III
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Non-aligned
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Native species:
Malcorians
Location:
Malcor
system
Alpha Quadrant
~87
light years
from
Sol
Malcor III
was the
inhabited
third
planet
in the
Malcor
system
. Located in the
Alpha Quadrant
, this planet was approximately 87
light years
from
Sol
.
This
Class M
planet had a northern and
southern continent
, and its
day
lasted twenty-nine
hours
. The nearest
star system
to Malcor III was the
Garth system
. This was the
homeworld
for the
Malcorians
, a
humanoid
species
. The
planet
was headed by a
chancellor
.
By
2367
, the
United Federation of Planets
had observers on the planet in preparation for
first contact
. A mishap occurred when
William T. Riker
, supporting the effort, became injured and was revealed as an
alien
. First contact was made in secret with
Avel Durken
, the planet's chancellor at this time. Durken asked the Federation to wait for formal first contact until their species was ready to
break
the
warp barrier
, so the
USS
Enterprise
-D
took their observers and left with their report. (
TNG
: "
First Contact
")
In the
The Nitpicker's Guide for Next Generation Trekkers Volume II
(p. 278), author
Phil Farrand
stated that the planet was named after fan Karen P. Malcor.
Both the
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 47) and
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Federation Historical Highlights, 2161-2385") ignored or didn't remember the stated distance from Malcor III to Sol and placed this planet in the much closer
MU Ceti
system. This
binary
system was in the Alpha Quadrant. This was canonized in the
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
episode "
Beta Test
".
The planet's class came from the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 13.
External links
Malcor III
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Re-Used Planets in TNG
at
Ex Astris Scientia

======================================================================
PAGE: Maldorian station
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Maldorian_station
======================================================================

The
Maldorian station
was a
space station
based in the
Delta Quadrant
.
In
2377
, the
USS
Voyager
had a maintenance layover at the Maldorian station. (
VOY
: "
Body and Soul
")
According to the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 13, "Maldorian" was the name for a space-faring
Delta Quadrant
civilization.

======================================================================
PAGE: Malon Prime
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Malon_Prime
======================================================================

Malon Prime
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2375
)
Native species:
Malon
Location:
Delta Quadrant
Malon Prime
was the
inhabited
prime
planet
in its
star system
, in the
Delta Quadrant
. This planet was the
homeworld
of the
Malon
species. It was said to be one of the most beautiful planets in the quadrant, despite the fact that Malon industry produced massive quantities of
antimatter waste
. This was made possible by
waste export vessels
, which transported the hazardous waste far from Malon home space. (
VOY
: "
Juggernaut
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 79), Malon Prime was a
Class M planet
. The official name of the government was the Malon Sanctity. Dominant species on Malon Prime was the
humanoid
Malon. Malon Prime had
warp capability
since antiquity.
Decipher
's
Aliens
sourcebook called the planet Malonis and described it as the second planet in the Neled system.

======================================================================
PAGE: Mantilles
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Mantilles
======================================================================

Mantilles
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Habitable
(
2269
)
Location:
Pallas 14 system
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Mantilles
was the
inhabited
second
planet
in the
Pallas 14
system
. This planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
. The planet was affiliated with the
United Federation of Planets
.
Prior to
stardate
5371
, a Federation
colony
was established on this planet. It was the most remote inhabited planet in the Federation, lying at the outer fringe of the
galaxy
. Mantilles was home to 82 million people, and was
governed
by
retired
Starfleet
commodore
Bob Wesley
.
In 2269, the position and
orbital
path of Mantilles was illustrated on a system map displayed on the
main viewscreen
of the
USS
Enterprise
bridge
.
Mantilles came under threat by an unknown
cosmic cloud
creature
. Governor Wesley's attitude prevented planet-wide panic, and
five thousand children
were successfully evacuated before the cloud reached the planet. The crew of the
Enterprise
managed to stop the creature before it consumed Mantilles. (
TAS
: "
One of Our Planets Is Missing
")
In
2293
, the location of Mantilles in the
Milky Way Galaxy
was labeled in a
star chart
that was in
Captain
James T. Kirk
's
quarters
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-A
. (
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
,
production art
)
Mantilles threatened by a cosmic cloud
Orbit of Mantilles
The location of the Pallas 14 system in "The Explored Galaxy" star chart
Appendices
Background information
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
.
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I"), Mantilles was listed as a member planet of the Federation in
2378
.
Apocrypha
In the
Star Trek Maps
, Mantilles became the 25th Federation member in
2248
.
Mantilles was featured in the
novelization
of "One of Our Planets Is Missing" in
Star Trek Log 1
and mentioned in the novelization of
TAS
: "
The Lorelei Signal
" in
Star Trek Log 2
.
In the
Pocket TOS
novel
Allegiance in Exile
Wesley's assignment there was due to fears of
Tholian
espionage
.
The
FASA
Federation Ship Recognition Manual
named a ship after this world, the USS Mantilles (
β
).
External link
Mantilles
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

Maranga IV

Status:
Habitable
(

)
Location:

Maranga IV
was the
inhabited
fourth
planet
in its
star system
. This was the location of a
Klingon
outpost
. Maranga IV was near the
border of the Federation
, from which it was located on the far side of the
Vodrey Nebula
.
In
2370
, the
USS
Enterprise
-D
visited Maranga IV to attend the
Kot'baval Festival
. While on the
surface
,
Worf
was apparently attacked by forces loyal to the
House of Duras
. However, the attack was actually arranged by a future version of
Alexander
, who had disguised himself as
K'mtar
with the intention of changing the past. (
TNG
: "
Firstborn
")
See also
Maranga IV inhabitants
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 19 classified Maranga IV as a
class M
planet.
According to the
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 65), the Maranga system was located in the
Beta Quadrant
. The system's primary was a
Class K star
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Marayna's space station
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Marayna's_space_station
======================================================================

Marayna's space station
Type:
Dampening field generator
Affiliation:
Marayna's species
Location:
In "
Marayna's nebula
"
Delta Quadrant
Status:
Active
(
2373
)
Crew:
1
Marayna
's space station
was a
space station
hidden in an
inversion nebula
in the
Delta Quadrant
.
Built by member's of Marayna's species, it was occupied by a single volunteer whose primary role was to prevent the
plasma fires
in the nebula from chain-reacting with a
dampening field
. On the
world
left behind, the occupant's species' enjoyed the beauty of the nebula.
In
2373
, the station was operated was Marayna, when she encountered the
USS
Voyager
traversing nearby
space
. The station was later boarded by
Tuvok
during his face-to-face encounter with her. (
VOY
: "
Alter Ego
")
The station's exterior was not seen in the episode.
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 87), the
Voyager
made contact with this station on
stardate
50460.

======================================================================
PAGE: Marcos XII
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Marcos_XII
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Marcos XII
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
(
3195
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3195
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Marcos XII
was the
inhabited
twelfth
planet
in its
star system
. Located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
, this planet was home to a
Federation
settlement
.
History
In
2268
, Marcos XII had a
population
in the millions, with nearly one million of them being
children
. It was the first planet which the
evil
angel
-
being
Gorgan
decided to conquer once he was set loose. Gorgan used the children of the
Starnes Exploration Party
to capture the
Constitution
-class
starship
USS
Enterprise
and was using the
ship
to go to there.
Tommy Starnes
claimed to have
relatives
on Marcos XII when asking
James T. Kirk
to take the
orphans
there. (
TOS
: "
And the Children Shall Lead
")
In
2328
, this was the destination for passengers traveling from
Manark IV City
via commercial
transport
.
Christopher Tezber
and
Duke Willer
traveled on the
SS
Kogin
. (
TNG-R
: "
Inheritance
",
okudagram
)
In the
32nd century
, Marcos XII was a
Federation
member
planet. It was listed with other members on a video wall in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
Kids These Days
")
Stellar cartography
In
2293
, the
location
of Marcos XII in the
Milky Way Galaxy
was labeled in a
star chart
in
Captain
James T. Kirk
's
quarters
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-A
. (
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
,
okudagram
)
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I") this planet was listed as a Federation member in
2378
.
Star Trek: Federation - The First 150 Years
used the spelling "Marcus XII".

at

, the wiki for licensed

at

Background information

,
the Marcusites never developed
spaceflight
, instead fighting a series of devastating
nuclear wars
. Theirs was described as being, by the late
2260s
, a dwindling
population
that had become noted mainly for its artistic achievements, including those of Sten.

Marijne VII
Class:
Gas giant
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2370
)
Native species:
Marijne VII subspace beings
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Marijne VII
was the
seventh
planet
in its
star system
. The
gas giant
had an unusual
atmosphere
, with much
subspace distortions
.
Ten
days
before
stardate
47215
, the
Federation
starship
USS
Hera
had passed near this planet.
After the passing of the
Hera
, the
USS
Raman
was assigned a sampling
mission
, wherein they would take a sample of the atmosphere from Marijne VII. During the mission, it came into contact with
subspace beings
native to the planet's lower atmosphere, which accidentally caused the
deaths
of the
crew
. The
USS
Enterprise
-D
, investigating the
Raman
's fate, encountered these beings as well. (
TNG
: "
Interface
")
According to the
script
, Marijne VII was a
Neptune
-like planet.

======================================================================
PAGE: Mariposa (planet)
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Mariposa_(planet)
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Mariposa
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Mariposans
and, later,
Bringloidi
United Federation of Planets
(
3195
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3195
)
Satellite(s):
A
ring system
Location:
Ficus
sector
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
For the ship, please see
SS
Mariposa
.
For the animal, please see
butterfly
.
Mariposa
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Ficus
sector
. The planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
. Classified as
M class
, this
ringed
world was settled by
Humans
from the
SS
Mariposa
in the mid-
22nd century
. This planet was only half a
light year
from the
Bringloid
system
. While attempting to land on the
surface
, the
hull
of the ship was breached, killing all but five of the colonists. As a result, the
Mariposa colony
was comprised of numerous
clones
.
The
USS
Enterprise
-D
evacuated the colonists from
Bringloid V
to Mariposa in
2365
. (
TNG
: "
Up The Long Ladder
")
In the
32nd century
, Mariposa was a
Federation
member
planet. It was listed with other members on a video wall in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
Kids These Days
")
A deleted scene from this episode, available on the
TNG Season 2 Blu-ray
, referred to this planet as NB2323.
The planet model for Mariposa was first used for
Gravesworld
, ("
The Schizoid Man
") and was used again for an unnamed planet. ("
The Dauphin
")
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I"), Mariposa was listed as a Federation member in
2378
.

at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
