======================================================================
PAGE: Moab IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Moab_IV
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Moab IV
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
(
3195
)
Status:
Inhabited
(
3195
)
Native species:
None
Location:
Moab
system
Moab
sector
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Moab IV
was the
inhabited
, barren
fourth
planet
in the
Moab
system
, located in the
Moab
sector
. It was home to a
Human
Genome colony
on the
southern continent
, settled in the
22nd century
.
The planet itself was entirely deserted and only the
artificial biosphere
constructed for the
colony
allowed for Human settlement.
The
location
of Moab IV was labeled on its
orbital
path around
Alpha Moab
on a system map that was seen in the
laboratory
of
Hannah Bates
on Moab IV. (
TNG
: "
The Masterpiece Society
",
set artwork
)
In
2368
, a
stellar core fragment
from a collapsed
neutron star
passed through the Moab sector. The passing fragment, which had a
density
of one hundred billion
kilograms per cubic centimeter
, posed a serious threat to the colony: its
mass
would create large
earthquakes
, far beyond what the colony was designed to withstand. Because of this unique danger, the
isolationist
colony made a one-time exception to their policy against outside contact and accepted assistance from the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. (
TNG
: "
The Masterpiece Society
")
In
2381
,
Admiral
Vassery
joked to
Captain
Carol Freeman
regarding
Rom
and
Leeta
's progressive reforms on
Ferenginar
: "
It makes Moab IV inside the dome look like Moab IV outside the dome!
" He then added, "
You'd get it if you'd ever been to Moab IV.
" (
LD
: "
Parth Ferengi's Heart Place
")
In the
32nd century
, Moab IV was a
Federation
member
planet. It was listed with other members on a video wall in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
Kids These Days
")
Moab system
Alpha Moab
:
I
•
II
•
III
•
IV
•
V
•
VI
Appendices
Background information
According to the
script
, the pronunciation for Moab was "MOH-ub".
[1]
According to the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 48, Moab IV was "
named for the
ancient kingdom
that flourished on
Earth
during Biblical times in what is now
Jordan
.
"
The
surface
of Moab IV was a
matte painting
created by
Illusion Arts, Inc.
. (
Cinefantastique
, Vol. 25, No. 6/Vol. 26, No. 1, p. 73)
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I"), Moab IV (Genome Colony) was listed as a member planet of the Federation in
2378
. The author,
Geoffrey Mandel
, incorrectly listed this planet as Moab VI.
External link
Re-Used Planets in TNG
at
Ex Astris Scientia

======================================================================
PAGE: Monac IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Monac_IV
======================================================================

Monac IV
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Cardassian Union
Dominion
(
2373
-
2375
)
Status:
Intact
(
3196
)
Location:
Monac
system
Alpha Quadrant
Monac IV
was the
fourth
planet
in the
Monac
system
,
Alpha Quadrant
. This planet was approximately 100
million
kilometers
from the
Monac sun
.
History
During the
Dominion War
, the
Dominion
-controlled
Monac shipyard
was located at this planet.
In
2375
, the shipyards were a crucial production facility for the Dominion effort to retake the
Chin'toka system
. An entire
Klingon
fleet
attempted to advance on Monac IV but was repelled with
casualties
estimated at thirty
percent
. (
DS9
: "
Image in the Sand
")
Shortly after, the
Klingon
Bird-of-Prey
IKS
Rotarran
, under the command of
General
Martok
, destroyed the shipyards by triggering a
solar flare
that engulfed the installation. (
DS9
: "
Shadows and Symbols
")
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of the Cardassian Union as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 50 stated that Monac IV was probably named for DS9 special effects supervisor
Gary Monak
.
External link
Monac
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Mordan IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Mordan_IV
======================================================================

Mordan IV
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2364
)
Native species:
Mordanites
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Mordan IV
was the
inhabited
fourth
planet
of its
star system
. This
Class M
planet was the
homeworld
of the
Mordanites
, a spacefaring
humanoid
species
. The Mordanite
government
was led by a
governor
. In
2364
, that role was filled by
Karnas
.
History
The banner of Mordan IV
Traditionally, the
population
of Mordan was made up of a number of different
tribes
, each with its ruling
chief
. In
2319
,
the leader
of one of the tribes was
assassinated
by his enemies from another tribe. His son, Karnas, was determined to avenge his father's
death
. To this end, he took hostage the 63 passengers aboard a
starliner
, and demanded
weapons
from
Starfleet
in exchange for their release. Two
negotiators
were sent in by Starfleet, and both of them were killed. Then
Captain
Mark Jameson
was dispatched to Mordan, successfully negotiating the release of the hostages. Unknown to Starfleet, Jameson had given Karnas the weapons he wanted, but troubled by his breaking of the
Prime Directive
, he also gave weapons to Karnas' enemies. This caused
a bloody civil war
between the two
factions
, which ended in Karnas becoming
leader
of Mordan in
2359
. Much of the original
principal city
was destroyed during the
civil war
, only the
tunnels
beneath the city surviving intact. The city was rebuilt over the tunnels.
Five
years
later, in
2364
, the
Federation
ambassador
,
Hawkins
, was taken hostage by Karnas in an attempt to lure Jameson to Mordan. Karnas blamed Jameson for the forty years of civil war. He initially blamed the
kidnappings
on
terrorists
, but Jameson realized that Karnas was responsible. He mounted a failed rescue attempt, and when Karnas requested his life in exchange for the
hostages
, he agreed. Jameson died in front of him due to an age-reversing drug he had taken, but Karnas was satisfied he was dead, and released the hostages. (
TNG
: "
Too Short A Season
")
Appendices
Background information
The planet model for Mordan IV was later modified and used to represent
Relva VII
in "
Coming of Age
" and
Starbase 179
in "
A Matter Of Honor
".
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 52 classified Mordan IV as a
class M
planet.
Mordan IV cityscape maquette
The maquette inspected by builders Okuda and Sternbach...
...and as featured
Overhead views of the Mordan IV cityscape maquette.
Surveying the maquette, featuring the transparent domes...
...one of them salvaged to double as Alexandra's toy
A view on Mordan IV city and its surrounding landscape was visible from
Karnas
's
office
, though only barely. The near indiscernible, almost throw-away visual, belied the rather substantial effort, that went into its creation. It was not even a
matte painting
, the method of choice in the franchise for creating long-view landscape visuals of vistas, but rather a cityscape
maquette
, combined with a background landscape painting. One of its creators,
Mike Okuda
had noted in this respect, "
Creating models wasn't really our job. We weren't set up for model making in the art department, and we were lucky enough to have the services of
Paramount
's effects shop, as well as talented outside vendors like [professional model makers]
Greg Jein
and
Tony Meininger
. But we tried to help out when the vendors were too busy, or if it was something small that we thought was cool but the budget didn't allow. Mostly we painted a few found objects and a tabletop to create a loose impression of a cityscape. I recall that we used a big wok for the dome of one large
building
.
" (
Star Trek: The Next Generation 365
, p. 43) In regard to the Mordan IV cityscape maquette, he had further remarked, "
Rick
made most of the model city buildings. He used hot melt glue to create some cool sculptural detail on the skyscrapers. Very simple, but it looked good when photographed. I did paint and tape detail on dome structures what were mostly made from found objects. I recall that one big dome was a cooking wok. Later, I borrowed the shapes of the buildings for the animated city map graphic on the
main viewer
.
"
[1]
The graphic, Okuda referred to, was the
LCARS
silhouette display on the
main viewscreen
of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
, consulted by the
bridge crew
in the episode. The Mordan IV maquette was only the second such model used in the
Star Trek: The Next Generation
series, after the
Farpoint Station landscape maquette
, used in the pilot "
Encounter at Farpoint
", utilizing the same background painting that was used with the maquette in that episode. Superseded by matte paintings, it would remain so for years, until the
season six
episode "
Birthright, Part I
", for which a smaller maquette of a
Romulan
prison camp was created. (
Star Trek: The Next Generation Companion
, 3rd ed., p. 43) Due to the usually large size of maquettes, like that of Mordan IV, they were almost always scrapped after use, this one no exception. Yet on rare occasions, parts of them were re-utilized for use in another capacity. One of the transparent domes turned up as
Alexandra
's
toy
in
Toya
's
quarters
aboard the
Enterprise
-D in the later first season episode "
When The Bough Breaks
".
External links
Mordan IV
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Re-Used Planets in TNG
at
Ex Astris Scientia

Moreyworld
Type:

Status:

(

)

Beta Quadrant
Moreyworld
was a
planet
in the
Beta Quadrant
.
In
2293
, Moreyworld's position was labeled in the
star chart
for the "
Operation: Retrieve
" command briefing. The chart was from the
Starfleet Command Intelligence Database
. This star chart was seen in the
office
of the
Federation President
on
Earth
. (
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
,
set artwork
)
This planet's quadrant of origin is inferred based on the position of neighboring locations –
Starbase 24
and
Rura Penthe
– as seen in the
star charts
appearing in the
Star Trek: Discovery
episode "
Magic to Make the Sanest Man Go Mad
" and in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
This chart was seen in greater resolution at threads at Flare.Solareclipse.net.
[1]
(X)
[2]
(X)
Many of these locations shared their names with the last names of crew who worked on the sixth film. This location was probably named after the first assistant photographer
Robert Morey
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Mudd
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Mudd
======================================================================

Mudd
AKA:
Robot Planet
Class:
K
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Harcourt Fenton Mudd
Status:
Intact
(
2268
)
Satellite(s):
A
ring system
Native species:
Mudd androids
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
The
planet
"
Mudd
", so
named
by
Harcourt Fenton Mudd
, also known as the
robot planet
, according to
James T. Kirk
, was an
uninhabited
K type
planet
located in
uncharted space
in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
. This planet had a
ring system
and was populated by a
species
of
androids
. (
TOS
: "
I, Mudd
";
TAS
: "
Mudd's Passion
")
History
The planet was home to one of the few exploratory
outposts
established by a now extinct species, known only as the
Makers
, who were from the
Andromeda Galaxy
, whose
homeworld
had been destroyed by its
star
going
supernova
. In the
23rd century
, the planet was inhabited only by the
android workers
who were sent there to build vast
libraries
, advanced medical research laboratories and engineering shops.
Sometime between
2266
and
2268
, after fleeing from
Deneb V
, the
fugitive
Harcourt "Harry" Mudd
stumbled upon the planet in a previously uncharted
star system
. The android workers dubbed him "Lord Mudd the First" and held him against his will, as they needed someone to provide them with a purpose. In 2268, "Lord" Mudd sent the android
Norman
to
hijack
the
Federation
starship
USS
Enterprise
in the hopes of trading the crew for his own freedom.
Mudd's plan was ultimately unsuccessful and
Starfleet
personnel were able to reprogram the androids to continue once again the project they had been sent to the planet to accomplish of making the planet productive. In addition, the androids were left with custody of Harry Mudd, not as a sovereign ruler, but instead as a "first-class example of a
Human
failure", to be observed in order to avoid similar
individuals
in the future. (
TOS
: "
I, Mudd
")
However, Harry soon escaped the planet by
stealing
a
spaceship
, and left to find haven on
Ilyra VI
, where he fooled the innocent and friendly inhabitants into buying
Starfleet Academy
from him. (
TAS
: "
Mudd's Passion
")
Stellar cartography
The location of Mudd in "The Explored Galaxy" star chart
In
2293
, the
location
of Mudd in the
Milky Way Galaxy
was labeled in a
star chart
that was in
Captain
James T. Kirk
's
quarters
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-A
. (
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
,
okudagram
)
Appendices
Background information
The original planet "Mudd"
For the
2006
"remastered" version of "I, Mudd", a new computer-generated version of Mudd was created.
Apocrypha
In
The Worlds of the Federation
, Mudd was the
second
planet
orbiting
a
star
named UFC 257704 (
β
).
In
FASA
's
RPG
sourcebook
The Federation
, Mudd was the second planet in the
Beta Aurigae
system.
In the
Decipher
RPG sourcebook
Worlds
, the planet Mudd was the
Galor IV
mentioned in
TNG
: "
The Offspring
".
External links
Mudd (planet)
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Planets in TOS and TOS Remastered
at
Ex Astris Scientia

======================================================================
PAGE: Murder Planet
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Murder_Planet
======================================================================

Murder Planet
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Active
(
2383
)
Native species:
Cilium-like vegetation
(
superorganism
)
Location:
Hirogen star system
Delta Quadrant
Murder Planet
was the
name
devised by
Jankom Pog
and ultimately chosen for the sole
class M
planet
found in the
Hirogen star system
. While choosing the name,
Rok-Tahk
offered the alternate suggestion of "
Larry
" and
Dal R'El
voted for the name "
Let's not visit M class planets again.
" When
Zero
attempted to
telepathically
ask the planet its name, they discovered that the planet wished only to
eat
them. (
PRO
: "
Terror Firma
")
Murder Planet had several
moons
, while initial
scans
of the planet itself indicated elevated
thoron emissions
and was found to be inhabited by some kind of
cilium-like vegetation
.
A
Klingon
Bird-of-Prey
once landed on the planet and never left;
Gwyn
was of the opinion that the crew did not survive the planet. There were
rations
left behind, as well as one warrior's
mek'leth
. (
PRO
: "
Terror Firma
") The planet was later explored by the crew of the
USS
Protostar
in
2383
, where it was discovered that despite its abundance of
flora
, the
soil
contained no
nutrients
. (
PRO
: "
Dream Catcher
")
On further investigation, it was determined that the planet was in fact a
sentient lifeform
– a
superorganism
that lured and consumed its
prey
for nutrients, by way of emitting
spores
that clouded the minds and senses of its visitors. The planet then sensed and fabricated its victim's dreams or nightmares onto its
vine
-like
cilium
, to entice them to stay, and once fully enthralled, would begin to wrap them in its cilium and gradually pull the unaware being into the ground. If the prey became aware of these tactics and resisted, the same tactic would also be used to pull or hold down landed starships, or destroy small craft and vehicles by crushing and pulling them beneath the surface, stranding their intended victims. (
PRO
: "
Dream Catcher
", "
Terror Firma
")
To further disorient its prey, as well as their
tricorders
, the planet was able to
terraform
its
surface
at will by suddenly moving
hills
. This tactic could be defeated by using a fixed point, such as the
stars
, for
navigation
.
As a final means of consuming its prey, the planet used
acid rain
to assist in their
digestion
. (
PRO
: "
Terror Firma
")
During their
first contact
mission to the next planet they visited, the
Cymari homeworld
, Rok described the threatening
cymatic phenomena
they experienced as "
it's Murder Planet all over again.
" (
PRO
: "
First Con-tact
")
External link
Murder Planet
at
StarTrek.com

======================================================================
PAGE: Mutara Interdimensional Deep Space Array System
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Mutara_Interdimensional_Deep_Space_Array_System
======================================================================

MIDAS Array
Type:
Communications Array
Owner:
United Federation of Planets
Operator:
Starfleet
Status:
Active
(
2378
)
The
Mutara Interdimensional Deep Space Array System
, or
MIDAS array
for short, was a
Federation
communications array
that was in service with
Starfleet
in the late
24th century
. Starfleet worked with
Vulcans
in deploying this array. The array was also referred to as the
Mutara Interdimensional Deep-Space-Transponder Array System
by
Lieutenant
Reginald Barclay
of the
Pathfinder Project
.
Located near a small gas cloud, it utilized
hyper-subspace
technology to greatly reduce the time it would take for a message to reach its destination. The array consisted of a hexagonal central dish, to which were attached three long arms mounting panels and three mobile emitters. When activated, the array launched a small targeting device from the center of the dish, which focused energy beams from the three emitters into a single beam.
In
2376
,
Deep Space 9
predicted that a
pulsar
would pass close to the array. By firing a
tachyon
beam at the pulsar,
Pathfinder Project
was able to create a
micro-wormhole
through which they made contact with
USS
Voyager
for a brief period. (
VOY
: "
Pathfinder
")
Later, Pathfinder found a way to amplify the signal from the MIDAS array by bouncing it off a
class B pulsar
. This allowed a brief compressed data stream to be sent to
Voyager
about every thirty-two days, coinciding with the pulsar's rotation cycle. Initially Starfleet sent many letters, as well as tactical updates and
stellar maps
. The rotation cycle peaked for seventeen hours, giving
Voyager
that long after receiving the data stream to send a response. Instead of letters,
Voyager
sent
The Doctor
(a
holographic program
), waiting thirty-two days before they could send or receive letters again so he could treat his creator,
Dr.
Lewis Zimmerman
, who was fatally ill. (
VOY
: "
Life Line
")
Deployable targeting device
Several data streams later, Pathfinder tried to send a
Reginald Barclay
hologram
, but it was intercepted by a
Ferengi marauder
which had passed within twenty
meters
of the array and stole the program. A month later, Pathfinder tried to send the data stream again – once again to be intercepted by the Ferengi, who took this chance to send an altered version of the first hologram with a plot to
abduct
Seven of Nine
for her
nanoprobes
.
As part of the cover story, the compromised Barclay hologram claimed that the transmission of the unabridged version of his program overloaded the MIDAS array, making Pathfinder miss the transmission. (
VOY
: "
Inside Man
")
By
2378
,
Voyager
was close enough to perform
Operation Watson
, which would allow eleven minutes of contact with the Alpha Quadrant through the array every day. (
VOY
: "
Author, Author
")
Background information
At one point Lieutenant Barclay calls it the "Mutara Inter-Dimensional Deep Space Transponder Array," and while this expanded name of the array doesn't translate easily to the acronym "MIDAS," computer panels later show the full name of the station as "Mutara Interdimensional Deep Space Array System".
The array was probably built sometime prior to
2370
, as it has a TNG-style Starfleet insignia on it that was no longer used afterward. It may have some connection to the
Mutara sector
, although this was not confirmed. The station
was
probably located somewhat near
Deep Space 9
, however, as that station produced
interstellar phenomena forecasts
that affected the area near the array.
For more information on the
studio model
, see
VOY studio models
.
Operation
"
The MIDAS array makes use of cosmic field collector and transmitter hardware also seen on the
Jupiter Station
. The array could be considered a "man-tended" facility, visited by Starfleet engineers for periodic maintenance or upgrades. The central faceted dish and three outer articulated antenna paddles can bore deep through subspace to pick up faint whispers of signals that might normally have been considered lost through energy dissipation over tens of thousands of light years. The longer, multi-panel collectors comprise the omnidirectional detector system, which tells the array to slew around to maximize signal energy input. Different energy levels can be focused by the dish into a mobile feed horn "cab" riding hundreds or even thousands of meters away from its docking base. The cab contains banks of subspace preprocessors running at FTL speeds and can transmit preliminary results to the array's main computers via the short-range AZUREZAHN link or directly to Starfleet by way of standard
subspace radio
.
" –
Rick Sternbach
,
DrexFiles Blog
(X)
External link
Mutara Interdimensional Deep Space Array System
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: NAR-30974
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/NAR-30974
======================================================================

NAR-30974
Type:
Dry dock
Registry:
NAR
-30974
Owner:
United Federation of Planets
Operator:
Starfleet
Location:
Earth
orbit
Sol system
Sector 001
Alpha Quadrant
Status:
Active
(
2293
)
NAR-30974
was a
Federation
dry dock
that was in service with
Starfleet
in the late
23rd century
. This facility was located in
orbit
above
Earth
.
In
2293
, the
USS
Enterprise
-B
was launched from this drydock. (
Star Trek Generations
)
Interior of the spacedock's station
Appendices
Background information
The registry on the model
This model was first seen in
Star Trek: The Motion Picture
as the
spacedock the USS
Enterprise
was launched from
. It was heavily modified and labeled with the
registry
of
NAR
-30974 for
Generations
. The registry can be seen on screen as the
Enterprise
-B leaves the structure.
While labeled as a drydock, this facility was only referred to as a spacedock in the script and on screen.
USS
Voyager
in drydock
The drydock was recreated at
Foundation Imaging
, under supervision of
Adam Lebowitz
, as a
CGI
model that the
USS
Voyager
was launched from at
Utopia Planitia Fleet Yards
in the episode "
Relativity
", as well as to represent several other spacedocks at the site. (
Star Trek: The Magazine
Volume 1, Issue 6
, p. 50) (A reverse view of the docked
Voyager
, not utilized in the episode, is afforded on Lebowitz's
blog
.) The
Voyager
drydock would have been smaller than the one the
Enterprise
was launched from, though, based on the
sizes
of the two vessels. The same CGI model was used as yet another differently sized drydock on the cover of the
Star Trek: Ships of the Line (2001)
calendar.
External links
TMP Drydock studio model
at
modelermagic.com
Starfleet drydock
at
Adam's Collection

(

Beta Quadrant
Nar'el
was an
inhabited
planet
and the
homeworld
of a
pre-warp civilization
.
In
2248
,
Captain
Robert April
of the
USS
Enterprise
sent his
science officer
to Na'rel, then at an
industrial age
degree of development, to solve the threat of an
extinction
-level
drought
by sharing
Federation
technology
, in violation of the
Prime Directive
.

Una Chin-Riley
's
court martial
in
2259
,
Neera Ketoul
cited the Na'rel incident as an example of when a
Starfleet
captain had exercised their judgment over the application of
law
for the greater good. (
SNW
: "
Ad Astra per Aspera
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Narendra III
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Narendra_III
======================================================================

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Narendra III
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Klingon Empire
Status:
Habitable
(
2344
)
Location:
Narendra
system
Unnamed sector
Beta Quadrant
Narendra III
was the
inhabited
third
planet
in the
Narendra
system
. This planet was located in a
sector
of the
Beta Quadrant
. (
TNG
: "
Yesterday's Enterprise
";
ENT
: "
Judgment
")
History
This planet was
colonized
by the
Klingons
prior to
2152
. (
ENT
: "
Judgment
")
The vast Klingon courtroom on Narendra III in 2152
In that
year
, the
Human
Captain
Jonathan Archer
was put on
trial
on Narendra III, under the suspicion of
conspiring
against the
Klingon Empire
. Archer was sentenced to lifelong
exile
on
Rura Penthe
, and his
advocate
Kolos
had to join him for one year. However, Archer was later rescued from Rura Penthe by his
crew
. (
ENT
: "
Judgment
")
In
2344
, the
outpost
was attacked and destroyed by four
Romulan
warbirds
, an
assault
which was later known as the
Battle of Narendra III
. The outpost's
distress call
was answered by the
Federation
starship
USS
Enterprise
-C
, and although outnumbered four-to-one engaged the Romulans, only to be destroyed during the battle. This course of action was considered as an act of
honor
by the
Klingon Empire
, which led to the signing of a new
peace treaty
.
In an
alternate timeline
, the
Enterprise
-C disappeared during the battle. The outpost was destroyed, and events led to a renewed
conflict
between the Federation and the Empire.
(
TNG
: "
Yesterday's Enterprise
", "
Redemption II
")
Appendices
Background information
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star charts
appearing in the
Star Trek: Discovery
episode "
Magic to Make the Sanest Man Go Mad
" and in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
In the
The Making of Yesterday's Enterprise
(p. 77) by
Eric A. Stillwell
, he stated that this planet was named after
Star Trek: The Next Generation
writer and science consultant
Naren Shankar
. "nah-REN-druh" was how the planet's
name
was phonetically spelled in the
script
pronunciation guide for "Yesterday's Enterprise".
[1]
Although the planet shown in "Judgment" was never identified as Narendra III on screen (the term "Narendra" was not mentioned there at all), the episode's final draft script referred to it as such.
[2]
In addition,
David A. Goodman
, the episode's writer, stated in a podcast that he intended for the settlement/installation shown to be a reference to the outpost from "Yesterday's Enterprise".
In the final draft script of "Judgment", an overview of an area on Narendra III shown in that installment was described as "
A large
city
on this Klingon outpost, with familiar Klingon-style
architecture
. In the center of
town
, a GRAND TRIBUNAL
BUILDING
which proudly displays the three-pointed Klingon symbol.
"
[3]
External link
Narendra III
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

in the

.

for the "

" command briefing. The chart was from the
Starfleet Command Intelligence Database
. This star chart was seen in the
office
of the
Federation President
on
Earth
. (
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
,
set artwork
)
This planet's quadrant of origin is inferred based on the position of neighboring locations –
Starbase 24
and
Rura Penthe
– as seen in the
star charts
appearing in the
Star Trek: Discovery
episode "
Magic to Make the Sanest Man Go Mad
" and in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
This chart was seen in greater resolution at threads at Flare.Solareclipse.net.
[1]
(X)
[2]
(X)
Many of these locations shared their names with the last names of crew who worked on the sixth film. This location was probably named after the director of photography
Hiro Narita
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Narj's Miraculous Menagerarium
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Narj's_Miraculous_Menagerarium
======================================================================

Narj's Miraculous Menagerarium
Narj's Miraculous Menagerarium
was a
menagerie
owned by
Narj
in the late
24th century
. Located in a
space station
above a
planet
, it was once
voted
one of the top thousand menageries in the
quadrant
. The menagerie housed an extensive collection of dangerous
lifeforms
, of which the
Pyrithian swamp gobblers
were the most lucrative attractions. Despite the dangers its exhibits posed if they were to escape, the station operated under a strict no
weapons
policy.
In
2381
, the Menagerarium accidentally received
two
Humans
within a shipment of
bipedals
, whom Narj put on display, as he had trouble telling non-
botanical
beings apart. Once this was discovered, an
away team
from the
USS
Cerritos
, consisting of
Commander
Jack Ransom
,
Lieutenant jg
Beckett Mariner
, and
Ensign
Gary
, was assigned to retrieve them. While they were aboard the station, a
moopsy
escaped and devoured two Pyrithian swamp gobblers and Narj, before causing the station's
orbit
to deteriorate by clambering over the
consoles
in the main
control room
.
Ransom and Beckett managed to lure the moopsy back into its enclosure with a trail of Ransom's
teeth
, after which they stabilized the station's orbit. They then found that the moopsy had been released by the displayed Humans, in the hopes of killing Narj and usurping ownership of his station. The
Cerritos
team thus decided to leave them as exhibits in the menagerie until someone else had time to pick them up. (
LD
: "
I Have No Bones Yet I Must Flee
")
Exhibits
Aldebaran serpent
Alfa 177 canine
Areolus
four
-
winged
turtles
Areolus
winged
goat
Bats
Bird-like
lizards
Birds
with
head
plumes
Birds
with
large
beaks
Black
birds
Butterflies
Cave
-dwelling
frog
Ceti eel
Desert
-
dwelling
arthropod
Eel
-like
creature
Florkas
Glommer

crabs
Gravity well planet
predator
Gray
snake

creature

koala

snake
Humans

-
eyed
reptiles

======================================================================
PAGE: Narva
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Narva
======================================================================

Narva
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2376
)
Location:
Delta Quadrant
"
Narva
" was a fictitious
planet
in the
Delta Quadrant
invented by the
con artists
Dala
,
Mobar
, and
Zar
. According to them, all of the adult settlers on Narva were killed after being exposed to toxic levels of
omega radiation
leaving many
orphans
on the planet. The con artists commonly used the story in their schemes to elicit sympathy from their
marks
.
When
Neelix
and
Tom Paris
first met
Dala
and
Mobar
in the
Wyanti system
, the two con artists were posing as
clerics
from a hermitic
religious
sect
whose crops, which they intended to give to the orphans on Narva in honor of the
Redeemer of Light
, had been infected by a
blight
.
Later, while
Dala
and
Mobar
were posing as
Kathryn Janeway
and
Tuvok
, they told the
Telsian
miners
that they needed to acquire
bolomite
to use in
omega radiation therapy
for the orphans. (
VOY
: "
Live Fast and Prosper
")
The planet might to be named after
Narva
(the easternmost city of
Estonia
), a major city on Earth.

. A

: "

")

======================================================================
PAGE: Navigational Control Post 24
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Navigational_Control_Post_24
======================================================================

Navigational control posts
were stations within a particular area of space which guided and managed vessels through that area.
The
Cardassians
maintained at least twenty-four navigational posts within their territory. They used their
sensors
to determine the types of vessels within their territory and decide if they were a threat or not. They were also able to dispatch
repair ships
to assist disabled vessels.
While these posts were able to scan and communicate within a wide area, the quality of communications degraded with distance from the post. At the extreme edge of scanner range, only audio contact was possible.
Kira Nerys
and
Miles O'Brien
used this to their advantage in
2370
when they infiltrated Cardassian space in a
runabout
disguised as the
Lissepian transport
Martuk
. Cardassian Navigational Control Post 24 detected their runabout as the
Lissepian
transport
; however, the modifications to the runabout's
field buffers
were interpreted by the post as malfunctioning
subspace field emitters
. Kira explained the malfunctions as trouble with the
phase modulators
, which they were attempting to correct. The
officer
asked them if they needed a repair ship, to which Kira said, although she appreciated the offer, that it was unnecessary. Seeing power fluctuations, the officer pressed if they needed help. Realizing he might be becoming suspicious, Kira changed tactics and said that they were carrying a shipment of
Rulot
seeds
to
Gul
Marayn
on
Cardassia IV
. She pressed that time was of the essence, as the
crop
might be ruined if they were delayed, as well as the gul's mood. The officer expressed his understanding and suggested they get their ship repaired after they delivered their cargo. He then ended the transmission, and the runabout was able to continue on its mission to rescue
Li Nalas
from the
Hutet labor camp
on Cardassia IV. (
DS9
: "
The Homecoming
")
See also
Navigational control satellite

======================================================================
PAGE: Nel III
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Nel_III
======================================================================

Nel III
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2360s
)
Location:
Epsilon Nel
system
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Nel III
was one of two
inhabited
planets
in the
Epsilon Nel
system
. By
2368
, the
cities
on this planet included
Alpha Municipality
,
Grant Municipality
, and
Epsilon Municipality
. Two of the
medical facilities
on this planet were the
Goodhartz Medical Facility
and
Farrell Hospital
.
This planet was one of eleven visited by a group of
Ullian
telepathic
historians
in the
2360s
, who collected a library of
memories
from the inhabitants. Soon after the visit, the planet reported two unexplained
comas
. The comas were later determined to be a result of
telepathic memory invasion
perpetuated by
Jev
. A person of interest in the case,
Tarmin
, was exonerated as he was on his
species
'
homeworld
at the time of the visit. (
TNG
: "
Violations
")
In the pre-remastered episode,
Gray Municipality
was a city on this planet, and the two medical facilities were the
Taylor Hospital
and
Wiemer Medical Facility
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Nelvana I
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Nelvana_I
======================================================================

Nelvana I
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2366
)
Location:
Nelvana
system
Federation-Romulan border
Beta Quadrant
Nelvana I
was the
uninhabited
first
planet
of the
Nelvana
system
. The planet was located inside the
Federation-Romulan border
in the
Beta Quadrant
.
In
2366
, Nelvana I's location was labeled on a
Starfleet
tactical intelligence
analysis
system map. This map illustrated the current position and
orbital
path of this planet. A tactical scan analysis of this planet by the
USS
Enterprise
-D
resulted in negative readings which
Data
interpreted as no unusual activity. The starship was scanning for artificially-generated
power
, reflected pattern return,
subspace distortion
,
electromagnetic
emissions, gravimetric perturbation,
microwave
enhancement,
background radiation
,
particulate
distribution, virtual interferometry, and low-level
gamma imaging
. (
TNG
: "
The Defector
",
okudagram
)
This planet was only mentioned in writing.
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
Nelvana I was named for the Canadian animation studio,
Nelvana Limited
. (
Star Trek: The Next Generation Companion
, 2nd ed., p. 111)

======================================================================
PAGE: Nelvana III
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Nelvana_III
======================================================================

Nelvana III
Type:
Planet
Native species:
None
Location:
Nelvana
system
Federation-Romulan border
Beta Quadrant
Intelligence scan of the Nelvana system
Nelvana III
was the
uninhabited
third
planet
of the
Nelvana
system
. The planet was located within the
Federation-Romulan border
in the
Beta Quadrant
. Its
surface
was nothing but barren
rock
, with no native
lifeforms
. (
TNG
: "
The Defector
")
History
In
2366
, Nelvana III was the subject of an elaborate ruse perpetrated by the
Romulan Star Empire
to expose
Admiral
Alidar Jarok
as a
traitor
and to provoke the
Federation
into violating the Neutral Zone.
Through deliberate
misinformation
, Jarok was led to believe that the
Romulans
had established a
cloaked
base on Nelvana III in preparation for a
preemptive strike
against the Federation. Faced with the prospect of war, Jarok defected to the
USS
Enterprise
-D
to warn them of the attack. To enhance the deception, the Romulans placed a cloaked
probe
in
orbit
of Nelvana III that emitted low-level
subspace
signals.
Before entering the
Neutral Zone
, the
Enterprise
-D completed a tactical scan
analysis
of the Nelvana system, which showed the orbit and
location
of Nelvana III in the system. A
long range sensor scan
of this planet resulted in negative readings which
Data
interpreted as no unusual activity. The starship was scanning for artificially generated power, reflected pattern return,
subspace distortion
,
electromagnetic
emissions, gravimetric perturbation,
microwave
enhancement,
background radiation
,
particulate
distribution, virtual interferometry, and low-level
gamma imaging
.
Later, the
Enterprise
-D entered the Zone for a more in-depth study of the planet. When the
Enterprise
-D arrived in orbit, there were no indications of a base at all. The Romulans de-cloaked and revealed the trap, but the
Enterprise
-D was able to withdraw thanks to hidden
Klingon
reinforcements. (
TNG
: "
The Defector
")
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
Nelvana III was named for the Canadian animation studio,
Nelvana Limited
. (
Star Trek: The Next Generation Companion
, 2nd ed., p. 111)
External links
Nelvana III
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Re-Used Planets in TNG
at
Ex Astris Scientia

Nelvana IV
was the
uninhabited
fourth
planet
of the
Nelvana
system
. The planet was located within the
Federation-Romulan border
in the
Beta Quadrant
.
In
2366
, Nelvana's location was labeled on a
Starfleet
tactical intelligence
analysis
system map. This map illustrated the current position and
orbital
path of this planet. A tactical scan analysis of this planet by the
USS
Enterprise
-D
resulted in negative readings which
Data
interpreted as no unusual activity. The starship was scanning for artificially generated
power
, reflected pattern return,
subspace distortion
,
electromagnetic
emissions, gravimetric perturbation,
microwave
enhancement,
background radiation
,
particulate
distribution, virtual interferometry, and low-level
gamma imaging
. (
TNG
: "
The Defector
",
okudagram
)
This planet was only mentioned in writing.
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".

Nelvana Limited
. (
Star Trek: The Next Generation Companion
, 2nd ed., p. 111)

======================================================================
PAGE: Nepenthe
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Nepenthe
======================================================================

Nepenthe
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2401
)
Satellite(s):
At least
three moons
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
For the
PIC
episode of the same name, please see "
Nepenthe
".
Nepenthe
was an
inhabited
planet
. It was
orbited
by
three
moons
and was located a few days' travel from the
Artifact
at
maximum warp
for a
Kaplan F17 Speed Freighter
.
The
soil
on Nepenthe possessed regenerative properties, which promoted
plant
growth and healing. The planet's surface featured vast
forests
, large
rock
arches, and pristine bodies of
water
. Local
fauna
included
bunnicorns
,
birds
such as the
Nepenthian red-winged banshee
, and
insects
. (
PIC
: "
Nepenthe
")
William T. Riker
and
Deanna Troi
settled on Nepenthe because of the planet's regenerative powers, after their son
Thad
was
diagnosed
with
mendaxic neurosclerosis
. They continued living on the planet after Thad's death. The Troi-Rikers installed an extensive security system around their house with
shields
and
sensors
to fend off the
Kzinti
, who were active in the region.
Pursued by
Romulans
in
2399
,
Jean-Luc Picard
and
Soji Asha
used the
spatial trajector
in the
queencell
of the Artifact to escape to Nepenthe. Upon their arrival, Picard and Soji met
Kestra Troi-Riker
, who showed them to
her parents' house
. A few days later,
La Sirena
retrieved Picard and Soji from the planet. (
PIC
: "
The Impossible Box
", "
Nepenthe
")
In 2399, the closest
Federation
starbase
to Nepenthe was
Deep Space 12
. (
PIC
: "
Broken Pieces
")
While they were held captive on the
Shrike
in
2401
, Riker and Troi discussed Nepenthe and found that neither of them liked living on the planet. In particular, they disliked the
architecture
of their
house
, the noise of the birds, and their isolation from other people. (
PIC
: "
Surrender
")
Locations
Infinity Lake
Infinity Lake Spaceport
Troi-Riker house
Nepenthe
is a
drug
from
Greek mythology
which grants forgetfulness.
A
planetary system
with a similar name, the Nepenthe system, was first introduced in the
video game
Star Trek: Bridge Commander
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Neptune
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Neptune
======================================================================

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Neptune
AKA:
Sol VIII
Class:
Gas Giant
Type:
Planet
Diameter:
49,528
kilometers
(30,782
miles
)
(
equatorial
diameter
)
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Intact
(
3196
)
Satellite(s):
Triton
, among other moons and a
ring system
Location:
Sol system
Sector 001
Alpha Quadrant
For the Roman god, please see
Neptune (mythology)
.
Neptune
(also known as
Sol VIII
) was the
eighth
planet
of the
Sol system
. The planet was located in
Sector 001
,
Alpha Quadrant
.
History
A painting of Neptune, as seen from it's moon Triton
In
2257
, the
USS
Discovery
passed through the Neptunian system on its course to
Vulcan
. After leaving the system, the
Federation
starship
went to
warp
. (
DIS
: "
Will You Take My Hand?
")
Arex
described the
cosmic cloud
, encountered by the
USS
Enterprise
in
2269
, as having twice the diameters of
Jupiter
,
Saturn
, and Neptune in size. (
TAS
: "
One of Our Planets Is Missing
")
A
painting
depicting Neptune as seen from
Triton
hung above
Geordi La Forge
's bed in his
quarters
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. (
TNG
: "
The Most Toys
", "
The Mind's Eye
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet was within the enclosed region. (
SA
: "
300th Night
")
See also
Neptune
-class
Stellar cartography
In
2024
, the position and orbital path of Neptune were depicted on a map of the Solar System, which was seen at the
gala
held for the
Europa Mission
astronauts
. (
PIC
: "
Two of One
")
"Chart 14A: The Sol System" showing the location of Neptune
At
warp 4.5
, it would take six minutes to travel from Earth to Neptune and back. (
ENT
: "
Broken Bow
")
In
1996
, of an
alternate timeline
, the position and
orbital
path of Neptune in the Sol system were depicted on a poster in the office where
Rain Robinson
worked, at the
Griffith Observatory
.
(
VOY
: "
Future's End
")
In
2267
, the position and orbital path of Neptune were illustrated on "Chart 14A: The Sol System". The chart was filed in the
USS
Enterprise
library computer
. The planet was identified by name and by its
astronomical symbol
. It was scanned by the
probe
Nomad
in
auxiliary control
. (
TOS
: "
The Changeling
"
production art
)
The Sol System
Star
:
Sol

:

•
Venus
•
Earth
(
Luna
) •
Mars
(
Deimos
) •

(
Io
;
Ganymede
;
Europa
) •

(

;

;

) •

•

(

) •

:

•

•

•

Appendices
Background information
Neptune was named after the
Roman
god
of
oceans
and
earthquakes
.
Diameter was from real-world sources.
The
painting
of Neptune and Triton was created by
Rick Sternbach
and lent to the production. It was originally published by
Astronomy Magazine
and
The Planetary Society
.
[1]
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I"), Neptune was a charter member of the
United Federation of Planets
in
2161
.
In the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 73, the authors wrote that Neptune orbited the sun at an average
distance
of thirty
au
.
Apocrypha
In
1975
, the
Mego
toy company produced a reptilian action figure doll called a "Neptunian," although such a creature never appeared at any time during the
Star Trek
franchise. Mego may have
intended
to create a
Skorr
or
Aquan
action doll, but it looked nothing like the animated species.
[2]

Neptune
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Neptune
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Nervala IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Nervala_IV
======================================================================

Nervala IV
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Habitable
(
2369
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Nervala IV
was the
uninhabited
fourth
planet
in its
star system
. The planet generated a distortion field, which prevented the use of
transporters
and
shuttles
. The distortion field could only be penetrated when Nervala moved closer to its
primary
, which happened every eight
years
. The planet also experienced
earthquakes
from time to time.
In the
2360s
, a
Starfleet
research station
,
Nervala station
, was located on this planet. The crew was forced to evacuate in
2361
due to the changing conditions. The
USS
Potemkin
arrived and sent an
away team
, including
Lieutenant
William T. Riker
. They were all successfully evacuated; however, unknown to them, a duplicate of Riker,
Thomas Riker
, was created by a
transporter accident
. Thomas remained on the planet while Will Riker made it back to the
Potemkin
. Thomas was rescued in
2369
by the
USS
Enterprise
-D
when it visited the planet to retrieve the data left by the research team. (
TNG
: "
Second Chances
")
In
2371
,
Odo
mentioned Nervala IV when informing
Gul
Dukat
on the events leading up to the transporter accident. (
DS9
: "
Defiant
")
According to the
script
for "
Second Chances
", the pronunciation for Nervala was "ner-VAHL-uh"; however, in the
script
for "
Defiant
", the pronunciation for Nervala was "NER-va-la".
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 74 classified Nervala IV as a
class M
planet.
External link
Nervala IV
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Neubilia Prime
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Neubilia_Prime
======================================================================

Neubilia Prime
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
22nd century
)
Native species:
Neubilia Prime natives
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Neubilia Prime
was the
inhabited
prime
planet
in its
star system
.
Prior to
2151
, this planet was once visited by the
father
of
Travis Mayweather
. Mayweather said that the
tribal
elders on Neubilia could challenge visitors to a duel if "you look at them the wrong way." Mayweather's father, who
captained
the
Earth
cargo ship
ECS
Horizon
, was challenged to such a duel. Mayweather suggested to
Hoshi Sato
that his father accepted the challenge and won the battle, rather than look for a loophole to get out of it. This inspired Hoshi and Travis to find a way for
Jonathan Archer
to defeat
Andorian
Commander
Shran
in a traditional Andorian honor duel (the
Ushaan
) without killing him. (
ENT
: "
Two Days and Two Nights
", "
United
")
Background information
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
In "
Two Days and Two Nights
", the NX-class
Enterprise
set a record by becoming the first Starfleet ship to travel ninety light years from its homeworld. Neubilia Prime was visited by
Enterprise
prior to its historic feat, suggesting it was less then ninety light years from Earth.
Neubilia Prime was named for high school teacher Karyn Newbill, the girlfriend of
Enterprise
producer
Michael Sussman
. (
Information provided by Mike Sussman
)
The DVD closed captioning spelled the name of this planet as "Nobelia Prime". However, "Neubilia" was the spelling used in the scripts. A
deleted scene
from "
E²
" (also written by Sussman) mentions a similarly named disease,
Neubilean fever
(also spelled Neubillian).

======================================================================
PAGE: Neural
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Neural
======================================================================

Neural
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2268
)
Native species:
Neural natives
Mugatos
Mahko root
Location:
Neural
system
Alpha Quadrant
~176
light years
from
Sol
Neural
was an
inhabited
M class
planet
in the
Neural
system
. The planet was located in the
Alpha Quadrant
and was approximately 176
light years
from
Sol
. This was the
homeworld
of a
native
pre-warp
species
. The planet was, also, the home for the
Mugato
and the
mahko root
.
History
Prior to
stardate
4211
, the
civilization
of Neural was protected under the terms of the
Prime Directive
.
The
surface
of Neural was generally quite similar to
Earth
, almost remarkably so, but with a few notable differences in native
life
. The native
culture
of Neural was tribal, with
tribes
of
Hill People
led by
female
shamans
known as
Kahn-ut-tu
. A major threat was the Mugato, a
white
-
furred
predatory
ape
-like
creature
having a lethally
poisonous
bite. The only known countermeasure was the native mahko root plant, which the Kahn-ut-tu were quite skilled at administering through a traditional healing ritual.
James T. Kirk
visited Neural as a young
lieutenant
in
2255
and conducted a planetary survey as his first command. During this mission, he befriended the hill man
Tyree
and, in a technical violation of the
Prime Directive
, revealed that he was from another world of more advanced technology. Tyree swore himself to secrecy and proclaimed Kirk his brother, vowing never to reveal what he knew. Lieutenant Kirk then completed his survey
mission
and found the inhabitants to be a peaceful, low-
technology
civilization that was
centuries
or
millennia
from developing any advanced technology.
In
2268
, a followup visit revealed that the inhabitants were now divided into warring
factions
, with some factions possessing
flintlock
firearms
that were centuries beyond their level of technology. It was quickly discovered that the
Klingon Empire
contacted the planet in
2267
and was tampering with the natives by arming some of the warring factions while giving them gradual weapon improvements to make it appear that the natives were developing them on their own, albeit at a suspiciously accelerated rate. Fearing that the planet could fall to Klingon influence, Kirk decided to violate the Prime Directive and contact opposing factions, helping them to fight the Klingon-backed tribes.
Using an analogy derived from the "
Brush Wars
" of the
20th century
where
major powers
fought their wars through smaller proxies, Kirk set up a Federation-backed faction on Neural by providing flintlocks to the hill people that were the victims of the Klingon-backed aggression. (
TOS
: "
A Private Little War
")
In 23rd century or 24th century, hunters (maybe Ferengis) came to capture mugatos. In 2381, some or all mugatos were found on Frylon IV. (
LD
: "
Mugato, Gumato
")
People
Named
Apella
Nona
Tyree
Yutan
Unnamed
See
:
Neural natives
Tyree of the Hill People
Apella of the Village People
A mugato
Appendices
Background information
The name Neural only appeared in the episode's
script
and was not mentioned on screen.
In the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 431, it was stated that the planet's name was referenced in an unfilmed line from the script.
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 34, "United Federation of Planets") and
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Federation Historical Highlights, 2161-2385"), this planet was known as Neural (Zeta Boötis III). This world became a
protectorate
of the Federation in 2268. The
capital city
was Kahn-ut-tu, and the dominant species were the Hill People and the Village People. In
2378
, there was an estimated 27.3 million people living on this planet. A major point of interest was the Peace Bridge, and first contact with this world was made by the
USS
Farragut
in
2254
.
Apocrypha
In
The Worlds of the Federation
, it was stated that in the aftermath of the events of "A Private Little War", the Hill and Village Peoples fragmented into five separate factions, all warring with each other, spurred on by the weapons provided to them by the Klingons and the Federation.
External links
Neural
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Planets in TOS and TOS Remastered
at
Ex Astris Scientia

======================================================================
PAGE: New Axton
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/New_Axton
======================================================================

New Axton
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Non-aligned
Status:
Habitable
(
2381
)
Satellite(s):
Two moons
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
New Axton
was an
inhabited
planet
with two
moons
. It was a center for
black market
and other disreputable trades; according to
Lieutenant
Shaxs
, it was "
twice as
lawless
as
Old Axton
, without any of the charm.
" The inhabitants tended to be sensitive about what they perceived as condescension from the
Federation
.
Despite its unsavory reputation, activities on and around New Axton were tightly regulated. The world was surrounded by a
planetary shield
. Unauthorized starships were barred from remaining in
orbit
, allegedly due to their effect on the
thermosphere
, and were instead required to park near the second moon. Travel to and from the surface was provided by a
moon shuttle
that ran every three hours;
transporter
usage was not permitted.
In
2381
, the
USS
Cerritos
visited New Axton in search of
Nick Locarno
, an expelled
Starfleet Academy
cadet
known to operate out of the
capital city
and believed by
Starfleet
to be being targeted by the pilot of an
unknown vessel
responsible for a recent series of starship thefts. Knowing the local attitude towards Starfleet,
Captain
Carol Freeman
took an
away team
down in full
uniform
, where they provoked an
information broker
into selling data on Locarno's whereabouts to anyone
other
than Starfleet. The information was then purchased by a
bounty hunter
, who was actually
Lieutenant Commander
Andy Billups
in disguise.
The away team arrived at
Locarno's hangar
to find him long gone.
Lieutenant jg
Sam Rutherford
discovered blueprints for the unknown vessel, revealing that Locarno, far from being targeted by the culprit, actually
was
the culprit. (
LD
: "
The Inner Fight
")
Locations
Pickpockets District
Mudds
Zorky's
Inhabitants
New Axton inhabitants

======================================================================
PAGE: New Bajor
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/New_Bajor
======================================================================

New Bajor
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2370
)
Location:
Gamma Quadrant
New Bajor
was the first
Bajoran
colony
in the
Gamma Quadrant
, established in
2370
.
Major
Kira Nerys
and
Dr.
Julian Bashir
assisted in the construction of the colony's
hospital
. New Bajor also boasted a rather impressive
irrigation
system. (
DS9
: "
Crossover
", "
The Jem'Hadar
")
Only a few
months
after its founding, New Bajor's inhabitants were massacred by the
Jem'Hadar
as part of a larger offensive against
Alpha Quadrant
ships and colonies.
Third
Talak'talan
told
Kira Nerys
that the colonists "fought well... for a spiritual people." (
DS9
: "
The Jem'Hadar
")
This would start a "
Cold War
" with the
United Federation of Planets
and sow the first seeds of the
Dominion War
.
In
2373
,
Garak
speculated that there might be
survivors
from the Dominion
attack
on New Bajor in an attempt to convince
Sisko
to launch a "
mission
of mercy" to the Gamma Quadrant after receiving a message from
Enabran Tain
. However, Garak and
Worf
encountered no Bajorans in
Internment Camp 371
. (
DS9
: "
In Purgatory's Shadow
")
This colony was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
(pp. 70 & 74), New Bajor was a
M-class
planet. The official
government
of the world was the New Bajor Colony. Five
years
after the colony was destroyed, the colony was resettled. In
2376
, New Bajor was admitted to the Federation. The
capital city
of this world was New Rakantha. Dominant
species
was the
humanoid
Bajorans. The
population
of this planet in
2378
was 138,000. New Bajor
orbited
a
G-class star
.
External link
New Bajor
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

in its

.

, New Brooklyn IX was listed on the
Deep Space 9
arrival roster
as the point of departure for the
USS

. (

: "

)

======================================================================
PAGE: New Chicago
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/New_Chicago
======================================================================

New Chicago
Type:
Fixed location
Status:
Inhabited
(
2285
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
New Chicago
was a fixed location within
Federation
space
in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
. This location provided
outposts
like the
Regula I
research station
with
goods
.
In the mid-
2280s
, a
cargo container
was sent by the
Federation
from New Chicago to
Regula
. This container was stored in a
cave
located deep under the
surface
of this
planetoid
. (
Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan
)
Appendix
Background information
This fixed location was only mentioned in writing.
Apocrypha
In the non-canonical sources, there were four locations named New Chicago – a
city
on
Luna
, a city on
Mars
, a city on
Cestus III
, and a planet in the Aiti system.

======================================================================
PAGE: New Earth
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/New_Earth
======================================================================

New Earth
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2372
)
Native species:
New Earth primates
Location:
Unnamed system
Delta Quadrant
For the novel series, please see
Star Trek: New Earth
.
For the Alpha Quadrant planet with this name in Latin, please see
Terra Nova
.
New Earth
was the
third
planet
of a seven-planet
star system
orbiting
a
yellow dwarf
star
in the
Delta Quadrant
.
The ambient
temperature
was sufficient to allow outdoor bathing for at least a part of the
year
. The planet differed from Earth in that
plasma storms
occurred randomly in the
upper atmosphere
.
The
name
of the planet was coined by
Captain
Kathryn Janeway
and
Commander
Chakotay
in
2372
, when they were forced to leave the
USS
Voyager
and live on the planet because of a rare
viral
infection that they'd contracted from an
insect
bite.
Janeway and Chakotay set up a
modular shelter
to serve as a permanent home. Although these facilities were basic, they were in keeping with their low-tech environment. The area contained a
forest
and at least one
river
, and was home to a type of burrowing insect, and a
primate species
, one of those primates who started following Janeway, alerting her to an incoming ion storm even though only successful at second time with Janeway first being preoccupied observing the creature attempting to communicate with it.
Janeway designated different areas of the planet; notably, one she designated Beta 4, which she used in function of her
insect trap
research.
The
Vidiians
were also aware of the planet and happened to have developed an anti-viral agent to combat the disease. (
VOY
: "
Resolutions
")
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 85), the New Earth system was a
binary star system
with a
G-class star
and a M-class star. The
USS
Voyager
visited this system on
stardate
49690. The
Federation
starship
traveled 70
light years
; then, on stardate 49892, the
Voyager
returned to New Earth to pick up Janeway and Chakotay.
External link
New Earth
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

CATEGORIES: Planets

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain

.
New France
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
(
3196
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Location:
Alpha Quadrant
New France
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Alpha Quadrant
.
History
This world was home to the
Federation
colony
New France
.
In the
32nd century
, New France was a
Federation
member
planet. It was listed with other members on a video wall in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
The Life of the Stars
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet was within the enclosed region. (
SA
: "
Rubincon
")
Stellar cartography
In
3195
, this planet's position was labeled on a
Federation
star chart
which was displayed in
Starfleet Academy
's
Stellar Cartography
laboratory
. (
SA
: "
Beta Test
")
In
3196
, during the
Omega-47
crisis, the location of New France was depicted on star charts and readouts used across several locations on the
USS
Athena
to locate, monitor, and respond to the Omega-47 mines laid by the Venari Ral. (
SA
: "
300th Night
", "
Rubincon
")
This planet was only mentioned in writing.
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I") and
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("History of the United Federation of Planets"), New France was listed as a Federation member in
2378
.
From the same sources, New France's system was depicted as a
binary
system with a
K-type
star and a
G-type
star. Furthermore, this system was shown to be in the vicinity of the
Arachnid Nebula
.

======================================================================
PAGE: New Kentanna
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/New_Kentanna
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
New Kentanna
AKA:
Draylon II
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Location:
Draylon
system
Bajoran
sector
Alpha Quadrant
New Kentanna
was the
inhabited
M-class
second
planet
in the
Draylon
system
. This planet was in the
Bajoran
sector
,
Alpha Quadrant
. The planet was near
Sefalla Prime
.
Draylon II had a stable
orbit
, temperate
climate
, and very low
surface
radiation
. In addition, this world had good
soil
for
farming
, a long
growing season
and mild
winters
.
History
New Kentanna was originally an
uninhabited
world named
Draylon II
.
In
2370
, the
Skrreea
came through the
Bajoran wormhole
and believed
Bajor
to be
Kentanna
, their mythical sanctuary. The
Bajoran Provisional Government
was forced to turn them away as
Bajor
was struggling to provide for its own people, before even considering an additional three million Skrreea.
Commander
Benjamin Sisko
of
Starfleet
and
Major
Kira Nerys
of the
Bajoran Militia
suggested Draylon II as an alternative, but the Skrreea initially refused. However, after one of them,
Tumak
, was killed while attempting to land on Bajor forcefully, they reluctantly accepted the offer to settle on Draylon II. (
DS9
: "
Sanctuary
")
Eventually, the planet was formally renamed to New Kentanna. (
SA
: "
300th Night
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet was within the enclosed region. (
SA
: "
Rubincon
")
Stellar cartography
In the
3190s
, this planet's position was labeled on a
Federation
star chart
which was displayed in
Starfleet Academy
's
stellar cartography
laboratory
. (
SA
: "
Beta Test
")
In
3196
, the location of Draylon (New Kentanna) was depicted on star charts and readouts used across several locations on the
USS
Athena
to locate, monitor, and respond to the Omega-47 mines laid by the Venari Ral. (
SA
: "
300th Night
", "
Rubincon
")
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 47) and
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Federation Historical Highlights, 2161-2385"), by
2378
, Draylon II had been renamed to New Kentanna. In the late
24th century
, it was an independent system.
External link
New Kentanna
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

holy city of

======================================================================
PAGE: New Qo'noS
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/New_Qo'noS
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
New Qo'noS
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Klingons
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Satellite(s):
One moon
Location:
Beta Quadrant
New Qo'noS
, formerly known as
Faan Alpha
, was an
inhabited
class M
planet
. Originally located in
Federation space
, it was colonized by the
Klingons
as their new
homeworld
following the devastation of
Qo'noS
in
the Burn
. It was
orbited
by a single
moon
. (
SA
: "
Vox In Excelso
")
Environment
Faan Alpha was virtually identical to Qo'noS in
temperature
,
topography
,
volcanic emissions
, and
geothermal energy
. Its environment was such that the Klingons were the only known
species
who would have any interest in settling it. (
SA
: "
Vox In Excelso
")
History
In
3195
,
Starfleet Command
determined Faan Alpha to be an ideal new
homeworld
for the Klingons. Many ships of the
Klingon diaspora
had already taken refuge in Faan Alpha's
sector
, but despite their worsening circumstances the Klingons ardently refused the Federation's offer of the planet itself. As Klingon
General
Obel Wochak
explained to
Captain
Nahla Ake
, the
Great Houses
would never accept such
charity
and would only unite over conquest, just as Qo'noS had been conquered by
Kahless the Unforgettable
.
Jay-Den Kraag
, the only Klingon
cadet
at
Starfleet Academy
, suggested a risky solution that would respect his people's
honor
and traditions. The
USS
Athena
arrived at Faan Alpha with five other
starships
, while
Fleet Admiral
Charles Vance
declared that the Klingon ships in the sector threatened Federation security and would henceforth be regarded as enemies. Recognizing their "
declaration of war
" for what it was, Wochak summoned the Klingon
fleet
and engaged. Following a perfunctory
exchange of fire
that dealt no damage to either side, Starfleet stood down and Wochak claimed Faan Alpha as
spoils of war
. Afterwards, Vance formally surrendered Faan Alpha to the Klingons, who began to settle their new homeworld. (
SA
: "
Vox In Excelso
")
By the
following year
, the planet had been officially renamed to New Qo'noS.
During
Nus Braka
's broadcasted "
trial of the Federation
", the
Venari Ral News Network
claimed that New Qo'noS had signaled willingness to discuss a
nonaggression pact
with the
Venari Ral
. However the planet was still within the area enclosed by the
Omega-47 mine
barrier, indicating that the Venari Ral were negotiating in bad faith. (
SA
: "
Rubincon
")
Because of the nature of the broadcast, the information about New Qo'noS' nonaggression pact may be a falsification by the Venari Ral.
Stellar Cartography
In
3196
, during the
Omega-47
crisis, the location of Faan Alpha was depicted on star charts and readouts used across several locations on the
USS
Athena
to locate, monitor, and respond to the Omega-47 mines laid by the Venari Ral. (
SA
: "
Rubincon
")

======================================================================
PAGE: New Sydney
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/New_Sydney
======================================================================

New Sydney
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Independent
Status:
Habitable
(
2375
)
Location:
Sappora
system
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
New Sydney
was the primary
inhabited
planet
in the
Sappora
system
. It was an independent planet where
Starfleet
had no
jurisdiction
. It was also the birthplace of
Ezri Tigan
. (
DS9
: "
Honor Among Thieves
", "
Prodigal Daughter
", "
Field of Fire
")
The planet had its own
law enforcement agency
, the
New Sydney Police
, whose control extended to the other planets in the system.
Lieutenant
Fuchida
was one such
police officer
who worked for it. The planet also suffered problems with
pollution
–
Miles O'Brien
, while investigating a
murder
on the planet, remarked that a
body
found in a
river
had been partially
decomposed
by organic solvents in the river.
By the
2370s
, the
Orion Syndicate
had begun to establish itself on the planet, represented by
Thadial Bokar
. However, Syndicate member
Liam Bilby
considered New Sydney a safer home for
his wife
and
children
than
Farius Prime
. (
DS9
: "
Honor Among Thieves
", "
Prodigal Daughter
")
Due to the vast
mining
in the system, New Sydney was inhabited and visited by a large variety of
species
, including
Farians
,
Humans
,
Trill
,
Ferengi
, and
Nausicaans
.
Lieutenant junior grade
Ezri Dax
traveled to
Sappora VII
to use her mother
Yanas Tigan
's influence with the New Sydney Police to help locate
Chief
Miles O'Brien
, who went missing on New Sydney in
2375
. (
DS9
: "
Prodigal Daughter
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 79 noted that the episode "
Honor Among Thieves
" suggested New Sydney was an
Earth colony
.
External link
New Sydney
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

PAGE: New Vulcan

Alternate reality
(
split 2233
)
New Vulcan
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Confederacy of Surak
Status:
Habitable
(
23rd century
)
Native species:
Vulcan
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
"
I have already located a suitable planet on which to establish a Vulcan colony.
"
–
Spock Prime
to
Spock
,
2258
(
Star Trek
)
New Vulcan
was an
alternate reality
Vulcan
colony
founded by
Spock Prime
as a home for the estimated ten thousand survivors of the
destruction of Vulcan
perpetrated by
Nero
in
2258
.
The
Spock
of this timeline thought that, logically, he should resign his
commission
in
Starfleet
to help restore Vulcan civilization, but his "older self" urged him to remain, so as not to be denied the potential accomplishments made with his
friendships
with the
crew
of the
USS
Enterprise
, specifically
James T. Kirk
. (
Star Trek
)
Within a year, New Vulcan had become the capital of the
Confederacy of Surak
. In
2259
, the
Enterprise
contacted this planet, where Spock Prime revealed the true nature of
Khan Noonien Singh
to the crew. (
Star Trek Into Darkness
)
In
2263
, Spock Prime
passed away
on New Vulcan. The younger Spock again considered resigning his commission and returning to New Vulcan to help rebuild the Vulcan race, but after
defeating
Krall
at
Starbase
Yorktown
, decided to remain in Starfleet and accompanied the others when the
USS
Enterprise
-A
was launched. (
Star Trek Beyond
)
Appendices
Appearances
Star Trek
films
:
Star Trek Into Darkness
(First appearance)
References
Star Trek
films
:
Star Trek
Star Trek Beyond
Background information
New Vulcan was classified as a
class M
planet by the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 162.
Apocrypha
New Vulcan is one of the settings of the
2013
Star Trek
video game, which takes place between the events of
Star Trek
and
Star Trek Into Darkness
. In the game, it is invaded by the
Gorn
, leading to the destruction of infrastructure and loss of life.
T'Mar (
β
), Spock, and Kirk on New Vulcan
Spock and T'Mar facing the colony
According to the "
Legacy of Spock
" arc of the
Star Trek: Ongoing
series, more than twenty-seven million Vulcans survived the destruction of Vulcan and planned on relocating to
Ceti Alpha V
. It wasn't until Ambassador Spock initiated a mind meld with the Vulcan elders that the Vulcans learned of Ceti Alpha V's eventual fate, and decided to settle on the second planet of the Simon-316 system instead. Spock Prime has a monument made there in his likeness that is still standing
three thousand years later
.

New Vulcan
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek

======================================================================
PAGE: Ni'Var
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ni'Var
======================================================================

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Ni'Var
AKA:
Vulcan
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Vulcan High Command
Coalition of Planets
United Federation of Planets
(
3196
)
Ni'Var Government
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Satellite(s):
None
↓
Native species:
Vulcan
Romulan
Location:
In
orbit
of
40 Eridani A
Vulcan system
Vulcan space
Vulcan sector
Beta Quadrant
~16
light years
from
Sol
For the
alternate reality
counterpart, please see
Vulcan (alternate reality)
.
For the Vulcan starship, please see
Ni'Var (starship)
.
"
I am home and I had almost forgotten its beauty.
"
–
Spock
,
2237
("
Yesteryear
")
Ni'Var
, formerly
Vulcan
, was an
inhabited
M-class
planet
orbiting
the
star
40 Eridani A
in the
Vulcan system
. The planet was located in
Vulcan space
within the
Vulcan sector
of the
Beta Quadrant
. Though the planet had no
moons
, it did form a
binary pair
with
T'Khut
and was considered its companion planet. (
TOS
: "
The Man Trap
", "
Amok Time
";
TAS
: "
Yesteryear
";
Star Trek: The Motion Picture
;
VOY
: "
In the Flesh
";
ENT
: "
Strange New World
";
DIS
: "
Lethe
", "
Magic to Make the Sanest Man Go Mad
", "
Unification III
";
SNW
: "
Charades
";
PRO
: "
Starstruck
")
Vulcan was a founding member of both the
Coalition of Planets
in
2155
and the
United Federation of Planets
in
2161
. During the
Dominion War
of the late
24th century
, Vulcan's strategic importance was on par with worlds like
Andor
,
Berengaria VII
, and
Earth
. In the post-
Burn
era, the planet – now called Ni'Var – became an independent world with the planetary
government
resolving
centuries
-old issues of reunification between the
Romulans
and the Vulcans. In
3189
, after the mystery of the Burn was solved and a repeat prevented, Ni'Var commenced negotiations to rejoin the Federation, doing so
the following year
. (
ENT
: "
Zero Hour
", "
Demons
", "
Terra Prime
", "
These Are The Voyages...
";
DS9
: "
In the Cards
", "
In the Pale Moonlight
";
DIS
: "
Unification III
", "
That Hope Is You, Part 2
", "
All Is Possible
")
Ni'Var was the
homeworld
of the
Vulcans
and the
ancestors
of the
Romulans
, who left the planet to
colonize
a new world in the wake of
Surak
's reforms thousands of years ago. (
TOS
: "
The Man Trap
", "
Balance of Terror
")
Planetary features
Vulcan from orbit in 2154
The USS
Enterprise
enters orbit around Vulcan in 2267
Vulcan had a considerably thinner
atmosphere
, and higher
temperature
than Earth. Its
climate
was generally harsh, with most of the
surface
consisting of large
deserts
or
mountain ranges
, along with scattered small
seas
. Desert areas were prone to large amounts of heat, light, and electrical
sandfire storms
. By the mid-
23rd century
, the phrase "hot as Vulcan" had become part of the vernacular. When
Doctor
Leonard McCoy
visited the planet in
2267
, he came to understand what the
phrase
meant. (
ENT
: "
The Forge
", "
Awakening
", "
Kir'Shara
";
TOS
: "
Amok Time
")
Vulcan was divided into
provinces
and
districts
. There were several tourist attractions on Vulcan, including active
volcanoes
, ancient ruins, and
lava fields
. When
Charles Tucker
visited the planet in
2154
, he didn't know which site he would look at first. (
ENT
: "
Home
";
TNG
: "
Gambit, Part II
",
remastered
)
Other points of interest include religious sites such as the
Temple of Amonak
, the
Temple of T'Panit
, and the
T'Karath Sanctuary
. (
VOY
: "
Hunters
", "
Riddles
", "
Ashes to Ashes
";
ENT
: "
The Forge
";
TNG
: "
Gambit, Part II
")
Vulcan had several
wilderness preserves
. (
DS9
: "
Life Support
")
Aurorae over Ni'Var in 3189
The Vulcan
sky
was typically
red
or
beige
in
color
, though on occasion it appeared
blue
. (
TOS
: "
Amok Time
";
ENT
: "
Strange New World
";
Star Trek
)
Aurorae
sometimes appeared over the planet. (
DIS
: "
Unification III
")
