Nibia
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2380
)
Satellite(s):
Several moons
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Nibia
was a
planet
with
multiple moons
. This planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
In
2285
, during his mission of vengeance against
James T. Kirk
,
Khan Noonien Singh
swore that he would chase Kirk "'round the moons of Nibia and 'round the
Antares maelstrom
and 'round
perdition
's flames" before giving up. (
Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan
)
In
2380
,
Ensign
Beckett Mariner
, while talking in her
sleep
, mumbled "
Buried alive…
marooned
for eternity… moons of Nibia.
" (
LD
: "
Envoys
")
Appendices
Background information
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
Apocrypha
In the
2015
Star Trek: Ships of the Line
calendar
, one of the moons of Nibia was a small,
cratered
world fortified by multiple large bases. Multiple
Federation
Saber
-class
starships once waged an assault upon this moon in the
24th century
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Nibiru
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Nibiru
======================================================================

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Nibiru
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Native species:
Nibiran
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Nibiru
was an
M-class
planet
, and the
homeworld
of the
Nibirans
, a primitive
pre-industrial society
. While the planet resembled
tropical
regions of
Earth
, it possessed red instead of green
flora
. It was also the home of an unknown species of docile quadruped mammals and large fish.
Alternate reality
In the
alternate reality
, the
USS
Enterprise
was sent to survey the planet in
2259
, where the crew discovered a
volcano
was going to erupt and render all life on the planet
extinct
. Though the
Prime Directive
forbade interfering in the planet's natural history,
Captain
James T. Kirk
decided to save Nibiru and its inhabitants.
The volcano on Nibiru
To avoid revealing themselves to the natives, a disguised Kirk entered the Nibiran settlement and
stole
their sacred scroll, drawing them into a chase.
McCoy
appeared with a quadruped to ride, but Kirk accidentally
stunned
it, forcing both of them to flee on foot and dive into the sea. Meanwhile,
Sulu
and
Uhura
rappelled
Spock
in an
environmental suit
down into the volcano's magma chamber from a shuttlecraft to stop the eruption with a
cold fusion
device.
From the
Enterprise
's
bridge
, Kirk, McCoy, Uhura, and Sulu learned they would not be able to
beam
Spock out of the magma chamber due to interference from the volcano. Despite Spock's demands to leave him while the device detonated so as to avoid breaking the Prime Directive any further, Kirk opted to lift the
Enterprise
out of the sea and fly to the volcano to get in range. Spock was beamed out in time, and the detonation flash-froze the magma chamber and rendered the volcano inert.
Although the Nibirans saw the
Enterprise
and began worshiping the starship as a god, Kirk covered up the incident in his
captain's log
, prompting Spock to write an accurate report.
Admiral
Christopher Pike
admonished Kirk on his return to Earth, accused him of "playing god", and informed him the Admiralty were taking the
Enterprise
from him and sending him back to
Starfleet Academy
. (
Star Trek Into Darkness
)
See also
Nibiru wildlife
Nibiran language
Background information
Creating the planet
Production Designer
Scott Chambliss
noted that everyone involved in
Star Trek Into Darkness
wanted the film to begin on a tropical planet which felt special and different from Earth.
[1]
"
The guys had written it as a beautiful island,
" remembered Chambliss, "
and it sounded like Fiji or somewhere like that, quite gorgeous and lush. The one thing that I immediately didn't want to do was go to
some place
that looked like a great vacation spot and shoot this.
" (
Star Trek Magazine
issue 172
, p. 67) Prop designer
John Eaves
observed that, as the film developed and subsequent revisions were made to its
script
, the "village shrunk in size drastically to fit budget constraints."
[2]
Bearing in mind that Nibiru was to seem particularly alien, Scott Chambliss thought, "
Well, we don't want it to be green.
"
[3]
In retrospect, he continued, "
So what are tropical environments other than green? They are lush and green, green, green, green. I thought, 'Let's flip that and go red, red, red, red. Let all the vegetation be red.' That was inspired by one of my favorite plants I see in Hawaii, which is a kind of bamboo.
" (
Star Trek Magazine
issue 172
, p. 67) This impetus was lipstick bamboo, portions of whose trunks are typically magenta.
Conceptual artwork featuring the red vegetation then started to be created. Scott Chambliss experimented with the idea of using the color via digital artwork. Said Chambliss, "
I took a photograph of timber bamboo, a beautiful jungle of it, and played with it in Photoshop and turned it really deep ruby red.
"
[4]
He further recalled, "
I just started with an assistant messing around in Photoshop, messing around with bamboo forests and inverting the colors so that they become this lush ruby red. In flipping some of the other tones around, they became this mossy golden color. Suddenly, it felt like a tropical world, but was utterly different than what we've seen.
" (
Star Trek Magazine
issue 172
, p. 67) Chambliss deemed the result "beautiful."
[5]
Concept images showing extensive views of the planet, featuring the natural red materials, were made available.
[6]
[7]
It was decided that
Industrial Light & Magic
would develop digital treatments to turn foliage into shades of red. (
Cinefex
, No. 134, p. 77)
The filmmakers experimented with the option of shooting the Nibiru scenes on location. Multiple locations were considered. (
Star Trek Magazine
issue 172
, p. 48;
Cinefex
, No. 134, p. 77) Several backlots were also viewed. (
Cinefex
, No. 134, p. 77)
One site scouted by Director
J.J. Abrams
was Hawaii, but he opted not to film the sequence there, as
color grading
the plants did not produce satisfactory results. "
In a funny way, I wanted to make it work,
" conceded
Roger Guyett
. "
I was kind of intrigued by it. The problem is of course, the jungle has many, many shades of green and it’s being lit by natural light which is not like a green highlight particularly, and it’s exterior, it’s very hot. We tried – we did a whole bunch of tests and thought there would be some mileage in there. What could work about this? Well, I felt, if you did that it would feel weird. Maybe that’s OK, because you’re in a
Star Trek
world. The thing is, it didn’t just look weird, it also looked very kind of false. Kind of an electronic thing and not natural.
"
[8]
The idea of moving the production company to a fairly distant location, incurring substantial logistical cost, was replaced by a more local approach. "
But there isn't any real jungle in Southern California,
" related J.J. Abrams, "
and the more 'foresty' it became, the less interested I was in trying to turn pine needles red.
"
J.J. Abrams suggested building a small section of red jungle as a practical set, to be created at
Raleigh Studios
'
Playa Vista
lot. This plan was inspired by Abrams having been amazed by noticing, while working on
Lost
, how quickly plants obscure the distance. "
My gut feeling was,
" he recalled, "
we wouldn't need much foliage, and we could put up a
bluescreen
behind that to extend it, if we needed.
" (
Cinefex
, No. 134, p. 77) Regarding the red-colored vegetation that had been decided on, Roger Guyett concluded, "
At the end of the day, we realized that to really successfully do something like that, we had to build part of the planet.
"
However, budget concerns were raised over the construction of the set. "
We ended up building a very small piece of forest, literally enough for them [Kirk and McCoy] to get up to speed and run,
" said Roger Guyett. (
Star Trek Magazine
issue 172
, p. 48) The set was outdoors, contained foliage props as well as part of the volcano, and was constructed in
Marina del Rey
in
California
.
[9]
Noted Guyett, "
We even had a little cliff edge that was probably three feet high.
" This set piece was used for the scene showing Kirk and McCoy, as portrayed by
Chris Pine
and
Karl Urban
respectively, diving into the Nibiran water. (
Star Trek Magazine
issue 172
, p. 48) The area of the set which included the alien-looking foliage was a 75-by-100-foot patch of forest, designed by Scott Chambliss and with vegetation comprised of bamboo and red leaves. (
Cinefex
, No. 134, p. 77)
Jeremy Raymond
, who portrayed the lead Nibiran, commented, "
Walking onto the Nibiru set for the first time was as close as I’ve ever come to being actually transported to an alien world. Everything was so wonderfully constructed, with a keen eye to detail, that you would walk up to any part of the set and scrutinize it closely and it would still seem real.
"
[10]
Due to the smallness of the set, much of the views of Nibiru had to be rendered digitally by the film's visual effects team. "
Just about every shot of the forest, everything past 25 feet, is us,
" explained Roger Guyett. "
It was a huge endeavor.
" (
Star Trek Magazine
issue 172
, p. 48) "
That being said,
" clarified Jeremy Raymond, "
there was far less
green screen
on
Star Trek
than most people might think.
"
[11]
Using
CGI
, ILM modeled a Nibiran temple and rendered volcanic terrain based on US Geological Survey data of Mount St. Helens. The visual effects group also generated jungle extensions, creating an entirely digital jungle for the film's opening shot, which descends through the clouds above Nibiru. "
We spent a couple of days photographing trees in different lighting scenarios,
" remembered
Barry Williams
, "
That enabled us to place trees behind the set wherever we needed, and we used those textures as reference for modeling. We had 20 types of trees and variations on each of those, making 60 unique trees. We randomized scales and rotations, and that created a really nice organic look.
" (
Cinefex
, No. 134, p. 77) The digital parts of the jungle were rendered in V-Ray.
[12]
The scene depicting Kirk and McCoy jumping off a cliff into water below additionally required much visual effects. Roger Guyett explained, "
Chris Pine and Karl Urban are literally jumping three feet [from the artificial cliff edge] onto a mat. Everything you see over the edge is basically CG.
" (
Star Trek Magazine
issue 172
, p. 48) As reference, the film's production crew filmed a down-angle of a cliff on the Californian coast. ILM then digitally manipulated the footage to exaggerate distance to the ocean. (
Cinefex
, No. 134, p. 77)
Trivia
When quizzed for an in-universe reason the
Enterprise
was hidden underwater,
Roberto Orci
said a "
line of sight [was] necessary given [the] unstable and shifting magnetic field of [the] super volcano on alien planet. That's why no beaming. Gotta physically get back to the ship.
"
[13]
Alex Kurtzman
came up with the image of the ship rising out of the sea, but they acknowledged fans would find it questionable so they wrote Scotty's line questioning Kirk's decision.
[14]
The docile animal Kirk shoots is a disguised version of the
Drakoulias
from
Star Trek
. "
We gave it a shave and a spray tan,
" remembered
Paul Kavanagh
, "
and we re-rigged it with some new techniques we've developed to handle quadropeds, which gave us better animation controls.
" (
Cinefex
, No. 134, p. 77) According to the website FXGuide, the filmmakers dubbed the Nibiran version of the animal "Niborilla".
[15]
However, according to
Cinefex
(No. 134, p. 77), this name is spelt "Nibirilla".
A deleted scene would have stated that Nibiru was newly discovered and the
Enterprise
mission was the one establishing that there was intelligent life there.
[16]
According to
Lauren Polizzi
, the area of Nibiru shown was a volcanic
island
.
[17]

======================================================================
PAGE: Nimbus III
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Nimbus_III
======================================================================

Nimbus III
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2376
)
Satellite(s):
At least two
moons
Location:
Nimbus
system
In the
Federation-Romulan border
Unnamed minor quadrant
Beta Quadrant
"
We're already prisoners here on this worthless lump of rock!
"
–
St. John Talbot
,
2287
(
Star Trek V: The Final Frontier
)
Nimbus III
was the
inhabited
third
planet
in the
Nimbus
star system
. This
Class M
planet was
orbited
by at least two
moons
. Nimbus III was established as a "Planet of Galactic Peace" in
2267
after the
Federation-Klingon War
. Located in a
minor quadrant
in the
Beta Quadrant
, this planet was found inside the
Federation-Romulan border
. (
Star Trek V: The Final Frontier
)
History
In 2267, after the war, the
United Federation of Planets
and the
Klingon
and
Romulan
Empires agreed to mutually develop the planet, in the hopes that it would serve as a model for a new age of
peace
and understanding. An
advertisement
broadcasted from the planet claimed it to be "
…famous for its wildlife and the
fishing
is terrific.
"
Individuals
who wished to move to this planet could finance their trip through
Federation Federal
. In reality, the planet was a
desert
world and the
colonists
chosen for the undertaking were among the most unscrupulous individuals in the galaxy, quickly taking to fighting amongst each other, fashioning their own crude
weapons
after the planetary
government
forbade weapons from them.
Paradise City at night
In
2287
, the
Human
St. John Talbot
was the Federation
representative
stationed on Nimbus III;
General
Korrd
was the
Klingon
representative, and
Caithlin Dar
the
Romulan
. Their "
office
" was in the backroom of the
The Watering Hole
saloon
. All three were captured and
telepathically
brainwashed by the renegade
Vulcan
Sybok
when his "
Galactic Army of Light
" took over the planet's
capital city
and only settlement,
Paradise City
. By taking the representatives hostage, his plan was to attract a
starship
to the planet, that he would commandeer to take them all to the planet thought to be
Sha Ka Ree
.
The
USS
Enterprise
-A
and
Klaa's Bird-of-Prey
responded to the hostage crisis. The
Enterprise
-A was commandeered by Sybok.
Captain
Klaa
followed them, after intercepting a
distress call
from Captain
James T. Kirk
. (
Star Trek V: The Final Frontier
)
In
2384
,
Lieutenant jg
Barniss Frex
of
relay station
CR-721
worried that the crew of the newly arrived
USS
Protostar
had been sent by
Starfleet
to transfer him somewhere even worse, like Nimbus III. (
PRO
: "
Asylum
")
People
See
:
Nimbus III inhabitants
Stellar cartography
Nimbus III in relation to
Daran V
,
Dimorus
,
Kaldra IV
, and
Peliar Zel
Nimbus III was located in the
Romulan Neutral Zone
, and in the same quadrant as the
Sol system
. (
Star Trek V: The Final Frontier
)
In
2371
, the
location
of Nimbus III was labeled in the
star chart
Data
and
Picard
were studying in
stellar cartography
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. It was also labeled on a star chart in
astrometrics
on
USS
Voyager
in
2376
. (
Star Trek Generations
,
okudagram
,
VOY
: "
Child's Play
",
okudagram
)
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
The Next Generation
".
Appendices
Background information
In a
deleted scene
from the
Star Trek: The Next Generation
episode "
Family
", the
hologram
of
Jack R. Crusher
stated that one of
Wesley
's
ancestors
was a
horse
thief
on Nimbus III.
[1]
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 83 classified Nimbus III as a
class M
planet.
Apocrypha
The computer
game
Star Trek Online
originally placed the Nimbus system in the Nimbus sector (
β
) at the junction between the Federation, the Klingon Empire and the Romulan Star Empire. Its location was subsequently changed to be very near the
Romulus system
, and far from the Klingon border.
Several
Star Trek Online
missions
take place on Nimbus III, which was depicted as having been taken over by the
Orion Syndicate
by 2409. The
Tal Shiar
and
Elachi
also had a secret base there.
In the
Federation: The First 150 Years
, the
idea
of cooperating with the Klingon and Romulan Star Empires in colonizing and developing Nimbus III was the brainchild of
Ambassador
Robert Fox
. It was stated that Fox later "regretted the whole thing," and James Kirk was quoted as saying "most of us knew Nimbus III was a
joke
."
External link
Nimbus III
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Noble Isle
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Noble_Isle
======================================================================

Noble Isle
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2384
)
Satellite(s):
At least one
moon
Location:
Romulan Neutral Zone
Beta Quadrant
Noble Isle
was an
inhabited
class M
planet
located in the
Romulan Neutral Zone
. It had at least one
moon
and was surrounded by a
debris
field. The planet had a turbulent
atmosphere
with perennial
ion storms
and dangerous
lightning
, which interfered with
spacecraft
navigation
and
transporters
. This seclusion made it a haven for unregulated and unsanctioned
scientific research
. The only way to or from the surface was via a
space elevator
, which functioned as the planet's
starport
.
The streets of Noble Isle
In
2384
,
Thadiun Okona
was hired by
Doctor
Jago
to deliver
gelatinous putrescine
to her
lab
on Noble Isle. En route, Okona met and joined the crew of the
USS
Protostar
, whom he later guided to the planet so they could effect repairs on the ship's
protostar drive
. During their stay, a
Romulan
Tal Shiar
team led by
Commander
Kaseth
attempted to seize the
Protostar
, but were unsuccessful. (
PRO
: "
Masquerade
")
Noble Isle is the name of the titular location in H. G. Wells' novel
The Island of Dr. Moreau
, which is also about a scientist who performs experiments frowned upon by society in an attempt to augment other species.

Nollman's Planet
Type:

Status:

(

Nollman's Planet
was a
planet
in the
Beta Quadrant
.
In
2293
, Nollman's Planet's position was labeled in the
star chart
for the "
Operation: Retrieve
" command briefing. The chart was from the
Starfleet Command Intelligence Database
. This star chart was seen in the
office
of the
Federation President
on
Earth
. (
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
,
set artwork
)
This planet's quadrant of origin is inferred based on the position of neighboring locations –
Starbase 24
and
Rura Penthe
– as seen in the
star charts
appearing in the
Star Trek: Discovery
episode "
Magic to Make the Sanest Man Go Mad
" and in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
This chart was seen in greater resolution at threads at Flare.Solareclipse.net.
[1]
(X)
[2]
(X)
Many of these locations shared their names with the last names of crew who worked on the sixth film. This location was probably named after the set designer
Eugene C. Nollman
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Norcadia Prime
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Norcadia_Prime
======================================================================

Norcadia Prime
Type:
Planet
Native species:
Norcadians
Location:
Delta Quadrant
Norcadia Prime
, or simply
Norcadia
, was the
inhabited
prime
planet
in its
star system
. The planet was located in the
Delta Quadrant
, and was near the
Pendari system
. The planet was the
homeworld
of the
Norcadians
, a
warp-capable
humanoid
species
.
Norcadia Prime was noted for its beautiful
beaches
and some of the finest
museums
in the
sector
. Among these museums was the
Norcadian Museum of Entomology
, which in
2376
had a special exhibit on
beetle
larvae
from the
equatorial
subcontinent
. The planet generated a huge percentage of its economic revenue through the
sport
tsunkatse
.
The
USS
Voyager
visited Norcadia Prime in 2376 for
shore leave
. (
VOY
: "
Tsunkatse
")
In
VOY
: "
Collective
",
Mezoti
described her homeworld as a
Theta-class
planetoid
in a
binary star system
, with a population of 260 million. However, it was never made clear if this was Norcadia Prime or a colony. The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 87 believed it was the former.
According to the
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 92), the Norcadia system was a single star system, with an
A-class star
.

.

2153
,
Enterprise
visited a
deuterium
station there while searching for the
Xindi
. While on Norellus,

Jonathan Archer
met
B'Rat Ud
, a
V'radian
chemist
who promised to sell Archer the formula for synthesizing
trellium-D
if they met at B'Rat's shop on the
Xanthan planet
. (

: "
Rajiin
")

======================================================================
PAGE: O'Ryan's Planet
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/O'Ryan's_Planet
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
O'Ryan's Planet
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
3195
)
Native species:
Shamin
Location:
Alpha Quadrant
O'Ryan's Planet
was an
inhabited
warp-capable
planet
in the
Alpha Quadrant
. This was the
homeworld
of the
Shamin
. It was one of five planets in its
star system
.
In the
2250s
, this planet had or was in the process of being inducted into the
United Federation of Planets
. This was after a successful
first contact
.
Stellar cartography
In
2259
, the location of this planet was labeled on a
stellar cartography
chart that was seen on the
USS
Enterprise
's
ready room
viewscreen
. This planet's symbol had a
blue
color
, indicating that it was affiliated with the
United Federation of Planets
. (
SNW
: "
Strange New Worlds
", "
Spock Amok
")
On
stardate
1292.4, the location of this planet was labeled in a chart used during a
Section 31
Alpha Team
briefing
. (
Star Trek: Section 31
)
The planet's location was labeled in a
Federation star chart
that was in
Fleet Admiral
Kirsten Clancy
's
office
at
Starfleet Headquarters
in
2399
and on the
bridge
of the
USS
Titan
-A
in
2401
. O'Ryan's Planet was in or near to Federation space. (
PIC
: "
Maps and Legends
", "
The Next Generation
", "
Disengage
")
In
3189
, the location of O'Ryan's Planet was denoted on a
holographic
star chart
of the galaxy at
Federation Headquarters
. (
DIS
: "
Die Trying
")
In
3195
, this planet's name appeared on a
video wall
in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
Kids These Days
")
This planet was only mentioned in writing.
O'Ryan's Planet was first developed by
Robert Fletcher
for
Star Trek: The Motion Picture
, as part of the backstory for the
Shamin
species (
The Making of Star Trek: The Motion Picture
, p. 132). In an interview published as part one of "The
Star Trek
Costumes" in the
February 1980
edition of
Fantastic Films
, Fletcher said of the Shamin:
"
This person comes from O'Ryan's planet discovered by
Paddy O'Ryan
in the late 22nd century. The planet, like its people, are in an early stage of social and technological development. It will be a long time, if ever, before they reach the capabilities of space travel. Their society is like the society of
America's western Indian civilizations
. The similarities are particularly striking when one realizes that no travelers from Earth, in recorded history, had ever set foot on the planet until Paddy's discovery of it.
"
[1]

======================================================================
PAGE: Ocampa (planet)
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ocampa_(planet)
======================================================================

Ocampa
AKA:
Ocampa homeworld, Ocampan homeworld
Class:
M
(formerly)
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Ocampa
Kazon-Ogla
Status:
Intact
(
2371
)
Native species:
Ocampa
Location:
Ocampa system
Delta Quadrant
Ocampa
, also known as the
Ocampa homeworld
or the
Ocampan homeworld
, was an
inhabited
fifth
planet
in the
Ocampa
system
. Situated near the
Caretaker's array
, this
Delta Quadrant
planet was located approximately fifty thousand
light years
from the
galactic core
and seventy-five thousand light years from the
Badlands
in the
Alpha Quadrant
. The
Ocampa
were native to this
world
.
Ocampa had all the characteristics of a
class-M planet
, with the sole exception of the absence of
nucleogenic particles
, which left the planet's
atmosphere
incapable of producing
rain
and turned its
surface
into a
desert
without a single
river
or
ocean
.
The city beneath Ocampa's surface
An underground garden
The barrier protecting the Ocampa city
This was not the planet's natural state (in fact, it still had dry
riverbeds
), but the result of an accident during the exploration of the planet by a group of
Nacene
ca. 500 Ocampan
generations
ago. The Ocampa referred to this era as "
the Warming
". The Nacene took responsibility for their error by constructing a vast underground
city
, two
miles
below the surface and protected by security barriers, to give the Ocampa access to the only water left on the planet. The Ocampa were brought to this city by
tunnels
. They also left behind two members of the exploration team –
Suspiria
, and an
individual
who the Ocampa came to know as the
Caretaker
– to care for the race.
Given that Ocampan females enter the
elogium
at age four to five, five hundred Ocampan generations might correspond to approximately two thousand to twenty-five hundred
years
. A statement by
Richard James
seems to corroborate this, as he referred to a set of ruins on the planet's surface as being two thousand years old (as of
2371
). (
The Official Star Trek: Voyager Magazine
issue 1
, p. 71)
Although short of
water
, the planet was rich in
cormaline
deposits. The
Kazon-Ogla
sect maintained an encampment on the planet's
southern continent
, near a dry riverbed at the foothills of a range of extinct
volcanoes
to mine its resources.
Jabin
was the
leader
of this encampment in the early
2370s
.
An image of Ocampa as displayed by a
Class 2 shuttle
In
2371
, the
USS
Voyager
was transported to the area near Ocampa. Shortly before the Caretaker passed away, it was said that the Ocampa had enough surplus energy to keep the city running for
five years
. (
VOY
: "
Caretaker
", "
Before and After
") At around this time,
Kes
, a female Ocampa, returned to the planet. (
VOY
: "
Fury
")
In
3189
, the location of Ocampa was denoted on a
holographic
star chart
of the galaxy at
Federation Headquarters
. (
DIS
: "
Die Trying
")
Appendices
Appearances
Star Trek: Voyager
"
Caretaker
" (
Season 1
)
"
Before and After
" (
Season 3
)
"
Fury
" (
Season 6
)
Background information
Due to the premise of
Star Trek: Voyager
, the Ocampa homeworld could not regularly be shown as a setting in the series. (
Star Trek: Voyager - A Vision of the Future
, p. 164) Scenes of the underground Ocampa city were filmed at the
Los Angeles Convention Center
.
[1]
The surface scenes were filmed at the
El Mirage Dry Lake Bed
.
[2]
The underground city on Ocampa was designed by
Robert Stromberg
. ("The Art of Alien Worlds",
VOY Season 4 DVD
special feature)
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 78), Ocampa (Ocampa V) was classified as a
class H planet
. Its
capital city
was Ocampa (
β
). The dominant
species
on this planet was the
humanoid
Ocampans. In
2378
, there were an estimated 230 million Ocampans living on this world. The planet's surface was devastated by the Nacene in the
14th century
.
External link
Ocampa V
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

PAGE: Oceanus IV

======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Oceanus IV
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
3195
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Oceanus IV
was the
inhabited
fourth
planet
in its
star system
. This planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
In
2368
,
Starfleet
added a
diplomatic
mission
to Oceanus IV to the mission roster for the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. This addition cut down the time to explore the
Phoenix Cluster
from five
weeks
down to two. (
TNG
: "
The Game
")
In
3195
, this planet's name appeared on a
video wall
in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
Kids These Days
")
Background information
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I"), Oceanus IV was listed as a
Federation
member
in
2378
.
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 99 identified Oceanus IV as a Federation planet.
This location shares a name with the
titan
Oceanus
. This may be a possible namesake - alternatively, this may imply it is an
ocean world
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Ockmenic 9
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ockmenic_9
======================================================================

Ockmenic 9
Class:
Brigadoon-type
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2381
)
Satellite(s):
None
Native species:
Ockmenians
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Ockmenic 9
was the
inhabited
ninth
planet
in its
star system
, and the
homeworld
of the
Ockmenians
. It was a
Brigadoon-type planet
that existed in another
dimension
for the most part and phased into normal
space-time
for only two
hours
per
year
. It did not have any
moons
. (
LD
: "
The Stars At Night
")
Astronomical data
Planetary data for Ockmenic 9
Ockmenic 9 had a
radius
of 5,834
kilometers
and an
equatorial circumference
of 43,933 kilometers. It had a
mass
of 6.94334
e23
×10
23
kilograms
, with a
density
of 5.522
grams per cubic centimeter
, yielding a
surface gravity
of 980
centimeters per second squared
and an
escape velocity
of 11.18
kilometers per second
. Its
orbit
had an
eccentricity
of 0.01245233 and an
inclination
of 0.00023
degrees
, with
sidereal rotation
and
orbital periods
of 0.933232
days
and 1.02322
years
, respectively. The
mean surface temperature
was 291
Kelvin
. (
LD
: "
The Stars At Night
")
History
As of the late
24th century
, the Ockmenians were aware of the
Federation
and had an agreement with
Starfleet
to receive supplies during the brief window that Ockmenic 9 existed in normal space. In
2381
,
Captain
Carol Freeman
convinced
Starfleet Command
to make this task the third leg of a "
second contact
race" between her ship, the
USS
Cerritos
, and the newly introduced
automated starship
USS
Aledo
, so as to prove the continued viability of the
California
-class
. The
Aledo
delivered its supplies to Ockmenic 9 as scheduled, whereas the
Cerritos
, due to a delay at
LT-358
, arrived too late to do the same before the planet phased into its other dimension, thereby losing the race. (
LD
: "
The Stars At Night
")
External link
Ockmenic IX
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

Ogat
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Klingon Empire
Status:
Habitable
(
2370
)
Location:
Ogat system
Beta Quadrant
Ogat
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Ogat
system
, in the
Beta Quadrant
.
In the
24th century
, this
Klingon
planet
was home to a
training academy
, where young Klingons studied the way of the
warrior
. The academy course was difficult, but the
teachers
were excellent.
In
2370
,
K'mtar
suggested that
Alexander Rozhenko
should leave
his father
and attend the academy on Ogat in order to better train for a warrior's life. Worf ultimately refused, and Alexander remained on the
USS
Enterprise
-D
a while longer. (
TNG
: "
Firstborn
")
This planet's quadrant of origin is inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Discovery
episode "
An Obol for Charon
".

at

, the wiki for licensed

Ogus II
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2367
)
Native species:
Cove palm
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Ogus II
was the
inhabited
second
planet
in its
star system
. This planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
The indigenous
cove palm
plant
produced a
fruit
inhabited by deadly
parasites
that were extremely
toxic
and infectious to
Humans
.
The
USS
Enterprise
-D
visited Ogus II for a two-day
liberty
in early
2367
.
Brothers
Jake
and
Willie Potts
played at the
laser duel
game in the
east
arcade
. Jake tricked his younger brother into believing that he had been seriously hurt by Willie, causing Willie to hide in a
forest
and ingest the fruit from a cove palm. The boy needed to be
quarantined
and rushed to
Starbase 416
for treatment. (
TNG
: "
Brothers
")
In
2371
, the
location
of Ogus II was labeled in the
star chart
Data
and
Picard
were studying in
stellar cartography
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. (
Star Trek Generations
,
okudagram
)

at

, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek

at

, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek

PAGE: Omega Cygni
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Omega_Cygni
======================================================================

Omega Cygni
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
23rd century
)
Native species:
Omega Cygni natives
Location:
Alpha Quadrant
Omega Cygni
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Alpha Quadrant
, and the
homeworld
of the
Omega Cygni natives
.
History
In the mid-
23rd century
,
Harry Mudd
visited Omega Cygni, where he turned a handsome
profit
selling the natives their own
oceans
. (
TAS
: "
Mudd's Passion
")
Stellar Cartography
This planet's system was visible from
Earth
in the
constellation
Cygnus
.
In
2293
, the
location
of Omega Cygni in the
Milky Way Galaxy
was labeled in a
star chart
that was in
Captain
James T. Kirk
's
quarters
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-A
. (
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
,
okudagram
)
Appendices
Background information
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
There is a typo on the "Explored Galaxy" image, and the planet's name written there is "Omega Cyngi".
In the final draft of the
script
for
TAS
: "
The Time Trap
",
Star Trek Concordance
(p. 290),
Star Trek Log 4
and
Star Trek: SCE
,
Magen
was from a planet located in the
Omega Cygni system
.
In the novelization of "Mudd's Passion" in
Star Trek Log 3
, the name "Omega VII" was used of this planet.
Apocrypha
The novels
Beneath the Raptor's Wing
and
Synthesis
suggest that Omega Cygni was related to
Cygnet XIV
.
External links
Omega Cygni
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

at
Wikipedia

at

======================================================================
PAGE: Omega IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Omega_IV
======================================================================

Omega IV
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2268
)
Native species:
Omega IV natives
(The
Kohms
and the
Yangs
)
Location:
Omega
system
Omega
sector
Beta Quadrant
Omega IV
was the
inhabited
fourth
planet
of the
Omega
system
. This
Class M
planet was located in the
Omega
sector
,
Beta Quadrant
.
This was the
homeworld
of the
Omega IV natives
, a
pre-warp
humanoid
species
. This species was divided into two ethnic groups, the
Kohms
and the
Yangs
, who had fought a
biological war
centuries
ago. (
TOS
: "
The Omega Glory
")
History
While the planet had different
land masses
from
Earth
, Omega IV shared a
history
nearly identical to Earth's, up to the
1990s
or slightly beyond. Omega IV once had a
nation
called "The United States" similar to Earth's
United States of America
, with the same handwritten
Constitution of the United States
, the
Pledge of Allegiance
and
United States flag
identical to that of Earth's.
The stand-off on Omega IV between the two
superpowers
had escalated into open biological warfare. One of the primary weapons of the war was
a virus
that disrupted the
body
at the
molecular
level, separating
water
molecules from other
organic
compounds until there was nothing of the body left but dehydrated
crystals
. The virus was still present on the planet in the
23rd century
, though only a brief exposure to the
atmosphere
at that time was needed to provide a lasting immunity to the virus. The Asiatic Kohms, originally called "Communists", won the war against the Caucasian Yangs originally called "Yankees". As the survivors of the war adapted to the toxic environment, they acquired a naturally long
life span
. They could live to be more than a thousand years old.
The Yangs were driven from their
cities
, and took to the ways of the
native Americans
for survival. By the 23rd century,
civilization
on Omega IV had returned to the
iron
-age level. The Yangs, dressed in tribal attire, lived in the
hills
and
mountains
surrounding the ruined cities. The cities were inhabited by the Kohms, who dressed similarly to Earth's Mongolians. The Yangs were attempting to take their old cities back from the Kohms.
Captain Ronald Tracey on Omega IV
In
2268
,
Captain
Ronald Tracey
of the
starship
USS
Exeter
beamed
down with a
landing party
to survey the planet for research purposes. He found the
Kohm city
and established friendly relations with the Kohms. However, when some of the landing party beamed back to the ship, they carried the virus back with them. Within a short time the entire crew of the
Exeter
was killed.
Driven insane by the death of his entire crew and the loss of his ship, Captain Tracey sided with the Kohm villagers in their conflict with the Yangs. He used his
phaser
to drive off a Yang attack in violation of the
Prime Directive
. When the
USS
Enterprise
arrived to investigate,
Captain
Kirk
refused to help Tracey in the battle against the Yangs. He took Tracey away from Omega IV and allowed its people to rediscover their own "history and liberty." (
TOS
: "
The Omega Glory
")
Stellar cartography
The location of Omega IV on "The Explored Galaxy" star chart
In
2293
, the
location
of Omega IV in the
Milky Way Galaxy
was labeled in a
star chart
that was in
Captain
James T. Kirk
's
quarters
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-A
. (
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
,
okudagram
)
Appendices
Background information
The planet was recreated by
CBS Digital
for the
2007
remastered version of "The Omega Glory".
In a deleted scene from the episode, the landing party speculated that the Yangs and Kohms might, in fact, be
Human
.
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 104 classified Omega IV as a
class M
planet.
Apocrypha
In the
Star Trek Maps
, (p. 28) Omega IV had a
population
of 130,000 and was located in the same Omega system that contained
Holberg 917G
, which the
Enterprise
visited in
TOS
: "
Requiem for Methuselah
" to obtain
ryetalyn
for treatment of an on-board outbreak of Rigellian fever. It was widely believed that the planet started out as a lost Human
colony
that had reverted to
barbarism
.
In the
Star Trek: Department of Temporal Investigations
novel
Forgotten History
, the American paraphernalia on Omega IV were not indigenous documents, but in fact replicas left behind by the crew of ECS
Philadelphia
(
β
) in the late-
2140s
, to inspire the native Yangs in the struggle for freedom against the Kohms.
In the
novel
Forged in Fire
, a key component in the virus used by
The Albino
to murder the
sons
of
Kang
,
Kor
, and
Koloth
came from Omega IV. The Albino also infected
Sulu
with the virus, but Sulu was immune to it because he had visited Omega IV.
External links
Omega IV
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Planets in TOS and TOS Remastered
at
Ex Astris Scientia

======================================================================
PAGE: Omekla III
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Omekla_III
======================================================================

Omekla III
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Cardassian Union
(
24th century
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Location:
Omekla
system
Alpha Quadrant
Omekla III
was the
inhabited
third
planet
of the
Omekla
system
,
Alpha Quadrant
.
History
In the
24th century
, this planet was within the bounds of
Cardassian
space
. In
orbit
of this world, there was a
shipyard
administered by the
Cardassian military
.
In
2371
,
Thomas Riker
set
course
for the shipyards at Omekla III after he destroyed Cardassian
Outpost 47
with the stolen
USS
Defiant
.
Riker's strategy changed after the ship was damaged by
Major
Kira Nerys
, and the
Defiant
was forced to take cover in a
nebula
while undergoing repairs. Following the completion of repairs, the
Orias system
became the ship's new target. (
DS9
: "
Defiant
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of the Cardassian Union as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
In the
script
, the pronunciation for Omekla was given as "OH-mek-la". Unlike in the episode, where the
Defient
did not travel to the Omekla system, in the script, the
Defiant
had entered the system and had attacked the Omekla III shipyard. These lines were deleted from the aired episode.
[1]
External link
Omekla III
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Omicron Ceti III
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Omicron_Ceti_III
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Omicron Ceti III
AKA:
Omicron III, Omicron Seti III
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Habitable
(
3195
)
Location:
Omicron Ceti
system
Alpha Quadrant
~300
light years
from
Sol
Omicron Ceti III
, also known as
Omicron III
or
Omicron Seti III
, was the
inhabited
third
planet
in the
Omicron Ceti
system
. This
Class M
planet was located in the
Alpha Quadrant
and was approximately 300
light years
from
Sol
.
This planet was suitable for
colonization
by
humanoid
lifeforms
if the colonists were able to counter the harmful side effects of
Berthold rays
. It was once considered the perfect planet for an
agricultural
colony
, as its
soil
would grow anything planted in it. The
climate
was moderate, having a moderate amount of
rain
all year round. Unfortunately, this perfection was marred by the fact that the planet was bombarded by Berthold rays at such an intensity that any
animal
life
could only survive for a
week
.
History
In the
23rd century
, multiple expeditions were made to colonize the planet, but all failed as the Berthold rays were not yet known to
Federation
science
. One of those attempts, the
Omicron colony
, led by
Elias Sandoval
, made planetfall in
2264
. This group was able to survive because of the colonists' discovery of the
pod plants
and their mysterious
spores
, which protected the colonists from the Berthold rays.
On stardate 3417 in
2267
, the
USS
Enterprise
arrived at the planet, following the discovery of Berthold rays, to ascertain the exact
fate
of the colonists. Upon learning that the colonists were still alive,
Captain
James T. Kirk
and
Commander
Spock
were able to develop a method of freeing them from the influence of the spores. The colonists were evacuated to
Starbase 27
. (
TOS
: "
This Side of Paradise
")
In the
2340s
, on
stardate
22927.5,
Lieutenant
Anaanda Ziff
was born on Omicron Ceti III. This lieutenant's
personnel file
appeared in a
hallucination
of
Deanna Troi
's. (
TNG
: "
Eye of the Beholder
"
display graphic
)
In
2402
,
Deanna Troi
considered Omicron Ceti III as a destination for a
beach
vacation
with
William T. Riker
. (
PIC
: "
The Last Generation
")
In
3195
, this planet's name appeared on a
video wall
in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
Kids These Days
")
Appendices
Background information
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system from real-world sources.
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 37, "United Federation of Planets I", "United Federation of Planets II") and
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Federation Historical Highlights, 2161-2385"), Omicron Ceti III was listed as a
Federation member
in
2378
.
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 104 classified Omicron Ceti III as a
class M
planet. This
reference work
stated that of the 150 colonists who made the trip to this planet in the 2260s, approximately fifty of them were still alive when the
Enterprise
came to check up on them. This could be based on the fact that the starship's
sensors
detected one settlement on the planet's surface. Initially there were three settlements, with Sandoval's settlement having 45 individuals. Although it is implied in the episode that the other two settlements are active in 2267, this was never directly confirmed in the episode.
External link
Omicron Ceti III
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Omicron IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Omicron_IV
======================================================================

Omicron IV
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
1968
)
Native species:
Omicron IV natives
Location:
Omicron
system
Omicron
sector
Beta Quadrant
Omicron IV
was the
inhabited
fourth
planet
in the
Omicron
system
. This planet was located in the
Omicron
sector
,
Beta Quadrant
.
Omicron IV went to a crisis that was similar to the one experienced by
Earth
in
1968
, when the
United States
launched a
nuclear warhead platform
to maintain the
balance of power
after another
major power
placed a
nuclear warhead
in
sub-orbit
. Omicron IV was almost destroyed due to this.
When Gary Seven was confronted with the 1968 situation on Earth, he called it "the same kind of nonsense" as to what happened on Omicron IV, which the
Beta 5 computer
agreed was a correct
analysis
. (
TOS
: "
Assignment: Earth
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
In the novelization of this episode by
James Blish
in
Star Trek 3
, this planet was named Omicron III (
β
).
External link
Omicron III
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Omicron Lyrae
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Omicron_Lyrae
======================================================================

Omicron Lyrae
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2257
)
Satellite(s):
At least three
moons
Location:
Alpha Quadrant
Omicron Lyrae
was a
planet
with at least
three moons
.
In the
23rd century
, the third moon of Omicron Lyrae hosted an
Ankeshtan K'til
Vulcan
criminal rehabilitation center
. (
SNW
: "
The Serene Squall
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
The planet's quadrant of origin was inferred from the location of other known planets and systems which were associated with the constellation Lyra.

======================================================================
PAGE: Omicron Theta
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Omicron_Theta
======================================================================

Omicron Theta
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2364
)
Satellite(s):
Two
moons
Location:
Omicron Theta
system
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
For the similarly named planet, please see
Theta Omicron IV
.
Omicron Theta
was a formerly
inhabited
planet
in the remote
Omicron Theta
system
. This planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
and had two
moons
. (
TNG
: "
Datalore
")
History
Omicron Theta was the
location
of an
Earth
colony
, the
Omicron Theta colony
. By
2328
, this planet was a destination on commercial
transport
routes. In the colony,
Dr.
Noonien Soong
and his
wife
Juliana
perfected their
positronic brain
and successfully created a series of
Soong-type androids
, including
Data
and
Lore
. At least one
archaeological
dig was conducted in the
mountains
. The colony also contained
farmland
. (
TNG
: "
Datalore
", "
Silicon Avatar
", "
Inheritance
")
An archaeology dig would imply that there had once been an indigenous
civilization
on Omicron Theta, or that the planet had been colonized by another civilization many
years
prior.
In
2338
, after the planet was attacked by the
Crystalline Entity
, the
cruiser
USS
Tripoli
picked up a signal, which led the
starship
to this planet. The cruiser reported finding a world with farmlands and a
landing party
traced the signal to a
device
on the
surface
. Near the signal device,
the party
discovered Data, who was activated by the device and told by
officers
that he was an
android
.
In
2364
, the
USS
Enterprise
-D
visited the planet for a few
hours
. The ship's
sensors
found a
class M
world with lifeless
vegetation
and devoid of
insects
and
soil
bacteria
. An
analysis
by an
away team
from the starship ascertained that at the time the
Tripoli
discovered Data, everything on the planet was dead or dying. (
TNG
: "
Datalore
", "
Rightful Heir
")
Inhabitants
See
:
Omicron Theta colony inhabitants
Andrew Probert
illustrated a handful of
drawings
to show how the set for Dr. Soong's
laboratory
might look. However, at least one of these concept artworks was labeled "Kiron III," the original
name
used for the planet. (
Star Trek: The Magazine
Volume 2, Issue 12
, p. 49) (The planet's moniker was also Kiron III in the
novelization
of
Encounter at Farpoint
). In the
script
for "
Datalore
", the planet was identified as Data's Planet. Omicron Theta was the name of the system, not the planet.
[1]
This changed in "
Silicon Avatar
" when the planet, for the first time, was canonically identified as Omicron Theta. "ohm-ih-kron THAY-tah" was the pronunciation for this planet's name from the script pronunciation guide.
[2]
The planet model for this world was later used to represent
Ornara
in "
Symbiosis
".
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I"), in
2378
, Omicron Theta was a
Federation member
.
External links
Omicron Theta
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Re-Used Planets in TNG
at
Ex Astris Scientia

======================================================================
PAGE: Oomaru
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Oomaru
======================================================================

Oomaru
Type:
Astronomical
object
Status:
Intact
(
2285
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Oomaru
was an
astronomical
object
in the same
region
as
Sol
/
Jupiter
. Its companion was
Yaan
.
In
2285
, Oomaru's position was displayed on a
star chart
on the
bridge
of the
USS
Enterprise
. (
Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan
)
This astronomical object was only mentioned in writing.
The image was taken from page T0:02:07:02 of the
Star Fleet Technical Manual
by
Franz Joseph
. The map claimed to depict principal
star systems
in the
Federation
. Based on the scale for the chart, Oomaru was located over 2
parsecs
from Sol. Oomaru and Yaan were stars in a
binary
star system.
External link
Oomaru
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Oonla
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Oonla
======================================================================

Oonla
was an inhabited location.
Michael Burnham
and
Cleveland Booker
once delivered
nitrium
to Oonla while they were working together as
couriers
in
3188
, during which Booker blew up some
noodle stands
. Burnham recalled the incident in
3190
, complaining that she was picking noodles out of her
braids
for days. Booker pointed out that it had been either the noodle stands or the
fusion reactor
. (
DIS
: "
The Examples
")

The Operation Retrieve star chart
The command briefing for
Operation Retrieve
included a
star chart
which located many prominent celestial bodies and the location of three
starships
in
Federation
and
Klingon
space. This chart was divided into squares. A thin black line demarcated the
border
between the two interstellar governments. (
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
)
A coordinate system has been arbitrarily assigned for this chart. The x-axis starts at A and ends at P (left to right), and the y-axis starts at 1 and ends at 7 (top to bottom).

======================================================================
PAGE: Ophiucus III
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ophiucus_III
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Ophiucus III
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
(
3196
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Location:
Ophiucus system
Beta Quadrant
~19
light years
from
Sol
Ophiucus III
was the
inhabited
Class M
third
planet
of the
Ophiucus
system
. Located in the
Beta Quadrant
, this planet was approximately 19
light years
from
Sol
.
History
In the mid-
2260s
, it was a frontier planet.
In
2266
, there was an
Earth colony
on Ophiucus.
Women
were scarce enough at this
colony
that intergalactic
rogue
Harcourt Mudd
saw an opportunity in bringing them in from other worlds. (
TOS
: "
Mudd's Women
";
TAS
: "
Mudd's Passion
")
In
2328
, it was the point of embarkation for passengers traveling to
Organia
via commercial
transport
.
Keven Scotti
,
Michael J. Lim
, and
Zoey Pentacoff
traveled there on the
SS
Kogin
. (
TNG-R
: "
Inheritance
",
okudagram
)
In the
32nd century
, Ophiucus III was a
Federation
member
planet. It was listed with other members on a video wall in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
Kids These Days
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet was within the enclosed region. (
SA
: "
300th Night
")
Appendices
Background information
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Discovery
episode "
An Obol for Charon
".
In the final draft
script
of "Mudd's Women", references to this planet in the final version of that episode were instead to
Ophiucus VI
.
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 108 and
StarTrek.com
classified Ophiucus III as a
class M
planet. (The authors believed it was Ophiucus III which was mentioned in "Mudd's Women", not Ophiucus VI.)
[1]
(X)
The
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I") listed Ophiucus III as a
Federation member
in
2378
.
The current location of Ophiucus III in the Beta Quadrant was based on the most recent star charts seen in
Star Trek: Strange New Worlds
and
Star Trek: Section 31
.
External links
Ophiucus III
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Ophiucus VI
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ophiucus_VI
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Ophiucus VI
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Location:
Ophiucus
system
Beta Quadrant
~19
light years
from
Sol
Ophiucus VI
was the
inhabited
sixth
planet
in the
Ophiucus
system
. This planet was located in the
Beta Quadrant
and was approximately 19
light years
from
Sol
.
In the mid-
23rd century
,
Harry Mudd
visited Ophiucus VI, where he conned two
miners
out of a
year
's supply of
dilithium crystals
using fake
Federation
vouchers
. (
TAS
: "
Mudd's Passion
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this system was within the enclosed region. (
SA
: "
300th Night
")
Appendices
Background information
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
The current location of Ophiucus VI in the Beta Quadrant was based on the most recent star charts seen in
Star Trek: Strange New Worlds
and
Star Trek: Section 31
.
Ophiucus VI was an early
name
for the planet which became
Ophiucus III
. As such, Ophiucus VI was repeatedly referred to in the final draft
script
of
TOS
: "
Mudd's Women
". In the
novelization
of "Mudd's Passion" (as published in
Star Trek Log 3
), Harry Mudd referred to this planet as a "charming frontier world."

======================================================================
PAGE: Oran'taku (planet)
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Oran'taku_(planet)
======================================================================

Oran'taku
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
22nd century
)
Native species:
Oran'taku
Location:
Delphic Expanse
Oran'taku
was an
inhabited
planet
, and the
homeworld
to the
humanoid
Oran'taku
, located in the
Delphic Expanse
about one half
light year
from the
Xanthan planet
.
Rajiin
claimed to have been born on Oran'taku. She was later
kidnapped
and sold into
slavery
. In
2153
,
Jonathan Archer
promised to try and locate the planet, and, if possible, return her to it. (
ENT
: "
Rajiin
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Orbital Drydock Facility
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Orbital_Drydock_Facility
======================================================================

Orbital Drydock Facility
Type:
Spacedock
Owner:
United Earth
Operator:
Starfleet
Status:
Active
(
2154
)
The
Orbital Drydock Facility
was a
Starfleet
spacedock
where the first
warp five
starship
,
Enterprise
NX-01
, was constructed in the mid-
22nd century
.
Service record
Enterprise
launching from the Orbital Drydock Facility
Enterprise
was launched from this facility early on a mission to the
Klingon homeworld
in
2151
. (
ENT
: "
Broken Bow
")
In
2153
,
Enterprise
was
retrofitted
in this
spacedock
to upgrade her for the mission to find the
Xindi
and stop a second
weapon
from being launched against Earth. (
ENT
: "
The Expanse
")
In May
2154
,
Enterprise
departed from this spacedock shortly before investigating the
Augment Crisis
. (
ENT
: "
Borderland
")
Layout
The Orbital Drydock Facility was made up of five cage segments, with one long section providing the
dorsal
part of the cage and two smaller sections on either side providing the
port
and
starboard
parts. These sections housed banks of lights facing inwards, towards where the ship would be located. The dorsal sections also supported a small windowed station in the center of the facility as well as rails that four movable construction arms were attached to. Housed in the center joint of the smaller sections making up the walls of the cage were small stations. Each station had a total of four
airlocks
at the
bow
and
stern
, one facing outwards along the centerline where other segments could be added as well as one airlock each facing port, starboard, and
ventral
. An airlock was also located on the centerline facing "outwards" from each station. One of the segments on the port side also had a large antennae farm located just below the station. (
ENT
: "
Broken Bow
", "
The Expanse
", "
Borderland
")
Interior of the spacedock, facing a window near the middle of the facility
Another view of the spacedock's interior, facing away from the same window
The underside of the spacedock's dorsal section, including the construction arms and a windowed location with a docking port
Appendices
Appearances
Star Trek: Enterprise
"
Broken Bow
"
"
The Expanse
"
"
Storm Front, Part II
"
"
Borderland
"
Background information
This docking facility may be part of the
Warp Five Complex
, but this possibility was never confirmed. The facility was referred to simply as a "
Spacedock
" in the
script
of
Star Trek: Enterprise
pilot episode "
Broken Bow
".
[1]
In a blueprint of the
Enterprise
's bridge that was auctioned off in the
It's A Wrap!
sale and auction
(item #4654), the spacedock is referred to as Orbital Drydock Facility 314, Station 15 B.
[2]
(X)
The
CGI model
for the
Orbital 6
inspection pod
used in "Broken Bow" also had markings on the side reading "Orbital Drydock Facility" and "
Shipyard Ops
". This is where Memory Alpha gets the name from.
Exterior design
The first concept sketch that
John Eaves
created for the spacedock
While the look of the spacedock was not yet decided, about four conceptual passes at designing the dock were tried out. The first of these, dated
February 2001
, was decidedly contemporary while also heavily influenced by the chronological setting of
Star Trek: Enterprise
. Senior illustrator
John Eaves
, who was involved in designing the dock, later explained, "
We were designing a new segment in time just a few decades prior to
the original series
. The idea was to retro back everything from the
60′s
iconic series to a more
NASA
and nuts and bolts kind of look. With that in mind we ventured into a new yet older style of spacedock/drydock design with a
skylab
feel to the aesthetics. The result of this path [was] [...] a large circular
space station
with massive solar arrays and domed details to bring us all back into a more present time of space architecture with a slightly futuristic feel.
" However, the designers instead finally settled on a more chunky version of
the traditional drydock
as established in
Star Trek: The Motion Picture
.
[3]
Pierre Drolet
, a visual effects artist at
Foundation Imaging
, was largely responsible for deciding how the spacedock turned out. "
He just said, 'Let me see what I can come up with,' and ultimately [the producers] went for it,
" remembered Foundation's
Robert Bonchune
. "
Pierre built something that was sort of a step backward from
The Motion Picture
drydock. A little beefier, a little more like
the dry dock
from
Generations
." (
Star Trek Monthly
issue 86
, p. 23) The dock included some other minor details that helped differ it from previous docks, such as the one in
The Motion Picture
. "
It is still a drydock shape; it's just clunkier and a little more primitive,
" said Bonchune. "
We put in little things like
grappler
hooks at the bottom to move things around. There are little details like gold foil and thermal blankets on some things.
" (
Star Trek: The Magazine
Volume 2, Issue 10
, p. 26)
Production illustrator
Doug Drexler
was not entirely satisfied with the final exterior design of this facility, commenting, "
The fiver seemed as big and complex (perhaps even more so) than the drydock from TMP.
" Drexler also admitted to having "never understood" the large antennae farm included as part of the five-fingered facility.
[4]
(X)
Depiction in launch sequence
Executive producers
Rick Berman
and
Brannon Braga
, in their capacity as the writers of "Broken Bow", originally wanted a vast stadium to be witness to the starship
Enterprise
's departure from the dock, though these scenes were thereafter moved to being set on an observation deck of the spacedock itself. "
We always imagined a stadium full of people, but you never quite end up with as much as you want,
" acknowledged Braga. ("Broken Bow"
audio commentary
,
ENT Season 1 DVD
)
The spacedock's observation deck was actually a redress of the
Enterprise
armory
set. ("Broken Bow"
text commentary
,
ENT Season 1 DVD
) The interior scenes set in this room were filmed on
5 June
2001
, on
Paramount Stage 18
. (
Broken Bow
call sheet)
Subsequently, the spacedock's observation deck was optically enlarged; visual effects added a secondary balcony to the room as well as more audience members than the number of extras who had been on the set. ("Broken Bow" text and audio commentaries,
ENT Season 1 DVD
) "
When we saw the dailies, Rick Berman decided that the event should be bigger and have more people, so I figured out a way I could hand-paint set extensions,
" recalled visual effects producer
Dan Curry
. "
By reducing and tweaking perspective and shooting background people against
greenscreen
, we were able to build layers of audience and make the room bigger than the original set had been.
" Curry was ultimately very proud of the launch sequence, which Rob Bonchune also worked on. (
Star Trek: The Magazine
Volume 2, Issue 10
, pp. 26-27)
Other representations
A
CGI
render of this spacedock can be found in the
ENT Season 1 DVD
, as part of a computer-animated sequence that introduces the main menu for each
disc
.
Another CG depiction of the drydock was published in the book
Ships of the Line
(p. 5), in which an image by Sean Scott shows the dock housing
Enterprise
while the ship is in an initial stage of construction.
Apocrypha
In the
novelization
of
Broken Bow
, this spacedock is referred to as consisting of geodesic parts that were assembled on Earth before being constructed in space, expanding the dock to its full size therein.

======================================================================
PAGE: Orbital office complex

Orbital office complex
Owner:
United Federation of Planets
Operator:
Starfleet
Location:
In
orbit
of
Earth
Sol system
Alpha Quadrant
Status:
Active
(
2270s
)
The
orbital office complex
was a
Starfleet
space station
in
orbit
of
Earth
during the late
23rd century
.
In the
2270s
,
Admiral
Kirk
was transported to the
USS
Enterprise
for the first time in two and a half years via
travel pod 05
from the orbital office complex. (
Star Trek: The Motion Picture
)
A graphic display of an orbital office complex appeared in the
USS
Defiant
's library computer
on several occasions during
2374
and
2375
. (
DS9
: "
Favor the Bold
", et al.)
The same graphic also appeared in the
USS
Voyager
's library computer
screen that was viewed by
One
after
Seven of Nine
had activated the drone's
linguistic database
, and allowed him to
assimilate
information. (
VOY
: "
Drone
")
A similar graphic was seen among the data that
Seven of Nine
assimilated in early
2376
. (
VOY
: "
The Voyager Conspiracy
")
Closeup of the center of the station
View looking down on the pods
Orbital office complex displayed as a LCARS graphic
Appendices
See also
Regula I
-type
Starbase 375
-type
Appearances
Star Trek: The Motion Picture
DS9
:
"
Favor the Bold
" (
LCARS graphic
)
"
Sacrifice of Angels
" (
LCARS graphic
)
"
Tears of the Prophets
" (

)

What You Leave Behind
" (
LCARS graphic
)
VOY
:
"
Drone
"(
LCARS graphic
)
"
The Voyager Conspiracy
" (
LCARS graphic
)
Background information
The script for
Star Trek: The Motion Picture
referred to this station as the "orbital drydock offices," further describing them "
rotating slowly but perceptibly against blue-black of space. Small figures in spacesuits working on construction, with work belts. Beyond and below… Earth.
" It further described "
the cluster is a fully-equipped dockyard engineering center and computer drafting office,
" with the location where Kirk beamed into and met Scotty, being the "inner office". Similarly, in
Gene Roddenberry
's
novelization
of
Star Trek: The Motion Picture
, the office complex was referred to as a "Centroplex".
"The long copper cylinders" on the original
studio model
According to model designer
Andrew Probert
, "the long copper cylinders" protruding from the top of the complex "
were botanical tanks, so you could grow
fruits
and
vegetables
without needing
soil
, and that in turn would help supply your
oxygen
as well as some of the station's food. That was my thinking, at the time, but I'm sure they're probably too big for that, or whatever.
"
[1]
The
okudagram
depicting the orbital office complex was originally sketched by
Doug Drexler
for the second edition of the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
.
For more information, see
studio models
.
The set for the office complex's interior, including the
travel pod
set, was constructed on
Paramount Stage 17
and cost US$60,000. (
The Making of Star Trek: The Motion Picture
, p. 95)
The set for the complex was one of the first sets from
The Motion Picture
to be taken down, following production on the film. By
5 January
1979
, not only had the set been struck for storage and folded but it had also been decided that the set would be moved to
Paramount Stage 9
for permanent storage. (
The Making of Star Trek: The Motion Picture
, p. 214)

Orbital office complex
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
R1 Administrator class
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Reuses of the orbital office complex
at
Ex Astris Scientia

PAGE: Orelious IX
CATEGORIES: Planets

======================================================================

Orelious IX
Type:
Planet
Status:
Destroyed
(
14th century
)
Location:
Orellius system
,
Alpha Quadrant
Orelious IX
was the
ninth
planet
in the
Orellius
system
. This planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha Quadrant
.
History
In the
14th century
, the
Battle of Orelious IX
was fought here, which led to the planet's destruction.
The
asteroid field
that was created from the remnants of the planet came to contain a
Menthar
booby trap, which ensnared vessels for centuries.
In
2366
, the
USS
Enterprise
-D
was exploring the
asteroid
remains of the planet, where they discovered the long dead
Promellian battle cruiser
,
Cleponji
, trapped among a field of
aceton assimilators
. Eventually, the
Enterprise
was able to escape, and destroy the trap. (
TNG
: "
Booby Trap
")
"oar-LEE-us" was the pronunciation for this planet from the
script
pronunciation guide.
[1]
Yet, at
StarTrek.com
, the pronunciation for Orelious was "oh-REE-lee-us".
[2]
(X)
External links
Orellius
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Orelious IX
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Orellius
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Orellius
======================================================================

Orellius
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Subject to the laws of the
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Native species:
Xupta tree
Location:
Orellius
system
Alpha Quadrant
Orellius
was an
inhabited
class M
planet
in the
Orellius
system
, in the
Alpha Quadrant
. This planet was 160 million
kilometers
from the
F-type star
Orellius Minor
and was far away from the
trade routes
. The
xupta tree
was native to this planet.
History
In the
2350s
,
Alixus
choose this planet for her planned
community
.
In
2360
, the
SS
Santa Maria
, a
colony ship
bound for
Gemulon V
, detected a low-level
duonetic field
on the planet's
surface
. The field had been established on the planet by Alixus, who helped design it. The field inhibited
energy
flow aboard the
starship
, causing a failure in its
life support system
. As the ship landed on the planet for repairs, the
engines
failed, resulting in a
crash landing
. The crew and passengers of the
Santa Maria
threw away parts of the ship, including
waveguides
,
conduits
, and
baffle plates
, which could not be used because of the field.
In the next ten
years
the community survived on the planet by, for example, treating disease with primitive
medicine
, finding new sources of food, and establishing a system of law and order. However, the absence of technology also resulted in multiple
deaths
, most notably as the result of dangerous
insect
bites that proved difficult to treat using only herbal medicine. Law and order took a primitive and brutal form based around authoritarian rule, with severe
punishments
such as a
punishment box
, a small and
claustrophobic
box barely large enough to hold a
Human
, in which colonists were locked for some amount of time to bake in the direct sunlight, without
water
, for
crimes
like
stealing
. However despite these issues, the colonists were in general happy with their new life. A few years before
2370
, the colonists discovered a native edible
pear
-like
fruit
growing along a
river
bank.
In
2370
,
Commander
Benjamin Sisko
and
Chief
Miles O'Brien
of the Starfleet
runabout
USS
Rio Grande
were prospecting the area for potential new colonies, when they also got stranded on the planet. They discovered that the stranding of the
Santa Maria
was not an accident, as the colonists had been led to believe, but rather an elaborate plot by Alixus. It was brought to light that she had planned everything in advance, and used a
power generator
to create the duogenic field herself, in order to bring her social theories into practice and create what she perceived as a perfect
community
. Alixus tried to manipulate the two
Starfleet
officers
into joining her community, using sexual favors and
torture
in the punishment box among other means. She also tried to auto-pilot their runabout in orbit into
the system's star
in an effort to evade further discovery. However, the runabout missed the target, and when it was eventually located by Starfleet, they were able to extrapolate its starting point. As a result, the
Federation
officially re-established contact with the colony. The deception by Alixus became fully known to the colony, too, but most thought that the community they had created was not worth giving up. (
DS9
: "
Paradise
")
The planet was not given a
name
in the episode. It was named at
StarTrek.com
and in the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 111.
[1]
(X)
This planet's quadrant of origin is inferred based on its proximity to the Bajoran system, whose position was seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
According to StarTrek.com, this system was located in
Sector 401
.
[2]
(X)

PAGE: Orendal V
CATEGORIES: Planets

======================================================================

Orendal V
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2376
)
Location:
Delta Quadrant
Orendal V
was the
fifth
planet
in its
star system
, in the
Delta Quadrant
.
In
2376
,
Lansor
claimed that the
Borg
synaptic relays
he offered
Seven of Nine
were obtained from a trader on Orendal V. (
VOY
: "
Survival Instinct
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 90), the Orendal system was located in non-aligned space. This system was a single star system. The primary was a
class M star
with a magnitude of +5, which was the same brightness as
Sol
.

An
organ bank
was a ready source of
organs
for
transplantaction
.
In
2371
,
The Doctor
didn't expect the
phage
-ridden
Vidiians
to stop chasing
USS
Voyager
, as it was "
the only organ bank within half a
parsec
.
" (
VOY
: "
Fury
")
