PAGE: Room assignment
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Room_assignment
======================================================================

A
room assignment
was the assigned
quarters
typically of an
enlisted
or
junior officer
.
In
2370
,
Ensign
Sam Lavelle
wished for a
promotion
to
lieutenant
so that he could have his own
room
. Ensign
Taurik
suggested that Lavelle request a new room assignment if he was
unhappy
sharing quarters with him. (
TNG
: "
Lower Decks
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Rooseworld
CATEGORIES: Planets

======================================================================

Rooseworld
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2293
)
Location:
Beta Quadrant
Rooseworld
was a
planet
in the
Beta Quadrant
.
In
2293
, Rooseworld's position was labeled in the
star chart
for the "
Operation: Retrieve
" command briefing. The chart was from the
Starfleet Command Intelligence Database
. This star chart was seen in the
office
of the
Federation President
on
Earth
. (
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
,
set artwork
)
This planet's quadrant of origin is inferred based on the position of neighboring locations –
Starbase 24
and
Rura Penthe
– as seen in the
star charts
appearing in the
Star Trek: Discovery
episode "
Magic to Make the Sanest Man Go Mad
" and in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
This chart was seen in greater resolution at threads at Flare.Solareclipse.net.
[1]
(X)
[2]
(X)
Many of these locations shared their names with the last names of crew who worked on the sixth film. This location was probably named after the editor
Ronald Roose
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Rosseau V
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Rosseau_V
======================================================================

Rosseau V
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
24th century
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Rosseau V
was the
fifth
planet
of its
star system
. The planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
and had an
asteroid belt
located around or near it. The
region
was unique in that it was surrounded by
neutrino clouds
that formed a
harmonic resonance
. Eventually the resonance becomes synchronous, making the
place
sound like it "has a
voice
."
In
2365
,
Wesley Crusher
took
Salia
to the
holodeck
and showed her, among other things, an environmental simulation of the asteroid belt of Rousseau V. (
TNG
: "
The Dauphin
")
The
script
spelling for this planet was "Rosseau V"
[1]
; however, several other sources, including the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 239, as well as the episode's closed captioning, spelled it as "Rousseau V."
In the same script, rather than neutrino clouds forming the harmonic resonance, it was stated that the harmonic resonance occurred after "
the
orbits
of the
moons
[became]
synchronous
.
"
Visual Effects Supervisor
Dan Curry
used
lava
rocks
he found in the buildings supply for this scene. He was very careful sorting the right rocks out. (
TNG Season 2 DVD
special feature "Departmental Briefing Year Two: Memorable Missions - The Dauphin"
)
External links
Rousseau
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Rosseau V
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Ruah IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ruah_IV
======================================================================

Ruah IV
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Non-aligned
Status:
Habitable
(
2369
)
Native species:
Proto-hominids
Location:
Ruah
system
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Ruah IV
was the
inhabited
fourth
planet
in the
Ruah
system
. This planet was classified as
M-class
. 67
%
of its
surface
was covered with
water
, and multiple
animal
species
lived on the
land mass
, including a
genus
of
proto-hominids
. As of
2369
, the planet showed no signs of any prior
civilization
.
Ruah IV was one of the planets seeded with part of a
genetic
program
by the
Progenitors
4.5 billion years ago
. In
2369
,
Professor
Galen
collected a
DNA
sample from Ruah IV as part of his quest to piece together the program before he traveled to the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. Following Galen's
death
, the
Enterprise
visited Ruah IV in an attempt to discover the
puzzle
that Galen was in the process of unraveling. Unable to ascertain why he traveled there, the
Enterprise
proceeded to Galen's next destination,
Indri VIII
. (
TNG
: "
The Chase
")

at

at

, the wiki for licensed

Rubicun III
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:

(

)
Native species:

Location:

system
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
For the similarly named planet, please see
Rubicon III
.
Rubicun III
was the
inhabited
third
planet
of the
Rubicun
star system
. This planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
and was located in a
star cluster
. Classified as
M class
, this was the
homeworld
for the
Edo
, a
pre-warp
humanoid
species
.
The Edo were protected by a trans-dimensional being who they
worshiped
as their
God
.
Data
suggested that the Edo may have been originally placed on the planet by this being. Random
punishment zones
were moved around on the planet's
surface
.
The
USS
Enterprise
-D
visited this planet after delivering
Earth colonists
to the neighboring
Strnad solar system
. (
TNG
: "
Justice
")
Beckett Mariner
once
stranded
Brad Boimler
on Rubicun III, according to Boimler, who reminded her of the incident in
2381
. She countered that he'd stranded himself. (
LD
: "
An Embarrassment Of Dooplers
")
In both the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 241 and the
Star Trek: The Next Generation Companion
, 2nd ed., p. 40, it was stated that the
surface
of Rubicun III was
shot at
the Van Nuys Japanese garden at the
Tillman Water Reclamation Plant
and the
Huntington Library
in Pasadena. The
Starfleet Academy
and
Starfleet Headquarters
scenes were also filmed at this location.
Rubicun III surface

PAGE: Rutia IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Rutia_IV
======================================================================

Rutia IV
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Non-Aligned
Native species:
Rutians
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Rutia IV
was the
inhabited
fourth
planet
of its
star system
. This
Class M
world was the
homeworld
for the
Rutians
, a technologically advanced non-aligned
warp capable
humanoid
species
. This planet had a long trading relationship with the
Federation
and was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
A banner seen on Rutia IV
The
Lumar Cafe
was a
cafe
in a
city
in the
eastern continent
.
Since
2296
, a separatist group from the
western continent
known as the
Ansata
tried to use
terrorism
to break away from the government of the eastern continent. They were demanding
autonomy
and
self-determination
for their homeland.
In
2366
, the
USS
Enterprise
-D
visited the planet to drop off medical supplies. During the visit, the
away team
witnessed first hand an act of Ansata terrorism, and
Dr.
Beverly Crusher
was
kidnapped
by the terrorists. The Rutian
government
worked with the
Enterprise
-D
crew
to free her, but during the rescue they underwent several more
attacks
before eventually retrieving Crusher. (
TNG
: "
The High Ground
")
In the
2370s
, a view of the
surface
of Rutia IV was depicted on several
viewscreens
on
Deep Space 9
's
Promenade
and
Replimat
. (
DS9
: "
Rules of Engagement
", "
A Simple Investigation
")
"RUE-tee-ah" was the pronunciation for Rutia from the script pronunciation guide.
[1]
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 243 and
StarTrek.com
classified Rutia IV as a
class M
planet.
[2]

.

, the

diplomatic

======================================================================
PAGE: Sahil's relay station
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Sahil's_relay_station
======================================================================

Sahil's relay station
Type:
Relay station
Owner:
United Federation of Planets
Operator:
Starfleet
Status:
Active
(
3189
)
Crew:
At least 1
Sahil's relay station
was a former
Federation
relay station
used as a
courier waypoint
in the
32nd century
. The station was damaged long ago, with the probable cause being a main
reactor breach
; it was located in a
debris field
that contained a wrecked
Constitution
-class
starship.
Decades
before
3188
, the
long range sensors
of the station had failed. With these sensors down, the station could only scan the nearest thirty
sectors
with access to more distant sectors restricted. In the surviving habitable sections of the station, the
furniture
and
control panels
were made of
programmable matter
.
For
forty
years
, this station was manned by
Aditya Sahil
, who every day scanned a six hundred
light year
radius
for traces of the collapsed Federation.
In 3188,
Cleveland Booker
led
Michael Burnham
to the station, looking for the
warp signature
of the
USS
Discovery
. Although the search failed, Burnham brought
hope
to Sahil and commissioned him as an
acting communications chief
tasked with finding her ship. Together, as
commissioned officers
, Burnham and Sahil raised the
flag of the Federation
by planting it on a
wall
in Sahil's
office
. (
DIS
: "
That Hope Is You, Part 1
")
In the
Star Trek: Discovery
novel
Wonderlands
, this station was referred to as Spaceport Devaloka.
External Link
Sahil's relay station
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

Sahndara
Type:
Planet
Status:
Destroyed
(

)
Native species:

Location:
In
orbit
of

Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Sahndara
was the
homeworld
of the
Platonians
. The
planet
was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
This
inhabited
planet
orbited
the
star
Sahndara
. Before the star went
supernova
, a mass
eugenics
program was instituted by the
society
. The creation of this program was thirty-eight
individuals
who were bred for their
self-reliance
,
contemplation
, and
longevity
. These individuals escaped their world before its
destruction
. (
TOS
: "
Plato's Stepchildren
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Salina Prime
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Salina_Prime
======================================================================

Salina Prime
Type:
Planet
Status:
Inhabited
(
2375
)
Location:
Delta Quadrant
You may be looking for
Selnia Prime
.
Salina Prime
was the
inhabited
prime
planet
in its
star system
, in the
Delta Quadrant
. There were military installations on the surface operated by a civilization that was at war with the
Druoda
until
2372
. The planet was protected by
subspace minefields
.
In
2375
, 34
series 5 long-range tactical armor units
were accidentally launched from the Druoda
Strategic Command Matrix
towards Salina Prime. They were stopped when one of the units, that had been damaged and repaired by the
USS
Voyager
crew, acknowledged the Druoda mission abort orders and detonated itself amid the others. (
VOY
: "
Warhead
")
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 247 identified this planet's civilization as the Salinian civilization.

Salius VI

)

Salius VI
was the
inhabited
sixth
planet
in its
star system
. It was the site of a
Federation
penal colony
in the
23rd century
.
In an
alternate timeline
where

,

in

and sent to Salius VI. She was still incarcerated there as of
2266
.
(

: "

")

PAGE: Saltah'na (planet)
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Saltah'na_(planet)
======================================================================

Saltah'na
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2369
)
Native species:
Saltah'na
(
extinct
)
Location:
Gamma Quadrant
Saltah'na
was the formerly
inhabited
fifth
planet
of
star system
in the
Gamma Quadrant
. It was the
homeworld
of the
Saltah'na
, until their
extinction
.
In
2369
, on a
bio-survey
mission
to the Gamma Quadrant, the
Klingon
Vor'cha
-class
starship
IKS
Toh'Kaht
visited the planet on the twenty-second
day
of its mission. After the planet was explored, it was rejected as a potential future site for a Klingon
colony
. The
crew
discovered the
energy spheres
which possessed a
telepathic
archive
. The archive described a power struggle which ended with the destruction of the Saltah'na. (
DS9
: "
Dramatis Personae
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
The
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 74,75) shows a
star
named Saltah'na, an
M-class star
. The system was a location visited by the Gamma Quadrant Bio-Survey (2369-75).

Salva II

(

system
Alpha Quadrant
Salva II
was the
second
planet
in the
Salva
system
,
Alpha Quadrant
.
History
The
Federation
established a
colony
on this planet.
Between
2368
and
2373
, this planet's system was in the
Demilitarized Zone
.
In
2370
, the planet was turned over the
Cardassians
in the
Federation-Cardassian Treaty
and the colonists were forced to evacuate. Most of the colonists refused Federation resettlement and moved to a
Maquis
encampment on
Marva IV
, where
Captain
Benjamin Sisko
visited in
2373
in search of Maquis
informant
Cing'ta
. (
DS9
: "

")

, the pronunciation for Salva was given as "SAL-vuh".

PAGE: Sanctuary Four
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Sanctuary_Four
======================================================================

Sanctuary Four
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
33rd century
)
Sanctuary Four
was an
inhabited
planet
. It was home to a
sanctuary
for the endangered
trance worms
. Access to this world was granted when a code, such as "
Mandelbrot Set code nautilus
", was accepted.
Cleveland Booker
worked with the personnel at this sanctuary in saving the trance worms and, by the time he delivered
Molly
in
3188
, he had successfully brought in at least one other trance worm.
In a
conversation
with
Michael Burnham
, Booker described
his planet
as being much like Sanctuary Four until he had to leave it. She had traveled with him to the planet when he delivered Molly. (
DIS
: "
That Hope Is You, Part 1
")
In
3191
, Booker planted the
World Root
cuttings
that he got from the
Eternal Gallery and Archive
on Sanctuary Four, resulting in a new World Root growing on the planet. (
DIS
: "
Life, Itself
")
By the
33rd century
, Burnham and Booker had established their family home on Sanctuary Four and the World Root cuttings had grown into a large tree. (
DIS
: "
Life, Itself
")
Appendices
External Link
Sanctuary Four
at

, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek

======================================================================
PAGE: Sappora VII
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Sappora_VII
======================================================================

Sappora VII
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
New Sydney
Location:
Sappora system
Sappora VII
was the
seventh
planet
in the
Sappora
planetary
system
, known for its richness in
pergium
. In the
24th century
, Sappora VII was under the jurisdiction of
New Sydney
, an independent planet heavily influenced by the
Orion Syndicate
. It was home to the
Tigan family
and their
mining business
.
Lieutenant junior grade
Ezri Dax
, née Tigan, traveled to Sappora VII to use her mother
Yanas Tigan
's influence with the
New Sydney Police
to help locate
Chief
Miles O'Brien
, who went missing on New Sydney in
2375
. (
DS9
: "
Prodigal Daughter
")
"Prodigal Daughter" only referred to New Sydney in the Sappora system in dialogue, though as a location different from the Tigan family home once the main characters get there. The planet was twice mentioned in script notes as Sappora VII.
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 250 also treated Sappora VII as a different planet than New Sydney.
External link
Sappora VII
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Sarnia Prime
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Sarnia_Prime
======================================================================

Sarnia Prime
AKA:
Sarnia
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2401
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Sarnia Prime
, or simply
Sarnia
, was the
inhabited
prime
planet
in its
star system
.
In
2401
, there was an outbreak of
Galarian fever
on Sarnia, which was
engineered
by local
warlords
to kill
refugees
in the free-zone in order to claim the territory. In response, the
Fenris Rangers
established a
quarantine zone
around the planet. The
SS
Eleos XII
transported
medical supplies
to alleviate the
pandemic
, with
Jack Crusher
bribing
the Rangers with
weapons
in exchange for access to the zone. However, the Rangers also surreptitiously alerted the "
Marked Woman
", whose forces subsequently attacked the
Eleos XII
; the ship fled to
Kaphar
, but was pursued. (
PIC
: "
The Next Generation
", "
Disengage
")
According to
Terry Matalas
, the name of this planet is a reference to
Sarnia
, (
Ontario
) Canada, where scenes for the city of Titan were shot for
12 Monkeys
.
[1]

Sarona VIII
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2364
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Sarona VIII
was the
inhabited
eighth
planet
in its
star system
. The planet was located soemwhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
In the
24th century
, this planet was visited by
Starfleet
crewmembers
for
shore leave
.
One of its most popular attractions was the
Blue Parrot Café
, which served blue concoctions, and was located across the
city square
from the
Zanza Men's Dance Palace
. Sometime before late
2364
,
William T. Riker
,
Deanna Troi
, and
Jean-Luc Picard
had visited this planet.
In 2364, upon the completion of its
rescue mission
at
Vandor IV
, Sarona VIII was the destination of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. (
TNG
: "
We'll Always Have Paris
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Sarpedion V
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Sarpedion_V
======================================================================

Sarpedion V
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Cardassian
Dominion
(
2373
-
2375
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Location:
Sarpedion
system
Cardassian space
Alpha Quadrant
For the sole planet of
Beta Niobe
, please see
Sarpeidon
.
Sarpedion V pointed out
Sarpedion V
was the
fifth
planet
in the
Sarpedion
system
,
Alpha Quadrant
. This planet was in
Cardassian
space
.
History
This planet was the headquarters of the Cardassian
Twelfth Order
and one of the most heavily fortified positions in the
Cardassian Union
during the
Dominion War
. At that time, a direct assault by fifteen
Vor'cha
-class
attack cruisers
was considered barely able to scratch the
planetary defenses
.
In
2375
,
Chancellor
Gowron
suggested that it should be the
Klingons
' next target despite the objections of
General
Martok
and
Commander
Worf
. Ultimately, Sarpedion was not attacked, as Gowron was killed by Worf in
ritual combat
shortly thereafter. (
DS9
: "
Tacking Into the Wind
")
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the Federation star chart appearing in the
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
episode "
300th Night
".
The map on which Gowron pointed out the planet appeared to be a Klingon analogue to a map also seen on Federation displays. Because of this, despite the Klingon writing, the blob to the left of Sarpedion can be identified as the
Badlands
, and
AR-558
and
Starbase 375
would also appear to be close.
External link
Sarpedion V
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

Sarpeid
Type:
Astronomical
object
Status:

(

)
Location:

Sarpeid
was an inhabited
astronomical
object
in the
Alpha Quadrant
.
Stellar cartography
In
2293
, Sarpeid's position in the
Milky Way Galaxy
was labeled in a
star chart
that was in
Captain
James T. Kirk
's
quarters
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-A
. (
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
,
okudagram
)
In
2365
, Sarpeid's location was labeled in a Subspace Comm Net Ops star chart, which was seen in the
courtroom
on
Starbase 173
. This location was connected to the
Sol system
by a subspace line. (
TNG
: "
The Measure Of A Man
")
This location was only mentioned in writing.
In August 2024, an annotated computer paste-up of the Subspace Comm Net Ops star chart was put up for auction in the Entertainment Memorabiliia Live Auction: Los Angeles Summer 2024 by Propstore Auction.
[1]
In
deleted lines
from the final draft
script
for
TOS
: "
All Our Yesterdays
" dated
12 December
1968
, the
name
of the
Sarpeidon natives
was given as "Sarpeids".

Sarpeidon

======================================================================
PAGE: Sarpeidon
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Sarpeidon
======================================================================

Sarpeidon
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Destroyed
(
2269
)
Native species:
Sarpeidon natives
Location:
In
orbit
of
Beta Niobe
Alpha Quadrant
For the Cardassian planet, please see
Sarpedion V
.
Sarpeidon
was the
first
and only
planet
orbiting
the
primary
Beta Niobe
. This star was located in the
Alpha Quadrant
.
This
inhabited
planet was classified as
M-class
. The planet was the
homeworld
for a pre-
spaceflight
humanoid
species
, the
Sarpeidon natives
. The planet was destroyed in the Beta Niobe
supernova
of
2269
. According to
Spock
, the planet was "
millions
of
light years
" from
Vulcan
.
Many events of Sarpeidon's past paralleled events in
Earth
history
, including an extended period of global glaciation and
paranoia
due to fears of
witchcraft
. The planet was once ruled by a
tyrant
named
Zor Kahn
.
Beta Niobe explodes, destroying Sarpeidon
In 2269,
hours
before the sun went nova, the
USS
Enterprise
arrived to warn the native species of their impending doom. However, they found that before the planet's destruction, all of its inhabitants had evacuated
back in time
, using a
time portal
known as the
atavachron
, into various eras of the planet's past. (
TOS
: "
All Our Yesterdays
";
PIC
: "
Maps and Legends
")
See also
Sarpeid
Appendices
Background information
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
For the
2007
remastered version of "All Our Yesterdays", Sarpeidon was given a CGI-makeover, now a unique planet rather than a reuse of stock footage.
In "All Our Yesterdays", Spock stated that Sarpeidon was "millions of
light years
away" from
Vulcan
.
TAS
: "
The Counter-Clock Incident
", "
The Explored Galaxy
"
star chart
, and the star chart from "Maps and Legends" established however the planet and star system were still located inside the
Milky Way Galaxy
.
Apocrypha
In the
novel
No Time Like the Past
, Spock speculated that the Sarpeidons pursued time travel rather than
space travel
as a means of escape as the lack of other planets in their solar system meant that they had no real incentive to develop space travel out of a lack of anywhere to go in their immediate vicinity. During the novel, a time-displaced
Seven of Nine
meets a projection of Nehwa, the original
inventor
of the atavachron, who revealed that he left his people for the distant past out of disgust at what his work had come to.
External links
Sarpeidon
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Planets in TOS and TOS Remastered
at
Ex Astris Scientia

Sarthong V
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2366
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Sarthong V
was the
inhabited
fifth
planet
in its
system
. Located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
, the planet was home to impressive
archaeological
ruins which were off-limits to outsiders.
Vash
considered a visit to Sarthong V in
2366
after parting company with
Jean-Luc Picard
on
Risa
. Despite his warnings about the manner in which
Sarthongians
treated
trespassers
, Vash did make the trip to the planet and was able to return with several impressive
artifacts
. (
TNG
: "
Captain's Holiday
", "
Qpid
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Saturn
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Saturn
======================================================================

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Saturn
AKA:
Sol VI
Class:
Class J
gas giant
Type:
Planet
Diameter:
120,516
kilometers
(74,914
miles
)
(
equatorial
diameter
)
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Intact
(
3189
)
Satellite(s):
Mimas
,
Rhea
, and
Titan
, plus many other moons and a
ring system
Location:
Sol system
Sector 001
Alpha Quadrant
Saturn
, also known as
Sol VI
, was the
sixth
planet
of the
Sol system
. This
Class J
gas giant
was located inside the
Sol sector
of the
Alpha Quadrant
.
Saturn was well known for its
rings
(
TOS
: "
Charlie X
";
VOY
: "
Future's End, Part II
") and was
orbited
by many
moons
; three of which were
Mimas
,
Rhea
, and
Titan
. (
TOS
: "
The Changeling
";
TNG
: "
The First Duty
";
ENT
: "
Strange New World
";
Star Trek
) It was also orbited by
navigational control satellites
. (
TNG
: "
The First Duty
") Saturn's rings were also the location of the
Academy Flight and Training Range
. (
TNG
: "
The First Duty
")
Saturnian rings
Saturn's rings
An image of Saturn's rings, taken by the
Voyager 2
probe
in
1981
, was displayed in
astrometrics
aboard the
USS
Voyager
in the year
2374
. (
VOY
: "
Year of Hell
",
et al.
) The rings of Saturn generated magnetic distortions. These distortions could make a
starship
invisible to another starship if the vessel could jump out of warp behind one of the planet's moons. (
Star Trek
)
History
Historical image of Saturn
Before the invention of the
telescope
,
Humans
discovered Saturn in the
night sky
. (
TNG
: "
Loud As A Whisper
"
okudagram
)
In the mid-
20th century
, Saturn was depicted on the
mission insignia
for
Apollo 17
. On the insignia the
sun
god
Apollo
was looking toward Saturn. (
ENT
: "
First Flight
"
[1]
)
On September 1,
1979
, the space
probe
Pioneer 11
became the first Earth
spacecraft
to visit this planet, having traveled six and a half
years
and 3.2 billion
kilometers
. The probe discovered that this planet had a
magnetic field
and a
magnetosphere
. (
TNG
: "
Loud As A Whisper
"
okudagram
)
Image produced by Cassini
The
Cassini
mission by
NASA
brought more images of Saturn, its rings, and its moons. (
DIS
: "
Brother
")
When she was a child,
Rain Robinson
was able to see Saturn's rings through her
brother
's telescope. She used to think that they looked like
jewels
from a
pirate
's treasure. All she ever wanted since then was to reach up and touch them. This was when she realized that she wanted to become an
astronomer
. (
VOY
: "
Future's End, Part II
")
In
1996
, the
Griffith Observatory
featured an exhibit about Saturn. A picture of the planet also decorated Rain Robinson's
office
. (
VOY
: "
Future's End
")
During the early
21st century
, the first manned
Earth-Saturn probe
was launched under the command of NASA's
Colonel
Shaun Geoffrey Christopher
. (
TOS
: "
Tomorrow is Yesterday
") Accompanying Christopher were
astronauts
Fontana
and
O'Herlihy
. (
ENT
: "
First Flight
")
An image of Saturn was contained in the
library computer
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
. This data was flashed on a
viewscreen
when the
Talosians
scanned the
Enterprise
computer
in 2254. (
TOS-R
: "
The Cage
")
Saturn was mentioned in a nonsense two-line
poem
that
Charlie Evans
forced
Spock
to recite in
2266
: "
Saturn rings around my
head
, down a
road
that's
Martian
red
.
" (
TOS
: "
Charlie X
")
In
2269
,
Arex
described the
cosmic cloud
encountered by the
USS
Enterprise
as having a
diameter
twice that of Jupiter, Saturn, and
Neptune
combined. (
TAS
: "
One of Our Planets Is Missing
")
By the early
24th century
a
shuttle
route had been established "
from
Jupiter
to Saturn and back, once a
day
, every day,
" know as "
the
Jovian Run
.
" Both
Edward Jellico
and
Geordi La Forge
used to
pilot
shuttles on this route early in their
Starfleet
careers
. (
TNG
: "
Chain Of Command, Part II
")
In early
2367
, the invading
Borg cube
passed Saturn on its way towards
Earth
. (
TNG
: "
The Best of Both Worlds, Part II
")
In
2368
, the
Nova Squadron
was set to perform a demonstration near Saturn that was be transmitted to that year's
Starfleet Academy
graduation ceremonies
. (
TNG
: "
The First Duty
")
Teero Anaydis
kept a
photograph
of Saturn in his home in
2377
. (
VOY
: "
Repression
")
In
3189
, the
USS
Discovery
spore-jumped
into the vicinity of Saturn on its way to Earth. (
DIS
: "
People of Earth
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet was within the enclosed region. (
SA
: "
300th Night
")
Saturn as seen from Rhea
The orbits of Saturn's satellites
Saturn on a classroom painting
Saturn viewed from Titan's atmosphere in 2258
Stellar cartography
The position and orbital path of Saturn on "Chart 14A: The Solar System"
In
2024
, the position and orbital path of Saturn were depicted on a map of the Solar System, which was seen at the
gala
held for the
Europa Mission
astronauts
. (
PIC
: "
Two of One
")
In
2267
, the position and orbital path of Saturn were depicted on "Chart 14A: The Sol System", stored in the
USS
Enterprise
library computer. The planet was identified by its name and
astronomical symbol
. The chart was scanned by the
probe
Nomad
in
auxiliary control
. (
TOS
: "
The Changeling
"
production art
)
The Sol System
Star
:
Sol

:
Mercury
•
Venus
•
Earth
(
Luna
) •
Mars
(
Deimos
) •
Jupiter
(
Io
;
Ganymede
;
Europa
) •
Saturn
(
Mimas
;
Rhea
;
Titan
) •

•

(

) •

:
Mars Defense Perimeter
•

•

•

Appendices
Background information
Saturn as seen in the first TNG opening sequence
In the final draft script of "Charlie X",
the nonsense verse
recited by Spock included only one line about Saturn, although the poem was originally longer with four lines instead of two. The line about Saturn stated, "
Saturn's rings upon my finger.
" This line was evidently meshed together with the others, resulting in the depiction of Saturn in the final version of the poem.
The sidebar planet template includes information from the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
(4th ed., vol. 2, pp. 149, 154),
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 39, "United Federation of Planets I"),
StarTrek.com
[2]
(X)
, and real-world. The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
described Saturn as having "
a mass 95 times greater than that of Earth
" and "
a diameter of some 120,800 kilometers, and orbits its sun at a mean distance of 1.4 billion kilometers
". All three references classified Saturn as a Class J gas giant. This planet was a charter member of the
United Federation of Planets
in
2161
.
Saturn was named after the
titan
(son of
Caelus
) from
Roman mythology
. It was unclear if Saturn itself was inhabited or not, though a reference made to
Saturnius harem girls
in
TOS
: "
Mudd's Women
" might suggest that it somehow was. The necessity of a daily shuttle run would also seem to support this possibility.
The
Janus loop
training program may be named after or related to
Janus
, another moon of Saturn.
A graphic of Saturn and the orbits of four of its moons was seen in
TNG
: "
The First Duty
" (depicted to the right above). The outermost and largest moon was identified as
Titan
. The remaining three inner moons weren't named but it was very likely that they were
Rhea
,
Dione
, and
Tethys
, as they are the largest moons with smaller orbits than Titan (for instance,
Iapetus
was larger than both Tethys and Dione but its orbit was further away from Saturn than Titan's) and were all discovered in the
17th century
and are well established.
A painting seen in
TNG
: "
Justice
", according to
Rick Sternbach
, was meant to show Saturn as seen from its moon Rhea.
Saturn was one of the planets seen in both versions of the opening sequence of
Star Trek: The Next Generation
beside the
Earth
,
Luna
,
Sol
, and
Jupiter
, shortly before the
USS
Enterprise
-D
started its exploration of the unknown space.
In the
shooting script
for
Star Trek: The Motion Picture
, the
Enterprise
passed through the Saturnian system on its course to intercept
V'ger
. This was changed to the Jovian system for the film.
Apocrypha
The
Spaceflight Chronology
(p. 45) identified the ship of the Earth-Saturn probe mission as the UNSS
Lewis and Clark
. The crew of this spacecraft discovered signs of past mining activity on the second and fourth moons of Saturn, first proof of the existence of extraterrestrial intelligent life. This last detail and "
One Small Step
" would put the mission after 2032.
External links
Saturn
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Saturn
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Sauria (planet)
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Sauria_(planet)
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Sauria
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
(
3196
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Native species:
Saurians
Location:
Sauria
system
Alpha Quadrant
~48
light years
from
Sol
Sauria
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Sauria
system
. This was the
homeworld
of the
Saurians
. This planet, located in the
Alpha Quadrant
, was approximately 48
light years
from
Sol
.
History
In the
32nd century
, Sauria was a
Federation
member
planet. It was listed with other members on a video wall in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
Kids These Days
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this system was within the blast radius of one of the mines. (
SA
: "
300th Night
")
Stellar cartography
In
3196
, during the
Omega-47
crisis, the location of Sauria (Psi Serpentis) was depicted on star charts and readouts used across several locations on the
USS
Athena
to locate, monitor, and respond to the
Omega-47 mines
laid by the Venari Ral. (
SA
: "
300th Night
")
This planet was only mentioned in writing.
The planet's location was inferred from the position of its system in the
Star Trek: Enterprise
episode "
Cold Front
".

======================================================================
PAGE: Scalos
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Scalos
======================================================================

Scalos
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2268
)
Native species:
Scalosians
(
extinct
)
Location:
Scalos system
Alpha Quadrant
Scalos
was a formerly
inhabited
planet
located in the
Scalos system
. The
Class M
planet was located in the
Alpha Quadrant
and was the
homeworld
of the
Scalosians
, an
extinct
humanoid
species
. This planet was located in an outer
quadrant
of the
galaxy
.
By
2259
, the
Federation
had established
first contact
with this planet. This civilization had developed warp travel and had or was in the process of being inducted into the Federation by this year. (
SNW
: "
Strange New Worlds
")
When the
USS
Enterprise
was exploring this quadrant, the starship picked up a
distress call
coming from a
city
on this planet. A
landing party
discovered that the world was barren. There were no apparent
animal
lifeforms
and sparse
vegetation
. According to the distress call, there were nine hundred thousand Scalosians who lived in a
nation
, 113,477 of them in one city. Their
civilization
was rated 7 on the
industrial scale
.
After the world was devastated by
volcanic eruptions
that contaminated the
water
with
radiation
, the Scalosians were
hyper-accelerated
. The
children
died, with many
females
being unable of carrying
children
, and the
males
were
sterile
. Desperate, the Scalosians looked for
mates
outside their world. When a
spaceship
passed by, a distress call was sent and, when the ship came in to investigate, the crew was captured. After being hyper-accelerated, they did not last long, so the Scalosians waited for the next ship as their
population
plummeted.
When the planet was visited by the
Starfleet
vessel
USS
Enterprise
in
2268
, only five hyper-accelerated individuals remained. After the
Enterprise
visited the planet, the
Federation
quarantined
the planet. (
TOS
: "
Wink of an Eye
")
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 255 classified Scalos as a
class M
planet.
For the
2006
remastered version of "Wink of an Eye", the generic planet representing Scalos (also seen as
Argelius II
) was replaced with a unique, computer-generated version. The cityscape on the
surface
of the planet was also replaced with a new, high definition
matte painting
– the original had been a reuse of the
Eminiar VII
city from "
A Taste of Armageddon
".
External links
Scalos
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Planets in TOS and TOS Remastered
at
Ex Astris Scientia

======================================================================
PAGE: Scenic route
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Scenic_route
======================================================================

The
scenic route
was a way of
arriving
at a
destination
that was
valued
for its scenery rather than expediency.
In
2152
,
Captain
Jonathan Archer
advised
Trip Tucker
not to take the scenic route to
Enterprise
NX-01
's
engineering section
, as his
EV suit
offered him only 22
minutes
of protection. (
ENT
: "
The Catwalk
")
In
2377
,
Tom Paris
remarked while
USS
Voyager
was pursuing a
Hirogen
ship that they weren't taking the scenic route.
Chakotay
reminded him that they weren't there to appreciate the view. (
VOY
: "
Flesh and Blood
")
External link
Scenic route
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Schoolyard
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Schoolyard
======================================================================

A
schoolyard
was an outdoor area adjoining a
school
where
students
could
play
.
When
Erica Ortegas
remarked that the
Romulans
started the
fight
between them,
Joseph M'Benga
countered, "This isn't a schoolyard." (i.e.
Starfleet
and the Romulans weren't
children
brawling in the
playground
, in which an argument of "they started it" would have merit). (
SNW
: "
A Quality of Mercy
")
External link
Schoolyard
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Science Station Tango Sierra
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Science_Station_Tango_Sierra
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Science Station Tango Sierra
Type:
Regula I
-type
science station
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Location:
Rachelis
system
Beta Quadrant
Status:
Active
(
3196
)
Science Station Tango Sierra
was a
Federation
Regula I
-type
science station
in the
Rachelis system
,
Beta Quadrant
. This station was operational by the mid-
24th century
.
History
In
2365
, the
USS
Enterprise
-D
transported a
cargo container
filled with
strains
of
plasma plague
from the
planet
'aucdet IX
to this station. The station's teams were tasked with identifying an
antidote
for an unidentified
plasma plague
that was ravaging the Rachelis system. The station received both the container and
medical trustee
Commander
Hester Dealt
. (
TNG
: "
The Child
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this station was within the enclosed region. (
SA
: "
Rubincon
")
Stellar cartography
In
3196
, this station's position was labeled on a
Federation star chart
which was displayed on the
main viewscreen
of the
USS
Athena
bridge
. (
SA
: "
Ko'Zeine
")
In
3196
, during the
Omega-47
crisis, the location of this station was depicted on star charts and readouts used across several locations on the
USS
Athena
to locate, monitor, and respond to the Omega-47 mines laid by the Venari Ral. (
SA
: "
Rubincon
")
For information on the model used for this design, please see
studio models
.
External link
Science Station Tango Sierra
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Secarus IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Secarus_IV
======================================================================

Secarus IV
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2370
)
Location:
Secarus
system
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Secarus IV
was the
fourth
planet
in the
Secarus
system
.
In
2345
,
the Albino
retreated to a fortified
compound
on this planet after he escaped from
Kang
,
Kor
,
Koloth
, and
Curzon Dax
on
Galdonterre
.
In mid-
2370
, Kang learned of the Albino's location and traveled to Secarus IV. When Kang arrived, the Albino immediately contacted him, offering Kang a proposal. The Albino offered Kang, as well as Koloth and Kor, one last glorious battle against forty of his
finest sentries
.
Three
months
later, the three
Klingon
warriors
, along with
Jadzia Dax
, returned to Secarus IV to finally face the treacherous Albino. After successfully destroying the compound's
armory
and
power station
, the Albino was confronted and killed, fulfilling the Klingons'
blood oath
and their eighty-
year
quest. (
DS9
: "
Blood Oath
")
Inhabitants
See:
Secarus IV inhabitants
Appendices
Background information
The planet's
name
is not from the episode. Rather, it is from the
script
The Secarus IV exteriors, showing the Albino's fortress, were filmed
on location
in
Pasadena
, at a large house designed by famed
architect
Frank Lloyd Wright
. (
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine Companion
, p. 132) A
matte painting
was incorporated into the exterior footage. (
Captains' Logs Supplemental - The Unauthorized Guide to the New Trek Voyages
, p. 72) The interiors of the fortress were built on
Paramount Stage 18
and an additional shot, depicting the
building
's
walls
being blown out, used a miniature which
Dan Curry
's team of visual effects artists did atop
Paramount
's Van Ness parking structure. (
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine Companion
, p. 132) "
The optical people blew up [the] miniature, and it looked fabulous,
" commented
David Livingston
. "
It was a tabletop miniature with fake
trees
and little bushes and when it blew up it looked like something out of
Lethal Weapon
." (
Captains' Logs Supplemental - The Unauthorized Guide to the New Trek Voyages
, p. 72)
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 260 classified Secarus IV as a
class M
planet.
According to the
script
, the planet's name was pronounced as "sec-A-rus".
[1]
External links
Secarus IV
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Re-Used Planets in DS9
at
Ex Astris Scientia

======================================================================
PAGE: Section 31 Headquarters
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Section_31_Headquarters
======================================================================

Section 31 Headquarters
Owner:
United Federation of Planets
Operator:
Section 31
Status:
Destroyed
(
2257
)
Defenses:
Blade mines
Blackout mines
Section 31 Headquarters
was a
space station
used during the mid-
23rd century
by the covert
Starfleet
organization
Section 31
.  Listed as an abandoned
penal colony
, the station was used by Section 31 as a
forward operating base
and housed the
AI
known as
Control
.
History
23rd century
Located in empty space at the coordinates 7-4 Mark 5.6, officially the base was listed as a former penal colony which, as of the mid-23rd century, had been abandoned over a century ago. It was surrounded by a dense
minefield
of
alien
origin that contained
blade mines
and
blackout mines
in addition to conventional explosive mines.  Incoming vessels were required to take a circuitous route to avoid them.
In
2257
, Control betrayed Starfleet and massacred the crew of Section 31 Headquarters by shutting off
life support
, though it was able to hide this fact by creating
holograms
of
Admiral
Patar
and other Section 31 leaders for communications.
Two weeks later, the
USS
Discovery
uncovered the station's location and was directed there by
Admiral
Katrina Cornwell
in an effort to reset Control.
Discovery
came under attack by the mines, which had been reprogrammed by Control, and barely managed to reach the station.
After a
boarding party
from
Discovery
arrived on the station and learned of the events that had transpired there, it was found that
Lieutenant Commander
Airiam
had been corrupted and was attempting to give Control information about artificial intelligence gathered by the
Sphere
. To stop her,
Commander
Nhan
was forced to eject Airiam into space. (
DIS
: "
Project Daedalus
")
Later, as part of the effort to erradicate Control,
Discovery
subsequently destroyed the station. (
DIS
: "
The Red Angel
")
Physical arrangement
A corridor exposed to the temperatures of space
Holding over from its use as a penal colony, some sections of the station were labeled
cells
.
Cardinal directions
were used to designate certain areas of the station, including the
east workshop
and
west workshop
. (
DIS
: "
Project Daedalus
")
24th century
According to
Luther Sloan
, by the
24th century
, Section 31 did not have headquarters or buildings whatsoever, instead keeping knowledge on its operations in the minds of a very select group of people, including Sloan. (
DS9
: "
Extreme Measures
")
It can be safely assumed that this decentralization was intended to prevent anyone or anything else from compromising the organization and turning its resources against the Federation like Control did.

PAGE: Sefalla Prime
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Sefalla_Prime
======================================================================

Sefalla Prime
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2370
)
Location:
Near
Draylon II
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Sefalla Prime
was the
prime
planet
in its
star system
. This planet was located near
Draylon II
.
When
Commander
Sisko
and
Major
Kira
were considering Draylon II as a possible site for relocating the
Skrreea
population in
2370
, they noted that the planet was near Sefalla Prime. (
DS9
: "
Sanctuary
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
Based on the planet's seemingly close proximity to Draylon II, it would seem that this planet is in, or is relatively near the Alpha Quadrant.
According to
StarTrek.com
, Sefalla Prime, in
orbit
of the
star
Sefalla, was located in the Sefalla system, which was located in the
Bajoran sector
.
[1]
(X)
[2]
(X)

======================================================================
PAGE: Selay (planet)
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Selay_(planet)
======================================================================

Selay
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Non-aligned
Status:
Habitable
(
2364
)
Native species:
Selay
Location:
Beta Renner
system
Sector 500
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Selay
was an
inhabited
major
planet
of the
Beta Renner
system
in
Sector 500
. This planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
and was the
homeworld
for the
Selay
, a
snake
-like
warp-capable
species
.
Since achieving
spaceflight
, Selay had been at war with the neighboring planet
Antica
. Prior to
stardate
41249
,
Starfleet
had detected
Ferengi
contacts on this planet. In 2364, Antica and Selay both applied for
Federation
membership. (
TNG
: "
Lonely Among Us
")
Appendices
Background information
The planet model for Selay was first used to represent
Delphi Ardu IV
in "
The Last Outpost
". In that episode, the model was
colored
blue
. In future uses of this model. the
green
color, first used here for Selay, would be retained. It would be used to represent
Persephone V
in "
Too Short A Season
",
Antede III
in "
Manhunt
", and
Krios Prime
in "
The Perfect Mate
".
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
(United Federation of Planets I & II), Selay, along with Antica, had become a member planet of the Federation by
2378
.
In an
Okudagram
seen in "
Conspiracy
", Antica was located in Sector 500, which implied that Selay, being a sister planet, was located here as well.
Apocrypha
In the
Star Trek: The Next Generation First Year Sourcebook
, Selay was the
first
planet in the Beta Renner system. In the
Worlds
sourcebook, it was the
fourth
planet.
External link
Selay (planet)
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

Selcundi Drema
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Non-aligned
Status:
Habitable
(
2369
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Selcundi Drema
was an
inhabited
planet
.
Lieutenant Commander
Jim Mees
, a
crewmember
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
, was born on this non-
Federation
planet
. In
2369
, his planet of origin was listed on the chart "USS
Enterprise
Personnel Database", which was seen on
Jean-Luc Picard
's
PADD
. (
TNG
: "
The Chase
"
okudagram
)
This planet was only mentioned in writing.

======================================================================

Seldonis IV
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
24th century
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Seldonis IV
was the
inhabited
fourth
planet
in its
star system
.
It was where the
Seldonis IV Convention
, governing the treatment of
prisoners of war
, was negotiated and agreed to.
Representatives
of the
United Federation of Planets
and the
Cardassian Union
were among those present who participated in the
negotiations
. (
TNG
: "
Chain Of Command, Part II
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.

Selek IV

Status:

(
2377
)
Location:
Delta Quadrant
Selek IV
was the
inhabited
fourth
planet
in its
star system
in the
Delta Quadrant
.
In
2377
,
Gar
was en route to a
gambling
tournament on Selek IV when he was apprehended by the
USS
Voyager
for stealing the
EMH
. (
VOY
: "
Critical Care
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 94), the
primary
of the Selek system was a
M-class star
.

Planet
Status:

(
24th century
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Sendu IV
was the
inhabited
fourth
planet
in its
star system
.
In the late
24th century
, for a
week
,
Brad Boimler
unknowingly
urinated
into the
natives
'
sinks
and not into their
toilets
. He only realized his mistake after a local went to wash their
hands
and found him doing so. (
LD
: "
Cupid's Errant Arrow
")

was the

fourth
planet
in its

. This planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or

.
This planet was the intended destination of the
USS
Lalo
in late
2366
. During its voyage, the
Lalo
went missing and was presumably destroyed by the
Borg
cube
which was on
course
for
Sector 001
. (

: "

======================================================================
PAGE: Septimus III
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Septimus_III
======================================================================

Septimus III
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Cardassian
Dominion
(
2373
-
2375
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Location:
Septimus system
Cardassian space
Alpha Quadrant
Septimus III
was the
inhabited
third
planet
in the
Septimus
system
,
Alpha Quadrant
. This planet was in
Cardassian
space
.
History
In the
2370s
, the headquarters of the Cardassian
Eleventh Order
was on this planet.
The planet was
attacked
by the
Klingon Empire
in late
2375
, near the end of the
Dominion War
. The
Klingons
landed fifteen
divisions
, wiping out the Eleventh Order. According to
Weyoun
, this meant the Klingons had expended valuable resources to capture a strategically worthless planet. However, the Klingon victory ended up costing the Dominion heavily nonetheless because the nonchalance of Weyoun sacrificing the 500,000 men strong Eleventh Order was the final indignity that caused
Legate
Damar
to instigate the
Cardassian Rebellion
. (
DS9
: "
Strange Bedfellows
")
This planet's quadrant was inferred from the position of its star system on the Federation star chart appearing in the
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
episode "
300th Night
".
According to the script, the pronunciation for Septimus was "SEPT-uh-muss".
[1]
External link
Septimus III
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Seros
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Seros
======================================================================

Seros
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2373
)
Native species:
Serosians
Location:
Delta Quadrant
The
planet
Seros
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Delta Quadrant
, and was the
homeworld
of the
Serosian
race
. It was home to the
programmers
that created
Dejaren
, a
HD25 Isomorphic Projection
.
Eight
months
prior to being discovered by the
USS
Voyager
, a
Serosian vessel
left Seros with a
crew
of six. (
VOY
: "
Revulsion
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
The
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 88) located this planet in the Seros system. This was a
trinary star system
with a
M-class star
, a G-class star, and a K-class star. This system was never visited by the
Voyager
.
External link
Seros
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Setlik I
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Setlik_I
======================================================================

Setlik I
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Inhabited
(
3196
)
Native species:
Setlik I natives
Location:
Setlik system
Kiley Local Sector
Alpha Quadrant
Setlik I
was the
inhabited
first
planet
in the
Setlik system
,
Alpha Quadrant
. This planet was the
homeworld
of a
warp-capable civilization
, which the
United Federation of Planets
was aware of by
2259
. That
year
, the planet was in the
Kiley Local
sector
. (
SNW
: "
Strange New Worlds
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet's system was within the blast radius of one of the mines. (
SA
: "
300th Night
", "
Rubincon
")
This planet was only mentioned in writing.

======================================================================
PAGE: Setlik II
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Setlik_II
======================================================================

Setlik II
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Satellite(s):
Ice moon
Location:
Setlik system
Kiley Local
sector
Alpha Quadrant
Setlik II
was the
inhabited
second
planet
in the
Setlik system
,
Alpha Quadrant
.  The world was
orbited
by a habitable "
ice moon
".
History
In the
23rd century
, this planet was in the
Kiley Local
sector
. (
SNW
: "
Strange New Worlds
")
The planet was home to a
Federation colony
, which had been in place for
generations
by the mid-
23rd century
.
In
2259
, the
USS
Enterprise
was assigned to transport
tritriticale
grain
, which had been specially made to grow in Setlik II's
biome
, to the colony. En route, they encountered a
time travel
incident at the
portal
on
Krulmuth-B
, and were subsequently forced to trade the tritriticale to the
Orion
science vessel
D'Var
to retrieve the portal from them. It was believed that without the grain, the
Enterprise
would have to relocate the colonists.
Originally,
Captain
Christopher Pike
wanted to celebrate his birthday on Setlik II by renting a
fishing
cabin
, have a
bottle
of
whiskey
and have an imaginary conversation with his
father
, however he cancelled those plans and ended up celebrating his birthday with his
senior staff
. (
SNW
: "
Those Old Scientists
")
In the
24th century
, this planet's system was near the
Federation-Cardassian border
. (
TNG
: "
The Wounded
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet's system was within the blast radius of one of the mines. (
SA
: "
300th Night
", "
Rubincon
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Setlik III
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Setlik_III
======================================================================

Setlik III
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Location:
Setlik
system
Kiley Local
sector
Alpha Quadrant
Setlik III
was the formerly
inhabited
third
planet
in the
Setlik system
,
Alpha Quadrant
.
History
In the
23rd century
, this planet was in the
Kiley Local
sector
. (
SNW
: "
Strange New Worlds
")
In the
24th century
, this planet was near to the
Federation-Cardassian border
. Sometime prior to
2347
, a
Federation
outpost
had been established on this planet.
During the
Federation-Cardassian War
, the planet was the target of a
sneak attack
by a
squad
of the
Cardassian military
, which resulted in close to a hundred
civilians
killed
, including
the family
of
Benjamin Maxwell
, in what became known as the
Setlik III massacre
. The
USS
Rutledge
was the first Federation
starship
to respond, though largely too late to prevent the
deaths
. (
TNG
: "
The Wounded
";
DS9
: "
Emissary
")
Miles O'Brien
became known as the "Hero of Setlik III" for leading a successful attack against the
Cardassian
Barrica encampment
during that action. (
DS9
: "
Empok Nor
") O'Brien also managed to repair a
field transporter
under combat conditions to allow himself and thirteen others to escape the planet's
surface
. (
DS9
: "
Paradise
")
During the battle of Setlik III,
Starfleet
officer
Raymond Boone
was captured and killed and then replaced by a
Cardassian spy
who continued to serve in Starfleet until discharged in
2362
.
Commander
Benjamin Sisko
exposed 'Boone' as a Cardassian
spy
in
2370
. (
DS9
: "
Tribunal
")
In
2367
,
Glinn
Daro
acknowledged that the attack on Setlik III had been "a terrible mistake", as the Cardassians had erroneously believed it to have been the staging ground for a massive Federation attack. (
TNG
: "
The Wounded
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet's system was within the blast radius of one of the mines. (
SA
: "
300th Night
", "
Rubincon
")
Appendices
Background information
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
In the
script
for "Emissary", the pronunciation for Setlik was "SET-lik".
External link
Setlik III
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Sha Ka Ree (planet)
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Sha_Ka_Ree_(planet)
======================================================================

Sha Ka Ree
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2287
)
Location:
Galactic core
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
"
Sha Ka Ree? A vision you created. An eternity I've been imprisoned in this place.
"
–
God
,
2287
(
Star Trek V: The Final Frontier
)
Sha Ka Ree
was a
planet
in the
galactic core
thought to be the site of the legendary
Sha Ka Ree
of
Vulcan mythology
. The planet was located at
coordinates
000 mark 2.
In the
distant past
, sometime before the
23rd century
,
a malevolent alien entity
was
imprisoned
on the planet. The
entity
was unable to leave the planet much less penetrate the
Great Barrier
; however, it could shake the planet and manipulate the
surface
. During its time, it became extremely bitter about its circumstances.
In
2287
, the renegade
Vulcan
Sybok
embarked on a quest to locate Sha Ka Ree, recruiting a large number of followers (the
Galactic Army of Light
) via
telepathic
manipulation. Using this technique to gain control of the
USS
Enterprise
-A
, Sybok informed
Captain
Kirk
that he had located Sha Ka Ree via visions given to him by
God
himself. Although Kirk initially thought Sybok to be mad, his visions seemed to be proven correct when, after successfully penetrating the Barrier, the
Enterprise
-A
crew
encountered the planet on the other side.
However, upon traveling to the surface in
a shuttlecraft
, Sybok, Kirk, Captain
Spock
, and
Doctor
McCoy
learned the truth about the being. The entity commanded them to allow it to join with the
Enterprise
-A; however, Sybok sacrificed himself to occupy it while Kirk, Spock, and McCoy escaped.
A Klingon Bird-of-Prey
had followed the
Enterprise
-A and further assisted Kirk's escape by firing at the entity. (
Star Trek V: The Final Frontier
)
Sha Ka Ree was one of the locations available to conquer in the Playstation 2 game
Star Trek: Conquest
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Shaandra
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Shaandra
======================================================================

Shaandra
Type:
Astronomical
object
Status:
Intact
(
2285
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
For the star or planet from which the
Platonians
originated, please see
Sahndara
or
Sahndara (planet)
.
Shaandra
was an
astronomical
object
in the same
region
as
Sol
/
Jupiter
.
In
2285
, Shaandra's position was displayed on a
star chart
on the
bridge
of the
USS
Enterprise
. (
Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan
)
This astronomical object was only mentioned in writing.
The image was taken from page T0:02:07:02 of the
Star Fleet Technical Manual
by
Franz Joseph
. The map claimed to depict principal
star systems
in the
Federation
. Based on the scale for the chart, Shaandra was located a tiny amount less than a
parsec
from
Sol
.
External link
Shaandra
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Shantil III
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Shantil_III
======================================================================

Shantil III
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
24th century
)
Location:
El-Adrel
sector
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Shantil III
was the
inhabited
third
planet
in its
star system
. Located in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
, it was in the same
sector
as
El-Adrel IV
,
Kanda IV
,
Malindi VII
, and
Razna V
.
It was the place of origin for a
mytho-historical
drama, in which the characters
Darmok
and
Jalad
faced a common foe on the
island
-
continent
of
Tanagra
and achieved mutual understanding in the process. The
story
was later incorporated into the
Tamarian language
. (
TNG
: "
Darmok
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
In the
script
, the pronunciation for Shantil was "SHAHN-til".
[1]
External link
Shantil
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

holoprogram

. (

William Shatner
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Sherbal V
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Sherbal_V
======================================================================

Sherbal V
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2381
)
Location:
Sherbal system
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
"
This planet... has no honor.
"
–
Ma'ah
,
2381
("
The Inner Fight
")
Sherbal V
was the uninhabited
fifth
planet
of the
Sherbal system
. It was located "
in the middle of nowhere
", far from
Klingon space
. The planet was heavily
forested
, with a variety of native
animals
and
plants
. It was noted for unusual
weather
phenomena, including copious
electrical storms
and
glass storms
that
rained
knife
-like shards of
glass
. As a result, the
Federation
had placed a
weather satellite
in
orbit
, which linked to an unmanned
monitoring station
on the surface. The bizarre weather and sparsity of
fruit
-bearing
trees
made the planet inhospitable.
In
2381
, the
commanders
of several
starships
, including
Ma'ah
,
Vrek
,
Cosmia
, and
Gem
, were marooned on Sherbal V after they had been
mutinied
upon by their
junior officers
with the secret aid of
Nick Locarno
. The
mutineers
would subsequently join
Nova Fleet
. The
IKS
Che'Ta'
remained near the planet under
cloak
to guard against intruders.
Later that year, the weather satellite stopped transmitting data. At
Lieutenant jg
D'Vana Tendi
's suggestion,
Captain
Carol Freeman
of the
USS
Cerritos
dispatched an
away team
to investigate, as a way to keep
Beckett Mariner
out of harm's way due to her recent spate of reckless behavior. The team found that the satellite's
communication relays
had been tampered with. After they completed repairs, their
shuttle
was attacked and destroyed by the
Che'Ta'
, though they were able to
beam
to the surface.
The
Starfleet
officers
discovered the other stranded commanders fighting one another for resources. After establishing a rapport with Ma'ah and consequently overcoming the mental health crisis that led to her reckless behavior, Mariner rallied everyone to work together towards rescue, though she was then abducted by Locarno's ship. Under Ma'ah's leadership, the others modified the monitoring station into a
distress beacon
, which lured the
Che'Ta'
close enough to the surface for them to board it and retake control. (
LD
: "
The Inner Fight
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Sherman's Planet
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Sherman's_Planet
======================================================================

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Sherman's Planet
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Klingon Empire
(
2256
-
57
)
United Federation of Planets
(
2293
)
Non-aligned
(
24th century
)
Status:
Habitable
(
2399
)
Location:
In
orbit
of a
G-type
star
Unnamed minor quadrant
Alpha Quadrant
Sherman's Planet
was an
inhabited
namesake
planet
orbiting
a
G-type
star
in the
Beta Quadrant
. The planet was located in the same
minor quadrant
as
Donatu V
,
Deep Space Station K-7
, and a
Klingon outpost
. (
TOS
: "
The Trouble with Tribbles
")
History
Sherman's Planet was mentioned in an historical archive
The region of
space
, in which Sherman's Planet was located, was first mapped by
astronomer
John Burke
in
2067
. (
TOS
: "
The Trouble with Tribbles
";
ENT
: "
In A Mirror, Darkly, Part II
",
display graphic
)
In the
Federation-Klingon War
of
2256
to
57
, this planet was invaded and occupied by the Klingons. (
DIS
: "
Magic to Make the Sanest Man Go Mad
", "
The War Without, The War Within
")
In the
alternate
2259
, before a
colony
could be founded on this planet, there was a dispute about the
Organian Peace Treaty
. This event was reported in a news ticker, which was in a collection of graphics and video media that were seen on a
powerwall
in the
offices
of
Christopher Pike
and
Alexander Marcus
at
Starfleet Headquarters
.
(
Star Trek Into Darkness
)
The ticker can be seen in the video "
Star Trek: Into Darkness
- User Interface VFX" at vimeo.com.
[1]
Following the
Federation-Klingon War
in
2267
, and per terms of the
Organian Peace Treaty
, ownership of Sherman's Planet could be assumed by the side that could most effectively develop it. The Federation's intentions for Sherman's Planet were to use it to cultivate
quadrotriticale
, a
grain
well adapted to the planet's
climate
. After the quadrotriticale was
poisoned
on
Deep Space Station K-7
, Starfleet diverted a
freighter
laded down with this grain to Sherman's Planet. The grain would be delivered a few
weeks
later than anticipated. (
TOS
: "
The Trouble with Tribbles
")
The Federation succeeded in best developing Sherman's Planet. However, by
2269
, the planet had been struck by
famine
. On
stardate
5392.4, the
USS
Enterprise
, under
Captain
James T. Kirk
, was assigned to escort two
robot ships
hauling an improved grain,
quintotriticale
, to the planet to alleviate the plight. (
TAS
: "
More Tribbles, More Troubles
")
In the
24th century
, the formerly
Lieutenant Commander
Brannon Braga
, a
crewmember
aboard the USS
Enterprise
, was born on this non-Federation world. In
2369
, his planet of origin was listed on the chart "USS
Enterprise
Personnel Database", which was displayed on a
PADD
accessed by Captain Jean-Luc Picard. (
TNG
: "
The Chase
",
okudagram
)
In
2328
, this was home to the
Sherman's Planet Spaceport
. (
TNG-R
: "
Inheritance
")
Stellar cartography
Alpha/Beta Quadrant Overview
In
2256
, this planet's location was labeled on the
star chart
"Alpha/Beta Quadrant Overview" in the
ready room
aboard the
USS
Discovery
. (
DIS
: "
Magic to Make the Sanest Man Go Mad
", "
The War Without, The War Within
")
In
2258
, the planet's location was labeled on a star chart seen on the
main viewscreen
of the
USS
Enterprise
. (
DIS
: "
Such Sweet Sorrow, Part 2
")
In
2259
, the location of this planet was labeled on a
stellar cartography
chart that was seen on the
USS
Enterprise
's
ready room
viewscreen
. This planet's symbol had a
blue
color
, indicating that it was affiliated with the
United Federation of Planets
. (
SNW
: "
Strange New Worlds
", "
Children of the Comet
", "
Spock Amok
")
In
2293
, the location of this planet was labeled in a
star chart
found on the
navigation console
of the
USS
Enterprise
-B
. The planet's location was denoted with a
Starfleet delta
, implying its affiliation with the Federation and Starfleet. (
Star Trek Generations
)
On
stardate
1292.4, the location of this planet was labeled in a chart used during a
Section 31
Alpha Team
briefing
. (
Star Trek: Section 31
)
In
2371
, the location of Sherman's Planet was labeled on the star chart
Data
and
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
were studying in
stellar cartography
on the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. (
Star Trek Generations
,
okudagram
)
The planet's location was labeled in a
Federation star chart
that was in
Fleet Admiral
Kirsten Clancy
's
office
at
Starfleet Headquarters
in
2399
and on the
bridge
of the
USS
Titan
-A
in
2401
. Sherman's Planet was in or near to neutral space. (
PIC
: "
Maps and Legends
", "
The Next Generation
", "
Disengage
")
Appendices
Background information
In the
reference works
The Trouble with Tribbles
and the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 272, it was stated that Sherman's Planet was named for Holly Sherman, a friend and college girlfriend of the episode's writer,
David Gerrold
.
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
(pp. 53, 57, 62, "United Federation of Planets I") and the
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Stellar Cartography", pp. 30-31; "Federation Historical Highlights, 2161-2385"), Sherman's Planet (
Klingonese
, "Sermanyu'q") (
FGC
-24187 V) was classified as a
M-class planet
. The official name for the planet's
government
was the Sherman's Planet Joint Administrative Territory. The two
capitals
of this planet were Port Emily (
β
) (Federation) and Ka'Hat (
β
) (Klingon). There were 98.0 million Humans and 116.2 million Klingons living on this world in the late 24th century. This planet was on a disputed border with the UFP (
2152
-2267), and was settled according to the terms of the
Organian Peace Treaty
in
2267
. In
2378
, Sherman's Planet was listed as a
Federation member
. The
primary
FGC-24187 was a
G-class star
. According to a map of the
Klingon Empire
– prepared by the Scribe K'Tark for the
Klingon High Council
in the
Year of Kahless
893 (
2266
) – Sherman's Planet was identified as a Klingon planet. This claim was propagandistic, as the Empire claimed many border systems and planets. This often put the Empire at odds with the Federation.
The current location of Sherman's Planet in the Beta Quadrant was based on the most recent star charts seen in
Star Trek: Strange New Worlds
and
Star Trek: Section 31
.
Apocrypha
In the
Star Trek Maps
(chart D), Sherman's Planet had six small
moons
called First, Second, Show, Last, Speed and Tiny one.
This planet was also mentioned in several
novels
and short-stories, including
Pawns and Symbols
,
A Singular Destiny
, as well as in the
Starfleet Corps of Engineers
-series.
In the novel
Best Defense
, the Klingon name for Sherman's Planet was Homog Three.
In
Star Trek Online
, Deep Space K-7 was in orbit around Sherman's Planet.
External links
Planets in Science Fiction
Sherman's Planet
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Sherman system
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Sheva II
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Sheva_II
======================================================================

Sheva II
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Cardassian Union
(
24th century
)
Dominion
(
2375
)
Location:
Sheva
system
Alpha Quadrant
Sheva II
was the
second
planet
of the
Sheva
system
,
Alpha Quadrant
.
History
In the
24th century
, this planet's system was in
Cardassian
space
.
During the
Dominion War
, this world was selected by the
Dominion
for a
Jem'Hadar
breeding facility. This facility was one of the targets of
General
Martok
's "cavalry raid" in
2375
. (
DS9
: "
Once More Unto the Breach
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet's system was within the blast radius of one of the mines. (
SA
: "
300th Night
", "
Rubincon
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of the Cardassian Union as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
In the script, the pronunciation for Sheva was given as "SHE-vuh".
[1]

Sigma Draconis I
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
3196
)
Location:
Sigma Draconis
system
Alpha Quadrant
~19
light years
from
Sol
Sigma Draconis I
was the
first
planet
in the
Sigma Draconis
system
. Located in the
Alpha Quadrant
, this planet was approximately 19
light years
from
Sol
. This was one of six planets in this system that was not classified as
M class
.
History
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet was within the enclosed region. (
SA
: "
300th Night
")
Stellar cartography
The position and
orbital
path of Sigma Draconis I were depicted on a system map that was displayed on the
main viewscreen
of the
USS
Enterprise
bridge
in
2268
. (
TOS
: "
Spock's Brain
")
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".

Sigma Draconis III
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Satellite(s):
None
Native species:
Sigma Draconis III natives
Location:
Sigma Draconis system
Alpha Quadrant
~19
light years
from
Sol
Sigma Draconis III
was the
inhabited
third
planet
in the
Sigma Draconis
system
. Located in the
Alpha Quadrant
, this planet was approximately 19
light years
from
Sol
. Classified as
M-class
, this
moonless
world was the
homeworld
for the
Sigma Draconis III natives
.
The planet was larger and more heavily populated than
Sigma Draconis IV
. By
2268
, the planet's inhabitants had a pre-spaceflight
culture
with an
industrial scale
rating of B, which was equivalent to
Earth
in
1485
, and a
technological rating
of 3.
History
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet was within the enclosed region. (
SA
: "
300th Night
")
Stellar cartography
The position and
orbital
path of Sigma Draconis III were depicted on system maps that were displayed on the
main viewscreen
of the
USS
Enterprise
bridge
in
2268
. (
TOS
: "
Spock's Brain
")
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
(pp. 18, 19, 44) and
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Federation Historical Highlights, 2161-2385"), the Sigma Draconis system was located in
Sector 004
(Sigma Draconis sector). This was a Federation system in the late
24th century
.

Warning!

Sigma Draconis IX
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2268
)
Location:
Sigma Draconis
system
Alpha Quadrant
~19
light years
from
Sol
Sigma Draconis IX
was the
ninth
planet
in the
Sigma Draconis
system
. Located in the
Alpha Quadrant
, this planet was approximately 19
light years
from
Sol
. This was one of six planets in this system that was not classified as
M class
.
This planet, unlike the other planets in the system, had a highly inclined
orbit
that brought it close to the orbital path of
Sigma Draconis VIII
.
History
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet was within the enclosed region. (
SA
: "
300th Night
")
Stellar cartography
The position and
orbital
path of Sigma Draconis IX were depicted on a system map that was displayed on the
main viewscreen
of the
USS
Enterprise
bridge
in
2268
. (
TOS
: "
Spock's Brain
")
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
(pp. 18, 19, 44) and
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Federation Historical Highlights, 2161-2385"), the Sigma Draconis system was located in
Sector 004
(Sigma Draconis sector). This was a Federation system in the late
24th century
.
