======================================================================
PAGE: Sigma Draconis VI
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Sigma_Draconis_VI
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Sigma Draconis VI
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Non-aligned
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Satellite(s):
1
moon
Native species:
Sigma Draconis VI natives
(
Morgs
&
Eymorgs
)
Location:
Sigma Draconis system
Alpha Quadrant
~19
light years
from
Sol
Sigma Draconis VI
was the
inhabited
sixth
planet
in the
Sigma Draconis
system
, The planet was located in the
Alpha Quadrant
and was approximately 19
light years
from
Sol
. Classified as
M-class
, the planet was the
home
of the
Morgs
and
Eymorgs
. The planet had one
moon
.
History
In
2268
, Sigma Draconis VI was several thousand years into a
glacial age
, with a maximum high
temperature
of 40
°F
. Only a small tropic zone existed on the planet that remained
ice
-free.
Sentient
life was plentiful on the planet but on a most primitive level. According to
probes
, the planet had no signs of industrial development or organized
civilization
. As the probes detected primitive
humanoids
at irregular intervals, the instrumentation of these
spacecraft
also detected regular pulsations of generated
energy
.
Overall, however, Sigma Draconis VI was a remarkable example of a retrograde civilization. At the peak, it was advanced beyond
23rd century
Federation
capabilities, but had de-evolved to a primitive level, beginning
10,000 years ago
when a glacial age reoccurred. An underground complex was developed for the women, while the men remained above, causing a male-female schism. This ultimately caused a cultural development which was last seen on old
Earth
when the
Romans
were warring with
barbarians
.
The
USS
Enterprise
visited the Sigma Draconis system in search of
Spock
's
brain
. Once reaching the system,
Uhura
detected high
energy
generation on the planet.
Kirk
took a gamble and
beamed
down to the planet's
surface
. Almost immediately they encountered five
male
humanoid
natives, known as Morgs.
The foundations for a dead and buried
city
were later found near an
elevator
built into a
cave
. Several hundred
meters
below the surface, they discovered a highly complex civilization built around the high energy generation source, based on
ion power
.
This civilization was discovered to be the home to what the Morgs referred to as the "givers of
pain
and
delight
," otherwise known as the Eymorgs.
The civilization, itself, was controlled by the
Controller
, which was responsible for keeping the Eymorgs alive for a period of 10,000 years, and for whom the Eymorgs lived to serve.
Following the disconnection of the Controller (which was "powered" by Spock's brain), by the crew of the
Enterprise
, the Morgs and Eymorgs were forced to live together once again, in a forced attempt to re-establish a civilization. (
TOS
: "
Spock's Brain
")
In the
24th century
,
Lieutenant Commander
Dan Curry
, a
crewmember
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
, was born on this non-
Federation
planet
. In
2369
, his planet of origin was listed on the chart "USS
Enterprise
Personnel Database", which was seen on
Jean-Luc Picard
's
PADD
. (
TNG
: "
The Chase
",
okudagram
)
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet was within the enclosed region. (
SA
: "
300th Night
")
Stellar cartography
The position and
orbital
path of Sigma Draconis VI was depicted on system maps that were displayed on the
main viewscreen
of the
USS
Enterprise
bridge
in
2268
. (
TOS
: "
Spock's Brain
")
Sigma Draconis system
Sigma Draconis VI
Another shot of the planet's surface
Sigma Draconis system

I

•

•
V

•
VII
•
VIII
•
IX
Appendices
Background information
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
Sigma Draconis VI from orbit as it appeared in the original version
For the
2007
remastered version of the episode "Spock's Brain", an all-new computer-generated version of Sigma Draconis VI was rendered. The new, more realistic planet featured a landscape more consistent with the look of the surface. Specifically, its non-ice-covered portions were changed from a
blue
hue that suggested large
oceans
to an ocher that matched the
color
of the surface. The glacier-free areas were also more narrowly concentrated around the tropics of the planet, as the
script
suggested they should have been.
In addition to new shots of the
Enterprise
entering and leaving the orbit of the planet, old footage of the
landing party
beaming
to the surface was tracked into a brand new
matte painting
which expanded the scope of the original shot, showing a more mountainous and
snow
-covered landscape.
[1]
While in command and recording his only log entry in TOS,
Sulu
mistakenly called the planet "Sigma Draconis VII". Kirk also mistakenly referred to the planet as "Sigma Draconis VII" in his own later
captain's log
, even though he referred to the planet as "VI" twice earlier in the episode.
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
(pp. 18, 19, 34, 44) and
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Federation Historical Highlights, 2161-2385"), the Sigma Draconis system was located in
Sector 004
(Sigma Draconis sector). The Morg and the Eymorg formed a congress in 2268 after the crew of the
USS
Enterprise
reintegrated the civilization. In
2370
, there were an estimated 1.3 million Morg and Eymorg living on Sigma Draconis VI. This was a Federation system in the late
24th century
.
External links
Sigma Draconis VI
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Planets in TOS and TOS Remastered
at
Ex Astris Scientia

Sigma Draconis VII

was the

planet
in the

system
. Located in the

from

. This was one of six planets in this system that was not classified as

.
History
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of

in

, this planet was within the enclosed region. (

: "

")

path of Sigma Draconis VII were depicted on a system map that was displayed on the

in

mistakenly called

PAGE: Sigma Iotia II
CATEGORIES: Planets

======================================================================

Sigma Iotia II
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2324
)
Native species:
Iotians
Location:
Sigma Iotia
system
Beta Quadrant
Sigma Iotia II
was the
inhabited
second
planet
in the
Sigma Iotia
system
. The
Class M
planet was located in the
Beta Quadrant
and was the
homeworld
of the
Iotians
, a
pre-warp
humanoid
species
.
The planet was located in the outer reaches of the
Milky Way Galaxy
, from where it took a hundred
years
for conventional (non-subspace)
radio
messages to reach the
United Federation of Planets
in the
2260s
. (
TOS
: "
A Piece of the Action
")
History
In the mid-
22nd century
, the Iotian
civilization
was in the beginning stages of
industrialization
. In
2168
, the
starship
Horizon
discovered the planet and made
first contact
with the Iotians. The Iotians were found to be an extremely intelligent and imitative species. The first contact occurred before the
Prime Directive
was adopted by
Starfleet
.
When the
Horizon
left the planet, they left behind a number of
textbooks
and
technical manuals
on how to make radio sets and other similar technology. The book that became most influential was a text published on
Earth
in
1992
, called
Chicago Mobs of the Twenties
. This text became something of a "
Bible
" for the Iotian
culture
for
decades
to come. The Iotian
population
completely mimicked their society on the criminal underworld of Prohibition-era
Chicago
, which was described in great detail in the book.
The
Horizon
was lost shortly after it left Sigma Iotia II. As a result of
Chicago Mobs of the Twenties
, cultural development on the planet stalled to the
1920s
Earth
level, as planetary resources were wasted on endless gang wars between the different territories.
Iotian gangsters
In
2268
,
USS
Enterprise
came to visit the planet and to investigate the
cultural contamination
caused by the first contact, after the last radio messages of the
Horizon
were received by the Federation. In an attempt to manage the contamination and to steer the Iotian civilization to a more ethical path,
Captain
James T. Kirk
of the
Enterprise
posed as a
gangster
and established a syndicate of the criminal organizations controlling the planet. He left
Bela Okmyx
, the Iotian crime
boss
of the
Northside Territory
, in charge and demanded an annual cut of 40% for the Federation. In reality, the 40% was to be put into the planetary treasury and used as a resource to guide the Iotians into adopting a more ethical system of
government
.
After the
Enterprise
left the planet,
Doctor
Leonard McCoy
thought he might have forgotten his
communicator
on the planet.
Spock
noted that if the Iotians took it apart, they would have access to
transtator
technology, the basis for every important piece of
23rd century
Starfleet equipment. (
TOS
: "
A Piece of the Action
")
Known locations
Northside Territory
- Sigma Iotia II's largest
country
Southside Territory
Appendices
Background information
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 279 described Sigma Iotia II as a
class M
planet located some 100
light years
beyond
Federation space
.
In the
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine Companion
, p. 384, it was stated that the original idea for celebrating
Star Trek
's
30th anniversary
on
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine
was to have the crew return to Sigma Iotia to find the Iotians imitating the
original series
crew. It would have been a tongue-in-cheek to the "typical"
Star Trek
fan. The idea was dropped in favor of what became "
Trials and Tribble-ations
".
For the
2007
remastered edition of "A Piece of the Action", Sigma Iotia II was recreated with computer-generated imagery, making it appear as a more realistic and Earth-like planet.
Apocrypha
In
The Worlds of the Federation
, the Iotians did, in fact, learn
transtator
-based Federation technology from McCoy's communicator. By the time the next starship arrived to the planet, the Iotians had built a Federation
starbase
-like installation in
orbit
and were all wearing
Starfleet uniforms
. By the
24th century
, the planet had become a Federation
protectorate
.
However, according to the comic "
... Let's Kill All the Lawyers!
", the Iotians never touched McCoy's communicator for fear of angering him and eventually returned it to him decades later; their society was still modeled on Chicago's gangs, but it was more peaceful and law-abiding.
The planet was visited by the
USS
Enterprise
-D
with the 1998 story "
A Piece of Reaction
" in the final issue of
Star Trek Unlimited
.
In the
Star Trek: Starfleet Corps of Engineers
short story "
Fables of the Prime Directive
", Sigma Iotia eventually joined the
Federation
after years of work behind the scenes by cultural specialists.
In the NES version of the
video game
Star Trek: 25th Anniversary
, it was revealed that the dimensional gate that tossed the
Enterprise
into uncharted
space
at the beginning of the game was created by the Iotians, who gained accelerated knowledge of technology through the communicator, but ultimately destroyed themselves because of it. The crew goes back in time to get McCoy to get back his communicator to save the planet and others from the gate.
The
Star Trek: Picard
novel
Rogue Elements
depicted the Iotians as maintaining their cultural fixation with the culture of Chicago gangs even into the late
24th century
. In that novel,
Cristóbal Rios
dealt with an Iotian gang, and even purchased
La Sirena
from them.
External links
Sigma Iotia II
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Planets in TOS and TOS Remastered
at
Ex Astris Scientia

Sigma X
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
3189
)
Sigma X
was the
tenth
planet
in its
star system
.
Cleveland Booker
and
Michael Burnham
had an adventure on this planet in the late
32nd century
. Some time later, during their escape attempt from the
Emerald Chain
abord the
USS
Discovery
, Burnham compared the current situation to their experience on this planet. (
DIS
: "
That Hope Is You, Part 2
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Sikaris
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Sikaris
======================================================================

Sikaris
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2371
)
Native species:
Sikarians
Location:
Unnamed star system
Delta Quadrant
Sikaris
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Delta Quadrant
and was the
homeworld
of the
Sikarians
. Its
system
had a single
sun
. It had a
mantle
of tetrahedral
quartz
twenty
kilometers
thick, which served as an amplifier for the Sikarians'
spatial trajector
technology.
The
USS
Voyager
visited Sikaris for
shore leave
in
2371
, at the invitation of the Sikarians. (
VOY
: "
Prime Factors
")
Appendices
Background information
Given that the
distance
between Sikaris and Alastria is "two and a half billion times the distance Sikaris is from its sun", Sikaris would be 160 million kilometers from its sun.
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 284 classified Sikaris as an
M-class
planet.
According to the
Star Trek: Star Charts
(pp. 78, 83), the
Voyager
visited this planet on
stardate
48642. Sikaris was located in a
binary star system
(contradicting the episode). Both the primary and the secondary were
K-class stars
.
Sikaris was the
third
planet in the Sikaris system. This M-class planet was governed by the Sikaris Canon from the
capital city
of L'hur. The dominant
species
was the
humanoid
Sikarians. In
2378
, there were 620.5 million Sikarians living on the planet. Sikarians have been
warp-capable
since antiquity.
External link
Sikaris III
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

PAGE: Simperia

Simperia
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Cardassian
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Location:
Simperia
system
Cardassian space
Alpha Quadrant
Simperia
was a
planet
in the
Simperia
system
,
Alpha Quadrant
. This planet was in
Cardassian
space
.
History
During the
Dominion War
, this planet was home to a
Cardassian Liberation Front
base
. The base was one of eighteen destroyed by
Dominion
and
Breen
forces in late
2375
. (
DS9
: "
The Dogs of War
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to the
script
, Simperia was pronounced as "sem-PAIR-ee-ah".
External link
Simperia (planet)
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Sirah (site)
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Sirah_(site)
======================================================================

Labeled on a map of Bajor
Sirah
was a location in the
Hedrikspool Province
on
Bajor
.
In
2401
,
Admiral
Jean-Luc Picard
kept a
map
of Bajor's major
archaeological
sites in the
study
at
Château Picard
that identified Sirah among other sites. (
PIC
: "
The Star Gazer
")
This location was only mentioned in writing.
Sirah may be a reference to
the unnamed village
from
DS9
: "
The Storyteller
", though that was identified as a village, and Sirah is labeled as a "shrine or historic site" rather than a "city or village" on
Geoffrey Mandel
's archaeological sites map created for
Star Trek: Picard
.
[1]

For the similarly named location, please see
Sirius
.
Sirius Delta
was an inhabited
location
in the
Milky Way Galaxy
.
In
2328
, this was the destination for
Mandy Templeton
. She had traveled from
Simi Valley
,
Earth
on the
SS
Kogin
. (
TNG-R
: "
Inheritance
",
okudagram
)
This location was only mentioned in writing.

Warning!

Sirius IX
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
23rd century
)
Location:
Sirius
system
Beta Quadrant
~9
light years
from
Sol
Sirius IX
was the
inhabited
ninth planet
in the
Sirius
system
. Located in the
Beta Quadrant
, this planet was approximately 9
light years
from
Sol
.
History
In the
2260s
, the
planet
had at least a
thousand
inhabitants
, who allegedly had a unique
biochemistry
.
At some point before
stardate
4978
,
Harry Mudd
sold a
love potion
to a
thousand people
on Sirius IX. When they became
ill
, Mudd quickly left the planet. (
TAS
: "
Mudd's Passion
";
TNG
: "
The Naked Now
"
production art
)
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet was within the enclosed region. (
SA
: "
300th Night
")
Background information
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: The Next Generation
episode "
The Naked Now
" and the position of certain stars and systems from that chart as seen in the star charts appearing in the
Star Trek: Discovery
episode "
Magic to Make the Sanest Man Go Mad
" and in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I"), Sirius IX listed as a
Federation member
in
2378
.
The current location of Sirius IX in the Beta Quadrant was based on the most recent star charts seen in
Star Trek: Strange New Worlds
and
Star Trek: Section 31
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Skygowan
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Skygowan
======================================================================

Skygowan
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Independent
Status:
Habitable
(
2261
)
Native species:
Cali-Katchna natives
,
Skygowan natives
Location:
Ba-Dates system
Alpha Quadrant
Skygowan
was an
inhabited
class M
planet
in the
Ba-Dates
star system
. It was the
homeworld
of two indigenous
humanoid
species
: the
Cali-Katchna natives
who inhabited the primary
city
of
Cali-Katchna
, and the
Skygowan natives
who resided on the rest of the planet. Skygowan was connected by several "
ley lines
" to other locations in
space
, enabling
interdimensional
travel between them. Among those locations were
Vadia IX
and an unstable
wormhole
.
Astronomical data
Skygowan planetary analysis
The planet was located at
coordinates
3448.7421. It had a
rotation period
of 25.1
hours
and a
revolution period
of 355
days
. Its
mass
was estimated to be 6.38941
e24
×10
24
kilograms
, and its age at 6.947 billion
solar years
. Its
atmosphere
was composed of 78% molecular
nitrogen
, 20% molecular
oxygen
, 0.8%
argon
, 0.8%
water
vapor
, and 0.3%
carbon dioxide
, with trace amounts of
methane
and
nitrous oxide
.
The surface of Skygowan was mostly covered by
ocean
, with a global
archipelago
of
islands
and a mean
temperature
of 14.1
degrees
Celsius
.
Lifeforms
documented from the planet included 289
mammalian
, 472
avian
, 543
reptilian
and
amphibian
, 719
insect
, and 975
aquatic
species.
History
Despite being
pre-warp
, by the
23rd century
the people of Skygowan engaged in interstellar
trade
with the
Orions
and other
species
, most of whom were hostile to the
Federation
. The planet's native defensive capability was minimal, but they potentially had access to advanced
weapons
and
warp
technology
through the Orions. Skygowan
culture
was based on the
worship
of the
Vezda
as
gods
; they believed that in the "final days", the Vezda would come to "liberate" them.
In
2261
,
Doctor
Roger Korby
arrived on Skygowan to investigate Cali-Katchna, whose name bore a close phonetic similarity to a phrase uttered by a Vezda who had possessed
Dana Gamble
. He found that the local
High Cleric
,
Zeperez
, was in fact the same Vezda in Gamble's reconstituted body, having escaped the
USS
Enterprise
's
transport buffer
. Zeperez used Korby to lure the
Enterprise
to Skygowan, as he required Doctor
Joseph M'Benga
to activate an
interdimensional portal
to Vadia IX. Once there, he intended to destroy the
Beholder
and free the rest of his kind to possess the
population
of Skygowan. The portal sealed shut after their passage, as it was designed to permit only the two of them through.
To force the doorway open again,
Lieutenant
Spock
melded minds
with
Lieutenant Commander
James T. Kirk
of the
USS
Farragut
, allowing them to act as one to fire two perfectly synchronized
phaser
beams from their respective
starships
at the portal.
Captains
Christopher Pike
and
Marie Batel
then crossed through to Vadia IX, where Batel took advantage of her
hybridized
anti-Vezda
genetic memory
to become the Beholder and reseal all the Vezda in their
prison
. With the Vezda gone, Skygowan's people began to recover, as though "
waking up from a terrible
dream
.
" Korby remained on the planet to study the history of the Vezda; his research revealed a large number of uncharted planets ripe for exploration. (
SNW
: "
New Life and New Civilizations
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Skylab
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Skylab
======================================================================

A NASA insignia for Skylab
Skylab
was a
Human
space station
active in the late
20th century
. The station was placed into
Earth
orbit by the
United States of America
with
NASA
overseeing the facility. The solar-powered Skylab could house three astronauts. These astronauts were transported to the facility and were returned to Earth by a
command/service module
from the
Apollo program
.
The first manned mission to Skylab was Skylab 2. The three astronauts selected for this mission were
Conrad
(veteran of
Apollo 12
),
Kerwin
, and
Weitz
.
In
2063
, the
assignment patch
for the Skylab Program was displayed in the
Crash-n-Burn Bar
in
Bozeman
,
Montana
. (
Star Trek: First Contact
) Later, in
2143
, the patch for Skylab 2 was seen in the
602 Club
. (
ENT
: "
First Flight
")
External links
Skylab
at
Wikipedia
Skylab 2
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Sobras
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Sobras

Sobras
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Kazon-Pommar
Status:
Habitable
(
2372
)
Location:
Delta Quadrant
Sobras
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Delta Quadrant
. By the early
2370s
, there was a settlement of the
Kazon-Pommar
on this planet. This settlement included a
bar
and an underground
prison
.
Neelix
had visited Sobras before joining
USS
Voyager
, and had a
Kazon
acquaintance named
Tersa
there. In
2372
,
Voyager
passed near the planet, and it was decided that Neelix would travel there in an attempt to open talks regarding an
alliance
between
Voyager
and the Pommar.
Later, a
conference
between several
Kazon
sects
, the
crew
of the
USS
Voyager
, and a
Trabe
representative
was hosted on Sobras. However, the conference was ended prematurely when the Trabe unsuccessfully tried to
assassinate
the present
First Majes
. (
VOY
: "
Alliances
")
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 84), this planet was located in the Sobras
system
. This was a
binary star system
with a
F-class star
and a G-class star. This was a
Takrit
system. The
Voyager
visited this system on
stardate
49337.
External link
Sobras
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Sol Station
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Sol_Station
======================================================================

Sol Station
Type:
Sol Station
-type
Owner:
United Federation of Planets
Operator:
Starfleet
Location:
In
orbit
of
Earth
Sol system
Alpha Quadrant
Status:
Active
(
2402
)
Sol Station
(ID number Sol 398392), also known as
Probert Station
but most commonly referred to as
Spacedock
, was a
Federation
space station
located in
Earth
orbit
and operated by
Starfleet
in the early
25th century
. It resembled its
predecessor
in design, but featured several additional structures. The station was armed with
phasers
and
photon torpedoes
. (
PIC
: "
The Next Generation
", "
The Last Generation
")
Service history
In
2401
,
Admiral
Jean-Luc Picard
and
Captain
William T. Riker
took the
USS
Titan
-A
out of the station on an unauthorized mission to find
Doctor
Beverly Crusher
. (
PIC
: "
The Next Generation
")
The following week, Sol Station was the site of celebrations for
Frontier Day
, for which the entire Federation
fleet
had been gathered. Amidst a
fireworks display
, the soon-to-be
decommissioned
USS
Enterprise
-F
was launched from the station for its farewell cruise, which was followed by a demonstration of
fleet formation mode
.
Sol Station's shields fail under fire from a Borg-controlled fleet
Shortly after, the fleet was taken over by the
Borg Collective
and ordered to target Sol Station and the rest of Earth's
planetary defenses
. The station managed to hold off the assault for some time, long enough for
President
Anton Chekov
to broadcast a
distress call
, but ultimately its
shields
failed and it suffered severe damage. As the station fell, so did Earth's
planetary shields
, leaving the planet defenseless. Earth was saved when the
USS
Enterprise
-D
destroyed the
Borg cube
controlling Starfleet. (
PIC
: "
Võx
", "
The Last Generation
")
The rebuilt Sol Station
One year later
, Sol Station had been fully repaired. It saw the launch of the newly rechristened
USS
Enterprise
-G
, which was attended by Admirals Jean-Luc Picard and Beverly Crusher. (
PIC
: "
The Last Generation
")
Appendices
Appearances
PIC
:
"
The Next Generation
"
"
Võx
"
"
The Last Generation
"
Background information
This station's other name, "Probert Station", appeared in an
okudagram
on Riker and Picard's shuttle in "
The Next Generation
", but was not clearly visible on screen. It honors artist
Andrew Probert
.
Apocrypha
In the game
Star Trek Online
, the old
Spacedock One
has been replaced by the Sol Station to fit with
Star Trek: Picard
Season 3
's canon standard. It was released on
9 January
2025
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Solais V
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Solais_V
======================================================================

Solais V
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2371
)
Native species:
Solari
Location:
Solais
system
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Solais V
was the
inhabited
fifth
planet
of the
Solais
system
. This
Class M
planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
and was the
homeworld
of the
Solari
, a
humanoid
species
.
In
2365
, after
fifteen centuries
of
warfare
, the two warring
Solari
factions
were on the brink of mutual
extinction
.
That
year
,
Riva
was called in to mediate
peace
negotiations
on the planet when he was attacked and his
chorus
was killed at the site of the historic
Battle of Zambrano
, located on the
northern
hemisphere
of the planet.
Despite the great loss, Riva remained on Solais V to continue the negotiations. (
TNG
: "
Loud As A Whisper
")
In late
2371
, the
SS
Xhosa
under
Kasidy Yates
hauled a shipment of
duridium
ore
to the planet. The
Xhosa
was scheduled to return to
Deep Space 9
about a
month
later. (
DS9
: "
The Adversary
")
"so-LAY-is" was the pronunciation for this planet's
name
from the
script
pronunciation guides for both "Loud as a Whisper" and "The Adversary".
[1]
[2]
The planet model first used here for Solais V was later used for the
Koinonian homeworld
("
The Bonding
") and
Kalla III
("
Firstborn
").
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 295 classified Solais V as a
class M
planet.
External links
Solais V
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Re-Used Planets in TNG
at
Ex Astris Scientia

PAGE: Solarion IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Solarion_IV

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Solarion IV
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
(
3196
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Location:
Solarion
system
Unnamed sector
Alpha Quadrant
Solarion IV
was the
fourth
planet
in the
Solarion
system
. This world was in a
sector
of the
Alpha Quadrant
.
History
In the
24th century
, this planet was near the
Federation-Cardassian border
. It was the home for a
Federation
colony
.
In
2368
, the
Cardassians
destroyed the colony in a plot to frame the
Bajorans
.
Survivors
were evacuated to
Lya Station Alpha
by the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. (
TNG
: "
Ensign Ro
")
In the
32nd century
, Solarion IV was a
Federation
member
planet. It was listed with other members on a video wall in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
Kids These Days
")
Stellar cartography
The
location
of Solarion IV was labeled on a tactical map during the
Dominion War
. (
DS9
: "
Tears of the Prophets
",
display graphic
)
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of the
Cardassian Union
in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
In the
script
, the pronunciation for Solarion was "soh-LEHR-ee-un".
[1]
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 47; "United Federation of Planets I") and
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Federation Historical Highlights, 2161-2385"), it was implied that the colony had been rebuilt on Solarion IV and, by
2378
, had become a
Federation member
.

at

, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek

PAGE: Solosos III
CATEGORIES: Planets

Solosos III
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Maquis
(formerly)
Cardassian Union
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Location:
Solosos
system
Alpha Quadrant
Solosos III
was the
inhabited
Class M
third
planet
of the
Solosos system
,
Alpha Quadrant
.
History
From
2368
to
2373
, this planet's system was in the
Demilitarized Zone
.
This planet was the site of a
Maquis
colony
. In
2373
,
Captain
Benjamin Sisko
ordered the
USS
Defiant
to fire two
quantum torpedoes
with a 400
kilogram
payload of
trilithium resin
into the
atmosphere
of Solosos III, rendering it uninhabitable to
Humans
for the following
fifty years
. His actions were designed to force Maquis
leader
Michael Eddington
to
surrender
himself and the
biogenic weapons
he had been deploying against the
Cardassians
. The planet was subsequently settled by Cardassian
refugees
displaced from
Veloz Prime
and
Quatal Prime
. (
DS9
: "
For the Uniform
")
According to the
script
, the pronunciation for Solosos was "sow-LOW-soze".
[1]
The planet's class came from the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 296.

at

, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek

Soltok IV
Type:

Affiliation:

-
73
)
Alpha Quadrant
Soltok IV
was the
inhabited
fourth
planet
in its
star system
. This planet was located in the
Alpha Quadrant
and was inside the
Demilitarized Zone
in the early
2370s
.
In
2370
,
Lieutenant Commander
Calvin Hudson
claimed that a
Federation merchant vessel
, fired upon by the
Cardassians
, was transporting
medical
supplies to Soltok IV.
Gul
Evek
claimed that the ship was carrying
weapons
and had refused to be boarded. (
DS9
: "
The Maquis, Part I
")
Background information
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to
StarTrek.com
, this planet, located in the Soltok system,
orbited
a
star
named Soltok.
[1]
(X)
;
[2]
(X)
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 245 and the closed captioning for the episode spelled the name of this planet as "Saltok IV". This spelling was used by
Geoffrey Mandel
for his star charts in the
Star Trek: Star Charts
when labeling the location of this system.
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 47, "United Federation of Planets I") and
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Federation Historical Highlights, 2161-2385"), the star of the Saltok was a
K-class star
. In

.

======================================================================
PAGE: Solum
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Solum
======================================================================

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Solum
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Elders
of Solum
The Order
(alternate timeline)
Status:
Inhabited
(
2385
)
Satellite(s):
Ring system
,
one moon
Native species:
Vau N'Akat
Location:
Delta Quadrant
Solum
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Delta Quadrant
, and the
homeworld
of the
Vau N'Akat
. It was surrounded by a
ring system
. (
PRO
: "
A Moral Star, Part 2
", "
Preludes
")
History
In the late
24th century
, Solum was habitable and prospering, the Vau N'Akat believing they were the only
sentient
species in existence. It was ruled by a High Council in
2384
. (
PRO
: "
Into the Breach, Part II
").
Original timeline
At some point after
2384
, the
United Federation of Planets
made
first contact
with the Vau N'Akat when a
Prometheus
-class
starship
arrived at Solum. Fifty years later, the planet had descended into
a
civil war
over whether to pursue relations with aliens, which resulted in the fall of their civilization and the near-
extinction
of the species. The Federation chose to not intervene in the conflict. (
PRO
: "
A Moral Star, Part 2
", "
Preludes
")
Solum devastated by civil war
When a new Starfleet ship, the
USS
Protostar
, ended up on Solum after passing through a temporal anomaly, the Vau N'Akat of
The Order
saw it as a chance to reset their world's history by destroying the Federation before First Contact. However, their plan was thwarted by
Captain
Chakotay
who remotely sent his weaponized ship back into the past. With only a small chance of success, the Order sent one hundred Vau N'Akat, each with a
Drednok
, after the
Protostar
before the collapse of the anomaly. (
PRO
: "
A Moral Star, Part 2
", "
Preludes
")
Altered timeline
In the prime timeline, the intervention of
Dal R'El
,
Jankom Pog
,
Zero
, and
Maj'el
saw the
Protostar
launching from Solum with
Chakotay
and
Adreek-Hu
on board, resulting in it never reaching
Tars Lamora
, altering history, while at the same time,
Asencia
moved to take over the Solum of
2384
to destroy the Federation by force. (
PRO
: "
Who Saves the Saviors
")
After solidifying her hold on the planet, Asencia began attempting to use temporal technology stolen from the mind of
Wesley Crusher
to destroy the Federation and ensure her planet's survival, turning into a tyrant in the process. Wesley was later rescued by Dal, Jankom, Zero,
Rok-Tahk
,
Gwyndala
,
Murf
, and Maj'el. (
PRO
: "
Ascension, Part I
", "
Ascension, Part II
", "
Brink
", "
Touch of Grey
")
Asencia subsequently attempted to launch her fleet, but was stopped by the crews of the
Protostar
and the
USS
Voyager
-A
. With the help of the Vau N'Akat, Gwyn defeated Asencia who was imprisoned by her people for her crimes. Although the
Loom
attacked Solum, they retreated after the
Protostar
was sent back in time, fixing the timeline. Afterwards, the Federation officially made peaceful
first contact
with Solum which headed into a new peaceful era, the
civil war
that had destroyed the planet averted. (
PRO
: "
Ouroboros, Part I
", "
Ouroboros, Part II
")
Wesley suggested that Ilthuran would end up leading his people into a better future after Asencia's defeat, although it was unclear if this was altered by Ilthuran's rescue from captivity which didn't happen in the future Wesley foresaw which had Ilthuran become the new leader of Solum following his defeat of Asencia. However, Ilthuran's leading presence at first contact with the Federation would suggest that he did indeed become the leader of his people or at least one of their leaders. (
PRO
: "
Brink
", "
Ouroboros, Part II
")
External link
Solum
at
StarTrek.com

======================================================================
PAGE: Son'a (location)
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Son'a_(location)
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Son'a
Status:
Intact
(
3196
)
Location:
Alpha Quadrant
Son'a
was a location in the
Alpha Quadrant
.
History
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this location was within the blast radius of one of the mines. (
SA
: "
Rubincon
")
Stellar Cartography
In
2256
, its location was labeled on the
star chart
"Alpha/Beta Quadrant Overview" in the
ready room
aboard the
USS
Discovery
. (
DIS
: "
Magic to Make the Sanest Man Go Mad
")
In
2257
, during the first
Federation-Klingon War
, this location was occupied by the
Klingons
. (
DIS
: "
The War Without, The War Within
")
In
2259
, Son'a's location was labeled on a
stellar cartography
chart that was seen on the
USS
Enterprise
's
ready room
viewscreen
. Son'a's symbol had a
blue
color
, indicating that it was affiliated with the
United Federation of Planets
. (
SNW
: "
Strange New Worlds
", "
Spock Amok
")
Son'a's location was labeled in a
Federation star chart
that was in
Fleet Admiral
Kirsten Clancy
's
office
at
Starfleet Headquarters
in
2399
and on the
bridge
of the
USS
Titan
-A
in
2401
. This location was in or near to neutral space. (
PIC
: "
Maps and Legends
", "
The Next Generation
", "
Disengage
")
In
3196
, during the
Omega-47
crisis, the location of Son'a was depicted on
star charts
and readouts used across several locations on the
USS
Athena
to locate, monitor, and respond to the Omega-47 mines laid by the Venari Ral. (
SA
: "
Rubincon
")
This location was only mentioned in writing.
The
Son'a
may be associated with this location.
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 62) and
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Federation Historical Highlights, 2161-2385"), Son'a was an
M-class star
.

Sothis III
Type:
Planet

Non-aligned

or

Sothis III
was the
inhabited
third
planet
in its
star system
.
It was the
homeworld
of the
Satarrans
, a
humanoid
civilization
who allegedly hated
mysteries
and were not as
technologically
advanced as the
Federation
. They possessed a unique
device
which allowed them to wipe the collective
memory
of the entire
crew
of a
starship
. They also possessed the capability to impersonate other humanoid appearances.
By
2368
, the Satarrans had been at
war
with the
Lysians
for
decades
. (
TNG
: "
Conundrum
", "
The Chase
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.

script
for "The Chase", the pronunciation for Sothis was "SO-this".

======================================================================
PAGE: Soukara
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Soukara
======================================================================

Soukara
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Dominion
(
2373
-
75
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Satellite(s):
At least
two moons
Location:
Soukara
system
Alpha Quadrant
Soukara
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Soukara
system
,
Alpha Quadrant
. Near to an
asteroid field
, this world had at least
two moons
.
History
During the
Dominion War
, this planet, in
Dominion
-controlled
space
, was home to a Dominion
base
. The base was deep inside a
jungle
, and was protected by
transport scramblers
and a
sensor grid
. This grid had three gaps, found in the asteroid field, which could be exploited by a hostile ship as it would be undetectable to the
sensors
.
In
2374
,
Worf
and
Jadzia Dax
were sent on a covert
mission
to Soukara to recover a Cardassian
defector
,
Lasaran
, who claimed to know the location and false identity of every
Founder
in the
Alpha Quadrant
. Unfortunately, Dax was severely injured by a
Jem'Hadar
patrol
, and Worf chose to abort the mission to seek medical care for her.
Starfleet Intelligence
later intercepted a transmission confirming Lasaran was killed trying to re-enter the Dominion base on Soukara. (
DS9
: "
Change of Heart
")
Later that
year
,
Romulan
Senator
Vreenak
met with
Weyoun
on Soukara. On the way back to Romulus, he took a secret detour to
Deep Space 9
at
Captain
Benjamin Sisko
's request. Some time after leaving Deep Space 9, Vreenak was killed, along with his staff, when a
bomb
planted on his
shuttle
by former
Obsidian Order
operative
Elim Garak
detonated, as part of a Federation plot to deceive the
Romulan Star Empire
to break its
nonaggression pact
with the Dominion. The
Tal Shiar
, upon investigating the wreckage, recovered a badly damaged
Cardassian
optolythic data rod
containing
evidence
the Dominion was planning to invade Romulus; unaware of Vreenak's detour to Deep Space 9 or that the evidence was in fact a
forgery
commissioned by Garak and Sisko as part of the plan, the Tal Shiar incorrectly concluded that Vreenak must have obtained the evidence on Soukara, and the Dominion
assassinated
him before he could expose them when he returned to Romulus (with their subsequent denials only adding veracity to the Romulans' conclusion). As a result, the Romulan Star Empire broke its non-aggression pact and formally declared war on the Dominion. (
DS9
: "
In the Pale Moonlight
")
Soukara and its moons
Soukara's moons as viewed planetside
In the
script
for "Change of Heart", the pronunciation for Soukara was given as "sue-KAR-uh".
External link
Soukara
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

International Space Station
Type:
Scientific Research Facility
Affiliation:
NASA
Russian Space Agency
European Space Agency
Location:
In
orbit
of
Earth
Sol
system
Sector 001
Alpha Quadrant
Status:
Active
(
2022
)
Crew:
At least 4
Docking facilities:
At least one airlock for
space shuttle orbiters
The
International Space Station
was a
space station
and one of
Earth
's first internationally combined efforts in exploring
space
. The station was jointly serviced by
American
space shuttle orbiters
and
Russian
Soyuz
and
Progress
spacecraft.
In
2000
,
astronaut
Lieutenant
McMillan
was scheduled to be the co-
pilot
on a joint mission between
NASA
and the
Europeans
for a four
month
tour on the International Space Station that began in
2003
. (
VOY
: "
11:59
")
The
space shuttle
Atlantis
delivered a
Spacehab
module to the ISS in the early
21st century
. (
Star Trek: Enterprise
, opening credits)
In
2020
,
Dragon 2
delivered astronauts to the station. (
PIC
: "
Two of One
")
The
assignment patches
for the ISS Program and for a three-man team were on display in the
602 Club
in
2143
. (
ENT
: "
First Flight
")
The ISS in the time stream
After
Jonathan Archer
restored a damaged timeline, the ISS could be seen in the
time stream
as the timeline realigned itself. (
ENT
: "
Storm Front, Part II
")
A sketch depicting an overhead view of the ISS was contained in the
library computer
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
in
2254
. (
TOS-R
: "
The Cage
")
A model of the Space Station Freedom
Beginning with the year
2370
,
Benjamin Sisko
had a
model
in his office aboard
Deep Space 9
of the
Space Station Freedom
, which had the space shuttle
Enterprise
docked with it. The model was housed in a glass casing in the beginning but was later displayed without the casing in his office next to
Ops
. (
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine
)
ISS crew
Gidzenko, Yuri
Krikalev, Sergei
McMillan
Shepherd, William

:
"

"

Background information
The model of the Space Station seen in
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine
was given to the series by
Majel Barrett
as a gift. The DS9 art department added a model of the
Enterprise
(OV-101) and displayed it docked to the space station. (
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 372)
Although often referred to as the International Space Station, the model is actually of the then-proposed 1993 American-led Space Station Freedom.
A montage of the International Space Station (including the since cancelled Russian Science Power and Research modules) being constructed is featured in the
opening credits
of
Star Trek: Enterprise
.
The names Gidzenko, Krikalyov, and Shepherd were names seen on a mission insignia in the 602 Club. They were the first inhabitants of the space station on Expedition 1. Their mission patch, seen in the 602 Club, showed the space station on a blue background and the names of the Russian astronauts were written in their native Cyrillic alphabet.
E. Michael Fincke
, who appeared along with fellow NASA astronaut
Terry Virts
in
ENT
: "
These Are The Voyages...
", has spent a year total aboard the ISS, from April to October 2004 (speaking with
Scott Bakula
while in space) and again from October 2008 to April 2009. Virts himself piloted the shuttle
Endeavour
on a mission to the station in February 2010. He later returned to the station in November 2014 as Flight Engineer of Expedition 42/43.
According to the reference work
Ships of the Line
(p. 8), in the 2150s, the ISS has remained in Low Earth Orbit and hasn't been destroyed by a controlled re-entry. (In the real world, the ISS is planned for controlled re-entry 2030, a similar fate to MIR.)
From the
Encyclopedia
(4th ed., vol. 1, p. 372), "
The International Space Station (ISS) was featured in the main title sequence of
Star Trek: Enterprise
, beginning with the first episode, "
Broken Bow
". The decision to add the ISS to the
Enterprise
titles was a last-minute decision on the part of the producers. Because there was so little time to obtain accurate reference information for the construction of a digital model for visual effects, NASA
engineer
(and
Star Trek
fan) Brian J. Young, who at the time was a technician working for the
United Space Alliance
at NASA's
Kennedy Space Center
in
Florida
, went back to work on a Friday night and faxed us the plans for the station!
"
The Window Observational Research Facility (WORF), installed in 2010, is an experiment facility, through which crew members and remotely operated payloads can perform Earth and space science research, including photography, at the US Laboratory (known as Destiny) Science Window on the International Space Station. It was admitted that the naming was in tribute to the
Star Trek
character and the mission patch produced even had Klingon text on it.
[1]
External links
International Space Station
at
Wikipedia
Expedition 1
at
Wikipedia
Space Station Freedom
at
Wikipedia
Window Observational Research Facility
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Space station
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Space_station
======================================================================

Deep Space 9
, a Cardassian-built Starfleet/Bajoran space station
Starbase 375
, a
Federation
space station near the Cardassian border
A
space station
was an artificial structure in
space
, often built to support
life
. Space stations could serve many purposes, including
research
,
defense
, and
starship
maintenance. Depending on their purpose, they may have been referred to by another term, such as a
spacelab
or outpost. Space stations could be in
orbit
of a
planet
, or be completely free floating in space.
Space stations were used by the
Federation
, the
Klingons
, the
Cardassians
, and other
races
. They could be used as
outposts
often located in "frontier" areas or along interstellar borders,
research stations
specialized in scientific research,
shipyards
specialized in the construction of starships, or as orbital components of
starbases
such as a
spacedock
.
Stations that were positioned on the edge of the Federation border (like
Deep Space K-7
) or, in the case of
Deep Space 9
, in neutral territory close to the Federation border, were labeled as Deep Space stations.
A
Vulcan
space station was located around fifty
light years
from Deep Space 9. (
DS9
: "
Take Me Out to the Holosuite
")
In an unusual use of the term,
Commander
William T. Riker
designated
Farpoint Station
as a space station in "
Encounter at Farpoint
".
See also
Fixed installations
External links
Space station
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Space station
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Spacedock One
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Spacedock_One
======================================================================

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Spacedock One
Type:
Spacedock
-type
Owner:
United Federation of Planets
Operator:
Fleet Museum
Starfleet
Location:
In
orbit
of
Athan Prime
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Formerly Earth Orbit
Status:
Active
(
2401
)
Spacedock One
was a
Federation
Spacedock
-type
space station
operated by
Starfleet
from the late
23rd
to the early
25th centuries
. The prototypical example of a "
spacedock
", it was located in
orbit
of
Earth
for most of its service, until it was moved to
Athan Prime
sometime before
2401
.
History
Spacedock under construction
Spacedock One was under construction in
2257
. (
DIS
: "
Will You Take My Hand?
")
By
2285
, Spacedock One was completed and fully operational. (
Star Trek III: The Search for Spock
)
In 2285, the
USS
Enterprise
returned to Spacedock One after serving as a
cadet
training ship
on an ill-fated mission in which
Khan
's plan to
steal
the
Genesis Device
and kill
Admiral
James T. Kirk
for revenge was thwarted. At Spacedock One, the
Enterprise
was to be decommissioned, rather than repaired. Kirk – with the help of
Captain
Scott
as well as
Commanders
Uhura
,
Sulu
and
Chekov
–
stole
the
Enterprise
to rescue
Spock
's body from the
Genesis Planet
. The
USS
Excelsior
attempted to give chase, but had been
sabotaged
by Captain Scott, and "stalled" when it attempted to jump to
transwarp
just outside the station. (
Star Trek III: The Search for Spock
)
Due to an unknown probe, Spacedock One is without power
In
2286
, an
unknown probe
entirely disabled Spacedock One upon reaching Earth. The shutdown happened before any vessels within, including the
Excelsior
, could be launched, resulting in the equally powerless vessels being trapped behind the space doors. When Kirk and his crew had resolved the crisis, power was restored as the probe left the planet. Kirk was
demoted
to captain, and offered command of a starship – namely the
USS
Enterprise
-A
, which was docked within Spacedock One. (
Star Trek IV: The Voyage Home
)
After a brief shakedown cruise, the
Enterprise
-A returned to Spacedock One while Captain Scott worked to resolve design flaws made apparent by the ship's maiden voyage. In
2287
the
starship
ultimately departed in a hurry, while repairs were still underway, to deal with the crisis on
Nimbus III
, with the final
shuttlecraft
carrying the
command crew
back from
shore leave
rendezvousing with the ship after it had cleared the station. (
Star Trek V: The Final Frontier
)
The USS
Enterprise
-A departing from Spacedock in 2293
The
Enterprise
-A was moored at Spacedock One prior to an ill-fated trip to escort
Klingon
Chancellor
Gorkon
to a peace conference on Earth in
2293
. Kirk ordered a departure from the facility at
impulse
speed, despite the fact that regulations specified
thrusters
only while inside the structure. Although
Lieutenant
Valeris
, the ship's
helmsman
, initially questioned the captain's order, she successfully performed the departure and later admitted she had "always wanted to try that."
Following the
Khitomer Conference
, the
Enterprise
-A was to return to Spacedock One to be decommissioned by the orders of
Starfleet Command
. (
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
)
In an
alternate timeline
,
Harry Kim
and
Tom Paris
stole the
prototype
runabout
USS
Yellowstone
from Spacedock One in
2372
.
(
VOY
: "
Non Sequitur
")
Captain
Carol Freeman
of the
USS
Cerritos
once escorted the
USS
Enterprise
out of Spacedock One. (
LD
: "
An Embarrassment Of Dooplers
")
By
2401
, Spacedock One had been retired from service and moved to
Athan Prime
to house the
Fleet Museum
. It was replaced by the larger and more advanced
Sol Station
, which was also commonly referred to as "Spacedock". (
PIC
: "
The Bounty
")
Personnel
See
:
Spacedock One personnel
Appendices
Appearances
Star Trek
films
:
Star Trek III: The Search for Spock
Star Trek IV: The Voyage Home
Star Trek V: The Final Frontier
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
VOY
: "
Non Sequitur
"
DIS
: "
Will You Take My Hand?
"
PIC
:
"
The Bounty
"
"
Võx
"
"
The Last Generation
"
Background information
Spacedock concept sketch
Production fact sheet
In script sources, this facility is most commonly known simply as "Spacedock", with an emphasis on the term being capitalized.
In one concept sketch that
Industrial Light & Magic
created for Spacedock, the facility had a
registry
number of NC-7011S. (
Star Trek: The Magazine
Volume 3, Issue 8
, p. 49)
Spacedock was imagined as having a total height of over three miles. At that size, the facility could easily be visible with the naked eye from the surface of Earth. (
text commentary
,
Star Trek III: The Search for Spock (Special Edition)
,
DVD
) This is corroborated with the specs released by
Dave Blass
, which gave the dimensions of the station as 3810 meters (2.4 miles) in diameter and 5795 meters (3.6 miles) in height.
[1]
Although windows providing a view of Spacedock's interior docking bay appear only in
Star Trek III: The Search for Spock
, they were also originally intended to be seen in
Star Trek IV: The Voyage Home
as well, with cheering audience members gathered by them. (
The Making of the Trek Films
, 3rd ed., p. 64)
It was not made clear on-screen whether the facility featured in "
Non Sequitur
" was intended to be the same spacedock as seen in
the films
, as the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
speculates. The only view of the station in the
Star Trek: Voyager
episode is of a set of doors, themselves stock footage of the
Dyson sphere
doors from
Star Trek: The Next Generation
's "
Relics
".
In response to a question on Twitter,
Dave Blass
stated that the station was moved from its original location to Athan Prime via the use of "
warp
tugs
".
[2]
Apocrypha
Star Trek: Starship Spotter
describes Earth's Spacedock, the first of its design, as being conceived during conflicts with the
Klingon Empire
, approved in
2259
, and completed in
2276
, with enlargements to two entry doors in the mid-24th century to accommodate larger vessels such as the
Galaxy
-class
. Their specifications, as of
2290
, include an overall length of 4,600 meters, overall beam of 4,600 meters, overall draft of 6,900-6,950 meters, crew complement of 76,625 (crew, civilians and transients.) Typical support craft include 285
workbees
, 100 shuttlecraft of various types, and 50
Danube
-class
runabouts
. (pp. 72-73)
For the month of December in the
2010
Star Trek: Ships of the Line
calendar, Spacedock is shown being constructed as the USS
Enterprise
's
2270s
refit
is nearing completion.
In an alternate timeline depicted in the game
Star Trek: Armada
, the
Borg
either destroyed or assimilated Spacedock.
In the game
Star Trek Online
, this station was replaced with another sometime before the year 2409. However, the new design was not popular amongst players, and there were many calls for it to be replaced with the classic facility. For STO's first anniversary, on
2 February
2011
, the developers replaced the new Spacedock with the original station, explaining it as being the work of
Q
. Though the external model, as visible from space, appeared to be the classic Spacedock, the interior area, which served as a major player hub, remained based on the original, launch design. With the release of the game's Season 9 update, the Earth Spacedock received a second revamp to correct that inconsistency, with the interior area receiving a major redesign to be more appropriate to the scale and presumed layout of the station. The change was justified in the story via the mission "Surface Tension", in which the
Undine
caused severe damage to the station during their attack on Earth, necessitating extensive repairs and relocation of player activities to a different area.
External links
Earth Spacedock
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Earth Spacedock
at
the
Star Trek Online
Wiki

Stakoron II

. This system was located in the

.

, the

had opened negotiations with the

ore
. (

and an A-class star.

======================================================================
PAGE: Stameris

Warning!

Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain

Stameris
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Location:
Lambda Serpentis
system
Alpha Quadrant
~39
light years
from
Sol
Stameris
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Lambda Serpentis
system
. Located in the
Alpha Quadrant
, this planet was approximately 39
light years
from
Sol
.
History
On this world, there was an active
slave market
in the
2150s
.
The
Ferengi
who attempted to
pirate
Enterprise
NX-01
in
2151
contemplated selling some of the
starship
's
women
at the
slave
market on Stameris. (
ENT
: "
Acquisition
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet was within the blast radius of one of the mines. (
SA
: "
300th Night
", "
Rubincon
")
Stellar cartography
In
3195
, the position of Stameris (Lambda Serpentis) was labeled on a
Federation
star chart
which was used in
Starfleet Academy
's
Temporal Mechanics
classroom
. (
SA
: "
Beta Test
")
In
3196
, during the
Omega-47
crisis, the location of Stameris (Lambda Serpentis) was depicted on star charts and readouts used across several locations on the
USS
Athena
to locate, monitor, and respond to the
Omega-47 mines
laid by the Venari Ral. (
SA
: "
300th Night
", "
Rubincon
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Stardust City Medical District
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Stardust_City_Medical_District
======================================================================

Raffi locates her son in the Stardust City Medical District
The
Stardust City Medical District
was a
district
of
Stardust City
.
It was here that many
medical
facilities such as
hospitals
were located.
Once such facility was the
Stardust City Reproductive Health Services
, which was where
Raffi Musiker
located her
son
,
Gabriel Hwang
. (
PIC
: "
Stardust City Rag
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Starfleet Recruitment
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Starfleet_Recruitment
======================================================================

The Starfleet Recruitment building on M'talas Prime
Starfleet Recruitment
was a
Starfleet
facility where individuals could
apply
to
enlist
in
Starfleet
, or be
recruited
into
Starfleet Academy
. This facility was located in
District Seven
on
M'talas Prime
. According to
Raffaela Musiker
, it was "
a mid-level Starfleet recruitment bureau.
"
A
red
statue
of
Captain
Rachel Garrett
stood at the front of the
building
, and some of the
locals
referred to it as the "
Red Lady
."
In
2401
, a
terrorist
plot against Starfleet Recruitment was uncovered by
Starfleet Intelligence
operative
Musiker, who had learned that the facility was to be
targeted
with a
quantum tunneling
weapon
that was developed at one of
Daystrom's
off-site
stations
.
Unable to hail the facility, she was too late to prevent the disaster, and as a result of the
weapon
's deployment, the entire
building
fell through a
wormhole
-like
tunnel
whose other end opened up high in the sky over the
city
, dropping the collapsed building onto the ground below. (
PIC
: "
The Next Generation
")
A
news
report later stated that there were
117 confirmed casualties
from the attack. (
PIC
: "
Disengage
")
The location of Starfleet Recruitment was depicted using footage of downtown Rio de Janeiro, Brazil. The building, created by CGI, replaced the famous soccer venue, Maracanã Stadium.
[1]
[2]
External link
Starfleet Recruitment
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

Star Trek Generations
)

======================================================================
PAGE: Straleb
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Straleb
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Straleb
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Coalition of Madena
Status:
Habitable
(
3195
)
Native species:
Straleb inhabitants
Location:
Omega Sagitta
system
Alpha Quadrant
Straleb
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Omega Sagitta
system
. The planet was located in the
Alpha Quadrant
.
History
In the early
2160s
, this planet, and its twin
Atlec
, were
colonized
by a
humanoid species
. These twin planets formed the
Coalition of Madena
, which was created by a
treaty
. In the mid-
24th century
, Straleb was ruled by a
governmental organization
called the
Legation of Unity
.
In
2365
, the
USS
Enterprise
-D
encountered a
ship
from Straleb. (
TNG
: "
The Outrageous Okona
")
People
Straleb inhabitants
Stellar cartography
In
2371
, the
location
of Straleb was labeled in the
star chart
Data
and
Picard
were studying in
stellar cartography
. (
Star Trek Generations
,
okudagram
)
In
3195
, this planet's position was labeled on a
Federation
star chart which was displayed in
Starfleet Academy
's Stellar Cartography
laboratory
. (
SA
: "
Beta Test
")
In
3196
, during the
Omega-47
crisis, the location of Omega Sagitta (Madena, Atlec, Straleb) was depicted on star charts and readouts used across several locations on the
USS
Athena
to locate, monitor, and respond to the
Omega-47 mines
laid by the
Venari Ral
. This system and its planets were outside the barrier. (
SA
: "
300th Night
", "
Rubincon
")
Appendices
Background information
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the star chart appearing in the
Star Trek: Discovery
episode "
An Obol for Charon
".
The reference from
Generations
identified this planet as "Straleb Madena".
At
StarTrek.com
, Straleb was identified as one half of a pair of
Trojan
planets in the Omegga Sagitta system.
[1]
(X)
External link
Straleb
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

PAGE: Styris IV

======================================================================

Styris IV
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Habitable
(
2364
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Styris IV
was the
inhabited
fourth
planet
in its
star system
. The planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
and was affiliated with the
Federation
.
In
2364
, a rare
vaccine
from
Ligon II
was needed to combat an outbreak of
Anchilles fever
on Styris IV and the
USS
Enterprise
-D
was tasked with
treaty
negotiations
to acquire it. During the delay in obtaining the vaccine,
Starbase 14
informed the
Enterprise
of the
infection rates
and projected
deaths
on the planet. (
TNG
: "
Code of Honor
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
External link
Styris IV
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Suliban (location)
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Suliban_(location)
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Suliban
was a location in the
Beta Quadrant
.
In
2259
, Suliban's location was labeled on a
stellar cartography
chart that was seen on the
USS
Enterprise
's
ready room
viewscreen
. This location's symbol had a
blue
color
, indicating that it was affiliated with the
United Federation of Planets
. (
SNW
: "
Strange New Worlds
", "
Children of the Comet
", "
Spock Amok
")
Suliban's location was labeled in a
Federation star chart
that was in
Fleet Admiral
Kirsten Clancy
's
office
at
Starfleet Headquarters
in
2399
and on the
bridge
of the
USS
Titan
-A
in
2401
. This location was in or near to
Federation space
. (
PIC
: "
Maps and Legends
", "
The Next Generation
", "
Disengage
")
In
3196
, during the
Omega-47
crisis, the location of "Suliban" was depicted on
star charts
and readouts used across several locations on the
USS
Athena
to locate, monitor, and respond to the
Omega-47 mines
laid by the
Venari Ral
. This location was inside the enclosed region. (
SA
: "
Rubincon
")
This location might be associated with the
Suliban homeworld
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Sullivan's Planet
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Sullivan's_Planet
======================================================================

Sullivan's Planet
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2261
)
Location:
Beta Quadrant
Sullivan's Planet
was an
inhabited
M-class
planet
. It was the
homeworld
of a
pre-warp civilization
and had a population of over one hundred million in the mid-
23rd century
. The planet possessed significant deposits of
aldentium
.
In
2261
,
Lieutenant
Spock
and
Ensign
Nyota Uhura
determined Sullivan's Planet to be the next target of a massive
scavenger ship
that had destroyed
Helicon Gamma
for its aldentium. The
USS
Farragut
managed to overtake and destroy the vessel before it reached its destination. (
SNW
: "
The Sehlat Who Ate Its Tail
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Sumiko III
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Sumiko_III
======================================================================

Sumiko III
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
24th century
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Sumiko III
was the
third
planet
in its
system
. This was home to an
Emerald Wading Pool
.
A simulation of the pool was available aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-E
in
2373
. (
Star Trek: First Contact
)
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
Sumiko III was described as the location of the Emerald Wading Pool in the
script
of
TNG
: "
Conundrum
" opposed to the
Cliffs of Heaven
on
Cirrus IV
. In the actual episode the references got mixed up and the pool ended up on Cirrus IV, while the Cliffs were localized on
Sumiko IV
. However, the reference in
First Contact
apparently used the script as a source, thus canonizing Sumiko III as another location of an Emerald Wading Pool.
See also
Sumiko IV

======================================================================
PAGE: Sumiko IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Sumiko_IV
======================================================================

Sumiko IV
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
24th century
)
Sumiko IV
was the
fourth
planet
in its
star system
. This planet was home to the
Cliffs of Heaven
and an
Emerald Wading Pool
.
The Cliffs of Heaven were recreated in
Holodeck Program 47-C
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. In
2368
,
Kristin
was injured twice while diving from the
cliffs
on Sumiko IV. (
TNG
: "
Conundrum
")
A simulation of the Emerald Wading Pool on Sumiko IV was also available aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-E
in
2373
. (
Star Trek: First Contact
)
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
The script originally referenced the Cliffs as being on
Cirrus IV
, and the
Emerald Wading Pool
being on
Sumiko III
.
[1]
The final cut of the episode instead referenced the Cliffs as being on Sumiko IV and the Wading Pool on Cirrus IV. However, a display of holoprograms seen in
Star Trek: First Contact
, apparently working from the
script
, listed a wading pool at Sumiko IV after all.
See also
Sumiko III

The Sunstone Villa

Surata IV
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2365
)
Native species:
Predatory vines
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Surata IV
was the
uninhabited
fourth
planet
in its
star system
. This
Class M
planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
In
2365
, the
USS
Enterprise
-D
visited here, where
William T. Riker
was stung by a
thorn
teeming with
microbes
which attacked his
central nervous system
. The
vine
was predatory, as it deliberately stabbed any
warm-blooded
creature
that happened by it. (
TNG
: "
Shades of Gray
")
In the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 346 and
StarTrek.com
, Surata IV was a
M-class
planet.
[1]
(X)
The planet model for Surata IV was first used for
Starbase Montgomery
("
The Icarus Factor
"). It was later used for
Angosia III
("
The Hunted
"),
Risa
("
Captain's Holiday
"),
Malcor III
("
First Contact
"),
Starbase 112
planet
("
Identity Crisis
"),
Kaelon II
("
Half a Life
"),
Starbase 234
planet
("
Redemption II
",)
Ruah IV
("
The Chase
"),
Kesprytt III
("
Attached
"), and
Dorvan V
("
Journey's End
").

at

, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Surplus Depot Z15
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Surplus_Depot_Z15
======================================================================

Surplus Depot Z15 in 2368
Surplus Depot Z15
was a
Federation
surplus depot
operated by the
Zakdorn
, located on and in
orbit
of
Qualor II
in
Sector 213
. The facility was responsible for salvaging useful components from
decommissioned
vessels before storing them in the
depot
. In the
2360s
, its
quartermaster
was
Klim Dokachin
.
In
2368
, the
USS
Enterprise
-D
traveled to Qualor II as part of its investigation of
debris
linked to the
Vulcan
starship
T'Pau
, in an attempt to determine the fate of the vessel. Upon arriving, it was discovered that the
T'Pau
– previously docked at section 18-Gamma-12 – was missing, taken by forces unknown. The
T'Pau
's
navigational deflector
, source of the debris, had been routed to the
USS
Tripoli
, a
holding vessel
for the depot located at the outer edge of the shipyard, at section 12-Delta-4. It emerged that the
Tripoli
too was missing, leading to the conclusion that another party was intercepting shipments bound for the
Tripoli
and routing them elsewhere.
Commander
Riker
powered down the
Enterprise
and lay in wait for the
smugglers
.
When the
smuggler vessel
arrived, the
Enterprise
attempted to hail it, but the exchange of fire that resulted, along with the vessel's large weapons cache, caused it to explode. It was later linked to a
Ferengi
arms trader,
Omag
, who had taken the
T'Pau
from the depot and sold it to a
Barolian
intermediary to be used in an abortive invasion of
Vulcan
by the
Romulans
. (
TNG
: "
Unification I
", "
Unification II
")
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 3rd ed., p. 483 refers to the depot as "Surplus Depot Zed-15." In the script, it is spelled out as "Surplus Depot Zed-One-Five" once.
[1]
The
LCARS
display seen in the episodes carry the identifier "Qualor II Depot Z15."
Surplus Depot Z15 starships
Surplus Depot Z15 starships
USS
Alka-Celsior
B-24-CLN
USS
Buran
USS
Chekov
USS
Excelsior
USS
Princeton
Unnamed
K't'inga
-class
Two
Unnamed
Miranda
-classes
Unnamed
Nebula
-class
External link
Surplus Depot Z15
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Suspiria's array
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Suspiria's_array
======================================================================

Suspiria's array
Type:
Nacene array
Affiliation:
Nacene
Ocampa
Location:
Delta Quadrant
Status:
Active
(
2372
)
Crew:
At least 2,000
Armament:
Energy
weapons
Suspiria's array
, also known as the
Ocampa array
, was a
space station
in the
Delta Quadrant
. Built by a
Nacene
individual
known as
Suspiria
, it was similar to the
Caretaker's array
in configuration and surface composition, but only a tenth its
size
. According to scans taken by the
USS
Voyager
, the facility was at least
three hundred years old
, as of
2372
.
Suspiria's array contained a
colony
of over two thousand
Ocampa
, in which Suspiria encouraged the development of their mental abilities. They had also extended their
life spans
. The facility didn't house Suspiria herself, as she instead resided in
Exosia
, a
subspace layer
accessible through a nearby point in
space
called
The Meeting Place
.
In 2372,
Voyager
found Suspiria's array, after detecting sporocystian
lifesigns
. (
VOY
: "
Cold Fire
")
According to the
reference book
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 84),
Voyager
visited this station on
stardate
49040.

Suvin IV
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2369
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Suvin IV
was the
fourth
planet
in its
star system
.
After reverting to childhood,
Jean-Luc Picard
contemplated his future and thought about accompanying
Dr.
Langford
on her
archaeological
exploration of the ruins on this planet in
2369
. (
TNG
: "
Rascals
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: T'Cha
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/T'Cha
======================================================================

T'Cha
was a location in the
Beta Quadrant
.
In
2257
, its location was labeled on the
star chart
showing the trajectory of
Lieutenant
Spock
's
shuttle
. (
DIS
: "
An Obol for Charon
")
This location was only mentioned in writing.
According to the
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Stellar Cartography", pp. 31; "Federation Historical Highlights, 2161-2385"), T'Cha was, according to a map of the
Klingon Empire
– prepared by the Scribe K'Tark for the
Klingon High Council
in the
Year of Kahless
893 (
2266
) – identified as a Klingon star.

======================================================================
PAGE: T'Khut
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/T'Khut
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
T'Khut
Class:
G
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
3196
)
Satellite(s):
T'Khut moon
Location:
In
orbit
of
40 Eridani A
Vulcan system
Vulcan space
Vulcan sector
Beta Quadrant
~16
light years
from
Sol
T'Khut
was an uninhabited
class G
planet
in the
Vulcan system
. It formed a
binary pair
with
Ni'Var
, both of which
orbited
the
star
40 Eridani A
, and had
one moon
. This planet was located inside
Vulcan space
in the
Vulcan sector
,
Beta Quadrant
, and was approximately 16
light years
from
Sol
.
History
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet was within the enclosed region. (
SA
: "
300th Night
")
Spock
visited this planet in
2273
during the
VGER Incident
to undergo
Kolinahr
but was rejected by the monks when
VGER
called him. (
Star Trek: The Motion Picture
)
Stellar cartography
In
2259
, T'Khut was depicted on a diagram of the Vulcan system displayed in the
shuttle
Cervantes
. (
SNW
: "
Charades
")
Appendices
Appearances
TAS
: "
Yesteryear
"
Star Trek: The Motion Picture
(original theatrical edit)
DIS
:
"
Lethe
"
"
Such Sweet Sorrow
"
"
Unification III
"
SNW
: "
Charades
"
Background information
This planet's name was only mentioned in writing.
The first depiction of T'Khut in "Yesteryear"
The idea of Vulcan having a sister planet was proposed by
D.C. Fontana
to resolve the contradiction between the appearance of a celestial body in Vulcan's sky in
TAS
: "
Yesteryear
", and Spock's statement in
TOS
: "
The Man Trap
" that "
Vulcan has no moon
." The planet was first named as "T'Kuht" in the "Landing Party Six" series of stories, published in
1975
as part of the
Warped Space
fanzine
headed up by Gordon Carleton. Subsequently, beginning with the
1984
novel
The Vulcan Academy Murders
by
Jean Lorrah
, the name was picked up by various non-canonical licensed works; the spelling changed to "T'Khut" in
Spock's World
by
Diane Duane
, published in
1988
.
[1]
The planet was alternatively named T'Rukh and "The Watcher" in the
1994
novel
Sarek
, and Valdena in the
1998
Last Unicorn Games
RPG sourcebook
The Way of Kolinahr
. In particular,
Sarek
notes that the Watcher has different names at different times of the Vulcan year.
T'Khut was finally canonized in
2023
in a display graphic of the Vulcan system created by
Star Trek: Strange New Worlds
art director
Timothy Peel
. It first appeared on an astrogator console display in the first season.
[2]
A modified version of the graphic, with additional information about the 40 Eridani system and
Kerkhov
, appeared in "Charades".
[3]
The current location of T'Khut in the Beta Quadrant was based on the most recent star charts seen in
Star Trek: Strange New Worlds
and
Star Trek: Section 31
.
External link
T'Khut
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: T'Lani III
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/T'Lani_III
======================================================================

T'Lani III
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2370
)
Native species:
T'Lani
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
T'Lani III
was the
third
planet
in its
star system
. This planet was formerly
inhabited
by the
T'Lani
species
, until the entire
population
was wiped out by
Harvesters
, deployed during the
war
against the
Kellerun
that ended in
2370
.
Miles O'Brien
and
Julian Bashir
hid in an abandoned command center on T'Lani III after the T'Lani and Kellerun authorities attempted to eliminate them and their knowledge of the Harvesters. (
DS9
: "
Armageddon Game
")
See also
T'Lani Prime
According to the
script
, the pronunciation for T'Lani was "tuh-LA-nee".
[1]
According to
StarTrek.com
, this planet
orbited
a
star
named T'Lani, and according to
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 356, in the entry for "T'Lani Prime", the system was named also T'Lani.
[2]
(X)
The
surface
of T'Lani III was depicted via a modified version of the
matte painting
previously used for the surface of
Turkana IV
, with new structures added in the foreground.

======================================================================
PAGE: T'Lani Prime
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/T'Lani_Prime
======================================================================

T'Lani Prime
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Non-aligned
Status:
Habitable
(
2370
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
T'Lani Prime
was the
inhabited
prime
planet
in its
star system
.
In
2370
, a
T'Lani
cruiser
was located in
orbit
of the
third planet
, and
Dr.
Bashir
and
O'Brien
were sent there to help the T'Lani and
Kelleruns
rid themselves of
Harvesters
, a
biological weapon
. After completing their task, there was to be a celebration held on T'Lani Prime, thus delaying their expected departure by a
day
.
Not long after,
Ambassador
E'Tyshra
told
Benjamin Sisko
that there would be a
memorial service
held for Bashir and O'Brien on the planet, after their supposed
deaths
. (
DS9
: "
Armageddon Game
")
See also
T'Lani III
Background information
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to the
script
, the pronunciation for T'Lani was " tuh-LA-nee".
[1]
According to
StarTrek.com
, this planet was the
homeworld
for the T'Lani and
orbited
a
star
named T'Lani.
[2]
(X)
;
[3]
(X)
According to
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 356, in the entry for "T'Lani Prime", the system was named T'Lani.
