T'lli Beta
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2367
)
Native species:
T'lli Betans
Location:
T'lli Beta
system
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
T'lli Beta
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
T'lli Beta
system
. This was the
homeworld
of the
T'lli Betans
. This planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
In
2367
, the
USS
Enterprise
-D
was en route to T'lli Beta before it was ensnared by the gravitational pull of a
cosmic string
fragment which a group of
two-dimensional beings
were attempting to enter. (
TNG
: "
The Loss
")
In
2371
, the
location
of T'lli Beta was labeled in the
star chart
Data
and
Picard
were studying in
stellar cartography
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. (
Star Trek Generations
,
okudagram
)
In the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 3rd ed., p. 489, it was stated that this planet was named after script writer
Hilary J. Bader
's grandmother, Tillie Bader.

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
TRAPPIST 1e
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
(
3196
)
Status:
Intact
(
3196
)
Location:
In
orbit
of
TRAPPIST 1
Alpha Quadrant
~41
light years
from
Sol
Trappist 1e
was a
planet
in
orbit
of the
red dwarf
primary
TRAPPIST 1
. Located in the
Alpha Quadrant
, this planet's star was visible from
Earth
in the
constellation
Aquarius
and was approximately 41
light years
from
Sol
.
History
In the
32nd century
, this planet was home to the
TRAPPIST 1e Shipyards
.
In
3195
, the
USS
Athena
departed from this planet on her first run, carrying
cadets
to
San Francisco
on
Earth
. (
SA
: "
Kids These Days
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this star was within the enclosed region. (
SA
: "
300th Night
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Tagra IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tagra_IV
======================================================================

Tagra IV
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Native species:
Tagrans
Location:
Tagra
system
Argolis Cluster
Kiley Local
sector
Alpha Quadrant
Tagra IV
was the
inhabited
fourth
planet
in the
Tagra
system
.
Argolis Cluster
,
Kiley Local
sector
,
Alpha Quadrant
. This
Class M
planet was the
homeworld
of the
Tagrans
, a
humanoid
species
.
History
By
2369
, the Tagrans' industrial activities had polluted the
atmosphere
of Tagra IV to a critical point, despite their installation of a thousand
barystatic filters
in an effort to clean the
air
. These filters had caused a severe
ionization
effect in the planet's atmosphere, preventing the function of
transporters
.
On
stardate
46192.3, the
USS
Enterprise
-D
mounted a relief
mission
to assist Tagra IV, one of the largest such missions they had ever mounted. This included the installation of a new
field modulator
in the reactor that powered the barystatic filters on the
southern continent
, which had been experiencing difficulties which could have required it to be shut down.
However, the damage to the reactor was worse than the Tagrans originally described, and it continued to destabilize even after the field modulator was installed.
Lieutenant Commander
Geordi La Forge
attempted to avert an overload with a
neutrino
infusion to smother the reaction, without success as the heat had fused the reactor injectors. A meltdown seemed imminent, threatening thousands of people in the area.
At that time,
Amanda Rogers
aboard the
Enterprise
exercised her abilities as a
Q
to stabilize the reactor, expressing that she could not allow so many people to die in spite of her previous pledge not use her powers. She then restored the
ecosystem
of Tagra IV to its natural state, dropping the atmospheric pollutants to less than one part per trillion. Realizing that she could no longer live as a
Human
, she then returned with
Q
to the
Q Continuum
. (
TNG
: "
True Q
")
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
The planet's class came from the
StarTrek.com
.
[1]
(X)
External link
Tagra IV
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Tagus III
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tagus_III
======================================================================

Tagus III
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2367
)
Native species:
Taguans
Location:
Tagus
system
Unnamed sector
Beta Quadrant
Tagus III
was the
inhabited
third
planet
in the
Tagus
system
. This planet was in a
sector
of the
Beta Quadrant
. This was the
homeworld
of the
Taguans
.
Civilization
existed on the planet for over
two billion years
and it was therefore one of the most important
archeological
sites in
the galaxy
. Up until
2367
, 947 archeological expeditions had been conducted, with 74 of them revealing findings of major importance, the first one
22,000 years prior
. A significant number of these excavations had been conducted by
Vulcans
through the
Vulcan Science Academy
. Other expeditions had been conducted by the
American Continent Institute
, the
University of Alpha Centauri
, the
University of Hawaii
, the
University of Betazed
, and the
Memory Alpha Cultural Institute
.
Y'zjete H'ieh City
was one target of such expeditions.
The
history
of the planet was divided into different periods, such as
Arnold Era
,
Post-Ramirez Era
,
Pre-Neskoromny Era
, and
Sepulveda Period
.
The Taguans sealed off the ruins to foreign visitors in the
23rd century
, but the ruins were still the focus of much interest. In 2367, the annual
symposium
of the
Federation Archaeology Council
was held in
orbit
of Tagus III on board the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. (
TNG
: "
Qpid
")
Appendices
Background information
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
Apocrypha
In the
novel
Q-Space
,
Q
took Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
into Tagus III's distant past to witness a prominent local ceremony of the era, where the Imotru (
β
) – the previous sentient inhabitants of Tagus, with the Taguans of the present apparently a later
evolution
of life on their world – perform a ceremony where a group glide for over two
days
and proceed to sky-dive into a ring of fire to evade man-eating sea snakes, Q noting that he enjoyed witnessing the rite in his youth as it reflected how lower beings were unafraid to take chances and refused to accept limitations in favor of doing something with their lives.
External link
Tagus III
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

Takara

Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2369
)
Native species:
Takaran
Takaran wildebeest

Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Takara
was an
inhabited
planet
, and was the
homeworld
of the
Takarans
.
The
Takaran wildebeest
was an
animal
from this planet. (
DS9
: "
Crossfire
")
In
2369
, Takaran
scientist
Doctor
Jo'Bril
attempted to
steal
a
prototype
metaphysic shield
to bring back to his homeworld and develop into a
weapon
, though his plan was foiled by Dr.
Beverly Crusher
of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. (
TNG
: "
Suspicions
")

Takara sector

======================================================================
PAGE: Talax
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Talax
======================================================================

Talax
AKA:
Talaxia
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Haakonian Order
Status:
Habitable
(
2378
)
Satellite(s):
Rinax
At least one other moon
Native species:
Talaxian
Fur fly
Location:
Talaxian
system
Delta Quadrant
Talax
, or
Talaxia
, was an
inhabited
planet
and the
homeworld
of the
Talaxian
species
. The planet was located in the
Talaxian
system
in the
Delta Quadrant
. From its
surface
, the lights of the
colonized
moon
Rinax
were visible. (
VOY
: "
Jetrel
", "
Tinker Tenor Doctor Spy
", "
Dragon's Teeth
")
Talax was seized by the
Haakonian Order
in the
year
2356
, after the Talaxian
unconditional surrender
concluded the
Talaxian-Haakonian War
. (
VOY
: "
Jetrel
") As of
2378
, the planet was still believed to be under
Haakonian
control. They did not treat the Talaxians well, leading some to leave. (
VOY
: "
Homestead
")
One of the most pleasant
places
on the planet was the
Paxau Resort
, located on
Paxau
, where the rich and privileged went to relax. (
VOY
: "
Warlord
") Notable landforms, remembered fondly by
Neelix
and
Dexa
, included the
Axiana Lakes
and the
Godo Mountain Range
. (
VOY
: "
Homestead
")
In
3189
, the location of Talax was denoted on a
holographic
star chart
of the galaxy at
Federation Headquarters
. (
DIS
: "
Die Trying
")
Due to the premise of
Star Trek: Voyager
, the Talaxian homeworld could not be regularly featured as a setting on the series. (
Star Trek: Voyager - A Vision of the Future
, p. 164)
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 78), by
2378
, the official
name
of the
government
on Talax (Talax IV) was the Autonomous Province of Talax. The government was seated in the
capital city
of Paxau. The dominant species were the humanoid Talaxians. An estimated 14.2 billion Talaxians lived on the planet. The Talaxians had been
warp-capable
since antiquity.
According to the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 367, Talax was a
M-class
planet.
External link
Talax
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

PAGE: Talos I
CATEGORIES: Planets

======================================================================

Talos I
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2254
)
Location:
Talos system
Talos star group
Alpha Quadrant
~2
light years
from
Starbase 11
Talos I
was the
first
planet
in the
Talos
system
. This planet was located in the
Talos star group
, a
stellar cluster
of the
Alpha Quadrant
. The planet was approximately 2
light years
from
Starbase 11
.
In
2254
, the position and
orbital
path of Talos I in a map of the inner system was displayed on the
bridge
of the
USS
Enterprise
. This planet was located over one
astronomical unit
from the stars at the system's center. (
TOS
: "
The Cage
", "
The Menagerie, Part I
",
production art
)
This planet was only mentioned in writing.
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the location of its sister planet
Talos IV
from the
Star Trek: Discovery
episode "
If Memory Serves
".
The
Star Trek: Star Charts
(pp. 37 & 41) classified Talos I as a
B-class planet
.

Talos III
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
(
2280s
)
Status:
Habitable
(
2280s
)
Satellite(s):
A
ring system
Location:
Talos system
Talos star group
Alpha Quadrant
~2
light years
from
Starbase 11
Talos III
was the
inhabited
ringed
third
planet
of the
Talos
system
. The planet was located in the
Talos star group
, a
stellar cluster
of the
Alpha Quadrant
. The planet was approximately 2
light years
from
Starbase 11
.
History
By the mid-
2280s
, the
United Federation of Planets
had established a presence on this planet. In
2285
, a
cargo container
from this world had been transported to
Regula
, where it was stored in a
cave
located deep under the
surface
of this
planetoid
. (
Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan
)
Stellar cartography
In
2254
, the position and
orbital
path of Talos III in an inner system map was displayed on the
bridge
of the
USS
Enterprise
. The planet was located 2.5
astronomical units
from the stars at the system's center. (
TOS
: "
The Cage
", "
The Menagerie, Part I
",
production art
)
This planet was only mentioned in writing.

from the

episode "
If Memory Serves
".

Star Trek: Star Charts
(pp. 37 & 40) classified Talos III as a

.

======================================================================
PAGE: Talos IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Talos_IV
======================================================================

Talos IV
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Non-aligned
Status:
Habitable
(
2328
)
Satellite(s):
None
Native species:
Talosians
Talosian singing plant
Location:
Talos system
Talos star group
Alpha Quadrant
~2
light years
from
Starbase 11
For the
mirror universe
counterpart, please see
Talos IV (mirror)
.
"
No vessel under any condition, emergency or otherwise, is to visit Talos IV.
"
–
Starfleet
General Order 7
,
2267
("
The Menagerie, Part I
")
Talos IV
was the
inhabited
M-class
fourth
planet
of the
Talos system
. The planet was located in the
Talos star group
, a
stellar cluster
of the
Alpha Quadrant
, and was approximately 2
light years
away from
Starbase 11
. (
DIS
: "
If Memory Serves
")
The planet was the
homeworld
of the
Talosians
, a
telepathic
humanoid
species
, as well as of the
Talosian singing plant
. The planet had an atmosphere of
oxygen
-
nitrogen
and a
gravity
that was 0.9 of
Earth
standard. (
TOS
: "
The Cage
", "
The Menagerie, Part I
")
History
Talos IV had a thriving
warp-capable
population, until
hundreds of thousands of years
before
2254
when a
nuclear war
caused a near-
extinction-level event
. (
DIS
: "
If Memory Serves
";
TOS
: "
The Cage
")
23rd century
Talos IV in 2254
Talos IV regained its ability to support surface life by the mid-
23rd century
. The planet's deep underground had been inhabited by the Talosians, who had
evolved
as an old and weakened species with enormous mental powers. In
2254
, the planet was visited by the
Federation
starship
USS
Enterprise
– under the command of
Captain
Christopher Pike
– after receiving a
distress call
faked by the Talosians supposedly from the
SS
Columbia
, that had
crash landed
on the planet in
2236
. However, the only survivor of this crash was
Vina
, a female
Human
. (
TOS
: "
The Cage
", "
The Menagerie, Part I
")
In an ultimately unused line of dialogue from the script of "The Cage", it was estimated that Talos IV couldn't have sustained the survivors from the
Columbia
for more than "a few months" before they would have starved to death.
The surface of Talos IV in 2254
The Federation imposed
General Order 7
on Talos IV at some point after Pike's original visit. This order prevented anyone from approaching or making contact with the planet, under
penalty of death
. (
TOS
: "
The Menagerie, Part I
",
DIS
: "
If Memory Serves
")
In
2257
,
Michael Burnham
discovered that a sequence of numbers
Spock
had been obsessively writing for the past few weeks were actually the coordinates of Talos IV. She subsequently laid in a course, hoping to find answers to Spock's condition there. (
DIS
: "
Light and Shadows
")
Talos IV in 2267
In
2267
, Talos IV was revisited by the
Enterprise
while commandeered by Spock, in order to deliver Captain Pike there. Though Pike had been crippled by
delta-particle radiation poisoning
, he was able to live a normal life on the planet, together with Vina. (
TOS
: "
The Menagerie, Part II
")
24th century
In
2328
, Talos IV was the destination for passengers traveling from
Triskelion Orbital Station
via commercial
transport
.
Ryan Adams
,
Wynsdey Adams
,
Loren Bivens
, and
Svetlana Reeves-Stevens
traveled on the
SS
Kogin
, and
Mike Echols
traveled on the
SS
Wisconsin
. (
TNG-R
: "
Inheritance
",
okudagram
)
This might imply that General Order 7 was rescinded before 2328.
Lieutenant
Adele Simmons
, a
crewmember
aboard both the
USS
Enterprise
-D
and
USS
Voyager
, was born on this
non-Federation
planet
. In
2369
, her planet of origin was listed on the chart "USS
Enterprise
Personnel Database", which was seen on
Jean-Luc Picard
's
PADD
. (
TNG
: "
The Chase
",
okudagram
)
Stellar cartography
The location of Talos IV
In
2254
, the position and
orbital
path of Talos IV in an inner system map was displayed on the bridge of the
USS
Enterprise
. This planet, located three
astronomical units
from the stars at the system's center, had a highly inclined orbit. (
TOS
: "
The Cage
", "
The Menagerie, Part I
",
production art
)
In
2257
, the
coordinates
of Talos IV were noted as 7-4-9 Mark 1-4-8. (
DIS
: "
Light and Shadows
")
The digits of these coordinates are given in the
FASA
RPG
book
The Federation
on page 31, where they are instead arranged as 7.49S 1.48E, placing the star group 74.9 parsecs to the south and 14.8 to the east of the center of Federation space.
In
2293
, the location of this planet was labeled in a
star chart
found on the
navigation console
of the
USS
Enterprise
-B
. (
Star Trek Generations
)
Appendices
Appearances
TOS
:
"
The Cage
"
"
The Menagerie, Part I
"
"
The Menagerie, Part II
"
DIS
: "
If Memory Serves
"
Background information
In the original story outline for "
The Cage
" (as fully reprinted in the
reference book
The Making of Star Trek
, pp. 47-65), Talos IV was named
Sirius IV
and was inhabited by
crab
-like aliens who kidnapped Captain
Robert April
. The outline additionally described the surface of Sirius IV as "arid, rocky" and "the arid waste."
Near the end of
August 1964
, Gene Roddenberry wrote a memo to his production manager,
Jim Paisley
, which asked such questions as, "
Regarding the surface of planet Talos IV, does the present description of the planet seem practical as regards available locations? Is the description and action presently in the rough draft suited for matching set construction, i.e. stage shooting of the more complicated dialogue portions of these Talos IV planet surface scenes.
" (
Star Trek Creator: The Authorized Biography of Gene Roddenberry
, p. 205)
In the script of "The Cage", Talos IV was described as "a violet-orange planet."
The set used for Talos IV's surface was built on
Stage 16
of
Desilu
's Culver City lot.
[1]
The set was designed by art directors
Franz Bachelin
and
Pato Guzman
. It included a massive painted backdrop that greatly enlarged the set's apparent size. Although the sky pictured in the backdrop was a cloudy gray, Director of Photography
William E. Snyder
and the production crew gave the sky a slight greenish tint using colored lights, thereby making the planetscape seem more alien. ("
The Menagerie, Part II
"
text commentary
,
TOS Season 1 DVD
) The Talosian elevator created as part of the set was built on a platform, allowing the lift to descend without necessitating excavation below the soundstage's floor. ("
The Menagerie, Part I
"
text commentary
,
TOS Season 1 DVD
) Underground interiors of Talos IV were filmed on
Desilu Culver Stage 15
.
[2]
Talos IV as it originally appeared in "The Cage" and "The Menagerie"
By
14 September
1964
, a budget of US$6,400 had been allocated for creation of the special effects necessary to show the exterior of Talos IV in "The Cage". (
Star Trek Creator: The Authorized Biography of Gene Roddenberry
, p. 206) To portray the planet as seen from the
Enterprise
upon approach in the original version of "
The Menagerie, Part II
", footage of Talos IV was reused from "The Cage". The film clip was upgraded and matted into the
Enterprise
's bridge viewscreen. ("
The Menagerie, Part II
"
text commentary
,
TOS Season 1 DVD
) The
2006
remastered
versions of "
The Menagerie, Part I
" and "The Menagerie, Part II", as well as the
2008
remastered version of "The Cage", feature several updated,
CG
shots of Talos IV.
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 34), Talos IV was described as a quarantined and non-aligned planet. Information on the planet's official name,
government
,
capital city
and
population
were not available to the
Federation
in the late
24th century
. The Talosians were
warp-capable
500,000 years ago
.
Apocrypha
In the
Star Trek Maps
(chart D), Talos IV had two moons named Mone and Toh.
In
The Worlds of the Federation
,
(p. 132), the indigenous name of the planet was Clesik. It was stated further that Starfleet declared any distress signal or other communication supposedly originating from anywhere in the entire Talosian region to be assumed false, with no exceptions.
External link
Talos IV
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Planets in TOS and TOS Remastered
at
Ex Astris Scientia

Mirror universe
(
coexisting on another dimensional plane
)
Talos IV
Type:
Planet
Status:
Depopulated
(
23rd century
)
Native species:
Talosians
Singing plant
Location:
Mirror universe
For the
prime universe
counterpart, please see
Talos IV
.
Talos IV
was a once-
inhabited
planet
home
to the
Talosians
, a
telepathic
humanoid
species
, as well as the
singing plants
.
After the Talosians attempted to deceive
Philippa Georgiou
with their telepathic abilities, she "blew" the Talosians and their singing plants "off the face of their planet." (
DIS
: "
If Memory Serves
")
The planet was not seen or named in the episode.

======================================================================
PAGE: Talos V
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Talos_V
======================================================================

Talos V
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2254
)
Satellite(s):
1
moon
Location:
Talos system
Talos star group
Alpha Quadrant
~2
light years
from
Starbase 11
Talos V
was the
fifth
planet
in the
Talos
system
. The planet was located in the
Talos star group
, a
stellar cluster
of the
Alpha Quadrant
, and had one
moon
. The planet was approximately 2
light years
from
Starbase 11
.
In
2254
, the position and
orbital
path of Talos V on an inner system map was displayed on the
bridge
of the
USS
Enterprise
. This
planet
was located 3.2
astronomical units
from the stars at the system's center. (
TOS
: "
The Cage
", "
The Menagerie, Part I
",
production art
)
This planet was only mentioned in writing.
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the location of its sister planet
Talos IV
from the
Star Trek: Discovery
episode "
If Memory Serves
".
The
Star Trek: Star Charts
(pp. 37 & 40) classified Talos V as a
D-class planet
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Tamaal
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tamaal
======================================================================

Tamaal
Type:
Planet
Status:
Inhabited
(
2152
)
Location:
In a
binary star system
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
For similarly named Maquis, please see
Tamal
.
Tamaal
was the
fourth
planet
of a
binary star
system
, located inside or near
Enolian
space. In
2152
, the planet was home to a trading
outpost
with two million inhabitants.
In 2152,
Enolian
criminal
Kuroda Lor-ehn
took over the
transport
that was delivering him to the
penal colony
of
Canamar
and had another prisoner,
Jonathan Archer
, set a course for Tamaal. Kuroda's plan was to rendezvous at Tamaal with another ship and leave the transport to disintegrate in the planet's
atmosphere
, killing the other prisoners.
The transport met up with the other vessel at Tamaal as planned. However, the crew of Archer's ship,
Enterprise
NX-01
, had taken over the ship and boarded the transport to rescue their comrades and the other passengers. Unwilling to return to prison, Kuroda chose to remain aboard the transport in a futile attempt to take the transport out of its
decaying orbit
. He died when the transport disintegrated over Tamaal during
atmospheric entry
. (
ENT
: "
Canamar
")
In the final draft script of "Canamar", Tamaal was identified as
class M
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Tandar Prime
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tandar_Prime
======================================================================

Tandar Prime
was the
inhabited
prime
planet
in its
star system
. This system was located in the
Tandar sector
. The planet was the
homeworld
of the
Tandarans
and the capital of their territory. As of
2152
, the legal system was administered by the
Central Magistrate's Office
.
One of the
provinces
of Tandar Prime was
Querella Province
. Prior to
2144
,
Danik
worked as
Director of Research
at an
engineering
institute there before being taken into custody for being
Suliban
. Danik's grandfather was one of the many
nomadic
Suliban looking for a new home after the
Suliban homeworld
was destroyed, and he chose to move his family to Tandar Prime.
The
capital city
of Tandar Prime was quite impressive. When
Colonel
Grat
sent
Enterprise
to Tandar, he suggested that he would try to pull some strings for an
ambassador
to give them a private tour. (
ENT
: "
Detained
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 60), Tandar Prime was located in the Tandar (
39 Tauri
)
star system
. The system's
primary
was a
G-class star
. Tandar system was located in the
Alpha Quadrant
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Tantalus V
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tantalus_V
======================================================================

Tantalus V
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Habitable
(
2328
)
Satellite(s):
A
ring system
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Tantalus V
was the
inhabited
ringed
fifth
planet
of its
star system
. The planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
and, in
2266
, was the home of the
Tantalus Penal Colony
, a
Federation
rehabilitation colony
.
That same year, the
USS
Enterprise
delivered
supplies
, including
infra-sensory drugs
, to this planet. (
TOS
: "
Dagger of the Mind
")
Appendices
Background information
For the Tantalus V entry in the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 371, the authors said that the planet was "
named for
the Greek mythic figure
who stole ambrosia from the tables of the gods, feeding it to mortals.
"
In the
script
of "Dagger of the Mind", Tantalus V was described as "
a planet almost featureless except for the ravages caused by its extreme climacteric.
" Also, the planet's landscape was described as "eerie".
In the original aired version of "Dagger of the Mind", the surface of Tantalus V was represented with a reused
matte painting
of
Delta Vega
, from "
Where No Man Has Gone Before
".
For the
remastered
edition of "Dagger of the Mind", Tantalus V was depicted with newly created
computer-generated
shots which added the planet's ring system. The digital footage also completely replaced the matte painting representing the planet's surface. The footage of the planet was co-created by
Max Gabl
, who commented, "
I only did the planet, the rings were added later. I remember putting them into the surface painting though.
"
[1]
External links
Tantalus
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Tantalus V
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Planets in TOS and TOS Remastered
at
Ex Astris Scientia

======================================================================
PAGE: Tanuga IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tanuga_IV
======================================================================

Tanuga IV
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Native species:
Tanugans
Location:
Tanuga
system
Unnamed sector
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Tanuga IV
was the
inhabited
fourth
planet
in the
Tanuga
system
. This was the
homeworld
for the
Tanugans
, a highly advanced
humanoid
species
. This planet was located in a
sector
of the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
A
lambda field generator
, one integral part of
Dr.
Nel Apgar
's work on
Krieger waves
(the other being the
Krieger wave converter
, located on the
Tanuga IV research station
in
orbit
of the
planet
), was located on Tanuga IV. Shortly before the destruction of the station,
Manua Apgar
and
Tayna
beamed
down to the planet, saving them from certain
death
. The
Tanugan security force
, represented by
Chief Investigator
Krag
, was operating from the planet.
In
2366
, the
USS
Enterprise
-D
visited the planet to investigate Apgar's work. Riker was investigated for the destruction of the station, but after comparing the different accounts, it became clear he was not responsible. (
TNG
: "
A Matter of Perspective
")
"TAN-ooh-guh" was the pronunciation for this planet from the
script
pronunciation guide.
[1]
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 371 classified Tanuga IV as a
M-class
planet.
External link
Tanuga IV
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Tanuga IV research station
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tanuga_IV_research_station
======================================================================

Tanuga IV research station
Type:
Regula I
-type
research station
Affiliation:
Tanugan
Location:
In
orbit
of
Tanuga IV
Tanuga
system
Unnamed sector
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Status:
Destroyed
(
2366
)
The
Tanuga IV research station
was a
Regula I
-type
research station
administrated by the
Tanugans
in the
24th century
. This station was in
orbit
of
Tanuga IV
at a minimum
distance
of 5,000
kilometers
. This planet was located in a
sector
of the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
. The station was destroyed in
2366
, on
stardate
43610.3.
Dr.
Nel Apgar
worked on his
Krieger wave converter
on the station with his
assistant
Tayna
. His
wife
,
Manua Apgar
, also stayed on the station. The station was designed to work in conjunction with a
lambda field generator
located on the
planet
's
surface
.
Though working with
Starfleet
on this project as a means of generating a new power source, Apgar misrepresented his work, hoping to
profit
from it as a
weapon
.
During the delivery of additional supply of
dicosilium
to the station in 2366,
Commander
William T. Riker
and
Geordi La Forge
stayed in the
guest quarters
overnight after they were dropped off at the station by the
USS
Enterprise
-D
.
While La Forge worked with Tayna on reviewing the
technology
, Riker and the Apgars
toasted
with a glass of
champagne
in the station's living area. Later,
Mrs.
Apgar showed Riker the guest quarters, where Dr. Apgar found Riker and his wife in a compromising position leading to a small fight between the two men.
The station was destroyed the following
day
by an
explosion
in the
laboratory
. When Commander Riker was beamed off the station, Dr. Apgar directed an energy charge from the Krieger wave converter at the commander. The energy was deflected from the
transporter beam
however, and hit the
reactor core
, thereby causing the explosion that destroyed the station and killing Apgar in the blast. (
TNG
: "
A Matter of Perspective
")
External link
Tanuga IV research station
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Tarakis
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tarakis
======================================================================

Tarakis
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2376
)
Satellite(s):
One
moon
Location:
Delta Quadrant
Tarakis
was an uninhabited
M-class
planet
in the
Delta Quadrant
. The planet had one
moon
.
The planet was once home to a
Nakan
colony
. In the
21st century
, a group of soldiers led by
Commander
Saavdra
was ordered to relocate the colonists, supposedly temporarily. However, the colonists resisted with arms, and the situation devolved into the
Nakan massacre
, in which 82 Nakan were killed.
Following the incident, a
memorial monument
was erected on the site of the massacre. The monument contained a
synaptic transmitter
, which emitted a
neurogenic pulse
that placed
memory engrams
from the soldiers involved in the massacre in the minds of any individuals passing through the Tarakis system. The monument was abandoned in the
22nd century
, and by the
24th century
its
power cells
began to fail, causing the memories to become disjointed.
In
2376
,
Chakotay
,
Tom Paris
,
Harry Kim
, and
Neelix
scouted Tarakis for
dilithium
on the
Delta Flyer
. Upon their return to
USS
Voyager
, they began to experience vivid memories of the Nakan massacre. After tracing the cause to Tarakis,
Captain
Kathryn Janeway
ordered that the monument be repaired and that a
warning buoy
be placed in orbit, so that future
passers-by
would know what to expect. (
VOY
: "
Memorial
")
Inscription at the base of the memorial (translated):
Words alone cannot convey the suffering.
Words alone cannot prevent what happened here from happening again.
Beyond words lies experience.
Beyond experience lies the truth.
Make this truth your own.

======================================================================
PAGE: Tarchannen III
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tarchannen_III
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Tarchannen III
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2367
)
Native species:
Tarchanne
Location:
Tarchannen
system
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Tarchannen III
was the
third
planet
in the
Tarchannen
system
. This
Class M
planet was home to the
Tarchanne
. This planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
The planet was characterized by perpetual twilight, frequent
sandstorms
and its sparse
vegetation
, including a variety of dark, spindly
plants
. The planet's
atmosphere
stretched up to an altitude of 210
kilometers
.
In
3195
, this planet's name appeared on a
video wall
in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
Kids These Days
")
Federation outpost
In the mid-
24th century
, the
United Federation of Planets
was unaware of the indigenous
population
. Starfleet built an
outpost
consisting of five
buildings
and manned it with 49 people. They had been infected by the
aliens
through a
parasite
which planted its
DNA
in living
hosts
to reproduce itself; the hosts then transformed into the primitive
reptile
-like
species
. In
2362
, the transformation manifested itself and they left the outpost. The
USS
Victory
arrived to investigate, and sent an
away team
led by
Lieutenant
Susanna Leijten
. The outpost showed no signs of an
attack
or a
natural disaster
– the people had just vanished. Without further evidence, they abandoned the planet.
Graphic of a plant native to the planet
It was not until
2367
that this
mystery
could be solved. Three members of the former away team (
Anthony Brevelle
,
Paul Hickman
and
Mendez
) suddenly deserted and attempted to return to Tarchannen; Hickman was killed in this attempt when his
shuttlecraft
exploded during
atmospheric entry
. The
USS
Enterprise
-D
was investigating these incidents with Leijten on board as an observer, tailing him. Both she and the last away team member,
Geordi La Forge
, underwent the same transformation. Leijten was kept in
sickbay
, while La Forge managed to
transport
down to the planet, close to Building One. The
Enterprise
-D
crew
discovered the nature of the transformation and was able to reverse it. However, there was no hope for Brevelle and Mendez and they had no option but to live the rest of their lives as the aliens on the planet.
Following this discovery,
warning beacons
were placed in
orbit
and on the
surface
of the planet to prevent similar incidents in the future. (
TNG
: "
Identity Crisis
")
Appendices
Background information
In the
script
, the pronunciation for Tarchannen was "tar-CHAN-in".
[1]
The script thrice described the planet as being a world of "perpetual twilight"; it was unclear whether this referred to
tidal locking
or merely possessing a dim sun.
The planet's class came from
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 371 and
StarTrek.com
.
[2]
(X)
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I"), Tarchannen III was listed as a
Federation
member world
in
2378
.
External link
Tarchannen III
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

Non-aligned
Status:
Intact
(

)

Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Tarella
was an
inhabited
planet
. Classified as
M-class
, this was the
homeworld
for a
warp-capable
humanoid
species
, the
Tarellians
. It was very similar to
Earth
.
A
civil war
between the planet's two main
land masses
led to the destruction of the Tarellian
civilization
after
biological weapons
were deployed. A few Tarellians managed to escape the planet in
ships
. (
TNG
: "
Haven
")

script
of the episode, Tarella was placed as the
second
planet in a system with six planets.

inhabited
third
planet
in the
Taresian

, in the
Delta Quadrant
. It was the
homeworld
of the
Taresians
and the
rikka flowers
.

satellites
was capable of projecting a high density
polaron grid

planet.

mentions that

has written a
holo-novel program

======================================================================
PAGE: Targalus IX
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Targalus_IX
======================================================================

Targalus IX
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Habitable
(
2382
)
Native species:
Targalans
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Targalus IX
was the
inhabited
ninth
planet
in its
star system
, and the
homeworld
of the
Targalan
civilization
. Other
lifeforms
native to the planet included the
tie-dyed coma beetle
and a small
lizard
-like
animal
.
In
2382
, Targalus IX transitioned from a
capitalist
economy
to a
post-scarcity
society
with the development of
replicator
technology. A planet-wide celebration was held, while all financial structures were to be converted into
museums
and
meditation gardens
. Although most Targalans embraced the new status quo, some of the former rich elite formed
terrorist
cells
across the
continent
in a futile attempt to regain their lost power.
Alongside their economic transition, Targalus IX also joined the
United Federation of Planets
. The
USS
Cerritos
was assigned to assist the Targalans in disposing of their
obsolescent
wealth
.
Captain
Carol Freeman
eventually entered into an agreement, brokered by
D'Vana Tendi
, for
House Tendi
of the
Orion Syndicate
to take over the disposal process. (
LD
: "
Shades of Green
")
Landmarks
Currency Disposal and Recycling Center
First Targalan Bank
Museum of Friendship
(formerly
First Global Bank of Targalus IX
)
Second Targalan Bank
External link
Targalus IX
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

, as a puppy in a
pound

PAGE: Tarkalea

Tarkalea
was the
homeworld
of the
humanoid
species
known as the
Tarkaleans
.
It was also the homeworld of at least two types of
raptor
species, the
Tarkalean condor
and
Tarkalean hawk
, as well as the source of
Tarkalean tea
. (
DS9
: "
Past Prologue
", "
Nor the Battle to the Strong
", "
The Begotten
", et al.) It was also the origin of the
viral
illness
Tarkalean flu
. (
VOY
: "
Body and Soul
")
In
2153
,
T'Pol
contacted Tarkalea to inform them of the loss of their
freighter
and that they were attempting to do everything they could for their
crewmen
. (
ENT
: "
Regeneration
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Tarlac system
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tarlac_system
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Tarlac system
Type:
Star system
Status:
Intact
(
3196
)
Location:
Alpha Quadrant
The
Tarlac system
was a a
star system
in the
Alpha Quadrant
.
History
In
2257
, during the first
Federation-Klingon War
, this system was occupied by the
Klingons
. (
DIS
: "
The War Without, The War Within
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this system was within the blast radius of one of the mines. (
SA
: "
Rubincon
")
Stellar cartography
In
2256
, its location was labeled on the
star chart
"Alpha/Beta Quadrant Overview" in the
ready room
aboard the
USS
Discovery
. (
DIS
: "
Magic to Make the Sanest Man Go Mad
")
In
2259
, Tarlac's location was labeled on a
stellar cartography
chart that was seen on the
USS
Enterprise
's ready room
viewscreen
. This location's symbol had a
blue
color
, indicating that it was affiliated with the
United Federation of Planets
. (
SNW
: "
Strange New Worlds
", "
Spock Amok
")
Tarlac's location was labeled in a
Federation star chart
that was in
Fleet Admiral
Kirsten Clancy
's
office
at
Starfleet Headquarters
in
2399
and on the
bridge
of the
USS
Titan
-A
in
2401
. This location was in or near to
Federation space
. (
PIC
: "
Maps and Legends
", "
The Next Generation
", "
Disengage
")
In
3196
, during the
Omega-47
crisis, the location of Tarlac was depicted on star charts and readouts used across several locations on the
USS
Athena
to locate, monitor, and respond to the Omega-47 mines laid by the Venari Ral. (
SA
: "
Rubincon
")
This location was only mentioned in writing.
The
Tarlac
might possibly have come from this location.
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 62) and
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Federation Historical Highlights, 2161-2385"), Tarlac was a
G-class star
.

======================================================================

======================================================================

Tarod IX
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Intact
(
2364
)
Location:
Tarod
system
On the Federation side of the
Romulan Neutral Zone
Sector 30
Beta Quadrant
Tarod IX
was the formerly
inhabited
ninth
planet
of the
Tarod
system
. This planet was located in
Sector 3-0
, a
spatial
region
in the
Beta Quadrant
, and was on the
Federation
side of the
Federation-Romulan border
. This planet was home for one of the nine Federation
outposts
located in this
sector
.
In
2364
, Tarod IX was the second stop of
USS
Enterprise
-D
on its tour of the destroyed outposts in sector 30. Near Tarod's system, on its passage to the next destroyed outpost, the
Enterprise
-D made contact with a
Romulan
D'deridex
-class
warbird
. (
TNG
: "
The Neutral Zone
") It was later determined that the outpost had been attacked and destroyed by the
Borg
. (
TNG
: "
Q Who
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred from the location of its star system as seen in the
Star Trek: Strange New Worlds
episode "
A Quality of Mercy
".
External link
Tarod IX
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Tarpahk
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tarpahk
======================================================================

Tarpahk
was a location known to
Klingons
for its dry
crater
, particularly in the middle of the
dry season
(when it was dry).
In
2374
, while
brainwashed
into thinking he was a Klingon,
Neelix
compared himself to the crater at Tarpahk. (
VOY
: "
The Killing Game, Part II
")
This location was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Tarsas III
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tarsas_III
======================================================================

Tarsas III
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Satellite(s):
One
moon
, plus
Starbase 74
Location:
Sector 001
Beta Quadrant
Tarsas III
was the
third
planet
of its
star system
in
Sector 001
. Located in the
Beta Quadrant
, the planet was
orbited
by a
Luna
-like
moon
and
Starbase 74
, a
Federation
Spacedock
-type
starbase
. (
TNG
: "
11001001
";
TNG
: "
Conspiracy
",
okudagram
)
In
2364
, the
USS
Enterprise
-D
docked at the starbase to undergo routine maintenance check and certain upgrades to its
computer systems
, performed by a team of
Bynars
. (
TNG
: "
11001001
")
Later that
year
, the
location
of Starbase 74 was labeled on the map
Starbase Ops Status Sol Sector
. (
TNG
: "
Conspiracy
",
okudagram
)
The location of Starbase 74
The footage of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
on approach to Starbase 74 was stock footage of the original
USS
Enterprise
entering the
Spacedock One
in orbit of
Earth
. For "
11001001
", the
Enterprise-D
was superimposed over the
Enterprise
.
The current location of Tarsas III in the Beta Quadrant was based on the
star chart
seen in the
Star Trek: Prodigy
episode "
Starstruck
", which showed that the bulk of Sector 001 was in this quadrant. The Sol system was located at the extreme edge of this sector in the
Alpha Quadrant
.
See also
Tarsas IV
External link
Re-Used Planets in TNG
at
Ex Astris Scientia

======================================================================
PAGE: Tarsas IV

Tarsas IV
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2328
)
Location:
Sector 001
Alpha Quadrant
For the similarly named planet, please see
Tarsus IV
.
Tarsas IV
was the
inhabited
fourth
planet
of its
system
. The system was located in
Sector 001
,
Alpha Quadrant
.
In
2328
, this was a destination for passengers traveling from
Planet Q
via commercial
transport
.
Lolita Fatjo
traveled on the
SS
Manoa
,
Jimmy Berndt
traveled on the
SS
Kogin
, and
John P. Farrell
and
Carl Banks
traveled on the
SS
Wisconsin
. (
TNG-R
: "
Inheritance
",
okudagram
)
This planet was only mentioned in writing.
This planet's quadrant of origin is inferred based on the position of
Starbase 74
, which was in
orbit
of its sister planet
Tarsas III
, as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: The Next Generation
episode "
Conspiracy
".

======================================================================
PAGE: Tarsus IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tarsus_IV
======================================================================

Tarsus IV
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Earth
Status:
Habitable
(
2328
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
For the similarly named planet, please see
Tarsas IV
.
Tarsus IV
was the
inhabited
fourth
planet
of its
star system
. This planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
and was the home to an
Earth
colony
.
History
In
2246
, an exotic
fungus
destroyed most of the colony's
food
supply, leaving the colonists, of which there were eight thousand, in serious danger of
starvation
.
Governor
Kodos
made a drastic decision: he ordered the
deaths
of four thousand colonists, so that the other four thousand might live, selecting those to be killed according to his personal theories of
eugenics
. A
supply ship
arrived earlier than expected, but too late to save the four thousand people Kodos
murdered
. All that
Earth Forces
found of Kodos was a burned
body
, and he was presumed
dead
, until his chance discovery on
Planet Q
twenty years later
.
Among the
survivors
of the Tarsus IV incident were
James T. Kirk
,
Kevin Riley
, and
Thomas Leighton
, who were three of only nine survivors who knew Kodos'
face
, and who would be able to identify him. In 2266, they were the only three of the original nine
eyewitnesses
still alive. (
TOS
: "
The Conscience of the King
")
In
2259
, the massacre on this planet was listed under training In James T. Kirk's
personnel file
. This file was accessed by
Captain
Christopher Pike
. (
SNW
: "
A Quality of Mercy
")
In
2328
, Tarsus IV was the destination for passengers traveling from
Gamma Trianguli VI
via commercial
transports
.
Zayra Cabot
had traveled on the
SS
Manoa
,
Jack Litchfeld
had traveled on the
SS
Kogin
, and
David Stipes
and
Lorine Mendell
had traveled on the
SS
Wisconsin
. (
TNG-R
: "
Inheritance
",
okudagram
)
Inhabitants
See
:
Tarsus IV inhabitants
Appendices
Background information
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
In the final revised draft of the script for "
The Conscience of the King
", the Tarsus IV massacre was referred to as "the
rebellion
on Tarsus IV". In the final version of the installment, though, no such rebellion is established as having taken place on the planet. In the same script, Kirk also described Tarsus IV as "
a colony which was disintegrating before my eyes! Starvation! Rioting! Disaster!
" The method of execution Kodos used was said to have been an
antimatter chamber
, which, after he threw a switch, completely disintegrated the four thousand colonists all at once.
In the final draft script of the
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine
episode "
The Jem'Hadar
",
Quark
told
Benjamin Sisko
that the
Ferengi
had never had an incident like what had happened on Tarsus IV. (
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine Companion - A Series Guide and Script Library
)
Michael Sussman
created a computer readout on the
USS
Defiant
in "
In A Mirror, Darkly, Part II
" in which
Hoshi Sato
was said to have been killed by Kodos on Tarsus Four. However, the relevant section of the bio did not appear in the finished cut of the episode.
In the
Star Trek
app accompanying
Star Trek Into Darkness
,
John Harrison
's bio claimed he was one of nine survivors of a 2246 attack on the planet with his parents Richard and Sara perishing. It was not made explicitly clear if this "attack" was the same event that happened in the regular timeline.
The
reference book
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I") listed Tarsus IV as a
Federation member
in
2378
.
In
The Conscience of the King
a traumatic event in
James T. Kirk
's life was the execution of 4,000 colonists of Tarsus IV; in the
mirror universe
James T. Kirk kills 5,000 colonists of
Vega IX
.
Apocrypha
In the
Shatnerverse
novel
Avenger
, the famine was an act of eco-
terrorism
, the testbed for a
plague
created by the Symmetrists (
β
), a radical environmentalist group who believed that the "cosmic plan" that formed the basis of the
Prime Directive
should apply to planetary ecologies as well – after the famine, they assisted Kodos in his escape and provided him with the name
Anton Karidian
.
In the
Enterprise Logs
short story "Though Hell Should Bar the Way", the
USS
Enterprise
,
under the command
of
Robert April
, was enroute to Tarsus IV with relief supplies when it was attacked by a
Klingon
vessel commanded by
Kor
– though the
Enterprise
was able to fight off the Klingon ship (and dishonor Kor by not destroying it), they made it to Tarsus only to find that the massacre had already taken place, leaving April haunted, wondering if, had it not been for the Klingons, he might have reached the planet in time to save the colonists.
The novel
Drastic Measures
gave a detailed accounting of the events on Tarsus IV. This took place roughly 10 years prior to the
Battle of the Binary Stars
.
External links
Tarsus
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Tarsus IV
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Tarsus IV Massacre
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

Tartaras V
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2369
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Tartaras V
was the
inhabited
fifth
planet
in its
star system
.
In
2369
, the ruins of a
Rokai
provincial
capital
was unearthed.
Quark
suggested the planet to
Vash
as a potential source of valuable
Rokain
artifacts
, which he could then
auction
for her. Vash decided to take him up on his offer. (
DS9
: "
Q-Less
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to the
script
, the pronunciation for Tartaras was "tar-TAR-uhs".
[1]

======================================================================
PAGE: Tartus IV

Tartus IV
was the fourth
planet
in its
star system
It had
27 moons
.
While briefly captured by an
alien bounty hunter
, an
android imposter
of
Harcourt Fenton Mudd
tried to plead for his freedom, inquiring if she were romantically involved and suggesting the two visit the 27 moons of Tartus IV. (
ST
: "
The Escape Artist
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Tau Alpha C
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tau_Alpha_C
======================================================================

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Tau Alpha C
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Neutral
Status:
Habitable
(
24th century
)
Native species:
The Traveler's species
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
~1,000
light years
from
Sol system
Tau Alpha C
was an
inhabited
planet
that was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
and was the reported home of
The Traveler
. The
USS
Enterprise
-D
library computer
was familiar with its
inhabitants
.
It was located "at least" 1,000
light years
from the
Sol system
. Its existence remained unknown well into the mid-
23rd century
. Up to that point the planet had not appeared on contemporary
Federation
star charts
. This was due to active concealment of the fact of the existence of the planet by its inhabitants. (
TOS
: "
Assignment: Earth
")
Considered to be very distant by
Starfleet
, at
warp 9.5
, it would take 123
days
to travel from
Starbase 133
to Tau Alpha C. A
subspace
message would take days to travel back and forth between both
locations
. This location was listed on
Federation
star charts
. (
TNG
: "
Where No One Has Gone Before
", "
Remember Me
")
In an
alternate universe
created after
Doctor
Beverly Crusher
became trapped within a
warp bubble
, Wesley tried to contact Tau Alpha C for assistance when
individuals
began disappearing from the
Enterprise
-D, with only Dr. Crusher noticing. Ultimately, that entire universe, including its Tau Alpha C, disappeared.
(
TNG
: "
Remember Me
")
Appendices
Background information
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
StarTrek.com
identified Tau Alpha C as a
star system
.
[1]
(X)
Under naming conventions for stars (ex.
Alpha Centauri C
), the name would suggest Tau Alpha C was the third star in a
trinary
star system
(ex.
Alpha Centauri C
) or the third component in a multiple-star system (ex.
Beta Lyrae
).
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 3rd ed., p. 499 described Tau Alpha C as being a "very distant planet". However, in "
Journey's End
", the Traveler was instead said to be from
Tau Ceti
, a star system located approximately 11
light years
from Earth.
An original story concept for
VOY
: "
Prime Factors
" involved the
USS
Voyager
finding this planet (i.e. "Gary Seven's homeworld") in the
Delta Quadrant
.
[2]
Apocrypha
In
The Rise and Fall of Khan Noonien Singh, Volume One
, the Assigners' planet was a cloaked planet of the Aegis (
β
) in system Zeta Gamma 537 (
β
) and was 50,000
light years
from Earth.
In the
Star Trek: Department of Temporal Investigations
novel
Watching the Clock
, the planet was described as having
orange
skies
,
silver
trees
, and
cities
made of
crystal
spires.
^
It was home to "vast, extraordinary beings, with...great
eyes
."
Notes
^
This is probably a reference to
Gallifrey
from
Doctor Who
.
External link
Tau Alpha C
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

station

from

(
2328
)
Tau Ceti Deep Space Station
was a
deep space
station
in the
Tau Ceti
system
. Located in the
Alpha Quadrant
, this station was approximately 12
light years
from
Sol
.
In
2328
, this station was the point of embarkation for passengers traveling to
Platonius
via commercial
transport
.
Kenneth Ross
and
Richard Barnett
traveled there on the
SS
Kogin
. (

: "

",
okudagram
)
This station was only mentioned in writing.

======================================================================
PAGE: Tau Ceti III
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tau_Ceti_III
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Tau Ceti III
AKA:
Kaferia
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
(
3196
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Native species:
Kaferians
Location:
Tau Ceti
system
Alpha Quadrant
~12
light years
from
Sol
Tau Ceti III
, also known as
Kaferia
, was the
inhabited
third
planet
in the
Tau Ceti
system
. Located in the
Alpha Quadrant
, this planet was approximately 12
light years
from
Sol
. This was the
homeworld
of the
Kaferians
. (
TNG
: "
The Naked Now
", "
The Big Goodbye
",
production art
)
History
Prior to
stardate
41775
,
Jean-Luc Picard
first met
Walker Keel
at "quite an exotic"
bar
on Tau Ceti III. (
TNG
: "
Conspiracy
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet was within the enclosed region. (
SA
: "
300th Night
")
Stellar cartography
In
2259
, the location of this planet was labeled on a
stellar cartography
chart that was seen on the
USS
Enterprise
's
ready room
viewscreen
. This planet's symbol had a
blue
color
, indicating that it was affiliated with the
United Federation of Planets
. (
SNW
: "
Strange New Worlds
", "
Spock Amok
", "
A Quality of Mercy
")
On
stardate
1292.4, the location of this system was labeled in a chart used during a
Section 31
Alpha Team
briefing
. (
Star Trek: Section 31
)
The planet's location was labeled in a
Federation star chart
that was in
Fleet Admiral
Kirsten Clancy
's
office
at
Starfleet Headquarters
in
2399
and on the
bridge
of the
USS
Titan
-A
in
2401
. Kaferia was in or near to
Federation space
. (
PIC
: "
Maps and Legends
", "
The Next Generation
", "
Disengage
")
In
2401
, the location of Kaferia was labeled on a star chart that was seen on the
USS
Stargazer
's
bridge
viewscreen
. The chart was used to show the location of the combined Starfleet-Borg armada and where it needed to go to face a galactic-level threat. (
PIC
: "
Farewell
")
Later that year, this planet's position was labeled on a
star chart
used by
Captain
William T. Riker
during his attempt at finding the last known location of the
SS
Eleos XII
. (
PIC
: "
The Next Generation
")
In
3196
, during the
Omega-47
crisis, the location of Kaferia (Tau Ceti) was depicted on star charts and readouts used across several locations on the
USS
Athena
to locate, monitor, and respond to the Omega-47 mines laid by the Venari Ral. (
SA
: "
300th Night
")
Appendices
Background information
In the
script
at
Star Trek
Minutiae
for "Conspiracy", Tau Ceti III was listed in the pronunciation guide, where it was pronounced as "tow (rhymes with cow)- set-ee 3".
Both the
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 44) and
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Federation Historical Highlights, 2161-2385"), identified the Kaferia (Tau Ceti) system as a Federation system, with Tau Ceti III being listed as a
Federation member
in
2378
.
Apocrypha
In
The Worlds of the Federation
(p. 112), Tau Ceti III was also known as Kaferia and to the natives as Kohath-Seredi. It was the
planet
of origin for the
Kaferian apples
and the
Kaferians
, an
insectoid
species
. Kaferia and Kaferians were also mentioned in various other
reference works
and
novels
, and Kaferians were made canon when a labeled illustration from the
FASA
RPG
module
The Federation
was used in an
okudagram
seen in "
The Big Goodbye
". The Kaferian apple planet was alluded to in "
Where No Man Has Gone Before
", and it was mentioned that
Gary Mitchell
visited it multiple times, but it was not named in the episode.
The novel
Tower of Babel
distinguished Outer Kaferia (
Tau Ceti IV
), which was a
Human
colony
from Inner Kaferia (Tau Ceti III), the homeworld of the insectoid Kaferians. Both planets were named after the Kaferi family who were the system's first
explorers
and
settlers
. Tau Ceti III and IV were conquered by the
Romulans
during the
Earth-Romulan War
, but the Kaferians managed to escape the
occupation
by
hibernating
underground.
External link
Kaferia
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

light years
from
Sol

was the

fourth
planet
in the

system
. Located in the

, this planet was approximately 12

from

.

For the
Kobayashi Maru
scenario
in

, the
spaceport
Amber

of the

PAGE: Tau Ceti Prime
CATEGORIES: Planets

======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Tau Ceti Prime
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Location:
Tau Ceti
system
Alpha Quadrant
~12
light years
from
Sol
Tau Ceti Prime
was a
prime
planet
in the
Tau Ceti
system
. Located in the
Alpha Quadrant
, this planet was approximately 12
light years
from
Sol
.
History
Vice Admiral
Janeway
died
on Tau Ceti Prime, when he
drowned
under an
ice cap
, in
2358
. (
VOY
: "
Coda
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet was within the enclosed region. (
SA
: "
300th Night
")
Apocrypha
Background information
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
In the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 375, it was stated that Tau Ceti Prime was the
first
planet in the Tau Ceti system.
Apocrypha
In
The Autobiography of Kathryn Janeway
(pp. 63-64), Admiral Janeway's death was described as occurring while piloting a test vehicle that could transition quickly from orbital to suborbital to underwater flight on Tau Ceti Prime because the planet was the closest approximation to Etaris IV, a planet in contention during the
Cardassian Wars
.
External link
Tau Ceti Prime
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Tau Cygna V
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tau_Cygna_V
======================================================================

Tau Cygna V
Class:
H
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Sheliak Corporate
Location:
Tau Cygna
system
de Laure belt
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Tau Cygna V
was the
inhabited
H-class
fifth
planet
in the
Tau Cygna
system
. This system was located over five thousand
light years
from the
Sol
sector
, within the
de Laure belt
. According to Section 133, Paragraph 77 of the
Treaty of Armens
, the planet had been ceded from the
Federation
to the
Sheliak Corporate
.
The planet was saturated with
hyperonic radiation
that made
Human
living very difficult, and often considered impossible. It contained
mountains
and
desert
landscape but also had
water
able to be collected.
Rock slides
were not unheard of.
Sounds of what appeared to be
crickets
and
dogs
barking were heard on the planet. The latter might merely have been related to
Ard'rian McKenzie
's robotic dog.
Federation colony
In
2274
, the
colony ship
SS
Artemis
headed for
Septimis Minor
, suffered failure of its
guidance system
, and went far off
course
, finding Tau Cygna V as the only suitable planet within reach on which to land. One third of the crew and passengers died of the radiation before they learned to adapt to it. The
survivors
established a new
colony
with their
technology
, building a
town
with living facilities in a desert area, made possible by an
aqueduct
with a
pumping station
originating on a nearby mountain. Many more died bringing water to the colony. They also cultivated large swaths of fields. The colonists remained lost to the Federation and completely unknown to the
Sheliak
as the colony grew over the next ninety-two
years
. At the colony, survival was valued over
history
, and while the colonists still knew their
ancestors
came from the Federation, some knowledge, such as what
phasers
were, was lost.
By
2366
, the
population
had reached 15,253, descending from over three
generations
of the original
settlers
. Their
leader
at that time was
Gosheven
, who inspired further progress of the colony's growth.
During that same year, the colonists had to be evacuated due to the Sheliak discovering them on the planet while planning for their own colony to be placed there. The
USS
Enterprise
-D
was notified of the
trespassing
and subsequently
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
sent
Lieutenant Commander
Data
to the
surface
in order to convince them it was futile to stay, as the Sheliak would simply kill them. While difficult, he succeeded and the colonists spent the next three
weeks
preparing for a Federation
transport ship
. After the evacuation, the Sheliak established their colony on the planet. (
TNG
: "
The Ensigns of Command
")
Inhabitants
Tau Cygna V inhabitants
External link
Tau Cygna V
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Taurus II
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Taurus_II
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Scouts
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Taurus II
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Non-aligned
Status:
Habitable
(
24th century
)
Native species:
Taurus II anthropoids
possibly
Nobonimbus
Location:
Unnamed system
Unnamed sector
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
3
light days
from
Makus III
For the second planet of the Taurean system, please see
Planet Two
.
Taurus II
was the
inhabited
second
planet
of its
star system
. The planet was located inside a
sector
of the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
. Classified as
M class
, this was the
homeworld
for
a species of anthropoids
.
The planet was in the vicinity of
Murasaki 312
, a
quasar
-like
phenomena
, and was three
days
away from
Makus III
at
warp 1
. (
TOS
: "
The Galileo Seven
")
History
As of
2267
, the planet had remained unexplored by the
Federation
. Long-range scans had revealed basic characteristics of this planet, such as that it had an
oxygen
-
nitrogen
atmosphere
.
That same year, the
shuttlecraft
Galileo
crash landed
on Taurus II while attempting to investigate the
Murasaki 312
. The
crew
, under the
command
of
Lieutenant Commander
Spock
, made
first contact
with the inhabitants, and lost crew members
Latimer
and
Gaetano
while on the
surface
.
The
inhabitants
of Taurus II were
order 480-G
anthropoids
, similar to
lifeforms
discovered on
Hansen's Planet
but much larger, ten to twelve
feet
in
height
. They were at approximately a
Stone Age
level of
technology
, slightly less advanced than a
tribal culture
. (
TOS
: "
The Galileo Seven
")
Lieutenant Commander
Ron Surma
, a
crewmember
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
, was born on this non-
Federation
planet
. In
2369
, his planet of origin was listed on the chart "USS
Enterprise
Personnel Database", which was displayed on a
PADD
accessed by
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
. (
TNG
: "
The Chase
"
okudagram
)
JR
,
Roo
, and
Sprocket
played a
holoprogram
in which they visited this planet when they brought a
nobonimbus
back home after it was
kidnapped
by
Shadowtrix
. (
SCO
: "
Holodeck Rescues, Number 2
")
It is unclear whether Nobonimbus are a real animal found on Taurus II due it being a holo-program for children.
Appendices
Background information
This planet was named after the
constellation
Taurus
. (
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 375) In fact, due to "Taurus" already being the name of a constellation when "The Galileo Seven" was written,
Kellam de Forest
suggested instead renaming this planet "Eta Tauri II" (in a series of research notes dated
8 September
1966
).
In the
script
of "The Galileo Seven", the surface of Taurus II was described as featuring "
a sort of a
crater
, with overhanging rock walls jutting up against a dull featureless
sky
. The sky is a particularly bilious shade of
green
. Only a few scrubby
plants
are seen. It is a most unprepossessing sight.
" The script's stage directions also established that some areas on the planet's surface were to be misty.
The surface of Taurus II was depicted with a set on
Desilu Stage 10
.
Apocrypha
In the
Star Trek Online
tutorial mission for
23rd century
Starfleet
captains, the player leads an
away team
to find survivors from a
Pioneer
-class
science vessel
that had crashed on Taurus II. The away team must repulse attacks from the indigenous Taureans and escape back to their ship in orbit with the survivors.
External links
Beta Tauri II
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Planets in TOS and TOS Remastered
at
Ex Astris Scientia

For the similar book title, please see
The Rings of Tautee
.
Tautine
was a location famous for its
rings
.
In
2369
,
Q
hoped to tempt
Amanda Rogers
with the possibilities of embracing her origins in the
Q Continuum
. He told her she could have whatever she wanted instantly, such as precious
jewels
, works of
art
, or even walking along the rings of Tautine. Rogers wasn't interested in any of his suggestions. (
TNG
: "
True Q
")
This location was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Tavela Minor
CATEGORIES: Planets

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Tavela Minor
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2368
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Tavela Minor
was an
inhabited
minor
planet
. The
planet
was home to the
Jokri River
.
Prior to
stardate
45852
,
Beverly Crusher
had visited this planet.
In 2368, aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
,
Alyssa Ogawa
's
boyfriend
asked her if she wanted to go with him to
Risa
during his
shore leave
.
Nurse
Ogawa felt that Risa had an uninhibited atmosphere, and she didn't know if she was ready for that much fun.
Dr.
Crusher suggested that she talk her boyfriend into going to Tavela Minor, where they could take a cruise down the Jokri River. (
TNG
: "
Imaginary Friend
")
In
3195
, this planet's name appeared on a
video wall
in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
Kids These Days
")
According to the
script
for "Imaginary Friend", this place was originally called "Telana Minor". It was changed prior to the airing of the episode.
[1]
According to the
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I"), Tavela Minor was listed as a
Federation member
.
According to the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 3rd ed., p. 499, Tavela Minor was a Federation planet.

Tavela Minor
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Taygeta I
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Taygeta_I
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Taygeta I
was the
inhabited
first
planet
in its
star system
.
In the late
32nd century
,
Nus Braka
controlled a stretch of
space
between two unstable
wormholes
that was the only travel route from Taygeta I to
Taygeta IV
that did not require
warp drive
. This once-lucrative position became defunct once the
Federation
began supplying
dilithium
to Taygeta I.
In
3195
, in exchange for offering information about the
Furies
, Braka demanded that the Federation cease its dilithium supplies to Taygeta I.
Federation President
Laira Rillak
reluctantly agreed, as the lives of five
Starfleet
cadets
were at stake. (
SA
: "
Come, Let's Away
")
Given the later revelation that Braka actually orchestrated the whole thing, this agreement was probably not upheld.
External link
Taygeta I
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

. This once-lucrative position became defunct once the
