======================================================================
PAGE: Thelonii
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Thelonii
======================================================================

Thelonii
Type:
Astronomical
object
Status:
Intact
(
2285
)
Location:
Alpha Quadrant
Thelonii
was a
astronomical
object
in the same
region
as
Sol
/
Jupiter
. Its companion was
Xanthii
.
In
2285
, Thelonii's position was displayed on a
star chart
on the
bridge
of the
USS
Enterprise
. (
Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan
)
This Astronomical object was only mentioned in writing.
The image was taken from page T0:02:07:02 of the
Star Fleet Technical Manual
by
Franz Joseph
. The map claimed to depict principal
star systems
in the
Federation
. According to the scale, Thelonii was located two
parsecs
from Sol. Thelonii and Xanthii were stars in a
trinary
star system
. The name of a third star, Alfr, was not seen in the movie.
External link
Thelonii
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Therbia
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Therbia
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Therbia
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Non-aligned
Status:
Habitable
(
2369
)
Location:
Therbia
system
Alpha Quadrant
Therbia
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Therbia (Epsilon Serpentis) system
. This was a non-
Federation
planet.
History
Rene Echevarria
, a
lieutenant commander
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
, was from this planet. In
2369
, his planet of origin was listed on the chart "USS
Enterprise
Personnel Database", which was displayed on a
PADD
accessed by
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
. (
TNG
: "
The Chase
",
okudagram
)
Stellar cartography
In
3196
, during the
Omega-47
crisis, the location of Aaamazzara (Therbia, Epsilon Serpentis) was depicted on star charts and readouts used across several locations on the
USS
Athena
to locate, monitor, and respond to the
Omega-47 mines
laid by the
Venari Ral
. This system was outside the affected region. (
SA
: "
300th Night
", "
Rubincon
")
Appendices
Background information
This planet was only mentioned in writing.
According to
Star Trek: The Motion Picture
production sources,  Therbia was the
homeworld
of the
Aaamazzarites
, an
alien
species
created for but barely seen in the film.
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I"), Therbia corresponded with the real star
Epsilon Serpentis
and was synonymous with
Aaamazzara
. (Fred Phillips confusingly described Aaamazzarites as "the Therbians from planet Aaamazzara" when he identified the species). The fact that Aaamazzara and Therbia were both mentioned in the same list would suggest they were different planets however.
External link
Therbia
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

PAGE: Theta Cygni XII

This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain

Theta Cygni XII
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
3196
)
Native species:
Theta Cygni XII natives
Location:
Theta Cygni
system
Alpha Quadrant
~60
light years
from
Sol
Theta Cygni XII
was the formerly
inhabited
twelfth
planet
in the
Theta Cygni
system
. Located in the
Alpha Quadrant
, this planet was approximately 60
light years
from
Sol
. The planet was located in the same section of the
galaxy
as the
Beta Portolan
system
,
Levinius V
,
Ingraham B
, and
Deneva
.
History
Sometime between
2067
and
2265
, Theta Cygni XII was invaded by
flying parasites
that came from Levinius V. The
civilization
on Theta Cygni XII was destroyed in the
mass insanity
caused by the parasites. The inhabitants transported this parasite over to Ingraham B. (
TOS
: "
Operation -- Annihilate!
")
In the late
32nd century
, this planet was near or in the
Venari Ral Zone
. (
SA
: "
300th Night
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
External links
Theta Cygni XII
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Theta Cygni
at
Wikipedia

location
in the

.

, Theta Mees' position was labeled on a
tactical situation monitor
in
Captain
Benjamin Maxwell
's
ready room
on the
USS
Phoenix
. (
TNG
: "
The Wounded
",
okudagram
)

2366
, in an
alternate timeline

Federation-Klingon War
, Theta Mees' position was labeled on a tactical situation monitor on the
bridge
and in the
ready room
of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
.
(
TNG
: "
Yesterday's Enterprise
",
okudagram
)

Altair
system
(
Altair III
),
Andor
,
Babel
,
Delta Vega
,
Eminiar
,
Janus VI
, and
Rigel
– as seen in the
star charts
appearing in

Theta VII
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2268
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Theta VII
was the
inhabited
seventh
planet
in its
system
. This planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
and was the home to the
Theta VII colony
.
In
2268
, the
USS
Enterprise
was to deliver
vaccines
to this planet, after rendezvousing with the
USS
Yorktown
, from which they were to procure the vaccines. They were delayed by an investigation into the
dikironium cloud creature
. (
TOS
: "
Obsession
")
See also
Theta III
Theta VIII
Appendices
Background information
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I"), Theta VII was a
Federation member
in
2378
.
Apocrypha
In the
Star Trek Maps
(p. 30), the Theta system consisted of a
blue giant
star
orbited
by eleven planets. Theta VII was the sole
M-class
planet.
In the
FASA
source book
The Federation
, Theta VII was located in the Theta Gamma (
β
) system.

at

======================================================================
PAGE: Theta VIII
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Theta_VIII
======================================================================

Theta VIII
Class:
K (transjovian)
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2365
)
Location:
Theta 116
system
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Theta VIII
(or
Theta Eight
) was the
uninhabited
eighth
planet
of the
Theta 116 solar system
. The planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
Planetary Characteristics
This world had an
orbital period
of 832
days
, and a rotational period of 15.3
hours
. A
K-class transjovian
planet, the
atmosphere
of Theta 116 VIII was composed of
nitrogen
(57%),
methane
(23%),
liquid
neon
(14%),
yurium
(2%),
keimium
compounds (2%),
ethane
(1%), and
tormium
(trace). The mean
surface temperature
was
−291
°C
, with
wind
speeds
of up to 320
meters per second
. Interesting
weather
phenomena
included
ammonia
tornadoes
. Its age was estimated at
7.2
e10
×10
10
solar years
, and during that time it had built up a
mass
of 4.35
e12
×10
12
.
No unit was specified for the planet's mass. See also
background information
.
History
In the
21st century
, the
NASA
spacecraft
Charybdis
was brought in
orbit
by an
unknown intelligence
which had inadvertently killed all but one of its
crew
,
Colonel
Stephen G. Richey
. The intelligence attempted to create an environment for him where he could live, in the form of a small pocket of
Earth
-like atmosphere on the
surface
. In it was the entryway to a replica of the
namesake hotel
from the
novel
Hotel Royale
carried by Richey.
Richey lived in
room
727 of the hotel until he
died
in his
sleep
in
2082
. The hotel was located on a plane of frozen methane with the dimensions of 5.02 × 7.9
kilometers
, in the middle of a tremendous storm belt, described as like being in the "eye of a
hurricane
." The Hotel itself had the following dimensions: 127 × 53 × 244
meters
.
Remains of the
Charybdis
were detected by a
Klingon
cruiser
in
2365
. They were found to have molecular disintegration similar to those caused by
24th century
-level weapon impacts. The
USS
Enterprise
-D
investigated the planet and discovered Richey's fate. (
TNG
: "
The Royale
")
Planetary scan – Theta eight
Surface feature analysis – Theta eight
Appendices
Background information
In the original (un-remastered) episode, the planet model for Theta VIII was first used for
Angel I
in "
Angel One
". It was used again for
Minos
in "
The Arsenal of Freedom
" and again for
Legara IV
in "
Sarek
".
The physical parameters of Theta VIII as seen on the planetary scan-
okudagram
included several curiosities:
In real-world
physics
,
absolute zero
wass defined as −273 °C, making Theta VII's mean surface temperature of −291 °C impossible. (
Note
: In the episode's
script
, −291 °
Fahrenheit
was used, i.e., about −179 °C.)
If we assume "solar years" implied Earth years, the planet's age of 7.2
e10
×10
10
(72 billion) solar years surpassed the
age of the universe
by a factor of approximately five. Using Theta VIII's given orbital period of 832 days would even imply the planet to be roughly ten times older than the
universe
.
If the planet's mass of 4.35
e12
×10
12
was meant to be in
kilograms
or
metric tons
, it would be extremely low and correspond to only a tiny fraction of
Earth
's mass, or up to twenty times the mass of
Halley's Comet
.
A scene ultimately removed from the final version of "The Royale" described Theta VIII further; "
Ship's log,
stardate
42625.4,
First Officer
Riker
reporting. We have achieved orbit around Theta Eight
[...]
the planet furthest from its
sun
in this previously unmapped solar system. It is a world of ruthless realities: particularly harsh, barren, and inhospitable.
"
[1]
Before "The Royale", two other planets with the word "Theta" in their names, planets in at least one other, previously-mapped solar system which may also have "Theta" in its name, have been mentioned in Star Trek:
Theta III
seen on
a star chart
and
Theta VII
, a
colony
planet, where
USS
Enterprise
delivered
vaccines
after the events in
TOS
: "
Obsession
" set in
2265
.
External links
Theta 116 VIII
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Re-Used Planets in TNG
at
Ex Astris Scientia

======================================================================
PAGE: Theta Zeta
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Theta_Zeta
======================================================================

Theta Zeta
Class:
Y
Type:
Planet
/
Planetoid
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Intact
(
3189
)
Satellite(s):
Several
ring systems
Location:
Verubin Nebula
"
A planet made of dilithium. The Federation could use that kind of news right about now.
"
–
Charles Vance
,
3189
("
Su'Kal
")
Theta Zeta
was the designation for an
uncatalogued
class Y
planet
or
planetoid
located near the centre of the
Verubin Nebula
. An extremely rare planetoid not only was made of dilithium but would continuously grow them as well.
It also had the unfortunate distinction of being
ground zero
for
The Burn
in
3069
. Ironically, when it was re-discovered
a century later
, its abundance of
dilithium
was crucial in allowing the
United Federation of Planets
to recover.
Planetary features
Theta Zeta's environment was extremely harsh, bombarded by lethal
radiation
consisting of two types of
theta radiation
,
delta radiation
,
electromagnetic radiation
,
subspace radiation
, and two
hitherto unknown forms of radiation
.
Its surface was marked by massive concavities and it was
orbited
by chains of numerous small
asteroidal
bodies. It had large quantities of
dilithium
and was identified with a
dilithium nursery
. (
DIS
: "
Su'Kal
")
History
Scan results of the planetoid's toxic radiation
In
3064
, the
KSF
Khi'eth
, a
Kelpien
research vessel,
crashed
on Theta Zeta while investigating the dilithium nursery.
Five years later
,
Su'Kal
, a child born to
Dr.
Issa
among the wreckage, witnessed the death of his mother by
radiation poisoning
and screamed in horror. Because his genes had mutated to interact with dilithium in unique ways, and dilithium had a
subspace
component, this scream unleashed
the Burn
across the galaxy without his knowledge. (
DIS
: "
Terra Firma, Part 1
", "
Su'Kal
", "
That Hope Is You, Part 2
")
In
3189
, the
Federation
starship
USS
Discovery
tracked the source of the Burn to Theta Zeta.
Cleveland Booker
was able to identify a pocket within the Verubin Nebula near the planet for
Discovery
to
spore-jump
to in an attempt to investigate the
crash site
of the
Khi'eth
, apparently the only structure on the planet/planetoid. When
Captain
Saru
,
Commander
Michael Burnham
, and
Doctor
Hugh Culber
transported
to the ship, they found the sole
life sign
to be Su'Kal, still alive and living in a
holographic simulation
. Saru, Culber, and
Adira Tal
stayed behind on the
Khi'eth
to attempt to reach Su'Kal while Burnham returned to
Discovery
with Booker to retake the starship from
Osyraa
and the
Emerald Chain
. (
DIS
: "
Su'Kal
")
Eager to
mine
dilithium from Theta Zeta, Osyraa tried to
torture
the planet's location within the nebula out of Booker, but Burnham rescued him before Osyraa could break him. After
Discovery
was re-taken, the vessel jumped back to Theta Zeta and rescued the away team and Su'Kal moments before the
Khi'eth
's remains finally fell apart.
In the aftermath, the Federation figured out how to safely mine Theta Zeta, and began distributing its dilithium to resource-starved worlds across the galaxy. (
DIS
: "
That Hope Is You, Part 2
")
Appendices
Background information
This planet's name was only mentioned in writing. It was described there as a "planet/planetoid", although in dialogue it was consistently referred to as a planet. Digital artist
Timothy Peel
referred to Theta Zeta as "the dilithium planet," noting on Twitter that it was "a complex planet to render and animate!"
[1]
In "
Su'Kal
",
Owosekun
says that the
Khi'eth
crash site on Theta Zeta is located "
two hundred kilometers into the nebula.
" Given the apparent size of the Verubin Nebula – large enough to make locating the planet difficult for Osyraa in "
There Is A Tide...
" – this comment should perhaps not be taken at face value.

======================================================================
PAGE: Theta Zibal III
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Theta_Zibal_III
======================================================================

Theta Zibal III
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
24th century
)
Native species:
Zibalians
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Theta Zibal III
was the
inhabited
third
planet
of its
star system
. It was the
homeworld
of the
warp-capable
humanoid
Zibalians
.
One of the
cities
on this planet was named
Tomobiki City
. The planet was mentioned in the
Federation biographical database
file of
Kivas Fajo
. (
TNG
: "
The Most Toys
"
okudagram
)
This planet was only mentioned in writing.
The planet might be associated with
Zibal
, which was a real
star
.
In the
Bayer designation
, which was a system of stellar classification, the Greek letter indicated the brightness of a star in a
constellation
. In this case, Theta Zibal would be the 8th brightest star in its constellation.

======================================================================
PAGE: Tholia
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tholia
======================================================================

Tholia
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Tholian Assembly
Tholian Republic
Status:
Habitable
(
32nd century
)
Native species:
Tholians
Location:
Tholia system
Tholian space
Alpha Quadrant
Tholia
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Tholia
system
. This
homeworld
of the
Tholians
was located inside
Tholian space
in the
Alpha Quadrant
. The planet was the
capital planet
of the
Tholian Assembly
and, later, the
Tholian Republic
. (
SNW
: "
Children of the Comet
")
In
2151
, "
Tholia
" was one of four proper
nouns
stated by the
Klingon
courier
Klaang
while being transported to
Qo'noS
aboard
Enterprise
NX-01
.
Ensign
Hoshi Sato
,
Enterprise
's
communications officer
, was unable to translate the word. (
ENT
: "
Broken Bow
")
In
2293
, the location of this planet was labeled in a
star chart
found on the
navigation console
of the
USS
Enterprise
-B
. (
Star Trek Generations
)
In early
24th century
, the location of Tholia was labeled on a
Section 31
star chart
used in a briefing presented by
Control
to Alpha Team.  (
Star Trek: Section 31
)
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 35),
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Stellar Cartography", p. 10), and
The Worlds of the Federation
(p. 150), Tholia was the
home planet
of the
Tholian
species
. A
class Y planet
with a searing hot
methane
environment, it was the second planet in the Tholian system. In general, an association of "Tholia" with the Tholians is sensible, as they are later revealed to be a faction (if a rather obscure one) of the
Temporal Cold War
– the very conflict that ended Klaang on
Enterprise
in the first place.

======================================================================
PAGE: Tholian asteroid dock
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tholian_asteroid_dock
======================================================================

Mirror universe
(
coexisting on another dimensional plane
)
Tholian asteroid dock
Type:
Spacedock
Affiliation:
Tholian Assembly
Status:
Destroyed
(
2155
)
Armament:
Particle beam weapon
The
Tholian
asteroid
dock
was a
drydock
facility constructed within an
asteroid
-
moon
that orbited a
gas giant
in the
Vintaak system
in the
mirror universe
. It was there that the
Tholian Assembly
, harbored the
USS
Defiant
in
2155
, unexpectedly obtained from the future of an
alternate universe
, for study and evaluation. (
TOS
: "
The Tholian Web
")
The
Defiant
begins its attack on the spacedock
Unbeknownst to the Assembly however, the
Terran Empire
became aware of the existence of the ship from the alternate universe future, and sent forth
ISS
Enterprise
to capture the ship. Though managing to destroy
Enterprise
in the ensuing battle, the Tholians were not able to prevent the capture of the
Defiant
, which, after coming under the control of a boarding party, managed to escape from their control and inflicted heavy damage on their facility, resulting in the structure exploding. (
ENT
: "
In A Mirror, Darkly
", "
In A Mirror, Darkly, Part II
")
Background information
The structure was not identified by name in the episode, nor in any other contemporary production source; its designer,
Doug Drexler
, coined the term "Tholian asteroid dock" after-the-fact.
[1]
(X)
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 393 uses the term "Tholian asteroid base" for this structure.
Digital model
The in-an-asteroid embedded Tholian spacedock for "
In A Mirror, Darkly
" nearly did not make it into the episode, as Producer
Michael Sussman
recalled; "
I remember writing a description of the asteroid base in the script for part one of 'Mirror Darkly', half expecting it to get thrown out for budgetary or other reasons. And then at the production meeting
Herman [Zimmerman]
held up the image of the
[Ralph] McQuarrie
asteroid base and asked the producers if they wanted something like this. I got a chill down my spine, realizing the McQuarrie painting had probably planted the idea. It was just too cool that an unused concept painting for
ST:TMP
by the master himself was recycled something like a quarter century later at a production meeting for a
Trek
episode I wrote. Still gives me chills.
"
[2]
(X)
McQuarrie's asteroid spacedock concept
Eden FX's asteroid spacedock
McQuarrie's interior asteroid spacedock concept
Eden FX's interior asteroid spacedock
The Tholian spacedock was designed and built as a
CGI
model
by Doug Drexler. Likewise, Drexler was inspired by pre-production paintings Ralph McQuarrie had done for an abandoned
1976
-
1977
project called
Star Trek: Planet of the Titans
(not
Star Trek: The Motion Picture
, as both Sussman and Drexler assumed). "
I've always loved the idea of this, and was fascinated by Ralph McQuarrie's TMP asteroid base painting,
" Drexler stated.
[3]
(X)
Using pre-existing CGI parts from various other models, Drexler constructed his model in one day. "
I had to do a variety of ideas in a couple of days so an afternoon is about it,
" he remembered.
[4]
(X)
"
Believe me, I had a kludge box full of parts and nernies. I couldn't afford to build it all from scratch. There are a lot of freighter parts in there!
"
[5]
(X)
Drexler sent off his model to
Eden FX
, where CGI modeler
Mike Stetson
expanded upon and fine-tuned the model, and where it was combined with the
Constitution
-class CG model
of the
USS
Defiant
, built by
Koji Kuramura
and
Robert Bonchune
for their appearance in the episode. Both models were re-used to represent the same scene in the
2006
reference book
Ships of the Line
(p. 29), and on the cover of the
Star Trek: Enterprise Calendar (2006)
.

PAGE: Tiburon
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tiburon
======================================================================

For the city with the same name, please see
Tiburon, California
.
Tiburon
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
3188
)
Native species:
Tiburonians
Location:
Tiburon system
Beta Quadrant
Tiburon
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Tiburon system
. Located in the
Beta Quadrant
, this was the
homeworld
for the
Tiburonians
, a
warp-capable
humanoid
species
. Parts of the planet were cold and experienced
pink
snow
. (
TOS
: "
The Savage Curtain
";
DIS
: "
Coming Home
")
History
Zora
was an infamous historical figure on Tiburon, known for her cruel and inhumane
experiments
on humanoid subjects to test the
body chemistry
of subject
tribes
. (
TOS
: "
The Savage Curtain
")
In
2149
,
Doctor
Phlox
attended an
IME
conference on Tiburon, where he briefly met a member of the
Mazarite
delegation
. Unknown to him at the time, this member was actually a
Klingon
expert
in
metagenics
research, named
Dr.
Antaak
. The Klingons were not invited to the conference. (
ENT
: "
Affliction
")
Tiburon was the former home of Dr.
Sevrin
in the mid-
23rd century
, where he studied to become an expert in the
fields
of
acoustics
,
electronics
, and
communications
. (
TOS
: "
The Way to Eden
")
In the early 24th century, the Tiburon system was marked on a
Section 31
map as controlled by the
Klingon Empire
. (
Star Trek: Section 31
)
Michael Burnham
and
Cleveland Booker
had a perilous escapade on Tiburon while they worked together as
couriers
in
3188
. Booker would later recall that this was when he realized that he had fallen in love with Burnham. (
DIS
: "
Coming Home
")
Appendices
Background information
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Discovery
episode "
An Obol for Charon
".
Apocrypha
In
FASA
's
The Federation
sourcebook, Tiburon was the
second
planet in the Beta Theseus system.
External link
Tiburon
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

PAGE: Tilonus IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tilonus_IV
======================================================================

Tilonus IV
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Non-aligned
Status:
Habitable
(
2369
)
Native species:
Tilonians
Spiny lobe-fish
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
A map of a populated area on Tilonus IV
Tilonus IV
was the
inhabited
fourth
planet
in its
star system
. It was the
homeworld
for the
Tilonians
, a highly advanced
humanoid
species
, and for the
spiny lobe-fish
.
A
Starfleet
research team was on the planet. They were housed in a small
building
in the southwest quarter of a
city
. This quarter's area was two hundred square
kilometers
.
In
2369
, after the
assassination
of the
Prime Minister
, the
government
of Tilonus IV collapsed into
anarchy
. The remnants of the government splintered into
factions
that were vying for control and were desperately looking for technology or
weapons
that would aid them in their cause. Some factions resorted to
torture
for information. Fearing for their safety, the research team on the planet went into hiding.
Aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
,
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
ordered
Commander
William T. Riker
to locate and rescue the research team there. He was captured by one of the factions and subjected to a
neurosomatic technique
used by
Suna
in an attempt at extracting strategic information from him. (
TNG
: "
Frame of Mind
")
According to the
script
, the pronunciation for Tilonus was "tie-LONE-us".
[1]
According to the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 398, Tilonus IV was a
class M
planet.

was the

second

in its

. It was located in the same
sector
as the

.
A few years before
2375
, the
Ferengi
discovered a large deposit of
pergium
on the planet, and opened what became the fifth largest pergium
mining
operation in the sector, knocking
Yanas Tigan
's facility on
Sappora VII
out of the top five. (
DS9
: "
Prodigal Daughter
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Torga IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Torga_IV
======================================================================

Torga IV
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2373
)
Location:
Gamma Quadrant
Torga IV
was the
uninhabited
fourth
planet
in its
system
, in the
Gamma Quadrant
. The planet was approximately three
weeks
from a
Dominion
outpost
, and fifty thousand
light years
from
Deep Space 9
. This planet possessed rich resources of
cormaline
.
In
2373
, a group of
Starfleet
officers
from Deep Space 9, led by
Captain
Benjamin Sisko
, visited the planet in a
runabout
in order to determine the feasibility of establishing a
mining
operation on the planet. Although the cormaline was only a few hundred
meters
below the planet's
surface
, the main threat to any possible mining operation was the planet's proximity to
Dominion space
.
Indeed, the Dominion arrived soon after, when a
Jem'Hadar
ship carrying a
Founder
crashed
on the planet. After Sisko and his team occupied it, another Jem'Hadar ship arrived and destroyed the team's
runabout
, killing its
crew
, including
Hoya
. A bitter siege ensued that led to the
deaths
of
Enrique Muniz
,
T'Lor
and the Founder, as well as
Kilana
's Jem'Hadar. The crew was later able to remove the ship from Torga IV with the help of the
USS
Defiant
. (
DS9
: "
The Ship
")
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 75), the
primary
of the Torga system was an
M-class star
.
According to
StarTrek.com
and the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 406, Torga IV was an
M-class
planet.
[1]
(X)
According to
Star Trek Encyclopedia
(4th ed., vol. 2, p. 406), the exterior scenes set on Torga IV were filmed at a
rock
quarry
north of
Los Angeles
.
External links
Torga IV
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Re-Used Planets in DS9
at
Ex Astris Scientia

.

Torna IV
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2372
)
Location:

or
Beta Quadrant
Torna IV
was the
inhabited
fourth
planet
in its
star system
.
Kor
visited a
tavern
on Torna IV on his way to
Deep Space 9
in
2372
. In the tavern, he became
drunk
and revealed that he found a
Hur'q
cloth that would lead him to the
Sword of Kahless
. Unfortunately,
Toral
of the
House of Duras
was also present and sent a
Lethean
,
Soto
, to follow Kor and learn more. (
DS9
: "
The Sword of Kahless
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to
StarTrek.com
, Torna IV was a
M-class
planet in the
Alpha Quadrant
.
[1]
(X)
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 167 characterized this planet as class M.
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 64) and
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Federation Historical Highlights, 2161-2385"), the Torna system was located in the
Beta Quadrant
. The system's
primary
was a
G-class star
.

at

======================================================================
PAGE: Torros III
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Torros_III
======================================================================

Torros III
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Cardassian Union
(
24th century
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Location:
Torros system
Alpha Quadrant
Torros III
was the
third
planet
in the
Torros system
,
Alpha Quadrant
.
History
In the
24th century
, this planet was inside
Cardassian space
and, in
2373
, was home to a
Dominion
shipyard
.
At the outbreak of the
Dominion War
in 2373, a
task force
comprised of
Starfleet
and
Klingon
forces attacked and destroyed the shipyards while Dominion forces were
occupied attacking
Deep Space 9
. Starfleet chose to launch an attack on the shipyards rather than send reinforcements to Deep Space 9, which was lost to the Dominion. However, DS9 personnel were able to establish a
minefield
at the mouth of the
wormhole
prior to being forced from the station, which severed the Dominion's supply line from their home
territory
in the
Gamma Quadrant
.
The destruction of the shipyards at Torros III, together with the mining of the Bajoran wormhole, was an early blow to the Dominion war effort in the Alpha Quadrant. Even so, the Dominion was able to launch a successful campaign into
Federation
territory
. (
DS9
: "
Call to Arms
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of the Cardassian Union as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
"TOR-oes" was the pronunciation for this planet's
name
from the
script
pronunciation guide for "
Call to Arms
".
External link
Torros III
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Torus 4 Deep Space Research Outpost
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Torus_4_Deep_Space_Research_Outpost
======================================================================

Torus 4 Deep Space Research Outpost
Type:
Starbase 1
-type
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Active
(
2230s
)
The
Torus 4 Deep Space Research Outpost
or
Torus 4 Outpost
was a
Starbase 1
-type
Federation
outpost
located in a turbulent region containing several celestial objects.
Mike
and
Gabrielle Burnham
resided on this outpost with
Section 31
in the early
2230s
. Also with them was their
daughter
,
Michael Burnham
, who had
bad dreams
due to the isolation. To ease her fears and get her back to sleep, her father told her the story of "
the girl who made the stars
" as a bedtime story.
Michael had a
model
of the outpost in her bedroom. (
ST
: "
The Girl Who Made the Stars
")
The outpost was not named in dialogue, but comes from the script seen in the
ST DVD
special feature "Bedtime Stories".

======================================================================
PAGE: Totoro
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Totoro
======================================================================

Totoro
AKA:
Braslota I
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2365
)
Location:
Braslota
system
Oneamisu
sector
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Totoro
, also known as
Braslota I
, was the
uninhabited
first
planet
in the
Braslota
system
. This system, which was in the
Oneamisu
sector
, was selected by
Starfleet Command
in
2365
as the
location
for the
Starfleet
battle simulation
Operation Lovely Angel
.
In the simulation, the
starships
USS
Hathaway
and
USS
Enterprise
-D
had simulated objectives that either ship had to complete before progressing to the next objective. In the first objective, either ship would attempt a successful capture of control of near-planetary
space
in the
region
of
Yuri
, while, at the same time, maintaining a defensive posture from a simulated threat from
Kei
. The winning ship would proceed to the next objective.
In the second objective, this ship would simulate an assault against predetermined planetside targets on Totoro. For success in this objective, the ship would have to incur fewer than 238 simulated damage points from the opponent.
Totoro was labeled on a diagram of the Braslota system. In this diagram, the planet's position and
orbital
path were depicted. This diagram was on display in the
observation lounge
of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. (
TNG
: "
Peak Performance
",
okudagram
)
This planet was only mentioned in writing.
Most of the simulation information was
in-joke
references to
anime
, including
My Neighbor Totoro
for this specific example. (
Star Trek: The Next Generation Companion
, 2nd ed., p. 92)
External link
Totoro
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
A
tourist attraction
or simply
attraction
was a
location
or
facility
that catered to
tourists
rather than locals.
In
1986
, a bag depicting various tourist attractions in
California
was carried by a
tourist
visiting the
Cetacean Institute
. (
Star Trek IV: The Voyage Home
)
Attractions were listed on
Yorktown
's
public transporter
menu
. (
Star Trek Beyond
)
In
2375
,
Captain
Kathryn Janeway
told
Seven of Nine
and
Tuvok
to plot a
course
for
USS
Voyager
's trip through
Devore
space
, asking them to avoid their
colonies
,
space stations
and tourist attractions, assuming the Devore had such things. (
VOY
: "
Counterpoint
")
Some of these attractions were referred to negatively as
tourist traps
.
Gedi Prime
was known as "
The
vacation
paradise that makes
Risa
look like a tourist trap.
" (
VOY
: "
Drive
")

PAGE: Tracken II
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tracken_II
======================================================================

Tracken II
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Maquis
(
2370s
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Location:
Tracken
system
Alpha Quadrant
Tracken II
was the habitable
second
planet
in the
Tracken
system
,
Alpha Quadrant
.
History
Between
2368
and
2373
, this planet's system was in the
Demilitarized Zone
.
Sometime within these
years
, the
Maquis
established a
colony
on Tracken II.
In
2373
,
Captain
Benjamin Sisko
threatened to render the world uninhabitable to
Humans
using
trilithium resin
unless
Michael Eddington
surrendered
himself and his stocks of
cobalt diselenide
. Eddington agreed, and the planet was spared. (
DS9
: "
For the Uniform
")
Later that
year
, however, the colony was destroyed by a
Dominion
-
Cardassian
fleet
. (
DS9
: "
By Inferno's Light
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
In the
script
of 'For the Uniform', the pronunciation for Tracken was given as "TRAK-un".
[1]

Trebus
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Independent
Status:
Inhabited
(
2384
)
Location:
Alpha Quadrant
Trebus
was an
inhabited
planet
near the
Federation-Cardassian border
, in what became the
Demilitarized Zone
. (
VOY
: "
Tattoo
";
PRO
: "
Observer's Paradox
")
History
It was the site of an ex-
Federation
colony
populated by
the descendants
of the
Ometepe
Rubber Tree
and
Nicarao
peoples of
Earth
, who moved to this world a few centuries before the
24th century
. (
VOY
: "
Tattoo
";
PRO
: "
Observer's Paradox
")
Chakotay
was
born
on Trebus in
2329
, at which time the planet had been settled for several
generations
. As of
2384
, he was the only resident of the colony in
Starfleet
. (
PRO
: "
Observer's Paradox
")
Kolopak
died defending the colony from the
Cardassians
.  (
VOY
: "
Tattoo
")
Inhabitants
Trebus inhabitants
Appendices
Background information
The naming of Chakotay's homeworld as Trebus was first given in the novel
Pathways
, before being confirmed on screen in "
Observer's Paradox
". Some other novels have identified Chakotay's homeworld as being
Dorvan V
.
The planet might have featured
forests
and
deer
, as
Chakotay
mentioned refusing to
hunt
them with his father in "
Waking Moments
".

at

, the wiki for licensed

,
Alpha Quadrant
.

24th century
, this planet's system was in

. This system was near the

.

starbase
as well as a

in

on the far side of the world, where, in mid-

, there were at least three

-class

protecting the base. (

When the

laid

around the core of

in

, this planet's system was within the blast radius of one of the mines. (

.

. When his brother

. (

======================================================================
PAGE: Triacus
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Triacus
======================================================================

Triacus
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Non-aligned
Status:
Habitable
(
2369
)
Native species:
Triacus marauders
(
extinct
)
Location:
Beta Quadrant
Triacus
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Beta Quadrant
. This
Class M
planet was located in the vicinity of the planets
Ariannus
and
Marcos XII
, the
Epsilon Indi
system
, and
Starbase 4
.
The
legend
of Triacus stated that the planet was the
homeworld
for a band of
marauders
who were making constant war throughout the system of
Epsilon Indi
. After many
centuries
, these marauders were defeated by the very
individuals
who they preyed upon. The legend also mentioned that a powerful
evil
presence remained on Triacus, awaiting a catalyst to revive it, allowing it to spread its evil across
the galaxy
. This power was personified by
Gorgan
, the last of the planet's marauders.
In
2268
, Triacus was the site of a
scientific colony
assembled by the
Starnes Exploration Party
, lead by
Professor
Starnes
. On
stardate
5038.3, one of the
archeologists
, Professor
Wilkins
, accidentally released Gorgan during an
excavation
of a
cave
near their encampment. It was noted that the destruction of whatever
civilization
that might have been there was produced by some natural catastrophe.
Gorgan influenced the
children
of the expedition and caused their
parents
to commit
suicide
. Gorgan made a further attempt, and was initially successful, to escape from Triacus aboard the
USS
Enterprise
to spread its evil on to Marcos XII. (
TOS
: "
And the Children Shall Lead
")
In the
24th century
,
Lieutenant Commander
Alexander Courage
, a
crewmember
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
, was born on this non-
Federation
planet. In
2369
, his planet of origin was listed on the chart "USS Enterprise Personnel Database", which was displayed on a
PADD
accessed by
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
. (
TNG
: "
The Chase
",
okudagram
)
Appendices
Background information
In the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 417, Triacus was classified as a
M-class
planet.
The quadrant of origin for this planet was inferred from it being near Starbase 4. It was said to be the "closer station" in the episode "And the Children Shall Lead". This base's position in the Beta Quadrant was labeled on a star chart from the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
Apocrypha
In the
Star Trek Maps
, Triacus was the
third
planet in the Epsilon Indi system.
In
FASA
's
The Federation
sourcebook, Triacus was the
ninth
planet in the
Alpha Lyrae
system.
External links
Triacus
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Planets in TOS and TOS Remastered
at
Ex Astris Scientia

======================================================================
PAGE: Trialas IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Trialas_IV
======================================================================

Trialas IV
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2154
)
Location:
Trialas system
Trialas IV
was the
fourth
planet
in the
Trialas
system
. This was a
Class M
planet
near the
Borderland
.
History
When
Doctor
Arik Soong
stole
Augment
embryos
from
Cold Station 12
in the
2130s
, he fled to Trialas IV, where he raised the Augments until he was captured by
Earth
forces ten years later.
The Bird of Prey in orbit of Trialas IV
The Augments raised themselves until
May
2154
, when
Malik
led them in commandeering a
Klingon Bird-of-Prey
. They all left the planet, except for
Udar
, who was deemed inferior by the others. A few days later,
Enterprise
NX-01
visited the planet to understand more about Soong and the Augments. When the ship left, Udar went with it. (
ENT
: "
Borderland
", "
Cold Station 12
")
Background information
The name of this planet was not specified in the episodes (the script of "Borderland" simply referred to it as "a Class-M planet"). However, the planet's name was given in the final draft script of "Cold Station 12". In the same script, Soong's compound on the planet's surface, in 2154, was described thus; "
It's a large, single-story structure that resembles a futuristic Quonset hut, nestled in a small canyon with exotic alien flora now starting to overrun the compound.
"
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 417 identified this planet as Trialas IV.
According to
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Federation Historical Highlights, 2161-2385"), the Trialas system was located in the
Beta Quadrant
.
The scene depicting the
Enterprise
in orbit of Trialas IV was a reuse of
Qo'noS
from
ENT
: "
Broken Bow
".

======================================================================
PAGE: Triannon (planet)
CATEGORIES: Planets

======================================================================

Triannon
Type:
Planet
Native species:
Triannons
Location:
Muratas Star Cluster
Delphic Expanse
Beta Quadrant
For the native species of this planet, please see
Triannon
.
Triannon
was an
inhabited
planet
located in the
Muratas Star Cluster
in the
Delphic Expanse
; it was home to the
Triannon
people. It was located 6.3
light years
from the
12th Sphere
.
Remains of Triannon structures
From the
2050s
, Triannon was wracked by a
religious
war
. This war finally ended in
March
2153
, but at a terrible cost. Both sides were decimated, all the major cities were destroyed. Millions of Triannons were killed. (
ENT
: "
Chosen Realm
")
A non-devastated Triannon from a deleted scene
In the final draft script of "Chosen Realm", the
devastated surface of Triannon
was referred to as the planet's
capital city
.
A
deleted scene
contained a picture of the surface of Triannon before destruction hit.
According to
StarTrek.com
and the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 418, Triannon was a
M-class
planet.
[1]
(X)

======================================================================
PAGE: Trillius Prime
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Trillius_Prime
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Trillius Prime
AKA:
Trill
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Diameter:
12986
km
Affiliation:
Trillian government
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Satellite(s):
One
moon
Native species:
Trill
Trill symbiont
Location:
Trill system
Alpha Quadrant
<90 light years from
Sol
Trillius Prime
, often shortened to
Trill
, was an
inhabited
class M
planet
, the
prime
planet in the
Trill
system
, located in the
Alpha Quadrant
. It had a single
moon
. This was the
homeworld
of two
sentient
species
who shared a
symbiotic
relationship: the
Trill
, a
warp-capable
humanoid
species
, and the
non-humanoid
Trill symbionts
. The planet was governed by the
Trillian government
, which enforced
Trill law
.
Mak'ala
was the
capital city
. (
DS9
: "
Invasive Procedures
", "
Equilibrium
";
DIS
: "
Forget Me Not
")
Astronomical data
Planetary data for Trill
Trill
orbited
its sun at a distance of 1.215
AU
, with an
orbital period
of 483
solar days
and a
Keplerian ratio
of 0.967. Its
rotation period
was 26.6
hours
long. The planet had a
radius
of 6,493
kilometers
and a surface area of five hundred million
square kilometers
. The estimated age of the planet was 4.682 billion years. Its
mass
was estimated to be 5.872
e24
×10
24
kilograms
, giving it a surface
gravity
of 0.98
g
.
The planet's
atmosphere
was composed of 76% molecular
nitrogen
, 21% molecular
oxygen
, 0.8%
argon
, 0.5%
water vapor
, and 0.3%
carbon dioxide
, with trace amounts of
methane
. The surface had an
atmospheric pressure
of 1.012
bar
and a mean
temperature
of 297
Kelvin
. (
DIS
: "
Jinaal
")
Environment
Forest near the Caves of Mak'ala (3189)
Natural features on Trillius Prime included
forests
,
lakes
,
canyons
, and
caves
. The planet's
oceans
had a
purple
tint when viewed from
space
. (
DS9
: "
Past Tense, Part I
", "
Equilibrium
";
DIS
: "
Forget Me Not
", "
Jinaal
")
Lifeforms
native to Trillius Prime included the
itronok
and the
ina raptor
, as well as
birds
and
flying fish
. (
DIS
: "
Forget Me Not
", "
Jinaal
")
Geographical features
Caves of Mak'ala
Tenaran Ice Cliffs
Points of interest
Hoobishan Baths
Symbiosis Commission
Trill Science Ministry
Trill Polytech
Animals
Itronok
Ina Raptor
History
Trill in 2371
The symbionts' ancestral home was in the underground
caves
, including the
Caves of Mak'ala
. The Trill had information on joinings as far back as the
12th century
. (
DIS
: "
Forget Me Not
")
Trillius Prime was one of the regular destinations of the
Earth Cargo Service
ships in the
22nd century
.
Travis Mayweather
visited this planet in the
sixth grade
. It took three years for the
ECS
Horizon
to get there. (
ENT
: "
Broken Bow
")
Travis Mayweather
was born in
2126
(
ENT
: "
Broken Bow
", "
Fortunate Son
") and was fifteen in
2141
(
ENT
: "
Bound
"). Assuming that the education system of the 22nd Century is largely the same as the 21st Century, and a sixth grader is normally eleven years old, he would have visited this planet in
2137
.
In
2367
, the
USS
Enterprise
-D
had to contact the Trill homeworld to send a replacement host for an injured
ambassador
,
Odan
. (
TNG
: "
The Host
")
In
2371
, the
USS
Defiant
traveled to Trill when
Jadzia Dax
's life was endangered by suppressed
memories
of her previous
host
,
Joran Belar
. (
DS9
: "
Equilibrium
")
In
2375
, the
USS
Destiny
was en route to Trill with the
Dax symbiont
, which had been injured, when the symbiont took a turn for the worse. As a result, it had to be joined with
Ezri Tigan
, the only Trill on the
Destiny
. (
DS9
: "
Shadows and Symbols
")
Jack Crusher
, under his
alias
"John Carson", was charged with disorderly
intoxication
on Trill in the late
24th century
. (
PIC
: "
Disengage
")
Advertisement for a beach on Trill
In
2402
,
Deanna Troi
and
William T. Riker
considered taking a
beach
vacation
on Trill. (
PIC
: "
The Last Generation
")
In
3069
,
The Burn
decimated the Trill
population
, resulting in the planet becoming more insular in an attempt to stave off
extinction
. Despite this,
a century later
there were still not enough viable hosts for the remaining symbionts. 
Trill was among the Federation members that chose to secede during this period.
It is unclear when the Trill first became a member of the Federation; its status in the 24th century is ambiguous.
In
3189
, the time-displaced
USS
Discovery
traveled to Trill to help
Adira Tal
access the
Tal symbiont
's memories. (
DIS
: "
Forget Me Not
") After the defeat of the
Emerald Chain
, Trill rejoined the United Federation of Planets. (
DIS
: "
That Hope Is You, Part 2
")
In the
32nd century
, Trill was a
Federation
member
. It was listed with other members on a video wall in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
Kids These Days
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet was within the blast radius of one of the mines. (
SA
: "
300th Night
", "
Rubincon
")
Stellar cartography
In
3196
, during the
Omega-47
crisis, the location of Trill (Trillius Prime) was depicted on star charts and readouts used across several locations on the
USS
Athena
to locate, monitor, and respond to the
Omega-47 mines
laid by the Venari Ral. (
SA
: "
300th Night
", "
Rubincon
")
In "
Two Days and Two Nights
", the
Enterprise
- an
NX-class
starship
- became the first
Earth
ship to travel 90 light years from its homeworld. Trillius Prime was visited by Humans prior to this event, and thus must be closer.
According to "
Horizon
", the
top speed
of the ECS
Horizon
was
warp 2
, which corresponded to 8 times the
speed of light
according to
the warp chart
seen in "
First Flight
". This suggests that Trillius Prime was located about 24 light years away from Earth.

Background information
In the series bible for
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine
, it was said that the underground of Trill and the planet's surface were where the Trill symbionts and humanoids lived, respectively. It was also stated that "an environmental disaster" led the two species to begin integrating with each other.
[1]
As there were two make-up configurations designed for the Trill,
Jadzia Dax
actress
Terry Farrell
once suggested that "the north of Trill" was the birthplace of the simpler-looking Trill whereas the planet's south was where the more elaborate physical design, involving spots, originated. (
Cinefantastique
, Vol. 23, No. 6, p. 21) While filming
DS9 Season 1
finale "
In the Hands of the Prophets
", Farrell also expressed hopes of seeing Trill in the
second season
of
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine
, saying, "
I'd like to go to the planet where I'm from.
" (
Cinefantastique
, Vol. 24, Nos. 3/4, p. 98.)
The name of the Trill homeworld was first established as "Trill" in the
second season
outing "
Invasive Procedures
". The planet makes its first appearance in the
third season
episode "
Equilibrium
". Portraying the planet in that episode proved highly expensive. Some of the Trill exteriors shown in "Equilibrium" were filmed on
location
in
Huntington Gardens
. "
I think there were a lot of interesting things about the Trill homeworld that you found out in the episode,
" commented
René Echevarria
, who wrote "Equilibrium"'s script. (
Captains' Logs Supplemental - The Unauthorized Guide to the New Trek Voyages
, pp. 84 & 85.)
For the
Star Trek: Discovery
episode "
Forget Me Not
", the exterior shots for Trill were filmed at the
Royal Botanical Gardens
rock garden in
Hamilton
,
Ontario
,
Canada
. The building, a visitor center for the garden, was designed by CS&P Architects.
[2]
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 33) and the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 421 identified the Trill homeworld as Trillius Prime and that it was governed by the Trill Symbiosis, admitted into the
Federation
in
2285
. As of
2370
, the planet was populated by 650 million Trill and 11 million symbionts, though the existence of symbionts was not widely known prior to
2367
. This connection was confirmed in official media on star charts seen in "
300th Night
".
According to
StarTrek.com
, Trillius Prime was located in the Alpha Quadrant.
[3]
(X)
Apocrypha
Worlds of Star Trek: Deep Space Nine, Volume Two
focuses on Trill and Bajor.
The
Star Trek: Federation Travel Guide
calls out local attractions including the Teneran Ice Cliffs (3/3 stars) in the southern hemisphere and the caves of Mak'ala (2/3 stars.) "The Interplanetary Bar and Grill" serving foods from hundreds of worlds is highlighted, noting "local cuisine failed to tempt our reviewers palate." The Selipsis is identified as Trill's largest and oldest beach resort. "Grow old in wisdom" is noted as a local key phrase to know.
Trill is depicted in several of the short stories in the
novel
anthology
The Lives of Dax
, the novel
Revenant
and the novella
Unjoined
.
External links
Trillius Prime
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Re-Used Planets in DS9
at
Ex Astris Scientia

======================================================================
PAGE: Triskelion
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Triskelion
======================================================================

Triskelion
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Providers
Status:
Habitable
(
2374
)
Native species:
Providers
Location:
M24 Alpha
system
Unnamed sector
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
~11.630
light years
from
Gamma system
Triskelion
was an
inhabited
planet
located in the
M24 Alpha
star system
. The planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
and was approximately 11.630
light years
from the
Gamma system
. This planet was located in what
Montgomery Scott
,
chief engineer
of the
USS
Enterprise
, described as an "empty
sector
". The planet was the
homeworld
of the
Providers
.
This world was ruled by a cabal of three powerful disembodied
brains
called the Providers. The Providers'
society
revolved around
betting
their
monetary
reserves (counted in a
currency
known as a
quatloo
) on various aspects of captured
slaves
' ("
thralls
") lives, including open combat with one another. The thralls lived in a compound on the lower
hemisphere
of the planet.
The
Federation
starship
Enterprise
under command of
Captain
James T. Kirk
was obligated to visit the planet to secure the release of several crew
kidnapped
by the Providers. Upon arrival, the
Enterprise
discovered numerous
species
enslaved on the
surface
as thralls, including their missing crew. Eventually, to free the thralls and his crewmates, Kirk entered into open combat against three
drill thralls
, betting not quatloos on his victory, but the condition of freedom for all if he was victorious, or enslavement of the
Enterprise
crew if he lost. He won, and the
Enterprise
left the system shortly afterward.
Another condition of the wager was that the Providers would educate the remaining thralls and allow the development of a free society on the planet. (
TOS
: "
The Gamesters of Triskelion
")
In
2328
, Triskelion was the home for the
Triskelion Orbital Station
. (
TNG-R
: "
Inheritance
")
In the
24th century
the
Cardassians
traded with Triskelion during the
Bajoran Occupation
, as did the
Bajorans
after they took control of
Deep Space 9
. (
DS9
: "
Necessary Evil
", "
Resurrection
")
An advertisement for Triskelion, depicting the Providers' symbol and a thrall, was present on
Starbase 80
in
2382
. (
LD
: "
Starbase 80?!
")
Inhabitants
See
:
Triskelion inhabitants
See also
Triskelion Prime
Appendices
Background information
The "Triskelion symbol" was designed by
Matt Jefferies
. (
Star Trek Sticker Book
, pg. 22) A
bottle
with a Triskelion emblem said to be used in several
Star Trek: Enterprise
episodes including "
Rajiin
" was sold off on the
It's A Wrap! sale and auction
on eBay.
[1]
(X)
Apocrypha
In the
Star Trek Maps
(p. 27), Triskelion was the
second
planet in the system. When the small and disparate
humanoid
colony
was given self-government, it had a
population
of 480.
The
novels
Strange New Worlds
and
Forged in Fire
depicted Triskelion joining intergalactic affairs, considering
Federation
membership, and signing a
nonaggression pact
with the
Romulan Star Empire
.
External links
Triskelion
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Planets in TOS and TOS Remastered
at
Ex Astris Scientia

(

)

was an
orbiting
space station
at the
planet
Triskelion
.
In
2328
, this was the point of embarkation for passengers traveling to
Talos IV
via commerical
transport
.
Ryan Adams
,
Wynsdey Adams
, Loren Bivens, and Svetlana Reeves-Stevens traveled on the
SS
Kogin
, and
Mike Echols
traveled on the
SS
Wisconsin
. (
TNG-R
: "
Inheritance
",
okudagram
)
This station was only mentioned in writing.

======================================================================
PAGE: Troyius
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Troyius
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Troyius
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Klingon Empire
(
2257
)
United Federation of Planets
(
2259
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Native species:
Troyians
Location:
Tellun
system
Federation-Klingon border
Beta Quadrant
Troyius
was an
inhabited
planet
, the outer of the two, the other being
Elas
, found within the
Tellun
system
. This planet was located in the
Beta Quadrant
and was the
homeworld
of the
Troyians
, a spacefaring
humanoid
species
.
History
In the mid-
23rd century
, this planet's system was located in a border area near the
Federation-Klingon border
.
In
2257
, during the first
Federation-Klingon War
, this planet's system was occupied by the
Klingons
. (
DIS
: "
The War Without, The War Within
")
The planet had rich deposits of
dilithium
("
radan
" in the Troyian
language
). In
2268
, both planets possessed the capability of mutual destruction. The
wedding
of the
Dohlman
of Elas,
Elaan
, to the
ruler of Troyius
preserved the
peace
. (
TOS
: "
Elaan of Troyius
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet was within the enclosed region. (
SA
: "
Rubincon
")
Stellar cartography
In
2256
, this planet was mentioned with the
star system
's name on the
star chart
"Alpha/Beta Quadrant Overview" in the
ready room
aboard the
USS
Discovery
. (
DIS
: "
Magic to Make the Sanest Man Go Mad
")
In
2258
, the planet's location was labeled on a star chart seen on the
main viewscreen
of the
USS
Enterprise
. (
DIS
: "
Such Sweet Sorrow, Part 2
")
In
2259
, the location of this planet was labeled on a
stellar cartography
chart that was seen on the
USS
Enterprise
's
ready room
viewscreen
. This planet's symbol had a
blue
color
, indicating that it was affiliated with the
United Federation of Planets
. (
SNW
: "
Strange New Worlds
", "
Children of the Comet
", "
Spock Amok
")
The planet's location was labeled in a
Federation star chart
that was in
Fleet Admiral
Kirsten Clancy
's
office
at
Starfleet Headquarters
in
2399
and on the
bridge
of the
USS
Titan
-A
in
2401
. Troyius was in or near to
Federation space
. (
PIC
: "
Maps and Legends
", "
The Next Generation
", "
Disengage
")
In 2401, this planet's position was labeled on a
star chart
used by
Captain
William T. Riker
during his attempt at finding the last known location of the
SS
Eleos XII
. (
PIC
: "
The Next Generation
")
In
3196
, during the
All-Worlds Day
break, the location of "Tellun (Elas, Troyius)" was depicted on a star chart on the main viewscreen of the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
Ko'Zeine
")
Later that year, during the
Omega-47
crisis, the location of "Tellun (Elas, Troyius)" was depicted on star charts and readouts used across several locations on the USS
Athena
to locate, monitor, and respond to the Omega-47 mines laid by the Venari Ral. (
SA
: "
Rubincon
")
Appendices
Background information
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I"), Troyius was listed as a
Federation member
in
2378
.
The current location of Troyius in the Beta Quadrant was based on the most recent star charts seen in
Star Trek: Strange New Worlds
and
Star Trek: Section 31
.
Apocrypha
In
FASA
's
The Federation
RPG
sourcebook, Troyius was the
fifth
planet in the Tellun system.
The
Star Trek: The Original Series - Core Game Book
said it was the
fourth
planet in the system.
External links
Troyius
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Planets in TOS and TOS Remastered
at
Ex Astris Scientia

======================================================================
PAGE: Tulgana IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tulgana_IV
======================================================================

Tulgana IV
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Non-aligned
Status:
Habitable
(
2381
)
Satellite(s):
At least one
moon
Location:
Tulgana system
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Tulgana IV
was the
inhabited
fourth
planet
in the
Tulgana system
. It was
orbited
by at least one large
moon
. It was protected by an
ionic field
that restricted
communicators
and
transporters
.
Ensign
Brad Boimler
considered Tulgana IV to be "a bustling, exotic planet". There was a
neutrality pact
in place on the planet.
Travel to and from the planet was controlled by
Tulgana IV Space Traffic Control
.
In
2380
, Ensigns Brad Boimler and
Beckett Mariner
of the
USS
Cerritos
delivered
General
K'orin
to the
Federation Embassy
on Tulgana IV.
The city in which the embassy was located also had several "ethnic" districts that celebrated the cultures of different planets. These included "
Little Qo'noS
" (the
Klingon
district), "
Little Risa
" (the
Risian
district), and
Andoria Town
(an
Andorian
district).
Romulan
and
Ferengi
buildings were also present. (
LD
: "
Envoys
")
Later that year, the
USS
Titan
headed to Tulgana IV, where Boimler and his new shipmates were to escort
Commander
Deanna Troi
to the surface. (
LD
: "
No Small Parts
")
In
2381
, the
Cerritos
returned to Tulgana IV to update the Federation Consulate's
power systems
. Given the straightforward nature of the task, Captain Freeman elected to also fill some Starfleet
quotas
at the same time, such as operating a
recruitment booth
on the surface. (
LD
: "
Reflections
")
People
See
:
Tulgana IV inhabitants
External link
Tulgana IV
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Turkana IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Turkana_IV
======================================================================

Turkana IV
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Non-aligned
Status:
Habitable
(
2371
)
Location:
Turkana
system
Beta Quadrant
For the planet visited by the
USS
Cerritos
in
2380
, please see
Tulgana IV
.
Turkana IV
was the
inhabited
fourth
planet
in the
Turkana
system
. The planet was located in the
Beta Quadrant
and was home to a failed
Earth
colony
.
Turkana City
was one of the settlements on this planet.
Turkana IV colony
In the early
24th century
, a
Section 31
team was ordered to Turkana IV by
Control
. (
Star Trek: Section 31
)
A failed experiment
Rape gangs in the underground of Turkana IV
The planet's
government
began breaking down in the
2330s
.
Dozens
of
factions
developed, and
civil war
broke out. The Turkana government gave
police
powers to the two largest factions, the
Coalition
and the
Alliance
, but it was quickly overthrown by those cadres; and the planet severed relations with the
Federation
by
2352
, the two factions declaring the planet's independence.
Lawlessness became the norm, and
rape gangs
became a common threat. For some of the
citizens
,
drugs
became an escape from the
poverty
and
violence
that they had to face every day. Few citizens escaped the planet. One of those fortunate enough to do so was
Tasha Yar
, who left the planet at age fifteen, and later went on to become a
Starfleet
officer
. (
TNG
: "
The Naked Now
", "
Where No One Has Gone Before
", "
Symbiosis
", "
Legacy
")
Re-establishing contact
In
2361
, the
USS
Potemkin
attempted to contact the colony. The
vessel
was warned away with the threat that anyone
beaming
down to the
surface
would be killed.
Six
years
later, the
USS
Enterprise
-D
visited Turkana IV, in response to a
distress call
from the
freighter
Arcos
. The
Arcos
was lost, but
survivors
crash landed
on the planet in an
escape pod
. At that time, the surface structures had all been destroyed by years of
civil war
, and the planet's inhabitants lived in structures that extended up to three
kilometers
below the surface.
Proximity detectors
were used to separate the two factions, and both sides' bases were heavily defended in order to prevent all-out conflict. (
TNG
: "
Legacy
")
See also
Turkana IV inhabitants
Stellar Cartography
In
2371
, the
location
of Turkana IV was labeled in a
star chart
Data
and
Captain
Picard
studied in
stellar cartography
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
in
2371
. (
Star Trek Generations
,
okudagram
)
Appendices
Background information
This planet's quadrant of origin is inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
In the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 427 and at
StarTrek.com
, Turkana IV was an
M-class
planet.
[1]
(X)
Apocrypha
The
FASA
Star Trek: The Next Generation First Year Sourcebook
referred to this remote
mining
world as "Hokma V".
External links
Turkana IV
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Re-Used Planets in TNG
at
Ex Astris Scientia

======================================================================
PAGE: Twin Gates
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Twin_Gates
======================================================================

The
Twin Gates
were a location on the
planet
Kellerun
.
In the
32nd century
, the
Kellerun
rebelled against the occupation of
Breen
Primarch
Tahal
at the Twin Gates. Tahal had all those involved
put to death
by the
venom
of the
tau ceti
viper
, including all of
Rayner
's kinsmen. She spared Rayner himself, as she was impressed by his ferociousness and tenacity. (
DIS
: "
Erigah
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Ty'Gokor orbital facility
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ty'Gokor_orbital_facility
======================================================================

The Ty'Gokor orbital facility
The
Ty'Gokor orbital facility
was a type of
Klingon
space station
located in
orbit
of
Ty'Gokor
. At least two of these stations were in orbit of the
planetoid
in
2373
. (
DS9
: "
Apocalypse Rising
")
Appendices
Background information
While the script mentions several "
defense installations, ship docks and weapons platforms
" in the scene, these "armored space stations" (as described in the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
) were the only installations to appear in the episode.
Studio model
The physical
studio model
at its 2006 auction…
…and its latter-day CGI counterpart
The physical model measured 39×26×24 inches and was designed and
kitbashed
by
Anthony Fredrickson
.
[1]
(X)
Michael Okuda
, who assisted, has noted, "
I thought the crowning stroke of ingenuity on this model was that we stuffed the interior with strings of white Christmas tree lights. We didn't have time to mount them properly or even to attempt to arrange them inside the model, but in this case, the random stuffing of lights worked well, I thought.
"
[2]
(X)
Years later colleague and childhood friend
Doug Drexler
added, "
The sewage strainer station! There were odds and ends, and Anthony was great at that. From time to time we'd do one together, we used to do that when we were 13! That was the thing he was best known for, being able to put together a bunker, or the
Planitia model
.
" (
Star Trek: The Official Starships Collection
, issue SP29, p. 11)
The Ty'Gokor station
studio model
appearing in the
40 Years of Star Trek: The Collection
auction as
Lot 482
(X)
, estimated at US$2,000-$3,000, was eventually sold on 6 October 2006 with a winning bid of US$7,000 ($8,400 including buyer's premium). However, the model re-appeared in a later
Christie's auction
, this time selling  on 30 May 2007 for US$5,500 ($6,600 including premium). It is likely the first sale was not completed. The second has been confirmed by the buyer, American collector Anthony Sforza.
[3]
(X)
CGI model
Considering the studio model's one-time only appearance in the live-action
franchise
(and therefore its subsequent and spontaneous kitbash nature), it was unsurprising that no digital counterpart was ever contemplated for production purposes afterwards.
The apocryphal fourth lower arm as seen on the display model of the
Official Starships Collection
Nonetheless, when the station was slated for its own entry in the British
Star Trek: The Official Starships Collection
partwork
publication, a
CGI model
was required, both for illustration purposes as well as to serve as the template for the collection's accompanying
display model
. Constructed in
LightWave 3D
– the software of choice of partwork publisher
Eaglemoss Collections
– , the magazine/model combo outing was released into the collection in March 2022. Modeler
Ed Giddings
however, took some artistic license when he switched the elaborate antenna array from the top (where it was located on the physical studio model) to the bottom of his digital recreation.
[4]
More egregious was the addition of a fourth, lower arm at the rear of the CGI model, which had never been there on the production-used studio model (as was demonstrated on the photographs Sforza took of the model after he received his auction winning
[5]
(X)
), making the digital counterpart apocryphal.
Further reading
Star Trek: The Official Starships Collection
, Special Issue 29, March 2022

PAGE: Tycho IV
CATEGORIES: Planets

======================================================================

Tycho IV
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Non-aligned
Status:
Habitable
(
2369
)
Native species:
Dikironium cloud creature
Location:
Tycho system
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
~1,000
light years
from
Argus X
Tycho IV
was the
inhabited
fourth
planet
of the
Tycho
system
. This
Class M
planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
and was a 1,000
light years
from
Argus X
. This was the
homeworld
of the sentient
dikironium cloud creature
.
Tycho IV was initially visited by the
Federation
starship
USS
Farragut
in
2257
. During the
mission
, the
Farragut
crew encountered the planet's native dikironium cloud creature, an encounter that ultimately cost the lives of two hundred men, over half the crew, including the ship's
commanding officer
Captain
Garrovick
.
Lieutenant
James T. Kirk
experienced
survivor guilt
at being one of the lucky half of
Farragut
's crew to leave Tycho alive.
Tycho IV after the antimatter explosion
While Kirk hunted the
creature
years
later after encountering it again on
Argus X
as captain of the
USS
Enterprise
, he followed up on a hunch which led him to Tycho IV. The creature was preparing to reproduce by fission, into thousands, and had to be stopped. Assisted by
Ensign
David Garrovick
, the
son
of his former captain, he
beamed
down to Tycho IV with an
antimatter
bomb
, which destroyed the creature,
cratered
the planet, and blasted half its
atmosphere
into
space
. (
TOS
: "
Obsession
")
In the
24th century
,
Lieutenant Commander
Arlene Fukai
, a
crewmember
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
, was born on this non-
Federation
planet
. In
2369
, her planet of origin was listed on the chart "USS
Enterprise
Personnel Database", which was seen on
Jean-Luc Picard
's
PADD
. (
TNG
: "
The Chase
",
okudagram
)
In
2328
, this was the home for the
Tycho IV Spaceport
. (
TNG-R
: "
Inheritance
")
StarTrek.com
classified Tycho IV as an
M-class
planet.
[1]
(X)
External links
Tycho IV
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Planets in TOS and TOS Remastered
at
Ex Astris Scientia

Tychus
Class:
Class G
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2261
)
Satellite(s):
Tychus-A
(Moon A)
Tychus-B
(Moon B)
Tychus-C
(Moon D)
Location:
Tychus system
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Tychus
was the
uninhabited
Class G
third
planet
in the
Tychus
star system
. As of
2261
, this planet had three moons –
Tychus-A
,
Tychus-B
, and
Tychus-C
. (
SNW
: "
What Is Starfleet?
")
This planet was only mentioned in writing.
The alternate names for the three moons would seem to imply that, in the past, that Tychus had four moons, and that something, unspecified, happened to this fourth moon.

======================================================================
PAGE: Tyree (planet)
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tyree_(planet)
======================================================================

Tyree
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2375
)
Native species:
Desert brush
Location:
Unnamed star system
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Tyree
was a
planet
located in a
binary
star system
. The planet featured a
desert
and was enveloped by an
ionized
atmosphere
that interfered with
ship
sensors
.
In
2375
,
Benjamin Sisko
traveled to Tyree with his father
Joseph Sisko
, his son
Jake Sisko
, and
Ezri Dax
to find the
Orb of the Emissary
, after receiving a series of
visions
from the
Prophets
of
Bajor
; the
Orb
was meanwhile hidden on the planet. After unearthing the Orb, Sisko opened its casing and released another Prophet, one who, decades earlier, had inhabited
Sarah Sisko
, in order to guide her to
marry
Joseph Sisko and, in turn, give
birth
to Benjamin Sisko. This Prophet had been lying in wait within the Orb of the Emissary until released by the
Emissary of the Prophets
so that it could return to the
Bajoran wormhole
and destroy a
Pah-wraith
that had entered it. (
DS9
: "
Image in the Sand
", "
Shadows and Symbols
")
Appendices
Background information
Tyree
is also the name of Captain
Kirk
's friend from the planet
Neural
in the
Original Series
episode "
A Private Little War
". In the
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine Companion
, p. 598,
Ira Steven Behr
stated that the origin of the planet's name did not come from
Michael Witney
's character, but from
Richard Harris
' character of Captain Benjamin Tyreen in
Sam Peckinpah
's film
Major Dundee
.
Tyree seems likely to be proximate to
Earth
and more familiar among
Federation
citizens, since Captain Sisko casually mentions the planet, obviously expecting Jake and his father to know it. Indeed, the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 431 described Tyree as a planet in Federation
space
, also characterizing it as
M-class
.
External link
Tyree (planet)
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

, It was the

======================================================================
PAGE: Tyrus VIIA research station
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tyrus_VIIA_research_station
======================================================================

Tyrus VIIA research station
Type:
Research station
Affiliation:
Tyran
Location:
In
orbit
of
Tyrus VIIA
Tyran
system
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Status:
Active
(
2381
)
The
Tyrus VIIA research station
was a
Tyran
research station
located in
orbit
of the
moon
Tyrus VIIA
.
History
This station was used for testing a radically new and highly innovative
mining
technology
known as a
particle fountain
, the brainchild of Tyran
Doctor
Farallon
, who began work on the project in
2363
. (
TNG
: "
The Quality of Life
")
Specifications
The particle fountain was generated by the
station core
, which was fed by multiple
power grids
, building
particle flux
within a
confinement field
which contains the
particle stream
.
One of the key components of the station was its
main particle impeller
, as well as its numerous
field generators
. Problems with the
phase selectors
were the primary cause for the station not to be able to reach its full capacity.
To initiate the emergency shut down routine required the activation of the station's
ionic dampers
. (
TNG
: "
The Quality of Life
")
Interior design
Station core chamber
The station core chamber was the main operations center aboard the station. Aside from the station core itself, the chamber contained a
transporter pad
and
console
. The room was also equipped with
field emitters
and
Tucker tubes
.
For the interior, the
script
described that "
the station core is unfinished, with equipment consoles scattered about. In several places, the
walls
are cut through with holes which lead away to
access tunnels
deep within the station. A
transporter pad
and console are in one corner. In the center of the
floor
is a large, glowing circular core (similar to the
Enterprise
's
warp core
).
"
The "
lateral neon "blinky" units
" (aka "Tucker tubes") that appeared in the lab were reused from the
Regula I
lab in
Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan
. (
Star Trek: The Next Generation Companion
, 1st ed., p. 227)
The station's power grids were down the various
conduits
that attached to the core chamber. One of which was located behind four
bulkheads
, two hundred
meters
from the station core chamber, down
conduit
A2. The station also had its
plasma conduits
located in one of the station's
access tunnels
attached to the core chamber. (
TNG
: "
The Quality of Life
")
Appendices
Appearances
TNG
: "
The Quality of Life
"
LD
: "
A Few Badgeys More
"
Background information
The script described it simply as "
a small
space station
.
"
The name "Tyrus VIIA station" appeared in an
okudagram
seen at Data's science station on the
bridge
of the
Enterprise
. This graphic was put up for auction in 2018.
[1]
The particle fountain miniature was a new
model
, "
touched up with cheap and quick detail and lighting by computer.
" (
Star Trek: The Next Generation Companion
, 1st ed., p. 227)

PAGE: Tzenketh
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tzenketh
======================================================================

Tzenketh
AKA:
Tzenkethi homeworld
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Native species:
Tzenkethi
Location:
Tzenketh system
Alpha Quadrant
Tzenketh
, also known as the
Tzenkethi homeworld
, was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Tzenketh
system
of the
Alpha Quadrant
. This planet was the
homeworld
of the
Tzenkethi
, a
warp-capable
species
. (
DS9
: "
The Adversary
")
Elim Garak
once conducted a
mission
on Tzenketh while an
agent
in the
Obsidian Order
. During the operation he was trapped when a
room
's
walls
collapsed on him, causing an attack of
claustrophobia
. (
DS9
: "
By Inferno's Light
")
Appendices
Background information
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue. It was first mentioned in "By Inferno's Light". In "The Adversary", it was referred to as the Tzenkethi homeworld.
According to the
script
for "By Inferno's Light", the pronunciation for this planet's name was "TSEN-keth".
[1]
Benjamin Sisko states in "The Adversary" that the Tzenkethi
leader
was called "the
Autarch
", suggesting that the Tzenkethi
government
was an
autocracy
.
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 36) and
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Stellar Cartography", p. 11),  it was suggested through inference, with the Tzenketh system being identified as the capital system, that Tzenketh was the
capital planet
of the Tzenkethi Coalition (
β
). According to the
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("The Dominion War: Strategy and Battles, 2373-75"), the Tzenkethi Coalition was neutral in the
Dominion War
.
Apocrypha
In the MMO
Star Trek Online
, Tzenketh is present in the Alpha Quadrant, but not currently accessible.
Season 12: Reckoning
introduces its dominant species, the Tzenkethi, as a new adversary.
External link
Ab-Tzenketh
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
