======================================================================
PAGE: Ukeck
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ukeck
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Ukeck
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Venari Ral Empire
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Location:
Ukeck system
Alpha Quadrant
Ukeck
was an
inhabited
class M
planet
in the
Ukeck system
. It was located in the
Alpha Quadrant
outside
Federation space
, over thirty hours' travel by
warp
from
Betazed
. The planet was a center for
trade
, particularly of
information
, though it was also rife with
criminal
activity and
violence
. (
SA
: "
300th Night
")
Astronomical data
Planetary data for Ukeck
Ukeck
orbited
at a distance of 2.034
astronomical units
, with a period of 593
sols
. It rotated once every 26.7 hours. The planet had a
radius
of 8024
kilometers
and an estimated
mass
of 9.23
e24
×10
24
kilograms
, giving it a surface
gravity
of 1.110
g
. Its age was estimated at 4.87 billion
solar years
. The surface was 57.5% land, with an
atmospheric pressure
of 0.975
bar
and a mean
temperature
of 304
kelvin
. (
SA
: "
300th Night
")
History
In
3196
,
Anisha Mir
traveled to Ukeck on the
cargo cruiser
Hemshir
. She sent an
encrypted
message to her
son
Caleb
, telling him to look for her at the
Ukeck Market
. At the time,
Starfleet
had declared a
level 4 threat
on the planet, as they had intelligence of an imminent
Venari Ral
invasion.
After receiving his
mother
's message, Caleb took an unauthorized
shuttle
from the
USS
Athena
, accompanied by fellow
Starfleet Academy
cadets
SAM
,
Genesis Lythe
, and
Darem Reymi
. Utilizing a
transwarp tunnel
, they arrived on Ukeck in under fifteen minutes. Caleb reunited with Anisha, shortly before the
Venari Ral
annexed
the planet and gave all
non-citizens
one hour to depart. The Venari Ral had also located the cadets' shuttle and captured SAM, Lythe, and Reymi, planning to publicly
execute
them as Federation
spies
. Caleb and Anisha intervened to save their lives, but Anisha was shot in the ensuing firefight.
Meanwhile,
Captain
Nahla Ake
had taken the
Athena
to Ukeck in pursuit of her
students
. As the Venari Ral had established an orbital
jamming field
to block
transporters
, she took the ship deep into the
atmosphere
to directly above the Ukeck Market, allowing her to beam the cadets and Anisha aboard. The
Athena
then fled back toward Federation space, pursued by three
Venari Ral ships
. (
SA
: "
300th Night
")
People
See
:
Ukeck inhabitants

PAGE: Ultima Thule
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ultima_Thule
======================================================================

Ultima Thule
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Location:
Ultimate Thule
system
Alpha Quadrant
Ultima Thule
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Ultima Thule
system
,
Alpha Quadrant
. This planet was the location of a
dolamide
purification plant
. Ultima Thule was the last stop on the route taken by
Valerian
traders
supplying weapons-grade dolamide to the
Cardassians
during the
Occupation of Bajor
.
The
Valerian transport
Sherval Das
visited the Ultima Thule station in
2369
a
week
prior to docking at
Deep Space 9
, suggesting that they were carrying dolamide. (
DS9
: "
Dramatis Personae
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to the
script
, the pronunciation for Ultima Thule was "ULL-tih-muh THOOL".
Ultima Thule was a
Latin
term that in the classic world denoted a place beyond the borders of the known world, typically in the far north as "Thule" (not to be confused with
Thule
) denoted a northernmost location of uncertain identification. It was also a
nickname
for the
Kuiper belt
object
486958 Arrokoth
.
External links
Ultima Thule
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Ultima Thule
at
Wikipedia

, 35 colonists were hospitalized after eating

from public

on this planet.

was believed responsible. (

======================================================================
PAGE: Unicomplex
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Unicomplex
======================================================================

Unicomplex
Affiliation:
Borg Collective
Status:
Destroyed
(
2378
)
Crew:
Trillions of drones
The
Unicomplex
was a vast
Borg
complex composed of thousands of connected structures and hubs spanning at least 600 kilometers, that housed
hundreds of
Borg cubes
and trillions of
drones
. (
VOY
: "
Dark Frontier
")
It was considered, by the independent drones, to be the home of the
Borg Queen
. (
VOY
: "
Unimatrix Zero
")  This complex is likely the closest thing that the Borg have ever had to a physical capital or headquarters.
The Unicomplex came under attack in
2375
, when
Kathryn Janeway
,
Tom Paris
,
Tuvok
, and
The Doctor
took the
Delta Flyer
to rescue
Seven of Nine
, who was blackmailed into leaving the crew of the
USS
Voyager
. (
VOY
: "
Dark Frontier
")
The Unicomplex was destroyed in
2378
, after the Borg Queen
assimilated
a
neurolytic pathogen
from the future
Admiral
Janeway, who had
traveled back in time
to bring
Voyager
home. (
VOY
: "
Endgame
")
Background information
It is unclear whether there was more than one Unicomplex. In
VOY
: "
Unimatrix Zero, Part II
", the Borg Drone
Axum
suggested that the independent drones attack the "Primary Unicomplex," implying that there are other, smaller unicomplexes.
When the Unicomplex was introduced in the script of "Dark Frontier", it was described thus: "
A vast infrastructure of interconnecting modules, grids, technology -- a 'metropolis' floating in space as far as the eye can see. Dozens of Borg cubes and spheres and other Geometric Shapes are flying around the perimeter. This is the sum total of Borg Perfection -- a purely technological world.
"
The construction of the Unicomplex had an unusual starting point. Visual Effects Supervisor
Mitch Suskin
explained, "
We started the Unicomplex with some building blocks that [Visual Effects Producer]
Dan Curry
laid out, which were literally built out of two blocks of wood and some Tinker Toys in his garage.
" (
Cinefantastique
, Vol. 31, No. 11, p. 33) Curry was intent on ensuring that the Unicomplex had an atypical shape that was nevertheless consistent with Borg design. (
Star Trek: The Magazine
Volume 1, Issue 1
, p. 56)
The job of modeling the Unicomplex with
CGI
, digitally reconstructing Dan Curry's maquette, was mostly achieved by
Foundation Imaging
staffer
Koji Kuramura
. (
Star Trek Monthly
issue 58
, p. 44) However, this task proved to be a difficult one. Remembered Mitch Suskin, "
The real thing, putting it in Borg space, making it look big enough, making it have enough detail, and rendering it, was nightmarish.
" (
Cinefantastique
, Vol. 31, No. 11, p. 33) Foundation Imaging's
Adam "Mojo" Lebowitz
commented, "
It was probably the biggest model, polygon-wise, memory-wise, that we've ever had to do for
Star Trek
. So it was a real trick to photograph it. They were really pushing our machines to the limit of what they could do. We had to break things up into multiple tasks. We would actually delete any part of the Unicomplex that wasn't seen by the camera from the screen to save memory [....] One of the animators added a little rail system that went through in one shot just to bring it to life. And we did some nice dark lighting on it to make it look pretty.
" (
Star Trek Monthly
issue 58
, p. 44)
Although lack of perspective in most exterior shots of spacecraft normally meant that moving the camera seemed as though the craft itself was instead moving, the multi-layered structure of the labyrinthine Unicomplex allowed for more freedom of the kinds of viewpoints that could be selected. "
With this huge Borg Unicomplex with all this perspective at contiguous levels,
" remarked Lebowitz, "
you could move the camera into a crane shot. We were able to do those kinds of shots in 'Dark Frontier' because of that model, and normally we can't. That was actually one of the first things I got excited about when we saw Dan [Curry]'s design. I knew we would be able to do some nice camera moves that we often can't do in space.
" (
Star Trek Monthly
issue 58
, pp. 44-45)

======================================================================
PAGE: Unit number
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Unit_number
======================================================================

Dixon Hill's office unit number
A
unit number
was a numerical designation for a particular unit in an
office
building
or
apartment
.
Gary Seven
worked out of 811
East 68th Street
,
apartment
unit number 12B. (
TOS
: "
Assignment: Earth
")
Dixon Hill
's
private investigator
office occupied unit 312 of the building it was located in. (
TNG
: "
The Big Goodbye
")
See also
Address

The following is a list of
unnamed
Spacedock
-type
stations
.
Spacedock #1
Another station in 2380
This
spacedock
was in
orbit
of a
planet
.
In 2380, two
California
-class
starships
- the
USS
Alhambra
and the USS
Cerritos
- and a third
ship
were in parked positions outside this station. A
shuttlecraft
from the
NCC-77567
was on approach to the station. (
LD
: "
Veritas
")

PAGE: Unnamed Starbase 1 type stations

List of
unnamed
Starbase 1
-type
space stations
.
Cornwell's starbase
Federation starbase
This
starbase
was an orbital docking station located in
orbit
around
a planet
.
This station was visited in
2256
by
Captain
Gabriel Lorca
of the
USS
Discovery
for a briefing with
flag officers
headed by
Admiral
Katrina Cornwell
. During the briefing, four Starfleet
ships
were either docked or docking at this station. (
DIS
: "
Choose Your Pain
")
The outpost was not named on screen; non-English
subtitles
on
Netflix
identify this outpost as the native-language equivalent of "
Starbase 28
". No corresponding subtitle appeared in the English language captioning on Netflix or
CBS All Access
, but the subtitles from the
DIS Season 1 Blu-ray
also label the facility as "Starbase 28".
Earth orbital stations
Earth orbital station
Several stations were located in
orbit
of
Earth
in
2257
. (
DIS
: "
Will You Take My Hand?
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Unnamed Starbase 375 type stations
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Unnamed_Starbase_375_type_stations
======================================================================

List of
unnamed
Starbase 375
-type
space stations
.
Gaila's prison starbase
Gaila's prison starbase
In late
2374
,
Gaila
was being held in
brig
at this
starbase
following his
arrest
on
Thalos VI
for
vagrancy
.
Quark
visited him and paid his
fine
.
At the time, the station was had
six
Miranda
-class
and
two
Excelsior
-class
starships
stationed nearby. (
DS9
: "
The Magnificent Ferengi
")
The script specified that the station was "not unlike
375
"; indeed, this base was footage of said starbase recycled from "
Behind the Lines
".
Lantaru sector research station
A research station in the Lantaru sector after an Omega molecule exploded
This
research station
was located in the
Lantaru sector
where it was used to conduct
classified
Federation
research
during the late
23rd century
, under
Doctor
Ketteract
. Ketteract and
his team
of
scientists
were working towards creation of a new inexhaustible source of
energy
.
When the team was able to synthesize a single
molecule
of the source, called
Omega
, it destabilized after a fraction of a
second
. The enormous amounts of energy released by the molecule breakdown damaged the research facility, killing all 127 scientists aboard. The
rescue teams
that attempted to reach the station found an unexpected secondary effect:
subspace ruptures
that extended out several
light years
, making it impassable to standard
warp drive
travel.
The accident and its consequences made
Starfleet
realize that only a few molecules could end warp travel throughout an entire quadrant or galaxy, which led them to enact the classified
Omega Directive
. (
VOY
: "
The Omega Directive
")

USS

and reported that they had not seen the

Nearest Cardassian base to McAllister C-5 Nebula
In
2369
, this
base
was the closest one to the
McAllister C-5 Nebula
.
For the
Cardassian
fleet
hidden in this nebula,
Captain
Edward Jellico
set the terms by which the fleet could leave the nebula. If they did not accept the terms, which they did by the way, they would be destroyed by
antimatter mines
. Each ship was to leave the nebula one by one and, as they did so, they were to eject their primary
phaser coil
. Then, they were to proceed to this base. (
TNG
: "
Chain Of Command, Part II
")
This base was only mentioned in writing.

, which were within

Station in the Portelo system

in the

. After the

returned to the

in

Space station #1
Space station #2

of

Scientific outpost raided by Gatherers
An outpost looted by Gatherers
A planet-based
scientific outpost
was the target of a
raid
by the
Gatherers
in
2366
. The
USS
Enterprise
-D
had
hailed
the two-person
outpost
for two days with no response. By the time
Commander
William T. Riker
, Dr.
Beverly Crusher
,
Data
and
Worf
beamed down to investigate, they had found the outpost's
two scientists
having suffered repeated
phaser
injuries, and the outposts equipment completely stripped, including their
reactor
.
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
considered the planet far enough away from the
Acamar system
that he was surprised the Gatherers would raid that deep into Federation territory, though Data confirmed the Gatherers had raided outposts in neighboring
sectors
. (
TNG
: "
The Vengeance Factor
")
Sector 30 outposts
In 2364, Starfleet assumed these seven
outposts
in
Sector 30
were destroyed by an unknown assailant. As they were located near the
Neutral Zone
border, it was initially thought that the
Romulans
were responsible. However, the Romulans had also lost
outposts
, and the
Borg
were later found to operate in a manner that explained how these and other Neutral Zone outposts were destroyed. (
TNG
: "
The Neutral Zone
", "
Q Who
")
These outposts were only mentioned in dialogue.

.

Star Trek Encyclopedia

We'd been told that the Klingon outpost was undefended. So when their warships

Nova Fleet
Nova Fleet station
(
LD
: "

Romulan
Neutral Zone Outposts
In 2364, a number of
Romulan outposts
bordering the Neutral Zone were attacked. Federation outposts in the area had also been attacked in exact same manner. Given the level of destruction, the Romulans realized the culprit could not be the Federation, and the the two
major powers
agreed to share
information
in solving the
mystery
. (
TNG
: "
The Neutral Zone
") Later, identical attacks were found to be committed by the
Borg
. (
TNG
: "
Q Who
")
These outposts were only mentioned in dialogue.
Romulan outposts attacked by Klingons
These
outposts
were located on the
Klingon-Romulan border
.
In
2372
, during a meeting,
Jadzia Dax
reported to the
senior staff
of

that the
Klingons
had attacked three more of these outposts. This was part of a larger agenda of the
Klingon Empire
to reassert themselves in the quadrant. (

These outposts were only mentioned in dialogue.

belonging to the

. The attack resulted in the death of all personnel assigned to the outpost and the

of

. (

Debris

in

kilometers
, composed of materials such as

.
There were
sixty Vostigye scientists
at the station, but no survivors were found. (

")
Other
Locarno's hangar

utilized

on

at some point after his

. (

Multiple realities

)
Warning!

, and thus may contain

.
Uranus
AKA:
Sol VII
Class:
Gas giant
Type:
Planet
Diameter:
51,118
kilometers
(31,770
miles
)
(
equatorial
diameter
)
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Intact
(
3196
)
Satellite(s):
Several
moons
and a
ring system
Location:
Sol system
Sector 001
Alpha Quadrant
Uranus
(also known as
Sol VII
) was the
seventh
planet
of the
Sol system
. The planet was located in
Sector 001
,
Alpha Quadrant
.
History
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet was within the enclosed region. (
SA
: "
300th Night
")
Stellar cartography
In an
alternate
1996
, the position and orbital path of Uranus in the Sol system were depicted on a poster in the office where
Rain Robinson
worked, at the
Griffith Observatory
.
(
VOY
: "
Future's End
")
In
2024
, the position and orbital path of Uranus were depicted on a map of the Solar System, which was seen at the
gala
held for the
Europa Mission
astronauts
. (
PIC
: "
Two of One
")
In
2267
, the position and orbital path of Uranus were illustrated on "Chart 14A: The Sol System". The chart was filed in the
USS
Enterprise
library computer
. The planet was identified by its name and by its
astronomical symbol
. It was scanned by the
probe
Nomad
in
auxiliary control
. (
TOS
: "
The Changeling
"
production art
)
The location of Sol on "The Explored Galaxy" star chart
"Chart 14A: The Sol System" showing the location of Uranus

Appendices
Uranus was named after the
Greek
god
who personified the
sky
and the
heavens
and fathered the
Titans
.
Diameter was from real-world sources.
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I"), this planet was identified as a charter member of the
United Federation of Planets
in
2161
.
The
studio model
,
USS
Trinculo
, spotted at a Planet Hollywood restaurant, was named after one of the
moons
of Uranus.

======================================================================
PAGE: Urna
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Urna
======================================================================

Urna
was a formerly
inhabited
planet
.
In the
23rd century
, Urna was an industrial seat, a hub for the enrichment of unstable
metals
. At the time, the
Federation
began sounding alarms that
toxicity
from these activities would thin the
atmosphere
, resulting in increased levels of
UVB radiation
that would trigger
mutations
in the native
lifeforms
.
Seeds
from Urna were stored aboard the Federation
seed vault
ship
USS
Tikhov
.
The Federation's warnings eventually came to pass, and Urna experienced an environmental collapse. By
3189
, it had been deserted for centuries. That year, a group of
Kili
stopped by the planet and ate
plants
from the surface that infected them with life-threatening
prions
. The
USS
Discovery
was able to obtain unmutated Urna seeds from the
Tikhov
that allowed the synthesis of an
antidote
. (
DIS
: "
Die Trying
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Ursa Nowhere
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ursa_Nowhere
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
"
Ursa Nowhere
" was what
Genesis Lythe
called the location where her
admiral
father
had been invited for a
symposium
during the
All-Worlds Day
break in early
3196
. (
SA
: "
Ko'Zeine
")
This location may be associated with the
Ursa Major
or
Ursa Minor
constellations.

A
vacation spot
was a
location
where
people
often chose to take
vacations
.
In
2371
while having a
discussion
,
Jake Sisko
told
Thomas Riker
that
Deep Space 9
was not exactly known as a vacation spot. (
DS9
: "
Defiant
")
See also
Tourist attraction

======================================================================
PAGE: Vacca VI
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Vacca_VI
======================================================================

Vacca VI
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Habitable
(
2370
)
Satellite(s):
One
moon
Native species:
Large roving
carnivores
Agricultural
plant
life
Location:
Vaccan
system
Cabral
sector
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Classified as
M-class
,
Vacca VI
was the
sixth
planet
in the
Vaccan
system
. The planet was located in the
Cabral sector
, an isolated
region
of
space
.
Planetary data
Vacca VI on a display in stellar cartography
The planet had a
rotation
of 22.59
hours
and one
moon
. The planet had a
water mass
of 77.23
TLM
and a
land mass
of 25.63 TLM.
Salt water
was 88.11 of
TWM
, while
fresh water
was 11.89 of TWM. The
polar region
was 24.68
square kilometers
.
The planet's
climate
was 5-30
degrees
. It was home to large roving
carnivores
. The
vegetation
was
agricultural
plant
life.
Desert
was 13.98 of TLM, while
forest
was 48.97 of TLM.
Mountains
covered 978 square kilometers. The
habitable sector
was 23.27 TLM. The natives were a "restless factor".
History
The previously uninhabited planet was settled in
2370
by a group of
Boraalans
who had fled their original
homeworld
of
Boraal II
, which was devastated by
atmospheric dissipation
. The move was conducted by
Dr.
Nikolai Rozhenko
and the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. Vacca VI was chosen by
Commander
Data
and Dr.
Beverly Crusher
over
Draygo IV
. The climate on the planet was slightly less hospitable than Draygo for the Boraalans, but resembled their former homeworld most accurately and was the safest location out of the available choices. (
TNG
: "
Homeward
")
According to the
script
, the pronunciation for Vacca was "VACK-uh".
[1]
The graphic "Class M Planet Search – 1126", with information on this planet and
Draygo IV
, was put up for auction in 2018. Only the lower portion of the graphic actually made it into the episode.
[2]
External links
Vacca VI
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Re-Used Planets in TNG
at
Ex Astris Scientia

======================================================================
PAGE: Vadia IX
CATEGORIES: Planets

======================================================================

Vadia IX
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
M'Kroon
Status:
Habitable
(
2261
)
Native species:
Q
Location:
Vadia
system
Alpha Quadrant
Vadia IX
was the
uninhabited
ninth
planet
in the
Vadia
star system
. It had a single
moon
. This was the long-forgotten
homeworld
of the
Q
, before their
evolution
into
non-corporeal
beings. Ancient remnants of their advanced
civilization
remained on the surface into the
23rd century
, at which time the planet was controlled by the
M'Kroon
.
Mount Caleb
was a famous
mountain
on this planet.
In
2261
,
Doctor
Roger Korby
conducted an
archaeological dig
on Vadia IX, assisted by
Nurse
Christine Chapel
. They were noticed by
Trelane
, who proceeded to follow them to
Starbase 1
and disrupt their relationship. (
SNW
: "
Wedding Bell Blues
")
Later that year, crew from the
USS
Enterprise
assisted Korby in exploring an ancient site on the planet, which they would discover to be a temporally-shifted prison for the
Vezda
. (
SNW
: "
Through the Lens of Time
")

Vadris III

Vadris III
was the

third

in its

.
Q
described Vadris as a "charming little world". The
natives
believed that they were the only intelligent life in the
universe
. Q offered to take
Vash
there in
2369
, but she refused. (
DS9
: "

")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.

script
, the pronunciation for Vadris was "van-DRIS".

======================================================================
PAGE: Vagra II
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Vagra_II
======================================================================

Vagra II
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Barely habitable
(
2381
)
Native species:
Vagrans
(formerly)
Armus
Location:
Zed Lapis sector
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Vagra II
was the formerly
inhabited
second
planet
of its
star system
. This
Class M
planet was located in the
Zed  Lapis
sector
, a
spatial
region
in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
Prior to
2364
, little
information
on the planet was kept in the
library computer
, other than the fact of its existence.
Sensors
indicated no signs of
lifeforms
, virtually no
vegetation
, and the minimum
atmosphere
requirements for
humanoids
.
A "
race
of
Titans
" once brought out from within themselves all
evil
and negative attributes that had bound them to destructiveness. As a by-product of this procedure, the unwanted substance spread and coalesced into a dank and vile second
skin
,
Armus
, a "skin of evil". The race rejected this malevolent
sentient
substance and abandoned it on Vagra II.
In
2364
, Armus threatened the
crew
of a
USS
Enterprise
-D
shuttlecraft
that had
crash landed
on the
surface
. It also killed
Lieutenant
Natasha Yar
, who was part of the
away team
assigned to recover them. After the crew of the
Enterprise
-D escaped, they destroyed the shuttle
wreckage
to trap Armus on the planet. Vagra II was subsequently placed under
quarantine
. (
TNG
: "
Skin Of Evil
")
In
2367
,
Ishara Yar
learned from
Data
that her
sister
had died on Vagra II. (
TNG
: "
Legacy
")
Vagra II in 2381
In
2381
,
Ensign
Beckett Mariner
and
Brad Boimler
used a
submanifold casting stone
so as to send a prank call to Vagra II in order to taunt Armus. (
LD
: "
The Spy Humongous
")
Appendices
Background information
In the
script
for "Skin Of Evil", Vagra II was a planet in the
Zed Lapis system
and Vagra was pronounced as "VAY-GRA".
[1]
StarTrek.com
described Vagra II as a minimally
M-class
planet.
[2]
(X)
In the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 448, it was stated that the species which created Armus were native to Vagra II.
The shot of Vagra II from
orbit
was first used to represent
Aldea
in "
When The Bough Breaks
".
Apocrypha
In
John Vornholt
's
novel
Contamination
, Vagra II was the site where a
scientist
working aboard the
Enterprise
-D trapped a species of sub-microbe able to pass through the most advanced
biofilter
.
Planets of the UFP
presented the Vagra (
β
) system, identifying Vagra II as
Class H
.
External links
Vagra
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Vagra II
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Valakis
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Valakis
======================================================================

Valakis
Class:
M-class
Type:
Planet
Native species:
Valakians
Menk
Location:
Valakis system
Alpha Quadrant
Valakis
was an
inhabited
M-class
planet
in the
Valakis system
, a
star system
located in the
Alpha Quadrant
. This was the
homeworld
to two
sentient
humanoid
species, the
Valakians
and the less
technologically
advanced
Menk
.
Despite their differences, both physically and mentally as well as
linguistically
, the Valakians and the Menk coexisted peacefully. The Valakians provided land, food and, in some cases, employment for the Menk.
During the
22nd century
, a
genetic
mutation
inherent to Valakian physiology resulted in a planet-wide
epidemic
and the death of some twelve million people. While some strides had been made in combating the illness, a
cure
evaded Valakian
medical science
, causing them to seek help from other worlds. Launching numerous
pre-warp
spacecraft, the Valakians attempted to enlist the aid of more advanced
alien
species. By
2150
, Valakis had been visited by the
Ferengi
and the
M'klexa
.
As of
2151
, Valakis was still a pre-warp world, but had many
ships
and
satellites
in
orbit
. That year, the planet was visited by the
starship
Enterprise
NX-01
, after that ship responded to a Valakian
ship in distress
. Returning ailing Valakian
astronauts
to their planet, the crew of
Enterprise
assisted in the search for a cure, until
Denobulan
Dr.
Phlox
uncovered evidence indicating that the illness was a step in an
evolutionary
process that would result in the
extinction
of the Valakians and the rise of the Menk. (
ENT
: "
Dear Doctor
")
Appendices
Background information
In the final draft script of "Dear Doctor", Valakis, as seen from space, was referred to as "Earth-like." The planet was scripted to appear in a scene set in
Captain
Archer
's
ready room
, visible outside his window, but Valakis doesn't appear in the final version of that scene. One shot of
Enterprise
in orbit of Valakis was a reversed reuse of a shot of
Qo'noS
from "
Broken Bow
", another was a reuse of
Sluggo's adoptive planet
from "
Fight or Flight
".
The depiction of a Valakian city was scripted to be a "
matte shot of an alien metropolis, sleek and modern.
" The visual of the city was a
digital
matte painting
created by
Eric Chauvin
of
Black Pool Studios
. The sky and river were taken from real photos, the river and being Paris'
Seine
, with alien buildings influenced by the Parisian source material. (
Star Trek: The Magazine
Volume 3, Issue 12
, pp. 24 & 28,
[1]
(X)
) The same shot was later reused to represent a
futuristic city on Earth
in the
timestream
in "
Storm Front
".
A scene description of a night view of the Menk village on the surface of Valakis stated, "
A cluster of primitive wooden structures are built close together on various levels along a hillside. The lights of the modern Valakian city shine in the distance [....] The village is serene, lit by the glow of torches and cooking fires.
"
In the opinion of
Star Trek: Enterprise
co-creator and Executive Producer
Brannon Braga
, there may have been many more species on Valakis than are represented in "Dear Doctor". "
That was only one continent,
" he noted. "
God
only knows what else was going on on the rest of the planet.
" (
Star Trek: Communicator
issue 139
, p. 25)
Apocrypha
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
(pp. 53 and 60), Valakis was identified as the
sixth
planet in the Valakis star system. An alternate name, "Menk", might have indicated a shift in power by the late
24th century
. The book also described the planet as a
Federation member
, admitted in
2236
, following their achievement of
warp drive
that same year. It went on to list the population of Menk as 2.8 billion, and Valakians as 730,000. The official name for the government was the Commonwealth of Menk and Valakis, and the planet had no capital city. (The fact that there were Valakians in the late 24th century would imply that a cure was found for the epidemic. as Dr. Phlox estimated that by the 2350s the Valakians would have gone extinct.) Sometime between September 15 and October 1, 2151, the
NX-class
starship
Enterprise
visited this system.
External link
Valakis
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Valeo Beta V
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Valeo_Beta_V
======================================================================

Valeo Beta V
Class:
L
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2259
)
Location:
Coordinates 2755.9753
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
"
Just like
Andoria
!
"
–
Hemmer
,
2259
("
All Those Who Wander
")
Valeo Beta V
was the
fifth
planet
in its
star system
. This was a
class L
world outside
Federation space
, with no traces of
civilization
. (
SNW
: "
All Those Who Wander
")
Astronomical data
Planetary data for Valeo Beta V
Valeo Beta V was located at coordinates 2755.9753. Its
rotation
and
revolution periods
were 18.9 hours and 221.4
solar days
respectively. Its
mass
was estimated to be 5.03912 x 10
24
kilograms
and its age at 4.3 billion
solar years
.
The surface of the planet was
rocky
and covered by jagged pinnacles. Conditions were
snowy
and frigid, with a mean
surface temperature
of -41
degrees
Celsius
. The
atmosphere
was composed of 61% molecular
nitrogen
, 34%
carbon dioxide
, 3% molecular
oxygen
, 0.4%
argon
, 0.3%
nitrous oxide
, and trace amounts of
methane
,
carbon monoxide
, and
neon
. The upper atmosphere was rich in charged
nitrate
ions
that produced
ionic interference
, precluding long-range
subspace communications
. The planet experienced severe
ice storms
that could last for hours. (
SNW
: "
All Those Who Wander
")
History
The
Peregrine
derelict on Valeo Beta V
In
2259
, the
USS
Peregrine
made an unscheduled emergency landing on Valeo Beta V after
Gorn
hatched onboard. The ship sent out a
distress signal
prior to descent, which was cut off as it passed through the atmosphere. The surviving crew were able to lure the Gorn
hatchlings
outside the ship to protect the
civilian
passengers, but all succumbed to
hypothermia
or Gorn attacks in the process.
Starfleet
subsequently dispatched the
USS
Enterprise
on a
priority one
mission
to investigate the crash site and locate any survivors. As the ship was already on a priority one mission to deliver
vidium power cells
to
Deep Space Station K-7
, a
landing party
was sent to the planet on the
shuttles
Cervantes
and
Galileo
. Despite losses, the team was able to neutralize the Gorn threat, rescue the lone survivor
Oriana
, and reactivate the
Peregrine
's systems, allowing the
Enterprise
to tow it off the surface. (
SNW
: "
All Those Who Wander
")
External link
Valeo Beta V
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Valo I
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Valo_I
======================================================================

Valo I
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Satellite(s):
Valo Ic
and at least two other
moons
Location:
Valo
system
Alpha Quadrant
Valo I
was the
inhabited
first
planet
in the
Valo
system
,
Alpha Quadrant
. . This
M-class
planet had at least three
moons
.
History
In the
24th century
, this planet's system was on the outskirts of
Cardassian
territory
.
During the
Occupation of Bajor
in this
century
,
Bajorans
settled
refugee camps
on this planet.
In
2368
,
Bajoran Resistance
leader
Orta
maintained his hidden
base
on
Valo Ic
, the third moon of Valo I. Based on a tip from
Keeve Falor
, the
USS
Enterprise
-D
traveled to Valo I and made contact with Orta, who informed them that the Bajorans were not responsible for the
attack
on
Solarion IV
. (
TNG
: "
Ensign Ro
")
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of the
Cardassian Union
in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
In the
script
, the pronunciation for Valo was "VAH-lo".
[1]
External link
Valo I
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

Valo II
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Location:
Valo
system
Alpha Quadrant
Valo II
was the
inhabited
second
planet
in the
Valo
system
,
Alpha Quadrant
. It was a
M-class
world.
History
In the
24th century
, this planet was on the outskirts of
Cardassian
territory
.
In that
century
, this planet was settled by
Bajoran
refugees
displaced by the
Cardassian
annexation
of
Bajor
. The Bajorans lived in desperate
poverty
on the
surface
, struggling to meet even their basic needs.
Ro Laren
spent some of her formative
years
at the encampment on the
southern continent
, where she became acquainted with an unofficial local
leader
named
Keeve Falor
.
In
2368
, the
USS
Enterprise
-D
sought out Keeve concerning a possible threat to the
Federation
made by
resistance
leader
Orta
.
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
elected to provide humanitarian assistance to the camp after witnessing their conditions first-hand. (
TNG
: "
Ensign Ro
")
Inhabitants
See
:
Valo II inhabitants
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of the
Cardassian Union
in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
In the
script
, the pronunciation for Valo was "VAH-lo".
[1]
External link
Valo II
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

Valt Minor
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2368
)
Native species:
Valtese
Kriosian
(formerly)
Location:
Valt
system
Beta Quadrant
Valt Minor
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Valt
system
, in the
Beta Quadrant
. This planet was named in
honor
of
Valt
, and was the
homeworld
of the
Valtese
and
historically
, the former homeworld of the
Kriosians
.
The
planet
was the site of the
Temple of Akadar
, the historic seat of power for the empire ruled by Valt and
Krios
, the two
brothers
who were the progenitors of the Valtese and Kriosian
races
before their love for
Garuth
caused a rift that would persist until the
marriage
of
Alrik
of Valt and
Kamala
of Krios in
2368
. (
TNG
: "
The Perfect Mate
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.

.

.

======================================================================
PAGE: Vanden Prime
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Vanden_Prime
======================================================================

Vanden Prime
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Cardassian
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Location:
Vanden
system
Cardassian space
Alpha Quadrant
Vanden Prime
was the
prime
planet
in the
Vanden
system
,
Alpha Quadrant
. This planet was in
Cardassian space
.
History
In
2375
, the planet was used by
Damar
and his
Cardassian Rebellion
as their headquarters. (
DS9
: "
When It Rains...
")
The base, along with seventeen other rebel installations, was located and destroyed by the
Dominion
, weeks later. (
DS9
: "
The Dogs of War
")
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
episode "
300th Night
".
The name of this planet was not spoken in dialogue but was taken from the final draft script of "When It Rains...". In the first draft of the script and even in the final draft's list of sets, the planet was called "Avandi Prime".
[1]
The first draft teleplay for "When It Rains..." additionally established that this planet was located near the
Likarian asteroid belt
and that the Cardassian Rebellion's base was situated "in a
cavern
on the tip of the southern
continent
."

======================================================================
PAGE: Vandros IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Vandros_IV
======================================================================

Vandros IV
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Iconians
(
200,000 years ago
)
Status:
Habitable
(
2372
)
Location:
Gamma Quadrant
Iconian gateway on Vandros IV
Vandros IV
was the
fourth
planet
in its
star system
. This planet, located in the
Gamma Quadrant
on the fringe of
Dominion
space
, was inhabited by the
Iconians
200,000 years ago
.
In
2372
, Dominion
scientists
discovered an intact
Iconian gateway
in the ruins of an ancient Iconian
city
. The gateway was seized by a company of rogue
Jem'Hadar
, who hoped to use it to launch a
coup d'état
within the Dominion. The gateway was subsequently destroyed by a combined team of
Starfleet
officers
led by
Captain
Benjamin Sisko
, and
Jem'Hadar
soldiers
led by
First
Omet'iklan
. After killing his
Vorta
field supervisor
Weyoun 4
, Omet'iklan and his troops elected to stay behind on the planet to hunt down the remaining enemy Jem'Hadar. (
DS9
: "
To the Death
")
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
(pp. 71, 74), the planet Vandros IV was
M-class
. This planet was
annexed
by the Dominion in
2372
. The
primary
of the Vandros system was an
M-class star
.
External links
Vandros IV
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Re-Used Planets in DS9
at
Ex Astris Scientia

' life when they were
ambushed

======================================================================
PAGE: Varala
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Varala
======================================================================

Varala
Type:
Planet
Status:
Inhabited
(
22nd century
)
Native species:
Varalan
Volg
Varala
was an
inhabited
planet
and the
homeworld
of the
Varalan
civilization. The planet had a
northern
and
southern continent
.
The world had not been visited by the
United Federation of Planets
as of
2375
.
Laas
, one of the
Hundred Changelings
sent out by the
Founders
, ended up on Varala and assumed
humanoid
form in the
22nd century
. He once assumed the form of a
volg
and
migrated
with a herd to the southern
continent
.
When asked by
Kira Nerys
to describe Varala, Laas told her it was "just like any other planet that's overrun with humanoids.
Cities
and
farms
everywhere – other
lifeforms
displaced from their habitats". (
DS9
: "
Chimera
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to the
script
, the pronunciation for Varala was "vuh-RAW-luh".
According to the
Star Trek Star Charts
(p. 74), Varala was located in the Varala
star system
, in the
Gamma Quadrant
. This was a
binary
with a
G-class star
and a K-class star.
External link
Varala
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Vashti
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Vashti
======================================================================

Vashti
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2399
)
Satellite(s):
At least one
moon
Location:
Qiris sector
Beta Quadrant
Vashti
was an
inhabited
planet
located in a
binary star system
in the
Qiris sector
, within the
Beta Quadrant
. It had at least one
moon
, and was settled by
Humans
and
Romulans
. The
indigenous lifeforms
on the planet included
insects
. Locations included
Central Station
,
North Station
and
Southeast Station
, with
roads
and
waterways
.
History
Prior to
2385
, this planet was chosen as a
Romulan Relocation Hub
by the
Federation
, receiving
refugees
from the
Romulan Star Empire
in anticipation of
Romulus
going
supernova
. Admiral
Jean-Luc Picard
was familiar with the world and was well-known to many of its inhabitants, including members of the
Qowat Milat
, who assisted the Federation with relocating over 250,000 refugees. The
Fenris Rangers
also assisted with the purchase and installation of a secondhand Romulan
planetary defense system
consisting of a
security net
and
orbital killer drones
.
By
2399
, the inhabitants of Vashti suffered from
poverty
, degradation, and
ethnic strife
, which had not previously been present on the planet. Members of the Qowat Milat still resided on Vashti, many of them serving as an unofficial security force along major transport routes.
Central Station
on the planet served as the primary point of contact for interplanetary visitors. Vashti was also a hotbed of activity for members of the
Romulan Rebirth movement
, including former Senator
Tenqem Adrev
.
Retired Admiral Picard visited Vashti again during that year for the first time since 2385, reuniting with members of the Qowat Milat and securing
Elnor
's companionship as a
qalankhkai
bound to his quest to locate
Bruce Maddox
and locate and protect
Soji Asha
. (
PIC
: "
Absolute Candor
")
A Vashti
postcard
was displayed on the wall of
10 Forward Avenue
in
2401
. (
PIC
: "
The Star Gazer
") It was later included in a
holodeck
recreation of 10 Forward Avenue as well. (
PIC
: "
No Win Scenario
")
Appendices
Background information
Vashti was first described in the
first season
press kit as originally being a Human
colony
on the edge of "the old
Romulan Neutral Zone
".
[1]
Apocrypha
In the first
Star Trek: Picard
novel
The Last Best Hope
, the relocation of Romulan refugees to Vashti was depicted and often served as Admiral Picard's shining example of what successfully rendered aid could look like, among other efforts that were causing political friction with the
Federation Council
and
Starfleet Command
.
External link
Vashti (planet)
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Vega IX
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Vega_IX
======================================================================

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Vega IX
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Location:
Vega
system
Alpha Quadrant
~25
light years
from
Sol
You may also be looking for the
Vega Nine probe
.
Vega IX
was the
ninth
planet
in the
Vega
system
. Located in the
Alpha Quadrant
, this planet was approximately 25
light years
from
Sol
.
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet was within the blast radius of one of the mines. (
SA
: "
300th Night
")
In the
mirror universe
, Vega IX was home to a
Terran
colony
. It was at this colony where
Terran Empire
Captain
James T. Kirk
executed
five thousand
colonists
in his second action as
commanding officer
of the
ISS
Enterprise
.
(
TOS
: "
Mirror, Mirror
")
Personnel
See
:
Vega IX inhabitants
See also
Vega colony
Vegan system
Appendices
Background information
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
In "Two Days and Two Nights", the
NX-class
Enterprise
, set a milestone
twenty-five years
after Travis Mayweather was born on his
father
's
ship
while it was on the Vega-
Draylax
cargo run, by becoming the first Earth ship to travel ninety light years away from the
Sol system
. This would be consistent with the location of the real star Vega, which is approximately twenty-five light years from the
Sol system
.
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I"), the
Vega colony
, on Vega IX, was listed as a
Federation member
in
2378
.
External links
Vega IX
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Vega IX (mirror)
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Velara III
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Velara_III
======================================================================

Velara III
AKA:
Sand of Home
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Non-aligned
Status:
Intact
(
2364
)
Native species:
"
Microbrains
"
Location:
Near the
Pleiades Cluster
Alpha Quadrant
"
Welcome to Velara III. Remember it as it is now, because in a couple of decades you won't recognize the place.
"
–
Luisa Kim
,
2364
("
Home Soil
")
Velara III
was the
inhabited
third
planet
of its
star system
. This
Alpha Quadrant
planet was in the vicinity of the
Pleiades Cluster
.
In
2360
, after a
Federation
reconnaissance expedition certified the planet was lifeless,
Terraform Command
established the
Velara Base
on Velara III. This base was responsible for a multi-
decade
terraforming
project with the aim of establishing an
M class
environment on this planet.
The terraforming was, however, canceled in
2364
, when it was discovered that the process was killing
silicon-based lifeforms
living in a thin layer of
water
between a
sandy
surface
layer, or
topsoil
, and a layer of
rock
. These "
microbrains
" called their planet
Sand of Home
.
Following the discovery of the microbrains and the retaliatory
death
of
hydraulics specialist
Arthur Malencon
by these lifeforms, an agreement between the little
crystals
and
Captain
Picard
of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
placed Velara III under an indefinite
quarantine
with the hope of restoring communications with the microbrains in
three hundred years
. (
TNG
: "
Home Soil
")
Appendices
Background information
The
script
described Velara III as a "
yellow
-
ochre
, featureless planet."
[1]
In the
script
, Velara III was located in the
Velara
system.
Andrew Probert
produced a
matte painting
of Velara III, but this was eventually unused and replaced by a matte painting illustrated by
Syd Dutton
. (
Star Trek: The Magazine
Volume 2, Issue 12
, p. 46)
The quadrant of origin for this planet was inferred from its proximity to the Pleiades Cluster, which was located in the Alpha Quadrant.
External links
Velara
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Velara III
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Re-Used Planets in TNG
at
Ex Astris Scientia

======================================================================
PAGE: Velos VII
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Velos_VII
======================================================================

Velos VII
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Cardassian Union
Status:
Habitable
(
2369
)
Location:
Alpha Quadrant
Velos VII
was the formerly
inhabited
seventh
planet
in its
star system
.
In or before
2357
, a
Cardassian
internment camp
was established on this planet.
Bajoran Resistance
members
Surmak Ren
and
Dekon Elig
, mastermind of the
aphasia virus
, were imprisoned there. Dekon Elig was killed attempting to escape the camp on
stardate
39355.5
. The internment camp was closed on stardate
46302
. (
DS9
: "
Babel
")
The mention of Velos VII on Dekon Elig's death certificate
The mention of Velos VII on Surmak Ren's biographical file
This planet's quadrant of origin is inferred based on the position of the
Cardassian Union
in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 46) and
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Federation Historical Highlights, 2161-2385"), the Velos system was a
binary
system with a
K-class star
and a F-class star. According to a map of the
Cardassian Union
– prepared by an anonymous astrography clerk, from the
Cardassian Central Archives
, in
2364
– Velos was identified as a Cardassian system.

======================================================================
PAGE: Veloz Prime
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Veloz_Prime
======================================================================

Veloz Prime
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Cardassian Union
(
24th century
)
Maquis
(
2373
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Location:
Veloz
system
Alpha Quadrant
Veloz Prime
was the
inhabited
prime
planet
in the
Veloz
system
,
Alpha Quadrant
.
History
In the
24th century
, Veloz Prime was in
Cardassian
space
. This planet was colonized by the
Cardassian Union
.
Between
2368
and
2373
, this world's system was in the
Demilitarized Zone
.
In 2373, the
Maquis
, led by
Michael Eddington
, detonated three
stratospheric
torpedoes
laden with
cobalt diselenide
in Veloz Prime's
atmosphere
. The
nerve
agent
spread across the planet, forcing the Cardassians to evacuate. The Maquis settled the planet shortly after. Later that
year
, it was among the Maquis colonies destroyed by the Cardassian-
Dominion
forces. (
DS9
: "
For the Uniform
", "
By Inferno's Light
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet's system was within the potential blast radius of one of the mines. (
SA
: "
300th Night
", "
Rubincon
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
In the
script
, the pronunciation for Veloz was given as "VEL-loze".
[1]

======================================================================
PAGE: Vendikar
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Vendikar
======================================================================

Vendikar
AKA:
Eminiar III
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2328
)
Native species:
Vendikans
Location:
Eminiar
system
NGC 321
Unnamed minor quadrant
Alpha Quadrant
Vendikar
, aka
Eminiar III
, was the
inhabited
third
planet
in the
Eminiar
system
. This planet was located within an
unnamed minor quadrant
of the
Alpha Quadrant
and was the
colony
of
Eminiar VII
, the principal planet of the
star cluster
NGC 321
. (
TOS
: "
A Taste of Armageddon
")
Government
For over five hundred
years
, Vendikar was governed by a planetary
government
. This government was headed by the
Vendikar High Council
. There was a direct channel between this council and the
High Council of Eminiar
, which, by
2267
, hadn't been used for
centuries
. (
TOS
: "
A Taste of Armageddon
")
History
Vendikar was established centuries before the
23rd century
by the
spacefaring
Eminians
.
These Eminians, later identified as
Vendikans
, would enter into an
interplanetary war
sometime during the
18th century
. The conditions of this war were stipulated in a
treaty
signed by both worlds. This was a simulated war with
casualties
determined by
computer
projections, and the people willingly killing themselves in
disintegration stations
. Legitimate targets in the war included
vessels
orbiting the planet. The reneging of this treaty by either side could result in a real war.
In 2267, the
USS
Enterprise
visited the planet Eminiar VII. During the visit, when his ship and crew were threatened with destruction,
Captain
James T. Kirk
ended the war abruptly by destroying the computers.
Ambassador
Robert Fox
began work with the two planets to establish a lasting peace. (
TOS
: "
A Taste of Armageddon
")
In
2328
, this planet was home for the
Vendikar Spaceport
. (
TNG-R
: "
Inheritance
",
okudagram
)
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
.
External link
Vendikar
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Vendor Prime
CATEGORIES: Planets

======================================================================

Vendor Prime
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2269
)
Native species:
Vendorians
Location:
Vendor system
Beta Quadrant
Vendor
redirects here; for the occupation, please see
vendor
.
Vendor Prime
, or simply
Vendor
, was the
inhabited
prime
planet
in the
Vendor
system
. The planet was located in the
Beta Quadrant
and was the
homeworld
of the
Vendorians
, a
non-humanoid
species
.
Prior to
stardate
5143
, Vendor was
quarantined
due to their
shapeshifting
abilities and lifestyle of deceit.
Carter Winston
's
trading vessel
crash landed
on Vendor in
2264
. He eventually died there in
2265
, under the care of a Vendorian who later assumed
his identity
. (
TAS
: "
The Survivor
")
An individual known as "
Fen
of Vendor" was a known associate of the
Romulan
dissident
Lurak T'Luco
that was listed in the
Starfleet Criminal Record
. (
PIC
: "
Disengage
")
Appendices
Background information
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
This planet's quadrant of origin was inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
In the
novelization
of "
The Survivor
", the Vendorian homeworld was consistently referred to as "Vendoria" and it was said that Romulans frequently visited the planet, during a period of several
years
prior to
2269
.
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 66, "United Federation of Planets I") and
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Stellar Cartography", p. 32; "Federation Historical Highlights, 2161-2385"), the quarantine on Vendor was still in effect in
2378
.
External link
Vendor
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

.
Vendu
was an inhabited
planet
.
In
2380
,
Lieutenant
Barbara Brinson
and
Ensign

met on the planet and became romantically involved a month prior to their mission to
Mixtus III
. (

: "

")

Boimler may also have acquired his
neural parasite
on Vendu.

Vener VII
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
24th century
)

Vener VII
was the
seventh
planet
in its
star system
.
In the
24th century
,
Durg
did a job for
Quark
on this planet. Contrary to what Quark had told Durg, about everything being taken care of, the job had unexpected surprises which almost cost Durg his life. He was later paid for the job by Quark. (
DS9
: "

StarTrek.com
.

script
for the episode.

======================================================================
PAGE: Ventax II
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ventax_II
======================================================================

Ventax II
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2367
)
Native species:
Ventaxians
Location:
Ventax
system
Unnamed sector
Beta Quadrant
Ventax II
was the
inhabited
second
planet
in the
Ventax
system
. Located in a
sector
of the
Beta Quadrant
, this was the
homeworld
for the
Ventaxians
, a
humanoid
species
.
The planet had east and west
magnetic poles
.
The central
government
building
of the Ventaxians was called the
Atheneum
. In
2367
, the
head of state
for the planet was
Acost Jared
.
History
Before the
14th century
, the Ventaxians were highly industrialized, and caused destruction in the planet's
ecology
through various conflicts. In the year 1367, the Ventaxian leaders began pacifying the planet and cleaning the environment, using the
story
of
Ardra
to bring their people together in a common contract. After that, they were a peaceful
agrarian society
and effectively isolated itself from the rest of
the galaxy
. (
TNG
: "
Devil's Due
")
In
2260
, Ventax II was within the area of effect of a
quantum uncertainty field
. (
SNW
: "
Subspace Rhapsody
")
First contact
after
centuries
was made by a
Klingon
expedition in
2297
. Hired Klingon
craftsmen
went on to construct homes on Ventax II. Sometime after this, a
Federation
research station
was established on the planet to study their
culture
. In 2367, this station was headed by
Dr.
Howard Clark
.
In 2367, the planet fell into chaos again when, according to the Ardra story, a fabulous thousand-year period of
peace
and wealth would come to an end and the planet would be turned over to Ardra. A
con artist
took advantage of this
myth
and modern
technology
to present herself as Ardra and to bring the planet under control. She used technology to cause
earthquakes
, for example. Her true identity was successfully revealed by the
crew
of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. (
TNG
: "
Devil's Due
")
In the episode, many of the details for the precedent-setting case were omitted. In the
script
, it was stated that in the Ventaxian
year
931
AA
(after Ardra), the
craftsman
Akul K'Ton
in the township of
Tau Velor
filed a
charge
against the homeowner
Garv Etes
. The case was decided in favor of the Ventaxian.
[1]
Syd Dutton
discussed the
matte painting
of the Ventax II
surface
in the
VOY Season 4 DVD
special feature "The Art of Alien Worlds". Describing the place as "the
Aztec
city
of the future", he also explained how the shot contained footage of a nearby dam, including its top as the ramp on which people were fleeing. He described the shot as including a
temple
and
cement
surfaces. Live-action footage for the matte painting was indeed filmed at
Sepulveda Dam
.
This planet's class came from the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 455.
External link
Ventax II
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

. (
TNG

======================================================================
PAGE: Venus
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Venus
======================================================================

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Venus
AKA:
Sol II
Type:
Planet
Diameter:
12,104
kilometers
(7,522
miles
)
(
equatorial
diameter
)
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Intact
(
3196
)
Satellite(s):
None
Location:
Sol system
Sector 001
Alpha Quadrant
"
And there was a rather peculiar limerick being delivered by someone in the shuttlecraft bay. I'm not sure I understand it. 'There was a young lady from Venus, whose body was shaped like a…'
"
"
Captain to security! Come in!
"
"…Did I say something wrong?
"
–
Lt. Commander
Data
and
Captain
Picard
,
2364
("
The Naked Now
")
Venus
(also known as
Sol II
) was the
inhabited
second
planet
of the
Sol
system
. The planet was located in
Sector 001
,
Alpha Quadrant
.
History
Before the invention of the
telescope
,
Humans
discovered Venus in the night sky. This planet was explored by unmanned
NASA
spacecraft
in the twenty-two
years
that followed the launch of
Sputnik
in
1957
. As of
1953
, Venus was also featured in
science fiction
magazines
, e.g., in
Incredible Tales of Scientific Wonder
with a
story
called "
Venusian Chronicles
" written by
Hugh Campbell
. (
TNG
: "
Loud As A Whisper
"
okudagram
;
DS9
: "
Far Beyond the Stars
")
In
2254
, the
Talosians
tapped into the
USS
Enterprise
's computer database, which included information on
Mariner 2
. While not shown on screen, this NASA probe was the first to successfully fly by Venus in 1964.
In an
alternate timeline
in which the Federation never formed due to
Romulan
interference,
United Earth
had colonized Venus by the mid-
23rd century
.
(
SNW
: "
Tomorrow and Tomorrow and Tomorrow
")
During his early career in the
2340s
,
Chakotay
spent a couple of
months
on Venus to learn how to
pilot
a
shuttle
in atmospheric storms. After he had finished his training, he spent a
semester
learning to dodge
asteroids
in the
asteroid belt
. (
VOY
: "
Future's End, Part II
")
In
2364
, when the
USS
Enterprise
-D
crew
was infected with
polywater intoxication
, an
officer
serving in the
shuttlebay
delivered an inappropriate
limerick
over the comm to other crewmembers. ("
There was a young lady from Venus whose
body
was shaped like a
…") Hearing this,
Lieutenant Commander
Data
began to repeat the limerick, but was cut off by
Captain
Picard
before completing it. (
TNG
: "
The Naked Now
")
By
2371
, Venus had a number of
terraforming stations
on its surface. (
DS9
: "
Past Tense, Part I
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet was within the enclosed region. (
SA
: "
300th Night
")
In an
alternate timeline
,
Miles O'Brien
, while still in the
24th century
, noted that the terraforming stations on Venus were
gone
, following the creation of an alternate timeline created when
Benjamin Sisko
,
Jadzia Dax
, and
Julian Bashir
disrupted
history
after
transporting
to the
21st century
.
(
DS9
: "
Past Tense, Part I
")
Stellar cartography
In the late
19th century
, the
orbital
path of Venus was depicted on a
German
map of the inner system. (
Star Trek: Enterprise
opening credits
)
In
2024
, the position and orbital path of Venus were depicted on a map of the Solar System, which was seen at the
gala
held for the
Europa Mission
astronauts
. (
PIC
: "
Two of One
")
In
2254
, the orbit of Venus was depicted on a map of the inner system, which was stored in the
USS
Enterprise
library computer
. This was one of the records scanned by the
Talosians
. (
TOS
: "
The Cage
"
production art
)
In
2267
, the orbit of Venus was depicted on "Chart 14A: The Sol System", which was stored in the
Enterprise
library computer. The planet was identified by name and by its
astronomical symbol
. This chart was scanned by the
probe
Nomad
in
auxiliary control
. (
TOS
: "
The Changeling
"
production art
)
The orbit of Venus

•

•

(
Luna
) •

(
Deimos
) •

(
Io
;
Ganymede
;
Europa
) •

(
Mimas
;
Rhea
;
Titan
) •

•

(
Triton
) •
Pluto

Other regions
:
Mars Defense Perimeter
•
Asteroid belt
•

•

Appendices
See also
Venus drug
Background information
Venus was named after the
Roman
goddess
of
love
,
beauty
,
fertility
, and
sexuality
.
Diameter was from real-world sources.
In a filmed but
deleted
Kirk
voice-over which originally introduced
TOS
: "
Where No Man Has Gone Before
", Venus was mentioned by Kirk as one of multiple "specks of dust" while the
USS
Enterprise
was on a heading out of the
Milky Way Galaxy
. ("Where No Man Has Gone Before" (Rare and Unaired Alternate Version),
TOS Season 3 Blu-ray
special features)
In the final revised draft script of
TOS
: "
The Conscience of the King
", a performance of
Hamlet
by the
Karidian Company of Players
was referred to as having "decor carried out in a strange Venusian version of
castle
and grounds."
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 38; "United Federation of Planets I"), Venus was classified as an
N-class planet
. This planet was a charter member of the
United Federation of Planets
in
2161
. In the late
24th century
, a major orbital facility was the Aphrodite Terraforming Station.
Apocrypha
In the
Fifteenth UK Story Arc
, set in the late 2260s, a research depot on Venus lent the
USS
Enterprise
experimental repair
robots
.

at

, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

at
Wikipedia

Verdanis
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2269
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Verdanis
was an
inhabited
Class M
planet
that was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
. This planet was located ten
days
away from
Terra 10
and Terra 10's
star
Cepheus
. It was described by
James T. Kirk
as "
very beautiful, much like
Earth
".
In
2269
, the
USS
Enterprise
transplanted the
Terratin
colony
from the dying Terra 10 to a
fertile
and well
watered
plain
on Verdanis. (
TAS
: "
The Terratin Incident
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
StarTrek.com
classified Verdanis as an
M-class
planet.
[1]
(X)
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I", "United Federation of Planets II"), the Verdanis
system
was located in the Alpha Quadrant. The system's
primary
was classified as a
G-class star
.

at

, the wiki for licensed

======================================================================
PAGE: Verex III
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Verex_III
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Verex III
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Orion Syndicate
Status:
Intact
(
3196
)
Location:
Verex system
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Verex III
was the
inhabited
third
planet
in its
system
.
In the
22nd century
, this system was located in the
Borderland
. In that era, the
Orion Syndicate
maintained a processing station for
slave
trade on this planet .
In
2154
, nine crew members were abducted from
Enterprise
NX-01
and taken to Verex III to be auctioned off.
Captain
Archer
was able to gain access to the processing station by using an old
authorization code
provided by
Arik Soong
, and discovered a way to disable the
neurolytic restraints
with which all of the slaves had been outfitted. With Soong's help, Archer disabled all of the restraints, causing a mass escape attempt. In the confusion, Archer was successfully able to rescue his people and escape. (
ENT
: "
Borderland
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet was within the enclosed region. (
SA
: "
Rubincon
")
See also
Verex III visitors
Background information
In the final draft script of "Borderland", Verex III was described as "
a barren-looking world. In the shadows of a large crater, we see the lights of a settlement – the slave market.
" The script also referred to the planet as "a busy place" and described the slave market as "
dark, smoky and alive with flitting shadows... the market is broken into fenced-off holding pens with slaves awaiting processing. The entire operation has a ratty, fly-by-night feel... as if it's forced to move around a lot.
"
Concerning Verex III,
Manny Coto
once commented, "
The idea of a slave world and slave auctions I found just wonderfully fascinating.
" ("Before Her Time: Decommissioning
Enterprise
, Part Two: Memorable Voyages",
ENT Season 4 Blu-ray
special features)
According to
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Federation Historical Highlights, 2161-2385"), the Verex system was located in the
Beta Quadrant
. The system's
primary
was a
M-class star
. In the late
24th century
, it was an independent system.
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 456 classified Verex III as a
M-class
planet.

======================================================================
PAGE: Vergessen
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Vergessen
======================================================================

Vergessen
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2386
)
Location:
Hypatia system
Vergessen
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Hypatia system
. Its surface featured large
gaseous
plumes,
mountains
, and fairly large clusters of
plant
life surrounded by bodies of
liquid
. It was the location of the
Seven Domes
, which contained a facility controlled by
Bjayzl
where
ex-Borg
were dismembered for parts.
In
2386
,
Seven of Nine
found
Icheb
on Vergessen, but arrived too late to save his life. (
PIC
: "
Stardust City Rag
")
"Vergessen" is the German word for "to forget" or "forgotten". Correctly translated, it would be "Planet Oblivion".
External link
Vergessen
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Veridian I
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Veridian_I

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Veridian I
Class:
Non-
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2371
)
Satellite(s):
1
moon
Location:
Veridian
system
Beta Quadrant
Veridian I
was the
first
planet
of the
Veridian
system
, in the
Beta Quadrant
. The planet had one
moon
, and was not an
M-class
planet. The planet had a
reddish
hue from
space
. In
2371
, the position and
orbital
path of Veridian I was illustrated in a system map displayed in
Stellar cartography
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. (
Star Trek Generations
,
display graphic
)
In an
alternate timeline
in 2371,
Dr.
Tolian Soran
's plan to collapse the
Veridian sun
succeeded and the entire
Veridian system
, including Veridian I, was annihilated by the resultant
shock wave
.
(
Star Trek Generations
)
This planet's quadrant of origin is inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Discovery
episode "
An Obol for Charon
".

2
moons
and a
ring system

second

in the

, in the

. This
ringed
planet had two
moons
. It was not an
M-class
planet and had a
yellowish
hue from
space
. In
2371
, the position and
orbital

aboard the

.
In an
alternate timeline
in
2371
,

's plan to collapse the

sun
succeeded and the entire
Veridian system

shock wave

======================================================================
PAGE: Veridian III
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Veridian_III
======================================================================

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Veridian III
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2371
)
Satellite(s):
3
moons
Location:
Veridian
system
Beta Quadrant
Veridian III
was the
uninhabited
third
planet
of the
Veridian
system
, in the
Beta Quadrant
. Classified as
M-class
, the planet had three
moons
. Its
surface
contained
rocky
mountainous
regions
as well as lush vegetative areas. There were also several large bodies of
water
. Its
ionosphere
created enough interference that it was hard to penetrate by
sensors
. In
2371
, the position and
orbital
path of Veridian III was illustrated in a system map displayed in
stellar cartography
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
.
Dr.
Tolian Soran
used Veridian III as the final key to his plan to return to the
Nexus
. He established a small launching site on the planet sometime prior to 2371, needing to destroy the
Veridian
star to change the gravitational forces in the
sector
and alter the
course
of the Nexus to Veridian III. He obtained
trilithium
for his weapon from the
Duras sisters
, and began destroying other stars in order to alter the path of the
temporal energy
ribbon towards Veridian III. After discovering his plan aboard the
Amargosa observatory
, the
Enterprise
chased Soran to Veridian III, where
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
beamed down to negotiate with him.
In the meantime, the
Enterprise
was
attacked
by the Duras sisters. While they were killed when the
Enterprise
destroyed
their ship
with a
photon torpedo
, they inflicted enough damage to cause a
coolant leak
which led to a
warp core breach
. The
saucer section
was safely
separated
before the
stardrive section
exploded, but the
ion shock wave
from the blast left the saucer completely disabled and forced it into the
atmosphere
of Veridian III, where it eventually
crash landed
in one of the
jungle
regions.
Dr. Soran eventually succeeded in launching his trilithium weapon, and the resultant collapse of the Veridian star destroyed Veridian III, the
Enterprise
saucer section, and everything within the Veridian system. Fortunately, Picard was swept into the Nexus along with Soran, and enlisted the help of Captain
James T. Kirk
. The two captains returned to a few moments before Soran launched the weapon and prevented the launch, saving the Veridian system and Veridian III from destruction. Unfortunately, Kirk was killed in the attempt. Picard buried Kirk on one of the mountains in the area.
Three
Starfleet
ships, including the
USS
Farragut
, a
Miranda
-class
starship
, and an
Oberth
-class
starship
, were dispatched to the planet in order to rescue
Enterprise
survivors
. (
Star Trek Generations
)
The crash-landed saucer of the
Enterprise
-D was lifted off Veridian III and put on display in the
Fleet Museum
, in order to avoid a scenario where the
people
of
Veridian IV
discovered the wreckage and used it to develop
warp drive
, as allowing this to happen would violate the
Prime Directive
. Commodore
Geordi La Forge
subsequently spent twenty years rebuilding the ship in secret, making it once again operational by
2401
. (
PIC
: "
Vox
")
Sometime between 2371 and 2401,
Section 31
retrieved the body of Captain Kirk from the planet and stored it on
Daystrom Station
for an operation called "Project Phoenix." (
PIC
: "
The Bounty
")
The crashed
Enterprise
saucer on Veridian III
The arid mountain range on Veridian III where James Kirk was buried in 2371
Veridian III destroyed in an
alternate timeline
See also
Battle of Veridian III
Appendices
Background information
This planet's quadrant of origin is inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Discovery
episode "
An Obol for Charon
".
View into the canyon from Soran's weapon platform
Scenes set on Veridian III were filmed in the
Valley of Fire
in
Overton
,
Nevada
, around an
hour
's drive from
Las Vegas
.
The exact
coordinates
of James T. Kirk's final resting place are 36.451679, -114.498770. It is a moderately difficult hike from a nearby parking lot.
Apocrypha
In the
novel
The Return
written by
William Shatner
,
Commander
Riker
returns to Veridian III to ensure that the
Enterprise
's saucer is fully recovered (to prevent its technology influencing the development of
civilization
on Veridian IV when its inhabitants inevitably develop space travel themselves), and also to provide transport to
Spock
who has come to see Kirk's grave. The saucer's remains are briefly attacked by the
Borg
-
Romulan
alliance
to provide a distraction while Kirk's
body
is stolen, and Spock – accompanied by Riker and
Deanna Troi
– subsequently accesses the remaining
computer
data banks
in the damaged saucer in an attempt to work out whether any of Kirk's old foes could have still been alive and recovered his body for some reason.
In the
third issue
of the
IDW Publishing
comic
Star Trek: Spock: Reflections
, Spock traveled to Veridian III after Picard sent him a message explaining what really happened to Kirk to retrieve his body and bring him back home to
Earth
to be buried at the Kirk family
farm
in
Iowa
.
External link
Veridian III
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

Vico V
Type:
Planet
Status:
Inhabited
(
2376
)
Location:
Alpha Quadrant
Vico V
was the
inhabited
fifth
planet
in its
star system
, said to have the "
wildest
sky
in the
Alpha Quadrant
".
Mortimer Harren
grew up on Vico V.
Kathryn Janeway
had never been there, but based on its reputation for starry skies, she commented that the location of his upbringing explained why he became a
cosmologist
. Harren didn't agree. (
VOY
: "
Good Shepherd
")
The authors of the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 458 identified the unnamed system as the 'Vico system'.

======================================================================
PAGE: Vilmor II
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Vilmor_II
======================================================================

Vilmor II
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2369
)
Native species:
Species
of
lichen
Location:
Vilmoran
system
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Vilmor II
was the
uninhabited
second
planet
in the
Vilmoran
system
. This planet was located outside
Federation
territory
.
Four and a half billion years ago
, the
Progenitors
seeded part of a
genetic
program there, but the planet was almost devoid of
life
as of the
24th century
, as its
oceans
had dried up. Only one pocket of life remained: a primitive
lichen
growing in a
fossilized
seabed
. A
DNA
sample from Vilmor II was the last fragment required to decipher the code programmed by the ancient humanoids. The Federation,
Romulans
,
Cardassians
, and
Klingons
traveled to Vilmor II in
2369
to obtain the DNA.
A standoff over the sample ensued, which was resolved when
Dr.
Beverly Crusher
completed the program using partially fossilized
organic
material from the seabed. A message by the ancient humanoids played, revealing that all
humanoid
species
of the
galaxy
have a common genetic heritage. After this revelation, the
USS
Enterprise
-D
stayed in
orbit
of Vilmor II for a period of time to conduct repairs on the
warp engines
. (
TNG
: "
The Chase
")
In
3191
,
Kovich
showed
Michael Burnham
archive footage of these events, including images of the surface of Vilmor II. (
DIS
: "
Red Directive
")
The planet's
name
was not mentioned in the episode. Instead, the name came from
StarTrek.com
and the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 459.
[1]
(X)
External link
Re-Used Planets in TNG
at
Ex Astris Scientia

was the
fourth
planet
in its
star system
.
While working together as
couriers
in
3188
,
Michael Burnham
and
Cleveland Booker
had an escapade on Vintar IV. In
3191
, Burnham suggested that they take the same approach to
Fred
's abode on
Q'mau
, though Booker favored the approach they took on
Rigel V
. (
DIS
: "
Red Directive
")

Vintar IV
at

, the wiki for licensed

inhabited

and the
homeworld
of the
Vissian

The planet was located in the
Vissia
star system
. The planet was located more than twenty-five
light years
from the
hypergiant
where the Vissians made contact with
Enterprise
NX-01
in
2153
.
The
Didiron mountain range
, on the
Great Continent
, was one of this planet's many geological landmarks.
Veylo
mentioned Vissia to
Malcolm Reed
in 2153. She noted that on Vissia, unlike relationships undertaken by those from
Earth
, a
woman
will only dine with a
man
after the two have had a
sexual encounter
. (
ENT
: "
Cogenitor
")
