======================================================================
PAGE: Volan II
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Volan_II
======================================================================

Volan II
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
(formerly)
Cardassian Union
(
24th century
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Location:
Volan
system
Alpha Quadrant
Volan II
was the
inhabited
second
planet
in the
Volan
system
,
Alpha Quadrant
. This was a
Class M
world.
History
The
Federation
colonized this planet, making it one of the
Volan colonies
.
Between
2368
and
2373
, this planet's system was in the
Demilitarized Zone
.
In
2370
, the planet changed
governments
to the
Cardassian Union
when it was moved to the
Cardassian
side of the
Demilitarized Zone
after the
Federation-Cardassian Treaty
.
William Samuels
and his
wife
Louise Samuels
came to the colony in
2350
. Until 2370, when he left the planet for
Deep Space 9
to take part in a
Maquis
plot
to bomb the
Bok'Nor
, William Samuels was employed as a
farmer
for the colony. (
DS9
: "
The Maquis, Part I
")
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of
Federation space
in
3196
, this planet's system was within the blast radius of one of the mines. (
SA
: "
300th Night
", "
Rubincon
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
In the
script
, the pronunciation for Volan was given as "VOE-lahn".
[1]
The planet's classification came from
StarTrek.com
.
[2]
(X)

======================================================================
PAGE: Volan III
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Volan_III
======================================================================

Volan III
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Maquis
Cardassian Union
(
24th century
)
Status:
Habitable
(
3196
)
Satellite(s):
At least
one moon
Location:
Volan
system
Alpha Quadrant
Volan III
was the
inhabited
third
planet
in the
Volan
system
,
Alpha Quadrant
. This
Class M
world had at least
one moon
.
History
The
Federation
colonized this planet, making it one of the
Volan colonies
.
Between
2368
and
2373
, this planet's system was in the
Demilitarized Zone
.
In
2370
, the planet changed
governments
to the
Cardassian Union
when it was moved to the
Cardassian
side of the
Demilitarized Zone
after the
Federation-Cardassian Treaty
.
Lieutenant Commander
Calvin Hudson
,
Starfleet
's
attaché
to the Federation colonies in the DMZ, was stationed on Volan III in 2370. There, he and various
leaders
from the other colonies met to discuss relevant matters, including the formation of the
Maquis
. That
year
,
Gul
Dukat
took
Commander
Benjamin Sisko
to Volan III to convince him that there were Federation colonists willing to commit acts of violence against the Cardassians, such as the destruction of the
Bok'Nor
. Upon their arrival, Gul
Evek
confronted the colony leaders with the information obtained from
William Samuels
, and accused them of participating in an official
Starfleet
plot
to secretly arm the colonists against the Cardassians – while at the same time the
Cardassian Central Command
was arming its own
citizens
in turn. (
DS9
: "
The Maquis, Part I
") Sisko visited Volan III a second time, to issue a warning to the Maquis, that by making the Cardassians their enemy, they were making the Federation their enemy as well. (
DS9
: "
The Maquis, Part II
")
Volan III, as seen through the
Iconian gateway
Raymond Boone
, or rather a
Cardassian operative
surgically altered
to resemble him, settled with his family on Volan III to operate a
ladarium
mining
sluice
following his return from Cardassian
custody
after
Setlik III
. (
DS9
: "
Tribunal
")
A view of Volan III was later seen through the
Iconian gateway
on
Vandros IV
in
2372
. (
DS9
: "
To the Death
")
Inhabitants
See
:
Volan III inhabitants
In the
script
, the pronunciation for Volan was given as "VOE-lahn".
[1]
The
matte
for Volan III was a reuse of a matte created for
Tau Cygna V
in
Star Trek: The Next Generation
episode "
The Ensigns of Command
". As such, the tube-like structure running up the mountain might be identified as an
aqueduct
.
The planet's classification came from
StarTrek.com
.
[2]
(X)
External link
Volan III
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

PAGE: Vor'nak

.
Vor'nak
was a location in the
Beta Quadrant
.

2257

star chart
showing the trajectory of
Lieutenant
Spock
's
shuttle

Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Stellar Cartography", pp. 31; "Federation Historical Highlights, 2161-2385"), Vor'nak was a
M-class star
. This star was located in
Klingon
space
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Vulcan (alternate reality)
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Vulcan_(alternate_reality)
======================================================================

Alternate reality
(
split 2233
)
Vulcan
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Vulcan High Command
Coalition of Planets
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Destroyed
(
2258
)
Satellite(s):
None
Native species:
Vulcans
Romulans
Location:
In
orbit
of
40 Eridani A
Vulcan system
Alpha Quadrant
~16.34
light years
(5
parsecs
) from
Sol
For the
prime universe
counterpart, please see
Ni'Var
.
Vulcan
was an
inhabited
M-class
planet
orbiting
the
star
40 Eridani A
in the
Vulcan system
. This planet was located in the
Alpha Quadrant
and was approximately 16.34
light years
(5
parsecs
) from
Sol
. This was the homeworld of the
Vulcan
species and was a founding member of both the
Coalition of Planets
in
2155
and the
United Federation of Planets
in
2161
.
Planetary features
Vulcan, as seen from
orbit
, was largely red in color, with some greens and browns.
Vulcan had a noticeably higher
gravity
and thinner
atmosphere
than
Earth
. It was a mostly arid planet, with many large jutting rock formations, with most of the surface consisting of large
deserts
or
mountain
ranges, along with scattered small
seas
. Desert areas were prone to large amounts of heat, light, and
electrical
sandfire storms
. (
ENT
: "
The Forge
", "
Awakening
", "
Kir'Shara
")
There were several tourist attractions on Vulcan, including active
volcanoes
, ancient ruins, and
lava
fields. (
ENT
: "
Home
")
History
See also:
Vulcan history
As early as the mid-
20th century
the planet Vulcan had contact with the
Tellarites
and, covertly, with
Humans
. By the late
21st
or early
22nd century
they had established contact with the Humans,
Cardassians
,
Klingons
,
Tholians
, and
Trill
, among others. (
ENT
: "
Carbon Creek
", "
Future Tense
";
DS9
: "
Destiny
")
In
2152
there were over a million
physicians
on Vulcan. (
ENT
: "
Stigma
")
During the mid-
22nd century
, the
death penalty
was still common practice in Vulcan courts for a small number of offenses, including
treason
. (
ENT
: "
Kir'Shara
")
Before co-founding the
Federation
in
2161
, Vulcan was in conflict with
Andoria
. It controlled the
Coridanite
government, and had exchanged
ambassadors
with
Earth
and
Qo'noS
, among others. (
ENT
: "
Zero Hour
", "
These Are The Voyages...
", "
Shadows of P'Jem
", "
Broken Bow
")
For centuries, the planet was governed by a government called the
Vulcan High Command
. The High Command was dissolved in
2154
when the
Kir'Shara
was discovered. (
ENT
: "
Kir'Shara
")
Vulcan's history is the same for both the prime and
alternate reality
versions as it predates the point of divergence in
2233
, and so does not differ from the prime universe.
By at least
2258
, however, the High Command was reinstated and remained as the governing body. (
Star Trek
)
Destruction
Vulcan consumed by an artificial black hole
Main article:
Destruction of Vulcan
In 2258, the planet had a population of six billion, when it was
attacked
by the
Romulan
Nero
in his vessel, the
Narada
. The
Narada
deployed a
drill platform
in the planet's atmosphere, and began boring into the surface. A
distress call
from the planet resulted in the deployment of eight Federation starships to the planet, where all but the
USS
Enterprise
were destroyed by the
Narada
. The drill platform was ultimately disabled, but not before the energy stream had penetrated the planet's
core
.
A canister containing
red matter
was then dropped into the drill hole and detonated when it was consumed by the planet's core. This created a
singularity
at the heart of the planet, which began consuming the planet from the inside out. With only minutes remaining, the planet's population attempted to evacuate. Acting
Enterprise
captain
Spock
rescued several members of the
Vulcan Council
before the planet was lost.
Since Spock found his parents and members of the
Vulcan High Council
in the
Katric ark
, a chamber within Mount Seleya, it is possible many of the greatest minds of Vulcan were saved through a transference of their stored
katra
, thus "preserving Vulcan's cultural heritage."
The number of survivors from the planet was estimated to be fewer than ten thousand. The total number of remaining Vulcans, including those off-world at the time, is uncertain but the number was low enough for Spock to consider his race an "endangered species". (
Star Trek
)
Spock's
prime reality counterpart
founded a
colony
for the remaining survivors, which by
the following year
was known as
New Vulcan
. (
Star Trek Into Darkness
)
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 44 classified Vulcan as a
Minshara
-class
planet.
This low number may refer only to those who were able to escape from Vulcan and may not take into account the unknown numbers of Vulcans living off-world on Federation planets and serving aboard Federation starships.
[1]
The planet's sky appears blue rather than red. Orci explained the color depends on the season in the same Q&A. There is also canon support: When
Jonathan Archer
asked
T'Pol
whether the Vulcan sky gets to be as blue as
Archer IV
's sky, T'Pol replied "occasionally".
(
ENT
: "
Strange New World
")
The day Spock was offered entrance to the Vulcan Science Academy appears to be one of those occasions.
Appendices
Appearances
Star Trek
films
:
Star Trek
(First appearance)
References
Star Trek
films
:
Star Trek Into Darkness
Star Trek Beyond
Background information
Vulcan, the primary of the Vulcan system, was located in the Alpha Quadrant on a star chart seen in the
Star Trek: Discovery
episode "
Magic to Make the Sanest Man Go Mad
".
Apocrypha
Vulcan resurrected by Nekron
In the comic crossover mini-series
Star Trek - Green Lantern: The Spectrum War
, an ancient cosmic being named
Nekron
, who had crossed over from another universe arrives where Vulcan used to be in
2262
and used his power to resurrect the planet and its inhabitants so that he could begin a new conquest of death in the universe. The
Enterprise
arrives with several Lanterns from the same universe as Nekron and battle him and his undead army. Kirk then has Spock wear all seven Lantern Power Rings, correctly guessing that Spock had the sufficient strength needed to channel the entirety of the
Emotional Spectrum
and summon the
Life Entity
to defeat Nekron. It then uses its power to cleanse every trace of Nekron's power, which results in the destruction of Vulcan once again.
External link
Vulcan
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

waste management plant

======================================================================
PAGE: Waterfront
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Waterfront
======================================================================

A
waterfront
was an area of a
city
or
town
near a
body of water
.
Sisters of Hope Infirmary
was a
hospital
near
San Francisco
's waterfront in the
19th century
. (
TNG
: "
Time's Arrow, Part II
")
External link
Waterfront
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Wenselworld
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Wenselworld
======================================================================

Wenselworld
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2293
)
Location:
Beta Quadrant
Wenselworld
was a
planet
in the
Beta Quadrant
In
2293
, Wenselworld's position was labeled in the
star chart
for the "
Operation: Retrieve
" command briefing. The chart was from the
Starfleet Command Intelligence Database
. This star chart was seen in the
office
of the
Federation President
on
Earth
. (
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
,
set artwork
)
This planet's quadrant of origin is inferred based on the position of neighboring locations –
Starbase 24
and
Rura Penthe
– as seen in the
star charts
appearing in the
Star Trek: Discovery
episode "
Magic to Make the Sanest Man Go Mad
" and in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
This chart was seen in greater resolution at threads at Flare.Solareclipse.net.
[1]
(X)
[2]
(X)
Many of these locations shared their names with the last names of crew who worked on the sixth film. This location was probably named after the production assistant
Brian Wensel
.

West's World
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2369
)
West's World
was a
planet
in
Sector 22757
.
In
2369
, the position of West's World in the
sector
was labeled on the
star chart
"Subspace Comm Net System 2387 - Sector 22757". This planet was linked to the
Bajoran wormhole
by a line. (
DS9
: "
Duet
")
A clear view of the star chart can be seen
here
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Work environment
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Work_environment
======================================================================

The
work environment
was a
term
used to
describe
the
location
where one's
employment
extended to.
Paragraph seventy-six, subsection three of
Rom
's
contract
stated that he couldn't leave the work environment (in this case,
Quark's
) during
business hours
unless their
employer
ordered them to. According to Rom, it was a standard provision in
Ferengi
labor contracts
. (
DS9
: "
Bar Association
")
See also
Workplace hazard

======================================================================
PAGE: Workplace
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Workplace
======================================================================

A
workplace
was the location where one performed the tasks of one's
occupation
, including, but not limited to, one's
Starfleet
posting
,
office
,
store
, or
restaurant
.
In
2268
, the
Yang
man and woman were in one
cell
,
Spock
and
Leonard McCoy
in another, when
Ronald Tracey
brought in Kirk at gunpoint. Tracey barked orders, "
Take the
doctor
back to his workplace.
" (
TOS
: "
The Omega Glory
")
See also
"
The Workplace Encounter
"
Workplace hazard
External link
Workplace
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Wormhole relay station
CATEGORIES: Space stations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Wormhole_relay_station
======================================================================

Wormhole relay station
Type:
Communications relay
Affiliation:
Federation
Bajoran
Cardassian
Location:
Gamma Quadrant
Status:
Destroyed
(
2373
)
The
wormhole relay station
or
relay satellite
was a
subspace relay station
that allowed
communications
from the
Alpha Quadrant
to pass through the
Bajoran wormhole
into the
Gamma Quadrant
between
stardates
48543.2
and
50564.2
.
History
Although communications through the
wormhole
were possible, it was unreliable and required the wormhole to be open while the transmissions occurred. Some attempts were made in
2370
to produce a reliable communications network that traversed the wormhole, but due to the wormhole's high interference, which consisted of unstable
subspace carrier waves
and high
phase variances
, the signal was not strong enough to pass through.
Subspace communications
that bypassed the wormhole could take over twenty years to reach the Gamma Quadrant. (
DS9
: "
Battle Lines
", "
The Alternate
", "
Whispers
")
The disastrous
first contact
between the
Dominion
and the
Federation
created a growing need to have a reliable communications conduit to the Gamma Quadrant so that Dominion activity could be monitored.
In
2371
, the
Bajorans
put behind
fifty years
of hatred towards the
Cardassians
when
Vedek
Bareil
, under the auspices of
Commander
Benjamin Sisko
, brokered an historic
peace treaty
between the
Cardassian Union
and
Bajor
which was signed by
Legate
Turrel
and
Kai
Winn
. This treaty paved the way for the wormhole relay station to be built. (
DS9
: "
Life Support
")
Construction and deployment
The Cardassian science team
The first joint Federation-Bajoran-Cardassian effort under the new peace treaty, called the "Wormhole Comm Relay Project", was to create a Gamma Quadrant relay station to aid communications between the two distant
quadrants
. The Cardassian government sent three of its
scientists
,
Ulani Belor
,
Gilora Rejal
, and
Dejar
, to participate in the effort. However, former Vedek
Yarka
warned the
Emissary of the Prophets
, Benjamin Sisko, that the Cardassian involvement in the project would lead to the destruction of the wormhole according to
Trakor
's
Third Prophecy
, which predicted that three vipers would destroy the
Celestial Temple
. Despite his objections, though, Commander Sisko ordered the project to proceed because the kai and
Vedek Assembly
had already agreed on the project.
The
Defiant
encounters trouble testing the relay
In accordance with the project plans, the
USS
Defiant
positioned the signaling
satellite
at the Bajoran end of the wormhole and deployed the relay station on the Gamma Quadrant side. While testing the relay station, the crew discovered a
comet
whose trajectory would pass close to the wormhole. Major Kira saw this as a support to Yarka's claim about the prophecy.
When the first test of the relay produced no results, the team conducted a second test using a different
subspace frequency
. However, the second test inadvertently opened the wormhole, which increased its gravity and set the comet on a collision course with the wormhole.
Simply destroying the comet could have created multiple fragments, so
Chief
O'Brien
modified the
phasers
of the
Defiant
to generate a wide beam that would encompass the entire comet and destroy it evenly. When the
Defiant
fired her phasers, however, a standard burst was fired instead, causing the comet to split into three pieces, all still on course for the wormhole. O'Brien's examination of the weapon system suggested he made a mistake, one that even a first-year
engineering
student wouldn't make. Gilora then informed him that Dejar was a member of the
Obsidian Order
and that she had
sabotaged
the phasers in an effort to undermine Cardassian-Bajoran relations by trying to let the relay project fail. It seemed the prophecy would be fulfilled just as Yarka had said.
To save the relay project from a disaster, Sisko positioned a
shuttle
between the three comet fragments in an attempt to contain them while they passed through the wormhole. Except for a small amount of
silithium
leakage from the comet, he and Kira successfully contained the fragments and kept the wormhole intact. More importantly, the silithium that was inadvertently released during the procedure created a small
subspace filament
in the wormhole, large enough for the wormhole relay station to work. Sisko had fulfilled the prophecy, although not in the way Yarka explained it, and ensured that the wormhole would never close again. (
DS9
: "
Destiny
")
Logistics
Schematic of the wormhole relay station
The wormhole relay station consisted of a Federation
signaling platform
that was positioned two
kilometers
from the wormhole entrance on the Gamma side. Using the platform's existing
power grid
, the Cardassian component consisted of a
transceiver
, devised by
Ulani Belor
. The transceiver assembly would then send a
subspace carrier wave
through the wormhole to a second similar transceiver, a
signaling array
located on Deep Space 9.
The transceiver used
soliton pulses
, carefully tuned to eliminate any phase variance below 0.005% to minimize the impact on the wormhole. Ulani and Gilora improved the station's signaling array so it could accommodate the new transmissions (and removed O'Brien's secondary backup of the station's system in the process); however, the simulations showed that transmission
frequencies
in the
delta band
would not work, and the
theta band
frequencies could create a
subspace inversion
. In essence, it was the subspace filament that allowed transmissions to occur; the filament (created when the comet's silithium reacted with the wormhole's ambient
radiation
) was a thin trail that acted like a subspace carrier wave and helped to carry transmissions through the bends of the wormhole. (
DS9
: "
Destiny
")
Service record
Defiant
alongside relay station
The subspace relay station played an important part in communications while
Starfleet
explored the Gamma Quadrant.
In
Julian Bashir
's
coma
-induced reality created by the
Lethean
Altovar
, a simulation of Chief O'Brien managed to communicate to the outside using the relay station. It revealed the voices of
Jadzia Dax
and Sisko in the real world, trying to revive Bashir from the
telepathic
damage caused by the Lethean. (
DS9
: "
Distant Voices
")
When
Enabran Tain
was about to destroy the
homeworld of the
Founders
, Starfleet ordered Sisko to stay put and protect Bajor. Sisko, though, wanted to rescue
Odo
, who was being held captive by Tain, and decided to pursue him. If Sisko found any Dominion forces coming through the wormhole, he would warn the Alpha Quadrant using the communications relay. (
DS9
: "
The Die is Cast
")
Before
General
Martok
invaded Cardassia and started the
Federation-Klingon War
, he was ordered to protect Deep Space 9 in event of an attack by the Dominion, who had placed Changelings throughout the Alpha Quadrant.
Captain
Sisko politely refused the offer for help, citing the fact that the communications relay picked up no
Jem'Hadar
activity near the wormhole. (
DS9
: "
The Way of the Warrior
")
Lieutenant
Arriaga
, under orders from
Admiral
Leyton
, attempted to mislead Starfleet into thinking the Dominion was sending cloaked ships to the Alpha Quadrant. He did so by attaching a subspace modulator to the communications satellite. The modulator had the effect of making the wormhole open and close at random. (
DS9
: "
Homefront
", "
Paradise Lost
")
When O'Brien was convicted of
spying
on the
Argrathi
government, the Argrathi communicated to the station via the wormhole relay. (
DS9
: "
Hard Time
")
Bashir used the relay station a number of times during his individual missions in the Gamma Quadrant, such as when he tried to cure the Jem'Hadar of their
ketracel-white
addiction on
Bopak III
, and when he cured the
Teplans
of the
Blight
. (
DS9
: "
Hippocratic Oath
", "
The Quickening
")
When Sisko,
Worf
,
Jadzia Dax
,
Muniz
, and O'Brien were trapped in a
Jem'Hadar attack ship
on
Torga IV
, Sisko used the communications relay to request the services of the
Defiant
. (
DS9
: "
The Ship
")
The relay station's most useful mission came when Worf and
Elim Garak
took a
runabout
to locate Tain, who was captured by the Dominion after his failed attempt to destroy the Founders' homeworld. When they found a Dominion fleet hiding in a
nebula
close to the wormhole, Worf managed to send a message to the relay and warned Deep Space 9 of the impending invasion. The relay's primary mission (to detect when the Dominion would invade the Alpha Quadrant) had been fulfilled.
With the message received, the wormhole relay station (and its associated outposts) were disabled by the Dominion fleet. (
DS9
: "
In Purgatory's Shadow
")
Background information
The relay station was introduced in "
Destiny
" as a B-story the producers had been looking to include for some time. "
It just took us a while to figure out how to do it,
" explained
Ronald D. Moore
. "
We wanted to deal with the Emissary, we wanted to do a Bajoran-orientated show. We also wanted a communication relay in the Gamma Quadrant anyway, and since it was following "
Life Support
", we could make the Cardassians integral to it and include the peace treaty. It all just started to come together for us.
" (
Captains' Logs Supplemental - The Unauthorized Guide to the New Trek Voyages
, p. 92)
The relay is a reuse of the
Amargosa observatory
model from
Star Trek Generations
. Gary Hutzel also had the job of adjusting it to appear in "Destiny". As he explained, "
In the feature, it has extremely long extensions on it. So basically, I took a saw to it! We cut off the extensions and redesigned a couple of elements and did a new paint job.
" The script describes it as being "
the size of a
runabout
, with high-tech antennae and communications gear.
" (
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine Companion
, p. 212;
[1]
[2]
)
External link
Wormhole relay station
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

. At the time it was  featured (September 2005), it was considered one of the best examples of the

's work. This article was  featured more than five years ago though, and its status should be

. You can use the links below to see how the article has changed since it was last featured.

PAGE: Wrigley's pleasure planet
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Wrigley's_pleasure_planet
======================================================================

Wrigley's pleasure planet
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
23rd century
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
The woman Darnell met on Wrigley's planet
Wrigley's pleasure planet
was an
inhabited
planet
, which was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
In the mid-
23rd century
,
Crewman
Darnell
met a
girl
on this
pleasure planet
. While on
M-113
, Darnell made comparisons between her and "
Nancy Crater
", before being rebuked by
Doctor
McCoy
. Neither realized that "
Nancy
" was in fact the
M-113 creature
in disguise; they were each perceiving separate
illusions
of a woman's appearance. (
TOS
: "
The Man Trap
")
Appendices
Background information
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
Wrigley's pleasure planet was considered as a
location
for
shore leave
in the
Star Trek: Enterprise
episode "
Two Days and Two Nights
", though it was decided that it sounded too
Human
and too well-known for what was supposed to be a relatively distant
region
, so it was replaced in the story with
Risa
. (
Star Trek: Communicator
issue 143
, p. 31)
Apocrypha
Wrigley's was the main setting for the
DS9
eBook
Lust's Latinum Lost (and Found)
. Locations include the
city
of Debauche (
β
) and the
resort
Desire Island (
β
).
External link
Wrigley's Pleasure Planet
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Wurna Minor
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Wurna_Minor
======================================================================

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Wurna Minor
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Inhabited
(
2261
)
Native species:
Wurna Minor natives
Location:
Alpha Quadrant
Wurna Minor
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Alpha Quadrant
.
History
In the
2250s
, the warp capable civilization on this planet had or was in the process of being inducted into the
United Federation of Planets
. (
SNW
: "
Strange New Worlds
")
In the
mirror universe
, Wurna Minor was listed, along with other locations, in the
personnel file
of
Captain
Sylvia Tilly
. During her distinguished service career, she earned the
nickname
"The
Witch
of Wurna Minor".
(
DIS
: "
Despite Yourself
")
Stellar Cartography
In
2259
, the location of this planet was labeled on
stellar cartography
charts displayed on the
USS
Enterprise
. Its symbol had a
blue
color
, indicating that it was affiliated with the
Federation
. (
SNW
: "
Strange New Worlds
", "
Children of the Comet
", "
Subspace Rhapsody
")
In
2260
, Wurna Minor was labeled on a
star chart
denoting the spread of a
quantum uncertainty
field across the region. It was outside of the area effect of the phenomena. It was marked as a Federation system. (
SNW
: "
Subspace Rhapsody
")
In
2261
, the location of Wurna Minor was depicted on a
star chart
used by the crew of the USS
Enterprise
to highlight the location of
Vadia
. It was labeled as a Federation system. (
SNW
: "
Through the Lens of Time
")

Wynsdey III
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
24th century
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Wynsdey III
was the
inhabited
third
planet
in its
system
. This planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
This planet was home to the
Adams Research Group
, which was credited with developing the
fissionable
explosive
plutonium ryanite
. (
TNG-R
: "
Night Terrors
",
display graphic
)
This planet was only mentioned in writing.
For the remastered episode "Night Terrors", the list of explosives was revised. This planet was mentioned in one of the new entries.

======================================================================
PAGE: Xahea
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Xahea
======================================================================

Xahea
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Diameter:
13,097
kilometers
Status:
Habitable
(
2257
)
Satellite(s):
Four
moons
Native species:
Xaheans
Location:
Xahea system
,
Grid 07, Quad 09, block 3, sector 1
Alpha Quadrant
Xahea
was an
inhabited
class M
planet
, the
homeworld
of the
warp-capable
Xaheans
. It had four
moons
and was surrounded by a large
gaseous
cloud. It was remarkable for its natural
dilithium
supply, and described as beautiful.  (
ST
: "
Runaway
";
DIS
: "
Such Sweet Sorrow
")
Astronomical data
Xahea data displayed
Xahea was located in the
Xahea system
, at
galactic coordinates
003-1040808.1230 003-111309.3, within
Grid 07, Quad 09, block 3, sector 1
of the
Alpha Quadrant
. In the
Starfleet database
, the planet was assigned
catalog number
015289430.
The planet revolved around its
sun
at a distance of 201.5 million
kilometers
, with an
orbital period
of four hundred
days
. It had a diameter of 13,097
kilometers
and a
gravity
of 9.9
kilograms
/
cubic meter
. Its
rotation period
was 24.8
hours
, resulting in a
day
length of 24.9 hours. The
temperature
on the surface averaged 14.9 degrees
Celsius
. (
DIS
: "
Such Sweet Sorrow
")
History and culture
According to the Xahean
citizen
Me Hani Ika Hali Ka Po
, there was a natural balance between the Xaheans and their planet, as they were born at the same time, making Xahea the
twin
sister of Po.
Dilithium
was
mined
in the
caves
on Xahea.
Around the
2250s
, there was an upheaval on the planet linked to the development of a
dilithium incubator
capable of recrystallizing dilithium, as well as due to the planet gaining
warp capability
. During this time, the king died and his sister, Me Hani Ika Hali Ka Po, was expected to be
crowned
as
queen
. She briefly ran away from the planet, causing the Xahean Council to send out a
diplomatic hail
(which Starfleet received), but she was convinced to take up her responsibilities after an encounter on the
USS
Discovery
. (
ST
: "
Runaway
")
USS
Discovery
arrives at Xahea in 2257
In
2258
, the fifth of seven
red bursts
appeared over the planet. This drew
Discovery
and the
USS
Enterprise
to the planet to investigate. When a fleet of hostile
Section 31
ships approached,
Captain
Christopher Pike
ordered
Discovery
and the
Enterprise
away from the planet so as not to involve the Xaheans in
the imminent battle
. (
DIS
: "
Such Sweet Sorrow
")
In
2259
, the location of this planet's system was labeled on a
stellar cartography
chart that was seen on the
USS
Enterprise
's
ready room
viewscreen
. By this year, the Xaheans had or were in the process of being inducted into the Federation. (
SNW
: "
Strange New Worlds
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Xanthii
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Xanthii
======================================================================

Xanthii
Type:
Astronomical
object
Status:
Intact
(
2285
)
Location:
Alpha Quadrant
Xanthii
was a
astronomical
object
in the same
region
as
Sol
/
Jupiter
. Its companion was
Thelonii
.
In
2285
, Xanthii's position was displayed on a
star chart
on the
bridge
of the
USS
Enterprise
. (
Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan
)
This astronomical object was only mentioned in writing.
The image was taken from page T0:02:07:02 of the
Star Fleet Technical Manual
by
Franz Joseph
. The map claimed to depict principal
star systems
in the
Federation
. Based on the scale for the chart, Xanthii was located two
parsecs
from Sol. Thelonii and Xanthii were stars in a
trinary
star system
. The name of a third star, Alfr, was not seen in the movie.
External link
Xanthii
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Xanthras III
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Xanthras_III
======================================================================

Xanthras III
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2366
)
Location:
Xanthras
system
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Xanthras III
was the
third
planet
in the
Xanthras
system
. This planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
The planet was the destination of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
after its
mission
to the
Gamma Erandi Nebula
in
2366
. The starship was scheduled to
rendezvous
with the
USS
Zapata
at this planet. (
TNG
: "
Ménage à Troi
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Xantoras (planet)
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Xantoras_(planet)
======================================================================

Xantoras
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Diameter:
11,854 km
Status:
Habitable
(
2153
)
Native species:
Xantoras
Xantoras
was an
M class
planet
, the
homeworld
of the
Xantoras
. The planet was known for its
geological
formations, and historically unstable
governments
.
History
The Xantoras government had been known to change hands every few years for a long time prior to the
2150s
. One particular government was in place in the late
2140s
and early 2150s, welcoming many
aliens
on their planet.
The
Denobulan
government sent a team of three
scientists
to study rare and unique
speleothems
that had formed in large underground tunnels on the planet. Several weeks later (in
2153
), however, the government which had been in power since the late 2140s was overthrown by a militant group. The members of the new militant group were
xenophobic
, and believed that aliens should not be allowed on the planet. The new government instituted a deadline for all off-worlders to leave or they would be
imprisoned
, and probably
executed
.
The Denobulan scientists were out of communication range when the new government took hold, which prevented the
Denobulan Science Academy
from contacting them. The nearest Denobulan starship was several days away, so the scientists asked
Enterprise
NX-01
to retrieve them.
While
Enterprise
was in
orbit
, the
crew
of an alien
starship
wanted to land their vehicle on the planet when the vessel's reactor breached. As the government was xenophobic, it turned the ship away even for emergency repairs.
Enterprise
agreed to dock with the craft and help with repairs and treating the injured.
Enterprise
also sent an
away team
to climb down the large caverns to retrieve the stranded Denobulan scientists, given a three-day deadline. While this rescue attempt was ongoing, Xantoras from the previous government and the new government fought each other above the caverns. The members of the new Xantoras government told
Enterprise
they were taking care of the situation.
The
Enterprise
away team took a few hours more than the deadline, and on the way back to the ship, a Xantoras vessel fired on
Enterprise
. However,
Commander
Tucker
believed it was just a "kick in the pants" intended to quicken the journey up to the ship.
Physical characteristics
The unique caverns on Xantoras
Xantoras had a rotational period of 25 hours, 23 minutes, 2.2 seconds; a mean solar distance of 151,000,000 km; a diameter of 11,854 km; and an equatorial circumference of 33,127 km. Its surface area was 34% land and 66% ocean. (
ENT
: "
The Breach
"
display graphic
)
Among
geologists
, Xantoras was known as one of the finest collections of preserved specimens known. Its surface was covered with many large and varied
mountains
and
canyons
, even visible from orbit. But under the surface, the planet was even more noteworthy, as it contained thousands of kilometers of
caves
and cliffs, and many more were expected to exist, though, by 2153, they hadn't been mapped yet.
The Denobulan science team that was exploring these caves specifically went to the planet to study the largest condensed collections of speleothems known. They contained
calcite
,
aragonite
, and even
botyroidal flowstones
. The leader of the team,
Yolen
, believed that studying them would provide endless clues into preventing seismic disasters on
Denobula
. (
ENT
: "
The Breach
")

Type:

Xendi Sabu IX
was the
inhabited
ninth
planet
in the
Xendi Sabu
star system
.
Lieutenant Commander
Brad Yacobian
, a
crewmember
aboard both the
USS
Enterprise
-D
and
USS
Voyager
, was born on this non-
Federation
planet
. In
2369
, his planet of origin was listed on the chart "USS
Enterprise
Personnel Database", which was seen on
Jean-Luc Picard
's
PADD
. (
TNG
: "
The Chase
"
okudagram
)
This planet was only mentioned in writing.
Based on the planet's seemingly close proximity to the
Maxia Zeta star system
, it would seem that this planet is in, or is relatively near the Alpha Quadrant.

======================================================================
PAGE: Xindus
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Xindus
======================================================================

Xindus
Type:
Planet
Status:
Destroyed
(
2030s
)
Native species:
Xindi-Aquatic
Xindi-Arboreal
Xindi-Avian
Xindi-Insectoid
Xindi-Primate
Xindi-Reptilian
Location:
Delphic Expanse
"
As well as being unstable politically, our planet was even more unstable geologically. In a final desperate act, the
Insectoids
and
Reptilians
detonated massive explosions beneath the eight largest seismic fissures. I'd like to think they didn't realize how devastating the result would be.
"
–
Gralik Durr
,
2153
("
The Shipment
")
Xindus
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Delphic Expanse
and the
homeworld
of the
Xindi
. It was the only planet in
its
solar system
.
The planet was
geologically
unstable and was destroyed in
2033
when, at the height of the
Xindi Civil War
, the
Reptilians
and
Insectoids
detonated large weapons beneath the eight largest
seismic fissures
of the planet. While five of the Xindi species escaped this destruction, the
Xindi-Avians
lacked the ability to flee the planet and were wiped out. The five surviving species
spread out
across the Expanse, settling on various planets. (
ENT
: "
The Shipment
")
In
2153
,
Enterprise
NX-01
discovered an
asteroid field
nearly 80 million
kilometers
long
orbiting
a yellow-orange
star
. This was what remained of Xindus. (
ENT
: "
The Xindi
", "
Azati Prime
")
Although the remains of this planet appear as early as
third season
opener "The Xindi", the planet wasn't named until the third last episode of that season, "
The Council
".
In the script of "The Xindi", the debris from Xindus is described as "a vast, cigar-shaped debris field stretching in a partial around [a] star."

======================================================================
PAGE: Ya'Seem
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ya'Seem
======================================================================

Ya'Seem
was a location of major
archaeological
interest.
Its discovery by
M'Tell
was considered one of the greatest archaeological discoveries ever, comparable to
Schliemann
finding
Troy
. (
TNG
: "
The Chase
")
This location was only mentioned in dialogue.
It was not specified what exactly Ya'Seem was, although the mention of M'Tell first stepping on it suggests it was something to be landed on.
According to the
script
, the pronunciation for Ya'Seem was "ya-SEEM".

======================================================================
PAGE: Yaan
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Yaan
======================================================================

Yaan
Type:
Astronomical
object
Status:
Intact
(
2285
)
Location:
Alpha Quadrant
Yaan
was a
astronomical
object
in the same
region
as
Sol
/
Jupiter
. Its companion was
Oomaru
.
In
2285
, Yaan's position was displayed on a
star chart
on the
bridge
of the
USS
Enterprise
. (
Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan
)
This astronomical object was only mentioned in writing.
The image was taken from page T0:02:07:02 of the
Star Fleet Technical Manual
by
Franz Joseph
. The map claimed to depict principal
star systems
in the
Federation
. Based on the scale for the chart, Yaan was located slightly less than a
parsec
and a half from
Sol
. Yaan and Oomaru were stars in a
binary
star system
.
External link
Yaan
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Yadalla Prime
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Yadalla_Prime
======================================================================

Yadalla Prime
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2370
)
Location:
Yadalla
system
Near the
Romulan Neutral Zone
(
2160
-
2387
)
Sector 2158
Beta Quadrant
Yadalla Prime
was the
prime
planet
in the
Yadalla
system
. This planet was within
Sector 2158
of the
Beta Quadrant
. It was located near the
Romulan Neutral Zone
.
Yadalla Prime was the
location
of a
Romulan
archaeological
site suggested as a possible raiding target for a band of
mercenaries
working for
Arctus Baran
in
2370
.
Starfleet Command
anticipated that Baran might attack this planet so a
Federation
starship
was sent there to intercept his ship. (
TNG
: "
Gambit, Part I
", "
Gambit, Part II
")
Background information
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
This planet's quadrant of origin is inferred based on the position of its star system as seen in the
star chart
appearing in the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
According to the
script
for "Gambit, Part I", the pronunciation for Yadalla was "yuh-DOLL-uh".
[1]
Yadalla Prime was called
Yonada Prime
in early drafts of "Gambit", a reference to the worldship
Yonada
from the
Star Trek: The Original Series
episode "
For the World is Hollow and I Have Touched the Sky
". The
name
was changed in later versions of the script due to the need for Romulan origins for the planet. (
Star Trek: The Next Generation Companion
, 2nd ed., p. 267) The original name was retained in an
okudagram
seen on the
bridge
of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. This graphic was put up for auction in 2018.
[2]

Yadera II
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2370
)
Location:
Unnamed system
Gamma Quadrant
Yadera II
was the
second
planet
in its
star system
, in the
Gamma Quadrant
.
Yadera II was named after
Yadera Prime
,
homeworld
of the
Yaderans
. It became the
refugee
Rurigan
's new home after the
Dominion
invaded his homeworld of Yadera Prime in
2340
. With the help of a
hologenerator
, he created
sentient holographic lifeforms
on the planet. (
DS9
: "
Shadowplay
")
Appendices
Background information
The planet's
name
is not from the episode. Rather, it is from
StarTrek.com
and the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 504.
[1]
(X)
According to the
script
, the pronunciation for Yadera was "yah-DER-ah".
[2]
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 75), Yadera II
orbited
a
K-class star
. Yadera II was a destination in the Gamma Quadrant Bio-Survey (
2369
-
75
).
According to the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
(4th ed., vol. 2, p. 504), the
matte painting
of the Yadera II landscape was re-used later for the planet
Ronara Prime
in "
Preemptive Strike
".

at

, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Re-Used Planets in DS9
at

======================================================================
PAGE: Yadera Prime
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Yadera_Prime
======================================================================

Yadera Prime
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Dominion
Status:
Habitable
(
2340
)
Native species:
Yaderans
Location:
Gamma Quadrant
Yadera Prime
was the
inhabited
prime
planet
in its
star system
. This system was located in the
Gamma Quadrant
.
The planet was the
homeworld
of the
Yaderans
, a
warp-capable
humanoid
species
. Around
2340
, the planet was conquered by the
Dominion
, which, according to
Rurigan
, caused the Yaderan way of life to change dramatically. (
DS9
: "
Shadowplay
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to the
script
, the pronunciation for Yadera was "yah-DER-ah".
[1]
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
(pp. 71, 74), Yadera Prime was a
M-class
planet. This planet was annexed by the Dominion in
2340
. The dominant species were the humanoid Yaderan. The Yaderan were warp-capable in circa
2200
. Their world
orbited
a
G-class star
.

at

, the wiki for licensed

======================================================================
PAGE: Yonada Prime
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Yonada_Prime
======================================================================

Yonada Prime
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2370
)
Location:
Unnamed system
Sector 2158
Beta Quadrant
Archaeological data analysis chart showing Yonada Prime
Yonada Prime
was a
prime
planet
in its
star system
, which was located near the
Calder
and
Draken systems
.
In the
centuries
before the
24th century
, this planet was occupied by the
Romulans
. In
2370
, this was one of several planets where the Romulan ruins were raided by a band of
mercenaries
led by
Arctus Baran
. The planet appeared on an archaeological data analysis
star chart
consulted on the
USS
Enterprise
-D
in
2370
. (
TNG
: "
Gambit, Part I
")
This planet's quadrant of origin is inferred based on the position of neighboring systems, such as the Calder system and the Draken system, as seen in the
star charts
appearing in the
Star Trek: Discovery
episode "
The War Without, The War Within
" and the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
Yadalla Prime
, which plays a larger role in the episode, was called Yonada Prime in early drafts of the episode, a reference to the worldship
Yonada
in the
Star Trek: The Original Series
episode "
For the World is Hollow and I Have Touched the Sky
". The
name
was changed in the
script
due to the need for
Romulan
origins for the planet. (
Star Trek: The Next Generation Companion
, 2nd ed., p. 267) However, the original name was still mentioned in an
okudagram
seen in the episode. This graphic was put up for auction in 2018.
[1]

Yosemite 3
Type:
Research station
Affiliation:
United Earth
Status:
Destroyed
(
2154
)
Crew:
30 to 40
Armament:
None
Yosemite 3
was an unarmed
United Earth
research station
that was in service in the mid-
22nd century
. The
space station
orbited
Earth
and was usually manned with thirty to forty
civilians
.
It was destroyed, presumably with all hands, during an attack by
Dolim
's
Xindi-Reptilian warship
at the end of the
Xindi incident
on
February 14
,
2154
. (
ENT
: "
Zero Hour
")
For more information on the
studio model
, see
VOY studio models
.
See also:
Objects in Earth orbit
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Ysida
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ysida
======================================================================

Ysida
Class:
P
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2384
)
Location:
Delta Quadrant
Ysida
was an
uninhabited
class P
planet
, located in the
Delta Quadrant
close to the boundary with the
Beta Quadrant
. Much of its surface was covered by an
ocean
of high-
density
vapor, with a scattering of
desert
islands
and
rocky
reefs
. The planet was home to large
eels
. (
PRO
: "
Last Flight of the Protostar, Part II
", "
A Tribble Called Quest
")
In
2374
, the
USS
Protostar
, carrying
Captain
Chakotay
and
Commander
Adreek-Hu
,
crashed
on Ysida after being launched through an unstable
wormhole
from
Solum
. Due to the threat of the
living construct
on board the ship, the two Starfleet officers ejected the
proto-core
and vented the
warp drive
, thus
marooning
themselves on the planet to protect the Federation. Adreek later vanished during an attempt to restore the ship's
warp core
using
antimatter
generated by Ysida's
ion maelstroms
, leaving Chakotay as the sole survivor.
In
2384
,
Dal R'El
and his friends found Chakotay and the
Protostar
with the help of
Wesley Crusher
. The young crew helped Chakotay to repair the derelict ship and restore power to the
impulse engines
and warp drive, allowing the
Protostar
to finally leave the planet. (
PRO
: "
The Devourer of All Things, Part II
", "
Last Flight of the Protostar, Part I
", "
Last Flight of the Protostar, Part II
")
The spelling of the planet's name was confirmed by
Aaron Waltke
.
[1]
The planet was named for writer
Diandra Pendleton-Thompson
‘s grandmother, Daisy, who passed away during the production of the season and was an inspiration to her.

======================================================================
PAGE: Yuri
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Yuri
======================================================================

For the person with the same name mentioned in the
Treaty of Armens
, please see
Yuri
.
For the Trelonian general, please see
Yiri
.
Yuri
AKA:
Braslota II
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
2365
)
Satellite(s):
A
ring system
Location:
Braslota
system
Oneamisu
sector
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Yuri
Yuri
, also known as
Braslota II
, was the
uninhabited
ringed
second
planet
in the
Braslota
system
. This system, which was in the
Oneamisu
sector
, was selected by
Starfleet Command
in
2365
as the location for the
Starfleet
battle simulation
Operation Lovely Angel
.
In the simulation, the
starships
USS
Hathaway
and
USS
Enterprise
-D
had simulated objectives that either ship had to complete before progressing to the next objective. In the first objective, either ship would attempt a successful capture of control of near-planetary
space
in the
region
of Yuri, while, at the same time, maintaining a defensive posture from a simulated threat from
Kei
. The winning ship would proceed to the next objective at
Totoro
.
Yuri was labeled on a diagram of the Braslota system. In this diagram, the planet's position and
orbital
path were depicted. This diagram was on display in the
observation lounge
of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. (
TNG
: "
Peak Performance
",
okudagram
)
The
name
of this planet came from the diagram.
According to the
script
, this was described as "a dusty
orange
planet".
Most of the simulation information was
in-joke
references to the Japanese
Anime
(Japanese animation) series
Dirty Pair
, this planet included. (
Star Trek: The Next Generation Companion
, 2nd ed., p. 92)

======================================================================
PAGE: ZIP code
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/ZIP_code
======================================================================

A
ZIP code
was a sequence of
numbers
and/or letters
assigned
to
certain
locations
, and were often used in conjunction with
addresses
.
In
2365
, while performing a
comedy
routine,
Data
said that "
I come from a
town
so small, we had a
fraction
for a ZIP code.
" (
TNG
: "
The Outrageous Okona
")
In a document prepared for
ENT
: "
Carpenter Street
",
First Street
was said to be situated within the ZIP code 40210.
[1]
,
[2]
External link
ZIP code
at
Wikipedia

Zadar IV
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Habitable
(
2402
)
Native species:
A
species
of
dolphins
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Zadar IV
was the
inhabited
fourth
planet
of its
star system
. The planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
. This planet was the
homeworld
for a
species
of
dolphins
.
Before
stardate
41509
,
Starfleet
oceanographer
Lieutenant
Harry Bernard, Sr.
was stationed on Zadar IV with his
son
Harry Jr.
. They lived on the
beach
and Harry Jr. often saw dolphins playing in the
ocean
there. They were later the inspiration for his first attempt at
sculpting
. (
TNG
: "
When The Bough Breaks
")
In
2402
,
Deanna Troi
and
William T. Riker
considered taking a
beach
vacation
on Zadar IV. (
PIC
: "
The Last Generation
")
Appendices
Background information
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I"), Zadar IV was a
Federation member
as of
2378
.
Apocryphal
In his
personnel file
in the non-canon
video game
Star Trek: Starship Creator
, Lieutenant
Barnaby
was born on Zadar IV in
2343
, where his mother Lee and father Edward resided.

at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

at

Zakdorn

)

Location:

system
Beta Quadrant
Zakdorn
was an
inhabited
planet
in the
Zakdorn
system
. This was the
homeworld
of the
Zakdorns
.
In
3195
, this planet's name appeared on a
video wall
in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
Kids These Days
")
This planet was only mentioned in writing.
The planet's location was inferred from the position of its system in the
Star Trek: Strange New Worlds
episode "
The Sehlat Who Ate Its Tail
".
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I"), Zakdorn was listed as a
Federation
member world
in
2378
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Zalkon
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Zalkon
======================================================================

Zalkon
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
Non-aligned
Status:
Habitable
(
2366
)
Native species:
Zalkonians
Location:
Zalkonian space
Sector 9569
Alpha Quadrant
~2.3
parsecs
(7.5
light years
)
from
Zeta Gelis star cluster
Zalkon
was an
inhabited
planet
in an unknown
star system
. It was located inside
Zalkonian space
in
Sector 9569
of the
Alpha Quadrant
and was the
homeworld
of the
warp-capable
humanoid
Zalkonians
. The
Zeta Gelis star cluster
, a
star cluster
in a neighboring
sector
, was located 2.3
parsecs
(7.5
light years
) from Zalkon.
In
2366
,
John Doe
and three other Zalkonians fled their homeworld in order to undergo the complete
metamorphosis
of their species from
corporeal
to
non-corporeal
beings. All but John Doe were hunted down by Zalkonian
authorities
before they could finish the transformation, however.
When the
crew
of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
found a
Zalkonian escape pod
on a
planet
in the Zeta Gelis star cluster, with a badly wounded John Doe on board, they were able to reconstruct the
course
of his ship, back to Zalkon. At the time, Zalkon was on the
Enterprise
-D's intended course, and was already projected to be reached within 3
weeks
, during their
star charting
mission
. (
TNG
: "
Transfigurations
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets II") and
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Stellar Cartography", pp. 18-19; "Federation Historical Highlights, 2161-2385"), Zalkon was located in the Zalkon system.

======================================================================
PAGE: Zayra IV
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Zayra_IV
======================================================================

Zayra IV
Type:
Planet
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Habitable
(
2360s
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Zayra IV
was an
inhabited
planet
in its
star system
. A
Federation
starbase
was located on this planet in the mid-
24th century
.
A few
years
prior to
2369
,
Miles O'Brien
was called to the facility to reroute an
emitter array
. The array system was infested with
Talarian hook spiders
. O'Brien overcame his
phobia
of spiders and successfully rerouted the array. (
TNG
: "
Realm Of Fear
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
Zayra IV's name was noted, in the
Star Trek: The Next Generation Companion
, 2nd ed., p. 216, to be an inside reference to production associate
Zayra Cabot
.
According to the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 3rd ed., p. 574, Zayra IV was in the Zayra system.

======================================================================
PAGE: Zeon (planet)
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Zeon_(planet)
======================================================================

Zeon
Class:
M
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2268
)
Satellite(s):
A
ring system
Native species:
Zeons
Location:
Unnamed system
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Zeon
was an
inhabited
planet
with a
ring system
in an
unnamed system
. This
Class M
planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
and was the
homeworld
of the
Zeons
, a
pre-warp
humanoid
species
. This planet was further out from its system's center than the sister planet
Ekos
.
By the mid-
23rd century
, Zeon had interplanetary capable ships and its
civilization
had enjoyed
dozens
of
generations
of
peace
. Shortly after the year
2218
, the Zeons undertook a mission to Ekos on a
mission
of civilizing the Ekosians. A Zeon colony was established on Ekos, which existed for the next half a century before being wiped out by the Ekosian
National Socialist Party
.
In
2268
, Zeon was briefly endangered when the
Ekosian
Nazi
regime planned to attack it with a
fleet
of manned and unmanned
weapons
. Promoted as the "
Final Decision
", the attack was barely averted by the crew of the
starship
USS
Enterprise
, working with the
Zeon
and native
Ekosian
anti-Nazi
underground
resistance. (
TOS
: "
Patterns of Force
")
Photograph of Ekos and Zeon
A
photograph
of Ekos and Zeon was displayed on one of the
walls
in dive
bar
on
Starbase 25
in
2381
. (
LD
: "
An Embarrassment Of Dooplers
")
Appendices
See also
Zeon Minor
Background information
The name Zeon was similar to "
Zion
", a
Hebrew
metonym
for ancient
Israel
. The altruistic immigrants
Isak
, his
brother
Abrom
, and
Davod
are also similar to Hebrew names.
StarTrek.com
located Zeon in the M34 Alpha system;
[1]
(X)
however, the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 513 located the planet in the M43 Alpha system. Furthermore, it was stated in the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
that Zeon was a
M-class
planet.
Apocrypha
In the
Star Trek Maps
(p. 27) and
The Worlds of the Federation
(p. 122), the name of the system was M43-Alpha. It was a
trinary
star system
, Zeon being the
fifth
planet. The total
population
of the two planets was 16.1 billion.
External links
Zeon (planet)
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Planets in TOS and TOS Remastered
at
Ex Astris Scientia

Zeta Borealis
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
23rd century
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Zeta Borealis
was a
planet
.
Christopher Pike
and
Joseph M'Benga
once undertook a
shuttle
flight to Zeta Borealis. The moment they landed, M'Benga kicked open the
storm doors
and
vomited
. Pike recalled the incident to M'Benga on the way to
Kenfori
in
2261
; he thought his flying had been at fault, while M'Benga blamed the
Vedalan
cigars
they had
smoked
the night before. (
SNW
: "
Shuttle to Kenfori
")

PAGE: Zetar
CATEGORIES: Planets

Zetar
Type:
Planet
Status:
Destroyed
(
23rd century
)
Native species:
Zetarians
(
extinct
)
Location:
Zetar system
Alpha Quadrant
Zetar
was a formerly
inhabited
planet
in the
Zetar system
. This planet was located in the
Alpha Quadrant
and was the
homeworld
for the
Zetarians
, an
extinct
humanoid
species
.
The planet was subject to a massive cataclysmic event which destroyed all corporeal life there. When all life died, only a hundred or so of the resident
civilization
lived on as a pattern of
lights
and
non-corporeal
energy
. (
TOS
: "
The Lights of Zetar
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.

at

This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain

.
Zimja
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(
3196
)
Location:
Kaminar
system
Alpha Quadrant
Zimja
was the
sixth
planet
in the
Kaminar
system
,
Alpha Quadrant
.
History
In the
23rd century
, this planet's system was in
Non-Federation space
.
When the
Venari Ral
laid
Omega-47 mines
around the core of Federation space in
3196
, this planet's system was within the blast radius of one of the mines. (
SA
: "
300th Night
", "
Rubincon
")
Stellar cartography
In
2257
, this planet was identified on
star charts
of the Kaminar system accessed by the
bridge crew
of the
USS
Discovery
. (
DIS
: "
The Sound of Thunder
")
In
3190
,
Saru
displayed a
hologram
of the Kaminar system, including Eresa, in the
Kaminar High Council
chambers. (
DIS
: "
Kobayashi Maru
")
Tim Peel
's team developed the planets as seen in "Kobayashi Maru".

Zimmerman's World
Type:
Planet
Status:
Intact
(

)

Alpha
or

Zimmerman's World
was a
planet
in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
In
2371
, its position was labeled on a
star chart
in the guidance system
S/COMS
panel on aboard the
Amargosa observatory
. (
Star Trek Generations
)
Background information
This planet was only mentioned in writing.

[1]
This location was named after
Herman Zimmerman
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Zoraster II
CATEGORIES: Planets
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Zoraster_II
======================================================================

Zoraster II
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
2370
)
Location:
Sector 2158
Beta Quadrant
Archaeological data analysis chart mentioning Zoraster II
Zoraster II
was the
second
planet
in its
star system
. The planet was located within
Sector 2158
of the
Beta Quadrant
.
In the
centuries
before the
24th century
, this planet was occupied by the
Romulans
. In
2370
, this was one of several planets where the
Romulan
ruins were raided by a band of
mercenaries
led by
Arctus Baran
. (
TNG
: "
Gambit, Part I
")
This planet's quadrant of origin is inferred based on the position of neighboring systems, such as the
Calder system
and the
Draken system
, as seen in the
star charts
appearing in the
Star Trek: Discovery
episode "
The War Without, The War Within
" and the
Star Trek: Picard
episode "
Maps and Legends
".
Information on this planet was from dialog and from an
okudagram
seen on the
bridge
of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. This graphic was put up for auction in 2018.
[1]

======================================================================
PAGE: Zyra
CATEGORIES: Locations
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Zyra
======================================================================

Zyra
Type:
Fixed location
Status:
Inhabited
(
2285
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Zyra
was a fixed location within
Federation
space
in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
. This location provided
outposts
like the
Regula I
research station
with
goods
.
A
cargo container
from Zyra was in storage in the
Genesis
cave
in the
planetoid
Regula
. (
Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan
)
This fixed location was only mentioned in writing.
Like
Bellus 4
, this reference was a
homage
to the film
When Worlds Collide
, in which Zyra was a planet to which Humanity escaped after its sun, Bellus, collided with Earth.
External link
Zyra
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Zytchin III
Type:
Planet
Status:
Habitable
(
24th century
)
Location:
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
Zytchin III
was the
inhabited
third
planet
in its
system
. This planet was located somewhere in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrant
.
This world was once visited by
Starfleet
officer
Jean-Luc Picard
, who spent four
days
on the planet. He later told
Dr.
Beverly Crusher
that he enjoyed his stay. When Dr. Crusher tried to recommend a
rest leave
for him in
2366
and reminded him of Zytchin, Picard claimed he had
lied
about his fondness for the
place
. (
TNG
: "
Captain's Holiday
")
In
3195
, this planet's name appeared on a
video wall
in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
Kids These Days
")
This planet was only mentioned in dialogue.
In the
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I"), Zytchin III was listed as a
Federation
member world
in
2378
.

Zytchin
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
